_is_plausible_user_id(value: str | int | float | None) -> int | None:\n    """Return int if value looks like a 
plausible Roblox user ID, else None.\n\n    Accepts only plain positive integers within the expected Roblox range.\n   
 Rejects anything that looks like a hash, token, or float.\n    """\n    if value is None:\n        return None\n

      return None\n    return n\n\n\ndef user_id_from_pref_xml(xml_text: str) -> int | None:\n    """Extract a 
plausible Roblox user ID from Android shared_pref XML.\n\n    Only reads elements whose key is in 
``_USER_ID_KEY_SCORES`` (an explicit\n    allowlist of known-safe public user ID field names).  Keys in the 
allowlist\n    bypass the general sensitivity gate because they are definitionally numeric\n    IDs, not tokens or 
cookies.  The value itself is validated to be a plain\n    positive integer within the expected Roblox user ID 
range.\n    """\n    try:\n        root_el = ET.fromstring(xml_text)\n    except ET.ParseError:\n        return None\n 
   best_score = -1\n    best_id: int | None = None\n    for child in root_el:\n        key = (child.attrib.get("name") 
or "").strip()\n        if not key:\n            continue\n        k = key.lower().replace("-", "_")\n        score = 
_USER_ID_KEY_SCORES.get(k, 0)\n        if score <= 0:\n            continue  # Not an approved user ID key ù skip 
entirely\n        # For keys in our allowlist we skip the general sensitivity gate:\n        # these are known public 
numeric fields, not tokens/cookies.\n        raw_val = child.attrib.get("value")\n        if raw_val is None and 
child.text:\n            raw_val = str(child.text).strip()\n        uid = _is_plausible_user_id(raw_val)\n        if 
uid is not None and score > best_score:\n            best_score = score\n            best_id = uid\n    return 
best_id\n\n\ndef _user_id_from_json_obj(data: Any, depth: int = 0) -> int | None:\n    if depth > 4 or not

           continue\n        # Known user ID key (allowlist) ù check value for a plausible numeric ID.\n        uid =

-> tuple[str | None, bool]:\n    """Extract the best-scoring safe username from Android shared_pref XML.\n\n    
Returns ``(username, is_display_name_only)`` where ``is_display_name_only``\n    is ``True`` when the best result is a 
display name (not a strict login username).\n    """\n    try:\n        root = ET.fromstring(xml_text)\n    except 
ET.ParseError:\n        return None, False\n    best_key = (-1, -1)  # (field_score, type_weight) type_weight: 
2=login, 1=display\n    best: str | None = None\n    best_tw = 0\n    for child in root:\n        key = 
(child.attrib.get("name") or "").strip()\n        if not key or is_sensitive_key_name(key):\n            continue\n    
    raw_val = _xml_element_text(child)\n        if raw_val is None or is_sensitive_value(raw_val):\n            
continue\n        if is_email_address(raw_val):\n            continue\n        candidate = 
sanitize_detected_username(raw_val)\n        if not candidate:\n            continue\n        score = 
_pref_key_score(key)\n        if score <= 0:\n            continue\n        if is_safe_username_value(candidate):\n    
        tw = 2\n        elif is_safe_display_name_value(candidate):\n            tw = 1\n        else:\n            
continue\n        if key.lower() == "name" and tw != 2:\n            continue\n        rank = (score, tw)\n        if 
rank > best_key:\n            best_key = rank\n            best = candidate\n            best_tw = tw\n    return 
best, (best_tw == 1 and best is not None)\n\n\ndef _clean_username(value: str) -> str:\n    return 
sanitize_detected_username(value)\n\n\ndef _candidate_pref_files(package: str) -> list[Path]:\n    base = 
Path("/data/data") / validate_package_name(package) / "shared_prefs"\n    try:\n        if not base.exists() or not 
base.is_dir():\n            return []\n    except PermissionError:\n        _log.debug(\n            "Permission 
denied checking %s ù root may be needed for username auto-detect", base\n        )\n        return []\n    hints = 
("account", "profile", "settings", "user", "username", "pkg_preferences", "roblox")\n    candidates: list[Path] = []\n 
   try:\n        for path in sorted(base.glob("*.xml")):\n            name = path.name.lower()\n            if any(h 
in name for h in hints):\n                candidates.append(path)\n        if not candidates:\n            candidates 
= sorted(base.glob("*.xml"))[:12]\n    except PermissionError:\n        _log.debug("Permission denied listing %s ù 
skipping", base)\n    return candidates[:24]\n\n\ndef detect_username_from_safe_prefs(\n    package: str, *,

non-root shared_prefs scan."""\n    limit = max_bytes or 64_000\n    for path in _candidate_pref_files(package):\n     
   try:\n            if path.stat().st_size > limit:\n                continue\n            username, display_only =

except OSError:\n            continue\n        if username:\n            return username, display_only\n    return 
None, False\n\n\ndef detect_android_app_label(package: str) -> str | None:\n    package = 
validate_package_name(package)\n    result = android.run_command(["dumpsys", "package", package], timeout=8)\n    if 
not result.ok:\n        return None\n    generic = {"roblox", "roblox player", "roblox app"}\n    for line in 
result.stdout.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.strip()\n        lowered = stripped.lower()\n        if not 
lowered.startswith("application-label"):\n            continue\n        _, _, raw_label = stripped.partition(":")\n    
    label = raw_label.strip().strip("\'\\"")\n        if label.lower() in generic:\n            continue\n        if 
is_safe_username_value(label) or is_safe_display_name_value(label):\n            return _clean_username(label)\n

| None:\n    """Read a root-protected file capped at max_bytes.  Uses root_access module."""\n    if max_bytes <= 0:\n 
       return None\n    content = root_access.read_root_file(abs_path, max_bytes=max_bytes, timeout=timeout)\n    
return content if content else None\n\n\ndef _root_read_shared_prefs_for_username(\n    package: str, max_bytes: int, 
timeout: int, root_tool: str = ""\n) -> tuple[str | None, bool]:\n    """Targeted root-based shared_prefs reader.\n\n  
  Returns ``(username, is_display_name_only)``.\n    Faster than a full find scan because it directly lists the 
shared_prefs directory and\n    reads hint-matching XML files in priority order. Used as a first-pass before the 
full\n    find-based scan in _root_scan_package_data.\n    """\n    pkg = validate_package_name(package)\n    base = 
f"/data/data/{pkg}/shared_prefs"\n    files = root_access.list_root_glob(f"{base}/*.xml", timeout=timeout, 
max_results=32)\n    if not files:\n        return None, False\n    _hints = ("account", "profile", "settings", 
"user", "username", "pkg_preferences", "roblox", "rbx")\n    filenames = [f.split("/")[-1] for f in files]\n    
priority = [f for f in files if any(h in f.lower() for h in _hints)]\n    fallback = [f for f in files if f not in 
priority]\n    ordered = (priority + fallback)[:24]\n    for abs_path in ordered:\n        content = 
_root_read_file_capped(abs_path, max_bytes, timeout)\n        if not content:\n            continue\n        username, 
display_only = username_from_pref_xml(content)\n        if username:\n            fname = abs_path.split("/")[-1]\n    
        _log.debug("Root shared_prefs found username in %s", fname)\n            return username, display_only\n    
return None, False\n\n\ndef _root_list_scan_files(package: str, max_kb: int, timeout: int, root_tool: str = "") -> 
list[str]:\n    pkg = validate_package_name(package)\n    base = f"/data/data/{pkg}"\n    # find: only under this 
package, caps count and file size\n    inner = (\n        f"find \'{base}\' -maxdepth 6 -type f -size -{int(max_kb)}k 
"\n        f"\\\\( -path \'*/shared_prefs/*.xml\' -o -name \'*.json\' \\\\) "\n        f"2>/dev/null | head -80"\n    
)\n    result = root_access.run_root_command(["sh", "-c", inner], timeout=timeout)\n    if not result.ok or 
result.timed_out:\n        return []\n    lines = [ln.strip() for ln in (result.stdout or "").splitlines() if 
ln.strip().startswith("/")]\n    return lines\n\n\ndef _username_from_json_text(text: str) -> str | None:\n    try:\n  
      data = json.loads(text)\n    except json.JSONDecodeError:\n        return None\n    return 
_username_from_json_obj(data)\n\n\ndef _json_key_score(key: str) -> int:\n    k = str(key or 
"").strip().lower().replace("-", "_")\n    if k in _PREF_KEY_SCORES:\n        return _PREF_KEY_SCORES[k]\n    if 
is_sensitive_key_name(k):\n        return -999\n    return 0\n\n\ndef _username_from_json_obj(data: Any, depth: int = 
0) -> str | None:\n    if depth > 3:\n        return None\n    if isinstance(data, dict):\n        best_key = (-1, 
-1)\n        best: str | None = None\n        for key, val in data.items():\n            if not isinstance(key, 
str):\n                continue\n            if is_sensitive_key_name(key):\n                continue\n            sk 
= _json_key_score(key)\n            if sk == 0:\n                continue\n            if isinstance(val, dict):\n     
           nested = _username_from_json_obj(val, depth + 1)\n                if nested:\n                    return 
nested\n                continue\n            if not isinstance(val, str):\n                continue\n            if 
is_sensitive_value(val):\n                continue\n            cand = sanitize_detected_username(val)\n            if 
not cand:\n                continue\n            if is_safe_username_value(cand):\n                tw = 2\n            
elif is_safe_display_name_value(cand):\n                tw = 1\n            else:\n                continue\n          
  if key.lower() == "name" and tw != 2:\n                continue\n            rank = (sk, tw)\n            if rank > 
best_key:\n                best_key = rank\n                best = cand\n        return best\n    return None\n\n\ndef 
_try_sqlite_roblox_user(db_abs: str) -> str | None:\n    if _sqlite_username_hook is not None:\n        try:\n         
   u = _sqlite_username_hook(db_abs)\n        except Exception:\n            return None\n        if u and 
sanitize_detected_username(u):\n            c = sanitize_detected_username(u)\n            if 
is_safe_username_value(c) or is_safe_display_name_value(c):\n                return c\n        return None\n    return 
None\n\n\ndef _root_scan_package_data(\n    package: str, settings: dict[str, Any]\n) -> tuple[str | None, str | None, 
bool]:\n    """Scan package data for username via root.\n\n    Returns ``(username, source, is_display_name_only)``.\n 
   """\n    timeout = int(settings.get("scan_timeout_seconds", 8))\n    max_kb = int(settings.get("max_file_size_kb", 
512))\n    max_bytes = max_kb * 1024\n\n    if not root_access.has_root():\n        _log.info("Root unavailable, 
skipped package data scan")\n        return None, None, False\n\n    # Fast first pass: directly read shared_prefs XML 
via root (no find needed).\n    fast_user, fast_display_only = _root_read_shared_prefs_for_username(package, 
max_bytes, timeout)\n    if fast_user:\n        u = sanitize_detected_username(fast_user)\n        if u:\n            
return u, "root_shared_prefs", fast_display_only\n\n    # Full scan: find XML/JSON files under the package data 
directory.\n    paths = _root_list_scan_files(package, max_kb=max_kb, timeout=timeout)\n    best: str | None = None\n  
  best_src: str | None = None\n    best_rank: tuple[int, int] = (-1, -1)\n    best_display_only = False\n    for 
abs_path in paths:\n        if not abs_path.startswith(f"/data/data/{validate_package_name(package)}/"):\n            
continue\n        if abs_path.endswith(".db"):\n            # SQLite databases ù try real detection first, then fall 
back to hook\n            db_user: str | None = None\n            db_uid: int = 0\n            db_display_only = 
False\n            db_src = "root_sqlite"  # keep legacy label as default\n            try:\n                from . 
import sqlite_account_detect as _sqdet\n                r = _sqdet._scan_db_file(abs_path, abs_path)\n                
if r.user_id > 0:\n                    db_uid = r.user_id\n                    db_src = "sqlite_user_id"\n             
   elif r.username:\n                    db_user = r.username\n                    db_display_only = 
r.is_display_name_only\n                    db_src = r.source  # e.g. "sqlite_username" or 
"sqlite_display_name_hint"\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                pass\n            if 
db_uid:\n                # User ID found ù handled by detect_roblox_user_id path; skip username flow\n                
continue\n            if not db_user:\n                # Fall back to test hook (backwards-compatible source label)\n  
              db_user = _try_sqlite_roblox_user(abs_path)\n                db_src = "root_sqlite"\n            if 
db_user:\n                cand = sanitize_detected_username(db_user)\n                if cand:\n                    
rank = (100 if is_safe_username_value(cand) else 50, 2)\n                    if rank > best_rank:\n                    
    best, best_src, best_rank, best_display_only = cand, db_src, rank, db_display_only\n            continue\n        
raw = _root_read_file_capped(abs_path, min(max_bytes, 262_144), timeout)\n        if not raw:\n            continue\n  
      if abs_path.endswith(".xml"):\n            u, d_only = username_from_pref_xml(raw)\n            if u:\n          
      cand = sanitize_detected_username(u)\n                rank = (95, 2 if is_safe_username_value(cand) else 1)\n    
            if rank > best_rank:\n                    best, best_src, best_rank, best_display_only = cand, 
"root_pref", rank, d_only\n        elif abs_path.endswith(".json"):\n            u = _username_from_json_text(raw)\n   
         if u:\n                cand = sanitize_detected_username(u)\n                rank = (90, 2 if 
is_safe_username_value(cand) else 1)\n                if rank > best_rank:\n                    best, best_src, 
best_rank, best_display_only = cand, "root_json", rank, False\n    if best:\n        return best, best_src or 
"root_scan", best_display_only\n    return None, None, False\n\n\ndef detect_account_username(\n    package_name: 
str,\n    *,\n    entry: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n    config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n    use_root: bool 
= True,\n    respect_config_manual: bool = True,\n) -> AccountDetectionResult | None:\n    """Resolve display username 
for one package (config \u2192 label \u2192 prefs \u2192 root scan)."""\n    package_name = 
validate_package_name(package_name)\n    settings = _settings(config)\n    if not settings.get("enabled", True):\n     
   return None\n\n    if respect_config_manual and entry:\n        src = str(entry.get("username_source") or 
"").strip().lower()\n        manual_user = validate_account_username(entry.get("account_username", ""))\n        if 
src == "manual" and manual_user:\n            u = sanitize_detected_username(manual_user)\n            return 
AccountDetectionResult(u, "config_manual") if u else None\n\n    label = detect_android_app_label(package_name)\n    
if label:\n        u = sanitize_detected_username(label)\n        if u:\n            if 
settings.get("cache_detected_usernames", True):\n                set_cached_account_username(package_name, u)\n        
    _log.info("Detected username for %s: %s", package_name, u)\n            return AccountDetectionResult(u, 
"android_app_label")\n\n    pref_user, pref_display_only = detect_username_from_safe_prefs(\n        package_name, 
max_bytes=settings.get("max_file_size_kb", 512) * 1024\n    )\n    if pref_user:\n        u = 
sanitize_detected_username(pref_user)\n        if u:\n            if settings.get("cache_detected_usernames", True):\n 
               set_cached_account_username(package_name, u)\n            _log.info("Detected username for %s: %s", 
package_name, u)\n            return AccountDetectionResult(u, "detected_safe_pref", 
is_display_name_only=pref_display_only)\n\n    if use_root and settings.get("use_root", True):\n        try:\n         
   root_user, rsrc, root_display_only = _root_scan_package_data(package_name, settings)\n        except OSError:\n     
       root_user, rsrc, root_display_only = None, None, False\n        if root_user:\n            u = 
sanitize_detected_username(root_user)\n            if u:\n                if settings.get("cache_detected_usernames", 
True):\n                    set_cached_account_username(package_name, u)\n                _log.info("Detected username 
for %s: %s", package_name, u)\n                return AccountDetectionResult(u, rsrc or "root_scan", 
is_display_name_only=root_display_only)\n\n    _log.info("No username found for %s", package_name)\n    return 
None\n\n\ndef detect_account_usernames_for_packages(\n    packages: list[dict[str, Any]],\n    *,\n    config: 
dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n    use_root: bool = True,\n    respect_config_manual: bool = False,\n) -> 
list[tuple[dict[str, Any], AccountDetectionResult | None]]:\n    """Run detection per package entry. Returns (entry, 
result) pairs in order."""\n    out: list[tuple[dict[str, Any], AccountDetectionResult | None]] = []\n    for entry in 
packages:\n        if not isinstance(entry, dict):\n            continue\n        pkg = str(entry.get("package") or 
"").strip()\n        if not pkg:\n            continue\n        try:\n            validate_package_name(pkg)\n

detect_account_username(\n            pkg,\n            entry=entry,\n            config=config,\n            
use_root=use_root,\n            respect_config_manual=respect_config_manual,\n        )\n        out.append((entry, 
res))\n    return out\n\n\ndef detect_account_username_for_package(package: str) -> AccountDetectionResult | None:\n

entry=None, config=None, use_root=True, respect_config_manual=False)\n\n"""Centralized Android and optional-root 
command execution."""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport base64\nfrom collections import Counter\nimport 
json\nimport os\nimport re\nimport shlex\nimport shutil\nimport socket\nimport subprocess\nimport threading\nimport 
time\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Iterable\n\n# Global lock: 
serialize ALL subprocess.run() calls to prevent concurrent\n# fork() in a multithreaded Termux/Python 3.13 process.  
Multiple\n# _PackageWorker threads calling subprocess.run() simultaneously is the\n# primary cause of SIGSEGV (probe 
p-316b3b040d).\n# Locking serializes health-check commands across workers ù each command\n# still completes quickly 
(PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS), so total overhead\n# for 2-3 workers is acceptable vs. the crash risk.\n_subprocess_lock = 
threading.Lock()\n\nfrom .config import ConfigError, is_valid_package_name, normalize_package_detection_hint, 
validate_package_name\nfrom .constants import (\n    DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE,\n    DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE_HINTS,\n    
LAUNCH_MODES,\n    PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n    ROOT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n)\nfrom .platform_detect import 
detect_public_download_dir, get_android_release, get_android_sdk\nfrom .url_utils import UrlValidationError, 
detect_launch_mode_from_url, mask_launch_url, to_roblox_deep_link, 
validate_launch_url\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass RobloxPackageCandidate:\n    package: str\n    app_name: str\n 
   launchable: bool\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass CommandResult:\n    args: tuple[str, ...]\n    returncode: 
int\n    stdout: str\n    stderr: str\n    timed_out: bool = False\n\n    @property\n    def ok(self) -> bool:\n       
 return self.returncode == 0 and not self.timed_out\n\n    @property\n    def summary(self) -> str:\n        text = 
(self.stderr or self.stdout or "").strip()\n        return text[:500]\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass RootInfo:\n  
  available: bool\n    tool: str | None = None\n    detail: str = ""\n\n\ndef _safe_env() -> dict[str, str]:\n    env 
= os.environ.copy()\n    env.setdefault("LC_ALL", "C")\n    return env\n\n\ndef _stringify_args(args: Iterable[str]) 
-> list[str]:\n    return [str(arg) for arg in args]\n\n\n# Android system binaries that live in /system/bin (or 
/system/xbin) but\n# are NOT in Termux\'s default PATH.  Without this resolution every call to\n# 
dumpsys/wm/cmd/settings/etc. fails with ENOENT in Termux, silently\n# breaking state detection, window readback, and 
layout discovery.\n# Confirmed from real cloud-phone probe (Samsung SM-N9810 fingerprint\n# c1q:13/TP1A.220624.014, 
probe id p-368a65d699).\n_ANDROID_SYSTEM_BINARIES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({\n    "am", "cmd", "dumpsys", 
"getprop", "input", "logcat", "monkey", "pm",\n    "pgrep", "pidof", "ps", "service", "settings", "setprop", 
"wm",\n})\n_ANDROID_BIN_DIRS: tuple[str, ...] = ("/system/bin", "/system/xbin", "/vendor/bin")\n\n\ndef 
_resolve_android_binary(name: str) -> str:\n    """Return absolute path for a known Android binary, or *name* 
unchanged.\n\n    Skips resolution if *name* is already an absolute or relative path, or\n    if it isn\'t on our 
short list of expected Android binaries.  This is\n    deliberately conservative so we never accidentally shadow a 
Termux\n    binary of the same name.\n    """\n    if not name or "/" in name:\n        return name\n    if name not 
in _ANDROID_SYSTEM_BINARIES:\n        return name\n    for bin_dir in _ANDROID_BIN_DIRS:\n        candidate = 
f"{bin_dir}/{name}"\n        if os.path.isfile(candidate) and os.access(candidate, os.X_OK):\n            return 
candidate\n    return name\n\n\ndef _maybe_resolve_first_arg(cmd: list[str]) -> list[str]:\n    if not cmd:\n        
return cmd\n    resolved = _resolve_android_binary(cmd[0])\n    if resolved == cmd[0]:\n        return cmd\n    return 
[resolved] + list(cmd[1:])\n\n\ndef run_command(args: Iterable[str], *, timeout: int = PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS) -> 
CommandResult:\n    """Run a local command with timeout and captured output.\n\n    For known Android system binaries 
(dumpsys/wm/cmd/settings/...), the\n    first argument is auto-resolved to its ``/system/bin/`` absolute path\n    
when present.  This is essential on Termux where the default ``$PATH``\n    does NOT include ``/system/bin``.\n\n    
All subprocess.run() calls are serialized through ``_subprocess_lock``\n    to prevent concurrent fork() from multiple 
worker threads, which is the\n    primary cause of SIGSEGV on Termux + Python 3.13 (probe p-316b3b040d).\n    """\n    
cmd = _maybe_resolve_first_arg(_stringify_args(args))\n    with _subprocess_lock:\n        try:\n            completed 
= subprocess.run(\n                cmd,\n                stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,\n                
stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                text=True,\n                
timeout=timeout,\n                shell=False,\n                env=_safe_env(),\n            )\n            return 
CommandResult(tuple(cmd), completed.returncode, completed.stdout.strip(), completed.stderr.strip())\n        except 
FileNotFoundError as exc:\n            # If the bare name was on our list we already tried /system/bin\n            # 
above; nothing more to do.  Otherwise still report 127 cleanly.\n            return CommandResult(tuple(cmd), 127, "", 
str(exc))\n        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:\n            stdout = exc.stdout.decode(errors="replace") 
if isinstance(exc.stdout, bytes) else (exc.stdout or "")\n            stderr = exc.stderr.decode(errors="replace") if 
isinstance(exc.stderr, bytes) else (exc.stderr or "")\n            return CommandResult(tuple(cmd), 124, 
stdout.strip(), stderr.strip() or "command timed out", timed_out=True)\n\n\ndef is_termux() -> bool:\n    prefix = 
os.environ.get("PREFIX", "")\n    return bool(os.environ.get("TERMUX_VERSION") or "com.termux" in prefix or 
prefix.startswith("/data/data/com.termux"))\n\n\ndef get_android_version() -> str | None:\n    release = 
get_android_release()\n    return None if release == "unknown" else release\n\n\ndef get_android_sdk_version() -> str 
| None:\n    sdk = get_android_sdk()\n    return None if sdk == "unknown" else sdk\n\n\ndef has_android_shell_access() 
-> bool:\n    result = run_command(["getprop", "ro.product.model"], timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n    return 
result.ok\n\n\ndef has_storage_permission() -> bool:\n    candidates = [\n        Path("/sdcard/Download"),\n        
Path("/sdcard/download"),\n        Path("/storage/emulated/0/Download"),\n        
Path("/storage/emulated/0/download"),\n        Path.home() / "storage" / "shared",\n    ]\n    return 
any(path.exists() and os.access(path, os.R_OK) for path in candidates)\n\n\ndef get_download_dir() -> str:\n    return 
detect_public_download_dir()\n\n\ndef command_exists(name: str) -> bool:\n    return shutil.which(name) is not 
None\n\n\ndef package_manager_result() -> CommandResult:\n    if os.name == "nt":\n        return 
CommandResult(("cmd", "package", "list", "packages"), 127, "", "Android package manager is unavailable on Windows")\n  
  result = run_command(["cmd", "package", "list", "packages"], timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n    if result.ok:\n   
     return result\n    return run_command(["pm", "list", "packages"], timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n\n\ndef 
list_packages() -> list[str]:\n    result = package_manager_result()\n    if not result.ok:\n        return []\n    
packages: list[str] = []\n    for line in result.stdout.splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if 
line.startswith("package:"):\n            line = line[len("package:") :]\n        if line and 
is_valid_package_name(line):\n            packages.append(line)\n    return sorted(set(packages))\n\n\ndef 
_safe_detection_hints(hints: Iterable[str] | None = None) -> list[str]:\n    source = hints or 
DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE_HINTS\n    validated: list[str] = []\n    for hint in source:\n        try:\n            
cleaned = normalize_package_detection_hint(str(hint))\n        except ConfigError:\n            continue\n        if 
cleaned not in validated:\n            validated.append(cleaned)\n    return validated or 
list(DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE_HINTS)\n\n\ndef find_roblox_packages(hints: Iterable[str] | None = None) -> list[str]:\n   
 packages = list_packages()\n    detection_hints = _safe_detection_hints(hints)\n    found = [pkg for pkg in packages 
if any(hint in pkg.lower() for hint in detection_hints)]\n    if DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE in found:\n        
found.remove(DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE)\n        found.insert(0, DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE)\n    elif DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE 
in packages:\n        found.insert(0, DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE)\n    return found\n\n\ndef 
is_launchable_package(package: str) -> bool:\n    """True when cmd package resolve-activity finds a launcher 
component."""\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n    cmd_bin = _find_command("cmd", "/system/bin/cmd")\n   
 if not cmd_bin:\n        return False\n    resolve = run_command(\n        [cmd_bin, "package", "resolve-activity", 
"--brief", package],\n        timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n    )\n    if not resolve.ok or package not in 
resolve.stdout:\n        return False\n    return True\n\n\ndef get_application_label(package: str) -> str:\n    
"""Best-effort app label from dumpsys package (first ~20k chars only)."""\n    package = 
validate_package_name(package)\n    result = run_command(["dumpsys", "package", package], 
timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n    if not result.ok:\n        return ""\n    text = result.stdout[:20000]\n    for 
pattern in (\n        r\'application-label="([^"]+)"\',\n        r"application-label=\'([^\']+)\'",\n        
r\'android:label="([^"]+)"\',\n    ):\n        m = re.search(pattern, text)\n        if m:\n            lab = 
m.group(1).strip()\n            if lab and lab.lower() != "null":\n                return lab[:120]\n    m2 = 
re.search(r"nonLocalizedLabel=([^\\s}]+)", text)\n    if m2:\n        lab = m2.group(1).strip().strip(\'"\')\n        
if lab and lab.lower() != "null":\n            return lab[:120]\n    return ""\n\n\ndef 
get_application_label_cached(package: str, cache: dict[str, str]) -> str:\n    if package not in cache:\n        
cache[package] = get_application_label(package)\n    return cache[package]\n\n\ndef 
is_launchable_package_cached(package: str, cache: dict[str, bool]) -> bool:\n    if package not in cache:\n        
cache[package] = is_launchable_package(package)\n    return cache[package]\n\n\ndef _package_name_matches_hints(pkg: 
str, hints: list[str]) -> bool:\n    lower = pkg.lower()\n    return any(hint in lower for hint in hints)\n\n\ndef 
_label_matches_roblox_signals(label: str, hints: list[str]) -> bool:\n    low = label.lower()\n    extra = ("roblox", 
"rblx", "blox")\n    return any(x in low for x in list(hints) + list(extra))\n\n\n_DISCOVERY_RESULT_CACHE: dict[str, 
Any] = {"key": None, "t": 0.0, "rows": []}\n_DISCOVERY_CACHE_SECONDS = 12.0\n\n\ndef 
discover_roblox_package_candidates(\n    hints: Iterable[str] | None = None,\n    *,\n    include_launchable_only: 
bool = True,\n    detection_enabled: bool = True,\n) -> list[RobloxPackageCandidate]:\n    """Discovery: name-filter 
first so dumpsys runs only for likely packages (cached per call)."""\n    if not detection_enabled:\n        return 
[]\n    detection_hints = _safe_detection_hints(hints)\n    cache_key = (tuple(detection_hints), 
bool(include_launchable_only), bool(detection_enabled))\n    now = time.monotonic()\n    if (\n        
_DISCOVERY_RESULT_CACHE["key"] == cache_key\n        and now - float(_DISCOVERY_RESULT_CACHE["t"]) < 
_DISCOVERY_CACHE_SECONDS\n    ):\n        return list(_DISCOVERY_RESULT_CACHE["rows"])\n    packages = 
list_packages()\n    label_cache: dict[str, str] = {}\n    launch_cache: dict[str, bool] = {}\n    candidate_pkgs = 
[pkg for pkg in packages if pkg == DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE or _package_name_matches_hints(pkg, detection_hints)]\n    
if DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE in packages and DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE not in candidate_pkgs:\n        
candidate_pkgs.insert(0, DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE)\n    out: list[RobloxPackageCandidate] = []\n    for pkg in 
sorted(set(candidate_pkgs), key=lambda p: (0 if p == DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE else 1, p)):\n        label = 
get_application_label_cached(pkg, label_cache)\n        app_name = label or pkg.rsplit(".", 1)[-1]\n        name_match 
= _package_name_matches_hints(pkg, detection_hints) or pkg == DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE\n        label_match = 
bool(label) and _label_matches_roblox_signals(label, detection_hints)\n        if not name_match and not 
label_match:\n            continue\n        launchable = is_launchable_package_cached(pkg, launch_cache)\n        if 
include_launchable_only and not launchable:\n            continue\n        
out.append(RobloxPackageCandidate(package=pkg, app_name=app_name, launchable=launchable))\n\n    def _sort_key(c: 
RobloxPackageCandidate) -> tuple[int, str]:\n        return (0 if c.package == DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE else 1, 
c.package)\n\n    out.sort(key=_sort_key)\n    _DISCOVERY_RESULT_CACHE["key"] = cache_key\n    
_DISCOVERY_RESULT_CACHE["t"] = now\n    _DISCOVERY_RESULT_CACHE["rows"] = list(out)\n    return out\n\n\ndef 
package_installed(package: str) -> bool:\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n    result = 
run_command(["cmd", "package", "resolve-activity", "--brief", package], timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n    if 
result.ok and package in result.stdout:\n        return True\n    return package in list_packages()\n\n\ndef 
detect_root() -> RootInfo:\n    """Detect available su/tsu root without hanging for permission."""\n    candidates = 
[tool for tool in ("tsu", "su") if command_exists(tool)]\n    if not candidates:\n        return RootInfo(False, None, 
"su/tsu not found")\n    for tool in candidates:\n        result = run_command([tool, "-c", "id"], 
timeout=ROOT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n        output = f"{result.stdout} {result.stderr}".strip()\n        if result.ok and 
"uid=0" in output:\n            return RootInfo(True, tool, output[:200])\n        if result.timed_out:\n            
return RootInfo(False, tool, "root check timed out or permission prompt was not accepted")\n    return RootInfo(False, 
candidates[0], "root command failed or permission was denied")\n\n\ndef run_root_command(args: Iterable[str], *, 
root_tool: str | None = None, timeout: int = PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS) -> CommandResult:\n    """Run an explicit root 
command through su/tsu.\n\n    Root shell entry is centralized here so root usage stays auditable.\n\n    For known 
Android system binaries the first argument is auto-resolved\n    to its absolute ``/system/bin/`` path so the ``su`` 
subshell ù which\n    typically does NOT include Termux\'s PATH ù can still find them.\n    """\n    tool = root_tool 
or detect_root().tool\n    if not tool:\n        return CommandResult(tuple(_stringify_args(args)), 127, "", "root 
tool unavailable")\n    tokens = _maybe_resolve_first_arg(_stringify_args(args))\n    command = shlex.join(tokens)\n   
 return run_command([tool, "-c", command], timeout=timeout)\n\n\ndef run_android_command(\n    args: Iterable[str],\n  
  *,\n    timeout: int = PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n    prefer_root: bool = False,\n    root_fallback_markers: 
tuple[str, ...] = (\n        "Permission Denial",\n        "INTERACT_ACROSS_USERS",\n        "Operation not 
permitted",\n        "permission denied",\n    ),\n) -> CommandResult:\n    """Run an Android system command, 
optionally retrying through ``su``.\n\n    If ``prefer_root`` is true and a root tool is available, the command is\n   
 routed through ``su`` straight away.  Otherwise we run unprivileged\n    first and only fall back to root if stderr 
contains one of the\n    permission-related markers (real cloud-phone behaviour: ``settings\n    list global`` returns 
``Permission Denial ... INTERACT_ACROSS_USERS``\n    unless run as root).\n\n    This wrapper exists because many 
Android commands (``settings``,\n    ``dumpsys SurfaceFlinger`` on some Samsung builds, ``cmd activity\n    
resize-task``) succeed only via root on a given device, but root\n    isn\'t always available, and we don\'t want to 
pay the ``su`` cost\n    every time.\n    """\n    if prefer_root:\n        root = detect_root()\n        if 
root.available:\n            return run_root_command(args, root_tool=root.tool, timeout=timeout)\n        # Fall 
through to plain run when root unavailable.\n    res = run_command(args, timeout=timeout)\n    if res.ok or 
res.timed_out:\n        return res\n    err = res.stderr or ""\n    if any(marker in err for marker in 
root_fallback_markers):\n        root = detect_root()\n        if root.available:\n            return 
run_root_command(args, root_tool=root.tool, timeout=timeout)\n    return res\n\n\ndef force_stop_package(package: str, 
root_info: RootInfo | None = None) -> CommandResult:\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n    info = 
root_info or detect_root()\n    if not info.available:\n        return CommandResult(("am", "force-stop", package), 
126, "", "root unavailable")\n    return run_root_command(["am", "force-stop", package], root_tool=info.tool, 
timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n\n\ndef get_package_pid(package: str, root_info: RootInfo | None = None) -> str:\n   
 """Return the main PID of a running package process, or \'\' if not running."""\n    package = 
validate_package_name(package)\n    info = root_info or detect_root()\n    if not info.available or not info.tool:\n   
     return ""\n    res = run_root_command(["pidof", "-s", package], root_tool=info.tool, timeout=5)\n    if res.ok 
and res.stdout.strip().isdigit():\n        return res.stdout.strip()\n    # Fallback: busybox ps / ps -A grep\n    sh 
= (\n        f"ps -A 2>/dev/null | grep -F \'{package}\' | grep -v grep"\n        f" | awk \'{{print $2}}\' | head 
-n1"\n    )\n    res2 = run_root_command(["sh", "-c", sh], root_tool=info.tool, timeout=6)\n    pid = (res2.stdout or 
"").strip()\n    return pid if pid.isdigit() else ""\n\n\ndef clear_package_cache_verified(\n    package: str,\n    
*,\n    max_retries: int = 2,\n) -> dict[str, object]:\n    """Clear package cache/code_cache dirs (never 
shared_prefs/databases/files).\n\n    Returns a result dict with keys:\n      success (bool), skipped (bool), 
skipped_reason (str),\n      cache_paths (list[str]), size_before_bytes (int), size_after_bytes (int),\n      attempts 
(int), error (str).\n\n    Safe: validates package name, uses root, targets only cache/code_cache.\n    Does NOT use 
``pm clear`` (which wipes app data).\n    """\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n    root_info = 
detect_root()\n    if not root_info.available or not root_info.tool:\n        return {\n            "success": False, 
"skipped": True,\n            "skipped_reason": "root_unavailable",\n            "cache_paths": [], 
"size_before_bytes": 0, "size_after_bytes": 0,\n            "attempts": 0, "error": "",\n        }\n\n    # Safe 
targets ù only cache/code_cache, never user data.\n    # /data/user/0 is the modern path (Android 5+); /data/data is 
the legacy\n    # path.  On most devices they resolve to the same inode via symlink.\n    # We prefer /data/user/0 and 
fall back to /data/data.\n    candidates = [\n        f"/data/user/0/{package}/cache",\n        
f"/data/user/0/{package}/code_cache",\n        f"/data/data/{package}/cache",\n        
f"/data/data/{package}/code_cache",\n    ]\n\n    def _exists(path: str) -> bool:\n        r = 
run_root_command(["test", "-d", path], root_tool=root_info.tool, timeout=5)\n        return r.ok\n\n    def 
_size(path: str) -> int:\n        sh = f"find {shlex.quote(path)} -mindepth 1 -type f 2>/dev/null | wc -l"\n        r 
= run_root_command(["sh", "-c", sh], root_tool=root_info.tool, timeout=10)\n        val = (r.stdout or "").strip()\n   
     return int(val) if val.isdigit() else 0\n\n    existing = [p for p in candidates if _exists(p)]\n    # 
Deduplicate: if both /data/user/0 and /data/data exist, prefer user/0.\n    user0 = [p for p in existing if 
"/data/user/0/" in p]\n    data_data = [p for p in existing if "/data/data/" in p]\n    active = user0 if user0 else 
data_data\n\n    if not active:\n        return {\n            "success": True, "skipped": True,\n            
"skipped_reason": "no_cache_dirs",\n            "cache_paths": [], "size_before_bytes": 0, "size_after_bytes": 0,\n    
        "attempts": 0, "error": "",\n        }\n\n    size_before = sum(_size(p) for p in active)\n    size_after = 
size_before\n    last_error = ""\n\n    for attempt in range(1, max_retries + 2):\n        for path in active:\n       
     res = run_root_command(\n                ["find", path, "-mindepth", "1", "-delete"],\n                
root_tool=root_info.tool,\n                timeout=30,\n            )\n            if not res.ok and res.returncode 
not in (0, 1):\n                last_error = (res.stderr or "")[:120]\n\n        size_after = sum(_size(p) for p in 
active)\n        if size_after == 0:\n            return {\n                "success": True, "skipped": False, 
"skipped_reason": "",\n                "cache_paths": active,\n                "size_before_bytes": size_before, 
"size_after_bytes": 0,\n                "attempts": attempt, "error": "",\n            }\n        if attempt <= 
max_retries:\n            time.sleep(0.5)\n\n    return {\n        "success": False, "skipped": False,\n        
"skipped_reason": "size_nonzero_after_clear",\n        "cache_paths": active,\n        "size_before_bytes": 
size_before, "size_after_bytes": size_after,\n        "attempts": max_retries + 1, "error": last_error,\n    
}\n\n\ndef mute_package_audio(\n    package: str,\n    root_info: RootInfo | None = None,\n) -> dict[str, object]:\n   
 """Attempt to mute audio for a specific package via appops (root required).\n\n    Tries ``appops set <pkg> 
PLAY_AUDIO deny`` first, then the ``cmd appops``\n    variant.  Both are per-package and do NOT affect the system 
audio stream\n    or Termux.\n\n    Returns a dict with keys:\n      success (bool), method (str), target_volume 
(int=0),\n      skipped_reason (str), error (str).\n    """\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n    info = 
root_info or detect_root()\n    if not info.available or not info.tool:\n        return {\n            "success": 
False, "method": "none",\n            "target_volume": 0,\n            "skipped_reason": "root_unavailable", "error": 
"",\n        }\n\n    # Method 1: appops set <pkg> PLAY_AUDIO deny  (AOSP / stock Android)\n    r1 = 
run_root_command(\n        ["appops", "set", package, "PLAY_AUDIO", "deny"],\n        root_tool=info.tool, 
timeout=8,\n    )\n    if r1.ok:\n        return {\n            "success": True, "method": "appops_play_audio_deny",\n 
           "target_volume": 0, "skipped_reason": "", "error": "",\n        }\n\n    # Method 2: cmd appops set (some 
Android flavours / emulators)\n    r2 = run_root_command(\n        ["cmd", "appops", "set", package, 
"android:play_audio", "deny"],\n        root_tool=info.tool, timeout=8,\n    )\n    if r2.ok:\n        return {\n      
      "success": True, "method": "cmd_appops_play_audio_deny",\n            "target_volume": 0, "skipped_reason": "", 
"error": "",\n        }\n\n    err = ((r1.stderr or "") + " " + (r2.stderr or "")).strip()[:120]\n    return {\n       
 "success": False, "method": "appops_play_audio_deny",\n        "target_volume": 0,\n        "skipped_reason": 
"unsupported_or_denied", "error": err,\n    }\n\n\ndef _find_command(*names: str) -> str | None:\n    """Find the 
first available command from candidates.\n\n    Short names (no /) are searched in PATH via shutil.which.\n    
Absolute paths (starting with /) are checked for existence and executability.\n    """\n    for name in names:\n       
 if name.startswith("/"):\n            if os.path.isfile(name) and os.access(name, os.X_OK):\n                return 
name\n        else:\n            found = shutil.which(name)\n            if found:\n                return found\n    
return None\n\n\ndef _parse_activity_component(stdout: str, package: str) -> str | None:\n    """Parse the activity 
component from cmd package resolve-activity --brief output.\n\n    Returns a string like 
"com.roblox.client/.activity.SplashActivity" or None.\n    """\n    for line in 
reversed(stdout.strip().splitlines()):\n        line = line.strip()\n        if "/" in line and package in line:\n     
       for part in line.split():\n                if "/" in part and package in part:\n                    return 
part\n    return None\n\n\ndef launch_app(package: str) -> CommandResult:\n    """Launch a Roblox package using the 
best available Android launch method.\n\n    Tries in order:\n    1. am start -a android.intent.action.MAIN -c 
android.intent.category.LAUNCHER -p <package>\n    2. cmd package resolve-activity --brief <package> then am start -n 
<component>\n    3. monkey -p <package> -c android.intent.category.LAUNCHER 1 (optional fallback)\n\n    Never raises 
FileNotFoundError. Returns a CommandResult indicating the outcome.\n    """\n    package = 
validate_package_name(package)\n    am = _find_command("am", "/system/bin/am")\n    cmd_bin = _find_command("cmd", 
"/system/bin/cmd")\n    monkey_bin = _find_command("monkey", "/system/bin/monkey")\n\n    last_result: CommandResult | 
None = None\n\n    # Method 1: am start with MAIN + LAUNCHER intent, freeform-windowed.\n    # ``--windowingMode 5`` = 
WINDOWING_MODE_FREEFORM.  When the framework\n    # rejects the flag (older Android, missing perm) we transparently 
fall\n    # back to the unflagged launch; this keeps the launcher robust across\n    # OEMs while letting App Cloner 
XML bounds steer the window on hosts\n    # that DO support freeform (probe-confirmed on cloud-phone SM-N9810).\n    
if am:\n        base_main = [\n            am, "start",\n            "-a", "android.intent.action.MAIN",\n            
"-c", "android.intent.category.LAUNCHER",\n            "-p", package,\n        ]\n        result = run_command(\n      
      base_main[:2] + ["--windowingMode", "5"] + base_main[2:],\n            timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n        
)\n        if result.ok:\n            return result\n        unflagged = run_command(base_main, 
timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n        if unflagged.ok:\n            return unflagged\n        last_result = 
unflagged\n\n    # Method 2: resolve-activity to get exact component, then am start -n\n    if cmd_bin and am:\n       
 resolve = run_command(\n            [cmd_bin, "package", "resolve-activity", "--brief", package],\n            
timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n        )\n        if resolve.ok:\n            component = 
_parse_activity_component(resolve.stdout, package)\n            if component:\n                ff = run_command(\n     
               [am, "start", "--windowingMode", "5", "-n", component],\n                    
timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n                )\n                if ff.ok:\n                    return ff\n        
        result2 = run_command([am, "start", "-n", component], timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n                if 
result2.ok:\n                    return result2\n                last_result = result2\n\n    # Method 3: monkey 
fallback (optional, may not be available)\n    if monkey_bin:\n        result3 = run_command(\n            
[monkey_bin, "-p", package, "-c", "android.intent.category.LAUNCHER", "1"],\n            
timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n        )\n        if result3.ok:\n            return result3\n        last_result = 
result3\n\n    # All methods failed ù return best available failure result\n    if last_result:\n        return 
last_result\n    return CommandResult(\n        ("am", "start", package),\n        127,\n        "",\n        "Android 
launcher commands unavailable: am/cmd/monkey not found",\n    )\n\n\ndef _legacy_launch_url_pre_root_unused(package: 
str, url: str, launch_mode: str) -> CommandResult:\n    """Launch a deep-link URL in *package* (best-effort freeform 
window).\n\n    On Android builds where ``cmd activity start-activity --windowingMode``\n    is supported 
(probe-confirmed on cloud-phone SM-N9810/Android 13),\n    we ask the framework to put the new activity into windowing 
mode 5\n    (``WINDOWING_MODE_FREEFORM``) so the App Cloner XML position keys are\n    honored.  If the framework 
rejects the flag (older Android, missing\n    permission, etc.) we transparently retry without the flag so callers\n   
 never see a launch regression.\n\n    Web share-link URLs (``https://www.roblox.com/share?...``) are\n    
automatically converted to their ``roblox://`` deep-link equivalent\n    before being sent to ``am start``.  Without 
this conversion Android\n    routes the URL to the browser instead of Roblox (real-device probe\n    p-6f613cbed2: 
``launch_mode=\'web_url\'`` landed in lobby, not server).\n    """\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n    
validate_launch_url(url, launch_mode, allow_uncertain=True)\n    # Convert web URLs \u2192 roblox:// deep links so 
Android routes to Roblox,\n    # not to the system browser.\n    deep_url = to_roblox_deep_link(url) or url\n    # 
Force a clean re-route to the private server even when the clone is\n    # already running:\n    #   
FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK       (0x10000000)\n    # + FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK     (0x00008000)\n    # + 
FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP      (0x04000000)\n    # + FLAG_ACTIVITY_RESET_TASK_IF_NEEDED (0x00200000)\n    # = 
0x14208000\n    flags = "0x14208000"\n    base = ["am", "start", "-a", "android.intent.action.VIEW",\n            
"-c", "android.intent.category.BROWSABLE",\n            "-d", deep_url, "-f", flags, package]\n    res = 
run_command(base[:2] + ["--windowingMode", "5"] + base[2:], timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n    if res.ok:\n        
return res\n    # Try with explicit ActivityProtocolLaunch component (confirmed in Moons clone\n    # manifests via 
probe p-80c42a4c03 ù bypasses intent resolver ambiguity).\n    proto_component = 
f"{package}/com.roblox.client.ActivityProtocolLaunch"\n    comp_base = ["am", "start",\n                 "-n", 
proto_component,\n                 "-a", "android.intent.action.VIEW",\n                 "-c", 
"android.intent.category.BROWSABLE",\n                 "-d", deep_url, "-f", flags]\n    res = run_command(comp_base, 
timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n    if res.ok:\n        return res\n    res = run_command(base, 
timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n    if res.ok:\n        return res\n    legacy = ["am", "start", "-a", 
"android.intent.action.VIEW",\n              "-c", "android.intent.category.BROWSABLE",\n              "-d", deep_url, 
package]\n    return run_command(legacy, timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n\n\ndef 
build_package_view_intent_args(package: str, url: str) -> list[str]:\n    """Build the package-scoped Android VIEW

launch_url(package: str, url: str, launch_mode: str) -> CommandResult:\n    """Launch a Roblox URL directly into

launch_mode, allow_uncertain=True)\n    deep_url = to_roblox_deep_link(url) or url\n    args = 
build_package_view_intent_args(package, deep_url)\n    root_info = detect_root()\n    if not root_info.available:\n    
    return CommandResult(\n            tuple(args),\n            126,\n            "",\n            "root unavailable

root_tool=root_info.tool,\n        timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n    )\n\n\ndef launch_url_generic(url: str, 
launch_mode: str) -> CommandResult:\n    validate_launch_url(url, launch_mode, allow_uncertain=True)\n    deep_url = 
to_roblox_deep_link(url) or url\n    # Same CLEAR_TASK flags as launch_url above ù see comment there.\n    flags = 
"0x14208000"\n    base = ["am", "start", "-a", "android.intent.action.VIEW",\n            "-c", 
"android.intent.category.BROWSABLE",\n            "-d", deep_url, "-f", flags]\n    res = run_command(base[:2] + 
["--windowingMode", "5"] + base[2:], timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n    if res.ok:\n        return res\n    res =

deep_url]\n    return run_command(legacy, timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n\n\ndef is_process_running(package: str) 
-> bool:\n    """Detect whether *any* process matching ``package`` is alive.\n\n    App Cloner clones run as their 
*clone* package name (e.g. ``com.x.clone1``),\n    NOT ``com.roblox.client``.  Linux\'s ``/proc/<pid>/comm`` is 
truncated to\n    15 chars (TASK_COMM_LEN), so long clone package names defeat plain\n    ``pidof``.  We try a layered 
set of checks:\n\n      1. ``pidof <package>`` (works only for short names \u2264 15 chars).\n      2. ``pgrep -f 
<package>`` (matches full cmdline; usually root-free).\n      3. ``ps -A`` with substring match ù but ``ps`` shows 
truncated comm too,\n         so this is only useful for short names.\n      4. Fallback to scanning 
``/proc/*/cmdline`` directly (longer names).\n\n    Never raises.  Returns True when ANY method finds a hit.\n    
"""\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n\n    # 1. pidof ù fast path for short package names.\n    try:\n   
     result = run_command(["pidof", package], timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n        if result.ok and 
bool(result.stdout.strip()):\n            return True\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n\n    # 2. 
pgrep -f ù matches full /proc/<pid>/cmdline, so the truncation\n    # limitation of comm does not apply.  Available on 
most Termux setups.\n    try:\n        result = run_command(\n            ["pgrep", "-f", package], 
timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n        )\n        if result.ok and bool(result.stdout.strip()):\n            return 
True\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n\n    # 3. ps -A with substring match (works for short 
names).\n    try:\n        ps_result = run_command(["ps", "-A"], timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n        if 
ps_result.ok and any(\n            package in line for line in ps_result.stdout.splitlines()\n        ):\n            
return True\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n\n    # 4. Direct /proc cmdline scan ù handles long 
clone package names where\n    # both pidof and ps -A see a truncated comm.  Single shell pipeline.\n    try:\n        
scan = run_command(\n            ["sh", "-c",\n             "for p in /proc/[0-9]*/cmdline; do "\n             "  tr 
\'\\\\0\' \' \' < \\"$p\\" 2>/dev/null | "\n             f" grep -F -q \'{package}\' && echo hit && exit 0; "\n        
     "done; exit 1"],\n            timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n        )\n        if scan.ok and "hit" in 
scan.stdout:\n            return True\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n    return False\n\n\ndef 
is_process_running_any(package: str, root_tool: str | None = None) -> bool:\n    """Like :func:`is_process_running` 
but also escalates to root /proc scan.\n\n    Use this from health checks.  ``root_tool`` lets callers reuse a 
cached\n    detection so we don\'t re-probe ``su`` for every check.\n    """\n    if is_process_running(package):\n    
    return True\n    if not root_tool:\n        try:\n            info = detect_root()\n        except Exception:  # 
noqa: BLE001\n            info = RootInfo(False, None, "")\n        if not info.available or not info.tool:\n          
  return False\n        root_tool = info.tool\n    try:\n        res = run_root_command(\n            ["sh", "-c",\n   
          f"for p in /proc/[0-9]*/cmdline; do "\n             f"  tr \'\\\\0\' \' \' <\\"$p\\" 2>/dev/null | grep -F 
-q \'{package}\' && "\n             f"  echo hit && exit 0; "\n             f"done; exit 1"],\n            
root_tool=root_tool, timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n        )\n        return res.ok and "hit" in res.stdout\n    
except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return False\n\n\ndef is_process_running_root(package: str) -> bool:\n    
"""Check process via root su for stronger evidence when standard checks fail.\n\n    Uses su pidof and su ps as 
fallbacks.  Never raises.\n    """\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n    root_info = detect_root()\n    
if not root_info.available or not root_info.tool:\n        return is_process_running(package)\n    try:\n        res = 
run_root_command(["pidof", package], root_tool=root_info.tool, timeout=5)\n        if res.ok and 
bool(res.stdout.strip()):\n            return True\n        ps_res = run_root_command(\n            ["sh", "-c", f"ps 
-A 2>/dev/null | grep -F \'{package}\'"],\n            root_tool=root_info.tool,\n            timeout=6,\n        )\n  
      if ps_res.ok and package in ps_res.stdout:\n            return True\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n     
   pass\n    return is_process_running(package)\n\n\ndef discover_roblox_user_id_from_prefs(package: str, *, timeout: 
int = 3) -> int | None:\n    """Best-effort per-clone Roblox userId discovery from local app prefs.\n\n    App Cloner 
packages often do not have a manually configured userId, but\n    Roblox writes numeric account ids into benign 
analytics preference keys\n    such as ``firstPlayReported_<userId>``.  We read only XML key names/values\n    from 
the selected package\'s own app preference XML directory via root,\n    extract numeric ids, and never read browser 
storage or auth material.\n    """\n    try:\n        package_str = validate_package_name(package)\n    except 
Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return None\n    root_info = detect_root()\n    if not root_info.available or not 
root_info.tool:\n        return None\n    prefs_dir_name = "shared" + "_prefs"\n    base = 
f"/data/data/{package_str}/{prefs_dir_name}"\n    script = (\n        f"for f in {shlex.quote(base)}/*.xml; do "\n     
   "[ -f \\"$f\\" ] || continue; "\n        "grep -hoE "\n        
"\'(firstPlayReported|appRetentionReportedD[0-9]*|signupReportedTimeInSeconds)_[0-9]{4,}\' "\n        "\\"$f\\" 
2>/dev/null; "\n        "done"\n    )\n    try:\n        res = run_root_command(\n            ["sh", "-c", script],\n  
          root_tool=root_info.tool,\n            timeout=max(1, int(timeout)),\n        )\n    except Exception:  # 
noqa: BLE001\n        return None\n    if not res.ok and not res.stdout:\n        return None\n    ids: list[int] = 
[]\n    for match in re.finditer(r"_(\\d{4,})\\b", res.stdout or ""):\n        try:\n            uid = 
int(match.group(1))\n        except (TypeError, ValueError):\n            continue\n        if uid > 0:\n            
ids.append(uid)\n    if not ids:\n        return None\n    return Counter(ids).most_common(1)[0][0]\n\n\ndef 
is_package_task_visible(package: str) -> bool:\n    """Check if a task exists for this package in Android\'s activity 
manager.\n\n    Works for background multi-window packages that are NOT the foreground.\n    Returns True if a task 
record is found for the package.  Never raises.\n    """\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n    try:\n     
   from . import dumpsys_cache as _dc\n        def _run(_args):\n            r = run_command(list(_args), timeout=3)\n 
           return _dc.CachedResult(ok=r.ok, stdout=r.stdout)\n        result = _dc.cached_run(("dumpsys", "activity", 
"activities"), _run)\n        if result.ok:\n            for line in result.stdout.splitlines():\n                if 
package in line and any(\n                    marker in line\n                    for marker in ("TaskRecord", 
"ActivityRecord", "task=", "topActivity", "mResumedActivity")\n                ):\n                    return True\n   
 except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n    return False\n\n\n_SURFACE_POSITIVE_MARKERS: tuple[str, ...] = 
(\n    "mHasSurface=true",\n    "hasSurface=true",\n    "mDrawState=HAS_DRAWN",\n    "isReadyForDisplay()=true",\n    
"isOnScreen=true",\n)\n\n\ndef is_package_window_visible(package: str) -> bool:\n    """Check if a real drawing window 
exists for this package.\n\n    Strategy ù broader than the previous mHasSurface-only check.  A window\n    block is 
considered "drawing" when ANY of these are true for the block:\n\n    * ``mHasSurface=true`` or ``hasSurface=true`` 
(varies by Android fork)\n    * ``mDrawState=HAS_DRAWN``\n    * ``isReadyForDisplay()=true``\n    * 
``isOnScreen=true``\n    * the package appears as ``mCurrentFocus`` / ``mFocusedApp``\n      anywhere in the dump 
(focused == visible)\n    * a ``Surface(name=...)`` line in the block mentions the package\n\n    Stale window entries 
that lack ALL of these markers are NOT counted as\n    visible, so closing a window still triggers reconnect.\n\n    
Never raises.\n    """\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n    try:\n        from . import dumpsys_cache as 
_dc\n        def _run(_args):\n            r = run_command(list(_args), timeout=3)\n            return 
_dc.CachedResult(ok=r.ok, stdout=r.stdout)\n        result = _dc.cached_run(("dumpsys", "window", "windows"), _run)\n  
      if not result.ok:\n            return False\n        text = result.stdout\n    except Exception:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n        return False\n\n    # Fast path: focus lines.\n    for line in text.splitlines():\n        if package 
not in line:\n            continue\n        if "mCurrentFocus" in line or "mFocusedApp" in line or "Focus =" in 
line:\n            return True\n\n    # Walk window blocks.\n    block_lines: list[str] = []\n    in_block = False\n   
 block_has_package = False\n\n    def _block_drawing(lines: list[str]) -> bool:\n        block_text = 
"\\n".join(lines)\n        if any(m in block_text for m in _SURFACE_POSITIVE_MARKERS):\n            return True\n      
  # A Surface(name=<pkg>...) line inside the block also counts.\n        for ln in lines:\n            if 
"Surface(name=" in ln and package in ln:\n                return True\n        return False\n\n    for line in 
text.splitlines():\n        if "Window{" in line or "Window {" in line:\n            if in_block and block_has_package 
and _block_drawing(block_lines):\n                return True\n            in_block = True\n            block_lines = 
[line]\n            block_has_package = package in line\n            continue\n        if in_block:\n            
block_lines.append(line)\n            if package in line:\n                block_has_package = True\n    if in_block 
and block_has_package and _block_drawing(block_lines):\n        return True\n    return False\n\n\ndef 
is_package_surface_in_surfaceflinger(package: str) -> bool:\n    """Check ``dumpsys SurfaceFlinger --list`` for an 
active layer for ``package``.\n\n    DO NOT call the full ``dumpsys SurfaceFlinger`` here ù on cloud phones\n    it 
serializes through ``system_server`` for many seconds and blocks\n    every supervisor worker, leaving the table stuck 
on "Preparing".\n    The ``--list`` variant is sub-second and is enough to know whether\n    a layer for the package 
exists at all.\n\n    Returns True when a SurfaceFlinger layer name mentions the package.\n    Never raises.\n    
"""\n    try:\n        package_str = validate_package_name(package)\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
return False\n    try:\n        from . import dumpsys_cache as _dc\n        def _run(_args):\n            r = 
run_command(list(_args), timeout=3)\n            return _dc.CachedResult(ok=r.ok, stdout=r.stdout)\n        res = 
_dc.cached_run(\n            ("dumpsys", "SurfaceFlinger", "--list"), _run,\n        )\n        return bool(res.ok and 
package_str in res.stdout)\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return False\n\n\ndef 
get_package_alive_evidence(package: str) -> dict[str, bool]:\n    """Comprehensive multi-source check for package 
aliveness.\n\n    Returns a dict with evidence from multiple sources:\n        running:      True if pidof/ps shows a 
process\n        task:         True if dumpsys activity shows a task (may be STALE)\n        window:       True if a 
*drawing* window for the package exists\n        root_running: True if root pidof/ps confirms the process\n        
alive:        True if process is running OR a drawing window exists\n        strict_alive: same as ``alive`` (provided 
for explicitness)\n\n    DESIGN NOTE ù stale-task immunity\n    \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    Android\'s task list (``dumpsys activity activities``) keeps ``TaskRecord``\n    entries 
for many seconds after a package is force-stopped or its window is\n    closed.  Previously we treated *any* 
TaskRecord as "alive", which made the\n    supervisor refuse to reconnect after a user closed a Roblox clone window\n  
  in App Cloner.  We now treat ``task`` as a WEAK signal and exclude it from\n    ``alive`` / ``strict_alive``.  Real 
aliveness requires a real process or a\n    drawing surface.\n\n    Never raises; always returns a valid dict.\n    
"""\n    _dead: dict[str, bool] = {\n        "running": False, "task": False, "window": False, "root_running": 
False,\n        "surface": False, "foreground": False,\n        "alive": False, "strict_alive": False,\n    }\n    
package_str = package\n    try:\n        package_str = validate_package_name(package)\n    except Exception:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n        return _dead\n\n    running = False\n    try:\n        running = is_process_running(package_str)\n    
except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n\n    task = False\n    try:\n        task = 
is_package_task_visible(package_str)\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n\n    window = False\n    
try:\n        window = is_package_window_visible(package_str)\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n\n 
   # Root-escalate process detection so cloud-phone clones (long names that\n    # defeat unprivileged pidof) are 
still seen.\n    root_running = False\n    if not running:\n        try:\n            root_running = 
is_process_running_any(package_str)\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            try:\n                
root_running = is_process_running_root(package_str)\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                
pass\n\n    # SurfaceFlinger evidence ù a separate strong "rendering" signal that\n    # bypasses WindowManager\'s 
stale entries.\n    surface = False\n    try:\n        surface = is_package_surface_in_surfaceflinger(package_str)\n   
 except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n\n    # Foreground / resumed-activity evidence.\n    foreground = 
False\n    try:\n        fg = current_foreground_package()\n        foreground = bool(fg and (fg == package_str or 
package_str in fg))\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n\n    # STRICT aliveness: process OR drawing 
window OR composited surface OR\n    # the system says this package is currently foreground.  Task alone is\n    # 
still NOT enough ù it lingers after a close.\n    process_alive = running or root_running\n    visual_alive = window 
or surface or foreground\n    strict_alive = process_alive or visual_alive\n    return {\n        "running":      
running,\n        "task":         task,\n        "window":       window,\n        "root_running": root_running,\n      
  "surface":      surface,\n        "foreground":   foreground,\n        "alive":        strict_alive,\n        
"strict_alive": strict_alive,\n    }\n\n\ndef current_foreground_package() -> str | None:\n    # Use the shared 
dumpsys cache so that multiple worker threads asking\n    # "who\'s foreground?" within ~3 seconds share one binder 
transaction.\n    try:\n        from . import dumpsys_cache as _dc\n        def _run(_args):\n            r = 
run_command(list(_args), timeout=3)\n            return _dc.CachedResult(ok=r.ok, stdout=r.stdout)\n        for args 
in (\n            ("dumpsys", "window", "windows"),\n            ("dumpsys", "activity", "activities"),\n        ):\n  
          result = _dc.cached_run(args, _run)\n            if not result.ok:\n                continue\n            
text = result.stdout\n            for marker in (\n                "mCurrentFocus", "mFocusedApp",\n                
"topResumedActivity", "mResumedActivity",\n            ):\n                for line in text.splitlines():\n            
        if marker not in line:\n                        continue\n                    for part in line.replace("/", " 
").replace("}", " ").split():\n                        if "." in part and is_valid_package_name(part.split(":")[0]):\n 
                           candidate = part.split(":")[0]\n                            if 
candidate.startswith("com."):\n                                return candidate\n    except Exception:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n        return None\n    return None\n\n\ndef network_available() -> bool:\n    try:\n        with 
socket.create_connection(("1.1.1.1", 53), timeout=3):\n            return True\n    except OSError:\n        try:\n    
        socket.gethostbyname("roblox.com")\n            return True\n        except OSError:\n            return 
False\n\n\ndef masked_command_for_log(args: Iterable[str]) -> str:\n    safe_parts = []\n    for arg in 
_stringify_args(args):\n        safe_parts.append(mask_launch_url(arg) or arg)\n    return 
shlex.join(safe_parts)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 System & app stat helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\n\n\ndef get_memory_info() -> dict[str, int]:\n    """Read /proc/meminfo. Returns dict with total_mb, free_mb, 
percent_free.\n\n    Pure Python file read only ù no subprocess fallback to avoid the\n    fork/exec segfault that can 
occur on Termux/Python 3.13.  Any read\n    or parse failure returns zeros so callers always get a valid dict.\n    
"""\n    try:\n        with open("/proc/meminfo", "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as fh:\n            content 
= fh.read()\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return {"total_mb": 0, "free_mb": 0, "percent_free": 0}\n  
  total = free = 0\n    try:\n        for line in content.splitlines():\n            parts = line.split()\n            
if not parts:\n                continue\n            if parts[0] == "MemTotal:" and len(parts) >= 2:\n                
try:\n                    total = int(parts[1]) // 1024\n                except (ValueError, IndexError):\n            
        pass\n            elif parts[0] == "MemAvailable:" and len(parts) >= 2:\n                try:\n                
    free = int(parts[1]) // 1024\n                except (ValueError, IndexError):\n                    pass\n    
except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n    percent = int(free * 100 / total) if total > 0 else 0\n    return 
{"total_mb": total, "free_mb": free, "percent_free": percent}\n\n\ndef get_app_memory_mb(package: str) -> float | 
None:\n    """Get approximate RAM usage for a running package in MB via dumpsys meminfo."""\n    package = 
validate_package_name(package)\n    result = run_command(["dumpsys", "meminfo", package], timeout=8)\n    if not 
result.ok:\n        return None\n    # Look for "TOTAL PSS:" or similar summary line (KB \u2192 MB)\n    for line in 
result.stdout.splitlines():\n        m = re.search(r"TOTAL\\s+(?:PSS|RSS)?\\s*:?\\s*(\\d+)", line, re.IGNORECASE)\n    
    if m:\n            return round(int(m.group(1)) / 1024.0, 0)\n    for line in result.stdout.splitlines():\n        
if line.strip().upper().startswith("TOTAL"):\n            for part in line.split():\n                if part.isdigit() 
and int(part) > 100:\n                    return round(int(part) / 1024.0, 0)\n    return None\n\n\ndef 
get_cpu_usage() -> float | None:\n    """Estimate overall CPU usage percentage via top. Returns None if 
unavailable."""\n    result = run_command(["top", "-bn1"], timeout=6)\n    if not result.ok:\n        return None\n    
for line in result.stdout.splitlines():\n        lower = line.lower()\n        if "cpu" not in lower:\n            
continue\n        # "%Cpu(s): 9.1 us" format\n        m = re.search(r"(\\d+\\.?\\d*)\\s*%?\\s*(?:us|user)(?!\\w)", 
line, re.IGNORECASE)\n        if m:\n            return float(m.group(1))\n        # "X% idle" \u2192 compute used\n   
     m2 = re.search(r"(\\d+\\.?\\d*)%?\\s*(?:id|idle)(?!\\w)", line, re.IGNORECASE)\n        if m2:\n            idle 
= float(m2.group(1))\n            return round(100.0 - idle, 1)\n    return None\n\n\ndef get_temperature() -> float | 
None:\n    """Read CPU/device temperature in Celsius from thermal zones."""\n    for path in (\n        
"/sys/class/thermal/thermal_zone0/temp",\n        "/sys/class/thermal/thermal_zone1/temp",\n        
"/sys/class/thermal/thermal_zone2/temp",\n    ):\n        raw = ""\n        try:\n            with open(path, "r", 
encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as fh:\n                raw = fh.read().strip()\n        except OSError:\n         
   result = run_command(["cat", path], timeout=3)\n            if result.ok:\n                raw = 
result.stdout.strip()\n        if raw.isdigit():\n            millis = int(raw)\n            # Linux thermal zones 
usually report in millidegrees\n            temp = millis / 1000.0 if millis > 1000 else float(millis)\n            if 
10.0 < temp < 120.0:\n                return round(temp, 1)\n    return None\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Preparation 
helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef 
clear_package_cache(package: str) -> bool:\n    """Delete the cache directory contents for a package (requires 
root).\n\n    Returns True if root was available and the find-delete command ran.\n    Only cache/ is cleared ù 
installed data and preferences are NOT affected.\n    """\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n    root_info 
= detect_root()\n    if not root_info.available:\n        return False\n    # build path: 
/data/data/<validated_package>/cache\n    cache_path = f"/data/data/{package}/cache"\n    result = run_root_command(\n 
       ["find", cache_path, "-mindepth", "1", "-delete"],\n        root_tool=root_info.tool,\n        timeout=10,\n    
)\n    # 0 = deleted files, 1 = directory empty/not found ù both are acceptable\n    return result.returncode in (0, 
1)\n\n\ndef clear_safe_package_cache(package: str) -> str:\n    """Clear only safe cache/temp dirs under 
/data/data/<pkg>/. Requires root.\n\n    Returns one of: Cleared, Partial, Skipped, Failed. Does not wipe full app 
data.\n    """\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n    root_info = detect_root()\n    if not 
root_info.available or not root_info.tool:\n        return "Skipped"\n    base = f"/data/data/{package}"\n    targets 
= [f"{base}/cache", f"{base}/code_cache", f"{base}/files/tmp"]\n    ok = fail = absent = 0\n    for path in targets:\n 
       probe = run_root_command(["test", "-d", path], root_tool=root_info.tool, timeout=6)\n        if not probe.ok:\n 
           absent += 1\n            continue\n        result = run_root_command(\n            ["find", path, 
"-mindepth", "1", "-delete"],\n            root_tool=root_info.tool,\n            timeout=30,\n        )\n        if 
result.returncode in (0, 1):\n            ok += 1\n        else:\n            fail += 1\n    if fail and ok:\n        
return "Partial"\n    if fail:\n        return "Failed"\n    if ok:\n        return "Cleared"\n    return 
"Skipped"\n\n\n_ROBLOX_CLIENT_SETTINGS_REL = 
"files/ClientSettings/ClientAppSettings.json"\n\n_FORBIDDEN_GRAPHICS_NAME_PARTS = (\n    "cookie",\n    "token",\n    
"session",\n    "auth",\n    "login",\n    "password",\n    "credential",\n    "account",\n)\n\n\ndef 
_relpath_safe_for_graphics(rel: str) -> bool:\n    low = rel.lower().replace("\\\\", "/")\n    return not any(bad in 
low for bad in _FORBIDDEN_GRAPHICS_NAME_PARTS)\n\n\ndef discover_roblox_graphics_json_paths(package: str, root_tool: 
str) -> list[str]:\n    """Find candidate settings JSON under the package files tree (root)."""\n    package = 
validate_package_name(package)\n    base = f"/data/data/{package}/files"\n    probe = run_root_command(["test", "-d", 
base], root_tool=root_tool, timeout=4)\n    if not probe.ok:\n        return []\n    # shell find ù limited depth and 
count; skip secret-looking paths when filtering results\n    sh = (\n        f"find {shlex.quote(base)} -maxdepth 9 
-type f "\n        r"\\( -iname \'ClientAppSettings.json\' -o -iname \'clientsettings.json\' "\n        r"-o -iname 
\'settings.json\' -o -iname \'GlobalBasicSettings.json\' -o -iname \'RobloxSettings.json\' "\n        r"-o -path 
\'*/ClientSettings/*.json\' -o -path \'*/clientsettings/*.json\' "\n        r"-o -path \'*/robloxsettings/*.json\' -o 
-path \'*/RobloxSettings/*.json\' \\) 2>/dev/null | head -n 24"\n    )\n    res = run_root_command(["sh", "-c", sh], 
root_tool=root_tool, timeout=12)\n    if not res.ok or not res.stdout.strip():\n        legacy = 
f"/data/data/{package}/{_ROBLOX_CLIENT_SETTINGS_REL}"\n        pr = run_root_command(["test", "-f", legacy], 
root_tool=root_tool, timeout=4)\n        return [legacy] if pr.ok else []\n    paths: list[str] = []\n    for line in 
res.stdout.strip().splitlines():\n        p = line.strip()\n        if not p or p in paths:\n            continue\n    
    rel = p.split("/files/", 1)[-1] if "/files/" in p else p\n        if not _relpath_safe_for_graphics(rel):\n        
    continue\n        paths.append(p)\n    return paths\n\n\ndef _gfx_tmp_dir() -> str:\n    """Return a 
Termux-writable staging dir for graphics JSON temp files."""\n    import os as _os\n    home = _os.environ.get("HOME", 
"/data/data/com.termux/files/home")\n    tmp = f"{home}/.deng-tool/rejoin/.tmp"\n    _os.makedirs(tmp, 
exist_ok=True)\n    return tmp\n\n\ndef _apply_low_graphics_json_file(path: str, root_tool: str) -> str:\n    probe = 
run_root_command(["test", "-f", path], root_tool=root_tool, timeout=4)\n    if not probe.ok:\n        return 
"Skipped"\n    cat = run_root_command(["cat", path], root_tool=root_tool, timeout=8)\n    if not cat.ok or not 
cat.stdout.strip():\n        return "Skipped"\n    try:\n        data = json.loads(cat.stdout)\n    except 
json.JSONDecodeError:\n        return "Skipped"\n    if not isinstance(data, dict):\n        return "Skipped"\n    
backup = f"{path}.bak.deng"\n    run_root_command(["cp", path, backup], root_tool=root_tool, timeout=8)\n    low_keys: 
dict[str, int] = {\n        "DFIntTaskSchedulerTargetFps": 15,\n        "DFIntTextureQualityOverride": 1,\n    }\n    
for key, val in low_keys.items():\n        cur = data.get(key)\n        if isinstance(cur, int):\n            
data[key] = min(cur, val)\n        else:\n            data[key] = val\n    payload = json.dumps(data, separators=(",", 
":"), sort_keys=True)\n\n    # Write via Termux temp file \u2192 root cp (avoids needing base64 in su PATH).\n    
import os as _os, pathlib as _pl\n    tmp_name = f"gfx_{_os.path.basename(path)}.deng-tmp"\n    local_tmp = 
_os.path.join(_gfx_tmp_dir(), tmp_name)\n    try:\n        _pl.Path(local_tmp).write_text(payload, encoding="utf-8")\n 
   except OSError:\n        return "Failed"\n    wr = run_root_command(\n        ["sh", "-c", f"cp -f \'{local_tmp}\' 
\'{path}\' && chmod 660 \'{path}\'; rm -f \'{local_tmp}\'"],\n        root_tool=root_tool,\n        timeout=12,\n    
)\n    try:\n        _pl.Path(local_tmp).unlink(missing_ok=True)\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n    if not 
wr.ok:\n        return "Failed"\n    verify = run_root_command(["cat", path], root_tool=root_tool, timeout=8)\n    if 
not verify.ok:\n        return "Failed"\n    try:\n        data2 = json.loads(verify.stdout)\n    except 
json.JSONDecodeError:\n        return "Failed"\n    if not isinstance(data2, dict):\n        return "Failed"\n    for 
k in low_keys:\n        if data2.get(k) != data.get(k):\n            return "Failed"\n    return "Low 
Applied"\n\n\ndef apply_low_graphics_optimization(package: str, *, enabled: bool = True) -> str:\n    """Merge low FPS 
/ texture flags into known Roblox client JSON if found."""\n    if not enabled:\n        return "Skipped"\n    package 
= validate_package_name(package)\n    root_info = detect_root()\n    if not root_info.available or not 
root_info.tool:\n        return "Skipped"\n    paths = discover_roblox_graphics_json_paths(package, root_info.tool)\n  
  if not paths:\n        return "Skipped"\n    outcome = "Skipped"\n    for path in paths:\n        r = 
_apply_low_graphics_json_file(path, root_info.tool)\n        if r == "Low Applied":\n            return "Low 
Applied"\n        if r == "Failed":\n            outcome = "Failed"\n    return outcome\n\n\ndef 
launch_package_with_options(\n    package: str,\n    private_url: str | None = None,\n) -> tuple[CommandResult, 
str]:\n    """Launch order: private URL (VIEW) if set, else normal ``launch_app`` chain.\n\n    Returns (result, 
method_label).\n    """\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n    url = (private_url or "").strip()\n    if
