url:\n        mode = detect_launch_mode_from_url(url)\n        if mode not in LAUNCH_MODES:\n            mode = 
"web_url"\n        try:\n            validate_launch_url(url, mode, allow_uncertain=True)\n        except 
UrlValidationError as exc:\n            return CommandResult(\n                ("am", "start", "-a", 
"android.intent.action.VIEW", "-p", package),\n                2,\n                "",\n                f"configured 
Roblox link is invalid: {exc}",\n            ), "invalid_url"\n        result = launch_url(package, url, mode)\n       
 return result, "root_am_view_package"\n    return launch_app(package), "am_or_resolve"\n\n\ndef 
launch_package_with_bounds(\n    package: str,\n    rect: tuple[int, int, int, int],\n    private_url: str | None = 
None,\n    *,\n    windowing_mode: int = 5,\n) -> tuple[CommandResult, str]:\n    """Launch with ``am start 
--windowingMode <mode> --activity-launch-bounds`` if supported.\n\n    ``windowing_mode=5`` is Android\'s freeform 
windowing mode.  Many cloud\n    phones support freeform at the framework level once\n    
``enable_freeform_support=1`` is set (see :mod:`agent.freeform_enable`).\n    If the ``--activity-launch-bounds`` flag 
is not recognized on this\n    Android build, we fall back gracefully to the bounds-less launch.\n\n    Returns 
``(result, method_label)``.  Never raises.\n    """\n    try:\n        package = validate_package_name(package)\n    
except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return CommandResult(("am", "start"), 2, "", "invalid package"), 
"invalid"\n\n    l, t, r, b = rect\n    bounds_arg = f"{int(l)} {int(t)} {int(r)} {int(b)}"\n    url = (private_url or 
"").strip()\n    # Convert web share-link URLs \u2192 roblox:// deep links before am start.\n    # Without this, 
Android resolves https://www.roblox.com/share?... to the\n    # browser instead of Roblox (probe p-6f613cbed2: 
launch_mode=\'web_url\'\n    # landed in lobby, not server).\n    if url:\n        return 
launch_package_with_options(package, url)\n    deep_url = (to_roblox_deep_link(url) or url) if url else ""\n\n    am = 
_find_command("am", "/system/bin/am")\n    if not am:\n        # Fall back to whatever the normal launcher chain can 
do.\n        return launch_package_with_options(package, private_url)\n\n    # Common stem options for every variant 
we try.\n    stem: list[str] = [\n        am, "start",\n        "--windowingMode", str(int(windowing_mode)),\n        
"--activity-launch-bounds", str(int(l)), str(int(t)),\n        str(int(r)), str(int(b)),\n    ]\n    method_label = 
f"am_bounds_mode{int(windowing_mode)}"\n\n    if deep_url:\n        mode = detect_launch_mode_from_url(deep_url)\n     
   if mode not in LAUNCH_MODES:\n            mode = "deeplink"\n        try:\n            
validate_launch_url(deep_url, mode, allow_uncertain=True)\n            # Preferred: bounds + URL + flags.\n            
#   FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK      (0x10000000)\n            #   FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK    (0x00008000)  ù ensures 
fresh start\n            #   FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP     (0x04000000)\n            #   
FLAG_ACTIVITY_RESET_TASK_IF_NEEDED (0x00200000)\n            # BROWSABLE category ensures Android routes roblox:// 
to\n            # ActivityProtocolLaunch (not the generic LAUNCHER).\n            cmd = stem + [\n                
"-a", "android.intent.action.VIEW",\n                "-c", "android.intent.category.BROWSABLE",\n                "-f", 
"0x14208000",\n                "-d", deep_url, package,\n            ]\n            res = run_command(cmd, 
timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n            if res.ok:\n                return res, method_label + "_url"\n          
  # Variant: target ActivityProtocolLaunch directly (bypasses intent\n            # resolver ambiguity ù confirmed 
present in Moons clone manifests by\n            # probe p-80c42a4c03 pm_dump).\n            proto_component = 
f"{package}/com.roblox.client.ActivityProtocolLaunch"\n            cmd_comp = stem + [\n                "-n", 
proto_component,\n                "-a", "android.intent.action.VIEW",\n                "-c", 
"android.intent.category.BROWSABLE",\n                "-f", "0x14208000",\n                "-d", deep_url,\n           
 ]\n            res = run_command(cmd_comp, timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n            if res.ok:\n                
return res, method_label + "_url_component"\n            # Retry without extra intent flags in case device rejects 
them.\n            cmd_nf = stem + ["-a", "android.intent.action.VIEW",\n                             "-c", 
"android.intent.category.BROWSABLE",\n                             "-d", deep_url, package]\n            res = 
run_command(cmd_nf, timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n            if res.ok:\n                return res, method_label 
+ "_url_noflag"\n            # Last URL fallback: drop --activity-launch-bounds entirely so the\n            # intent 
is delivered without any positioning flags that might\n            # confuse Android\'s activity resolver on this 
device.  The window\n            # position is already pre-written to the App Cloner XML and will\n            # be 
fixed by the post-launch stack resize.\n            res_nb = launch_url(package, deep_url, mode)\n            if 
res_nb.ok:\n                return res_nb, method_label + "_url_nobounds"\n        except UrlValidationError:\n        
    pass\n        # URL launch failed on all variants ù fall through to MAIN/LAUNCHER.\n\n    # Plain MAIN/LAUNCHER 
intent with launch bounds.\n    cmd = stem + [\n        "-a", "android.intent.action.MAIN",\n        "-c", 
"android.intent.category.LAUNCHER",\n        "-p", package,\n    ]\n    res = run_command(cmd, 
timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n    if res.ok:\n        return res, method_label\n\n    # Fallback when 
--activity-launch-bounds isn\'t recognized: try just\n    # --windowingMode (older API levels accept this on its 
own).\n    cmd2 = [\n        am, "start",\n        "--windowingMode", str(int(windowing_mode)),\n        "-a", 
"android.intent.action.MAIN",\n        "-c", "android.intent.category.LAUNCHER",\n        "-p", package,\n    ]\n    
res2 = run_command(cmd2, timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n    if res2.ok:\n        return res2, method_label + 
"_no_bounds"\n\n    # Final fallback: normal launcher chain without any flags.\n    return 
launch_package_with_options(package, private_url)\n\n\ndef force_stop_packages_except(\n    keep_packages: 
list[str],\n    detection_hints: list[str] | None = None,\n) -> list[str]:\n    """Force-stop all detected Roblox 
packages not in keep_packages.\n\n    Tries without root first (works in many Termux ADB setups), then falls\n    back 
to root. Returns list of package names that were successfully stopped.\n    """\n    keep = set(keep_packages)\n    
all_roblox = find_roblox_packages(detection_hints)\n    to_stop = [p for p in all_roblox if p not in keep]\n    if not 
to_stop:\n        return []\n    root_info = detect_root()\n    stopped: list[str] = []\n    for pkg in to_stop:\n     
   result = run_command(["am", "force-stop", pkg], timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n        if result.ok:\n           
 stopped.append(pkg)\n        elif root_info.available:\n            result2 = force_stop_package(pkg, root_info)\n    
        if result2.ok:\n                stopped.append(pkg)\n    return stopped\n\n\n# Packages that must NEVER be 
killed during the boosting phase.\n_KILL_PROTECTED_PREFIXES: tuple[str, ...] = (\n    "com.termux",\n    "android",\n  
  "com.android",\n    "com.google.android",\n    "com.samsung",\n    "com.sec",\n    "com.qualcomm",\n    
"com.mediatek",\n)\n\n\ndef kill_all_background_apps(keep_packages: list[str]) -> dict[str, list[str]]:\n    """Clear 
cached/background processes to free RAM before launching clones.\n\n    Uses ``am kill-all`` which clears processes in 
the CACHED (background)\n    state without touching running foreground apps or system services.  We\n    deliberately 
do NOT enumerate and individually force-stop third-party\n    packages ù that was too aggressive and killed the Moons 
cloner service\n    that the clones depend on (probe p-e81414d9f4: apps launched ok but\n    immediately died because 
their parent service was force-stopped).\n\n    Returns a dict with keys ``killed_bg`` (kill-all ran) and 
``skipped``.\n    Never raises.\n    """\n    result: dict[str, list[str]] = {"killed_bg": [], "skipped": 
list(keep_packages)}\n\n    bg = run_android_command(["am", "kill-all"], prefer_root=True, timeout=10)\n    if 
bg.ok:\n        result["killed_bg"].append("am kill-all")\n    return result\n\n"""Exponential backoff 
helper."""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom .constants import MAX_BACKOFF_SECONDS, 
MIN_BACKOFF_SECONDS\n\n\ndef calculate_backoff_seconds(failure_count: int, min_seconds: int = MIN_BACKOFF_SECONDS, 
max_seconds: int = MAX_BACKOFF_SECONDS) -> int:\n    """Return capped exponential backoff seconds for a consecutive 
failure count."""\n    failure_count = max(0, int(failure_count))\n    min_seconds = max(MIN_BACKOFF_SECONDS, 
int(min_seconds))\n    max_seconds = min(MAX_BACKOFF_SECONDS, max(min_seconds, int(max_seconds)))\n    if 
failure_count <= 0:\n        return min_seconds\n    delay = min_seconds * (2 ** (failure_count - 1))\n    return 
min(max(delay, min_seconds), max_seconds)\n\n"""Small, dependency-free Termux banner."""\n\nfrom __future__ import 
annotations\n\nimport os\nimport re\nimport sys\n\nfrom .constants import PRODUCT_NAME, VERSION\n\nRED = 
"\\033[31m"\nRESET = "\\033[0m"\n\nASCII_DENG = r"""\n\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2557 
\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2557\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2557   \u2588\u2588\u2557 \u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\
u2588\u2588\u2557\n\u2588\u2588\u2554\u2550\u2550\u2588\u2588\u2557\u2588\u2588\u2554\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u255d\u25
88\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2557  \u2588\u2588\u2551\u2588\u2588\u2554\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u255d\n\u2588\u2588\u2551  
\u2588\u2588\u2551\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2557  \u2588\u2588\u2554\u2588\u2588\u2557 
\u2588\u2588\u2551\u2588\u2588\u2551  \u2588\u2588\u2588\u2557\n\u2588\u2588\u2551  
\u2588\u2588\u2551\u2588\u2588\u2554\u2550\u2550\u255d  
\u2588\u2588\u2551\u255a\u2588\u2588\u2557\u2588\u2588\u2551\u2588\u2588\u2551   \u2588\u2588\u2551\n\u2588\u2588\u2588
\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2554\u255d\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2557\u2588\u2588\u2551 \u255a\u2588\u2588\u25
88\u2588\u2551\u255a\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2554\u255d\n\u255a\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u255d 
\u255a\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u255d\u255a\u2550\u255d  \u255a\u2550\u2550\u2550\u255d 
\u255a\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u255d\n""".strip("\\n")\n\nANSI_RE = re.compile(r"\\x1b\\[[0-9;]*m")\n\n\ndef 
supports_color() -> bool:\n    """Return true when ANSI color is likely useful."""\n    if 
os.environ.get("NO_COLOR"):\n        return False\n    if os.environ.get("TERM", "").lower() in {"dumb", ""} and not 
os.environ.get("TERMUX_VERSION"):\n        return False\n    return bool(getattr(sys.stdout, "isatty", lambda: 
False)()) or bool(os.environ.get("TERMUX_VERSION"))\n\n\ndef visible_width(text: str) -> int:\n    """Return printable 
width after removing ANSI sequences."""\n    return len(ANSI_RE.sub("", text))\n\n\ndef banner_text(use_color: bool | 
None = None) -> str:\n    """Build the DENG banner with optional ANSI red styling.\n\n    Each line gets its own RESET 
to prevent background-strip artifacts in Termux\n    (the BOLD attribute was removed to avoid black-bar rendering with 
box-drawing chars).\n    """\n    if use_color is None:\n        use_color = supports_color()\n    if use_color:\n     
   colored_lines = [f"{RED}{line}{RESET}" for line in ASCII_DENG.splitlines()]\n        logo = 
"\\n".join(colored_lines)\n    else:\n        logo = ASCII_DENG\n    logo_width = max(visible_width(line) for line in 
ASCII_DENG.splitlines())\n    subtitle = f"{PRODUCT_NAME.replace(\'DENG Tool: \', \'Tool: \')} 
v{VERSION}".center(logo_width)\n    return f"{logo}\\n{subtitle}"\n\n\ndef print_banner(use_color: bool | None = None) 
-> None:\n    """Print the product banner."""\n    text = banner_text(use_color=use_color)\n    try:\n        
print(text)\n    except UnicodeEncodeError:\n        if hasattr(sys.stdout, "buffer"):\n            
sys.stdout.buffer.write(text.encode("utf-8") + b"\\n")\n            sys.stdout.buffer.flush()\n        else:\n         
   print(text.encode("ascii", errors="replace").decode("ascii"))\n\n"""Bootstrap shell scripts for GET /install/* 
(Termux-friendly, LF-only).\n\nTwo install modes:\n\n1. **Direct install** (preferred, used for test/latest and public 
stable releases):\n   :func:`render_direct_install_bootstrap` downloads the *full* package tarball\n   immediately.  
No license key is required during install.  The license is only\n   prompted inside the real tool on first run (inside 
the menu flow).\n\n2. **Launcher-deferred install** (legacy, kept for reference):\n   :func:`render_public_bootstrap` 
downloads a small launcher tarball and writes\n   ``.install_requested``.  License entry runs only after 
``deng-rejoin``\n   (:mod:`agent.deferred_bundle_install`).\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\n# NOTE: The 
installer scripts are POSIX-sh only.  No bash features.\n#\n# Termux\'s ``/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/bin/sh`` is 
**dash**.\n# When the user invokes ``curl ... | sh``, the shebang on line 1 is\n# ignored and dash executes the bytes 
ù it would choke on\n# ``set -o pipefail``, ``[[ ... ]]``, ``shopt``, arrays, or\n# ``${VAR:0:N}`` substring 
expansion.  We previously tried a\n# "re-exec into bash" preamble, but ``exec bash "$0"`` failed on\n# Termux because 
``$0`` was the dash *binary* itself (not a script\n# file).  The robust answer is to use POSIX-sh syntax 
throughout.\n\n_INSTALL_PART_BEFORE_HEREDOC = r"""\ncommand -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "Install curl first: pkg 
install -y curl" >&2; exit 1; }\ncommand -v tar >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "Install tar first: pkg install -y tar" >&2; 
exit 1; }\ncommand -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "Install python first: pkg install -y python" >&2; exit 1; 
}\nLAUNCHER_URL="$DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API/install/launcher/bundle.tar.gz"\nTMP="$(mktemp)"\nSTAGE="$(mktemp -d)"\ntrap 
\'rm -f "$TMP"; rm -rf "$STAGE"\' EXIT\ncurl -fsSL "$LAUNCHER_URL" -o "$TMP" || { echo "Could not download launcher 
bundle." >&2; echo "URL: $LAUNCHER_URL" >&2; exit 1; }\ntar -xzf "$TMP" -C "$STAGE" || { echo "Could not extract 
launcher bundle archive." >&2; exit 1; }\nDEF_CK="$STAGE/agent/deferred_bundle_install.py"\nif [ ! -f "$DEF_CK" ]; 
then\n  echo "Failed launcher self-check." >&2\n  echo "agent/deferred_bundle_install.py missing from launcher 
tarball." >&2\n  echo "APP_HOME=$APP_HOME" >&2\n  echo "Launcher bundle URL: $LAUNCHER_URL" >&2\n  exit 1\nfi\nif ! 
grep -q "resolve_install_api" "$DEF_CK" 2>/dev/null; then\n  echo "Failed launcher self-check." >&2\n  echo 
"agent/deferred_bundle_install.py is missing resolve_install_api." >&2\n  echo "The launcher bundle is stale or 
corrupted." >&2\n  echo "APP_HOME=$APP_HOME" >&2\n  echo "Launcher bundle URL: $LAUNCHER_URL" >&2\n  echo "Deferred 
file path (staging): $DEF_CK" >&2\n  exit 1\nfi\nmkdir -p "$APP_HOME/agent"\nrm -f 
"$APP_HOME/agent/deng_tool_rejoin.py" "$APP_HOME/agent/deferred_bundle_install.py" "$APP_HOME/agent/__init__.py"\n# 
POSIX-sh equivalent of bash\'s ``shopt -s nullglob`` + array copy:\n# iterate the glob, skip the literal-unmatched 
case explicitly.\n_COPIED=0\nfor _f in "$STAGE"/agent/*.py; do\n  [ -e "$_f" ] || continue\n  cp -a "$_f" 
"$APP_HOME/agent/" || { echo "Failed to copy $_f into APP_HOME." >&2; exit 1; }\n  _COPIED=1\ndone\nif [ "$_COPIED" 
-ne 1 ]; then\n  echo "Failed launcher self-check: no agent/*.py in bundle." >&2\n  echo "Launcher bundle URL: 
$LAUNCHER_URL" >&2\n  exit 1\nfi\nif ! grep -q "resolve_install_api" "$APP_HOME/agent/deferred_bundle_install.py" 
2>/dev/null; then\n  echo "Failed launcher self-check: installed deferred_bundle_install.py still missing 
resolve_install_api." >&2\n  echo "APP_HOME=$APP_HOME" >&2\n  echo "Deferred path: 
$APP_HOME/agent/deferred_bundle_install.py" >&2\n  exit 1\nfi\necho "Launcher bundle verified."\n\n# Prefer 
$PREFIX/bin (Termux: on PATH). Always mkdir ù do not rely on [ -w ]\nUSING_HOME_BIN=0\nBIN=""\nif [ -n "${PREFIX:-}" 
]; then\n  if mkdir -p "${PREFIX}/bin" 2>/dev/null; then\n    BIN="${PREFIX}/bin"\n  fi\nfi\nif [ -z "$BIN" ]; then\n  
BIN="$HOME/bin"\n  mkdir -p "$BIN"\n  export PATH="$HOME/bin:$PATH"\n  USING_HOME_BIN=1\n  _MARK=\'# DENG Tool: Rejoin 
- PATH (added by installer)\'\n  _LINE=\'export PATH="$HOME/bin:$PATH"\'\n  for _rc in "$HOME/.bashrc" 
"$HOME/.profile"; do\n    touch "$_rc"\n    if ! grep -qF "$_MARK" "$_rc" 2>/dev/null; then\n      printf 
\'\\n%s\\n%s\\n\' "$_MARK" "$_LINE" >> "$_rc"\n    fi\n  done\nfi\n\necho "Installing deng-rejoin wrapper to 
$BIN/deng-rejoin"\ncat > "$BIN/deng-rejoin" << \'DENG_REJOIN_WRAPPER\'\n"""\n\n_INSTALL_PART_AFTER_HEREDOC = 
r"""\nDENG_REJOIN_WRAPPER\nchmod +x "$BIN/deng-rejoin" || { echo "chmod failed: $BIN/deng-rejoin" >&2; exit 1; }\nhash 
-r 2>/dev/null || true\n\n_fail_install() {\n  echo "Failed to create deng-rejoin command." >&2\n  echo "Wrapper path: 
$BIN/deng-rejoin" >&2\n  echo "PATH: $PATH" >&2\n  echo "PREFIX: ${PREFIX:-<unset>}" >&2\n  echo "Try: ls -la 
$BIN/deng-rejoin" >&2\n  exit 1\n}\n\n[ -s "$BIN/deng-rejoin" ] || _fail_install\n[ -x "$BIN/deng-rejoin" ] || 
_fail_install\n{ [ -f "$APP_HOME/.install_api" ] && [ -s "$APP_HOME/.install_api" ]; } || _fail_install\n[ -f 
"$APP_HOME/agent/deng_tool_rejoin.py" ] || _fail_install\n[ -f "$APP_HOME/agent/deferred_bundle_install.py" ] || 
_fail_install\n\nDR_RESOLVED=""\nif command -v deng-rejoin >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n  DR_RESOLVED="$(command -v 
deng-rejoin)"\nfi\nif [ -z "$DR_RESOLVED" ]; then\n  echo "command -v deng-rejoin did not resolve after install." 
>&2\n  _fail_install\nfi\n\nif ! PYTHONPATH="$APP_HOME" python3 -c "import agent.deferred_bundle_install" 2>/dev/null; 
then\n  echo "Failed: launcher Python modules did not import. APP_HOME: $APP_HOME" >&2\n  _fail_install\nfi\n\nset 
+e\n_PY_ERR="$(PYTHONPATH="$APP_HOME" DENG_REJOIN_HOME="$APP_HOME" python3 -c "from agent.deferred_bundle_install 
import resolve_install_api" 2>&1)"\n_PY_RC=$?\nset -e\nif [ "$_PY_RC" -ne 0 ]; then\n  echo "Failed launcher 
self-check." >&2\n  echo "agent/deferred_bundle_install.py is missing resolve_install_api or import failed." >&2\n  
echo "The launcher bundle may be stale or corrupted." >&2\n  echo "APP_HOME=$APP_HOME" >&2\n  echo "Launcher bundle 
URL: $LAUNCHER_URL" >&2\n  echo "Expected file: $APP_HOME/agent/deferred_bundle_install.py" >&2\n  echo "Python 
error:" >&2\n  echo "$_PY_ERR" >&2\n  exit 1\nfi\n\n# Prove first-run API resolves from ~/.install_api without install 
shell env\nAPI_R="$(\n  PYTHONPATH="$APP_HOME" DENG_REJOIN_HOME="$APP_HOME" \\\n  python3 -c \'import os; 
os.environ.pop("DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API", None); from agent.deferred_bundle_install import resolve_install_api; 
print(resolve_install_api())\'\n)" || API_R=""\nif [ -z "$API_R" ]; then\n  echo "Failed: could not resolve install 
API (resolve_install_api)." >&2\n  _fail_install\nfi\nif [ "$API_R" != "$DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API" ]; then\n  echo 
"Install API URL mismatch after resolve." >&2\n  echo "Expected: $DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API" >&2\n  echo "Got: $API_R" 
>&2\n  exit 1\nfi\n\necho "Wrapper path: $BIN/deng-rejoin"\necho "command -v deng-rejoin -> $DR_RESOLVED"\necho 
"resolve_install_api (env unset) -> $API_R"\nif [ "$USING_HOME_BIN" -eq 1 ]; then\n  echo \'Note: If deng-rejoin is 
not found in this shell, run once:\'\n  echo \'  export PATH="$HOME/bin:$PATH"\'\n  echo \'  hash -r\'\nfi\necho 
""\necho "Install complete."\necho "Next: run deng-rejoin"\n"""\n\n\ndef _sh_default_for_param_expansion(url: str) -> 
str:\n    """Escape for use inside bash ``${{VAR:-{here}}}`` default segment."""\n    return (url or 
"").replace("\\\\", "\\\\\\\\").replace("}", "\\\\}")\n\n\ndef wrapper_body_sh(install_api_base: str) -> str:\n    
"""POSIX wrapper: set HOME, default API in env, python + entry checks, exec."""\n    d = 
_sh_default_for_param_expansion(install_api_base.rstrip("/"))\n    return (\n        "#!/usr/bin/env sh\\n"\n        
\'export DENG_REJOIN_HOME="${DENG_REJOIN_HOME:-$HOME/.deng-tool/rejoin}"\\n\'\n        f\'export 
DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API="${{DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API:-{d}}}"\\n\'\n        "if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; 
then\\n"\n        \'  echo "deng-rejoin: python3 not found. Install: pkg install python" >&2\\n\'\n        "  exit 
127\\n"\n        "fi\\n"\n        \'if [ ! -f "$DENG_REJOIN_HOME/agent/deng_tool_rejoin.py" ]; then\\n\'\n        \'  
echo "deng-rejoin: missing $DENG_REJOIN_HOME/agent/deng_tool_rejoin.py" >&2\\n\'\n        "  exit 1\\n"\n        
"fi\\n"\n        \'exec python3 "$DENG_REJOIN_HOME/agent/deng_tool_rejoin.py" "$@"\\n\'\n    )\n\n\ndef 
render_public_bootstrap(\n    *,\n    base_url: str,\n    requested: str,\n    bootstrap_session: str = "",\n    
installer_title: str = "DENG Tool: Rejoin Installer",\n    banner_lines: tuple[str, ...] = (),\n    bundle_etag: str = 
"",\n) -> str:\n    """*requested* is ``latest``, ``v1.0.0``, ``main-dev``, ``test-latest``, etc."""\n    base = 
base_url.rstrip("/")\n    if bootstrap_session:\n        safe_sess = _escape_double(bootstrap_session)\n        
sess_export = f\'export BOOTSTRAP_SESSION="{safe_sess}"\'\n    else:\n        sess_export = "unset BOOTSTRAP_SESSION 
2>/dev/null || true"\n\n    banner_part = ""\n    if banner_lines:\n        banner_part = "\\n".join(f\'echo 
"{_escape_double(line)}"\' for line in banner_lines) + "\\n"\n\n    safe_title = _escape_double(installer_title)\n    
safe_req = _escape_double(requested)\n\n    tail = (\n        _INSTALL_PART_BEFORE_HEREDOC\n        + 
wrapper_body_sh(base)\n        + _INSTALL_PART_AFTER_HEREDOC\n    )\n\n    # Inject a cache-busting query parameter so 
CDN edges don\'t serve a stale bundle.\n    if bundle_etag:\n        _old = 
\'"$DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API/install/launcher/bundle.tar.gz"\'\n        _new = 
f\'"$DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API/install/launcher/bundle.tar.gz?v={bundle_etag}"\'\n        tail = tail.replace(_old, 
_new, 1)\n\n    return (\n        "#!/usr/bin/env sh\\n"\n        # POSIX-sh only.  No bash features anywhere ù 
Termux\'s /usr/bin/sh\n        # is dash, and the shebang on line 1 is ignored when invoked as\n        # ``curl ... | 
sh``.  See module docstring.\n        "set -eu\\n"\n        + f\'echo "{safe_title}"\\n\'\n        +         
f"{banner_part}"\n        + f\'export DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API="{base}"\\n\'\n        f"{sess_export}\\n"\n        
\'APP_HOME="${DENG_REJOIN_HOME:-$HOME/.deng-tool/rejoin}"\\n\'\n        \'mkdir -p "$APP_HOME"\\n\'\n        f\'printf 
\\\'%s\\\\n\\\' "{safe_req}" > "$APP_HOME/.install_requested"\\n\'\n        \'printf \\\'%s\\\\n\\\' 
"$DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API" > "$APP_HOME/.install_api"\\n\'\n        \'if [ -n "${BOOTSTRAP_SESSION:-}" ]; then\\n\'\n  
      \'  printf \\\'%s\\\\n\\\' "$BOOTSTRAP_SESSION" > "$APP_HOME/.bootstrap_session"\\n\'\n        "else\\n"\n       
 \'  rm -f "$APP_HOME/.bootstrap_session"\\n\'\n        "fi\\n"\n        f"{tail.lstrip()}"\n    )\n\n\ndef 
render_direct_install_bootstrap(\n    *,\n    base_url: str,\n    package_sha256: str,\n    installer_title: str = 
"DENG Tool: Rejoin Installer",\n    banner_lines: tuple[str, ...] = (),\n) -> str:\n    """Generate a bash installer 
that downloads the full package directly.\n\n    Behavior (in this order, hard-failing on any step):\n\n    1. 
Download ``test/package.tar.gz`` with a cache-busting query param.\n    2. Verify SHA-256 matches the manifest value 
embedded in the script.\n    3. Stop any running ``deng-rejoin`` process (best-effort).\n    4. Preserve user data 
(``config.json``, ``data/``, ``logs/``,\n       ``license.json``, ``.install_api``, ``backups/``) by leaving them\n    
   untouched.\n    5. **Delete** the code directories (``agent/``, ``bot/``, ``scripts/``,\n       ``docs/``, 
``examples/``, ``assets/``) plus every ``__pycache__/`` and\n       ``*.pyc``.  This guarantees orphan files from the 
previous build\n       cannot shadow the new install.\n    6. Extract the verified tarball.\n    7. Write 
``.installed-build.json`` with the verified SHA, git commit,\n       channel, install time, install API, and the URLs 
that produced this\n       install.\n    8. Recreate ``$PREFIX/bin/deng-rejoin`` (or ``$HOME/bin/deng-rejoin``).\n    
9. Run a Python import probe against the new modules.\n    10. Run ``deng-rejoin version`` to prove the wrapper 
executes the new\n        installed code and emits the expected commit / SHA.\n    11. Abort cleanly on any failure ù 
the user does not get a half-installed\n        tool.\n    """\n    base = base_url.rstrip("/")\n    banner_part = 
""\n    if banner_lines:\n        banner_part = "\\n".join(f\'echo "{_escape_double(line)}"\' for line in 
banner_lines) + "\\n"\n\n    safe_title = _escape_double(installer_title)\n    safe_sha = 
_escape_double(package_sha256)\n\n    pre_heredoc = (\n        \'echo "=============================="\\n\'\n        
\'echo "DENG Tool: Rejoin Installing"\\n\'\n        \'echo "------------------------------"\\n\'\n        \'echo 
"Version: main-dev"\\n\'\n        \'echo "=============================="\\n\'\n        + "command -v curl >/dev/null 
2>&1 || { echo \\"Install curl first: pkg install -y curl\\" >&2; exit 1; }\\n"\n        "command -v tar >/dev/null 
2>&1 || { echo \\"Install tar first: pkg install -y tar\\" >&2; exit 1; }\\n"\n        "command -v python3 >/dev/null 
2>&1 || { echo \\"Install python first: pkg install -y python\\" >&2; exit 1; }\\n"\n        f\'export 
DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API="{base}"\\n\'\n        \'APP_HOME="${DENG_REJOIN_HOME:-$HOME/.deng-tool/rejoin}"\\n\'\n        
\'mkdir -p "$APP_HOME"\\n\'\n        # Cache-bust the package URL so neither curl, transparent proxies,\n        # nor 
a CDN edge can hand us a stale tarball.\n        \'CACHE_BUSTER="$(date +%s)-$$"\\n\'\n        
\'PACKAGE_URL_BASE="$DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API/install/test/package.tar.gz"\\n\'\n        
\'PACKAGE_URL="$PACKAGE_URL_BASE?t=$CACHE_BUSTER"\\n\'\n        
\'INSTALLER_URL="$DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API/install/test/latest"\\n\'\n        f\'EXPECTED_SHA256="{safe_sha}"\\n\'\n    
    \'TMP="$(mktemp)"\\n\'\n        "trap \'rm -f \\"$TMP\\"\' EXIT\\n"\n        \'curl -fsSL \'\n        \'-H 
"Cache-Control: no-cache" -H "Pragma: no-cache" \'\n        \'-A "deng-rejoin-installer/1.0" "$PACKAGE_URL" -o "$TMP" 
|| {\\n\'\n        \'  echo "Download failed." >&2\\n\'\n        \'  echo "URL: $PACKAGE_URL_BASE" >&2\\n\'\n        " 
 exit 1\\n"\n        "}\\n"\n        "ACTUAL_SHA=\\"$(python3 -c \'import 
hashlib,sys;d=open(sys.argv[1],\\"rb\\").read();print(hashlib.sha256(d).hexdigest())\' \\"$TMP\\" 2>/dev/null)\\" || 
ACTUAL_SHA=\\"\\"\\n"\n        \'if [ "$ACTUAL_SHA" != "$EXPECTED_SHA256" ]; then\\n\'\n        \'  echo "Package 
checksum mismatch. The download may be corrupted or stale." >&2\\n\'\n        \'  echo "Expected: $EXPECTED_SHA256" 
>&2\\n\'\n        \'  echo "Got:      $ACTUAL_SHA" >&2\\n\'\n        \'  echo "This means the installer script and the 
package are out of sync." >&2\\n\'\n        "  exit 1\\n"\n        "fi\\n"\n        # Stop any running deng-rejoin 
process so we never overwrite live code.\n        \'_stop_running() {\\n\'\n        \'  if command -v pkill >/dev/null 
2>&1; then\\n\'\n        "    pkill -f \'agent/deng_tool_rejoin.py\' 2>/dev/null || true\\n"\n        \'  fi\\n\'\n    
    \'  if [ -f "$APP_HOME/data/rejoin.pid" ]; then\\n\'\n        \'    _pid="$(cat "$APP_HOME/data/rejoin.pid" 
2>/dev/null || true)"\\n\'\n        \'    if [ -n "$_pid" ]; then\\n\'\n        \'      kill "$_pid" 2>/dev/null || 
true\\n\'\n        \'    fi\\n\'\n        \'  fi\\n\'\n        \'}\\n\'\n        \'_stop_running\\n\'\n        # 
\u2500\u2500 PURGE OLD RUNTIME \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        # Remove code directories 
entirely ù NOT just extract-over-them.\n        # This guarantees orphan modules from the previous build cannot 
shadow\n        # the new install.  User data (config, license, logs) lives at the top\n        # level or under data/ 
which is intentionally NOT in this list.\n        \'for _d in agent bot scripts docs examples assets; do\\n\'\n        
\'  rm -rf "$APP_HOME/$_d" 2>/dev/null || true\\n\'\n        \'done\\n\'\n        \'rm -f "$APP_HOME/BUILD-INFO.json" 
"$APP_HOME/.installed-build.json" 2>/dev/null || true\\n\'\n        # Reliable pycache purge: -depth ensures children 
before parents,\n        # then a second *.pyc pass catches any stragglers.\n        # POSIX-sh compatible ù no bash 
arrays, no process substitution.\n        \'find "$APP_HOME" -depth -name __pycache__ -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 
2>/dev/null || true\\n\'\n        \'find "$APP_HOME" -name "*.pyc" 2>/dev/null -exec rm -f {} + || true\\n\'\n        
# \u2500\u2500 EXTRACT FRESH ARTIFACT \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        \'tar -xzf "$TMP" -C "$APP_HOME" || { echo "Could not 
extract package." >&2; exit 1; }\\n\'\n        # Post-extraction pycache sweep (the tarball must not contain any, 
but\n        # verify and clean regardless so the install state is always known-clean).\n        \'find "$APP_HOME" 
-depth -name __pycache__ -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true\\n\'\n        \'find "$APP_HOME" -name "*.pyc" 
2>/dev/null -exec rm -f {} + || true\\n\'\n        \'if [ ! -f "$APP_HOME/agent/deng_tool_rejoin.py" ]; then\\n\'\n    
    \'  echo "Install error: agent/deng_tool_rejoin.py missing from package." >&2\\n\'\n        "  exit 1\\n"\n        
"fi\\n"\n        \'[ -f "$APP_HOME/BUILD-INFO.json" ] || { echo "Install error: BUILD-INFO.json missing ù tarball is 
corrupt." >&2; exit 1; }\\n\'\n        # Record the verified API base under the install root.\n        "printf 
\'%s\\\\n\' \\"$DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API\\" > \\"$APP_HOME/.install_api\\"\\n"\n        # Write the install-time 
metadata file.  We parse git_commit and\n        # probe_id out of the just-extracted BUILD-INFO.json so the runtime\n 
       # can show them without re-running git.\n        \'_GIT_COMMIT="$(python3 -c \\\'import json,os,sys; 
p=sys.argv[1]; \'\n        \'print((json.load(open(p)).get("git_commit","")) if os.path.isfile(p) else "")\\\' \'\n    
    \'"$APP_HOME/BUILD-INFO.json" 2>/dev/null)" || _GIT_COMMIT=""\\n\'\n        \'_PROBE_ID="$(python3 -c \\\'import 
json,os,sys; p=sys.argv[1]; \'\n        \'print((json.load(open(p)).get("probe_id","")) if os.path.isfile(p) else 
"")\\\' \'\n        \'"$APP_HOME/BUILD-INFO.json" 2>/dev/null)" || _PROBE_ID=""\\n\'\n        \'_FILE_COUNT="$(find 
"$APP_HOME/agent" -type f | wc -l 2>/dev/null | tr -d "[:space:]" || echo 0)"\\n\'\n        
\'_INSTALL_TIME_ISO="$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")"\\n\'\n        \'cat > "$APP_HOME/.installed-build.json" 
<<EOF\\n\'\n        \'{\\n\'\n        \'  "artifact_sha256": "$EXPECTED_SHA256",\\n\'\n        \'  "git_commit": 
"$_GIT_COMMIT",\\n\'\n        \'  "probe_id": "$_PROBE_ID",\\n\'\n        \'  "channel": "main-dev",\\n\'\n        \'  
"install_time_iso": "$_INSTALL_TIME_ISO",\\n\'\n        \'  "install_api": "$DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API",\\n\'\n        
\'  "package_url": "$PACKAGE_URL_BASE",\\n\'\n        \'  "installer_url": "$INSTALLER_URL",\\n\'\n        \'  
"extracted_file_count": $_FILE_COUNT\\n\'\n        \'}\\n\'\n        \'EOF\\n\'\n        # Wrapper install (unchanged 
choice of $PREFIX/bin vs $HOME/bin).\n        "USING_HOME_BIN=0\\n"\n        \'BIN=""\\n\'\n        \'if [ -n 
"${PREFIX:-}" ]; then\\n\'\n        \'  if mkdir -p "${PREFIX}/bin" 2>/dev/null; then\\n\'\n        \'    
BIN="${PREFIX}/bin"\\n\'\n        "  fi\\n"\n        "fi\\n"\n        \'if [ -z "$BIN" ]; then\\n\'\n        \'  
BIN="$HOME/bin"\\n\'\n        \'  mkdir -p "$BIN"\\n\'\n        \'  export PATH="$HOME/bin:$PATH"\\n\'\n        "  
USING_HOME_BIN=1\\n"\n        "  _MARK=\'# DENG Tool: Rejoin - PATH (added by installer)\'\\n"\n        "  
_LINE=\'export PATH=\\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\\"\'\\n"\n        \'  for _rc in "$HOME/.bashrc" "$HOME/.profile"; do\\n\'\n    
    \'    touch "$_rc"\\n\'\n        \'    if ! grep -qF "$_MARK" "$_rc" 2>/dev/null; then\\n\'\n        "      printf 
\'\\\\n%s\\\\n%s\\\\n\' \\"$_MARK\\" \\"$_LINE\\" >> \\"$_rc\\"\\n"\n        "    fi\\n"\n        "  done\\n"\n        
"fi\\n"\n        # Remove any stale wrapper before writing the new one so a prior\n        # symlink or read-only file 
cannot block recreation.\n        \'rm -f "$BIN/deng-rejoin" 2>/dev/null || true\\n\'\n        "cat > 
\\"$BIN/deng-rejoin\\" << \'DENG_REJOIN_WRAPPER\'\\n"\n    )\n\n    post_heredoc = (\n        
"DENG_REJOIN_WRAPPER\\n"\n        "chmod +x \\"$BIN/deng-rejoin\\" || { echo \\"chmod failed: $BIN/deng-rejoin\\" >&2; 
exit 1; }\\n"\n        "hash -r 2>/dev/null || true\\n"\n        \'[ -s "$BIN/deng-rejoin" ] || { echo "Failed to 
create deng-rejoin wrapper." >&2; exit 1; }\\n\'\n        \'[ -x "$BIN/deng-rejoin" ] || { echo "Failed: wrapper not 
executable." >&2; exit 1; }\\n\'\n        \'[ -f "$APP_HOME/agent/deng_tool_rejoin.py" ] || { echo "Failed: 
deng_tool_rejoin.py missing." >&2; exit 1; }\\n\'\n        \'[ -f "$APP_HOME/BUILD-INFO.json" ] || { echo "Failed: 
BUILD-INFO.json missing in package." >&2; exit 1; }\\n\'\n        \'[ -f "$APP_HOME/.installed-build.json" ] || { echo 
"Failed: .installed-build.json was not written." >&2; exit 1; }\\n\'\n        \'DR_RESOLVED=""\\n\'\n        \'if 
command -v deng-rejoin >/dev/null 2>&1; then\\n\'\n        \'  DR_RESOLVED="$(command -v deng-rejoin)"\\n\'\n        
"fi\\n"\n        \'if [ -z "$DR_RESOLVED" ]; then\\n\'\n        \'  echo "command -v deng-rejoin did not resolve after 
install." >&2\\n\'\n        \'  if [ "$USING_HOME_BIN" -eq 1 ]; then\\n\'\n        "    echo \'Run: export 
PATH=\\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\\" && hash -r && deng-rejoin\' >&2\\n"\n        "  fi\\n"\n        "  exit 1\\n"\n        
"fi\\n"\n        # Import check on the new modules.  If any of these fail, the install\n        # is corrupted or the 
tarball is missing files.\n        \'if ! PYTHONPATH="$APP_HOME" python3 -c \'\n        "\'import agent.commands, 
agent.supervisor, agent.roblox_presence, agent.freeform_enable, agent.playing_state, agent.dumpsys_cache, 
agent.window_apply\' "\n        \'2>/dev/null; then\\n\'\n        \'  echo "Install verification failed: required 
modules did not import." >&2\\n\'\n        \'  echo "APP_HOME=$APP_HOME" >&2\\n\'\n        "  exit 1\\n"\n        
"fi\\n"\n        # Prove the wrapper executes the new code by invoking `version`.\n        
\'_VERSION_OUT="$("$BIN/deng-rejoin" version 2>/dev/null)" || _VERSION_OUT=""\\n\'\n        \'if ! echo 
"$_VERSION_OUT" | grep -q "^artifact_sha256: " ; then\\n\'\n        \'  echo "Install verification failed: deng-rejoin 
version did not return artifact SHA." >&2\\n\'\n        "  exit 1\\n"\n        "fi\\n"\n        
\'_INSTALLED_SHORT_SHA="$(echo "$_VERSION_OUT" | grep "^artifact_sha256: " | head -n1 | awk \\\'{print 
$2}\\\')"\\n\'\n        # POSIX-sh substring: bash\'s ``${VAR:0:12}`` is unsupported by dash.\n        # ``printf 
\'%.12s\'`` works in every POSIX printf.\n        "_EXPECTED_SHORT_SHA=\\"$(printf \'%.12s\' 
\\"$EXPECTED_SHA256\\")\\"\\n"\n        \'if [ "$_INSTALLED_SHORT_SHA" != "$_EXPECTED_SHORT_SHA" ]; then\\n\'\n        
\'  echo "Install verification failed: installed SHA mismatch." >&2\\n\'\n        \'  echo "Expected (short): 
$_EXPECTED_SHORT_SHA" >&2\\n\'\n        \'  echo "Got (short):      $_INSTALLED_SHORT_SHA" >&2\\n\'\n        "  exit 
1\\n"\n        "fi\\n"\n        # \u2500\u2500 RUNTIME LEGACY DETECTOR CHECK \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        # Verify the installed supervisor does not import the old 
broken\n        # experience_detector module, and does not contain forbidden state names.\n        # Implementation 
uses grep directly on the extracted supervisor file so\n        # no single-quotes appear inside shell single-quoted 
strings (POSIX-safe).\n        \'_LEGACY_IMPORT="NO"\\n\'\n        \'_SV_FILE="$APP_HOME/agent/supervisor.py"\\n\'\n   
     \'if [ -f "$_SV_FILE" ]; then\\n\'\n        \'  if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*(from|import)[^#]*experience_detector" 
"$_SV_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then\\n\'\n        \'    _LEGACY_IMPORT="YES (WARNING: old detector in supervisor)"\\n\'\n    
    \'  fi\\n\'\n        \'fi\\n\'\n        # \u2500\u2500 AGENT FILE PROOF \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\n        \'_AGENT_FILE="$(PYTHONPATH="$APP_HOME" python3 -c "import agent; print(agent.__file__)" 
2>/dev/null)" || _AGENT_FILE=""\\n\'\n        # \u2500\u2500 CLEAN PYCACHE before proof block \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        # Running Python above creates __pycache__; clear it so the 
proof block\n        # reflects a clean post-install state (no stale bytecode).\n        \'find "$APP_HOME" -depth 
-name __pycache__ -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true\\n\'\n        \'find "$APP_HOME" -name "*.pyc" 
2>/dev/null -exec rm -f {} + || true\\n\'\n        # \u2500\u2500 FINAL PROOF BLOCK \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\n        \'echo "Install complete."\\n\'\n        \'echo "=============================="\\n\'\n    )\n\n    
return (\n        "#!/usr/bin/env sh\\n"\n        # POSIX-sh only.  See module docstring: Termux\'s /usr/bin/sh is\n   
     # dash, the shebang is ignored when invoked via ``curl ... | sh``,\n        # and any bash feature would abort 
with "Illegal option" or\n        # "binary file" errors before this script gets to do anything useful.\n        + 
"set -eu\\n"\n        + pre_heredoc\n        + wrapper_body_sh(base)\n        + post_heredoc\n    )\n\n\ndef 
_escape_double(s: str) -> str:\n    return (s or "").replace("\\\\", "\\\\\\\\").replace(\'"\', \'\\\\"\')\n\n\n# 
\u2500\u2500 POSIX-sh discipline \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n#\n#
 We previously attempted a "re-exec into bash" preamble.  It failed on the\n# real device:\n#\n#     $ curl -fsSL 
https://rejoin.deng.my.id/install/latest | sh\n#     /data/data/com.termux/files/usr/bin/sh: 
/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/bin/sh: cannot execute binary file\n#\n# Root cause: when ``curl ... | sh`` runs on 
Termux, dash\'s ``$0`` is the\n# **dash binary itself** (``/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/bin/sh``), not\n# the name 
of a script file.  Our preamble did ``exec bash "$0"``, which\n# made bash try to execute the dash ELF binary as a 
shell script.\n#\n# Lesson learned: do not try to be clever.  Both bootstrap renderers above\n# emit **strictly 
POSIX-sh** scripts: ``set -eu`` only, ``[ ... ]`` only,\n# ``case`` instead of ``[[ == *pattern* ]]``, ``printf 
\'%.Ns\'`` instead of\n# ``${VAR:0:N}``, explicit glob loops instead of arrays + ``shopt nullglob``.\n# These run 
cleanly under dash, busybox sh, ash, bash, and any other POSIX\n# shell.  No re-exec, no temp files, no PATH 
lookups.\n\n"""Discord embed branding: hosted logo URL for thumbnails only.\n\nDiscord embeds cannot use local 
filesystem paths as thumbnail URLs.\n\nPriority for the default logo URL:\n\n1. ``DENG_BRANDING_LOGO_URL`` (full HTTPS 
URL).\n2. ``LICENSE_API_PUBLIC_URL`` + ``/assets/denghub_logo.png`` when the public\n   base URL is set (served by 
:mod:`bot.license_api`).\n3. Otherwise no thumbnail (localhost-only API URLs are not used ù Discord\n   cannot fetch 
them).\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport os\nfrom typing import Any\n\n_PUBLIC_LOGO_PATH = 
"/assets/denghub_logo.png"\n\n\ndef get_branding_logo_url() -> str:\n    """Return a non-empty URL when a hosted logo 
can be resolved."""\n    explicit = (os.environ.get("DENG_BRANDING_LOGO_URL") or "").strip()\n    if explicit:\n       
 return explicit\n    pub = (os.environ.get("LICENSE_API_PUBLIC_URL") or "").strip().rstrip("/")\n    if pub:\n        
low = pub.lower()\n        if low.startswith("http://localhost") or low.startswith("http://127."):\n            return 
""\n        return f"{pub}{_PUBLIC_LOGO_PATH}"\n    return ""\n\n\ndef apply_branding_to_embed_dict(\n    embed_dict: 
dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    include_thumbnail: bool = True,\n) -> None:\n    """Mutate *embed_dict* in place: set 
``thumbnail.url`` when configured.\n\n    Use ``include_thumbnail=False`` for ephemeral interaction replies so only\n  
  the main public panel post shows the DENG Hub logo.\n    """\n    if not include_thumbnail:\n        
embed_dict.pop("thumbnail", None)\n        return\n    url = get_branding_logo_url()\n    if not url:\n        
return\n    embed_dict["thumbnail"] = {"url": url}\n\n\ndef apply_branding_to_discord_embed(embed: Any) -> Any:\n    
"""Apply thumbnail to a discord.py ``Embed`` when a logo URL is set."""\n    url = get_branding_logo_url()\n    if url 
and hasattr(embed, "set_thumbnail"):\n        embed.set_thumbnail(url=url)\n    return embed\n\n\ndef 
apply_branding(embed_dict: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n    """Alias for :func:`apply_branding_to_embed_dict` (mutates 
embed JSON dict)."""\n    apply_branding_to_embed_dict(embed_dict)\n\n"""Runtime build-proof helpers for DENG Tool: 
Rejoin.\n\nThe tool reads two on-disk files to prove which build is actually running on\nthe device:\n\n* 
``BUILD-INFO.json`` ù embedded inside the published tarball by\n  
:func:`agent.internal_test_artifact.build_internal_test_tarball`.  Carries\n  the git commit + build time + channel.  
This file is shipped with the code\n  and is always co-located with ``agent/`` after install.\n\n* 
``.installed-build.json`` ù written by the installer bash script at install\n  time, sitting at the install-root (one 
level above ``agent/``).  Carries\n  the verified ``artifact_sha256``, ``install_time``, ``package_url`` and\n  
``installer_url`` that the installer just downloaded.\n\n``deng-rejoin version`` and ``deng-rejoin doctor install`` 
consume the merged\nview from this module.  The functions here have no side effects and use only\nthe standard library 
so that even when modules are partially broken the\nversion / doctor commands still run.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ 
import annotations\n\nimport hashlib\nimport json\nimport os\nimport shutil\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import 
Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Paths \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n#
 install root = directory containing this ``agent`` package\nINSTALL_ROOT = 
Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nBUILD_INFO_PATH = INSTALL_ROOT / "BUILD-INFO.json"\nINSTALLED_BUILD_PATH = 
INSTALL_ROOT / ".installed-build.json"\nINSTALL_API_PATH = INSTALL_ROOT / ".install_api"\n\n\ndef _read_json(path: 
Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    try:\n        if not path.is_file():\n            return {}\n        return 
json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))\n    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n        return {}\n\n\ndef 
load_build_info() -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Load the embedded ``BUILD-INFO.json``; empty dict if missing."""\n    
return _read_json(BUILD_INFO_PATH)\n\n\ndef load_installed_build() -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Load the 
installer-written ``.installed-build.json``; empty if missing."""\n    return 
_read_json(INSTALLED_BUILD_PATH)\n\n\ndef _read_install_api() -> str:\n    try:\n        if not 
INSTALL_API_PATH.is_file():\n            return ""\n        return 
INSTALL_API_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()\n    except OSError:\n        return ""\n\n\ndef 
find_wrapper_path() -> str | None:\n    """Best-effort: where the ``deng-rejoin`` shell wrapper lives on PATH."""\n    
return shutil.which("deng-rejoin")\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Required modules / symbols that prove the new build is 
loaded \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n# Per task: doctor install must 
verify these modules exist and these symbols\n# resolve.  Adding a key here is the only way to make a feature 
mandatory.\nREQUIRED_MODULES: tuple[str, ...] = (\n    "agent.commands",\n    "agent.supervisor",\n    
"agent.roblox_presence",\n    "agent.window_apply",\n    "agent.freeform_enable",\n    "agent.playing_state",\n    
"agent.dumpsys_cache",\n    "agent.android",\n    "agent.launcher",\n    "agent.termux_minimize",\n)\n\n# (module, 
symbol) pairs that must resolve at import time.\nREQUIRED_SYMBOLS: tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (\n    
("agent.roblox_presence", "fetch_presence"),\n    ("agent.roblox_presence", "lookup_user_id"),\n    
("agent.dumpsys_cache", "cached_run"),\n    ("agent.freeform_enable", "setup_freeform_capabilities"),\n    
("agent.playing_state", "StateTracker"),\n    ("agent.android", "launch_package_with_bounds"),\n    
("agent.termux_minimize", "minimize_termux_to_dock"),\n)\n\n\ndef _module_file_path(modname: str) -> str | None:\n    
"""Return the on-disk ``__file__`` for *modname* without importing it.\n\n    We resolve through 
``importlib.util.find_spec`` so a missing module simply\n    returns ``None`` and never raises during ``doctor 
install``.\n    """\n    try:\n        import importlib.util\n\n        spec = importlib.util.find_spec(modname)\n     
   if spec is None or not spec.origin:\n            return None\n        return spec.origin\n    except Exception:  # 
noqa: BLE001 ù best-effort probe.\n        return None\n\n\ndef _hash_file(path: Path) -> str:\n    h = 
hashlib.sha256()\n    try:\n        with path.open("rb") as f:\n            for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(64 * 
1024), b""):\n                h.update(chunk)\n    except OSError:\n        return ""\n    return 
h.hexdigest()\n\n\ndef _short(sha: str, n: int = 12) -> str:\n    sha = (sha or "").strip()\n    return sha[:n] if sha 
else ""\n\n\ndef _wrapper_target_install_root() -> str | None:\n    """Inspect the wrapper script and try to extract 
its install-root path.\n\n    The wrapper we generate exports ``DENG_REJOIN_HOME=...`` and ultimately\n    executes 
``$DENG_REJOIN_HOME/agent/deng_tool_rejoin.py``.  Older wrappers\n    set ``APP_HOME=...`` instead.  Return the path 
string if discoverable\n    (with bash parameter expansion stripped to its default value), else\n    ``None``.\n    
"""\n    wrapper = find_wrapper_path()\n    if not wrapper:\n        return None\n    try:\n        text = 
Path(wrapper).read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")\n    except OSError:\n        return None\n    for line in 
text.splitlines():\n        s = line.strip()\n        # Examples we accept:\n        #   export 
DENG_REJOIN_HOME="${DENG_REJOIN_HOME:-$HOME/.deng-tool/rejoin}"\n        #   
DENG_REJOIN_HOME="$HOME/.deng-tool/rejoin"\n        #   APP_HOME="$HOME/.deng-tool/rejoin"\n        for prefix in 
("export DENG_REJOIN_HOME=", "DENG_REJOIN_HOME=", "APP_HOME="):\n            if s.startswith(prefix):\n                
raw = s[len(prefix):].strip().strip(\'"\').strip("\'")\n                # Strip ${VAR:-default} \u2192 default\n       
         if raw.startswith("${") and ":-" in raw and raw.endswith("}"):\n                    raw = raw[raw.index(":-") 
+ 2 : -1]\n                return raw\n    return None\n\n\ndef collect_version_info() -> dict[str, Any]:\n    
"""Return the merged dict consumed by ``deng-rejoin version``.\n\n    Never raises; missing pieces produce empty 
strings.\n    """\n    from .constants import VERSION, PRODUCT_NAME\n\n    bi = load_build_info()\n    ib = 
load_installed_build()\n    info: dict[str, Any] = {\n        "product": PRODUCT_NAME,\n        "product_version": 
str(VERSION),\n        "channel": str(ib.get("channel") or bi.get("channel") or ""),\n        "git_commit": 
str(ib.get("git_commit") or bi.get("git_commit") or ""),\n        "git_commit_short": _short(\n            
str(ib.get("git_commit") or bi.get("git_commit") or "")\n        ),\n        "artifact_sha256": 
str(ib.get("artifact_sha256") or ""),\n        "artifact_sha256_short": _short(str(ib.get("artifact_sha256") or 
"")),\n        "built_at_iso": str(bi.get("built_at_iso") or ""),\n        "install_time_iso": 
str(ib.get("install_time_iso") or ""),\n        "install_api": str(ib.get("install_api") or _read_install_api() or 
""),\n        "package_url": str(ib.get("package_url") or ""),\n        "installer_url": str(ib.get("installer_url") 
or ""),\n        "install_root": str(INSTALL_ROOT),\n        "python_executable": sys.executable,\n        
"python_version": sys.version.split()[0],\n        "wrapper_path": find_wrapper_path() or "",\n        
"wrapper_target_root": _wrapper_target_install_root() or "",\n        "build_info_path": str(BUILD_INFO_PATH) if 
BUILD_INFO_PATH.is_file() else "",\n        "installed_build_path": str(INSTALLED_BUILD_PATH)\n        if 
INSTALLED_BUILD_PATH.is_file()\n        else "",\n        "modules": {},\n    }\n    for mod in REQUIRED_MODULES:\n    
    info["modules"][mod] = _module_file_path(mod) or ""\n    return info\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 doctor install \u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n
\n\ndef _orphan_pycache_dirs() -> list[str]:\n    """Find ``__pycache__`` directories where the parent has no matching 
``.py``.\n\n    These are leftover compiled modules from a previous install and they can\n    silently shadow 
source-not-present imports on some Pythons.  The installer\n    purges them; doctor install reports them.\n    """\n   
 out: list[str] = []\n    for root in (INSTALL_ROOT / "agent", INSTALL_ROOT / "bot", INSTALL_ROOT / "scripts"):\n      
  if not root.is_dir():\n            continue\n        for pyc_dir in root.rglob("__pycache__"):\n            try:\n   
             for pyc in pyc_dir.iterdir():\n                    if pyc.suffix != ".pyc":\n                        
continue\n                    # __pycache__/foo.cpython-311.pyc \u2192 foo.py expected in parent\n                    
name = pyc.stem.split(".")[0] + ".py"\n                    if not (pyc_dir.parent / name).is_file():\n                 
       out.append(str(pyc))\n            except OSError:\n                continue\n    return out\n\n\ndef 
doctor_install_checks() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    """Run the doctor-install probes and return one dict per 
check.\n\n    Each dict has keys: ``name`` (str), ``ok`` (bool), ``detail`` (str).\n    """\n    out: list[dict[str, 
Any]] = []\n\n    # 1) wrapper present\n    wrapper = find_wrapper_path()\n    out.append(\n        {\n            
"name": "wrapper_present",\n            "ok": bool(wrapper),\n            "detail": wrapper or "deng-rejoin not on 
PATH",\n        }\n    )\n\n    # 2) wrapper APP_HOME matches our install root\n    wrapper_root = 
_wrapper_target_install_root()\n    if wrapper_root:\n        # Compare normalised paths; tolerate trailing slashes 
and Termux vars.\n        expanded = os.path.expandvars(os.path.expanduser(wrapper_root))\n        ok = 
Path(expanded).resolve() == INSTALL_ROOT\n        out.append(\n            {\n                "name": 
"wrapper_targets_install_root",\n                "ok": ok,\n                "detail": f"wrapper 
APP_HOME={wrapper_root} install_root={INSTALL_ROOT}",\n            }\n        )\n\n    # 3) BUILD-INFO.json present\n  
  bi = load_build_info()\n    out.append(\n        {\n            "name": "build_info_present",\n            "ok": 
bool(bi),\n            "detail": str(BUILD_INFO_PATH) if bi else "missing BUILD-INFO.json",\n        }\n    )\n\n    # 
4) .installed-build.json present\n    ib = load_installed_build()\n    out.append(\n        {\n            "name": 
"installed_build_metadata",\n            "ok": bool(ib),\n            "detail": str(INSTALLED_BUILD_PATH) if ib else 
"missing .installed-build.json",\n        }\n    )\n\n    # 5) artifact SHA recorded by installer\n    sha_present = 
bool(str(ib.get("artifact_sha256") or "").strip())\n    out.append(\n        {\n            "name": 
"artifact_sha_recorded",\n            "ok": sha_present,\n            "detail": _short(str(ib.get("artifact_sha256") 
or "")) or "no SHA",\n        }\n    )\n\n    # 6) Required modules importable (we use find_spec ù no side effects).\n 
   missing_modules: list[str] = []\n    for mod in REQUIRED_MODULES:\n        if not _module_file_path(mod):\n         
   missing_modules.append(mod)\n    out.append(\n        {\n            "name": "required_modules_present",\n          
  "ok": not missing_modules,\n            "detail": "all present" if not missing_modules else f"missing: {\', 
\'.join(missing_modules)}",\n        }\n    )\n\n    # 7) Required symbols resolvable on import.\n    missing_symbols: 
list[str] = []\n    for mod, sym in REQUIRED_SYMBOLS:\n        try:\n            import importlib\n\n            m = 
importlib.import_module(mod)\n            if not hasattr(m, sym):\n                
missing_symbols.append(f"{mod}.{sym}")\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            
missing_symbols.append(f"{mod}.{sym}")\n    out.append(\n        {\n            "name": 
"required_symbols_resolvable",\n            "ok": not missing_symbols,\n            "detail": "all resolve"\n          
  if not missing_symbols\n            else f"missing: {\', \'.join(missing_symbols)}",\n        }\n    )\n\n    # 8) 
No orphan __pycache__ shadowing missing sources.\n    orphans = _orphan_pycache_dirs()\n    out.append(\n        {\n   
         "name": "no_orphan_pycache",\n            "ok": not orphans,\n            "detail": "clean" if not orphans 
else f"{len(orphans)} orphan .pyc files",\n        }\n    )\n\n    # 9) No stale "deferred installer" path from 
earlier builds.\n    stale_candidates = [\n        INSTALL_ROOT / "deferred_install.py",\n        INSTALL_ROOT / 
"deferred_installer.py",\n        INSTALL_ROOT / "agent" / "deferred_install.py",\n        INSTALL_ROOT / "scripts" / 
"deferred_install.sh",\n    ]\n    stale = [str(p) for p in stale_candidates if p.is_file()]\n    out.append(\n        
{\n            "name": "no_stale_deferred_installer",\n            "ok": not stale,\n            "detail": "clean" if 
not stale else f"found: {\', \'.join(stale)}",\n        }\n    )\n\n    # 10) Modules resolve under INSTALL_ROOT (not 
from some other PYTHONPATH).\n    misrouted: list[str] = []\n    for mod in REQUIRED_MODULES:\n        p = 
_module_file_path(mod)\n        if not p:\n            continue\n        try:\n            
Path(p).resolve().relative_to(INSTALL_ROOT)\n        except ValueError:\n            misrouted.append(f"{mod}={p}")\n  
  out.append(\n        {\n            "name": "modules_under_install_root",\n            "ok": not misrouted,\n        
    "detail": "all routed correctly"\n            if not misrouted\n            else f"shadowed: {\', 
\'.join(misrouted)}",\n        }\n    )\n\n    # 11) BUILD-INFO.json contains probe_id (proves this is a hardened 
build).\n    probe_id = str(bi.get("probe_id") or "").strip()\n    out.append(\n        {\n            "name": 
"build_info_has_probe_id",\n            "ok": bool(probe_id and probe_id.startswith("p-")),\n            "detail": 
probe_id if probe_id else "BUILD-INFO.json missing probe_id",\n        }\n    )\n\n    # 12) No legacy 
experience_detector imported by live supervisor.\n    sv_path = _module_file_path("agent.supervisor")\n    legacy_ok = 
True\n    legacy_detail = "clean"\n    if sv_path:\n        try:\n            import re as _re\n\n            sv_src = 
Path(sv_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")\n            # Match actual import statements, not 
comments\n            if _re.search(r"^\\s*(?:from|import)[^\\n#]*experience_detector", sv_src, _re.MULTILINE):\n      
          legacy_ok = False\n                legacy_detail = f"supervisor imports experience_detector ({sv_path})"\n   
     except OSError:\n            legacy_detail = "could not read supervisor source"\n    out.append(\n        {\n     
       "name": "no_legacy_detector_in_supervisor",\n            "ok": legacy_ok,\n            "detail": 
legacy_detail,\n        }\n    )\n\n    # 13) No Joining / Join Unconfirmed state names present in live supervisor.\n  
  joining_ok = True\n    joining_detail = "clean"\n    if sv_path:\n        try:\n            import re as _re\n\n     
       sv_src = Path(sv_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")\n            # Detect literal string 
"Joining" or "Join Unconfirmed" as state values\n            matches: list[str] = []\n            if 
_re.search(r"""[\'"](Joining)[\'"]\\s*[,)]""", sv_src):\n                matches.append("Joining")\n            if 
_re.search(r"""[\'"](Join Unconfirmed)[\'"]\\s*[,)]""", sv_src):\n                matches.append("Join Unconfirmed")\n 
           if matches:\n                joining_ok = False\n                joining_detail = f"forbidden state names 
in supervisor: {\', \'.join(matches)}"\n        except OSError:\n            joining_detail = "could not read 
supervisor source"\n    out.append(\n        {\n            "name": "no_joining_state_in_supervisor",\n            
"ok": joining_ok,\n            "detail": joining_detail,\n        }\n    )\n\n    # 14) No ANY __pycache__ directories 
under INSTALL_ROOT (all should be gone post-install).\n    pycache_dirs: list[str] = []\n    for root_dir in 
(INSTALL_ROOT / "agent", INSTALL_ROOT / "bot"):\n        if not root_dir.is_dir():\n            continue\n        
try:\n            for pc in root_dir.rglob("__pycache__"):\n                if pc.is_dir():\n                    
pycache_dirs.append(str(pc))\n        except OSError:\n            pass\n    out.append(\n        {\n            
"name": "no_pycache_dirs",\n            "ok": not pycache_dirs,\n            "detail": "clean"\n            if not 
pycache_dirs\n            else f"{len(pycache_dirs)} __pycache__ dirs remain",\n        }\n    )\n\n    return 
out\n\n\ndef doctor_install_overall_ok(results: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> bool:\n    """Return ``True`` only if every 
entry\'s ``ok`` is truthy."""\n    return all(bool(r.get("ok")) for r in results)\n\n\n# Alias used in commands.py 
(collect vs collected ù keep both working)\ncollected_version_info = collect_version_info\n\n"""YesCaptcha API client 
for CAPTCHA solving.\n\nProvides a minimal wrapper around the YesCaptcha REST API.\nConfigure the ``yescaptcha_key`` 
field in config.json to enable.\n\nThe key is never logged or transmitted to any server other than 
api.yescaptcha.com.\nThis module only handles the API call mechanics ù CAPTCHA detection and result\napplication are 
handled by the caller.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport time\nimport 
urllib.error\nimport urllib.request\nfrom typing import Any\n\nYESCAPTCHA_API_BASE = 
"https://api.yescaptcha.com"\n_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 3\n_DEFAULT_MAX_WAIT_SECONDS = 60\n\n\nclass 
CaptchaError(Exception):\n    """Raised when CAPTCHA solving fails or the API returns an error."""\n\n\ndef 
_post_json(url: str, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    data = json.dumps(payload).encode("utf-8")\n    
request = urllib.request.Request(\n        url,\n        data=data,\n        headers={"Content-Type": 
"application/json"},\n        method="POST",\n    )\n    try:\n        with urllib.request.urlopen(request, 
timeout=30) as response:  # noqa: S310\n            return json.loads(response.read().decode("utf-8", 
errors="replace"))\n    except urllib.error.URLError as exc:\n        raise CaptchaError(f"YesCaptcha API request 
failed: {exc}") from exc\n    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:\n        raise CaptchaError(f"YesCaptcha returned 
invalid JSON: {exc}") from exc\n\n\ndef _create_task(api_key: str, task: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    """Submit a task 
and return its task_id string."""\n    if not api_key:\n        raise CaptchaError("yescaptcha_key is not 
configured")\n    result = _post_json(f"{YESCAPTCHA_API_BASE}/createTask", {"clientKey": api_key, "task": task})\n    
if result.get("errorId", 0) != 0:\n        raise CaptchaError(f"YesCaptcha error {result.get(\'errorId\')}: 
{result.get(\'errorDescription\', \'unknown\')}")\n    task_id = result.get("taskId")\n    if not task_id:\n        
raise CaptchaError("YesCaptcha did not return a taskId")\n    return str(task_id)\n\n\ndef _get_task_result(api_key: 
str, task_id: str, *, max_wait_seconds: int = _DEFAULT_MAX_WAIT_SECONDS) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Poll for a task 
result until ready or timed out."""\n    deadline = time.time() + max_wait_seconds\n    while time.time() < 
deadline:\n        result = _post_json(f"{YESCAPTCHA_API_BASE}/getTaskResult", {"clientKey": api_key, "taskId": 
task_id})\n        if result.get("errorId", 0) != 0:\n            raise CaptchaError(f"YesCaptcha error: 
{result.get(\'errorDescription\', \'unknown\')}")\n        if result.get("status") == "ready":\n            solution = 
result.get("solution")\n            if not solution:\n                raise CaptchaError("YesCaptcha returned ready 
status but solution is missing")\n            return solution\n        time.sleep(_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS)\n    raise 
CaptchaError(f"YesCaptcha task timed out after {max_wait_seconds}s (taskId={task_id})")\n\n\ndef solve_funcaptcha(\n   
 api_key: str,\n    public_key: str,\n    *,\n    website_url: str = "https://www.roblox.com",\n    max_wait_seconds: 
int = _DEFAULT_MAX_WAIT_SECONDS,\n) -> str:\n    """Solve a FunCaptcha / Arkose Labs challenge.\n\n    Returns the 
arkose token string to submit with the login form.\n    Raises CaptchaError on failure.\n    """\n    task = {\n       
 "type": "FunCaptchaTaskProxyLess",\n        "websiteURL": website_url,\n        "websitePublicKey": public_key,\n    
}\n    task_id = _create_task(api_key, task)\n    solution = _get_task_result(api_key, task_id, 
max_wait_seconds=max_wait_seconds)\n    token = solution.get("token")\n    if not token:\n        raise 
CaptchaError("FunCaptcha solution is missing the \'token\' field")\n    return token\n\n\ndef solve_recaptcha_v2(\n    
api_key: str,\n    site_key: str,\n    *,\n    website_url: str = "https://www.roblox.com",\n    max_wait_seconds: int 
= _DEFAULT_MAX_WAIT_SECONDS,\n) -> str:\n    """Solve a reCAPTCHA v2 challenge.\n\n    Returns the gRecaptchaResponse 
token string.\n    Raises CaptchaError on failure.\n    """\n    task = {\n        "type": "NoCaptchaTaskProxyless",\n 
       "websiteURL": website_url,\n        "websiteKey": site_key,\n    }\n    task_id = _create_task(api_key, task)\n 
   solution = _get_task_result(api_key, task_id, max_wait_seconds=max_wait_seconds)\n    token = 
solution.get("gRecaptchaResponse")\n    if not token:\n        raise CaptchaError("reCAPTCHA v2 solution is missing 
the \'gRecaptchaResponse\' field")\n    return token\n\n\ndef get_balance(api_key: str) -> float:\n    """Return 
current YesCaptcha account balance. Raises CaptchaError on failure."""\n    if not api_key:\n        raise 
CaptchaError("yescaptcha_key is not configured")\n    result = _post_json(f"{YESCAPTCHA_API_BASE}/getBalance", 
{"clientKey": api_key})\n    if result.get("errorId", 0) != 0:\n        raise CaptchaError(f"YesCaptcha error: 
{result.get(\'errorDescription\', \'unknown\')}")\n    balance = result.get("balance")\n    if balance is None:\n      
  raise CaptchaError("YesCaptcha balance response missing \'balance\' field")\n    return float(balance)\n\n"""CLI 
command handlers for DENG Tool: Rejoin."""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport 
json\nimport os\nimport re\nimport shutil\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nimport tempfile\nfrom datetime import 
datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom . import account_detect, android, db, 
root_access, safe_io\nfrom .banner import print_banner\nfrom .config import (\n    ConfigError,\n    
POST_LAUNCH_ACTION_NONE,\n    POST_LAUNCH_ACTION_OPEN_APP,\n    POST_LAUNCH_ACTION_OPEN_CONFIGURED_LINK,\n    
POST_LAUNCH_ACTIONS,\n    default_config,\n    effective_private_server_url,\n    enabled_package_entries,\n    
enabled_package_names,\n    ensure_app_dirs,\n    load_config,\n    mask_license_key,\n    
normalize_package_detection_hint,\n    package_display_name,\n    package_entry,\n    post_launch_action_label,\n    
MAPPING_STATUSES,\n    safe_config_view,\n    save_config,\n    validate_account_username,\n    validate_config,\n    
validate_license_key,\n    validate_package_detection_hints,\n    validate_package_entries,\n    
validate_package_name,\n    validate_username_source,\n)\nfrom .constants import (\n    CONFIG_PATH,\n    DB_PATH,\n   
 DEFAULT_LICENSE_SERVER_URL,\n    DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE,\n    DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE_HINTS,\n    GITHUB_REMOTE,\n    
LOCK_PATH,\n    LOG_PATH,\n    PID_PATH,\n    PRODUCT_NAME,\n    RAW_INSTALL_URL,\n    TERMUX_BOOT_SCRIPT,\n
