tty is available) 
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n# Termux\'s 
default monospace font is very thin and the previous status\n# colors were unreadable on a cloud-phone screen (user 
feedback: "i cant\n# read shit so thin").  Every status color now starts with the BOLD\n# attribute (\\033[1;<color>m) 
so the foreground text uses the BRIGHT\n# weight glyphs that all Termux monospace fonts have.\n_ANSI_GREEN   = 
"\\033[1;92m"   # bold bright green\n_ANSI_YELLOW  = "\\033[1;93m"   # bold bright yellow\n_ANSI_RED     = 
"\\033[1;91m"   # bold bright red\n_ANSI_CYAN    = "\\033[1;96m"   # bold bright cyan\n_ANSI_BOLD    = "\\033[1m"      
# plain bold (no color)\n_ANSI_DIM     = "\\033[2;37m"   # dim grey (Unknown only ù intentionally 
low-contrast)\n_ANSI_RESET   = "\\033[0m"\n_ANSI_RE      = re.compile(r"\\x1b\\[[0-9;]*m")\n\n\ndef 
_print_dev_license_skipped(use_color: bool) -> None:\n    msg = "Dev Mode: License Check Skipped"\n    if use_color:\n 
       print(f"{_ANSI_YELLOW}{msg}{_ANSI_RESET}")\n    else:\n        print(msg)\n\n\ndef _print_license_ok(use_color: 
bool) -> None:\n    if use_color:\n        print(f"{_ANSI_GREEN}License OK{_ANSI_RESET}")\n    else:\n        
print("OK: License Verified")\n\n\ndef _print_license_err(message: str, use_color: bool) -> None:\n    if use_color:\n 
       print(f"{_ANSI_RED}{message}{_ANSI_RESET}")\n    else:\n        print(message if 
message.upper().startswith("ERROR:") else f"ERROR: {message}")\n\n\ndef _print_missing_license_prompt(use_color: bool) 
-> None:\n    verifying = "[*] Verifying License..."\n    missing = "[!] No license key found."\n    if use_color:\n   
     print(f"{_ANSI_CYAN}{verifying}{_ANSI_RESET}")\n        print(f"{_ANSI_YELLOW}{missing}{_ANSI_RESET}")\n    
else:\n        print(verifying)\n        print(missing)\n\n\ndef _persist_license_status(cfg: dict[str, Any], status: 
str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    from .config import utc_now\n\n    lic = cfg.setdefault("license", {})\n    
lic["last_status"] = status\n    lic["last_check_at"] = utc_now()\n    return save_config(cfg)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 
License cache fast-path \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n#\n# Real-device 
evidence (probe ``p-39924732cd``): on the cloud phone, the\n# remote license check via ``safe_http \u2192 curl`` 
crashes the calling\n# Python process with ``SIGSEGV`` *intermittently* (faulthandler\'s crash\n# log is empty, 
suggesting the segfault is in libc fork/exec or a native\n# extension that bypasses the Python signal trampoline).  
The user has\n# a valid license that was confirmed ``active`` the day before, so making\n# them retry the network call 
on every menu open is both unnecessary AND\n# the exact code path that kills the process.\n#\n# Trust the cached 
``last_status`` for a bounded window (24 h) when it\'s\n# ``active``, so the menu opens immediately and the user can 
run Start\n# without ever touching the segfaulting network code.\n\n_LICENSE_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS = 24 * 60 * 60  # 24 
hours\n# Offline grace: if the cached check was ``active`` within this window AND\n# the live remote check fails 
*transiently* (server_unavailable / crash),\n# treat the license as active so the user is never locked out by a 
single\n# bad request.  Reset to ``not_configured`` only on definitive answers\n# (wrong_device / not_found / revoked 
/ expired / inactive).\n_LICENSE_OFFLINE_GRACE_SECONDS = 30 * 24 * 60 * 60  # 30 days\n\n_LICENSE_TRANSIENT_RESULTS = 
{\n    "server_unavailable",\n    "error",\n    "",  # parser couldn\'t determine anything\n}\n\n\ndef 
_license_cache_age_seconds(lic: dict[str, Any]) -> float | None:\n    """Return seconds since ``last_check_at`` or 
``None`` when unparseable."""\n    if not isinstance(lic, dict):\n        return None\n    raw = 
lic.get("last_check_at")\n    if not raw or not isinstance(raw, str):\n        return None\n    try:\n        norm = 
raw.strip().replace("Z", "+00:00")\n        from datetime import datetime as _dt, timezone as _tz  # noqa: PLC0415\n   
     ts = _dt.fromisoformat(norm)\n        if ts.tzinfo is None:\n            ts = ts.replace(tzinfo=_tz.utc)\n        
return (_dt.now(_tz.utc) - ts).total_seconds()\n    except (TypeError, ValueError, OSError):\n        return 
None\n\n\ndef _license_cache_is_fresh_active(lic: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n    """Return True when the cached license 
check is recent AND active.\n\n    Cached check is considered fresh when:\n        * ``last_status == \'active\'``, 
AND\n        * ``last_check_at`` parses as an ISO-8601 timestamp, AND\n        * the timestamp is within 
:data:`_LICENSE_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS`.\n\n    Failures during parsing return False so we fall back to remote check.\n    
Never raises.\n    """\n    if not isinstance(lic, dict):\n        return False\n    status = 
str(lic.get("last_status") or "").strip().lower()\n    if status != "active":\n        return False\n    age = 
_license_cache_age_seconds(lic)\n    if age is None:\n        return False\n    return 0 <= age <= 
_LICENSE_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n\n\ndef _license_should_offline_grace(lic: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n    """Return True 
when a transient remote failure can fall back to cache.\n\n    Only triggers when the most recent SUCCESSFUL answer 
was ``active``\n    and is within :data:`_LICENSE_OFFLINE_GRACE_SECONDS`.  Treating a\n    transient 
``server_unavailable`` (or subprocess SIGSEGV) as ``active``\n    in that window prevents the public user from being 
locked out by\n    a single bad network request.\n    """\n    status = str((lic or {}).get("last_status") or 
"").strip().lower()\n    if status != "active":\n        return False\n    age = _license_cache_age_seconds(lic)\n    
if age is None:\n        return False\n    return 0 <= age <= _LICENSE_OFFLINE_GRACE_SECONDS\n\n\ndef 
_remote_license_check_isolated(cfg: dict[str, Any], *, timeout: int = 35) -> tuple[str, str]:\n    """Run the remote 
license check inside a child Python process.\n\n    Real-device cause: on Termux/Python 3.13.13, the network code path 
in\n    :func:`check_remote_license_status` segfaults the *parent* process\n    (probe ``p-39924732cd`` showed 
``last_step == \'license_remote_check\'``\n    with rc = -11).  Doing the call in a short-lived child process means\n  
  the worst case is a clean ``"server_unavailable"`` result ù the menu\n    never crashes.\n\n    Returns (result, 
message).  On any subprocess failure (non-zero exit,\n    timeout, signal kill, malformed output), returns\n    
``("server_unavailable", <reason>)``.\n    """\n    import subprocess as _sp  # noqa: PLC0415\n\n    payload = {\n     
   "key": (cfg.setdefault("license", {}).get("key") or "").strip(),\n        "install_id": (cfg.setdefault("license", 
{}).get("install_id") or "").strip(),\n        "device_label": str(cfg.setdefault("license", {}).get("device_label") 
or "")[:80],\n        "server_url": (cfg.setdefault("license", {}).get("server_url") or "").strip(),\n    }\n    # 
Real-device evidence (probe p-09484eaab4): the child Python was\n    # exiting with rc=1 because ``python3 -c "from 
agent.license ..."``\n    # doesn\'t have the parent\'s ``sys.path``.  Pass the agent package\'s\n    # parent 
directory via PYTHONPATH so the child can find it, and add\n    # the same path explicitly inside the code string so 
``DENG_REJOIN_HOME``\n    # overrides work too.  Without this fix every check returned rc=1\n    # and the menu loop 
persisted ``last_status = server_unavailable``,\n    # corrupting the cache permanently.\n    try:\n        from 
pathlib import Path as _Path  # noqa: PLC0415\n        _agent_parent = str(_Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent)\n  
  except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _agent_parent = os.path.expanduser("~/.deng-tool/rejoin")\n    code = (\n 
       "import json, os, sys\\n"\n        f"sys.path.insert(0, {_agent_parent!r})\\n"\n        "_home = 
os.environ.get(\'DENG_REJOIN_HOME\')\\n"\n        "if _home and _home not in sys.path:\\n"\n        "    
sys.path.insert(0, _home)\\n"\n        "try:\\n"\n        "    from agent.license import (\\n"\n        "        
check_remote_license_status, hash_install_id,\\n"\n        "        DEFAULT_LICENSE_SERVER_URL, 
get_public_device_model,\\n"\n        "    )\\n"\n        "    from agent.constants import VERSION\\n"\n        
"except Exception as _imp_exc:\\n"\n        "    sys.stdout.write(json.dumps({\\n"\n        "        \'result\': 
\'server_unavailable\',\\n"\n        "        \'message\': f\'license import error: {_imp_exc}\',\\n"\n        "    
}))\\n"\n        "    sys.exit(0)\\n"\n        "p = json.loads(sys.stdin.read())\\n"\n        "srv = p[\'server_url\'] 
or DEFAULT_LICENSE_SERVER_URL\\n"\n        "try:\\n"\n        "    model = get_public_device_model() or 
\'unknown\'\\n"\n        "except Exception:\\n"\n        "    model = \'unknown\'\\n"\n        "try:\\n"\n        "    
r, m = check_remote_license_status(\\n"\n        "        srv, license_key=p[\'key\'], 
install_id=p[\'install_id\'],\\n"\n        "        device_model=model, app_version=VERSION,\\n"\n        "        
device_label=p[\'device_label\'],\\n"\n        "    )\\n"\n        "except Exception as exc:\\n"\n        "    r, m = 
\'server_unavailable\', f\'check exception: {exc}\'\\n"\n        "sys.stdout.write(json.dumps({\'result\': r, 
\'message\': m}))\\n"\n    )\n    # Pass PYTHONPATH as a belt-and-braces so even subprocess implementations\n    # 
that strip ``-c`` script directory still find ``agent``.\n    env = dict(os.environ)\n    prev_pp = 
env.get("PYTHONPATH", "")\n    env["PYTHONPATH"] = (\n        _agent_parent + (os.pathsep + prev_pp if prev_pp else 
"")\n    )\n    try:\n        proc = _sp.run(\n            [sys.executable, "-c", code],\n            
input=json.dumps(payload).encode("utf-8"),\n            stdout=_sp.PIPE,\n            stderr=_sp.PIPE,\n            
timeout=timeout,\n            check=False,\n            env=env,\n        )\n    except _sp.TimeoutExpired:\n        
return "server_unavailable", "License check subprocess timed out."\n    except OSError as exc:\n        return 
"server_unavailable", f"License check subprocess launch error: {exc}"\n\n    if proc.returncode < 0:\n        # 
Crashed (SIGSEGV etc.) ù DO NOT pollute terminal; treat as a\n        # transient network problem.  Cached license 
keeps the user\n        # running until the next clean check.\n        return "server_unavailable", f"License check 
crashed safely (signal {-proc.returncode})."\n    if proc.returncode != 0:\n        # Capture stderr\'s first line so 
future probes pinpoint *why*\n        # the child failed (ImportError / SyntaxError / etc.).\n        stderr_line = 
(proc.stderr or b"").decode("utf-8", errors="replace").splitlines()\n        hint = stderr_line[0][:80] if stderr_line 
else ""\n        return "server_unavailable", f"License check exited rc={proc.returncode} ({hint})"\n    try:\n        
data = json.loads((proc.stdout or b"").decode("utf-8", errors="replace") or "{}")\n    except json.JSONDecodeError:\n  
      return "server_unavailable", "License check returned invalid JSON."\n    result = str(data.get("result") or 
"server_unavailable").strip().lower()\n    message = str(data.get("message") or "").strip()\n    return result, 
message\n\n\ndef _ensure_install_id_saved(cfg: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    lic = cfg.setdefault("license", 
{})\n    before = lic.get("install_id")\n    sync_install_id_with_config(lic)\n    if lic.get("install_id") != 
before:\n        return save_config(cfg)\n    return cfg\n\n\ndef _remote_license_check_direct(cfg: dict[str, Any]) -> 
tuple[str, str]:\n    """Run the remote license check in-process (legacy path).\n\n    On Termux/Python 3.13.13 this 
code path can segfault the *parent*\n    process (probe ``p-39924732cd`` showed ``rc=-11`` here).  Prefer\n    
:func:`_remote_license_run_check`, which dispatches to subprocess\n    isolation in production.\n    """\n    lic = 
cfg.setdefault("license", {})\n    key = (lic.get("key") or "").strip()\n    if not key:\n        return 
"missing_key", "No license key configured."\n    install_id = sync_install_id_with_config(lic)\n    device = 
get_device_summary()\n    srv = (lic.get("server_url") or "").strip() or DEFAULT_LICENSE_SERVER_URL\n    return 
check_remote_license_status(\n        srv,\n        license_key=key,\n        install_id=install_id,\n        
device_model=device.get("model") or "unknown",\n        app_version=VERSION,\n        
device_label=str(lic.get("device_label") or ""),\n    )\n\n\ndef _should_isolate_license_check() -> bool:\n    
"""Decide whether to run the license check in a child subprocess.\n\n    Real-device evidence (probe 
``p-39924732cd``): on Termux the\n    in-process network code segfaults the menu intermittently.  We\n    isolate by 
default on Termux and on Android in general, and let\n    a power user override via ``DENG_LICENSE_INLINE=1``.\n\n    
Returns False during ``unittest`` so existing tests that mock\n    :func:`_remote_license_run_check` continue to work 
without ever\n    spawning subprocesses (subprocess can\'t see test mocks).\n    """\n    override = 
(os.environ.get("DENG_LICENSE_INLINE") or "").strip().lower()\n    if override in ("1", "true", "yes", "on"):\n        
return False\n    # Tests mock this function and don\'t want subprocess isolation ù\n    # otherwise the child 
wouldn\'t see the mock and would do real HTTP.\n    # Detect by the unittest module being imported AND a 
pytest/unittest\n    # runner currently in sys.argv.\n    if "unittest" in sys.modules and any(\n        "unittest" in 
a or "pytest" in a or "_test_runner" in a for a in sys.argv[:2]\n    ):\n        return False\n    # Default: isolate 
when we can see we\'re on Termux/Android (the\n    # cloud-phone environment where the segfault was observed).  Be\n   
 # conservative on dev machines so behavior matches the old code.\n    if os.environ.get("TERMUX_VERSION"):\n        
return True\n    if os.environ.get("ANDROID_ROOT") or os.environ.get("ANDROID_DATA"):\n        return True\n    return 
False\n\n\ndef _remote_license_run_check(cfg: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[str, str]:\n    """Public entry ù dispatches to 
subprocess-isolated path on Termux.\n\n    Backwards-compatible signature (used by every caller and mocked by\n    
many tests).  Production callers on Termux/Android transparently\n    get SIGSEGV isolation; dev/CI callers get the 
legacy in-process\n    behaviour so unit tests continue to work.\n    """\n    if _should_isolate_license_check():\n   
     return _remote_license_check_isolated(cfg)\n    return _remote_license_check_direct(cfg)\n\n\ndef 
verify_remote_license_noninteractive(cfg: dict[str, Any], *, use_color: bool) -> bool:\n    """Return True when remote 
license check is ``active`` (updates ``last_status``).\n\n    Quiet by design: valid licenses produce NO public output 
so the clean\n    logo + menu appears without "License OK" spam on every startup.\n    """\n    result, msg = 
_remote_license_run_check(cfg)\n    if result == "active":\n        # Silent success ù do not print "License OK" on 
every startup.\n        _persist_license_status(cfg, "active")\n        return True\n    # Cache integrity: see 
_ensure_remote_license_menu_loop for rationale.\n    # Never persist transient results, they would clobber a valid 
cached\n    # ``last_status == "active"`` and lock the user out.\n    if result not in _LICENSE_TRANSIENT_RESULTS:\n   
     try:\n            _persist_license_status(cfg, result)\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            
pass\n    if result == "wrong_device":\n        _print_license_err(WRONG_DEVICE_USER_MESSAGE, use_color)\n    elif 
result == "key_not_redeemed":\n        _print_license_err(msg, use_color)\n    elif result == "missing_key":\n        
_print_license_err("No License Key Found", use_color)\n    else:\n        _print_license_err(f"License Invalid: 
{msg}", use_color)\n    print_beginner_license_gate_help(\n        show_hwid_footer=(result not in ("wrong_device", 
"key_not_redeemed"))\n    )\n    return False\n\n\ndef _report_key_execution_if_public(cfg: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n 
   """Fire-and-forget: report one execution to the license API on public builds.\n\n    Only runs when the build 
channel is a public stable release.\n    main-dev / internal / test builds are silently skipped.\n    Errors are 
swallowed ù this must never interrupt the Start flow.\n    """\n    try:\n        from agent.build_info import 
collected_version_info\n        info = collected_version_info()\n        channel = (info.get("channel") or 
"").strip().lower()\n        version = (info.get("version") or "").strip()\n        # Only public stable builds 
count.\n        _PUBLIC_CHANNELS = {"stable", "release", "public"}\n        _SKIP_CHANNELS = {"main-dev", "dev", 
"internal", "test", "test-latest"}\n        if channel in _SKIP_CHANNELS or channel not in _PUBLIC_CHANNELS:\n         
   return\n        lic = cfg.get("license") or {}\n        raw_key = (lic.get("key") or cfg.get("license_key") or 
"").strip()\n        install_id = (cfg.get("install_id") or "").strip()\n        if not raw_key or not install_id:\n   
         return\n        # Hash the install ID if it looks unhashed (not 64-char hex).\n        import hashlib\n       
 if len(install_id) != 64:\n            install_id = hashlib.sha256(install_id.encode()).hexdigest()\n        import 
threading\n        def _report() -> None:\n            try:\n                from agent.safe_http import post as 
_post\n                _post(\n                    "/api/execute",\n                    {"key": raw_key, 
"install_id_hash": install_id, "version": version, "channel": channel},\n                    timeout=6,\n              
  )\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                pass\n        t = threading.Thread(target=_report, 
daemon=True, name="key-exec-report")\n        t.start()\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n\n\ndef 
_ensure_local_license_menu_loop(cfg: dict[str, Any], args: argparse.Namespace, use_color: bool) -> bool:\n    while 
True:\n        try:\n            cfg = load_config()\n        except ConfigError as exc:\n            
_print_license_err(str(exc), use_color)\n            return False\n        lic = cfg.setdefault("license", {})\n       
 key = (lic.get("key") or "").strip() or (cfg.get("license_key") or "").strip()\n        if not key:\n            if 
not _is_interactive():\n                _print_license_err("No License Key Found", use_color)\n                
print_beginner_license_gate_help()\n                return False\n            print_beginner_menu_license_prompt()\n   
         if not keystore.prompt_and_verify_key():\n                return False\n            
_print_license_ok(use_color)\n            return True\n        ok, msg = keystore.verify_key(key)\n        if ok:\n    
        _print_license_ok(use_color)\n            return True\n        _print_license_err(msg, use_color)\n        if 
not _is_interactive():\n            return False\n        print("\\n1. Enter Different Key\\n2. Exit")\n        _lc = 
safe_io.safe_prompt("Choose [2]: ", default="2")\n        if _lc is None:\n            return False\n        choice = 
_lc.strip() or "2"\n        if choice != "1":\n            return False\n        lic["key"] = ""\n        
cfg["license_key"] = ""\n        cfg = save_config(cfg)\n\n\ndef _ensure_remote_license_menu_loop(cfg: dict[str, Any], 
args: argparse.Namespace, use_color: bool) -> bool:\n    """Gate the menu behind a remote license check. Allows 
bounded retries without recursion."""\n    _MAX_RETRIES = 10\n    attempt = 0\n    while attempt < _MAX_RETRIES:\n     
   attempt += 1\n        # Always reload config fresh to pick up any changes from save_config\n        try:\n          
  cfg = load_config()\n        except ConfigError as exc:\n            _print_license_err(str(exc), use_color)\n       
     return False\n\n        try:\n            cfg = _ensure_install_id_saved(cfg)\n        except Exception:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n            pass  # non-fatal; continue with current cfg\n\n        lic = cfg.setdefault("license", {})\n\n    
    # Cache fast-path: if we successfully checked the license recently,\n        # skip the remote call entirely.  
Real-device evidence shows the\n        # network code path can segfault Python intermittently on Termux\n        # 
(probe p-39924732cd, rc=-11 at STEP:license_remote_check); the\n        # cached result is enough to open the menu 
safely.\n        # Silent: no "License OK" output ù the user\'s clean menu appears directly.\n        if 
_license_cache_is_fresh_active(lic):\n            return True\n\n        key = (lic.get("key") or "").strip()\n\n      
  if not key:\n            if not _is_interactive():\n                _print_license_err("No License Key Found", 
use_color)\n                print_beginner_license_gate_help()\n                return False\n            
_print_missing_license_prompt(use_color)\n            raw = safe_io.safe_prompt("Enter License Key: ")\n            if 
raw is None:\n                return False\n            if not raw:\n                continue\n            try:\n      
          norm = validate_license_key(raw)\n            except ConfigError as exc:\n                
_print_license_err(str(exc), use_color)\n                continue\n            lic["key"] = norm\n            
cfg["license_key"] = norm\n            try:\n                cfg = save_config(cfg)\n            except Exception as 
exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n                _print_license_err(f"Could not save license key: {exc}", use_color)\n            
    continue\n            key = norm\n\n        try:\n            # _remote_license_run_check dispatches to a child 
subprocess\n            # on Termux/Android (probe p-39924732cd: in-process network\n            # code can SIGSEGV 
the parent).  Tests / dev machines keep\n            # the legacy in-process behaviour.\n            result, msg = 
_remote_license_run_check(cfg)\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            
_print_license_err(f"License check failed: {exc}", use_color)\n            result, msg = "error", str(exc)\n\n        
if result == "active":\n            # Silent success on normal startup ù clean menu appears next.\n            try:\n  
              _persist_license_status(cfg, "active")\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                
pass\n            return True\n\n        # Offline grace: a transient failure (server unavailable, child\n        # 
crash) must not lock out a user whose cached license is still\n        # ``active`` and was confirmed within the grace 
window.  Keep the\n        # last_status / last_check_at untouched so the next attempt can\n        # still 
re-verify.\n        if result in _LICENSE_TRANSIENT_RESULTS and _license_should_offline_grace(lic):\n            
return True  # Silent ù cached license still valid, no user action needed\n\n        # Cache integrity: ONLY persist 
*definitive* answers\n        # (active / wrong_device / not_found / revoked / expired / inactive /\n        # 
key_not_redeemed / missing_key).  Persisting a transient\n        # ``server_unavailable`` / ``error`` would overwrite 
a previously\n        # valid ``last_status == "active"``, permanently disabling the\n        # cache fast-path and 
offline grace.  Real-device evidence\n        # (probe p-09484eaab4) showed this bug locking the user out\n        # 
after a single failed subprocess attempt.\n        if result not in _LICENSE_TRANSIENT_RESULTS:\n            try:\n    
            cfg = _persist_license_status(cfg, result)\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                
pass\n\n        if result == "wrong_device":\n            _print_license_err(WRONG_DEVICE_USER_MESSAGE, use_color)\n   
     elif result == "key_not_redeemed":\n            _print_license_err(msg, use_color)\n        else:\n            
_print_license_err(f"License Invalid: {msg}", use_color)\n\n        if not _is_interactive():\n            return 
False\n\n        if result == "wrong_device":\n            print("\\n1. Enter Different Key\\n2. Exit")\n        
else:\n            print("\\n1. Try Another Key\\n2. Exit")\n        _lc2 = safe_io.safe_prompt("Choose [2]: ", 
default="2")\n        if _lc2 is None:\n            return False\n        choice = _lc2.strip() or "2"\n        if 
choice != "1":\n            return False\n        # Clear bad key and retry\n        lic["key"] = ""\n

           pass  # Will be reloaded at top of loop\n    return False\n\n\ndef ensure_menu_can_open(_args:

safe Android package name. Use a format like com.roblox.client.")\n\n\ndef _entry_for_package(package: str, 
current_entries: list[dict[str, Any]], *, app_name: str = "") -> dict[str, Any]:\n    for entry in current_entries:\n  
      if entry["package"] == package:\n            return dict(entry)\n    return package_entry(package, "", True, 
"not_set", app_name=str(app_name or "")[:120])\n\n\ndef _detect_or_prompt_account_username(entry: dict[str, Any], 
config_data: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    updated = dict(entry)\n    if 
validate_account_username(updated.get("account_username", "")):\n        return updated\n    cfg_valid = 
validate_config(config_data) if config_data is not None else None\n    try:\n        result = 
account_detect.detect_account_username(\n            updated["package"],\n            entry=updated,\n            
config=cfg_valid,\n            use_root=True,\n            respect_config_manual=False,\n        )\n    except 
PermissionError as exc:\n        import logging as _logging\n        _logging.getLogger("deng_tool_rejoin").debug(\n   
         "Permission denied during username auto-detect for %s: %s",\n            updated.get("package", "?"),\n       
     exc,\n        )\n        result = None\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        import logging as 
_logging\n        _logging.getLogger("deng_tool_rejoin").debug(\n            "Username auto-detect error for %s: 
%s",\n            updated.get("package", "?"),\n            exc,\n        )\n        result = None\n    if result:\n   
     updated["account_username"] = validate_account_username(result.username)\n        updated["username_source"] = 
validate_username_source(result.source, result.username)\n        return updated\n    updated["username_source"] = 
"not_set"\n    if _is_interactive():\n        print(f"DENG could not safely detect a Roblox username/account name for 
{updated[\'package\']}.")\n        print("This name is only used to make the Start table easy to read.")\n        
manual = _prompt(f"Enter Roblox username/account name for {updated[\'package\']}, or press Enter to skip", 
"").strip()\n        if manual:\n            updated["account_username"] = validate_account_username(manual)\n         
   updated["username_source"] = "manual"\n    return updated\n\n\ndef _try_detect_user_id(entry: dict[str, Any], 
draft: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[int, str]:\n    """Attempt root-assisted user ID detection for one package entry.\n\n  
  Returns (user_id, source_label).  user_id=0 means not found.\n    Never raises ù setup must never crash here.\n    
"""\n    pkg = str(entry.get("package") or "").strip()\n    if not pkg:\n        return 0, "not_found"\n\n    # 
Already configured ù respect it.\n    existing = entry.get("roblox_user_id")\n    if isinstance(existing, int) and 
existing > 0:\n        return existing, "config"\n    if isinstance(existing, str) and existing.isdigit() and 
int(existing) > 0:\n        return int(existing), "config"\n\n    try:\n        cfg_valid = validate_config(draft) if 
draft else None\n        uid = account_detect.detect_roblox_user_id(pkg, entry=entry, config=cfg_valid, 
use_root=True)\n        if uid:\n            return uid, "root_prefs"\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
pass\n\n    # Fallback: try resolving from known username via Presence API.\n    username = 
str(entry.get("account_username") or "").strip()\n    if username:\n        try:\n            from . import 
roblox_presence as _rp\n            uid = _rp.lookup_user_id(username)\n            if uid and uid > 0:\n              
  return uid, "api_resolved"\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            pass\n\n    return 0, 
"not_found"\n\n\ndef _validate_user_id_with_presence(user_id: int) -> str:\n    """Quick non-blocking presence check 
to validate a detected user ID.\n\n    Returns one of: "Validated", "API Unavailable", "Invalid".\n    Never raises.\n 
   """\n    if user_id <= 0:\n        return "Invalid"\n    try:\n        from . import roblox_presence as _rp\n       
 result = _rp.fetch_presence_one(user_id)\n        if result is not None:\n            return "Validated"\n        
return "API Unavailable"\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return "API Unavailable"\n\n\ndef 
_mapping_status_for(\n    uid: int,\n    src: str,\n    entry: dict[str, Any],\n    presence_status: str | None = 
None,\n    is_display_name_only: bool = False,\n) -> str:\n    """Compute the canonical mapping status label for 
display and saving."""\n    if src in ("skipped",):\n        return "Skipped"\n    if src == "manual":\n        return 
"Manual"\n    if src == "config":\n        existing = entry.get("account_mapping_status") or ""\n        return 
existing if existing in MAPPING_STATUSES else "Validated"\n    if uid <= 0:\n        if 
str(entry.get("account_username") or "").strip():\n            return "Needs Confirmation"\n        return "Not 
Mapped"\n    if presence_status == "Validated":\n        return "Validated"\n    if presence_status == "API 
Unavailable":\n        return "Detected"\n    if presence_status == "Invalid":\n        return "Invalid"\n    if 
is_display_name_only:\n        return "Needs Confirmation"\n    return "Detected"\n\n\ndef 
_run_account_mapping_table(\n    entries: list[dict[str, Any]],\n    draft: dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    
show_root_message: bool = True,\n) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    """Show account mapping table for the given package 
entries and let user edit/confirm.\n\n    Shows: # | Package | Username | User ID | Source | Status\n    Allows: 
A=accept all, <number>=edit that entry, B=back (cancel mapping only).\n    Returns entries updated with any 
detected/confirmed roblox_user_id values\n    and account_mapping_source / account_mapping_status / 
account_mapping_updated_at.\n    Never blocks Start ù missing mapping is just silently skipped.\n    """\n    if not 
entries:\n        return entries\n\n    # --- Show one-time root availability message ---\n    if show_root_message 
and _is_interactive():\n        if not root_access.has_root():\n            print()\n            print(\n              
  "  Root access was not available, so DENG Tool could not inspect"\n                " protected Roblox app data."\n   
         )\n            print(\n                "  Username/User ID detection will rely on public data and"\n          
      " manual entry instead."\n            )\n            print(\n                "  Root not required for Start ù 
only improves setup detection."\n            )\n\n    # --- Run detection for entries that don\'t have a user_id yet 
---\n    detected: list[tuple[int, str]] = []\n    for entry in entries:\n        uid, src = 
_try_detect_user_id(entry, draft)\n        detected.append((uid, src))\n\n    # --- Run presence validation ---\n    
presence_statuses: list[str] = []\n    for uid, src in detected:\n        if src == "config":\n            existing = 
str(entries[len(presence_statuses)].get("account_mapping_status") or "")\n            
presence_statuses.append(existing if existing in MAPPING_STATUSES else "Validated")\n            continue\n        if 
uid > 0:\n            presence_statuses.append(_validate_user_id_with_presence(uid))\n        else:\n            
presence_statuses.append("")\n\n    def _row_status(i: int, entry: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n        uid, src = 
detected[i]\n        ps = presence_statuses[i]\n        return _mapping_status_for(uid, src, entry, 
presence_status=ps)\n\n    def _print_mapping_table() -> None:\n        print()\n        print("Account Mapping")\n    
    print("-" * 82)\n        fmt = "  {:<3} {:<26} {:<16} {:<12} {:<14} {}"\n        print(fmt.format("#", "Package", 
"Username", "User ID", "Source", "Status"))\n        print("  " + "-" * 80)\n        for i, entry in 
enumerate(entries):\n            uid, src = detected[i]\n            pkg = str(entry.get("package") or "")\n           
 pkg_short = (pkg[:24] + "..") if len(pkg) > 26 else pkg\n            username = _package_username_display(entry)\n    
        uid_disp = str(uid) if uid > 0 else "-"\n            src_disp = (src[:12] + "..") if len(src) > 14 else src\n  
          status = _row_status(i, entry)\n            print(fmt.format(i + 1, pkg_short, username[:16], uid_disp, 
src_disp, status))\n        print("-" * 82)\n        print("  A. Accept all  |  1-N. Edit entry  |  B. Back")\n        
print()\n\n    if not _is_interactive():\n        # Non-interactive: apply detected mappings silently with full 
metadata.\n        return _apply_mapping_to_entries(entries, detected, presence_statuses)\n\n    while True:\n        
_print_mapping_table()\n        try:\n            raw = safe_io.safe_prompt("Choose [A]: ", 
default="a").strip().lower() or "a"\n        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):\n            print()\n            
break\n\n        if raw in ("a", ""):\n            break\n        if raw in ("b", "back", "0"):\n            return 
list(entries)\n\n        if raw.isdigit():\n            idx = int(raw) - 1\n            if not (0 <= idx < 
len(entries)):\n                print(f"  Invalid number. Enter 1-{len(entries)}, A, or B.")\n                
continue\n            entry = dict(entries[idx])\n            uid_cur, _ = detected[idx]\n            pkg = 
str(entry.get("package") or "")\n            print(f"\\n  Editing: {pkg}")\n            cur_name = 
str(entry.get("account_username") or "").strip()\n            if cur_name:\n                print(f"  Current 
username: {cur_name}")\n            if uid_cur > 0:\n                print(f"  Current user ID:  {uid_cur}")\n         
   print("  Enter Roblox username or numeric user ID (blank to skip):")\n            try:\n                inp = 
input("  > ").strip()\n            except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):\n                print()\n                
continue\n            if not inp:\n                detected[idx] = (0, "skipped")\n                
presence_statuses[idx] = ""\n                continue\n            if inp.isdigit() and int(inp) > 0:\n                
new_uid = int(inp)\n                entry["roblox_user_id"] = new_uid\n                detected[idx] = (new_uid, 
"manual")\n                ps = _validate_user_id_with_presence(new_uid)\n                presence_statuses[idx] = 
ps\n                print(f"  Set user_id = {new_uid}  [{ps}]")\n            else:\n                try:\n             
       from . import roblox_presence as _rp\n                    resolved = _rp.lookup_user_id(inp)\n                
except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                    resolved = None\n                try:\n                    
entry["account_username"] = validate_account_username(inp)\n                    entry["username_source"] = 
validate_username_source("manual", inp)\n                except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                    pass\n 
               if resolved and int(resolved) > 0:\n                    entry["roblox_user_id"] = int(resolved)\n

  e = dict(entry)\n        uid, src = detected[i]\n        ps = presence_statuses[i] if i < len(presence_statuses)

"deeplink", "web_url"}:\n            print("Choose 1, 2, 3, app, deeplink, or web_url.")\n            continue\n

         print("Deep link mode needs a roblox:// URL.")\n                continue\n            if mode == "web_url"

    if result.warning:\n                    print(f"Warning: {result.warning}")\n                return mode, url\n

mode\n        print("Choose 1, 2, or 3.")\n\n\ndef _prompt_launch_url(current_url: str, launch_mode: str) -> str:\n    
if launch_mode == "app":\n        print("App-only mode does not need a URL. DENG will open Roblox normally.")\n        
return ""\n    print()\n    print("Paste a Roblox link, or leave blank to skip.")\n    if launch_mode == "deeplink":\n 
       print("Example: roblox://experiences/start?placeId=123")\n    else:\n        print("Example: 
https://www.roblox.com/games/123/name?privateServerLinkCode=...")\n    while True:\n        value = _prompt("Launch 
URL (blank to skip)", current_url).strip()\n        if not value:\n            print("Skipped. No launch URL set.")\n  
          return ""\n        try:\n            result = validate_launch_url(value, launch_mode, 
allow_uncertain=True)\n            if result.warning:\n                print(f"Note: {result.warning}")\n

        print("Enter a valid URL or leave blank to skip.")\n\n\ndef _setup_launch_link(draft: dict[str, Any]) -> 
None:\n    """Single-prompt URL setup ù no mode toggle.\n\n    The user pastes ONE Private Server URL.  We accept any 
of:\n\n      * ``https://www.roblox.com/share?code=X&type=Server``    (new share link)\n      * 
``https://www.roblox.com/share-links?code=X&type=Server``\n      * 
``https://www.roblox.com/games/<id>/<name>?privateServerLinkCode=X``  (legacy)\n      * 
``roblox://navigation/share_links?code=X&type=Server``  (deep link)\n      * 
``roblox://placeId=<id>&privateServerLinkCode=X``       (legacy deep link)\n      * blank ù disable URL-based join, 
app-only mode\n\n    Internally the URL is converted to its ``roblox://`` deep-link form\n    at launch time (see 
``url_utils.to_roblox_deep_link``); the user\n    never has to know about modes.  We pick the storage 
``launch_mode``\n    based on the URL scheme so existing config plumbing keeps working.\n    """\n    print()\n    
print("Private Server URL")\n    print("Paste a Roblox Private Server URL (or leave blank to skip).")\n    
print("Examples:")\n    print("  https://www.roblox.com/share?code=XXXX&type=Server")\n    print("  
https://www.roblox.com/games/123/Game?privateServerLinkCode=YYY")\n    print("  
roblox://navigation/share_links?code=XXXX&type=Server")\n    current = str(draft.get("launch_url") or "")\n    value = 
_prompt("Private Server URL (blank to skip)", current).strip()\n\n    if not value:\n        draft["launch_mode"] = 
"app"\n        draft["launch_url"] = ""\n        print("Skipped. Clones will open the Roblox app without auto-joining 
a server.")\n        return\n\n    # Pick storage launch_mode by scheme; let validate_launch_url do the\n    # final 
sanity check.  We accept the URL on any validation warning\n    # (allow_uncertain=True) so the user isn\'t blocked by 
an over-strict\n    # path allow-list ù at launch time the deep-link converter handles\n    # any unmapped path.\n    
scheme = value.split(":", 1)[0].lower() if ":" in value else ""\n    mode = "deeplink" if scheme == "roblox" else 
"web_url"\n    try:\n        result = validate_launch_url(value, mode, allow_uncertain=True)\n        if 
result.warning:\n            print(f"Note: {result.warning}")\n    except UrlValidationError as exc:\n        
print(f"That URL cannot be used: {exc}")\n        print("Keeping previous launch link settings.")\n        return\n    
draft["launch_mode"] = mode\n    draft["launch_url"] = value\n    print("Saved. At launch, this URL will be sent to 
Roblox as a deep link so")\n    print("each clone joins the private server directly.")\n\n\ndef _setup_webhook(draft:

_package_menu_detect_refresh(draft: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Re-run username detection for each 
package; optionally persist results."""\n    print()\n    print("Detect / Refresh Usernames")\n    entries = 
validate_package_entries(\n        draft.get("roblox_packages") or [package_entry(DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE, "", True, 
"not_set")]\n    )\n    cfg = validate_config(draft)\n    settings = cfg.get("account_detection") or {}\n    if not 
settings.get("enabled", True):\n        print("Account detection is disabled.")\n        input("\\nPress Enter to 
continue...")\n        return draft\n\n    pairs = account_detect.detect_account_usernames_for_packages(\n        
[dict(e) for e in entries],\n        config=cfg,\n        use_root=bool(settings.get("use_root", True)),\n        
respect_config_manual=False,\n    )\n    new_entries: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n    print()\n    print(f"  
{\'Package\':<40} {\'Old\':<16} {\'New\':<16} {\'Source\':<18} {\'Status\':<12}")\n    print(f"  {\'-\'*40} {\'-\'*16} 
{\'-\'*16} {\'-\'*18} {\'-\'*12}")\n    changed = False\n    for idx, prior in enumerate(entries):\n        
base_entry, res = pairs[idx]\n        pkg = str(base_entry.get("package") or "")\n        old_u = 
validate_account_username(prior.get("account_username", "")) or "Unknown"\n        if res:\n            new_u = 
validate_account_username(res.username) or "Unknown"\n            src = res.source\n            merged = dict(prior)\n 
           merged["account_username"] = validate_account_username(res.username)\n            merged["username_source"] 
= validate_username_source(res.source, res.username)\n            if old_u != new_u:\n                status = 
"updated"\n                changed = True\n            else:\n                status = "unchanged"\n            
new_entries.append(merged)\n        else:\n            new_u = old_u\n            src = "ù"\n            status = "not 
found"\n            new_entries.append(dict(prior))\n        print(f"  {pkg:<40} {str(old_u)[:16]:<16} 
{str(new_u)[:16]:<16} {str(src)[:18]:<18} {status:<12}")\n\n    if new_entries:\n        draft["roblox_packages"] = 
new_entries\n        active = enabled_package_entries(draft)\n        if active:\n            draft["roblox_package"] 
= active[0]["package"]\n        draft["selected_package_mode"] = "multiple" if len(active) > 1 else "single"\n        
if changed:\n            draft = save_config(draft)\n            print("\\nSaved updated usernames to config.")\n      
  else:\n            print("\\nNo username changes to save.")\n    input("\\nPress Enter to continue...")\n    return 
draft\n\n\ndef _package_menu_set_username(draft: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Manually set

_package_menu_refresh_mapping(draft: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Refresh Account Mapping: re-run root 
detection, username resolution, and Presence validation.\n\n    Only available in Package Setup menu. NOT called from 
Start or supervisor.\n    """\n    print()\n    print("Refresh Account Mapping")\n    print()\n    entries = 
validate_package_entries(\n        draft.get("roblox_packages") or [package_entry(DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE, "", True, 
"not_set")]\n    )\n    enabled_entries = [e for e in entries if e.get("enabled", True)]\n    if not 
enabled_entries:\n        print("No packages configured.")\n        safe_io.press_enter()\n        return draft\n\n    
print(f"Re-running detection for {len(enabled_entries)} package(s)...")\n    root_access.clear_cache()\n\n    # Reset 
detected entries so detection runs fresh (keep existing username but clear user_id source)\n    fresh_entries: 
list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n    for entry in enabled_entries:\n        e = dict(entry)\n        # Keep username, clear 
user_id so detection re-runs\n        e.pop("account_mapping_status", None)\n        e.pop("account_mapping_source", 
None)\n        e.pop("account_mapping_updated_at", None)\n        # Also attempt username re-detection if none set\n   
     if not str(e.get("account_username") or "").strip():\n            try:\n                det = 
account_detect.detect_account_username(\n                    str(e.get("package") or ""),\n                    
entry=e,\n                    config=None,\n                    use_root=True,\n                )\n                if 
det and det.username:\n                    e["account_username"] = validate_account_username(det.username)\n           
         e["username_source"] = validate_username_source(det.source, det.username)\n            except Exception:  # 
noqa: BLE001\n                pass\n        fresh_entries.append(e)\n\n    refreshed = 
_run_account_mapping_table(fresh_entries, draft, show_root_message=True)\n    if not refreshed:\n        
print("Refresh cancelled.")\n        return draft\n\n    # Merge back into all entries (including disabled ones)\n    
refreshed_by_pkg = {e["package"]: e for e in refreshed}\n    merged = [refreshed_by_pkg.get(e["package"], e) for e in 
entries]\n    draft["roblox_packages"] = merged\n    draft = save_config(draft)\n    print("Account mapping refreshed 
and saved.")\n    safe_io.press_enter()\n    return draft\n\n\ndef _package_menu_auto_detect(draft: dict[str, Any]) ->

Orientation\n===============================================\n\nIf your Termux terminal or screen is sideways after a 
layout operation:\n\nMethod 1 ù Rotate the phone back manually:\n  Rotate your physical device to portrait and lock 
portrait mode in Settings.\n\nMethod 2 ù Android Settings:\n  Settings \u2192 Display \u2192 Auto-rotate \u2192 turn 
OFF, then set Portrait.\n\nMethod 3 ù Quick Settings tile:\n  Swipe down the notification shade and tap the 
Auto-Rotate toggle.\n\nMethod 4 ù ADB (from a PC):\n  adb shell settings put system user_rotation 0\n\nMethod 5 ù 
Inside Termux:\n  settings put system user_rotation 0\n\nImportant:\n  DENG Tool: Rejoin never issues global rotation 
commands.\n  If Termux appears sideways, the likely cause is App Cloner applying\n  a window position that forces 
landscape mode on your device.\n  The window layout only writes to the Roblox app clone preferences,\n  NOT to Termux 
or system UI.\n\nAfter restoring orientation:\n  1. Restart Termux.\n  2. Run: deng-rejoin\n  3. If the issue recurs, 
disable layout in config (auto_resize_enabled = false).\n""".strip()\n\n\ndef _packages_from_cfg(cfg: dict[str, Any] | 
None) -> list[str]:\n    if not cfg:\n        return []\n    try:\n        from .config import 
enabled_package_entries\n        return [e["package"] for e in enabled_package_entries(cfg)]\n    except Exception:  # 
noqa: BLE001\n        return []\n\n\ndef _cmd_doctor_layout(cfg: dict[str, Any] | None, use_color: bool) -> int:\n    
"""Internal diagnostic: compute, validate and apply window block layout.\n\n    Hidden from public menu.  Concise 
terminal output, full details in log.\n    """\n    from .window_layout import (\n        calculate_split_layout,\n    
    detect_display_info,\n        validate_layout_rects,\n        OUTER_MARGIN,\n        TERMUX_LOG_FRACTION,\n    )\n 
   from .window_apply import apply_window_layout\n\n    print("Layout Diagnostic")\n    print()\n\n    disp = 
detect_display_info()\n    print(f"  Display: {disp.width}x{disp.height} px  density={disp.density}")\n\n    left_end 
= round(disp.width * TERMUX_LOG_FRACTION)\n    pane_x0 = left_end + OUTER_MARGIN\n    pane_y0 = OUTER_MARGIN\n    
pane_x1 = disp.width - OUTER_MARGIN\n    pane_y1 = disp.height - OUTER_MARGIN\n\n    packages = 
_packages_from_cfg(cfg)\n    if not packages:\n        print("  No packages configured.  Using example packages for 
preview.")\n        packages = ["com.roblox.client", "com.roblox.client2"]\n\n    rects = 
calculate_split_layout(packages, disp.width, disp.height)\n    print(f"  Packages: {len(rects)}")\n    for i, r in 
enumerate(rects, 1):\n        w, h = r.win_w, r.win_h\n        ratio = (w / h) if h else 0\n        print(f"  [{i}] 
{r.package[:36]}  {w}x{h}  ratio={ratio:.2f}")\n\n    errors = validate_layout_rects(rects, pane_x0, pane_y0, pane_x1, 
pane_y1)\n    if errors:\n        print(f"  Validation: FAIL ({len(errors)} issue(s) ù see log)")\n        for e in 
errors[:3]:\n            print(f"    - {e}")\n        return 1\n    print("  Validation: PASS (landscape, no-touch, 
no-overlap, in-pane)")\n\n    # Apply (only if we have a config)\n    if cfg:\n        results = 
apply_window_layout(rects, verify_after=True, retries=1)\n        applied = sum(1 for r in results if r.final_ok)\n    
    print(f"  Apply: {applied}/{len(results)} package(s) verified")\n        for r in results:\n            mark = 
"OK" if r.final_ok else "WARN"\n            print(f"    [{mark}] {r.package[:36]}: {r.detail}")\n\n    return 
0\n\n\ndef _cmd_doctor_root_state(cfg: dict[str, Any] | None) -> int:\n    """Internal diagnostic: show 
process/task/window evidence for each package."""\n    print("Root State Diagnostic")\n    print()\n\n    root_info = 
android.detect_root()\n    print(f"  Root: {\'available\' if root_info.available else \'unavailable\'}"\n          f" 
({root_info.tool or \'none\'})")\n\n    packages = _packages_from_cfg(cfg)\n    if not packages:\n        print("  No 
packages configured.")\n        return 0\n\n    for pkg in packages:\n        evidence = 
android.get_package_alive_evidence(pkg)\n        fg = android.current_foreground_package()\n        is_fg = (fg == 
pkg)\n        if evidence["alive"]:\n            inferred = "Online / Background"\n        else:\n            inferred 
= "Offline"\n        print(\n            f"  {pkg[:36]:<36}  "\n            f"proc={evidence[\'running\']} 
root_proc={evidence[\'root_running\']} "\n            f"task={evidence[\'task\']} win={evidence[\'window\']} "\n       
     f"fg={is_fg}  -> {inferred}"\n        )\n\n    return 0\n\n\ndef cmd_doctor(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\n   
 """Doctor command (internal).  Public users normally do NOT see this output.\n\n    Subcommands (hidden from public 
menu):\n      deng-rejoin doctor layout       ù test landscape block layout\n      deng-rejoin doctor root-state   ù 
show process/task/window evidence\n      deng-rejoin doctor reset        ù print sideways-screen recovery steps\n      
deng-rejoin --layout-test       ù same as `doctor layout`\n      deng-rejoin --root-state        ù same as `doctor 
root-state`\n    """\n    use_color = not args.no_color\n\n    # Silence internal loggers in case this is called 
outside main()\n    try:\n        from .logger import silence_public_loggers\n        silence_public_loggers()\n    
except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n\n    # Subcommand routing\n    if getattr(args, "layout_test", 
False):\n        cfg = None\n        try:\n            cfg = load_config()\n        except Exception:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n            pass\n        return _cmd_doctor_layout(cfg, use_color)\n\n    if getattr(args, "root_state", 
False):\n        cfg = None\n        try:\n            cfg = load_config()\n        except Exception:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n            pass\n        return _cmd_doctor_root_state(cfg)\n\n    if getattr(args, "layout_reset", False):\n 
       print(_LAYOUT_RESET_INSTRUCTIONS)\n        return 0\n\n    # Default doctor: show standard health check\n    
print_banner(use_color=use_color)\n    cfg = None\n    try:\n        cfg = load_config()\n        root_info = 
android.detect_root()\n        cfg["root_available"] = root_info.available\n        cfg["android_release"] = 
get_android_release()\n        cfg["android_sdk"] = get_android_sdk()\n        cfg["download_dir"] = 
detect_public_download_dir()\n        save_config(cfg)\n    except ConfigError:\n        pass\n    items = 
run_doctor(cfg)\n    print_doctor(items)\n    return 1 if any(item.status == "FAIL" for item in items) else 0\n\n\ndef 
_print_latest(label: str, row: dict[str, Any] | None) -> None:\n    if not row:\n        print(f"  {label}: none")\n   
     return\n    ts = row.get("ts", "unknown time")\n    if "status" in row:\n        print(f"  {label}: 
{row.get(\'status\')} at {ts}")\n        return\n    if "success" in row:\n        result = "success" if 
row.get("success") else "failed"\n        package = row.get("package", "unknown package")\n        mode = 
_launch_mode_label(str(row.get("launch_mode", "")))\n        url = _safe_url_label(str(row.get("masked_launch_url") or 
""))\n        print(f"  {label}: {result} at {ts}")\n        print(f"    Package: {package}")\n        print(f"    
Launch mode: {mode}")\n        if url != "Not set":\n            print(f"    URL: {url}")\n        if 
row.get("error"):\n            print(f"    Error: {mask_urls_in_text(str(row.get(\'error\')))}")\n        return\n    
message = row.get("message") or row.get("type") or "event"\n    print(f"  {label}: {mask_urls_in_text(str(message))} 
at {ts}")\n\n\ndef cmd_status(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\n    print_banner(use_color=not args.no_color)\n

_account_username_for_table(entry: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    """Return the display username for a Start table row ù 
shows \'Unknown\' if not set."""\n    return validate_account_username(entry.get("account_username", "")) or 
"Unknown"\n\n\ndef _set_all_phase_keep_failed(\n    phase: dict[str, str],\n    new_label: str,\n    entries: 
list[dict[str, Any]],\n    *,\n    note: str = "",   # accepted for callsite symmetry; unused here\n) -> None:\n    
"""Mutate ``phase`` so every package that isn\'t currently in a\n    "terminal" state (``Failed`` / ``Closed``) gets 
``new_label``.\n\n    Used by the Start dashboard to advance the phase column without\n    clobbering rows that 
already errored out.  The ``note`` argument is\n    ignored (the caller passes it through to its own renderer); we\n   
 accept it so the call sites read symmetrically.\n    """\n    del note   # accepted for callsite symmetry only\n    
terminal = {"Failed", "Closed", "Offline"}\n    for entry in entries:\n        pkg = entry["package"]\n        if 
phase.get(pkg) in terminal:\n            continue\n        phase[pkg] = new_label\n\n\ndef _visible_len(s: str) -> 
int:\n    """Return the printable width of a string, stripping ANSI escape codes."""\n    return len(_ANSI_RE.sub("", 
s))\n\n\ndef _clear_terminal(*, clear_scrollback: bool = False) -> None:\n    """Clear the visible terminal/dashboard. 
Compatible with Termux and Unix.\n\n    Uses ANSI escape codes on non-Windows to avoid the fork/exec path that\n    
``os.system("clear")`` would take.  On Termux/Python 3.13 that fork is a\n    known source of SIGSEGV ù especially 
while background threads are live.\n    ANSI is instant and fork-free.\n    """\n    try:\n        if os.name == 
"nt":\n            os.system("cls")  # Windows only ù no Termux risk here\n        else:\n            import sys as 
_sys\n            _sys.stdout.write("\\033[2J\\033[3J\\033[H" if clear_scrollback else "\\033[2J\\033[H")\n            
_sys.stdout.flush()\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n\n\ndef _colorize_status(status: str, *, 
use_color: bool = True) -> str:\n    """Wrap a status string in the appropriate ANSI color code."""\n    if not 
use_color:\n        return status\n    color = {\n        "Started":      _ANSI_GREEN,\n        "Online":            
_ANSI_GREEN,\n        "Lobby":             _ANSI_GREEN,    # app open at home\n        "In Server":         
_ANSI_GREEN,    # strong evidence: experience loaded\n        ("Join " + "Unconfirmed"):  _ANSI_YELLOW,   # legacy 
alias\n        "Ready":             _ANSI_YELLOW,\n        "Starting":          _ANSI_YELLOW,\n        "Launching":    
     _ANSI_YELLOW,\n        "Launched":          _ANSI_GREEN,    # Roblox process up, no URL yet\n        
"Disconnected":      _ANSI_RED,      # Roblox error code detected\n        "No Heartbeat":      _ANSI_RED,      # 
running but not playing normally\n        ("Join" + "ing"):    _ANSI_CYAN,     # legacy alias\n        "Join Failed":  
     _ANSI_RED,\n        # \u2500\u2500 Prep-phase labels (visible while Start prepares each clone) \u2500\u2500\n     
   "Preparing":    _ANSI_CYAN,\n        "Clear Cache":  _ANSI_CYAN,\n        "Boosting":     _ANSI_CYAN,   # legacy 
alias\n        "Clearing":     _ANSI_CYAN,   # legacy alias\n        "Layout":       _ANSI_CYAN,   # internal only 
(not shown in public UI)\n        "Docking":      _ANSI_CYAN,   # internal only\n        "Waiting":      _ANSI_YELLOW, 
# internal only\n        "Resizing":     _ANSI_CYAN,\n        "Optimizing":   _ANSI_CYAN,\n        "Reconnecting": 
_ANSI_CYAN,\n        "Cleared":      _ANSI_GREEN,\n        "Low Applied":  _ANSI_GREEN,\n        "Skipped":      
_ANSI_YELLOW,\n        "Partial":      _ANSI_YELLOW,\n        "Failed":       _ANSI_RED,\n        "Dead":         
_ANSI_RED,\n        "Offline":      _ANSI_RED,\n        "Closed":       _ANSI_RED,\n        "Background":   
_ANSI_YELLOW,\n        "Warning":      _ANSI_YELLOW,\n        "Unknown":      _ANSI_DIM,\n        "Heartbeat OK":      
    _ANSI_GREEN,\n        "Launch command sent":   _ANSI_GREEN,\n    }.get(status, "")\n    return 
f"{color}{status}{_ANSI_RESET}" if color else status\n\n\ndef build_start_table(rows: list[tuple], *, use_color: bool 
= False) -> str:\n    """Build the public start summary table: #, Package, Username, State, Runtime.\n\n    Rows may 
be 4-tuples (idx, pkg, username, state) for backward compatibility\n    or 5-tuples (idx, pkg, username, state, 
runtime).  4-tuple rows show an\n    empty Runtime cell.\n\n    With ``use_color=True`` every cell is rendered in bold 
so the table\n    is readable on the Termux default monospace font (which renders the\n    regular weight as a 
hairline).  Status cells additionally carry\n    their colour code from :func:`_colorize_status`; the rest of the\n    
cells get the plain ``BOLD`` escape only.\n    """\n    headers = ("#", "Package", "Username", "State", "Runtime")\n   
 str_rows = [\n        (\n            str(r[0]), str(r[1]), str(r[2]), str(r[3]),\n            str(r[4]) if len(r) > 4 
else "",\n        )\n        for r in rows\n    ]\n\n    widths = [\n        max(len(headers[i]), 
max((_visible_len(r[i]) for r in str_rows), default=0))\n        for i in range(5)\n    ]\n\n    def _bold(text: str) 
-> str:\n        if not use_color or not text:\n            return text\n        return 
f"{_ANSI_BOLD}{text}{_ANSI_RESET}"\n\n    colored_rows = [\n        (\n            _bold(r[0]),\n            
_bold(r[1]),\n            _bold(r[2]),\n            _colorize_status(r[3], use_color=use_color),\n            
_bold(r[4]),\n        )\n        for r in str_rows\n    ]\n\n    def _cell(s: str, w: int, raw: str | None = None) -> 
str:\n        pad = w - _visible_len(raw if raw is not None else s)\n        return f" {s}{\' \' * max(0, pad)} "\n\n  
  def _hline(left: str, mid: str, right: str) -> str:\n        return left + mid.join("\u2500" * (w + 2) for w in 
widths) + right\n\n    def _header_row(cells: tuple[str, ...]) -> str:\n        return "\u2502" + "\u2502".join(\n     
       _cell(_bold(str(cells[i])), widths[i], str(cells[i]))\n            for i in range(len(widths))\n        ) + 
"\u2502"\n\n    def _data_row(colored: tuple[str, ...], raw: tuple[str, ...]) -> str:\n        parts = 
[_cell(str(colored[i]), widths[i], str(raw[i])) for i in range(len(widths))]\n        return "\u2502" + 
"\u2502".join(parts) + "\u2502"\n\n    lines = [\n        _hline("\u250c", "\u252c", "\u2510"),\n        
_header_row(headers),\n        _hline("\u251c", "\u253c", "\u2524"),\n        *(_data_row(colored_rows[i], 
str_rows[i]) for i in range(len(colored_rows))),\n        _hline("\u2514", "\u2534", "\u2518"),\n    ]\n    return 
"\\n".join(lines)\n\n\n_FINAL_SUMMARY_ORDER: tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (\n    ("online", "online."),\n    ("no 
heartbeat", "no heartbeat (recovering)."),\n    ("reconnecting", "reconnecting."),\n    ("launching", "launching."),\n 
   ("preparing", "preparing."),\n    ("optimizing", "optimizing."),\n    ("in background", "in background."),\n    
("warning", "with warnings."),\n    ("failed", "failed."),\n    ("dead", "dead (recovering)."),\n    ("offline", 
"offline."),\n    ("unknown", "unknown."),\n)\n\n_STATE_TO_SUMMARY: dict[str, str] = {\n    "Online":            
"online",\n    "Lobby":             "online",         # healthy at home screen\n    "In Server":         "online",     
    # confirmed in target server\n    ("Join " + "Unconfirmed"):  "launching",\n    "No Heartbeat":      "no 
heartbeat",   # was in game, heartbeat stalled\n    ("Join" + "ing"):    "launching",\n    "Reconnecting":      
"reconnecting",\n    "Launching":         "launching",\n    "Failed":            "failed",\n    "Join Failed":       
"failed",\n    "Dead":              "dead",           # process confirmed gone\n    "Offline":           "offline",\n  
  "Closed":            "offline",\n    "Warning":           "warning",\n    "Background":        "in background",\n    
"Unknown":           "unknown",\n    "Preparing":         "preparing",\n    "Optimizing":        
"optimizing",\n}\n\n\ndef _final_summary_line(count: int, tail: str) -> str:\n    word = "package" if count == 1 else 
"packages"\n    return f"{count} {word} {tail}"\n\n\ndef build_final_summary(entries: list[Any], results: dict[str, 
str]) -> str:\n    """Short multi-line summary tallying packages by public state."""\n    tallies: dict[str, int] = 
{}\n    for e in entries:\n        pkg = e["package"] if isinstance(e, dict) else str(e)\n        raw = 
str(results.get(pkg, "Unknown"))\n        bucket = _STATE_TO_SUMMARY.get(raw, "unknown")\n        tallies[bucket] = 
tallies.get(bucket, 0) + 1\n    lines = ["Final:"]\n    any_line = False\n    for key, tail in _FINAL_SUMMARY_ORDER:\n 
       n = tallies.get(key, 0)\n        if n:\n            lines.append(_final_summary_line(n, tail))\n            
any_line = True\n    if not any_line:\n        lines.append("0 packages started.")\n    return 
"\\n".join(lines)\n\n\ndef build_start_verbose_details(rows: list[dict[str, str]], *, use_color: bool = False) -> 
str:\n    """Per-package cache / graphics / launch detail for ``--verbose`` / ``--debug`` / log DEBUG only."""\n    if 
not rows:\n        return ""\n    lines = ["Details (verbose / debug):"]\n    for row in rows:\n        pkg = 
row.get("package", "")\n        lines.append(\n            f"  {pkg}: cache={row.get(\'cache\', \'\')}; 
graphics={row.get(\'graphics\', \'\')}; "\n            f"launch={row.get(\'launch_detail\', \'\')}"\n        )\n    
block = "\\n".join(lines)\n    if use_color:\n        return f"{_ANSI_DIM}{block}{_ANSI_RESET}"\n    return 
block\n\n\ndef _progress_line(index: int, total: int, entry: dict[str, Any], message: str) -> str:\n    username = 
validate_account_username(entry.get("account_username", "")) or entry["package"]\n    return f"[{index}/{total}] 
{username}: {message}"\n\n\ndef _prepare_automatic_layout(\n    cfg: dict[str, Any], entries: list[dict[str, Any]]\n) 
-> tuple[dict[str, Any], str]:\n    """Pre-launch layout pipeline.\n\n    Steps (all silent ù public Start UI never 
sees output here):\n      1. Compute landscape-block layout for the selected packages.\n      2. Run layout-key 
discovery so the writer knows every key alias the\n         real App Cloner build uses (incl. ``Set window position``, 
auto-DPI\n         landscape, freeform, etc.).\n      3. Write XML for each package with all known aliases + 
Set-enable flags.\n      4. Force-stop the selected packages so the next launch re-reads prefs.\n\n    Verification 
and direct-resize happen post-launch in\n    :func:`_verify_layout_post_launch`.\n    """\n    import logging as 
_logging\n    _layout_log = _logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.layout")\n    try:\n        packages = [entry["package"] 
for entry in entries]\n        n        = len(packages)\n\n        # Compute layout rectangles\n        try:\n         
   display = window_layout.detect_display_info()\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            display = 
window_layout.DisplayInfo(width=1080, height=1920, density=420)\n\n        # Pass the user\'s Termux dock fraction so 
the right pane (clone\n        # area) starts exactly where Termux ends.  Without this, Termux at\n        # 50 % 
overlaps the clone area (which previously assumed 35 % left\n        # reservation).  Probe ``p-47fa33562a`` showed 
clones overlapping\n        # the Termux pane on a 720 ╫ 1280 phone ù fixed by keeping the two\n        # fractions in 
lock-step.\n        _dock_frac = float(cfg.get("termux_dock_fraction", 0.50))\n        rects = 
window_layout.calculate_split_layout(\n            [p for p in packages if not 
window_layout._is_layout_excluded(p)],\n            display.width, display.height,\n            
termux_log_fraction=_dock_frac,\n        )\n        try:\n            cfg["last_layout_preview"] = [r.as_dict() for r 
in rects]\n            save_config(cfg)\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            pass\n\n        # 
\u2500\u2500 Discovery: identify real key aliases per package 
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        try:\n            from . 
import layout_discovery as _ld\n            try:\n                root_info = android.detect_root()\n                
root_tool = root_info.tool if root_info.available else None\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n           
     root_tool = None\n            log_path, _discs = _ld.run_discovery_and_log(\n                packages, 
root_tool=root_tool, refresh=False,\n            )\n            _layout_log.debug("layout-key discovery saved: %s", 
log_path)\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            _layout_log.debug("layout-key discovery error 
(non-fatal): %s", exc)\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 Pre-launch apply: write XML + force-stop so prefs are honored\n       
 # \u2500\u2500 on the imminent relaunch (cmd_start will issue the launches).\n        try:\n            from . import 
window_apply\n            results = window_apply.apply_window_layout(\n                rects,\n                
force_stop_before=True,    # MUST force-stop so prefs reload\n                relaunch_after=False,\n                
verify_after=False,        # verification deferred to post-launch\n                retries=0,\n            )\n         
   for r in results:\n                _layout_log.debug(\n                    "pre-launch apply: %s ok=%s method=%s 
status=%s attempts=%s",\n                    r.package, r.pre_write_ok, r.pre_write_method, r.status,\n                
    "; ".join(r.attempts),\n                )\n            cfg["_layout_rects"] = [r.desired.as_dict() for r in 
results]\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            _layout_log.debug("pre-launch apply error 
(non-fatal): %s", exc)\n\n        return cfg, f"layout_prepared n={n}"\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n 
       _layout_log.debug("Layout error (non-fatal): %s", exc)\n        return cfg, "layout_error"\n\n\ndef 
_save_start_diagnostics(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n    """Write 
``~/.deng-tool/rejoin/data/last_start_diagnostics.json`` (silent).\n\n    Used by Start to drop a small JSON file with 
desired vs actual bounds,\n    layout method used, state evidence, and a few other fields.  Public\n    users never 
see this file; it exists so we can diagnose without asking\n    them to run support-bundle.\n\n    Never raises.\n    
"""\n    try:\n        import json as _json\n        import logging as _logging\n        from pathlib import Path\n\n  
      target_dir = Path.home() / ".deng-tool" / "rejoin" / "data"\n        target_dir.mkdir(parents=True, 
exist_ok=True)\n        target = target_dir / "last_start_diagnostics.json"\n        # Sanitize: convert any 
non-JSON-serializable values to strings.\n        def _scrub(x: Any) -> Any:\n            if isinstance(x, dict):\n    
            return {str(k): _scrub(v) for k, v in x.items()}\n            if isinstance(x, (list, tuple)):\n           
     return [_scrub(v) for v in x]\n            if isinstance(x, (str, int, float, bool)) or x is None:\n              
  return x\n            return str(x)\n        with target.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:\n            
_json.dump(_scrub(payload), fh, indent=2, sort_keys=True)\n        _logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin").debug(\n          
  "Start diagnostics saved to %s", target,\n        )\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        import 
logging as _logging\n        _logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin").debug(\n            "save_start_diagnostics error: %s", 
exc,\n        )\n\n\ndef _verify_layout_post_launch(\n    cfg: dict[str, Any], entries: list[dict[str, Any]]\n) -> 
tuple[dict[str, bool], list[dict[str, Any]]]:\n    """Post-launch verification + direct-resize retry.\n\n    Runs 
silently after the launch grace.  Returns a tuple of\n    ``({package: applied_ok}, [diagnostic_rows...])`` so callers 
can include\n    per-package details in the start diagnostics JSON.\n    """\n    import logging as _logging\n    
_layout_log = _logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.layout")\n    out: dict[str, bool] = {}\n    diag_rows: list[dict[str, 
Any]] = []\n    try:\n        display = window_layout.detect_display_info()\n        _dock_frac2 = 
float(cfg.get("termux_dock_fraction", 0.50))\n        rects = window_layout.calculate_split_layout(\n            
[e["package"] for e in entries if not window_layout._is_layout_excluded(e["package"])],\n            display.width, 
display.height,\n            termux_log_fraction=_dock_frac2,\n        )\n        from . import window_apply\n        
results = window_apply.apply_window_layout(\n            rects,\n            force_stop_before=False,\n            
relaunch_after=False,\n            verify_after=True,\n            retries=0,  # MUST be 0: retries>0 force-stops 
running apps\n        )\n        for r in results:\n            out[r.package] = r.final_ok\n            
diag_rows.append({\n                "package":        r.package,\n                "desired":        
r.desired.as_dict() if hasattr(r.desired, "as_dict") else {\n                    "left": r.desired.left, "top": 
r.desired.top,\n                    "right": r.desired.right, "bottom": r.desired.bottom,\n                },\n        
        "actual_bounds":  r.actual_bounds,\n                "actual_method":  r.actual_method,\n                
"status":         r.status,\n                "pre_write_ok":   r.pre_write_ok,\n                "pre_write_method": 
r.pre_write_method,\n                "direct_resize_ok": r.direct_resize_ok,\n                "attempts":       
r.attempts,\n                "final_ok":       r.final_ok,\n            })\n            _layout_log.debug(\n           
     "post-launch verify: %s ok=%s actual=%s method=%s attempts=%s",\n                r.package, r.final_ok, 
r.actual_bounds, r.actual_method, "; ".join(r.attempts),\n            )\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n        _layout_log.debug("verify_layout_post_launch error: %s", exc)\n    return out, diag_rows\n\n\ndef 
_run_preparation_phase(\n    entries: list[dict[str, Any]],\n    cfg: dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    use_color: bool = 
True,\n    verbose: bool = True,\n) -> None:\n    """Preparation phase: stop background Roblox apps, clear cache.\n\n  
  Set ``verbose=False`` to run silently (no terminal output).\n    Steps:\n    1. Force-stop all Roblox packages NOT 
in the selected set (close background APKs).\n    2. Clear the cache directory for each selected package (root 
required).\n    """\n    G   = _ANSI_GREEN  if use_color else ""\n    Y   = _ANSI_YELLOW if use_color else ""\n    C   
= _ANSI_CYAN   if use_color else ""\n    RST = _ANSI_RESET  if use_color else ""\n\n    packages = [e["package"] for e 
in entries]\n    hints = cfg.get("package_detection_hints")\n\n    # Step 1 ù close background Roblox processes\n    
if verbose:\n        print(f"  {C}\u23f3 Stopping background Roblox processes...{RST}")\n    stopped = 
android.force_stop_packages_except(packages, hints)\n    if verbose:\n        if stopped:\n            for pkg in 
stopped:\n                print(f"  {G}\u2713{RST} Stopped: {pkg}")\n        else:\n            print(f"  
{Y}\u2139{RST}  No background Roblox processes found.")\n\n    # Step 2 ù safe cache dirs for each selected package 
(mandatory when Start calls prep)\n    if verbose:\n        print(f"  {C}\u23f3 Clearing safe cache (cache, 
code_cache, files/tmp)...{RST}")\n    any_cleared = False\n    for entry in entries:\n        pkg = entry["package"]\n 
       username = _account_username_for_table(entry)\n        label = android.clear_safe_package_cache(pkg)\n        
if verbose:\n            print(f"  {G if label == \'Cleared\' else Y}ò{RST} {pkg}: {label} ({username})")\n        if 
label == "Cleared":\n            any_cleared = True\n    if verbose:\n        if not any_cleared:\n            
print(f"  {Y}\u2139{RST}  Cache cleanup: mostly skipped (root/offline) or no cache dirs.")\n        print(f"  
{G}\u2713{RST} Preparation complete.")\n        print()\n\n\ndef _termux_exit_clean() -> None:\n    """Bypass Python 
finalization on Termux to avoid libc-shutdown segfaults.\n\n    Real-device evidence (probe ``p-47fa33562a``): on 
Termux + Python\n    3.13, after a clean supervisor shutdown the process sometimes\n    segfaults during interpreter 
teardown (atexit handlers, threading\n    join, file-handle close on the curl subprocess pipes).  The user\n    sees 
``Segmentation fault`` even though every worker had already\n    stopped cleanly.  Using ``os._exit(0)`` after we\'ve 
flushed logs\n    and persisted state skips the buggy native finalizers ù the kernel\n    reclaims the file 
descriptors and the process exits with code 0.\n\n    Non-Termux contexts (CI, tests, dev boxes) return without 
exiting\n    so unittest can still introspect the return value and exit cleanly\n    via the normal interpreter 
shutdown.\n    """\n    if not os.environ.get("TERMUX_VERSION"):\n        return\n    if 
os.environ.get("DENG_DISABLE_TERMUX_HARD_EXIT") == "1":\n        return  # escape hatch for debugging\n    # Flush 
stdout/stderr so the dashboard\'s final state isn\'t lost.\n    try:\n        sys.stdout.flush()\n    except 
Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n    try:\n        sys.stderr.flush()\n    except Exception:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n        pass\n    os._exit(0)\n\n\ndef cmd_start(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\n    use_color = not 
args.no_color\n    _start_lock: LockManager | None = None\n    _shutdown_reason = "normal_exit"\n    _supervisor_ref: 
WatchdogSupervisor | None = None\n    # Silence all internal loggers so warnings/errors go to file, never stdout.\n    
from .logger import silence_public_loggers\n    silence_public_loggers()\n\n    def _release_start_lock(reason: str) 
-> None:\n        nonlocal _start_lock\n        if _start_lock is None:\n            return\n        lock_removed = 
"false"\n        try:\n            _start_lock.release()\n            lock_removed = "true"\n        except Exception: 
 # noqa: BLE001\n            pass\n        try:\n            from .logger import configure_logging, log_event\n        
    _lg = configure_logging()\n            log_event(\n                _lg, "info", "[DENG_REJOIN_SHUTDOWN]",\n        
        reason=reason,\n                supervisor_stopped=str(bool(_supervisor_ref)).lower(),\n                
children_stopped="true",\n                lock_removed=lock_removed,\n            )\n        except Exception:  # 
noqa: BLE001\n            pass\n        _start_lock = None\n\n    _clear_terminal(clear_scrollback=True)\n    try:\n   
     cfg = load_config()\n        cfg = _ensure_install_id_saved(cfg)\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 License gate \u2500\u25

500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        if not keystore.DEV_MODE:\n

"").strip()\n                    if _key:\n                        ok, msg = keystore.verify_key(_key)\n               
         if not ok:\n                            _print_license_err(f"License key error: {msg}", use_color)\n          
                  print_beginner_license_gate_help()\n                            return 1\n                    
else:\n                        _print_license_err("No License Key Found", use_color)\n                        
print_beginner_license_gate_help()\n                        return 1\n                else:\n                    if 
not verify_remote_license_noninteractive(cfg, use_color=use_color):\n                        return 1\n\n        #

_report_key_execution_if_public(cfg)\n\n        entries = enabled_package_entries(cfg)\n        if not 
cfg.get("first_setup_completed"):\n            print("First-time setup is required before starting.")\n            if 
_is_interactive():\n                _run_first_time_setup_wizard(cfg, args, start_after_save=True)\n                
return 0\n            print("Run: deng-rejoin and choose First Time Setup Config.")\n            return 2\n\n        
if not entries:\n            print("No Roblox Package Selected")\n            print()\n            print("Run Setup / 
Edit Config, then choose Roblox Package Setup.")\n            return 2\n\n        try:\n            from .logger 
import configure_logging, log_event\n            _lock_logger = configure_logging()\n            _start_lock = 
LockManager()\n            try:\n                _start_lock.acquire()\n                log_event(\n                   
 _lock_logger, "info", "[DENG_REJOIN_INSTANCE_LOCK]",\n                    action="created",\n                    
pid=os.getpid(),\n                    lock_path=str(LOCK_PATH),\n                )\n            except LockError:\n    
            stopped, stop_msg = stop_running_agent(timeout=5)\n                log_event(\n

  pid="",\n                    lock_path=str(LOCK_PATH),\n                    result=stop_msg,\n                )\n    
            if stopped:\n                    _start_lock.acquire()\n                    log_event(\n                   
     _lock_logger, "info", "[DENG_REJOIN_INSTANCE_LOCK]",\n                        action="created",\n                 
       pid=os.getpid(),\n                        lock_path=str(LOCK_PATH),\n                    )\n

\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        import

    if cfg.get("root_mode_enabled"):\n            root_info = android.detect_root()\n
