len(entries)\n        sup = cfg.get("supervisor") if isinstance(cfg.get("supervisor"), dict) else {}\n\n        # 
\u2500\u2500 Phase-tracked dashboard \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        # User feedback: "text state never change from 
preparing because\n        # preparing has many stages (preparing, boosting, clearing cache,\n        # etc) meaning 
this never work and must copy kaeru."  The old\n        # code rendered ONE "Preparing" table at the top of cmd_start 
and\n        # didn\'t update it until 30 seconds later when supervisor took\n        # over ù so the user thought the 
tool was frozen.  We now re-\n        # render the table after each prep phase with a distinct label.\n\n        # 
Per-package phase label.  Updated in place by _phase() below.\n        phase: dict[str, str] = {e["package"]: 
"Preparing" for e in entries}\n\n        # RAM info is cached between renders (updated every ~9s) to avoid\n        # 
reading /proc/meminfo on every 3-second render tick.  Defined here\n        # (before _render_phase) so _render_phase 
can call _get_ram_label\n        # safely during the Preparing / Boosting / Launching phases.\n        _ram_cache: 
dict[str, Any] = {"info": None, "next_update": 0.0}\n\n        def _get_ram_label() -> str:\n            """Return 
compact \'RAM: XMB (Y%)\\\\n[\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2591\u2591\u2591\u2591]\' or brief fallback.\n\n            
Two-line format:  line 1 = bold coloured label,\n                              line 2 = ASCII progress bar matching 
available %.\n            Caller should split on \'\\\\n\' and indent each line individually.\n            """\n       
     try:\n                import time as _t\n                now = _t.monotonic()\n                if now >= 
_ram_cache["next_update"]:\n                    try:\n                        info = android.get_memory_info()\n       
                 _ram_cache["info"] = info\n                    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                    
    _ram_cache["info"] = None\n                    _ram_cache["next_update"] = now + 9.0\n                info = 
_ram_cache["info"]\n                if not info:\n                    return "RAM: Unknown"\n                free_mb  
= int(info.get("free_mb", 0))\n                pct_free = int(info.get("percent_free", 0))\n\n                label = 
f"RAM: {free_mb}MB ({pct_free}%)"\n                if use_color:\n                    col = (\n                        
_ANSI_GREEN if pct_free >= 40\n                        else (_ANSI_YELLOW if pct_free >= 20 else _ANSI_RED)\n          
          )\n                    label = f"{_ANSI_BOLD}{col}{label}{_ANSI_RESET}"\n\n                _bar_width = 20\n 
               _filled = max(0, min(_bar_width, int(pct_free / 100 * _bar_width)))\n                _bar = "[" + 
"\\u2588" * _filled + "\\u2591" * (_bar_width - _filled) + "]"\n                return f"{label}\\n{_bar}"\n           
 except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                return "RAM: Unknown"\n\n        def _render_phase(_unused_note: 
str = "") -> None:\n            """Clear + redraw the dashboard with the current phase per package.\n\n            
Shows only: logo + table + available RAM.\n            State labels in the table already describe what is happening;\n 
           additional notes below the table are suppressed (user feedback:\n            "useless text explaining 
state" when state is in the table).\n            Uses clear_scrollback=True on every call so old banner/table lines\n  
          from prior phases never bleed through on slow Termux terminals.\n            """\n            
_clear_terminal(clear_scrollback=True)\n            print_banner(use_color=use_color)\n            print()\n           
 rows = [\n                (i + 1, e["package"], _account_username_for_table(e),\n                 
phase.get(e["package"], "Preparing"), "")\n                for i, e in enumerate(entries)\n            ]\n            
print(build_start_table(rows, use_color=use_color))\n            try:\n                ram = _get_ram_label()\n        
        if ram:\n                    print()\n                    for _ram_line in ram.split("\\n"):\n                 
       print(f"  {_ram_line}")\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                pass\n            
print(flush=True)\n\n        def _set_all_phase(label: str, note: str = "") -> None:\n            for pkg in phase:\n  
              phase[pkg] = label\n            _render_phase(note)\n\n        # 1) "Preparing" ù force-stop each 
configured package individually,\n        #    verify it is dead, and clear background apps to free RAM.\n        #    
Only configured/selected packages are targeted; Termux and\n        #    system apps are never touched.\n        
_set_all_phase("Preparing", "Stopping configured packages...")\n        packages_sl = [e["package"] for e in 
entries]\n        keep_alive  = ["com.termux"] + packages_sl\n\n        # 1a) Clear cached/background processes (am 
kill-all is safe: only\n        #     kills CACHED-state background apps, never foreground services).\n        try:\n  
          android.kill_all_background_apps(keep_alive)\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            
_start_log.debug("start: kill_all_background_apps error (non-fatal)")\n\n        # 1b) Also force-stop any OTHER 
detected Roblox packages not in our list.\n        try:\n            android.force_stop_packages_except(packages_sl, 
cfg.get("package_detection_hints"))\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            _start_log.debug("start: 
force_stop_packages_except error (non-fatal)")\n\n        # 1c) Force-stop each CONFIGURED package individually with 
PID verification.\n        #     Termux (com.termux) and system apps are excluded by design.\n        import logging 
as _logging\n        _prep_root = android.detect_root()\n        for _prep_entry in entries:\n            _prep_pkg = 
_prep_entry["package"]\n            _pid_before = ""\n            _pid_after  = ""\n            _stop_ok    = False\n  
          _stop_err   = ""\n            try:\n                _pid_before = android.get_package_pid(_prep_pkg, 
_prep_root)\n                _stop_res   = android.force_stop_package(_prep_pkg, _prep_root)\n                _stop_ok 
   = bool(_stop_res.ok)\n                if not _stop_ok:\n                    _stop_err = (_stop_res.stderr or 
"")[:80]\n                # Verify process is dead; retry once if still alive.\n                import time as _t\n    
            _t.sleep(0.3)\n                _pid_after = android.get_package_pid(_prep_pkg, _prep_root)\n               
 if _pid_after:\n                    _t.sleep(0.8)\n                    android.force_stop_package(_prep_pkg, 
_prep_root)\n                    _t.sleep(0.3)\n                    _pid_after = android.get_package_pid(_prep_pkg, 
_prep_root)\n                _stop_ok = not bool(_pid_after)\n            except Exception as _exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n  
              _stop_err = str(_exc)[:80]\n            _start_log.info(\n                "[DENG_REJOIN_PREPARE_PACKAGE] 
package=%s force_stop_attempt=1"\n                " pid_before=%s pid_after=%s success=%s error=%s",\n                
_prep_pkg, _pid_before or "none", _pid_after or "none",\n                str(_stop_ok).lower(), _stop_err,\n           
 )\n\n        # 2) "Clear Cache" ù clear only cache/code_cache for each configured\n        #    package.  
shared_prefs/databases/files are never touched.\n        #    Size is verified to reach 0; retry on non-zero 
remainder.\n        prep_gfx: dict[str, str] = {}\n        opt = cfg.get("optimization") if 
isinstance(cfg.get("optimization"), dict) else {}\n        # Set all packages to "Clear Cache" and render ONCE before 
the loop.\n        for entry in entries:\n            phase[entry["package"]] = "Clear Cache"\n        
_render_phase()\n        # Restore auto-rotation silently (am kill-all can kill the rotation service).\n        try:\n 
           android.run_android_command(\n                ["settings", "put", "system", "accelerometer_rotation", 
"1"],\n                prefer_root=True,\n            )\n            android.run_android_command(\n                
["settings", "put", "system", "user_rotation", "0"],\n                prefer_root=True,\n            )\n        except 
Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            _start_log.debug("start: rotation restore error (non-fatal)")\n        for 
entry in entries:\n            pkg = entry["package"]\n            _cache_result: dict[str, object] = {}\n            
try:\n                _cache_result = android.clear_package_cache_verified(pkg)\n            except Exception as _exc: 
 # noqa: BLE001\n                _cache_result = {\n                    "success": False, "skipped": False, 
"skipped_reason": "",\n                    "cache_paths": [], "size_before_bytes": 0, "size_after_bytes": 0,\n         
           "attempts": 0, "error": str(_exc)[:80],\n                }\n            _start_log.info(\n                
"[DENG_REJOIN_CLEAR_CACHE] package=%s cache_paths=%s"\n                " size_before_bytes=%s size_after_bytes=%s 
attempt=%s"\n                " success=%s error=%s",\n                pkg,\n                ",".join(str(p) for p in 
_cache_result.get("cache_paths") or []) or "none",\n                _cache_result.get("size_before_bytes", 0),\n       
         _cache_result.get("size_after_bytes", 0),\n                _cache_result.get("attempts", 0),\n                
str(_cache_result.get("success", False)).lower(),\n                _cache_result.get("error", ""),\n            )\n    
        low = bool(opt.get("low_graphics_enabled", True)) and bool(entry.get("low_graphics_enabled", True))\n          
  try:\n                prep_gfx[pkg] = android.apply_low_graphics_optimization(pkg, enabled=low)\n            except 
Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                prep_gfx[pkg] = "error"\n            _start_log.debug("start: prep pkg=%s 
cache_ok=%s gfx=%s",\n                             pkg, _cache_result.get("success"), prep_gfx[pkg])\n\n        # 3) 
Compute window layout silently (no public phase change).\n        try:\n            cfg, _layout_note = 
_prepare_automatic_layout(cfg, entries)\n            _start_log.debug("start: layout note=%s", _layout_note)\n        
except Exception as _exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            _start_log.debug("start: layout error (non-fatal): %s", 
_exc)\n\n        # 4) Dock Termux silently (no public phase change).\n        _termux_minimize_result: dict[str, Any] 
= {}\n        try:\n            _dock_enabled = bool(cfg.get("termux_dock_enabled", True))\n            if 
_dock_enabled:\n                from . import termux_minimize as _tm  # noqa: PLC0415\n                _frac = 
cfg.get("termux_dock_fraction", 0.50)\n                _res = _tm.minimize_termux_to_dock(fraction=float(_frac))\n     
           _termux_minimize_result = _res.as_dict()\n                _start_log.debug(\n                    
"termux_minimize: ok=%s method=%s task=%s desired=%s actual=%s",\n                    _res.ok, _res.method, 
_res.task_id, _res.desired, _res.actual,\n                )\n            else:\n                
_termux_minimize_result = {"ok": False, "skipped": True,\n                                           "reason": 
"disabled by config"}\n        except Exception as _exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            
_start_log.debug("termux_minimize error (non-fatal): %s", _exc)\n            _termux_minimize_result = {"ok": False, 
"skipped": True,\n                                       "reason": f"exception: {_exc}"}\n\n        now_iso = 
datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n        start_times: dict[str, str] = dict(cfg.get("package_start_times") or 
{})\n        for entry in entries:\n            start_times[entry["package"]] = now_iso\n        
cfg["package_start_times"] = start_times\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 Launch URL confirmation (safe ù never expose the 
raw URL) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        _global_url = 
str(effective_private_server_url(runtime_entries[0] if runtime_entries else {}, runtime_cfg) or "").strip() \\\n       
     if runtime_entries else str(runtime_cfg.get("private_server_url") or runtime_cfg.get("launch_url") or 
"").strip()\n        if _global_url:\n            _start_log.info("start: Launch URL configured ù sending private 
server deep link to each clone")\n        else:\n            _start_log.info("start: No launch URL configured ù clones 
will open Roblox home")\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 Launch each package \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   
     # 5) "Launching" ù set ALL packages to "Launching" at once before\n        # the loop and render a single clean 
"all starting" screen.  Only\n        # re-render AFTER each launch (to show success/failure result).\n        
launch_ok:  dict[str, bool] = {}\n        launch_err: dict[str, str]  = {}\n        launch_attempted: dict[str, bool] 
= {}\n        for entry in entries:\n            phase[entry["package"]] = "Launching"\n        
_render_phase("Launching clones...")\n        for index, entry in enumerate(entries, start=1):\n            package = 
entry["package"]\n            runtime_entry = runtime_entry_by_pkg.get(package, entry)\n            
launch_attempted[package] = True\n            package_cfg = dict(runtime_cfg)\n            
package_cfg["roblox_package"] = package\n            _has_url = bool(str(\n                
effective_private_server_url(runtime_entry, runtime_cfg) or ""\n            ).strip())\n            result = 
perform_rejoin(package_cfg, reason="start", package_entry=runtime_entry)\n            launch_ok[package]  = 
result.success\n            launch_err[package] = result.error or ""\n            _start_log.info(\n                
"[DENG_REJOIN_LAUNCH_PACKAGE] package=%s launcher=%s"\n                " result=%s return_code=%s success=%s",\n       
         package,\n                "private_url" if _has_url else "app_only",\n                result.error or "ok", 0 
if result.success else 1,\n                str(result.success).lower(),\n            )\n            # Try to mute the 
package audio (per-package, root, non-fatal).\n            if result.success:\n                try:\n                  
  _mute = android.mute_package_audio(package)\n                    _start_log.info(\n                        
"[DENG_REJOIN_PACKAGE_VOLUME] package=%s method=%s"\n                        " target_volume=0 success=%s 
skipped_reason=%s error=%s",\n                        package,\n                        _mute.get("method", ""),\n     
                   str(_mute.get("success", False)).lower(),\n                        _mute.get("skipped_reason", 
""),\n                        _mute.get("error", ""),\n                    )\n                except Exception:  # 
noqa: BLE001\n                    pass\n            # Update result state and render once per completed launch.\n      
      phase[package] = "Launching" if result.success else "Failed"\n            _render_phase()\n            
_start_log.debug(\n                "start: launch pkg=%s ok=%s err=%s",\n                package, result.success, 
result.error or "",\n            )\n\n        # 6) Grace wait before verifying layout ù keep packages shown as\n       
 #    "Launching" (no "Waiting" label shown in public UI).\n        grace_wait = int(sup.get("launch_grace_seconds", 
15))\n        import time as _time\n        _time.sleep(max(5, grace_wait))\n\n        # 8) Verify layout silently ù 
no "Resizing" label shown (user feedback:\n        #    showing "Resizing" after launching is confusing/useless; the\n 
       #    supervisor\'s real-time detection will update the state next).\n        _layout_verify: dict[str, bool] = 
{}\n        _layout_diag: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n        try:\n            _layout_verify, _layout_diag = 
_verify_layout_post_launch(cfg, entries)\n            _start_log.debug("post-launch layout verify: %s", 
_layout_verify)\n        except Exception as _exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            _start_log.debug("post-launch verify 
error: %s", _exc)\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 Save start diagnostics JSON (silent, internal only) 
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        try:\n            
import time as _t\n            _evidence_summary: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n            for entry in entries:\n       
         pkg = entry["package"]\n                try:\n                    ev =

{}\n                _evidence_summary.append({\n                    "package":     pkg,\n

         "window":      bool(ev.get("window")),\n                    "surface":     bool(ev.get("surface")),\n         
           "task":        bool(ev.get("task")),\n                    "foreground":  bool(ev.get("foreground")),\n      
              "alive":       bool(ev.get("alive")),\n                    "private_url_set": bool(\n                    
    str(effective_private_server_url(runtime_entry_by_pkg.get(pkg, entry), runtime_cfg) or "").strip()\n               
     ),\n                })\n            try:\n                from . import freeform_enable as _ff\n

      _ff_ok, _ff_total = 0, 0\n            _save_start_diagnostics({\n                "timestamp_unix":

         cfg.get("__version", ""),\n                "selected_packages":  [e["package"] for e in entries],\n           
     "freeform_caps":      {"ok": _ff_ok, "total": _ff_total},\n                "layout_verify":

                pkg: {"ok": launch_ok.get(pkg, False),\n                          "error": launch_err.get(pkg, "") or 
""}\n                    for pkg in launch_ok\n                },\n            })\n        except Exception as _exc:  
# noqa: BLE001\n            _start_log.debug("save start diagnostics error: %s", _exc)\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 Build 
initial status table \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        initial_status: dict[str, str] = {}\n        table_rows: list[tuple] = []\n        
detail_rows: list[dict[str, str]] = []\n        for index, entry in enumerate(entries, start=1):\n            pkg      
= entry["package"]\n            username = _account_username_for_table(entry)\n            cstat    = 
prep_cache.get(pkg, "Skipped")\n            gstat    = prep_gfx.get(pkg, "Skipped")\n            if not 
launch_ok[pkg]:\n                err = launch_err[pkg]\n                state = "Failed"\n                safe_err = 
mask_urls_in_text(err) or "Launch failed"\n                stat_internal = (safe_err[:120] + "...") if len(safe_err) > 
123 else safe_err\n            elif android.is_process_running(pkg):\n                # Process is up.  
WatchdogSupervisor will classify on first check.\n                # Use Launching for all cases.\n                
state = "Launching"\n                stat_internal = "process running"\n            else:\n                state = 
"Launching"\n                stat_internal = "launch command sent"\n            initial_status[pkg] = state\n          
  table_rows.append((index, pkg, username, state))\n            detail_rows.append(\n                {"package": pkg, 
"cache": cstat, "graphics": gstat, "launch_detail": stat_internal}\n            )\n\n        # Log verbose detail to 
debug log only ù never to stdout\n        show_detail = (\n            bool(getattr(args, "verbose", False))\n         
   or bool(getattr(args, "debug", False))\n            or str(cfg.get("log_level", "")).upper() == "DEBUG"\n        
)\n        if show_detail:\n            for row in detail_rows:\n                _start_log.debug(\n                   
 "start detail: pkg=%s cache=%s gfx=%s launch=%s",\n                    row["package"], row["cache"], row["graphics"], 
row["launch_detail"],\n                )\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 Webhook \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        snapshot_path = None\n        if 
cfg.get("webhook_enabled") and cfg.get("webhook_snapshot_enabled"):\n            from . import snapshot as _snapshot\n 
           _snapshot.cleanup_old_snapshots(int(cfg.get("snapshot_max_age_seconds", 300)))\n            snapshot_path,

= str(snapshot_path)\n\n        if cfg.get("webhook_enabled"):\n            mem_info = android.get_memory_info()\n     
       cpu_pct = android.get_cpu_usage()\n            temp_c = android.get_temperature()\n            cfg["_mem_info"] 
= mem_info\n            cfg["_cpu_pct"] = cpu_pct\n            cfg["_temp_c"] = temp_c\n\n            app_stats: 
dict[str, Any] = {}\n            for entry in entries:\n                pkg = entry["package"]\n

else None\n                app_stats[pkg] = {\n                    "online": is_online,\n                    
"memory_mb": mem_mb,\n                    "cpu_pct": None,\n                    "uptime_start": 
start_times.get(pkg),\n                }\n\n            ok_wh, message_wh, message_id = webhook.send_webhook_update(\n 
               cfg,\n                event="start",\n                snapshot_path=snapshot_path,\n                
force=True,\n                app_stats=app_stats,\n            )\n            if message_id:\n

save_config(cfg)\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 Check if any package launched \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        attempted_count = sum(1 for v in launch_attempted.values() if 
v)\n        success_count = sum(1 for pkg, attempted in launch_attempted.items() if attempted and 
launch_ok.get(pkg))\n        if attempted_count and success_count == 0:\n            reasons = [v for v in 
launch_err.values() if v]\n            best_reason = reasons[0][:80] if reasons else "all launch attempts failed"\n    
        _clear_terminal()\n            print_banner(use_color=use_color)\n            print()\n            
print("Launch Failed")\n            print()\n            print("  Package was selected but Android did not launch 
it.")\n            print()\n            print(f"  Detail: {best_reason}")\n            
_release_start_lock("normal_exit")\n            return 1\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 Supervisor loop ù dashboard takes 
over entirely 
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   
     # From this point on, _live_dashboard() clears the terminal and\n        # redraws logo + table on every refresh. 
 No other text is printed.\n        # Keep the live supervisor config valid.  The dashboard still refreshes\n        # 
frequently below; worker health checks must stay within config bounds.\n        _live_cfg = dict(runtime_cfg)\n        
_live_cfg["package_start_times"] = start_times\n        _sup_sub = dict(\n            cfg.get("supervisor") if 
isinstance(cfg.get("supervisor"), dict) else {}\n        )\n        _hci_raw = 
int(_sup_sub.get("health_check_interval_seconds", 10))\n        _sup_sub["health_check_interval_seconds"] = max(10, 
_hci_raw)\n        _live_cfg["supervisor"] = _sup_sub\n        # [DENG_REJOIN_WATCHDOG_FIX] Use WatchdogSupervisor: 
sequential per-package\n        # loop, process-check-first, 4 states only, never stops after Online.\n        
_supervisor = WatchdogSupervisor(runtime_entries, _live_cfg, initial_status=initial_status)\n        _supervisor_ref = 
_supervisor\n        _live_map = _supervisor.status_map  # dict mutated in-place by watchdog loop\n\n        # Public 
state map: internal states \u2192 user-facing labels.\n        # Allowed public states: Online, No Heartbeat, Dead, 
Launching,\n        #   Clear Cache, Preparing, Failed.\n        # Internal/noisy states (Docking, Layout, Waiting, 
Checking etc.)\n        # never reach the live supervisor dashboard.\n        _STATE_DISPLAY_MAP: dict[str, str] = {\n 
           # Live watchdog states ù keep as-is.\n            "No Heartbeat":     "No Heartbeat",\n            
"Online":           "Online",\n            "Dead":             "Dead",\n            # Transient post-launch / startup 
\u2192 Launching\n            "Preparing":        "Launching",\n            "Unknown":          "Launching",\n         
   # Alive + in-game states \u2192 Online\n            "Launched":         "Online",\n            "In Server":        
"Online",\n            "Background":       "Online",\n            "Warning":          "Online",\n            # Lobby = 
app open but not in game \u2192 No Heartbeat (In-Lobby removed)\n            "Lobby":            "No Heartbeat",\n     
       # Recovery / disconnect states\n            "Reconnecting":     "No Heartbeat",\n            "Disconnected":    
 "Dead",\n            "Offline":          "Dead",\n        }\n\n        def _fmt_runtime(secs: float) -> str:\n        
    """Format elapsed seconds as compact d/h/m/s string."""\n            s = int(secs)\n            if s <= 0:\n       
         return "0s"\n            d, s = divmod(s, 86400)\n            h, s = divmod(s, 3600)\n            m, s = 
divmod(s, 60)\n            if d:\n                return f"{d}d {h}h {m}m {s}s"\n            if h:\n                
return f"{h}h {m}m {s}s"\n            if m:\n                return f"{m}m {s}s"\n            return f"{s}s"\n\n       
 def _live_dashboard() -> None:\n            """Clear screen and redraw banner + table with live status values.\n\n    
        Uses clear_scrollback=True so the prep-phase banner/table cannot\n            bleed through at the 
Start\u2192supervisor transition (dirty-UI fix).\n            Checking Package X/Y text is removed from public UI 
(probe-only).\n            Runtime column shows elapsed time since package first became Online.\n            """\n     
       import time as _ts_time\n            _now_ts = _ts_time.time()\n\n            def _get_runtime(pkg: str) -> 
str:\n                raw_state = _live_map.get(pkg, "Unknown")\n                disp = 
_STATE_DISPLAY_MAP.get(raw_state, raw_state)\n                if disp != "Online":\n                    return ""\n    
            start_ts = getattr(_supervisor, "_online_start_ts", {}).get(pkg, 0.0)\n                if not start_ts:\n  
                  return ""\n                return _fmt_runtime(max(0.0, _now_ts - start_ts))\n\n            
_clear_terminal(clear_scrollback=True)\n            print_banner(use_color=use_color)\n            print()\n           
 ram_label = _get_ram_label()\n            if ram_label:\n                for _ram_line in ram_label.split("\\n"):\n   
                 print(f"  {_ram_line}")\n                print()\n            live_rows = [\n                (i + 1, 
e["package"], _account_username_for_table(e),\n                 _STATE_DISPLAY_MAP.get(\n                     
_live_map.get(e["package"], "Unknown"),\n                     _live_map.get(e["package"], "Unknown"),\n                
 ),\n                 _get_runtime(e["package"]))\n                for i, e in enumerate(entries)\n            ]\n     
       print(build_start_table(live_rows, use_color=use_color))\n            print(flush=True)\n\n        # One-shot 
scrollback clear at the prep\u2192supervisor transition so the\n        # last "Waiting" prep screen is completely 
gone before the live\n        # dashboard takes over.  Prevents any remaining prep-phase text from\n        # being 
visible above the first _live_dashboard frame.\n        _clear_terminal(clear_scrollback=True)\n\n        # Use 
3-second display interval (like Kaeru\'s blinking real-time table).\n        try:\n            
_supervisor.run_forever(\n                render_callback=_live_dashboard,\n                display_interval=3.0,\n    
        )\n        except KeyboardInterrupt:\n            # Silent: signal handler already set stop_event.  Caller 
will rerun cleanly.\n            _shutdown_reason = "ctrl_c"\n            _supervisor.stop()\n        except Exception 
as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            _start_log.debug("Supervisor terminated with error: %s", exc)\n            
_supervisor.stop()\n        # Best-effort clean exit: clear screen so terminal is not littered.\n        try:\n        
    _clear_terminal()\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            pass\n        
_release_start_lock(_shutdown_reason)\n        # Real-device evidence (probe ``p-47fa33562a``): on Termux, Python\n    
    # finalization after a supervisor stop sometimes segfaults inside\n        # libc cleanup (atexit handlers / 
threading shutdown / file-handle\n        # close on the curl subprocess pipes), printing ``Segmentation\n        # 
fault`` to the public terminal even though everything stopped\n        # cleanly.  Skip Python finalization by calling 
``os._exit`` when\n        # running on Termux/Android ù the supervisor has already flushed\n        # logs and 
persisted state.  Non-Termux contexts (CI, tests, dev\n        # box) keep the normal return path so unittest can 
introspect.\n        _termux_exit_clean()\n        return 0\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        # Pre-supervisor 
Ctrl+C: just exit quietly, no traceback.\n        _shutdown_reason = "ctrl_c"\n        try:\n            if 
_supervisor_ref is not None:\n                _supervisor_ref.stop()\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n      
      pass\n        _release_start_lock(_shutdown_reason)\n        try:\n            _clear_terminal()\n        except 
Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            pass\n        _termux_exit_clean()\n        return 0\n    except Exception as 
exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - command boundary.\n        import logging as _logging\n        
_logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.start").debug("cmd_start error: %s", exc)\n        try:\n            if 
_supervisor_ref is not None:\n                _supervisor_ref.stop()\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n      
      pass\n        _release_start_lock("normal_exit")\n        print(f"Agent start failed: {exc}")\n        return 
1\n\n\ndef _run_diag_in_subprocess(args: list[str], *, timeout: int = 8) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    """Run a diagnostic 
shell command in an isolated child process.\n\n    If the child crashes (SIGSEGV) the parent survives ù no 
"Segmentation\n    fault" text reaches the user\'s terminal.  Returns ``(ok, output)`` where\n    output is bounded to 
~4 KB.  Never raises.\n    """\n    try:\n        result = subprocess.run(\n            args,\n            
stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,\n            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n            stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n            
text=True,\n            timeout=timeout,\n            shell=False,\n        )\n        body = (result.stdout or 
"")[:4096]\n        if result.returncode != 0 and not body:\n            return False, (result.stderr or 
"").strip()[:4096]\n        return True, body\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return False, ""\n    except 
subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n        return False, "(timed out)"\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
return False, f"(child error: {exc})"\n\n\ndef cmd_support_bundle(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\n    """Hidden: 
write a support bundle for offline diagnosis.\n\n    SAFETY CONTRACT (must not regress):\n      * READ-ONLY.  Does NOT 
modify any config, prefs XML, database, or files\n        outside the bundle path under LOG_DIR.\n      * Does NOT 
start any supervisor / threads.\n      * Does NOT alter terminal mode (no readline calls, no raw mode, no\n        
signal handler installs).\n      * All risky shell commands (``dumpsys``, ``cmd activity``, ``pidof``)\n        run in 
isolated subprocesses with captured stdout/stderr ù a SIGSEGV\n        in any child does NOT propagate to the parent 
terminal.\n      * Catches every exception and returns 0 (or a small non-zero) ù never\n        raises a Python

"""Hidden: capture sanitized device evidence for a real fix.\n\n    Always runs read-only.  Writes a single JSON file 
at\n    ``~/.deng-tool/rejoin/data/probes/probe-<ts>-<id>.json``.  With\n    ``--upload`` it POSTs the same JSON to 
the install API and prints the\n    short probe id the user can paste in chat.\n\n    The command never raises; every 
collection step is guarded and any\n    failure becomes a ``probe["errors"]`` entry.\n    """\n    from . import probe 
as _p\n    from .logger import configure_logging, log_event\n\n    include_diag = bool(getattr(args, "diag", False))\n 
   eta = "~30s" if include_diag else "~10s"\n    sys.stdout.write(f"Collecting device evidence... ({eta})\\n")\n    
sys.stdout.flush()\n    started = _p.time.monotonic()\n    first_run = True\n    try:\n        
_p.PROBE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        first_run = not any(_p.PROBE_DIR.glob("probe-*.json"))\n    
except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n    try:\n        data = 
_p.collect_probe(include_diag_startup=include_diag)\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 ù should be 
impossible, but be safe.\n        sys.stdout.write(f"probe failed: {exc}\\n")\n        return 1\n    elapsed = 
_p.time.monotonic() - started\n    path = _p.save_probe(data)\n    size = path.stat().st_size\n    sys.stdout.write(\n 
       f"probe saved: {path} ({size / 1024:.1f} KB, "\n        f"{len(data.get(\'errors\') or [])} step errors, 
{elapsed:.1f}s)\\n"\n    )\n\n    if getattr(args, "upload", False):\n        sys.stdout.write("uploading...\\n")\n    
    sys.stdout.flush()\n        ok, info = _p.upload_probe(data)\n        try:\n            log_event(\n               
 configure_logging(), "info", "[DENG_REJOIN_PROBE_UPLOAD]",\n                first_run=str(first_run).lower(),\n       
         probe_dir=str(_p.PROBE_DIR),\n                probe_file=str(path),\n                created_now="true",\n    
            upload_attempted="true",\n                upload_success=str(ok).lower(),\n                error="" if ok 
else str(info)[:180],\n            )\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            pass\n        if ok:\n    
        sys.stdout.write(f"probe_id: {info}\\n")\n            sys.stdout.write("share this id in chat.\\n")\n        
else:\n            sys.stdout.write(f"upload failed: {info}\\n")\n            sys.stdout.write(f"probe file: 
{path}\\n")\n            return 1\n    else:\n        try:\n            log_event(\n                
configure_logging(), "info", "[DENG_REJOIN_PROBE_UPLOAD]",\n                first_run=str(first_run).lower(),\n        
        probe_dir=str(_p.PROBE_DIR),\n                probe_file=str(path),\n                created_now="true",\n     
           upload_attempted="false",\n                upload_success="false",\n                error="",\n            
)\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            pass\n        sys.stdout.write("to share, either paste the 
JSON file in chat, or run:\\n")\n        sys.stdout.write("  deng-rejoin probe --upload\\n")\n    return 0\n\n\ndef 
cmd_doctor_install(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\n    """Verify the on-disk install is wired to the new build.\n\n 
   Prints one line per check (PASS / FAIL + detail) and exits 0 only if\n    every check passes.  This is hidden from 
the public menu but documented\n    for cloud-phone install verification.\n    """\n    from . import build_info as 
_bi\n\n    results = _bi.doctor_install_checks()\n    ok = _bi.doctor_install_overall_ok(results)\n    for r in 
results:\n        tag = "PASS" if r["ok"] else "FAIL"\n        sys.stdout.write(f"[{tag}] {r[\'name\']}: 
{r[\'detail\']}\\n")\n    summary = "OK" if ok else "FAILED"\n    sys.stdout.write(f"\\ndoctor install: 
{summary}\\n")\n    return 0 if ok else 1\n\n\ndef cmd_diag_startup(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\n    """Hidden: 
walk every step of the menu-startup chain, printing markers.\n\n    Each marker is flushed *before* the step runs.  If 
any sub-routine\n    segfaults (real device incident: probe ``p-b30c47d37f``), the parent\n    captures the last 
successful ``STEP:<name>`` line and the failing one\n    is the next call.  Errors are caught and printed as 
``ERROR:<step>``\n    so a Python exception doesn\'t masquerade as a crash.\n\n    Designed to be invoked by 
:func:`agent.probe._capture_diag_startup`\n    via ``subprocess.run`` so a SIGSEGV in any module is *isolated to\n    
this child* ù the probe parent survives and uploads the partial\n    output as evidence.\n    """\n    def _step(name: 
str) -> None:\n        sys.stdout.write(f"STEP:{name}\\n")\n        sys.stdout.flush()\n\n    def _ok(name: str, 
detail: str = "") -> None:\n        suffix = f" {detail}" if detail else ""\n        
sys.stdout.write(f"OK:{name}{suffix}\\n")\n        sys.stdout.flush()\n\n    def _err(name: str, exc: BaseException) 
-> None:\n        # Truncated single line.  Real trace goes to the file logger\n        # via the global except in 
main(); this is just a marker.\n        sys.stdout.write(f"ERROR:{name} {type(exc).__name__}: {str(exc)[:160]}\\n")\n  
      sys.stdout.flush()\n\n    _step("entered")\n\n    try:\n        _step("ensure_app_dirs")\n        
ensure_app_dirs()\n        _ok("ensure_app_dirs")\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
_err("ensure_app_dirs", exc)\n        return 2\n\n    try:\n        _step("check_crash_log")\n        notice = 
safe_io.check_and_report_crash_log()\n        _ok("check_crash_log", f"notice={\'yes\' if notice else \'no\'}")\n    
except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _err("check_crash_log", exc)\n\n    try:\n        
_step("keystore_dev_mode")\n        dev = bool(keystore.DEV_MODE)\n        _ok("keystore_dev_mode", f"dev={dev}")\n    
except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _err("keystore_dev_mode", exc)\n\n    try:\n        
_step("load_config")\n        cfg = load_config()\n        _ok("load_config")\n    except ConfigError as exc:\n        
_err("load_config_config_error", exc)\n        cfg = default_config()\n        _ok("load_config_fallback_default")\n   
 except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _err("load_config", exc)\n        # Don\'t bail ù fall through with 
safe defaults so later steps\n        # still surface evidence (the goal is *narrow*, not *abort*).\n        cfg = 
default_config()\n        _ok("load_config_fallback_default")\n\n    try:\n        _step("sync_install_id")\n        
cfg = _ensure_install_id_saved(cfg)\n        _ok("sync_install_id")\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n    
    _err("sync_install_id", exc)\n\n    try:\n        _step("license_section_read")\n        lic = 
cfg.setdefault("license", {})\n        mode = str(lic.get("mode") or "remote").strip().lower()\n        
_ok("license_section_read", f"mode={mode}")\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
_err("license_section_read", exc)\n        return 4\n\n    try:\n        _step("license_cache_fast_path")\n

f"hit={cached}")\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _err("license_cache_fast_path", exc)\n\n    # 
Granular trace of the remote check.  Real-device probe\n    # ``p-39924732cd`` showed the parent process dies at\n    
# ``STEP:license_remote_check`` ù but didn\'t tell us WHICH sub-call.\n    # We now print a STEP for each sub-call so 
the next crash points\n    # at the exact line that segfaults libc / openssl / urllib.\n    try:\n        
_step("license_sync_install_id")\n        from .license import sync_install_id_with_config  # noqa: PLC0415\n        
_iid = sync_install_id_with_config(cfg.setdefault("license", {}))\n        _ok("license_sync_install_id", 
f"iid_short={(_iid or \'\')[:8]}")\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
_err("license_sync_install_id", exc)\n\n    try:\n        _step("license_get_device_model")\n        from .license 
import get_public_device_model  # noqa: PLC0415\n        _model = get_public_device_model()\n        
_ok("license_get_device_model", f"model={_model[:32]}")\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
_err("license_get_device_model", exc)\n\n    try:\n        _step("license_safe_http_backend")\n        from . import 
safe_http as _sh  # noqa: PLC0415\n        _backend = _sh._http_backend()\n        _ok("license_safe_http_backend", 
f"backend={_backend}")\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _err("license_safe_http_backend", 
exc)\n\n    try:\n        _step("license_curl_available")\n        from . import safe_http as _sh2  # noqa: PLC0415\n  
      _have = _sh2._curl_available()\n        _ok("license_curl_available", f"have={_have}")\n    except Exception as 
exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _err("license_curl_available", exc)\n\n    try:\n        
_step("license_remote_check_isolated")\n        # Routes through the same code path cmd_menu now uses (child\n        
# subprocess), so a SIGSEGV here is captured as a clean\n        # returncode in the diagnoser ù the probe sees a 
normal\n        # OK / ERROR line and we can keep tracing past it.\n        result, msg = 
_remote_license_check_isolated(cfg, timeout=20)\n        _ok("license_remote_check_isolated", f"result={result}")\n    
except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _err("license_remote_check_isolated", exc)\n\n    try:\n        
_step("license_remote_check_direct")\n        # The unprotected call ù only run AFTER the isolated one so the\n        
# child still finishes the rest of the diagnostic if this dies.\n        # If the child segfaults right here, 
last_step ==\n        # license_remote_check_direct in the parent probe.\n        result2, msg2 = 
_remote_license_run_check(cfg)\n        _ok("license_remote_check_direct", f"result={result2}")\n    except Exception 
as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _err("license_remote_check_direct", exc)\n\n    try:\n

exc)\n\n    try:\n        _step("detect_display_info")\n        from . import window_layout as _wl2  # noqa: PLC0415\n 
       di = _wl2.detect_display_info()\n        _ok("detect_display_info", f"{di.width}x{di.height} @ {di.density}")\n 
   except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _err("detect_display_info", exc)\n\n    try:\n

    ok_count, total = setup_freeform_capabilities_silent()\n        _ok("freeform_probe", f"ok={ok_count}/{total}")\n  
  except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _err("freeform_probe", exc)\n\n    try:\n

banner_text(use_color=False)\n        _ok("banner_print")\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
_err("banner_print", exc)\n\n    _step("finished")\n    return 0\n\n\ndef cmd_enable_boot(args: argparse.Namespace) ->

"__pycache__", "data", "logs", "run", "backups", "config.json"}\n    for item in source.iterdir():\n        if

          shutil.copytree(item, target, dirs_exist_ok=True, ignore=shutil.ignore_patterns("__pycache__"))\n

run_menu(args, _handlers())\n\n\ndef parse_args(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> argparse.Namespace:\n    parser =

             help=argparse.SUPPRESS)\n    parser.add_argument("--root-state", dest="root_state",

                      help=argparse.SUPPRESS)\n    parser.add_argument("--upload", dest="upload", 
action="store_true",\n                        help=argparse.SUPPRESS)\n    parser.add_argument("--diag-startup", 
dest="diag_startup", action="store_true",\n                        help=argparse.SUPPRESS)\n    # ``--diag`` opts INTO 
the heavy child-subprocess diag step (45 s\n    # timeout) inside ``deng-rejoin probe``.  Off by default ù without\n   
 # it the probe completes in ~10 s and reliably fits the 4 MB upload\n    # cap even after long test sessions.\n    
parser.add_argument("--diag", dest="diag", action="store_true",\n                        help=argparse.SUPPRESS)\n\n   
 # Pre-process argv so hidden positional subcommands don\'t trip choices validation.\n    import sys as _sys\n    if 
argv is None:\n        argv = _sys.argv[1:]\n    argv = list(argv)\n\n    # Standalone aliases: hidden subcommand 
spellings \u2192 flag.\n    if argv and argv[0] in ("support-bundle", "support_bundle"):\n        argv[0] = 
"--support-bundle"\n    if argv and argv[0] in ("discover-layout-keys", "discover_layout_keys"):\n        argv[0] = 
"--discover-layout-keys"\n    if argv and argv[0] in ("probe",):\n        argv[0] = "--probe"\n\n    # Use 
parse_known_args so that `deng-rejoin doctor layout` doesn\'t fail\n    # with "unrecognized arguments: layout".\n    
try:\n        ns, _unknown = parser.parse_known_args(argv)\n    except SystemExit:\n        # argparse 
choice-validation failure ù fall back to safe defaults.\n        ns = parser.parse_args([])\n        _unknown = 
list(argv)\n\n    # Map positional sub-subcommands for `doctor`: "doctor layout", "doctor root-state".\n    
ns.doctor_install = False\n    if ns.command == "doctor" and _unknown:\n        sub = (_unknown[0] or 
"").lower().replace("-", "_")\n        if sub in ("layout", "layout_test"):\n            ns.layout_test = True\n       
 elif sub in ("root_state",):\n            ns.root_state = True\n        elif sub in ("reset", "layout_reset"):\n      
      ns.layout_reset = True\n        elif sub in ("bundle", "support_bundle"):\n            ns.support_bundle = 
True\n        elif sub in ("install", "installation", "build"):\n            ns.doctor_install = True\n        # Any 
other doctor sub is just dropped silently ù no traceback.\n\n    flag_to_command = {\n        "setup": ns.setup,\n     
   "first-setup": ns.first_setup,\n        "doctor": ns.doctor,\n        "status": ns.status,\n        "once": 
ns.once,\n        "start": ns.start,\n        "stop": ns.stop,\n        "reset": ns.reset,\n        "config": 
ns.config_flag,\n        "logs": ns.logs,\n        "version": ns.version,\n        "menu": ns.menu,\n        
"license": ns.license,\n        "new-user-help": ns.new_user_help,\n        "enable-boot": ns.enable_boot,\n        
"update": ns.update,\n    }\n    selected = [command for command, enabled in flag_to_command.items() if enabled]\n    
if ns.command and selected:\n        parser.error("use either positional command or --command flag, not both")\n    if 
len(selected) > 1:\n        parser.error("choose only one command")\n\n    # Hidden diagnostics: route to internal 
commands without showing in public menu.\n    if getattr(ns, "discover_layout_keys", False):\n        
ns.resolved_command = "discover-layout-keys"\n    elif getattr(ns, "support_bundle", False):\n        
ns.resolved_command = "support-bundle"\n    elif getattr(ns, "probe", False):\n        ns.resolved_command = "probe"\n 
   elif getattr(ns, "diag_startup", False):\n        ns.resolved_command = "diag-startup"\n    elif getattr(ns, 
"doctor_install", False):\n        ns.resolved_command = "doctor-install"\n    elif getattr(ns, "layout_test", False) 
or getattr(ns, "root_state", False) or getattr(ns, "layout_reset", False):\n        ns.resolved_command = "doctor"\n   
 else:\n        ns.resolved_command = ns.command or (selected[0] if selected else "menu")\n    return ns\n\n\ndef 
main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:\n    """CLI entrypoint with global safety wrappers.\n\n    faulthandler is 
enabled with a FILE target (never stderr) so SIGSEGV crash\n    tracebacks never reach the public terminal.  All 
Python-level exceptions\n    are caught here; the public user never sees a raw traceback or signal text.\n    """\n    
safe_io.setup_faulthandler()\n    # Silence internal namespace loggers so warnings/errors never leak to terminal.\n    
try:\n        from .logger import silence_public_loggers\n        silence_public_loggers()\n    except Exception:  # 
noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n    try:\n        args = parse_args(argv)\n        return 
_handlers()[args.resolved_command](args)\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        # Silent exit ù return cleanly to 
shell, no public text.\n        # On Termux, also skip Python finalization (libc cleanup\n        # segfaults ù probe 
``p-47fa33562a``).\n        _termux_exit_clean()\n        return 0\n    except EOFError:\n        
_termux_exit_clean()\n        return 0\n    except SystemExit as _exc:\n        _code = _exc.code\n        return 
_code if isinstance(_code, int) else (0 if _code is None else 1)\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
import logging as _logging\n        _logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.cli").debug("Unhandled CLI error", 
exc_info=True)\n        print(\n            "\\nThe tool hit an internal error. "\n            "Please send support 
the latest crash log.",\n            file=sys.stderr,\n        )\n        return 1\n\n\ndef _handlers() -> dict[str, 
Any]:\n    return {\n        "setup": cmd_setup,\n        "first-setup": cmd_first_setup,\n        "doctor": 
cmd_doctor,\n        "status": cmd_status,\n        "once": cmd_once,\n        "start": cmd_start,\n        "stop": 
cmd_stop,\n        "reset": cmd_reset,\n        "config": cmd_config,\n        "logs": cmd_logs,\n        "version": 
cmd_version,\n        "menu": cmd_menu,\n        "license": cmd_license,\n        "new-user-help": 
cmd_new_user_help,\n        "enable-boot": cmd_enable_boot,\n        "update": cmd_update,\n        "support-bundle": 
cmd_support_bundle,\n        "discover-layout-keys": cmd_discover_layout_keys,\n        "doctor-install": 
cmd_doctor_install,\n        "probe": cmd_probe,\n        "diag-startup": cmd_diag_startup,\n    }\n\n\nif __name__ == 
"__main__":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n\n"""Config creation, persistence, and validation."""\n\nfrom __future__

    device_name = DEFAULT_DEVICE_NAME\n    return {\n        "device_name": device_name,\n        "agent_version":

merged["webhook_snapshot_enabled"] = False\n        merged["webhook_send_snapshot"] = False\n\n    webhook_mode =

raise ConfigError("webhook_mode must be new_message or edit_message")\n    merged["webhook_mode"] = webhook_mode\n    
merged["webhook_url"] = str(merged.get("webhook_url") or "").strip()\n    if merged["webhook_enabled"]:\n        
try:\n            merged["webhook_url"] = validate_webhook_url(merged["webhook_url"])\n        except ValueError as 
exc:\n            raise ConfigError(str(exc)) from exc\n    merged["webhook_message_id"] = 
str(merged.get("webhook_message_id") or "").strip()\n    merged["webhook_last_message_id"] = 
str(merged.get("webhook_last_message_id") or "").strip()\n    merged["webhook_last_sent_at"] = 
merged.get("webhook_last_sent_at") or 0\n    if merged["webhook_enabled"]:\n        try:\n            
merged["webhook_interval_seconds"] = validate_webhook_interval(merged.get("webhook_interval_seconds", 300))\n        
except ValueError as exc:\n            raise ConfigError(str(exc)) from exc\n    else:\n        try:\n            
merged["webhook_interval_seconds"] = int(merged.get("webhook_interval_seconds", 300))\n        except (TypeError, 
ValueError):\n            merged["webhook_interval_seconds"] = 300\n\n    auto_resize_mode = 
str(merged.get("auto_resize_mode", "off")).strip().lower()\n    if auto_resize_mode not in AUTO_RESIZE_MODES:\n        
raise ConfigError("auto_resize_mode must be off, auto, or preview")\n    merged["auto_resize_mode"] = 
auto_resize_mode\n\n    merged["reconnect_delay_seconds"] = _as_int(\n        "reconnect_delay_seconds", 
merged.get("reconnect_delay_seconds"), MIN_RECONNECT_DELAY_SECONDS\n    )\n    merged["health_check_interval_seconds"] 
= _as_int(\n        "health_check_interval_seconds", merged.get("health_check_interval_seconds"), 
MIN_HEALTH_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS\n    )\n    merged["foreground_grace_seconds"] = _as_int(\n        
"foreground_grace_seconds", merged.get("foreground_grace_seconds"), MIN_FOREGROUND_GRACE_SECONDS\n    )\n    
merged["max_fast_failures"] = _as_int("max_fast_failures", merged.get("max_fast_failures"), 1)\n    
merged["backoff_min_seconds"] = _as_int("backoff_min_seconds", merged.get("backoff_min_seconds"), 
MIN_BACKOFF_SECONDS)\n    merged["backoff_max_seconds"] = _as_int("backoff_max_seconds", 
merged.get("backoff_max_seconds"), MIN_BACKOFF_SECONDS, MAX_BACKOFF_SECONDS)\n    merged["window_gap_px"] = 
_as_int("window_gap_px", merged.get("window_gap_px"), 0)\n    merged["snapshot_max_age_seconds"] = 
_as_int("snapshot_max_age_seconds", merged.get("snapshot_max_age_seconds"), 30)\n    if merged["backoff_max_seconds"] 
< merged["backoff_min_seconds"]:\n        raise ConfigError("backoff_max_seconds must be greater than or equal to 
backoff_min_seconds")\n\n    log_level = str(merged.get("log_level", "INFO")).upper()\n    if log_level not in 
{"DEBUG", "INFO", "WARN", "WARNING", "ERROR"}:\n        raise ConfigError("log_level must be DEBUG, INFO, WARN, 
WARNING, or ERROR")\n    merged["log_level"] = "WARNING" if log_level == "WARN" else log_level\n\n    if not 
merged.get("created_at"):\n        merged["created_at"] = utc_now()\n    merged["updated_at"] = utc_now()\n    if not 
isinstance(merged.get("last_layout_preview"), list):\n        merged["last_layout_preview"] = []\n    
merged["snapshot_temp_path"] = str(merged.get("snapshot_temp_path") or "")\n\n    # Nested license section (public 
installs use remote API by default)\n    raw_lic = merged.get("license")\n    if not isinstance(raw_lic, dict):\n      
  raw_lic = {}\n    merged_lic: dict[str, Any] = dict(default_config()["license"])\n    merged_lic.update({k: v for k, 
v in raw_lic.items() if k in merged_lic})\n    # Sanitize sub-fields\n    merged_lic["disabled_by_user"] = 
_as_bool(merged_lic.get("disabled_by_user", False))\n    merged_lic["enabled"] = _as_bool(merged_lic.get("enabled", 
True))\n    _lic_mode = str(merged_lic.get("mode") or "remote").strip().lower()\n    if _lic_mode not in {"local", 
"remote"}:\n        _lic_mode = "remote"\n    merged_lic["mode"] = _lic_mode\n    merged_lic["key"] = 
str(merged_lic.get("key") or "").strip()[:256]\n    merged_lic["server_url"] = str(merged_lic.get("server_url") or 
"").strip()[:512]\n    merged_lic["install_id"] = str(merged_lic.get("install_id") or "").strip()[:64]\n    
merged_lic["device_label"] = str(merged_lic.get("device_label") or "").strip()[:80]\n    merged_lic["last_status"] = 
str(merged_lic.get("last_status") or "not_configured").strip()[:32]\n    _ch = str(merged_lic.get("channel") or 
CHANNEL_STABLE).strip().lower()\n    merged_lic["channel"] = _ch if _ch in VALID_CHANNELS else CHANNEL_STABLE\n    # 
last_check_at: keep None or string, do not coerce\n    lca = merged_lic.get("last_check_at")\n    
merged_lic["last_check_at"] = str(lca) if lca is not None else None\n\n    install_profile = 
str(merged.get("install_profile", "public")).strip().lower()\n    if install_profile not in {"public", "developer"}:\n 
       install_profile = "public"\n    merged["install_profile"] = install_profile\n\n    # Migrate old public 
installs that shipped with license disabled / local / blank server.\n    if install_profile == "public" and not 
merged_lic["disabled_by_user"]:\n        merged_lic["enabled"] = True\n        merged_lic["mode"] = "remote"\n        
if not merged_lic["server_url"]:\n            merged_lic["server_url"] = DEFAULT_LICENSE_SERVER_URL\n    if 
install_profile == "developer" and merged_lic["mode"] == "remote" and not merged_lic["server_url"]:\n        
merged_lic["server_url"] = DEFAULT_LICENSE_SERVER_URL\n\n    # Keep flat license_key and license.key identical when 
either is set.\n    _lic_key_raw = str(merged_lic.get("key") or "").strip()\n    _flat_raw = 
str(merged.get("license_key") or "").strip()\n    _canon = ""\n    try:\n        if _lic_key_raw:\n            _canon 
= validate_license_key(_lic_key_raw)\n        elif _flat_raw:\n            _canon = validate_license_key(_flat_raw)\n  
  except ConfigError:\n        _canon = ""\n    merged["license_key"] = _canon\n    merged_lic["key"] = _canon\n    
merged["license"] = merged_lic\n\n    # YesCaptcha API key (stored verbatim; not validated beyond length)\n    
merged["yescaptcha_key"] = str(merged.get("yescaptcha_key") or "").strip()[:256]\n\n    # Webhook tags (user-defined 
labels shown in webhook embeds)\n    raw_tags = merged.get("webhook_tags")\n    if not isinstance(raw_tags, list):\n   
     raw_tags = []\n    merged["webhook_tags"] = [str(t).strip()[:80] for t in raw_tags if str(t).strip()][:20]\n\n    
# Package start times (ISO timestamps of last launch per package)\n    raw_start_times = 
merged.get("package_start_times")\n    if not isinstance(raw_start_times, dict):\n        raw_start_times = {}\n    
merged["package_start_times"] = {\n        k: str(v)\n        for k, v in raw_start_times.items()\n        if 
is_valid_package_name(str(k))\n    }\n\n    opt_default = default_config()["optimization"]\n    raw_opt = 
merged.get("optimization")\n    if not isinstance(raw_opt, dict):\n        raw_opt = {}\n    optimization: dict[str, 
Any] = dict(opt_default)\n    optimization.update({k: v for k, v in raw_opt.items() if k in optimization})\n    
optimization["low_graphics_enabled"] = _as_bool(optimization.get("low_graphics_enabled", True))\n    
optimization["keep_screen_awake"] = _as_bool(optimization.get("keep_screen_awake", True))\n    
optimization["reduce_android_animations"] = _as_bool(optimization.get("reduce_android_animations", True))\n    
merged["optimization"] = optimization\n\n    sup_default = default_config()["supervisor"]\n    raw_sup = 
merged.get("supervisor")\n    if not isinstance(raw_sup, dict):\n        raw_sup = {}\n    supervisor: dict[str, Any] 
= dict(sup_default)\n    supervisor.update({k: v for k, v in raw_sup.items() if k in supervisor})\n    
supervisor["enabled"] = _as_bool(supervisor.get("enabled", True))\n    supervisor["auto_reopen_enabled"] = 
_as_bool(supervisor.get("auto_reopen_enabled", True))\n    supervisor["auto_reconnect_enabled"] = 
_as_bool(supervisor.get("auto_reconnect_enabled", True))\n    supervisor["launch_grace_seconds"] = _as_int(\n        
"supervisor.launch_grace_seconds", supervisor.get("launch_grace_seconds"), 5, 600\n    )\n    
supervisor["health_check_interval_seconds"] = _as_int(\n        "supervisor.health_check_interval_seconds",\n        
supervisor.get("health_check_interval_seconds"),\n        MIN_HEALTH_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS,\n        3600,\n    )\n   
 supervisor["restart_backoff_seconds"] = _as_int(\n        "supervisor.restart_backoff_seconds", 
supervisor.get("restart_backoff_seconds"), 5, 600\n    )\n    supervisor["max_restart_attempts_per_hour"] = _as_int(\n 
       "supervisor.max_restart_attempts_per_hour", supervisor.get("max_restart_attempts_per_hour"), 1, 200\n    )\n    
merged["supervisor"] = supervisor\n\n    return {key: merged[key] for key in default_config().keys()}\n\n\ndef 
load_config(config_path: Path = CONFIG_PATH) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    ensure_app_dirs()\n    if not 
config_path.exists():\n        cfg = validate_config(default_config())\n        save_config(cfg, 
config_path=config_path)\n        return cfg\n    try:\n        loaded = 
json.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))\n    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:\n        raise 
ConfigError(f"failed to read config: {exc}") from exc\n    return validate_config(loaded)\n\n\ndef 
save_config(config_data: dict[str, Any], config_path: Path = CONFIG_PATH) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    ensure_app_dirs()\n  
  validated = validate_config(config_data)\n    config_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    
config_path.write_text(json.dumps(validated, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\\n", encoding="utf-8")\n    
db.upsert_config(validated)\n    return validated\n\n\ndef safe_config_view(config_data: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, 
Any]:\n    view = dict(config_data)\n    view["launch_url"] = mask_launch_url(view.get("launch_url")) or ""\n    
view["private_server_url"] = mask_launch_url(view.get("private_server_url")) or ""\n    _pkgs = 
view.get("roblox_packages")\n    if isinstance(_pkgs, list):\n        masked_pkgs: list[Any] = []\n        for item in 
_pkgs:\n            if isinstance(item, dict):\n                row = dict(item)\n                
row["private_server_url"] = mask_launch_url(row.get("private_server_url")) or ""\n                
masked_pkgs.append(row)\n            else:\n                masked_pkgs.append(item)\n        view["roblox_packages"] 
= masked_pkgs\n    view["webhook_url"] = mask_webhook_url(view.get("webhook_url"))\n    view["license_key"] = 
mask_license_key(view.get("license_key", ""))\n    view["yescaptcha_key"] = "***" if view.get("yescaptcha_key") else 
""\n    if isinstance(view.get("license"), dict):\n        lic_view = dict(view["license"])\n        lic_view["key"] = 
mask_license_key(lic_view.get("key", ""))\n        view["license"] = lic_view\n    return view\n\n"""Shared constants

8\nROOT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 6\nLOG_MAX_BYTES = 1_000_000\nLOG_BACKUP_COUNT = 3\n\nPACKAGE_NAME_REGEX = 
r"^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]*(\\.[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]*)+$"\n\nTERMUX_BOOT_SCRIPT = Path.home() / ".termux" / "boot" / 
"deng-tool-rejoin.sh"\n\nLICENSE_KEY_PREFIX = "DENG"\n# Canonical display: DENG-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX (16 uppercase hex, 
dashed groups).\nLICENSE_KEY_PATTERN = r"^DENG-[0-9A-F]{4}-[0-9A-F]{4}-[0-9A-F]{4}-[0-9A-F]{4}$"\n\n# Public beta 
license + download API (HTTPS only for production clients).\nDEFAULT_LICENSE_SERVER_URL = 
"https://rejoin.deng.my.id"\nYESCAPTCHA_API_BASE = "https://api.yescaptcha.com"\n\nSENSITIVE_URL_PARAM_NAMES = {\n
