"ticket",\n    "token",\n}\n\nAPP_DIRS = (APP_HOME, DATA_DIR, LOG_DIR, RUN_DIR, LAUNCHER_DIR, CACHE_DIR, 
SNAPSHOT_DIR)\n\n"""SQLite storage for local events, attempts, heartbeats, and state."""\n\nfrom __future__ import 
annotations\n\nimport json\nimport sqlite3\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom 
typing import Any\n\nfrom .constants import DB_PATH\n\nSCHEMA = """\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS config(\n  key TEXT 
PRIMARY KEY,\n  value TEXT NOT NULL,\n  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL\n);\n\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events(\n  id 
INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n  ts TEXT NOT NULL,\n  level TEXT NOT NULL,\n  type TEXT NOT NULL,\n  message TEXT 
NOT NULL,\n  meta_json TEXT\n);\n\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rejoin_attempts(\n  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY 
AUTOINCREMENT,\n  ts TEXT NOT NULL,\n  reason TEXT NOT NULL,\n  package TEXT NOT NULL,\n  launch_mode TEXT NOT NULL,\n 
 masked_launch_url TEXT,\n  root_used INTEGER NOT NULL,\n  success INTEGER NOT NULL,\n  error TEXT\n);\n\nCREATE TABLE 
IF NOT EXISTS heartbeats(\n  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n  ts TEXT NOT NULL,\n  status TEXT NOT NULL,\n  
meta_json TEXT\n);\n\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_state(\n  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  value TEXT NOT NULL,\n  
updated_at TEXT NOT NULL\n);\n"""\n\n\ndef utc_now() -> str:\n    return 
datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()\n\n\ndef connect(db_path: Path = DB_PATH) -> 
sqlite3.Connection:\n    db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    conn = sqlite3.connect(str(db_path), 
timeout=10)\n    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row\n    return conn\n\n\ndef init_db(db_path: Path = DB_PATH) -> None:\n  
  with connect(db_path) as conn:\n        conn.executescript(SCHEMA)\n        conn.commit()\n\n\ndef 
upsert_config(config: dict[str, Any], db_path: Path = DB_PATH) -> None:\n    init_db(db_path)\n    now = utc_now()\n   
 with connect(db_path) as conn:\n        for key, value in config.items():\n            conn.execute(\n                
"INSERT INTO config(key, value, updated_at) VALUES(?, ?, ?) "\n                "ON CONFLICT(key) DO UPDATE SET 
value=excluded.value, updated_at=excluded.updated_at",\n                (key, json.dumps(value, sort_keys=True), 
now),\n            )\n        conn.commit()\n\n\ndef load_config_from_db(db_path: Path = DB_PATH) -> dict[str, Any]:\n 
   init_db(db_path)\n    with connect(db_path) as conn:\n        rows = conn.execute("SELECT key, value FROM 
config").fetchall()\n    result: dict[str, Any] = {}\n    for row in rows:\n        try:\n            
result[row["key"]] = json.loads(row["value"])\n        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n            result[row["key"]] = 
row["value"]\n    return result\n\n\ndef insert_event(level: str, event_type: str, message: str, meta: dict[str, Any] 
| None = None, db_path: Path = DB_PATH) -> None:\n    init_db(db_path)\n    with connect(db_path) as conn:\n        
conn.execute(\n            "INSERT INTO events(ts, level, type, message, meta_json) VALUES(?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",\n          
  (utc_now(), level.upper(), event_type, message, json.dumps(meta or {}, sort_keys=True)),\n        )\n        
conn.commit()\n\n\ndef insert_rejoin_attempt(\n    *,\n    reason: str,\n    package: str,\n    launch_mode: str,\n    
masked_launch_url: str | None,\n    root_used: bool,\n    success: bool,\n    error: str | None = None,\n    db_path: 
Path = DB_PATH,\n) -> None:\n    init_db(db_path)\n    with connect(db_path) as conn:\n        conn.execute(\n         
   "INSERT INTO rejoin_attempts(ts, reason, package, launch_mode, masked_launch_url, root_used, success, error) "\n    
        "VALUES(?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",\n            (utc_now(), reason, package, launch_mode, masked_launch_url, 
int(root_used), int(success), error),\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n\n\ndef insert_heartbeat(status: str, meta: 
dict[str, Any] | None = None, db_path: Path = DB_PATH) -> None:\n    init_db(db_path)\n    with connect(db_path) as 
conn:\n        conn.execute(\n            "INSERT INTO heartbeats(ts, status, meta_json) VALUES(?, ?, ?)",\n           
 (utc_now(), status, json.dumps(meta or {}, sort_keys=True)),\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n\n\ndef 
set_agent_state(key: str, value: Any, db_path: Path = DB_PATH) -> None:\n    init_db(db_path)\n    with 
connect(db_path) as conn:\n        conn.execute(\n            "INSERT INTO agent_state(key, value, updated_at) 
VALUES(?, ?, ?) "\n            "ON CONFLICT(key) DO UPDATE SET value=excluded.value, 
updated_at=excluded.updated_at",\n            (key, json.dumps(value, sort_keys=True), utc_now()),\n        )\n        
conn.commit()\n\n\ndef get_agent_state(key: str, db_path: Path = DB_PATH) -> Any:\n    init_db(db_path)\n    with 
connect(db_path) as conn:\n        row = conn.execute("SELECT value FROM agent_state WHERE key = ?", 
(key,)).fetchone()\n    if not row:\n        return None\n    try:\n        return json.loads(row["value"])\n    
except json.JSONDecodeError:\n        return row["value"]\n\n\ndef latest_row(table: str, db_path: Path = DB_PATH) -> 
dict[str, Any] | None:\n    if table not in {"events", "rejoin_attempts", "heartbeats"}:\n        raise 
ValueError("unsupported latest_row table")\n    init_db(db_path)\n    with connect(db_path) as conn:\n        row = 
conn.execute(f"SELECT * FROM {table} ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1").fetchone()\n    return dict(row) if row else 
None\n\n"""Deferred-install helpers ù kept for backward compatibility and utility functions.\n\nThe active installer 
(``/install/test/latest``) now downloads the full package\ndirectly during ``bash install.sh`` via 
:func:`agent.bootstrap_installer.render_direct_install_bootstrap`.\nNo license key is required at install time; 
license verification happens inside the\nreal tool on first run (inside the menu flow of 
:mod:`agent.commands`).\n\nThis module is retained for:\n- ``resolve_install_api`` and HTTP helper utilities used by 
other code.\n- Graceful handling of old launcher bundles that still have ``.install_requested``.\n\nStdlib only ù safe 
inside the minimal launcher tarball.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport hashlib\nimport json\nimport 
os\nimport sys\nimport tarfile\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom urllib.error import HTTPError\nfrom urllib.request 
import Request, urlopen\n\n# Official production API host (no localhost; used when env and file are 
absent).\nDEFAULT_PUBLIC_INSTALL_API = "https://rejoin.deng.my.id"\n\n# Installer User-Agent ù identifies the DENG 
Rejoin installer to the server.\n# Cloudflare Browser Integrity Check blocks the default Python-urllib UA with\n# 
error 1010.  Using a descriptive but non-browser UA passes the check.\n_INSTALLER_UA = 
"deng-rejoin-installer/1.0"\n\n\ndef _app_home() -> Path:\n    raw = (os.environ.get("DENG_REJOIN_HOME") or 
"").strip()\n    if raw:\n        return Path(raw).expanduser().resolve()\n    return Path.home() / ".deng-tool" / 
"rejoin"\n\n\ndef _install_api_file(app_home: Path) -> Path:\n    return app_home / ".install_api"\n\n\ndef 
resolve_install_api(app_home: Path | None = None) -> str:\n    """Public base URL for install/authorize (no trailing 
slash).\n\n    Priority:\n    1. ``DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API`` environment variable\n    2. 
``$DENG_REJOIN_HOME/.install_api`` (first line), written by the shell installer\n    3. 
:data:`DEFAULT_PUBLIC_INSTALL_API`\n    """\n    env_raw = (os.environ.get("DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API") or "").strip()\n 
   if env_raw:\n        return env_raw.rstrip("/")\n\n    home = app_home if app_home is not None else _app_home()\n   
 p = _install_api_file(home)\n    if p.is_file():\n        try:\n            text = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8", 
errors="replace")\n        except OSError:\n            text = ""\n        line = text.strip().splitlines()[0].strip() 
if text.strip() else ""\n        if line:\n            return line.rstrip("/")\n\n    return 
DEFAULT_PUBLIC_INSTALL_API.rstrip("/")\n\n\ndef _requested_path(app_home: Path) -> Path:\n    return app_home / 
".install_requested"\n\n\ndef _http_json_post(url: str, payload: dict) -> tuple[int, dict, str]:\n    """POST JSON; 
return status, parsed JSON object (may be empty), and raw response text.\n\n    Sends a descriptive User-Agent so 
Cloudflare Browser Integrity Check does not\n    block the request with error 1010 (which it does for the default 
Python-urllib UA).\n    """\n    data = json.dumps(payload).encode("utf-8")\n    req = Request(\n        url,\n        
data=data,\n        headers={\n            "Content-Type": "application/json",\n            "Accept": 
"application/json",\n            "User-Agent": _INSTALLER_UA,\n        },\n        method="POST",\n    )\n    try:\n   
     with urlopen(req, timeout=120) as resp:  # noqa: S310\n            raw = resp.read().decode("utf-8", 
errors="replace")\n            try:\n                body = json.loads(raw) if raw.strip() else {}\n            except 
json.JSONDecodeError:\n                body = {}\n            return int(getattr(resp, "status", 200) or 200), body, 
raw\n    except HTTPError as exc:\n        raw = exc.read().decode("utf-8", errors="replace")\n        try:\n          
  body = json.loads(raw) if raw.strip() else {}\n        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n            body = {}\n        
return int(exc.code), body, raw\n\n\ndef _safe_response_snippet(raw: str, max_len: int = 500) -> str:\n    if not 
raw:\n        return ""\n    cleaned = " ".join(raw.split())\n    if len(cleaned) > max_len:\n        return 
cleaned[:max_len] + "à"\n    return cleaned\n\n\ndef _is_cloudflare_block(status: int, body: dict, raw: str) -> 
bool:\n    """Detect a Cloudflare edge-block response (BIC / WAF / Bot Fight / 1010).\n\n    Cloudflare returns HTML 
when it blocks a request ù the body is never valid JSON\n    from our backend.  We detect this by looking for 
characteristic CF markers in\n    the raw response text combined with the HTTP 403 status.\n    """\n    if status != 
403:\n        return False\n    raw_lower = (raw or "").lower()\n    if body:\n        return False\n    cf_markers = 
("cloudflare", "error code: 1010", "error code:1010", "cf-ray", "attention required")\n    return any(m in raw_lower 
for m in cf_markers) or "<html" in raw_lower\n\n\ndef _is_server_side_error(status: int, body: dict, raw: str) -> 
bool:  # noqa: ARG001\n    """Return True when retrying the same key cannot resolve the error.\n\n    Server-side 
errors (build not configured, artifact missing, server unavailable)\n    are permanent until the server is fixed ù 
re-prompting the user for the same\n    key would loop forever and confuse them.\n    """\n    if status >= 500:\n     
   return True\n    result = str(body.get("result") or "").strip().lower()\n    if result in {"server_unavailable", 
"forbidden", "no_release", "missing_version"}:\n        return True\n    if result == "not_found":\n        msg_lower 
= str(body.get("message") or "").strip().lower()\n        key_phrases = ("key not found", "check the key", "not found. 
check")\n        return not any(p in msg_lower for p in key_phrases)\n    return False\n\n\ndef 
describe_install_authorize_failure(\n    status: int,\n    body: dict | None,\n    raw: str | None,\n) -> str:\n    
"""One-line message for stderr when POST /api/install/authorize did not return active.\n\n    Uses ``message``, 
``error``, ``detail``, ``result``, ``code`` from JSON when present.\n    If the response is a Cloudflare edge-block 
(error code 1010 / BIC / WAF), returns a\n    clear human-readable explanation instead of dumping HTML.  Never echoes 
secrets that\n    are not in the server response (license key is only client-side and not passed here).\n    """\n    
body = body or {}\n    raw = raw or ""\n\n    if _is_cloudflare_block(status, body, raw):\n        return (\n          
  "Install request was blocked by server protection (Cloudflare) before license "\n            "verification could 
run.\\n"\n            "This is NOT a license key issue ù the network/security layer rejected the "\n            
f"request before it reached the server [HTTP {status} ù error code 1010].\\n"\n            "Contact support at 
https://rejoin.deng.my.id if this persists."\n        )\n\n    msg = str(body.get("message") or "").strip()\n    err = 
str(body.get("error") or "").strip()\n    detail = str(body.get("detail") or "").strip()\n    res = 
str(body.get("result") or "").strip()\n    code = body.get("code")\n\n    parts: list[str] = []\n\n    if msg and 
msg.lower() not in {"install denied.", "install denied"}:\n        parts.append(msg)\n    if err and err not in parts 
and err.lower() not in " ".join(parts).lower():\n        parts.append(err)\n    if detail and detail not in " 
".join(parts):\n        parts.append(detail)\n\n    if res and res != "active":\n        blob = "; ".join(parts)\n     
   if res not in blob and (not msg or res not in msg):\n            parts.append(f"result={res}")\n\n    if code is 
not None and str(code).strip() != "":\n        c_str = str(code).strip()\n        if c_str not in " ".join(parts):\n   
         parts.append(f"code={c_str}")\n\n    if not parts:\n        snippet = _safe_response_snippet(raw)\n        if 
snippet:\n            parts.append(f"response: {snippet}")\n        elif status:\n            parts.append(f"empty 
body (HTTP {status})")\n\n    headline = "; ".join(parts) if parts else "unknown error"\n    out = f"Install denied: 
{headline}"\n    if status >= 400 and f"HTTP {status}" not in out and f"{status}" not in headline:\n        out += f" 
[HTTP {status}]"\n    return out\n\n\ndef _http_download(url: str, dest: Path) -> None:\n    req = Request(url, 
headers={"User-Agent": _INSTALLER_UA})\n    with urlopen(req, timeout=300) as resp:  # noqa: S310\n        
dest.write_bytes(resp.read())\n\n\ndef _sha256_file(path: Path) -> str:\n    h = hashlib.sha256()\n    with 
path.open("rb") as f:\n        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(65536), b""):\n            h.update(chunk)\n    return 
h.hexdigest()\n\n\ndef _extract_tar_gz(archive: Path, dest: Path) -> None:\n    with tarfile.open(archive, "r:gz") as 
tf:\n        tf.extractall(dest)\n\n\ndef run() -> int:\n    """Handle the old launcher stub entrypoint.\n\n    The 
current installer downloads the full package directly during ``bash install.sh``\n    and does not use the 
deferred-install mechanism.  If this function is reached, the\n    user has an **old launcher bundle** (installed 
before the direct-install update).\n\n    Direct them to re-run the installer to get the full package.\n    """\n    
app_home = _app_home()\n    marker = _requested_path(app_home)\n\n    reinstall_cmd = "curl -fsSL 
https://rejoin.deng.my.id/install/test/latest -o install.sh && bash install.sh"\n\n    if marker.is_file():\n        
print(\n            "Your launcher is outdated and cannot complete the install.\\n"\n            "Please re-run the 
installer to download the full DENG Tool: Rejoin package:\\n"\n            f"\\n  {reinstall_cmd}\\n",\n            
file=sys.stderr,\n        )\n    else:\n        # No marker ù tool may already be installed but pointing to this 
stub.\n        main_py = app_home / "agent" / "deng_tool_rejoin.py"\n        if main_py.is_file():\n            # Real 
entrypoint is present; exec it directly.\n            exe = sys.executable\n            os.execv(exe, [exe, 
str(main_py), *sys.argv[1:]])\n            raise RuntimeError("execv returned unexpectedly")  # pragma: no cover\n     
   print(\n            "DENG Tool: Rejoin is not fully installed.\\n"\n            "Run the installer to set up the 
full package:\\n"\n            f"\\n  {reinstall_cmd}\\n",\n            file=sys.stderr,\n        )\n    return 
1\n\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    raise SystemExit(run())\n\n#!/usr/bin/env python3\n"""DENG Tool: Rejoin CLI 
entrypoint."""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n# 
[DENG_REJOIN_SEGFAULT_FIX] probe_id=p-ea167faf5f; supersedes probe p-9e3f2a8d1c.\n# faulthandler with file=<open_fd> + 
all_threads=True caused SIGSEGV on\n# Python 3.13 ARM/Termux in threaded processes (supervisor spawns multiple\n# 
worker threads; faulthandler\'s all_threads walk races with thread teardown).\n# The open file descriptor was also 
inherited by every su/am subprocess, leaking\n# it into root commands.  Removed entirely ù Python 3.13 prints native 
SIGSEGV\n# traces to stderr by default without any faulthandler setup.\n\nif __package__ in {None, ""}:\n    
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]))\n    from agent.commands import main\nelse:\n    from 
.commands import main\n\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n\n"""Server-side persistence for 
dev-probe uploads.\n\nWe keep each probe under ``data/dev_probes/`` as a single JSON file 
named\n``p-<short-id>.json``.  The operator reads them directly off the PM2 host\nfilesystem; the HTTP layer only 
writes.\n\nDesign rules:\n\n* Never trust the client to choose an id ù we generate one server-side.\n* Keep storage 
cheap; we rotate when the directory exceeds 200 files by\n  deleting the oldest.  No DB.\n* Reads return the parsed 
JSON dict so the API can render it as-is.\n* All paths derived from a single PROBE_ROOT.  Override via env var\n  
``DENG_DEV_PROBE_DIR`` so tests can isolate.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport 
os\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import 
Any\n\nDEFAULT_PROBE_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "data" / "dev_probes"\nMAX_PROBES = 200\n\n\ndef 
_root() -> Path:\n    override = os.environ.get("DENG_DEV_PROBE_DIR")\n    return Path(override) if override else 
DEFAULT_PROBE_ROOT\n\n\ndef _ensure_root() -> Path:\n    p = _root()\n    p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    
return p\n\n\ndef _next_id() -> str:\n    return "p-" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:10]\n\n\ndef _rotate(root: Path) -> None:\n  
  """Delete oldest files when count exceeds ``MAX_PROBES``."""\n    try:\n        files = 
sorted(root.glob("p-*.json"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime)\n    except OSError:\n        return\n    excess = 
len(files) - MAX_PROBES\n    for f in files[:excess]:\n        try:\n            f.unlink()\n        except OSError:\n 
           pass\n\n\ndef store_probe(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[str, Path]:\n    """Persist *payload* and 
return (probe_id, file path).\n\n    Adds ``received_at_iso`` so the operator can correlate against PM2 logs.\n    
"""\n    root = _ensure_root()\n    probe_id = _next_id()\n    enriched = dict(payload)\n    
enriched["received_at_iso"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")\n    enriched["probe_id"] = 
probe_id\n    path = root / f"{probe_id}.json"\n    # Write atomically by writing to a temp file then renaming.\n    
tmp = path.with_suffix(".tmp")\n    tmp.write_text(json.dumps(enriched, indent=2, sort_keys=True), encoding="utf-8")\n 
   tmp.replace(path)\n    _rotate(root)\n    return probe_id, path\n\n\ndef read_probe(probe_id: str) -> dict[str, 
Any] | None:\n    """Return the JSON dict for ``probe_id`` or None when missing/invalid."""\n    root = _root()\n    
path = root / f"{probe_id}.json"\n    if not path.is_file():\n        return None\n    try:\n        return 
json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))\n    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n        return 
None\n\n\ndef list_probes(limit: int = 50) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    """Return up-to ``limit`` probe metadata 
entries, newest first."""\n    root = _root()\n    if not root.is_dir():\n        return []\n    try:\n        files = 
sorted(root.glob("p-*.json"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)\n    except OSError:\n        return []\n 
   out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n    for path in files[:limit]:\n        try:\n            stat = path.stat()\n      
  except OSError:\n            continue\n        out.append({\n            "probe_id": path.stem,\n            
"size_bytes": stat.st_size,\n            "mtime_iso": datetime.fromtimestamp(stat.st_mtime, tz=timezone.utc)\n         
       .strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),\n        })\n    return out\n\n"""Doctor diagnostics for Termux, Android, 
root, config, DB, and locks."""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport platform\nimport sys\nfrom dataclasses

       "Account names are optional, but adding usernames or Main/Alt names makes the start table easier to read.",\n   
         )\n        )\n    else:\n        items.append(_item("FAIL", "Selected Roblox packages", "not configured",

"Discord webhook", "disabled", ""))\n\n    if cfg.get("webhook_enabled") and cfg.get("webhook_snapshot_enabled"):\n

launch commands are required for rejoin attempts.",\n        )\n    )\n\n    try:\n        db.init_db(DB_PATH)\n       
 items.append(_item("PASS", "SQLite database", str(DB_PATH), ""))\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 -

str(LOG_DIR), "Run setup again."))\n\n    manager = LockManager()\n    pid = read_pid()\n    if 
manager.is_running():\n        items.append(_item("WARN", "Agent lock", f"agent already running with PID {pid}", "Use 
stop before starting another copy."))\n    elif pid:\n        items.append(_item("WARN", "Agent lock", f"stale or 
unconfirmed PID {pid}", "Run stop to clean stale state."))\n    else:\n        items.append(_item("PASS", "Agent 
lock", "no duplicate agent detected", ""))\n\n    return items\n\n\ndef summarize(items: list[DoctorItem]) -> 
dict[str, int]:\n    return {\n        "PASS": sum(1 for item in items if item.status == "PASS"),\n        "WARN": 
sum(1 for item in items if item.status == "WARN"),\n        "FAIL": sum(1 for item in items if item.status == 
"FAIL"),\n    }\n\n\ndef print_doctor(items: list[DoctorItem]) -> None:\n    print("Doctor checks your Termux, 
Android, Roblox, and DENG setup.")\n    print("PASS means OK. WARN means usable but needs attention. FAIL means a 
feature cannot work yet.")\n    print()\n    for item in items:\n        print(f"[{item.status}] {item.name}: 
{item.detail}")\n        if item.suggestion and item.status != "PASS":\n            print(f"       Suggestion: 
{item.suggestion}")\n    summary = summarize(items)\n    print()\n    print(f"Summary: PASS={summary[\'PASS\']} 
WARN={summary[\'WARN\']} FAIL={summary[\'FAIL\']}")\n\n"""Tiny shared cache for ``dumpsys`` / ``cmd`` outputs.\n\nWhy 
this exists\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nEach 
supervisor worker thread independently calls ``dumpsys window windows``,\n``dumpsys activity activities``, ``dumpsys 
SurfaceFlinger``, and\n``current_foreground_package``.  Every dumpsys call is a binder transaction\nthat can take 1-5 
seconds on a cloud phone, and binder serializes calls\nthrough ``system_server`` ù so with 3 packages ╫ 5 dumpsys 
calls ╫ 2 s each\n= ~30 seconds before the first row\'s state can transition out of\n"Preparing".  The user sees a 
tool stuck in Preparing.\n\nThis module exposes a 2-3 second TTL cache keyed by the command argv.\nMultiple threads 
can ask for the same dumpsys output and only one process\nactually runs; the rest read the cached text.\n\nThe cache 
is intentionally TINY and short-lived ù Android state genuinely\nchanges every few seconds, so we don\'t want to serve 
stale data for more\nthan a heartbeat or two.  Tests and tools that need a fresh probe can\ncall :func:`invalidate` or 
pass ``ttl=0`` to :func:`cached_run`.\n\nNever raises.  Safe across threads.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import 
annotations\n\nimport threading\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom typing import Callable, 
Sequence\n\nDEFAULT_TTL: float = 2.5\n\n# Per-key locks so that simultaneous calls for the same dumpsys output\n# 
coalesce into a single child process instead of N concurrent ones.\n_KEY_LOCKS: dict[tuple[str, ...], threading.Lock] 
= {}\n_KEY_LOCKS_GUARD = threading.Lock()\n\n_CACHE: dict[tuple[str, ...], tuple[float, bool, str]] = {}\n_CACHE_LOCK 
= threading.Lock()\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass CachedResult:\n    ok: bool\n    stdout: str\n\n\ndef 
_key_lock(key: tuple[str, ...]) -> threading.Lock:\n    with _KEY_LOCKS_GUARD:\n        lock = _KEY_LOCKS.get(key)\n   
     if lock is None:\n            lock = threading.Lock()\n            _KEY_LOCKS[key] = lock\n        return 
lock\n\n\ndef cached_run(\n    args: Sequence[str],\n    runner: Callable[[Sequence[str]], CachedResult],\n    *,\n    
ttl: float = DEFAULT_TTL,\n) -> CachedResult:\n    """Return cached result for *args*; otherwise call 
``runner(args)``.\n\n    ``runner`` must take the argv and return a :class:`CachedResult`.  It\n    is invoked under a 
per-key lock, so concurrent requests for the same\n    argv coalesce into one child process.\n\n    Setting ``ttl`` to 
0 forces a fresh call and updates the cache.\n    """\n    key = tuple(args)\n    now = time.monotonic()\n\n    if ttl 
> 0:\n        with _CACHE_LOCK:\n            hit = _CACHE.get(key)\n            if hit and (now - hit[0]) < ttl:\n     
           return CachedResult(ok=hit[1], stdout=hit[2])\n\n    lock = _key_lock(key)\n    with lock:\n        # 
Re-check inside the lock ù someone else may have populated it.\n        if ttl > 0:\n            with _CACHE_LOCK:\n   
             hit = _CACHE.get(key)\n                if hit and (time.monotonic() - hit[0]) < ttl:\n                    
return CachedResult(ok=hit[1], stdout=hit[2])\n\n        try:\n            result = runner(args)\n        except 
Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            result = CachedResult(ok=False, stdout="")\n\n        with _CACHE_LOCK:\n      
      _CACHE[key] = (time.monotonic(), bool(result.ok), str(result.stdout or ""))\n        return result\n\n\ndef 
invalidate(prefix: Sequence[str] | None = None) -> None:\n    """Drop cached entries; if ``prefix`` is given, only 
matching commands.\n\n    Used by tests and by the worker after a force-stop / am start (so the\n    next read sees 
the new state, not the pre-launch snapshot).\n    """\n    if prefix is None:\n        with _CACHE_LOCK:\n            
_CACHE.clear()\n        return\n    prefix_tup = tuple(prefix)\n    plen = len(prefix_tup)\n    with _CACHE_LOCK:\n    
    for key in list(_CACHE.keys()):\n            if key[:plen] == prefix_tup:\n                del 
_CACHE[key]\n\n"""Best-effort Android experience-state detector for Roblox.\n\nClassifies what Roblox is currently 
doing by combining evidence from:\n  1. dumpsys activity (top/resumed activity name and task stack)\n  2. logcat 
(bounded recent entries for this PID)\n  3. uiautomator dump (optional XML UI hierarchy, graceful failure)\n\nDesign 
goals:\n  * Never raises ù all paths silently degrade to weak evidence.\n  * Never reads secrets (cookies, tokens, 
sessions, account files).\n  * Bounded execution ù each probe has an explicit short timeout.\n  * All side-effects via 
android.run_command which is already testable.\n  * Runs only when transitioning FROM Launching/Joining states, never 
on\n    steady-state healthy polls (no performance cost in normal operation).\n"""\nfrom __future__ import 
annotations\n\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import IntEnum\nfrom typing import 
Optional\n\nfrom . import android\nfrom .config import validate_package_name\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Evidence Levels \u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n#\n# 
Ordered from weakest to strongest.  The detector picks the highest level that\n# any available signal can 
support.\n\n\nclass EvidenceLevel(IntEnum):\n    """Strength of evidence about the current Roblox runtime 
state."""\n\n    PROCESS_ONLY             = 1  # Process exists; foreground/activity unknown\n    FOREGROUND_APP       
    = 2  # Package confirmed as foreground; activity unclear\n    ROBLOX_HOME_OR_LOBBY     = 3  # Signal indicates 
home screen / lobby / menu\n    JOINING_PRIVATE_URL      = 4  # URL intent dispatched; waiting for join\n    
EXPERIENCE_LIKELY_LOADED = 5  # Strong signal: game / experience screen active\n    JOIN_FAILED_OR_HOME      = 6  # 
After URL: evidence shows home/lobby; join likely failed\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass ExperienceEvidence:\n    
"""Evidence record returned by ``detect_experience_state``."""\n\n    level: EvidenceLevel\n    detail: str\n    
source: str           # "logcat", "dumpsys_activity", "uiautomator", "foreground", "none"\n    raw_snippet: str = "" # 
Brief safe excerpt for debug logging (no secrets)\n\n    def is_in_game(self) -> bool:\n        """True when evidence 
strongly suggests an experience/game is loaded."""\n        return self.level == 
EvidenceLevel.EXPERIENCE_LIKELY_LOADED\n\n    def is_home_or_lobby(self) -> bool:\n        """True when evidence 
indicates the app is at home/lobby, not in-game."""\n        return self.level in (\n            
EvidenceLevel.ROBLOX_HOME_OR_LOBBY,\n            EvidenceLevel.JOIN_FAILED_OR_HOME,\n        )\n\n\n# 
\u2500\u2500\u2500 Logcat patterns \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n#\n# Conservative patterns; only fire when clearly distinguishable from other noise.\n\n# 
Signals that indicate Roblox has loaded an experience/game.\n# Sorted by specificity ù more specific 
first.\n_LOGCAT_INGAME_PATTERNS: list[re.Pattern[str]] = [\n    re.compile(r"\\bGameLoaded\\b", re.I),\n    
re.compile(r"\\bJoinedGame\\b|\\bJoinedPlace\\b", re.I),\n    
re.compile(r"\\bPlaceLauncher\\b.*\\b(join|joined|connected)\\b", re.I),\n    
re.compile(r"\\bGameJoin(Received|Success)\\b", re.I),\n    
re.compile(r"\\b(DataModel|LuaGameScript|GameClient)\\b.*\\b(ready|loaded|start)\\b", re.I),\n    
re.compile(r"\\bReplicationInfo\\b", re.I),\n    re.compile(r"\\bClientDataModel\\b.*\\b(creat|ready|loaded)\\b", 
re.I),\n    re.compile(r"\\bRoblox.*Experience.*load", re.I),\n]\n\n# Signals that indicate Roblox is at home screen / 
lobby / menu.\n_LOGCAT_HOME_PATTERNS: list[re.Pattern[str]] = [\n    
re.compile(r"\\b(HomeScene|HomeMenu|HomeScreen|LuaHome)\\b", re.I),\n    
re.compile(r"\\bRomarkApp\\b.*\\b(home|ready|launch)\\b", re.I),\n    
re.compile(r"\\b(LaunchingToHome|ReturnToHome|BackToHome)\\b", re.I),\n    
re.compile(r"\\b(BootstrapScene|LandingScene|UniverseScene)\\b", re.I),\n    
re.compile(r"\\bAppBootstrap\\b.*\\b(home|ready)\\b", re.I),\n]\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Dumpsys / Activity patterns \u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n# Activity class name fragments \u2192 in-game\n_ACTIVITY_INGAME_FRAGMENTS = 
(\n    "gameactivity",\n    "robloxgame",\n    "gameclient",\n    "experienceactivity",\n    "placejoin",\n)\n\n# 
Activity class name fragments \u2192 home / lobby\n_ACTIVITY_HOME_FRAGMENTS = (\n    "splashactivity",\n    
"mainactivity",\n    "launchactivity",\n    "launcheractivity",\n    "homeactivity",\n    "landingactivity",\n    
"bootstrapactivity",\n)\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 UIAutomator patterns \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n# Text nodes in the home/lobby UI.  If multiple of these are visible at once,\n# it 
strongly suggests the home screen (not an experience).\n_UIA_HOME_TEXT = re.compile(\n    
r\'text="(Home|Discover|Play|Friends|Avatar|Robux|Shop|Activity Feed|Profile)"\',\n    re.I,\n)\n\n# Text that 
indicates game/experience HUD is active.\n_UIA_INGAME_TEXT = re.compile(\n    r\'text="(Leave Game|Back to 
Home|Respawn|Report Abuse|Settings|Reset Character)"\',\n    re.I,\n)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Individual probes \u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef 
_pid_for_package(package: str) -> Optional[str]:\n    res = android.run_command(["pidof", package], timeout=4)\n    if 
res.ok and res.stdout.strip():\n        return res.stdout.strip().split()[0]\n    return None\n\n\ndef 
_probe_logcat(package: str, pid: Optional[str] = None) -> Optional[ExperienceEvidence]:\n    """Read a short bounded 
logcat window and look for game/home signals."""\n    try:\n        # Prefer PID-scoped logcat to avoid noise from 
other packages.\n        if pid:\n            res = android.run_command(["logcat", "-d", "-t", "80", "--pid", pid], 
timeout=5)\n        else:\n            res = android.run_command(["logcat", "-d", "-t", "100"], timeout=5)\n\n        
if not res.ok or not res.stdout.strip():\n            return None\n\n        blob = res.stdout[-6000:]  # cap to last 
~6 KB\n\n        # Check for in-game signals first (higher specificity).\n        for rx in _LOGCAT_INGAME_PATTERNS:\n 
           m = rx.search(blob)\n            if m:\n                snip = m.group(0)[:80]\n                return 
ExperienceEvidence(\n                    level=EvidenceLevel.EXPERIENCE_LIKELY_LOADED,\n                    
detail="logcat: in-game signal detected",\n                    source="logcat",\n                    
raw_snippet=snip,\n                )\n\n        # Check for home/lobby signals.\n        for rx in 
_LOGCAT_HOME_PATTERNS:\n            m = rx.search(blob)\n            if m:\n                snip = m.group(0)[:80]\n   
             return ExperienceEvidence(\n                    level=EvidenceLevel.ROBLOX_HOME_OR_LOBBY,\n               
     detail="logcat: home/lobby signal detected",\n                    source="logcat",\n                    
raw_snippet=snip,\n                )\n\n        return None  # logcat available but no specific signal\n    except 
Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return None\n\n\ndef _probe_dumpsys_activity(package: str) -> 
Optional[ExperienceEvidence]:\n    """Read dumpsys activity to determine the top resumed activity class."""\n    
try:\n        # `dumpsys activity activities` gives task/activity stack.\n        res = 
android.run_command(["dumpsys", "activity", "activities"], timeout=6)\n        if not res.ok or not 
res.stdout.strip():\n            return None\n\n        # Find lines that reference the target package and contain an 
activity component.\n        pkg_lines: list[str] = []\n        for line in res.stdout.splitlines():\n            if 
package in line:\n                pkg_lines.append(line)\n            if len(pkg_lines) > 60:\n                
break\n\n        blob = "\\n".join(pkg_lines)\n        if not blob.strip():\n            return None\n\n        # 
Extract activity class name fragments from component strings like\n        # "com.roblox.client/.SplashActivity" or 
"com.roblox.client/GameActivity".\n        component_rx = re.compile(\n            r"(?:" + re.escape(package) + 
r"/|component=" + re.escape(package) + r"/)"\n            r"([A-Za-z0-9._$]+)",\n        )\n        found_activities: 
list[str] = [\n            m.group(1).lower() for m in component_rx.finditer(blob)\n        ]\n\n        for act in 
found_activities:\n            for fragment in _ACTIVITY_INGAME_FRAGMENTS:\n                if fragment in act:\n      
              return ExperienceEvidence(\n                        level=EvidenceLevel.EXPERIENCE_LIKELY_LOADED,\n      
                  detail=f"dumpsys: in-game activity ({act})",\n                        source="dumpsys_activity",\n   
                     raw_snippet=act[:80],\n                    )\n            for fragment in 
_ACTIVITY_HOME_FRAGMENTS:\n                if fragment in act:\n                    return ExperienceEvidence(\n       
                 level=EvidenceLevel.ROBLOX_HOME_OR_LOBBY,\n                        detail=f"dumpsys: home/lobby 
activity ({act})",\n                        source="dumpsys_activity",\n                        
raw_snippet=act[:80],\n                    )\n\n        # Also try `dumpsys activity top` for a faster single-activity 
snapshot.\n        top_res = android.run_command(["dumpsys", "activity", "top"], timeout=5)\n        if top_res.ok and 
package in top_res.stdout:\n            top_lines = [ln for ln in top_res.stdout.splitlines() if package in ln][:20]\n 
           top_blob = "\\n".join(top_lines).lower()\n            for fragment in _ACTIVITY_INGAME_FRAGMENTS:\n         
       if fragment in top_blob:\n                    return ExperienceEvidence(\n                        
level=EvidenceLevel.EXPERIENCE_LIKELY_LOADED,\n                        detail=f"dumpsys_top: in-game activity fragment 
({fragment})",\n                        source="dumpsys_activity",\n                        raw_snippet=fragment,\n    
                )\n            for fragment in _ACTIVITY_HOME_FRAGMENTS:\n                if fragment in top_blob:\n   
                 return ExperienceEvidence(\n                        level=EvidenceLevel.ROBLOX_HOME_OR_LOBBY,\n       
                 detail=f"dumpsys_top: home activity fragment ({fragment})",\n                        
source="dumpsys_activity",\n                        raw_snippet=fragment,\n                    )\n\n        return 
None  # dumpsys ran, package found, but no specific activity signals\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
return None\n\n\ndef _probe_uiautomator(package: str) -> Optional[ExperienceEvidence]:\n    """Parse uiautomator dump 
to look for home vs in-game UI signals.\n\n    Gracefully returns None if uiautomator is unavailable or fails.\n    
Reads /dev/stdout output only ù never writes files.\n    """\n    try:\n        # Try uiautomator dump to stdout.  
Some devices support --compressed,\n        # others do not ù try both and fall back.\n        for extra in 
(["--compressed"], []):\n            res = android.run_command(\n                ["uiautomator", "dump", 
"/dev/stdout"] + extra,\n                timeout=8,\n            )\n            if res.ok and "<hierarchy" in 
res.stdout:\n                break\n        else:\n            return None\n\n        xml = res.stdout[:32000]  # cap 
to 32 KB to avoid huge parse\n\n        # Only consider nodes that belong to the target package.\n        if package 
not in xml:\n            return None\n\n        # Check for in-game HUD text (e.g. "Leave Game", "Reset Character").\n 
       ingame_hits = len(_UIA_INGAME_TEXT.findall(xml))\n        home_hits   = len(_UIA_HOME_TEXT.findall(xml))\n\n    
    if ingame_hits >= 1:\n            return ExperienceEvidence(\n                
level=EvidenceLevel.EXPERIENCE_LIKELY_LOADED,\n                detail=f"uiautomator: {ingame_hits} in-game HUD 
element(s) found",\n                source="uiautomator",\n                raw_snippet=f"ingame_hits={ingame_hits}",\n 
           )\n\n        if home_hits >= 2:\n            # Seeing at least 2 known lobby-nav items \u2192 likely home 
screen.\n            return ExperienceEvidence(\n                level=EvidenceLevel.ROBLOX_HOME_OR_LOBBY,\n           
     detail=f"uiautomator: {home_hits} home navigation element(s) found",\n                source="uiautomator",\n     
           raw_snippet=f"home_hits={home_hits}",\n            )\n\n        return None  # uiautomator ran but no clear 
signal\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return None\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Main public API \u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef 
detect_experience_state(\n    package: str,\n    *,\n    url_launched: bool = False,\n) -> ExperienceEvidence:\n    
"""Return the best available evidence about the current Roblox state.\n\n    Probes dumpsys activity, logcat, and 
optionally uiautomator in order of\n    reliability/speed.  Never raises.  Returns a weak ``FOREGROUND_APP``\n    
evidence record if no Android tools are available (e.g. on Windows during\n    unit tests).\n\n    Args:\n        
package: The Roblox Android package name.\n        url_launched: True when a private URL intent was the last launch 
method.\n                      Influences interpretation but not the raw detection.\n    """\n    try:\n        
package = validate_package_name(package)\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return ExperienceEvidence(\n  
          level=EvidenceLevel.PROCESS_ONLY,\n            detail="invalid package name",\n            source="none",\n  
      )\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 1. dumpsys activity (fastest, most reliable signal) 
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   
 dumpsys_ev = _probe_dumpsys_activity(package)\n    if dumpsys_ev is not None and dumpsys_ev.level >= 
EvidenceLevel.ROBLOX_HOME_OR_LOBBY:\n        # Upgrade JOIN_FAILED_OR_HOME when a URL was recently used and we see 
home evidence.\n        if url_launched and dumpsys_ev.level == EvidenceLevel.ROBLOX_HOME_OR_LOBBY:\n            
return ExperienceEvidence(\n                level=EvidenceLevel.JOIN_FAILED_OR_HOME,\n                
detail=dumpsys_ev.detail + " [after url launch]",\n                source=dumpsys_ev.source,\n                
raw_snippet=dumpsys_ev.raw_snippet,\n            )\n        return dumpsys_ev\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 2. Logcat 
(PID-scoped preferred) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\n    pid = _pid_for_package(package)\n    logcat_ev = _probe_logcat(package, pid=pid)\n    if logcat_ev 
is not None and logcat_ev.level >= EvidenceLevel.ROBLOX_HOME_OR_LOBBY:\n        if url_launched and logcat_ev.level == 
EvidenceLevel.ROBLOX_HOME_OR_LOBBY:\n            return ExperienceEvidence(\n                
level=EvidenceLevel.JOIN_FAILED_OR_HOME,\n                detail=logcat_ev.detail + " [after url launch]",\n           
     source=logcat_ev.source,\n                raw_snippet=logcat_ev.raw_snippet,\n            )\n        return 
logcat_ev\n\n    # UIAutomator probe intentionally disabled: on Termux with App Cloner packages,\n    # `uiautomator 
dump` can trigger SIGSEGV in the calling Python process via the\n    # Android Accessibility Service.  Logcat + 
dumpsys are sufficient for detection.\n    # (probe p-f1a4aaafe5)\n\n    # Merge partial positive evidence from the 
two fast probes.\n    best_partial: Optional[ExperienceEvidence] = None\n    for ev in (dumpsys_ev, logcat_ev):\n      
  if ev is not None:\n            if best_partial is None or ev.level > best_partial.level:\n                
best_partial = ev\n    if best_partial is not None:\n        return best_partial\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 4. Fallback: 
process running but nothing specific detected 
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    #\n    # The health check already 
confirmed the package is running/foreground,\n    # so we know at minimum FOREGROUND_APP.  We cannot claim in-game 
without\n    # evidence, especially after a URL launch.\n    return ExperienceEvidence(\n        
level=EvidenceLevel.FOREGROUND_APP,\n        detail="app is running; no specific experience signal detected",\n        
source="none",\n    )\n\n"""Enable Android freeform / resizable-activity capabilities (root only).\n\nWhy this 
exists\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nOn many 
cloud-phone builds, freeform multi-window is DISABLED at the system\nlevel (``enable_freeform_support=0``).  When 
freeform is disabled, no amount\nof App Cloner XML or per-task resize calls will actually move/resize a\nRoblox clone 
ù the system refuses to honor a non-fullscreen windowing mode\nfor "non-resizable" activities like Roblox.\n\nCheap 
market "resize window" tools work because the FIRST thing they do is\nflip the four global settings below.  Once those 
are set to 1, the system\nallows freeform windows and treats every activity as resizable, so launch\nbounds and ``cmd 
activity resize-task`` actually take effect.\n\nWhat this module does\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nProbes ``settings get global <key>`` 
for each capability flag, then (if root\nis available) writes ``1`` for any that are 0/unset.  Always logs the\nresult 
to the layout-discovery log; never prints publicly.\n\nIdempotent and safe-to-call-many-times.  Never raises.\n\nWe 
deliberately do NOT touch ``adb_enabled``, ``usb_debugging``, any\nSELinux mode, any cookie/token/credential store, or 
any user-data path.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, 
field\nfrom typing import Iterable, Sequence\n\nfrom . import android\n\n_log = 
logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.freeform_enable")\n\n# Global settings that unlock freeform window mode and 
force-resizable apps.\n# Source: AOSP services/core/java/com/android/server/wm/ActivityTaskManagerService\n# 
(force_resizable_activities, enable_freeform_support) and frameworks/base\n# (freeform_window_management).  
development_settings_enabled is the master\n# gate for the other three on many OEM builds.\n_FREEFORM_GLOBAL_KEYS: 
tuple[str, ...] = (\n    "enable_freeform_support",\n    "force_resizable_activities",\n    
"freeform_window_management",\n    "development_settings_enabled",\n)\n\n# These are "secure" namespace mirrors used 
by some OEM forks (Samsung One UI,\n# MIUI, ColorOS, OxygenOS).  Probing both namespaces covers more 
devices.\n_FREEFORM_SECURE_KEYS: tuple[str, ...] = (\n    "enable_freeform_support",\n    
"force_resizable_activities",\n    "freeform_window_management",\n)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass _ProbeResult:\n    key: 
str\n    namespace: str           # "global" | "secure" | "system"\n    before: str | None       # value before write 
(or None if unreadable)\n    after: str | None        # value after write (or None if write failed)\n    wrote: bool = 
False\n    error: str = ""\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass FreeformSetupResult:\n    """Outcome of enabling freeform/resizable 
capabilities."""\n\n    root_available: bool = False\n    root_tool: str | None = None\n    probes: list[_ProbeResult] 
= field(default_factory=list)\n    enabled_keys: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n    already_enabled_keys: 
list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n    failed_keys: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n    @property\n   
 def any_change(self) -> bool:\n        return bool(self.enabled_keys)\n\n    @property\n    def healthy(self) -> 
bool:\n        """True when every required flag is currently 1."""\n        ok_keys = set(self.enabled_keys) | 
set(self.already_enabled_keys)\n        return all(k in ok_keys for k in _FREEFORM_GLOBAL_KEYS)\n\n\ndef 
_settings_get(namespace: str, key: str) -> str | None:\n    """``settings get <ns> <key>`` ù returns stripped value or 
None.\n\n    Uses :func:`android.run_android_command` so that:\n\n    * the bare name ``settings`` is auto-resolved to 
``/system/bin/settings``\n      (Termux\'s ``$PATH`` excludes ``/system/bin``);\n    * the call is auto-retried 
through ``su`` if Samsung One UI returns\n      ``Permission Denial: getCurrentUser ... INTERACT_ACROSS_USERS`` for\n  
    the unprivileged invocation.  Without this, every freeform-capability\n      probe on Samsung devices silently 
reports the flag as unset and the\n      "freeform_caps: ok=0/4" diagnostic remains misleadingly red.\n    """\n    
try:\n        res = android.run_android_command(["settings", "get", namespace, key], timeout=5)\n        if not 
res.ok:\n            return None\n        v = (res.stdout or "").strip()\n        if v == "" or v.lower() == "null":\n 
           return None\n        return v\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return None\n\n\ndef 
_settings_put_root(\n    namespace: str, key: str, value: str, *, root_tool: str\n) -> bool:\n    """``su -c "settings 
put <ns> <key> <value>"`` ù returns True on success."""\n    try:\n        # Use a single-string command via sh -c so 
the SELinux domain matches\n        # what App Ops / settings expects when called from root context.\n        res = 
android.run_root_command(\n            ["sh", "-c", f"settings put {namespace} {key} {value}"],\n            
root_tool=root_tool,\n            timeout=5,\n        )\n        return res.ok\n    except Exception:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n        return False\n\n\ndef _is_truthy(value: str | None) -> bool:\n    if not value:\n        return 
False\n    return value.strip().lower() in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")\n\n\ndef setup_freeform_capabilities(\n    *,\n  
  desired_value: str = "1",\n    keys_global: Iterable[str] = _FREEFORM_GLOBAL_KEYS,\n    keys_secure: Iterable[str] = 
_FREEFORM_SECURE_KEYS,\n) -> FreeformSetupResult:\n    """Probe and (with root) enable freeform-window 
capabilities.\n\n    Never raises.  Always returns a populated :class:`FreeformSetupResult`.\n\n    Pipeline:\n      
1. Detect root.\n      2. For every (namespace, key) pair, ``settings get`` the current value.\n      3. If the value 
is not truthy AND root is available, write\n         ``desired_value`` and read back to verify.\n      4. Record the 
result on the FreeformSetupResult.\n    """\n    out = FreeformSetupResult()\n    try:\n        root = 
android.detect_root()\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        root = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n    
if root is not None and root.available and root.tool:\n        out.root_available = True\n        out.root_tool = 
root.tool\n\n    def _try(ns: str, key: str) -> None:\n        before = _settings_get(ns, key)\n        probe = 
_ProbeResult(key=key, namespace=ns, before=before, after=before)\n        if _is_truthy(before):\n            
out.already_enabled_keys.append(key)\n            out.probes.append(probe)\n            return\n        if not 
out.root_available or not out.root_tool:\n            # Without root we can probe but cannot write.\n            
probe.error = "root unavailable"\n            out.failed_keys.append(key)\n            out.probes.append(probe)\n      
      return\n        wrote = _settings_put_root(ns, key, desired_value, root_tool=out.root_tool)\n        if not 
wrote:\n            probe.error = "settings put failed"\n            out.failed_keys.append(key)\n            
out.probes.append(probe)\n            return\n        after = _settings_get(ns, key)\n        probe.after = after\n    
    probe.wrote = True\n        if _is_truthy(after):\n            out.enabled_keys.append(key)\n        else:\n       
     probe.error = "post-write value still not truthy"\n            out.failed_keys.append(key)\n        
out.probes.append(probe)\n\n    for k in keys_global:\n        _try("global", k)\n    for k in keys_secure:\n        
_try("secure", k)\n\n    _log.debug(\n        "freeform_setup: root=%s enabled=%s already=%s failed=%s",\n        
out.root_available, out.enabled_keys, out.already_enabled_keys, out.failed_keys,\n    )\n    return out\n\n\ndef 
setup_freeform_capabilities_silent() -> tuple[int, int]:\n    """Silent wrapper: returns ``(enabled+already, 
total_global)``.  Never raises."""\n    try:\n        r = setup_freeform_capabilities()\n    except Exception as exc:  
# noqa: BLE001\n        _log.debug("setup_freeform_capabilities_silent error: %s", exc)\n        return 0, 
len(_FREEFORM_GLOBAL_KEYS)\n    ok = len(r.enabled_keys) + len(\n        [k for k in r.already_enabled_keys if k in 
_FREEFORM_GLOBAL_KEYS]\n    )\n    return ok, len(_FREEFORM_GLOBAL_KEYS)\n\n\ndef render_freeform_log_block(result: 
FreeformSetupResult) -> list[str]:\n    """Render lines for the discovery log (no public output)."""\n    lines: 
list[str] = [\n        "== Freeform / resizable-activity capabilities ==",\n        f"  root_available: 
{result.root_available} (tool={result.root_tool})",\n        f"  enabled_keys: {result.enabled_keys}",\n        f"  
already_enabled_keys: {result.already_enabled_keys}",\n        f"  failed_keys: {result.failed_keys}",\n    ]\n    for 
p in result.probes:\n        lines.append(\n            f"  - [{p.namespace}] {p.key}: before={p.before!r} 
after={p.after!r} "\n            f"wrote={p.wrote} error={p.error!r}"\n        )\n    return lines\n\n"""Who may use 
fixed internal test installers (e.g. GET /install/test/latest).\n\nUses env lists only ù no discord.py imports (safe 
for license API).\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport os\nfrom functools import 
lru_cache\n\n\n@lru_cache(maxsize=4)\ndef _parse_env_discord_ids_cached(raw_norm: str) -> frozenset[str]:\n    out: 
set[str] = set()\n    for part in raw_norm.split(","):\n        p = part.strip()\n        if p.isdigit():\n            
out.add(p)\n    return frozenset(out)\n\n\ndef parse_env_discord_ids(raw: str | None) -> frozenset[str]:\n    
"""Comma-separated numeric Discord IDs from an env-style string."""\n    return _parse_env_discord_ids_cached(raw or 
"")\n\n\ndef internal_test_install_allowlisted_discord_ids() -> frozenset[str]:\n    """Union of 
LICENSE_OWNER_DISCORD_IDS and REJOIN_TESTER_DISCORD_IDS."""\n    owners = 
parse_env_discord_ids(os.environ.get("LICENSE_OWNER_DISCORD_IDS"))\n    testers = 
parse_env_discord_ids(os.environ.get("REJOIN_TESTER_DISCORD_IDS"))\n    return frozenset(owners | testers)\n\n\ndef 
is_internal_test_install_allowed(owner_discord_id: str | None) -> bool:\n    """True if *owner_discord_id* may 
authorize ``requested_version=test-latest``."""\n    if owner_discord_id is None:\n        return False\n    oid = 
str(owner_discord_id).strip()\n    if not oid.isdigit():\n        return False\n    return oid in 
internal_test_install_allowlisted_discord_ids()\n\n\ndef clear_install_internal_access_cache() -> None:\n    """For 
tests only ù reset :func:`lru_cache` on env parsers."""\n    _parse_env_discord_ids_cached.cache_clear()\n\n"""Version 
registry + artifact paths for protected installs (data/rejoin_versions.json).\n\nUsed by the license API ù no discord 
imports.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import 
Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom agent.rejoin_versions import default_versions_manifest_path\n\n\ndef 
public_install_base_url() -> str:\n    """HTTPS base for curl installers (no trailing slash)."""\n    for key in 
("REJOIN_PUBLIC_INSTALL_URL", "LICENSE_API_PUBLIC_URL"):\n        raw = (os.environ.get(key) or 
"").strip().rstrip("/")\n        if raw:\n            return raw\n    return "https://rejoin.deng.my.id"\n\n\ndef 
load_registry_rows() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    path = default_versions_manifest_path()\n    if not 
path.is_file():\n        return []\n    try:\n        data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))\n    except 
(OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n        return []\n    return [r for r in data if isinstance(r, dict)]\n\n\ndef 
row_enabled(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n    return row.get("enabled") is not False\n\n\ndef _semver_tuple(version: 
str) -> tuple[int, ...]:\n    v = (version or "").strip().lstrip("v").strip()\n    if not v:\n        return ()\n    
parts: list[int] = []\n    for seg in re.split(r"[^\\d]+", v):\n        if not seg:\n            continue\n        
try:\n            parts.append(int(seg))\n        except ValueError:\n            parts.append(-1)\n    return 
tuple(parts)\n\n\ndef is_public_stable_row(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n    """Row eligible for public 
/install/latest candidate list."""\n    if not row_enabled(row):\n        return False\n    ch = 
str(row.get("channel") or "stable").strip().lower()\n    if ch != "stable":\n        return False\n    vis = 
str(row.get("visibility") or "").strip().lower()\n    if vis in {"admin", "internal", "private", "owner"}:\n        
return False\n    if vis != "public":\n        # Legacy rows without visibility: allow only if visible is not 
explicitly false\n        if row.get("visible") is False:\n            return False\n    ref = 
str(row.get("install_ref") or row.get("ref") or "").strip()\n    if ref.startswith("refs/heads/"):\n        return 
False\n    if ref in {"", "main"}:\n        return False\n    return True\n\n\ndef resolve_latest_public_stable() -> 
dict[str, Any] | None:\n    rows = [r for r in load_registry_rows() if is_public_stable_row(r)]\n    if not rows:\n    
    return None\n    rows.sort(key=lambda r: _semver_tuple(str(r.get("version") or "")), reverse=True)\n    return 
rows[0]\n\n\ndef is_admin_internal_row(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n    """Manifest-only internal builds (e.g. 
main-dev): admin visibility + branch ref."""\n    if not row_enabled(row):\n        return False\n    vis = 
str(row.get("visibility") or "").strip().lower()\n    if vis not in {"admin", "internal", "private", "owner", 
"tester"}:\n        return False\n    ref = str(row.get("install_ref") or row.get("ref") or "").strip()\n    return 
ref.startswith("refs/heads/")\n\n\ndef get_exact_registry_row(version: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n    want = 
(version or "").strip()\n    if not want:\n        return None\n    for row in load_registry_rows():\n        if not 
row_enabled(row):\n            continue\n        if str(row.get("version") or "").strip() == want:\n            return 
row\n    return None\n\n\ndef resolve_requested_public_version(requested: str) -> tuple[dict[str, Any] | None, str | 
None]:\n    """Return (row, error_message). requested is ``latest`` or ``vX.Y.Z``."""\n    req = (requested or 
"").strip().lower()\n    if req == "latest":\n        row = resolve_latest_public_stable()\n        if row is None:\n  
          return None, "No public stable release is configured yet."\n        return row, None\n    row = 
get_exact_registry_row(requested.strip())\n    if row is None:\n        return None, f"Unknown or disabled version: 
{requested}"\n    if not is_public_stable_row(row):\n        return None, "This version is not available for public 
install."\n    return row, None\n\n\ndef artifact_path_for_row(row: dict[str, Any], root: Path) -> Path | None:\n    
rel = str(row.get("artifact_path") or "").strip().strip("/")\n    if not rel:\n        return None\n    candidate = 
(root / rel).resolve()\n    try:\n        candidate.relative_to(root.resolve())\n    except ValueError:\n        
return None\n    return candidate\n\n\ndef get_artifact_root() -> Path | None:\n    for key in 
("REJOIN_ARTIFACT_ROOT", "LICENSE_DOWNLOAD_ROOT"):\n        raw = (os.environ.get(key) or "").strip()\n        if not 
raw:\n            continue\n        p = Path(raw).expanduser().resolve()\n        if p.is_dir():\n            return 
p\n    return None\n\n"""HMAC signatures for internal-only bootstrap URLs (/install/dev/main)."""\n\nfrom __future__ 
import annotations\n\nimport hashlib\nimport hmac\nimport os\nimport time\nfrom urllib.parse import urlencode\n\n\ndef 
signing_secret() -> str:\n    return (os.environ.get("REJOIN_INSTALL_SIGNING_SECRET") or "").strip()\n\n\ndef 
sign_internal_path(subpath: str, *, expires_at: int | None = None, ttl_seconds: int = 86400) -> str:\n    """Return 
query string ``exp=...&sig=...`` for subpath (e.g. ``dev/main``)."""\n    secret = signing_secret()\n    if not 
secret:\n        return ""\n    exp = expires_at if expires_at is not None else int(time.time()) + ttl_seconds\n    
msg = f"{subpath}:{exp}".encode()\n    sig = hmac.new(secret.encode(), msg, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()\n    return 
urlencode({"exp": str(exp), "sig": sig})\n\n\ndef verify_internal_path(subpath: str, exp_str: str, sig: str) -> 
bool:\n    secret = signing_secret()\n    if not secret or not exp_str or not sig:\n        return False\n    try:\n   
     exp = int(exp_str)\n    except ValueError:\n        return False\n    if time.time() > exp:\n        return 
False\n    expected = hmac.new(secret.encode(), f"{subpath}:{exp}".encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()\n    return 
hmac.compare_digest(expected, sig)\n\n"""Sanitized tarball for internal ``main-dev`` protected installs.\n\nBuilt 
offline from the repo tree ù excludes secrets, databases, caches, VCS,\nand paths whose segments suggest credentials 
or sessions.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport datetime as _dt\nimport hashlib\nimport io\nimport 
json\nimport subprocess\nimport tarfile\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nMAIN_DEV_ARCHIVE_REL_PATH = 
"releases/main-dev/deng-tool-rejoin-main-dev.tar.gz"\n\n_TOPLEVEL_DIRS = ("agent", "bot", "scripts", "docs", 
"examples", "assets")\n\n_ROOT_FILES = (\n    "install.sh",\n    "README.md",\n    "VERSION",\n    "SECURITY.md",\n    
"requirements-bot.txt",\n    "requirements.txt",\n    "pyproject.toml",\n)\n\n_SKIP_DIR_NAMES = frozenset(\n    {\n    
    ".git",\n        "__pycache__",\n        ".pytest_cache",\n        ".mypy_cache",\n        "node_modules",\n       
 "htmlcov",\n        ".idea",\n        ".vscode",\n        ".cursor",\n        "logs",\n        "run",\n        
"backups",\n        "launcher",\n        "dist",\n        "build",\n        ".egg-info",\n    
}\n)\n\n_SENSITIVE_MARKERS = ("token", "secret", "password", "credential", "cookie", "session")\n\n\ndef 
path_should_exclude(rel_posix: str) -> bool:\n    """Return True if *rel_posix* must not appear in the internal test 
tarball."""\n    lower = rel_posix.strip().lower().replace("\\\\", "/")\n    if not lower or 
lower.startswith("../"):\n        return True\n    parts = lower.split("/")\n    # Junk / secrets-by-location\n    if 
parts[0] in {"data", "tests"}:\n        return True\n    if lower == ".env" or lower.endswith("/.env"):\n        
return True\n    if lower.endswith((".db", ".sqlite", ".sqlite3")):\n        return True\n    for p in parts:\n        
pl = p.lower()\n        if pl == ".env.example":\n            continue\n        if p in _SKIP_DIR_NAMES or 
(p.startswith(".") and pl != ".env.example"):\n            return True\n        if pl.endswith(".pyc") or 
pl.endswith(".pyo"):\n            return True\n        for m in _SENSITIVE_MARKERS:\n            if m in pl:\n         
       return True\n    # Root-only junk Filenames\n    if lower.endswith(\n        (\n            ".pid",\n           
 ".sock",\n            "pm2-out.log",\n            "pm2-error.log",\n            "ecosystem.config.js.map",\n        
)\n    ):\n        return True\n    return False\n\n\ndef iter_internal_test_pack_files(repo_root: Path) -> 
list[tuple[str, Path]]:\n    """Sorted (archive path posix, absolute file path) pairs."""\n    repo_root = 
repo_root.resolve()\n    out: list[tuple[str, Path]] = []\n\n    for name in _ROOT_FILES:\n        p = repo_root / 
name\n        if p.is_file():\n            arc = name.replace("\\\\", "/")\n            if not 
path_should_exclude(arc):\n                out.append((arc, p))\n\n    for dirname in _TOPLEVEL_DIRS:\n        base = 
repo_root / dirname\n        if not base.is_dir():\n            continue\n        for path in base.rglob("*"):\n       
     if path.is_dir():\n                continue\n            try:\n                rel = 
path.relative_to(repo_root).as_posix()\n            except ValueError:\n                continue\n            if 
path_should_exclude(rel):\n                continue\n            out.append((rel, path))\n\n    out.sort(key=lambda x: 
x[0])\n    return out\n\n\ndef _git_commit_short(repo_root: Path) -> str:\n    """Best-effort: ``git rev-parse 
--short=12 HEAD`` from the repo.\n\n    Returns empty string if git is unavailable or the directory is not a repo.\n   
 """\n    try:\n        res = subprocess.run(\n            ["git", "rev-parse", "--short=12", "HEAD"],\n            
cwd=str(repo_root),\n            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,\n            
text=True,\n            timeout=5,\n        )\n        sha = res.stdout.strip()\n        return sha if res.returncode 
== 0 and sha else ""\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return ""\n\n\ndef _make_build_info_bytes(\n    
repo_root: Path, *, channel: str = "main-dev",\n) -> bytes:\n    """Render the BUILD-INFO.json payload embedded into 
the tarball.\n\n    The hash of the tarball itself is computed AFTER write, so it\'s added\n    to 
``.installed-build.json`` at install time, not here.  This file\n    carries the parts the runtime cannot otherwise 
discover: git commit\n    hash, build time, channel, and a unique probe_id for this build.\n    """\n    import 
hashlib\n\n    commit = _git_commit_short(repo_root)\n    built_at_iso = 
_dt.datetime.now(_dt.timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")\n    built_at_unix = int(time.time())\n    # 
probe_id: short stable ID that uniquely identifies this build.\n    # Derived from commit + timestamp so it differs 
even for same-commit rebuilds.\n    probe_seed = f"{commit}{built_at_unix}{channel}"\n    probe_id = "p-" + 
hashlib.sha256(probe_seed.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]\n    info = {\n        "channel": channel,\n        "git_commit": 
commit,\n        "built_at_iso": built_at_iso,\n        "built_at_unix": built_at_unix,\n        "product": "DENG 
Tool: Rejoin",\n        "artifact_format_version": 2,\n        "probe_id": probe_id,\n    }\n    return 
json.dumps(info, indent=2, sort_keys=True).encode("utf-8")\n\n\ndef build_internal_test_tarball(repo_root: Path, 
output_tar_gz: Path) -> str:\n    """Write gzip tarball and return lowercase SHA-256 hex digest of the file.\n\n    
Also embeds a top-level ``BUILD-INFO.json`` with git commit + build time\n    so the runtime can prove what build it 
is even before the installer\n    drops ``.installed-build.json``.\n    """\n    repo_root = repo_root.resolve()\n    
output_tar_gz = output_tar_gz.resolve()\n    output_tar_gz.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    pairs = 
iter_internal_test_pack_files(repo_root)\n    if not pairs:\n        raise RuntimeError("No files matched internal 
test artifact rules.")\n\n    build_info_bytes = _make_build_info_bytes(repo_root)\n\n    buf = io.BytesIO()\n    
digest = hashlib.sha256()\n    with tarfile.open(fileobj=buf, mode="w:gz", compresslevel=9) as tf:\n        # Sources 
first.\n        for arcname, path in pairs:\n            tf.add(path, arcname=arcname, recursive=False)\n        # 
Then the build-info file.\n        bi = tarfile.TarInfo(name="BUILD-INFO.json")\n        bi.size = 
len(build_info_bytes)\n        bi.mtime = int(time.time())\n        bi.mode = 0o644\n        tf.addfile(bi, 
io.BytesIO(build_info_bytes))\n    raw = buf.getvalue()\n    digest.update(raw)\n    output_tar_gz.write_bytes(raw)\n  
  return digest.hexdigest()\n\n\ndef verify_tarball_exclusions(tar_bytes: bytes) -> None:\n    """Raise AssertionError 
if forbidden names appear inside tarball bytes.\n\n    Also asserts the tarball contains a top-level 
``BUILD-INFO.json`` so\n    every published artifact carries its own build proof.\n    """\n    with 
tarfile.open(fileobj=io.BytesIO(tar_bytes), mode="r:gz") as tf:\n        names = [n for n in tf.getnames() if 
n.rstrip("/")]\n    lowered = [n.lower().replace("\\\\", "/") for n in names]\n    forbidden_roots = 
frozenset({"data", "tests", ".git"})\n    for n in lowered:\n        segs = n.split("/")\n        assert 
forbidden_roots.isdisjoint(segs), n\n        assert "node_modules" not in segs, n\n        assert "__pycache__" not in 
segs, n\n        assert ".pytest_cache" not in segs, n\n        assert not n.endswith(".db"), n\n        assert not 
n.endswith(".sqlite"), n\n        assert not n.endswith(".sqlite3"), n\n        if n == ".env" or 
n.endswith("/.env"):\n            raise AssertionError(f"unexpected env file in tarball: {n}")\n    if 
"BUILD-INFO.json" not in names:\n        raise AssertionError("tarball missing BUILD-INFO.json ù build proof is 
required")\n\n"""DENG Tool: Rejoin ù Development key generator.\n\nUsage (run from project root):\n    python -m 
agent.keygen              # generate one key\n    python -m agent.keygen list         # list all keys in local DB\n    
python -m agent.keygen revoke KEY   # revoke a key\n    python -m agent.keygen reset KEY    # unbind key from 
device\n\nOnly works when DENG_DEV=1 is set or the keydb.json is present.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import 
annotations\n\nimport sys\n\nfrom .keystore import (\n    create_key_in_db,\n    generate_key,\n    list_keys_in_db,\n 
   revoke_key_in_db,\n    unbind_key,\n    KEYDB_PATH,\n    KeyError,  # noqa: A004\n)\n\n\ndef _print_header() -> 
None:\n    print()\n    print("\u2501" * 56)\n    print("  DENG Tool: Rejoin ù Dev Key Generator")\n    print(f"  DB: 
{KEYDB_PATH}")\n    print("\u2501" * 56)\n\n\ndef cmd_generate(note: str = "") -> None:\n    key = 
create_key_in_db(note=note)\n    print(f"  \u2713 Generated key: {key}")\n    print(f"    Note: {note or 
\'(none)\'}")\n    print()\n\n\ndef cmd_list() -> None:\n    rows = list_keys_in_db()\n    if not rows:\n        
print("  No keys in database.")\n        return\n    header = f"  {\'Key\':<42} {\'Device\':<12} {\'Valid\':<6} 
Note"\n    print(header)\n    print("  " + "\u2500" * 70)\n    for row in rows:\n        key_display = row["key"][:40] 
+ ".." if len(row["key"]) > 40 else row["key"]\n        uuid_display = str(row["device_uuid"])[:10]\n        valid = 
"Yes" if row["valid"] else "REVOKED"\n        note = row["note"][:20]\n        print(f"  {key_display:<42} 
{uuid_display:<12} {valid:<6} {note}")\n\n\ndef cmd_revoke(key: str) -> None:\n    try:\n        
revoke_key_in_db(key)\n        print(f"  \u2713 Revoked: {key}")\n    except KeyError as exc:\n        print(f"  
\u2717 {exc}")\n\n\ndef cmd_reset(key: str) -> None:\n    try:\n        unbind_key(key)\n        print(f"  \u2713 
Device binding removed for: {key}")\n    except KeyError as exc:\n        print(f"  \u2717 {exc}")\n\n\ndef main() -> 
int:\n    _print_header()\n    argv = sys.argv[1:]\n    sub = argv[0].lower() if argv else "generate"\n\n    if sub in 
("generate", "gen", "new"):\n        note = " ".join(argv[1:])\n        cmd_generate(note=note)\n    elif sub in 
("list", "ls"):\n        cmd_list()\n    elif sub in ("revoke",) and len(argv) >= 2:\n        cmd_revoke(argv[1])\n    
elif sub in ("reset", "unbind") and len(argv) >= 2:\n        cmd_reset(argv[1])\n    else:\n        print(f"  Unknown 
sub-command: {sub!r}")\n        print("  Usage: python -m agent.keygen [generate|list|revoke KEY|reset KEY]")\n        
return 2\n    print()\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n\n"""DENG Tool: 
Rejoin ù Key licensing 
system.\n\nArchitecture\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n \u250c\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n \u2502 Key format: 
 DENG-<hex\u22658>  (e.g. DENG-38ab1234cd56ef78) \u2502\n \u2502 1 key = 1 device UUID                                 
   \u2502\n \u2502 UUID = Android serial / MAC / generated UUID (persisted) \u2502\n \u2502 DB   = 
~/.deng-tool/rejoin/keydb.json  (local, dev)      \u2502\n \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n\nDevelopment 
mode\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n Set 
``DEV_MODE = True`` (or env DENG_DEV=1) to bypass server checks.\n In dev mode the local keydb.json is the source of 
truth.\n Generate keys with: python -m agent.keygen\n\nSecurity 
note\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n This module reads/writes only 
local files under APP_HOME and makes\n no network calls. The UUID and key are stored in keydb.json (local\n only). 
Production builds should swap ``_load_keydb`` / ``_save_keydb``\n with authenticated API calls.\n"""\n\nfrom 
__future__ import annotations\n\nimport hashlib\nimport json\nimport os\nimport re\nimport secrets\nimport time\nfrom 
pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom .constants import APP_HOME, LICENSE_KEY_PATTERN\n\n# \u2500\u2500 
Dev mode flag \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nDEV_MODE: bool = bool(os.environ.get("DENG_DEV", ""))\n\nKEYDB_PATH = APP_HOME / 
"keydb.json"\nDEVICE_UUID_PATH = APP_HOME / "device_id"\n\n_KEY_RE = re.compile(LICENSE_KEY_PATTERN, 
re.IGNORECASE)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Custom exceptions \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nclass KeyError(Exception):          # noqa: A001 - shadows built-in 
intentionally\n    """Raised when key validation fails."""\n\nclass KeyDeviceMismatch(KeyError):\n    """Raised when 
the key is bound to a different device."""\n\nclass KeyNotFound(KeyError):\n    """Raised when the key does not exist 
in the database."""\n\nclass KeyInvalid(KeyError):\n    """Raised when the key format or signature is wrong."""\n\n\n# 
\u2500\u2500 Device UUID \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef _get_android_serial() -> str | None:\n    """Try to read 
the Android device serial number."""\n    try:\n        import subprocess  # noqa: PLC0415\n        for cmd in (\n     
       ["getprop", "ro.serialno"],\n            ["getprop", "ro.boot.serialno"],\n        ):\n            result = 
subprocess.run(\n                cmd,\n                stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,\n                
stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                text=True,\n                
timeout=4,\n                shell=False,\n            )\n            if result.returncode == 0:\n                
serial = result.stdout.strip()\n                if serial and serial not in ("unknown", "0", ""):\n                    
return serial\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n    return None\n\n\ndef _derive_device_uuid(raw: 
str) -> str:\n    """Derive a stable hex UUID from a raw device identifier string."""\n    return 
hashlib.sha256(raw.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:32]\n\n\ndef get_device_uuid() -> str:\n    """Return the stable 
device UUID for this installation.\n\n    Priority:\n    1. Previously persisted UUID file (fastest; works offline).\n 
   2. Android serial number (hashed to 32 hex chars).\n    3. Generated random UUID (saved immediately for future 
runs).\n    """\n    DEVICE_UUID_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    if DEVICE_UUID_PATH.exists():\n   
     stored = DEVICE_UUID_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()\n        if re.fullmatch(r"[0-9a-f]{32}", 
stored):\n            return stored\n\n    serial = _get_android_serial()\n    if serial:\n        uuid = 
_derive_device_uuid(serial)\n    else:\n        uuid = secrets.token_hex(16)  # 32-char random hex\n\n    
DEVICE_UUID_PATH.write_text(uuid + "\\n", encoding="utf-8")\n    return uuid\n\n\ndef reset_device_uuid() -> str:\n    
"""Wipe and regenerate the device UUID (key reset helper)."""\n    if DEVICE_UUID_PATH.exists():\n        
DEVICE_UUID_PATH.unlink()\n    return get_device_uuid()\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Local key database (JSON) \u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef _load_keydb() -> dict[str, Any]:\n    
KEYDB_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    if not KEYDB_PATH.exists():\n        return {}\n    try:\n   
     return json.loads(KEYDB_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))\n    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n        
return {}\n\n\ndef _save_keydb(db: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n    KEYDB_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, 
exist_ok=True)\n    KEYDB_PATH.write_text(json.dumps(db, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\\n", encoding="utf-8")\n\n\n# 
\u2500\u2500 Key generator \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef generate_key(prefix: str = "DENG") -> str:\n    """Generate a new 
random license key in ``PREFIX-<32hex>`` format."""\n    return f"{prefix}-{secrets.token_hex(16).upper()}"\n\n\ndef 
create_key_in_db(key: str | None = None, *, note: str = "") -> str:\n    """Generate and store a new key in the local 
keydb (dev mode).\n\n    Returns the key string.\n    """\n    key = key or generate_key()\n    key = key.upper()\n    
if not _KEY_RE.match(key):\n        raise KeyInvalid(f"Key format invalid: {key}")\n    db = _load_keydb()\n    if key 
in db:\n        raise KeyError(f"Key already exists: {key}")\n    db[key] = {\n        "device_uuid": None,\n        
"bound_at": None,\n        "created_at": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime()),\n        "note": note,\n
