      "valid": True,\n    }\n    _save_keydb(db)\n    return key\n\n\ndef revoke_key_in_db(key: str) -> None:\n    
"""Mark a key as revoked in the local keydb."""\n    key = key.upper()\n    db = _load_keydb()\n    if key not in 
db:\n        raise KeyNotFound(f"Key not found: {key}")\n    db[key]["valid"] = False\n    _save_keydb(db)\n\n\ndef 
list_keys_in_db() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    """List all keys in the local keydb (dev utility)."""\n    db = 
_load_keydb()\n    rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n    for key, entry in db.items():\n        rows.append({\n         
   "key": key,\n            "device_uuid": entry.get("device_uuid") or "(unbound)",\n            "bound_at": 
entry.get("bound_at") or "(never)",\n            "note": entry.get("note") or "",\n            "valid": 
entry.get("valid", True),\n        })\n    return rows\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Key binding \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nd
ef _validate_format(key: str) -> str:\n    upper = key.strip().upper()\n    if not _KEY_RE.match(upper):\n        
raise KeyInvalid("Key must be in format DENG-<hex8+> (e.g. DENG-38ab1234cd56ef78)")\n    return upper\n\n\ndef 
bind_key(key: str) -> str:\n    """Bind (or verify existing binding of) key to this device UUID.\n\n    Returns 
device_uuid on success.\n    Raises:\n      KeyInvalid       ù bad format\n      KeyNotFound      ù key not in DB\n    
  KeyDeviceMismatch ù key is already bound to a different device\n    """\n    key = _validate_format(key)\n    
device_uuid = get_device_uuid()\n    db = _load_keydb()\n\n    if key not in db:\n        raise KeyNotFound(\n         
   f"License key not found in the key database.\\n"\n            f"  Key: {key[:12]}...\\n"\n            "  Contact 
the distributor to obtain a valid key."\n        )\n\n    entry = db[key]\n    if not entry.get("valid", True):\n      
  raise KeyInvalid("This license key has been revoked.")\n\n    bound_uuid = entry.get("device_uuid")\n    if 
bound_uuid and bound_uuid != device_uuid:\n        raise KeyDeviceMismatch(\n            "\u26a0  This key is already 
bound to a different device.\\n"\n            "   1 key = 1 device.  Contact support to reset the key binding.\\n"\n   
         f"   Bound device: {bound_uuid[:8]}...  This device: {device_uuid[:8]}..."\n        )\n\n    if not 
bound_uuid:\n        # First use ù bind to this device\n        db[key]["device_uuid"] = device_uuid\n        
db[key]["bound_at"] = time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime())\n        _save_keydb(db)\n\n    return 
device_uuid\n\n\ndef unbind_key(key: str) -> None:\n    """Remove the device binding from a key (admin / 
key-reset)."""\n    key = _validate_format(key)\n    db = _load_keydb()\n    if key not in db:\n        raise 
KeyNotFound(f"Key not found: {key}")\n    db[key]["device_uuid"] = None\n    db[key]["bound_at"] = None\n    
_save_keydb(db)\n\n\ndef verify_key(key: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    """Verify a key against this device.  Returns 
(ok, message).\n\n    Does NOT bind the key ù call bind_key() first to lock it.\n    """\n    try:\n        
device_uuid = bind_key(key)\n        return True, f"Key verified. Device: {device_uuid[:8]}..."\n    except 
KeyDeviceMismatch as exc:\n        return False, str(exc)\n    except (KeyNotFound, KeyInvalid) as exc:\n        
return False, str(exc)\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return False, f"Key verification failed: 
{exc}"\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Interactive key entry \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef prompt_and_verify_key() -> bool:\n    """Prompt the user to enter a license key, verify it, 
and bind it.\n\n    Returns True if verification succeeded, False if user chose to exit.\n    Prints status messages 
directly.\n    """\n    print()\n    print("\u2500" * 48)\n    print("  DENG Tool: Rejoin ù License Verification")\n   
 print("\u2500" * 48)\n    print("  Paste your license key to continue.")\n    print("  Format: DENG-<hex>  (e.g. 
DENG-38ab1234cd56ef78)")\n    print()\n    while True:\n        try:\n            raw = input("  Paste your license 
key (or \'q\' to quit): ").strip()\n        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):\n            print()\n            
return False\n        if raw.lower() in ("q", "quit", "exit"):\n            return False\n        if not raw:\n        
    print("  Please enter your license key.")\n            continue\n        ok, message = verify_key(raw)\n        if 
ok:\n            print(f"  \u2713 {message}")\n            print()\n            return True\n        print(f"  \u2717 
{message}")\n        print("  Try again or press \'q\' to quit.")\n        print()\n\n"""Formatting helpers for 
Discord Key Stats (no Discord imports)."""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom datetime import datetime, 
timezone\nfrom typing import Any\n\n# Embed colors (Discord integer colors)\nCOLOR_STATS_UNUSED = 
0x27AE60\nCOLOR_STATS_USED = 0x2F80ED\nCOLOR_STATS_BAD = 0xE74C3C\n\n\ndef relative_time_ago(iso_str: str | None) -> 
str | None:\n    """Human-readable relative time like \'3 day(s) ago\', or None if unparseable."""\n    if not 
iso_str:\n        return None\n    try:\n        text = iso_str.replace("Z", "+00:00")\n        dt = 
datetime.fromisoformat(text)\n        if dt.tzinfo is None:\n            dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n        
delta = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - dt\n        secs = int(max(0, delta.total_seconds()))\n        if secs < 60:\n    
        return "just now"\n        mins = secs // 60\n        if mins < 60:\n            return f"{mins} minute(s) 
ago" if mins != 1 else "1 minute ago"\n        hours = mins // 60\n        if hours < 24:\n            return 
f"{hours} hour(s) ago" if hours != 1 else "1 hour ago"\n        days = hours // 24\n        if days < 30:\n            
return f"{days} day(s) ago" if days != 1 else "1 day ago"\n        months = days // 30\n        if months < 12:\n      
      return f"{months} month(s) ago" if months != 1 else "1 month ago"\n        years = months // 12\n        return 
f"{years} year(s) ago" if years != 1 else "1 year ago"\n    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n        return 
None\n\n\ndef format_stats_header_plain(*, total: int, page: int, total_pages: int) -> str:\n    """Plain (non-embed) 
header line for Key Stats messages."""\n    return f"Your License Keys (Total: {total} | Page {page + 
1}/{total_pages})"\n\n\ndef format_stats_copy_block_for_slice(rows_slice: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:\n    from 
agent.license_panel import format_copy_license_keys_lines\n\n    keys = [str(r["full_key_plaintext"]) for r in 
rows_slice if r.get("full_key_plaintext")]\n    return format_copy_license_keys_lines(keys)\n\n\ndef 
format_stats_page_content_header(\n    rows_slice: list[dict[str, Any]], *, total: int, page: int, total_pages: int\n) 
-> str:\n    """Message content: just the page header. Keys are shown inside the embed (no top copy block)."""\n    
return format_stats_header_plain(total=total, page=page, total_pages=total_pages)\n\n\ndef format_stats_embed_title(*, 
total: int, page: int, total_pages: int) -> str:\n    """Deprecated: use :func:`format_stats_header_plain` for Key 
Stats."""\n    return format_stats_header_plain(total=total, page=page, total_pages=total_pages)\n\n\ndef 
build_key_stats_embed_dict(row: dict[str, Any], *, number: int = 0) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """One Discord embed dict 
for a single license key row.\n\n    The key itself is shown as a numbered line inside the embed description.\n    
Device is combined into the status line. No separate top \'Copy License Key:\' block.\n    """\n    full = 
row.get("full_key_plaintext")\n    masked = row.get("masked_key") or "???"\n\n    lic = (row.get("license_status") or 
"active").lower()\n    used = bool(row.get("used"))\n\n    lines: list[str] = []\n\n    # --- Key display (numbered) 
---\n    prefix = f"{number}. " if number > 0 else ""\n    if full:\n        lines.append(f"{prefix}`{full}`")\n    
elif lic in {"revoked", "expired"}:\n        lines.append(f"{prefix}**{masked}** *(reference only, not copyable)*")\n  
  else:\n        # Show masked reference and note that the full key is not recoverable\n        
lines.append(f"{prefix}**{masked}** *(full key not recoverable)*")\n\n    # --- Status line (device combined) ---\n    
device = row.get("device_display") if used and lic not in {"revoked", "expired"} else None\n\n    if lic == 
"revoked":\n        lines.append("Status: \U0001f534 Revoked")\n        color = COLOR_STATS_BAD\n    elif lic == 
"expired":\n        lines.append("Status: \U0001f534 Expired")\n        color = COLOR_STATS_BAD\n    elif used:\n      
  if device:\n            lines.append(f"Status: Used / Device bound on {device}")\n        else:\n            
lines.append("Status: Used / Device bound")\n        color = COLOR_STATS_USED\n    else:\n        
lines.append("Status: Unused / No device linked")\n        color = COLOR_STATS_UNUSED\n\n    # --- Last active ---\n   
 if used and lic not in {"revoked", "expired"}:\n        last_seen = row.get("last_seen_at")\n        if last_seen:\n  
          rel = relative_time_ago(last_seen)\n            lines.append(f"Last Active: {rel or last_seen[:19]}")\n      
  else:\n            lines.append("Last Active: Never")\n\n    return {\n        "description": "\\n".join(lines),\n   
     "color": color,\n        "footer": {"text": "DENG Tool \\u00b7 Key Stats"},\n    }\n\n\ndef 
build_key_stats_embed_dicts(rows_slice: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    return 
[build_key_stats_embed_dict(r, number=i) for i, r in enumerate(rows_slice, start=1)]\n\n\ndef 
build_key_stats_empty_embed_dict() -> dict[str, Any]:\n    return {\n        "description": (\n            "You do not 
have any license keys yet.\\n"\n            "Click **Generate Key** on the panel to create one."\n        ),\n        
"color": COLOR_STATS_USED,\n        "footer": {"text": "DENG Tool \\u00b7 Key Stats"},\n    }\n\n\ndef 
format_key_block(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    """Legacy: flatten one key to markdown (tests / introspection 
only)."""\n    d = build_key_stats_embed_dict(row)\n    return d.get("description") or ""\n\n\ndef 
build_key_stats_description(rows_slice: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:\n    """Legacy: join per-key descriptions (avoid 
for Discord; use embeds instead)."""\n    if not rows_slice:\n        return ""\n    parts = [format_key_block(r) for 
r in rows_slice]\n    return "\\n\\n".join(parts)\n\n\ndef build_key_stats_download_body(*, discord_user_id: str, 
rows: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:\n    from datetime import datetime, timezone\n\n    now = 
datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%a, %d %b %Y %H:%M:%S UTC")\n    header = [\n        f"License Keys For User ID: 
{discord_user_id}",\n        f"Generated: {now}",\n        f"Total Keys: {len(rows)}",\n        "=" * 43,\n        
"",\n    ]\n    body: list[str] = []\n    for i, row in enumerate(rows, start=1):\n        full = 
row.get("full_key_plaintext")\n        masked = row.get("masked_key") or "???"\n        used = bool(row.get("used"))\n 
       lic = (row.get("license_status") or "active").lower()\n\n        if lic == "revoked":\n            status_word 
= "Revoked"\n        elif lic == "expired":\n            status_word = "Expired"\n        elif used:\n            
status_word = "Used / Device bound"\n        else:\n            status_word = "Unused / Ready for first device"\n\n    
    if full:\n            body.append(f"{i}. {full} - {status_word}")\n        else:\n            body.append(f"{i}. 
[Full key not available for copy] - {status_word}")\n            if lic not in {"revoked", "expired"}:\n               
 body.append(\n                    "   Full key is not recoverable for this key because export storage "\n             
       "was not enabled when it was created (or ciphertext is missing)."\n                )\n            else:\n       
         body.append(f"   Reference: {masked}")\n\n        if device := row.get("device_display"):\n            if 
used and lic not in {"revoked", "expired"}:\n                body.append(f"   Device: {device}")\n\n        last_seen 
= row.get("last_seen_at")\n        if used and lic not in {"revoked", "expired"}:\n            if last_seen:\n         
       rel = relative_time_ago(last_seen)\n                body.append(f"   Last Active: {rel or last_seen[:19]}")\n   
         else:\n                body.append("   Last Active: Never")\n\n        body.append("")\n\n    return 
"\\n".join(header + body).rstrip() + "\\n"\n\n"""Safe Roblox launch and rejoin orchestration."""\n\nfrom __future__ 
import annotations\n\nimport os\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom typing import Any\nfrom 
urllib.parse import urlparse\n\nfrom . import android, db\nfrom .config import effective_private_server_url, 
enabled_package_entries, validate_config\nfrom .logger import configure_logging, log_event\nfrom .url_utils import 
mask_launch_url, mask_urls_in_text, to_roblox_deep_link\n\n\ndef _first_log_lines(text: str, *, limit: int = 4) -> 
str:\n    lines = [line.strip() for line in (text or "").splitlines() if line.strip()]\n    return " | 
".join(lines[:limit])[:500]\n\n\ndef _url_host_for_log(url: str) -> str:\n    try:\n        parsed = urlparse(url)\n   
     return parsed.hostname or parsed.netloc or parsed.scheme or ""\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
return ""\n\n\ndef _result_used_root(result: android.CommandResult) -> bool:\n    if not result.args:\n        return 
False\n    exe = os.path.basename(str(result.args[0]))\n    return exe in {"su", "tsu"}\n\n\ndef 
_proc_scan_alive(package: str) -> bool:\n    """Pure Python /proc scan ù no subprocess, no fork, no segfault risk.\n\n 
   Returns True when any running process has ``package`` in its cmdline.\n    This is the safest possible alive check 
for Termux/Python 3.13 where\n    ``fork()`` in threaded processes can produce SIGSEGV.\n    """\n    try:\n        
for entry in os.listdir("/proc"):\n            if not entry.isdigit():\n                continue\n            try:\n   
             with open(f"/proc/{entry}/cmdline", "rb") as fh:\n                    cmdline = 
fh.read().replace(b"\\x00", b" ").decode("utf-8", errors="replace")\n                if package in cmdline:\n          
          return True\n            except (OSError, PermissionError):\n                continue\n    except (OSError, 
PermissionError):\n        pass\n    return False\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass RejoinResult:\n    success: 
bool\n    root_used: bool\n    error: str | None = None\n    warning: str | None = 
None\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass LaunchAttemptResult:\n    package: str\n    attempted: bool\n    success: 
bool\n    method: str\n    reason: str\n    stdout: str\n    stderr: str\n\n    def as_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n 
       return {\n            "package": self.package,\n            "attempted": self.attempted,\n            
"success": self.success,\n            "method": self.method,\n            "reason": self.reason,\n            
"stdout": self.stdout,\n            "stderr": self.stderr,\n        }\n\n\ndef perform_rejoin(\n    config_data: 
dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    reason: str = "manual",\n    package_entry: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n    
no_force_stop: bool = False,\n) -> RejoinResult:\n    """Perform one safe rejoin attempt and record the result 
locally."""\n    # [DENG_REJOIN_RESURRECT_DECISION] probe_id=p-ea167faf5f\n    # validate_config was previously called 
OUTSIDE the try/except block.\n    # A ConfigError (e.g. "health_check_interval_seconds must be at least 10")\n    # 
would propagate to the supervisor worker\'s outer except, get logged as\n    # worker_error, and loop forever without 
ever actually relaunching the package.\n    # Wrapping it here converts any config failure into a 
RejoinResult(False)\n    # so the supervisor receives a clean failure and applies its backoff.\n    try:\n        cfg 
= validate_config(config_data)\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _pkg_fallback = 
str(config_data.get("roblox_package") or "unknown")\n        _err = str(exc)\n        db.insert_rejoin_attempt(\n      
      reason=reason,\n            package=_pkg_fallback,\n            launch_mode=str(config_data.get("launch_mode") 
or "app"),\n            masked_launch_url=None,\n            root_used=False,\n            success=False,\n            
error=_err,\n        )\n        db.insert_event("ERROR", "rejoin_failed", _err, {"reason": reason, "package": 
_pkg_fallback})\n        return RejoinResult(False, root_used=False, error=_err)\n    logger = 
configure_logging(level=cfg.get("log_level", "INFO"))\n    ents = enabled_package_entries(cfg)\n    entry = 
package_entry\n    if entry is None:\n        entry = next((e for e in ents if e["package"] == cfg["roblox_package"]), 
ents[0])\n    package = entry["package"]\n    launch_mode = cfg["launch_mode"]\n    launch_url = 
str(cfg.get("launch_url") or "").strip()\n    effective_url = str(effective_private_server_url(entry, cfg) or 
"").strip()\n    legacy_url_mode = launch_mode in {"deeplink", "web_url"} and bool(launch_url)\n    # Always use any 
configured URL for launch ù if a URL is set anywhere,\n    # every launch (including reopening dead packages) must use 
it.\n    url_for_launch = effective_url or launch_url\n    # Kaeru-equivalent fix (probe p-1239f2b5f9): Roblox\'s 
https share\n    # URL is resolved by Android to the *browser*, which lands the user\n    # in the Roblox app\'s lobby 
instead of the private server.  The\n    # working tool (Kaeru) launches the roblox:// deep link directly.\n    # 
``to_roblox_deep_link`` is a no-op for already-deep-link URLs and\n    # for URLs whose pattern we don\'t know how to 
translate.\n    if url_for_launch:\n        deep = to_roblox_deep_link(url_for_launch)\n        if deep and deep != 
url_for_launch:\n            url_for_launch = deep\n    masked_url = mask_launch_url(url_for_launch) if url_for_launch 
else None\n    root_used = False\n    warning: str | None = None\n\n    log_event(\n        logger,\n        "info",\n 
       "rejoin_start",\n        reason=reason,\n        package=package,\n        launch_mode=launch_mode,\n        
url=masked_url or "",\n    )\n\n    try:\n        if not android.package_installed(package):\n            raise 
RuntimeError(f"Roblox package is not installed: {package}")\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 ALWAYS force-stop before launch \
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        # User feedback 
(post-probe-1239f2b5f9): "I messed up the open\n        # window then restarted; the tool failed to fix it. Kaeru 
fixes\n        # it every time."  Kaeru ALWAYS force-stops the clone before\n        # launching so the new ``am start 
--windowingMode 5\n        # --activity-launch-bounds ...`` produces a *fresh* task with\n        # the requested 
geometry; otherwise Android brings the existing\n        # task to the foreground and silently drops the bounds.\n     
   #\n        # We try unprivileged ``am force-stop`` first (works in most\n        # Termux + ADB setups) and fall 
back to root only when needed.\n        # Honors ``no_force_stop`` for callers that want the old behavior.\n        if 
not no_force_stop:\n            stopped_via = ""\n            try:\n                pre_stop = 
android.force_stop_package(package)\n                if pre_stop.ok:\n                    if 
_result_used_root(pre_stop):\n                        root_used = True\n                        stopped_via = 
f"root({os.path.basename(str(pre_stop.args[0]))})"\n                    else:\n                        stopped_via = 
"am_force_stop"\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                pass\n\n            if not stopped_via 
and cfg.get("root_mode_enabled"):\n                root_info = android.detect_root()\n                if 
root_info.available:\n                    root_used = True\n                    log_event(\n                        
logger, "info", "root_command",\n                        command=f"am force-stop {package}",\n                        
root_tool=root_info.tool or "su",\n                    )\n                    try:\n                        root_stop 
= android.force_stop_package(\n                            package, root_info=root_info,\n                        )\n  
                      if root_stop.ok:\n                            stopped_via = f"root({root_info.tool})"\n          
              else:\n                            warning = (\n                                f"root force-stop 
failed: "\n                                f"{mask_urls_in_text(root_stop.summary)}"\n                            )\n  
                          log_event(\n                                logger, "warning", "force_stop_failed",\n        
                        error=warning, root_used=root_used,\n                            )\n                    except 
Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n                        warning = f"root force-stop error: {exc}"\n                 
       log_event(\n                            logger, "warning", "force_stop_error",\n                            
error=warning, root_used=root_used,\n                        )\n                else:\n                    warning = 
(\n                        "root mode is enabled, but root is unavailable; "\n                        "launching 
without root"\n                    )\n                    log_event(logger, "warning", "root_unavailable",\n           
                   warning=warning)\n\n            if stopped_via:\n                log_event(logger, "info", 
"pre_launch_stop",\n                          method=stopped_via, package=package)\n                # Brief grace so 
the WindowManager actually tears down\n                # the activity before we re-create it.\n                
time.sleep(min(2.5, max(1.0, int(cfg.get("reconnect_delay_seconds", 2)) / 2)))\n            else:\n                # 
Even without successful stop, log so the operator can\n                # correlate "bounds not honored" with "task 
still alive".\n                log_event(logger, "warning", "pre_launch_stop_skipped",\n                          
package=package, root_mode=cfg.get("root_mode_enabled"))\n\n        # Layer 2: launch with explicit windowing-mode + 
launch-bounds so the\n        # window is placed correctly from the first frame.  Falls back to\n        # bounds-less 
launch automatically if `--activity-launch-bounds`\n        # isn\'t supported on this build.\n        #\n        # 
Real-device evidence (probe ``p-47fa33562a``): the old call passed\n        # ``[package]`` (ONE package) to 
``calculate_split_layout``, so every\n        # clone was launched into the *same* single-package rect and they all\n  
      # overlapped.  Build the layout for the FULL list of enabled clones\n        # and pick the rect that matches 
``package``.\n        _bounds_rect: tuple[int, int, int, int] | None = None\n        try:\n            from . import 
window_layout\n            _display = window_layout.detect_display_info()\n            _all_pkgs = [e["package"] for e 
in ents] or [package]\n            if package not in _all_pkgs:\n                _all_pkgs.append(package)\n           
 _dock_frac = float(cfg.get("termux_dock_fraction", 0.50))\n            _rects = 
window_layout.calculate_split_layout(\n                _all_pkgs, _display.width, _display.height,\n                
termux_log_fraction=_dock_frac,\n            )\n            _r_for_pkg = next(\n                (r for r in (_rects or 
[]) if getattr(r, "package", None) == package),\n                None,\n            )\n            if _r_for_pkg is 
not None:\n                _bounds_rect = (\n                    _r_for_pkg.left, _r_for_pkg.top,\n                    
_r_for_pkg.right, _r_for_pkg.bottom,\n                )\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            
_bounds_rect = None\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 URL-first launch (probe p-316b3b040d fix) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\n        # When private_server_url is SET, the URL itself IS the join intent.\n        # Pass it directly 
to the first (and only) am-start call so Android\n        # routes the roblox:// VIEW intent to 
ActivityProtocolLaunch\n        # immediately ù exactly like the user manually tapping the link.\n        # No 
app-first phase, no waiting, no second phase URL delivery.\n        #\n        # When private_server_url is BLANK, a 
plain MAIN/LAUNCHER intent is\n        # used.  Roblox opens to home/lobby only; no join is attempted.\n        
log_event(\n            logger, "debug", "launch_url_first",\n            package=package, 
url_set=bool(url_for_launch),\n            masked_url=masked_url or "",\n        )\n        launch_started = 
time.monotonic()\n        if _bounds_rect is not None:\n            result, _method = 
android.launch_package_with_bounds(\n                package, _bounds_rect, url_for_launch or None,\n            )\n   
     else:\n            result, _method = android.launch_package_with_options(\n                package, 
url_for_launch or None,\n            )\n        launch_elapsed_ms = int((time.monotonic() - launch_started) * 1000)\n  
      if _result_used_root(result) or _method.startswith("root_"):\n            root_used = True\n\n        if 
url_for_launch:\n            log_event(\n                logger,\n                "info",\n                
"[DENG_REJOIN_URL_LAUNCH]",\n                package=package,\n                
action=str(cfg.get("post_launch_action") or ""),\n                url_present="true",\n                
url_host=_url_host_for_log(url_for_launch),\n                url_length=len(url_for_launch),\n                
command_mode=_method,\n                return_code=result.returncode,\n                
stdout_first_lines=_first_log_lines(mask_urls_in_text(result.stdout)),\n                
stderr_first_lines=_first_log_lines(mask_urls_in_text(result.stderr)),\n                
elapsed_ms=launch_elapsed_ms,\n            )\n\n        if not result.ok:\n            error = 
mask_urls_in_text(result.summary or "Android launch command failed")\n            raise RuntimeError(error)\n\n        
log_event(\n            logger, "info", "launch_result",\n            package=package, method=_method, ok=True,\n      
      url_set=bool(url_for_launch), masked_url=masked_url or "",\n        )\n\n        db.insert_rejoin_attempt(\n     
       reason=reason,\n            package=package,\n            launch_mode=launch_mode,\n            
masked_launch_url=masked_url,\n            root_used=root_used,\n            success=True,\n            error=None,\n  
      )\n        db.insert_event(\n            "INFO",\n            "rejoin_success",\n            f"rejoin succeeded 
for {package}",\n            {"reason": reason, "package": package, "root_used": root_used, "launch_mode": 
launch_mode},\n        )\n        log_event(logger, "info", "rejoin_success", package=package, 
root_used=str(root_used).lower())\n        return RejoinResult(True, root_used=root_used, warning=warning)\n    except 
Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - CLI boundary records any operational failure.\n        error = 
mask_urls_in_text(str(exc))\n        db.insert_rejoin_attempt(\n            reason=reason,\n            
package=package,\n            launch_mode=launch_mode,\n            masked_launch_url=masked_url,\n            
root_used=root_used,\n            success=False,\n            error=error,\n        )\n        
db.insert_event("ERROR", "rejoin_failed", error, {"reason": reason, "package": package, "root_used": root_used})\n     
   log_event(logger, "error", "rejoin_failed", error=error, package=package, root_used=str(root_used).lower())\n       
 return RejoinResult(False, root_used=root_used, error=error, warning=warning)\n\n\ndef 
launch_package_structured(config_data: dict[str, Any], entry: dict[str, Any]) -> LaunchAttemptResult:\n    """Launch a 
single package with URL \u2192 am fallback; never raises."""\n    cfg = validate_config(config_data)\n    package = 
entry["package"]\n    if not android.package_installed(package):\n        return LaunchAttemptResult(\n            
package=package,\n            attempted=False,\n            success=False,\n            method="none",\n            
reason="not_installed",\n            stdout="",\n            stderr="",\n        )\n    url = 
str(effective_private_server_url(entry, cfg) or "").strip()\n    result, method = 
android.launch_package_with_options(package, url or None)\n    reason = "" if result.ok else (result.stderr or 
result.stdout or "launch_failed")\n    return LaunchAttemptResult(\n        package=package,\n        
attempted=True,\n        success=result.ok,\n        method=method,\n        reason=reason,\n        
stdout=result.stdout,\n        stderr=result.stderr,\n    )\n\n\ndef launch_configured_packages(config_data: dict[str, 
Any], *, reason: str = "start") -> list[RejoinResult]:\n    """Launch all configured Roblox packages safely, one 
package at a time."""\n    cfg = validate_config(config_data)\n    packages = [entry["package"] for entry in 
enabled_package_entries(cfg)]\n    results: list[RejoinResult] = []\n    delay = max(5, 
int(cfg.get("reconnect_delay_seconds", 8)))\n    for index, package in enumerate(packages):\n        pkg_entry = 
next(e for e in enabled_package_entries(cfg) if e["package"] == package)\n        package_cfg = dict(cfg)\n        
package_cfg["roblox_package"] = package\n        result = perform_rejoin(package_cfg, reason=reason, 
package_entry=pkg_entry)\n        results.append(result)\n        if index < len(packages) - 1:\n            
time.sleep(delay)\n    return results\n\n\ndef launch_package_for_current_config(\n    entry: dict[str, Any],\n    
cfg: dict[str, Any],\n    reason: str = "watchdog_recovery",\n) -> "RejoinResult":\n    """Canonical launcher selector 
for watchdog recovery and initial Start.\n\n    Behavior:\n    - If ``private_server_url`` is configured (per-entry or 
global):\n        Calls ``perform_rejoin`` which sends the roblox:// deep-link intent.\n        The private server URL 
is preserved including ``&type=Server`` params.\n    - If ``private_server_url`` is blank:\n        Calls 
``perform_rejoin`` with no URL ù Roblox opens to home/lobby only\n        (app-only launch, no join intent sent).\n\n  
  This function MUST be used by:\n    - Initial Start launch (via cmd_start loop)\n    - Dead recovery (watchdog 
detects process gone)\n    - No Heartbeat recovery (watchdog force-stops then relaunches)\n    - No Heartbeat recovery 
(private URL relaunch when configured)\n    - Supervisor resurrection\n\n    [DENG_REJOIN_CANONICAL_LAUNCHER] 
probe_id=p-ea167faf5f\n    Private Server URL is optional. Blank URL = app-only. Configured URL = private join.\n    
``perform_rejoin`` already selects the correct am-start command based on\n    ``effective_private_server_url(entry, 
cfg)`` ù this wrapper just sets the\n    package and delegates.\n    """\n    pkg = str(entry.get("package") or "")\n  
  pkg_cfg = dict(cfg)\n    pkg_cfg["roblox_package"] = pkg\n    return perform_rejoin(pkg_cfg, reason=reason, 
package_entry=entry)\n\n"""Generate public market-style Python launcher files."""\n\nfrom __future__ import 
annotations\n\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .constants import LAUNCHER_DIR\nfrom .platform_detect import 
PUBLIC_DOWNLOAD_CANDIDATES, fallback_launcher_path\n\nLAUNCHER_FILENAME = "deng-rejoin.py"\n\nLAUNCHER_CONTENT = 
\'\'\'import os\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\n\nhome = os.path.expanduser("~")\nproject = os.path.join(home, 
".deng-tool", "rejoin")\nmain = os.path.join(project, "agent", "deng_tool_rejoin.py")\n\nif not 
os.path.exists(main):\n    print("DENG Tool: Rejoin is not installed.")\n    print("Run the GitHub install command 
first.")\n    sys.exit(1)\n\nos.chdir(project)\nraise SystemExit(subprocess.call(["python", main, "menu"] + 
sys.argv[1:]))\n\'\'\'\n\n\ndef write_launcher(path: Path) -> bool:\n    try:\n        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, 
exist_ok=True)\n        path.write_text(LAUNCHER_CONTENT, encoding="utf-8")\n        return True\n    except 
OSError:\n        return False\n\n\ndef create_market_launchers() -> list[Path]:\n    """Create launchers in public 
Download folders and a Termux-home fallback."""\n    created: list[Path] = []\n    for directory in 
PUBLIC_DOWNLOAD_CANDIDATES:\n        if directory.exists():\n            path = directory / LAUNCHER_FILENAME\n        
    if write_launcher(path):\n                created.append(path)\n\n    fallback = fallback_launcher_path()\n    if 
write_launcher(fallback):\n        created.append(fallback)\n\n    LAUNCHER_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   
 local = LAUNCHER_DIR / LAUNCHER_FILENAME\n    if local != fallback and write_launcher(local):\n        
created.append(local)\n\n    return created\n\n"""Real App Cloner / cloud-phone layout-key discovery.\n\nWhy this 
exists\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nPrevious "force 
resize/position" attempts wrote the four ``app_cloner_current_window_*``\nints into ``pkg_preferences.xml`` and 
assumed App Cloner would honor them.  Real\ncloud-phone builds (and many App Cloner versions) ignore those four ints 
unless\nthe matching "Set window position" / "Set window size" enable flags are also\ntrue, and unless the 
per-orientation portrait/landscape keys exist.  A Kaeru\nclue pointed at: ``Set`` in auto-DPI portrait/landscape 
settings.\n\nWhat this module does (read-only)\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\nFor each package, walk every shared_prefs XML it owns (direct file read first;\nroot cat fallback when the 
file is not group-readable).  Parse every\n``<int|string|boolean|long|float>`` entry and categorize the key name 
against\na set of regular expressions covering:\n\n  * position bounds (left/top/right/bottom, x/y, position_x/y)\n  * 
size (width/height)\n  * orientation flags (portrait/landscape/orientation/force_landscape)\n  * auto-DPI flags 
(auto_dpi[_portrait|_landscape])\n  * the critical ``Set`` enable booleans\n    (``set_window_position``, 
``set_window_size``, ``set_dpi``,\n    ``enable_resize``, ``enable_custom_screen_size``, ``custom_screen_size``,\n    
``freeform``, ``floating``, ``custom_resolution``, ``saved_bounds``)\n  * multi/clone/per-clone display 
markers\n\nDiscovery results are written to ``~/.deng-tool/rejoin/logs/layout-discovery.log``\n(masked, one block per 
package) ù never to public stdout.\n\nPublic users never invoke this directly.  ``cmd_start`` calls it silently 
when\nno per-package key map is cached yet.  Hidden command ``--discover-layout-keys``\nexposes one terminal line: 
``Layout key discovery saved: <path>``.\n\nAll file access is wrapped in try/except.  Never raises.  Never 
prints.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport re\nimport time\nimport 
xml.etree.ElementTree as ET\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing 
import Iterable, Optional\n\nfrom . import android\nfrom .constants import LOG_DIR\n\n_log = 
logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.layout_discovery")\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Discovery log path \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nLAYOUT_DISCOVERY_LOG = LOG_DIR / 
"layout-discovery.log"\n\n# Cap on how many shared_prefs files we inspect per package (defence against\n# pathological 
clone packages with hundreds of XMLs).\n_MAX_PREF_FILES_PER_PACKAGE = 32\n_MAX_KEYS_PER_FILE = 2000\n\n# \u2500\u2500 
Categorisation patterns \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n#\n# Each (category, 
regex) ù case-insensitive.  Multiple categories may match a\n# single key (a key like 
``app_cloner_set_window_position_landscape_left`` will\n# match position, landscape, set_enable, 
position_left).\n\n_CATEGORY_PATTERNS: tuple[tuple[str, re.Pattern[str]], ...] = (\n    # Position bounds\n    
("position_left",     re.compile(r"(?:^|[._-])(left|x|pos[ _-]*x|window[_ -]*x|window[ _-]*position[_ 
-]*x)(?:$|[._-])", re.I)),\n    ("position_top",      re.compile(r"(?:^|[._-])(top|y|pos[ _-]*y|window[_ -]*y|window[ 
_-]*position[_ -]*y)(?:$|[._-])", re.I)),\n    ("position_right",    re.compile(r"(?:^|[._-])(right|window[ 
_-]*right)(?:$|[._-])", re.I)),\n    ("position_bottom",   re.compile(r"(?:^|[._-])(bottom|window[ 
_-]*bottom)(?:$|[._-])", re.I)),\n    ("position_generic",  re.compile(r"(window[_ -]*position|saved[_ 
-]*bounds|task[_ -]*bounds|stack[_ -]*bounds|win[_ -]*bounds|bounds)", re.I)),\n    # Size\n    ("size_width",        
re.compile(r"(?:^|[._-])(width|window[_ -]*width|screen[_ -]*width)(?:$|[._-])", re.I)),\n    ("size_height",       
re.compile(r"(?:^|[._-])(height|window[_ -]*height|screen[_ -]*height)(?:$|[._-])", re.I)),\n    ("size_generic",      
re.compile(r"(window[_ -]*size|custom[_ -]*size|screen[_ -]*size|custom[_ -]*resolution|resolution)", re.I)),\n    # 
Orientation\n    ("orient_landscape",  re.compile(r"(landscape|land[_ -]*mode|force[_ -]*landscape)", re.I)),\n    
("orient_portrait",   re.compile(r"(portrait|port[_ -]*mode|force[_ -]*portrait)", re.I)),\n    ("orient_generic",    
re.compile(r"(orientation|orient)", re.I)),\n    # Auto DPI\n    ("auto_dpi",          re.compile(r"(auto[_ 
-]*dpi|dpi[_ -]*auto)", re.I)),\n    ("dpi_value",         re.compile(r"(?:^|[._-])(dpi|density|target[_ 
-]*dpi|override[_ -]*dpi)(?:$|[._-])", re.I)),\n    # The CRITICAL "Set" / enable flags\n    ("set_position_enable", 
re.compile(\n        r"(set[_ -]*window[_ -]*position|set[_ -]*pos|enable[_ -]*position|"\n        r"use[_ -]*custom[_ 
-]*position|custom[_ -]*position[_ -]*enabled|"\n        r"override[_ -]*position)", re.I)),\n    ("set_size_enable",  
 re.compile(\n        r"(set[_ -]*window[_ -]*size|set[_ -]*size|enable[_ -]*resize|"\n        r"enable[_ -]*custom[_ 
-]*screen[_ -]*size|enable[_ -]*custom[_ -]*size|"\n        r"custom[_ -]*screen[_ -]*size[_ -]*enabled|"\n        
r"custom[_ -]*size[_ -]*enabled|override[_ -]*size|"\n        r"force[_ -]*resize|allow[_ -]*resize)", re.I)),\n    
("set_dpi_enable",    re.compile(r"(set[_ -]*dpi|override[_ -]*dpi|enable[_ -]*dpi|custom[_ -]*dpi[_ -]*enabled)", 
re.I)),\n    ("freeform",          re.compile(r"(freeform|floating[_ -]*window|float[_ -]*mode|pip[_ -]*mode)", 
re.I)),\n    # Per-clone / display\n    ("multi_clone",       re.compile(r"(per[_ -]*clone|multi[_ -]*window|clone[_ 
-]*window)", re.I)),\n    ("display_info",      re.compile(r"(display[_ -]*id|target[_ -]*display)", re.I)),\n)\n\n\n# 
\u2500\u2500 Data classes \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n@dataclass\nclass LayoutKey:\n    """One discovered preference key.\n\n    
``categories`` may be multiple (e.g. a key that is both landscape and\n    position_left).  ``raw_value`` is the value 
as a string for safe logging.\n    """\n\n    file: str\n    name: str\n    tag: str            # "int" | "boolean" | 
"string" | "long" | "float" | ...\n    raw_value: str\n    categories: tuple[str, ...]\n    writable_direct: bool\n\n  
  def as_dict(self) -> dict[str, object]:\n        return asdict(self)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass PackageDiscovery:\n    
"""All keys discovered for one package, grouped by category."""\n\n    package: str\n    files_scanned: list[str] = 
field(default_factory=list)\n    keys: list[LayoutKey] = field(default_factory=list)\n    used_root: bool = False\n    
error: str | None = None\n\n    def by_category(self, category: str) -> list[LayoutKey]:\n        return [k for k in 
self.keys if category in k.categories]\n\n    def has_category(self, category: str) -> bool:\n        return 
any(category in k.categories for k in self.keys)\n\n    def summary(self) -> dict[str, int]:\n        counts: 
dict[str, int] = {}\n        for k in self.keys:\n            for c in k.categories:\n                counts[c] = 
counts.get(c, 0) + 1\n        return counts\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef 
_classify(key: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:\n    """Return categories that match ``key`` (may be empty)."""\n    out: 
list[str] = []\n    for cat, pat in _CATEGORY_PATTERNS:\n        if pat.search(key):\n            out.append(cat)\n    
return tuple(out)\n\n\ndef _list_pref_files_direct(package: str) -> list[Path]:\n    """List shared_prefs XML files 
via direct filesystem access."""\n    root = Path("/data/data") / package / "shared_prefs"\n    try:\n        if not 
root.is_dir():\n            return []\n        files = []\n        for entry in root.iterdir():\n            if 
entry.is_file() and entry.suffix.lower() == ".xml":\n                files.append(entry)\n        return 
sorted(files)[:_MAX_PREF_FILES_PER_PACKAGE]\n    except OSError:\n        return []\n\n\ndef 
_list_pref_files_root(package: str, root_tool: str) -> list[str]:\n    """List shared_prefs XML files via root ``ls``. 
 Returns absolute path strings."""\n    path = f"/data/data/{package}/shared_prefs"\n    try:\n        res = 
android.run_root_command(\n            ["sh", "-c", f"ls -1 \'{path}\'/*.xml 2>/dev/null"],\n            
root_tool=root_tool,\n            timeout=5,\n        )\n        if not res.ok:\n            return []\n        lines 
= [ln.strip() for ln in (res.stdout or "").splitlines()]\n        return [ln for ln in lines if 
ln.endswith(".xml")][:_MAX_PREF_FILES_PER_PACKAGE]\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return []\n\n\ndef 
_read_pref_file_direct(path: Path) -> str | None:\n    try:\n        return path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", 
errors="replace")\n    except OSError:\n        return None\n\n\ndef _read_pref_file_root(path_str: str, root_tool: 
str) -> str | None:\n    try:\n        res = android.run_root_command(\n            ["sh", "-c", f"cat \'{path_str}\' 
2>/dev/null"],\n            root_tool=root_tool,\n            timeout=5,\n        )\n        if not res.ok:\n          
  return None\n        return res.stdout\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return None\n\n\ndef 
_parse_xml_keys(\n    xml_text: str, file_label: str, writable_direct: bool\n) -> list[LayoutKey]:\n    """Parse one 
XML; return categorized keys.  Never raises."""\n    if not xml_text or not xml_text.strip():\n        return []\n    
try:\n        root_el = ET.fromstring(xml_text)\n    except ET.ParseError:\n        return []\n\n    out: 
list[LayoutKey] = []\n    count = 0\n    for child in root_el:\n        if count >= _MAX_KEYS_PER_FILE:\n            
break\n        name = child.attrib.get("name") or ""\n        if not name:\n            continue\n        cats = 
_classify(name)\n        if not cats:\n            continue\n        tag = child.tag\n        if "value" in 
child.attrib:\n            value = child.attrib.get("value") or ""\n        else:\n            value = (child.text or 
"").strip()\n        out.append(\n            LayoutKey(\n                file=file_label,\n                
name=name,\n                tag=tag,\n                raw_value=value[:200],\n                categories=cats,\n       
         writable_direct=writable_direct,\n            )\n        )\n        count += 1\n    return out\n\n\n# 
\u2500\u2500 Public API \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef discover_for_package(\n    package: str,\n    *,\n    root_tool: 
str | None = None,\n) -> PackageDiscovery:\n    """Scan all shared_prefs XML files for ``package`` and categorize 
keys.\n\n    Tries direct read first; falls back to root cat when direct read fails or\n    yields nothing (private 
data dirs are typically not group-readable).\n\n    Returns a populated :class:`PackageDiscovery`.  Never raises.\n    
"""\n    disc = PackageDiscovery(package=package)\n    try:\n        direct_files = _list_pref_files_direct(package)\n 
   except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        direct_files = []\n        disc.error = f"list_direct: {exc}"\n\n 
   seen_files: set[str] = set()\n\n    for path in direct_files:\n        label = str(path)\n        text = 
_read_pref_file_direct(path)\n        if text is None:\n            continue\n        seen_files.add(label)\n        
disc.files_scanned.append(label)\n        disc.keys.extend(_parse_xml_keys(text, label, writable_direct=True))\n\n    
if root_tool:\n        try:\n            root_files = _list_pref_files_root(package, root_tool)\n        except 
Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            root_files = []\n            disc.error = (disc.error or "") + f"; 
list_root: {exc}"\n        for path_str in root_files:\n            if path_str in seen_files:\n                # 
Already read directly; no need to re-read via root, but mark\n                # writable_direct=False on root-only 
files only.\n                continue\n            text = _read_pref_file_root(path_str, root_tool)\n            if 
text is None:\n                continue\n            seen_files.add(path_str)\n            disc.used_root = True\n     
       disc.files_scanned.append(path_str)\n            disc.keys.extend(_parse_xml_keys(text, path_str, 
writable_direct=False))\n\n    return disc\n\n\ndef discover_all(\n    packages: Iterable[str],\n    *,\n    
root_tool: str | None = None,\n) -> dict[str, PackageDiscovery]:\n    """Run discovery for each package; return 
``{package: PackageDiscovery}``."""\n    out: dict[str, PackageDiscovery] = {}\n    for pkg in packages:\n        
try:\n            out[pkg] = discover_for_package(pkg, root_tool=root_tool)\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n            disc = PackageDiscovery(package=pkg)\n            disc.error = f"discover: {exc}"\n            
out[pkg] = disc\n    return out\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Discovery log writer \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef write_discovery_log(\n    discoveries: dict[str, PackageDiscovery],\n    
*,\n    path: Path | None = None,\n) -> Path:\n    """Write a human-readable discovery log.  Returns the path.\n\n    
Never raises.  Best-effort: returns the intended path even if writing\n    failed (so callers can surface the path to 
the user; the log might just be\n    incomplete).\n    """\n    target = path or LAYOUT_DISCOVERY_LOG\n    try:\n      
  target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        ts = time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")\n        lines = 
[f"# Layout key discovery ù {ts}", ""]\n        for pkg, disc in discoveries.items():\n            lines.append(f"== 
Package: {pkg} ==")\n            lines.append(f"  used_root: {disc.used_root}")\n            lines.append(f"  
files_scanned: {len(disc.files_scanned)}")\n            for f in disc.files_scanned:\n                lines.append(f"  
  - {f}")\n            if disc.error:\n                lines.append(f"  error: {disc.error}")\n            counts = 
disc.summary()\n            lines.append(f"  category_counts: {counts}")\n            if disc.keys:\n                
lines.append("  keys:")\n                for k in disc.keys:\n                    lines.append(\n                      
  f"    - [{\',\'.join(k.categories)}] "\n                        f"name={k.name} tag={k.tag} value={k.raw_value!r} 
"\n                        f"writable_direct={k.writable_direct} file={Path(k.file).name}"\n                    )\n    
        else:\n                lines.append("  keys: (none matched any layout category)")\n            
lines.append("")\n        target.write_text("\\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")\n    except Exception as exc:  # 
noqa: BLE001\n        _log.debug("write_discovery_log error: %s", exc)\n    return target\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Cache 
(process-local) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n#\n# Discovery is 
expensive (dumpsys+cat for many files).  Cache the result for\n# the lifetime of the supervisor process and refresh 
when explicitly asked.\n\n_DISCOVERY_CACHE: dict[str, PackageDiscovery] = {}\n_DISCOVERY_CACHE_TS: float = 
0.0\n_DISCOVERY_CACHE_TTL = 300.0  # 5 minutes\n\n\ndef get_cached_or_discover(\n    packages: Iterable[str],\n    
*,\n    root_tool: str | None = None,\n    refresh: bool = False,\n) -> dict[str, PackageDiscovery]:\n    """Return 
cached discoveries or run a fresh discovery.\n\n    Uses an in-memory cache.  Pass ``refresh=True`` to force 
re-discovery.\n    """\n    global _DISCOVERY_CACHE_TS\n    now = time.time()\n    pkg_list = list(packages)\n    
pkg_set = set(pkg_list)\n    cached_keys = set(_DISCOVERY_CACHE.keys())\n    have_all = 
pkg_set.issubset(cached_keys)\n    fresh = (now - _DISCOVERY_CACHE_TS) < _DISCOVERY_CACHE_TTL\n    if not refresh and 
have_all and fresh:\n        return {p: _DISCOVERY_CACHE[p] for p in pkg_list}\n\n    discoveries = 
discover_all(pkg_list, root_tool=root_tool)\n    _DISCOVERY_CACHE.update(discoveries)\n    _DISCOVERY_CACHE_TS = now\n 
   return discoveries\n\n\ndef clear_cache() -> None:\n    global _DISCOVERY_CACHE_TS\n    _DISCOVERY_CACHE.clear()\n  
  _DISCOVERY_CACHE_TS = 0.0\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Convenience: render to log + return path \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef run_discovery_and_log(\n    packages: Iterable[str],\n    
*,\n    root_tool: str | None = None,\n    refresh: bool = True,\n    path: Path | None = None,\n) -> tuple[Path, 
dict[str, PackageDiscovery]]:\n    """Run discovery, write the log file, and return ``(path, discoveries)``.\n\n    
Never raises.  Always returns the path (even if writing failed).\n    """\n    try:\n        discoveries = 
get_cached_or_discover(\n            packages, root_tool=root_tool, refresh=refresh\n        )\n    except Exception 
as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _log.debug("run_discovery_and_log error: %s", exc)\n        discoveries = {}\n    
out_path = write_discovery_log(discoveries, path=path)\n    return out_path, discoveries\n\n"""DENG Tool: Rejoin ù 
Shared license key utilities.\n\nKey format:  DENG-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX\n             \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2518 \u2514\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n      
      Prefix   16 hex chars (4 groups of 4)\n\nRules:\n  ò Uppercase on display\n  ò Accept lowercase input and input 
without inner dashes\n  ò Trim spaces; normalize inner grouping dashes\n  ò Display full key in user copy views 
(Discord panel, Key Stats when export exists)\n  ò Store only the SHA-256 hash in any database (plus optional 
encrypted export blob)\n  ò install_id is a privacy-safe random UUID persisted locally\n  ò Never read IMEI, phone 
number, or Roblox session/cookie/token\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport hashlib\nimport 
json\nimport re\nimport secrets\nimport subprocess\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom 
.constants import APP_HOME, DEFAULT_LICENSE_SERVER_URL, VERSION\nfrom . import safe_http\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Constants \u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n# Path for persisted install ID (not the legacy device_id used by 
keystore.py)\nINSTALL_ID_PATH = APP_HOME / "install_id"\n\n# Canonical display format: 
DENG-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX\n_CANONICAL_RE = re.compile(\n    
r"^DENG-([0-9A-F]{4})-([0-9A-F]{4})-([0-9A-F]{4})-([0-9A-F]{4})$"\n)\n\n# Input pattern: accept with or without inner 
grouping dashes, any case\n_INPUT_HEX_RE = re.compile(r"^[0-9A-Fa-f]{16}$")\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Custom exceptions \u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nclass 
LicenseKeyError(ValueError):\n    """Raised for invalid license key format."""\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Key generation \u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\n\ndef generate_license_key() -> str:\n    """Generate a new random license key in DENG-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX 
format."""\n    raw = secrets.token_hex(8).upper()  # 16 uppercase hex chars\n    return 
f"DENG-{raw[:4]}-{raw[4:8]}-{raw[8:12]}-{raw[12:16]}"\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Normalization and validation \u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef normalize_license_key(raw: str) -> str:\n    """Normalize a license key to 
canonical DENG-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX form.\n\n    Accepts:\n      DENG-8f3a-b3c4-d5e6-44f0   (with inner dashes, 
lowercase)\n      DENG-8F3AB3C4D5E644F0       (without inner dashes)\n      deng-8f3a-b3c4-d5e6-44f0   (all 
lowercase)\n\n    Raises:\n      LicenseKeyError for any format that cannot be normalized.\n    """\n    s = (raw or 
"").strip().upper()\n    if not s.startswith("DENG-"):\n        raise LicenseKeyError(\n            "License key must 
start with DENG-  (e.g. DENG-8F3A-B3C4-D5E6-44F0)"\n        )\n    payload = s[5:]                   # Everything 
after "DENG-"\n    hex_only = payload.replace("-", "")  # Remove optional inner dashes\n    if not 
_INPUT_HEX_RE.match(hex_only):\n        raise LicenseKeyError(\n            "License key must contain exactly 16 hex 
characters after DENG- "\n            "(e.g. DENG-8F3A-B3C4-D5E6-44F0)"\n        )\n    return 
f"DENG-{hex_only[:4]}-{hex_only[4:8]}-{hex_only[8:12]}-{hex_only[12:16]}"\n\n\ndef validate_license_key(raw: str) -> 
str:\n    """Validate and normalize a license key.  Empty string = key not set.\n\n    Returns the normalized key 
string or empty string.\n    Raises LicenseKeyError if non-empty and invalid.\n    """\n    cleaned = (raw or 
"").strip()\n    if not cleaned:\n        return ""\n    return normalize_license_key(cleaned)\n\n\ndef 
mask_license_key(key: str) -> str:\n    """Return a display-safe masked version of a license key.\n\n    
DENG-8F3A-B3C4-D5E6-44F0  \u2192  DENG-8F3A...44F0\n    Old flat format:            \u2192  DENG-8F3A...44F0\n    
Empty / not set:            \u2192  Not set\n    """\n    if not key:\n        return "Not set"\n    s = (key or 
"").strip().upper()\n    if not s.startswith("DENG-"):\n        return "Not set"\n    parts = s.split("-")\n    if 
len(parts) == 5:\n        # New format: DENG-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX\n        return f"DENG-{parts[1]}...{parts[4]}"\n    
if len(parts) == 2:\n        # Old flat format: DENG-XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX\n        hex_part = parts[1]\n        if 
len(hex_part) >= 8:\n            return f"DENG-{hex_part[:4]}...{hex_part[-4:].lower()}"\n    return 
"DENG-***"\n\n\ndef hash_license_key(key: str) -> str:\n    """Return the SHA-256 hex digest of the normalized license 
key.\n\n    This is the value stored in the database.  The raw key is NEVER stored.\n    """\n    normalized = 
normalize_license_key(key)\n    return hashlib.sha256(normalized.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 
Install ID (privacy-safe device identity) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\n\ndef get_or_create_install_id() -> str:\n    """Return the install ID for this device, creating one if 
needed.\n\n    The install ID is a randomly generated 32-char hex string that persists\n    locally at 
``~/.deng-tool/rejoin/install_id``.\n\n    Privacy guarantee:\n      ò No IMEI, MAC address, phone number, or hardware 
serial is read.\n      ò Only the SHA-256 hash of this ID is ever transmitted to any server.\n      ò The ID can be 
reset by deleting the file or resetting from Discord.\n    """\n    INSTALL_ID_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, 
exist_ok=True)\n    if INSTALL_ID_PATH.exists():\n        stored = 
INSTALL_ID_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()\n        if re.fullmatch(r"[0-9a-f]{32}", stored):\n            
return stored\n    install_id = secrets.token_hex(16)  # 32 lowercase hex chars\n    
INSTALL_ID_PATH.write_text(install_id + "\\n", encoding="utf-8")\n    return install_id\n\n\ndef 
hash_install_id(install_id: str) -> str:\n    """Return the SHA-256 hash of the install ID.\n\n    Always send this 
hash to remote servers ù never the raw install_id.\n    """\n    return 
hashlib.sha256(install_id.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Device summary (public / safe properties 
only) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef get_device_summary() -> dict[str, str]:\n    """Return 
a privacy-safe dict of public device properties.\n\n    ``model`` uses :func:`get_public_device_model` 
(getprop-based).\n    """\n    return {"model": get_public_device_model()}\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Remote license API 
(POST /api/license/check) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nWRONG_DEVICE_USER_MESS
AGE = (\n    "Wrong device. Open DENG Tool: Rejoin Panel and use Reset HWID."\n)\n\nKEY_NOT_REDEEMED_API_MESSAGE = (\n 
   "This key has not been redeemed yet. Redeem it in the DENG Tool: Rejoin Panel first."\n)\n\nREDEEM_IN_PANEL_HINT = 
"Redeem this key in the Discord panel first."\n\n\ndef _getprop(prop: str) -> str:\n    """Read a single Android 
system property (Termux). Returns \'\' on failure."""\n    try:\n        result = subprocess.run(\n            
["getprop", prop],\n            stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,\n            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n            
stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,\n            text=True,\n            timeout=4,\n            shell=False,\n        )\n      
  if result.returncode == 0:\n            v = (result.stdout or "").strip()\n            if v.lower() in ("", 
"unknown"):\n                return ""\n            return v[:120]\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
pass\n    return ""\n\n\ndef get_public_device_model() -> str:\n    """Public handset identifier for license binding, 
webhooks, and stats.\n\n    Priority:\n      1. ``ro.product.model`` (e.g. SM-S9160, Pixel 9 Pro XL)\n      2. 
``ro.product.manufacturer`` + ``ro.product.model``\n      3. ``ro.product.device``\n\n    Never blocks license; 
returns ``Unknown`` if nothing usable.\n    """\n    model = _getprop("ro.product.model")\n    manufacturer = 
_getprop("ro.product.manufacturer")\n    device = _getprop("ro.product.device")\n\n    if model:\n        return 
model[:120]\n    if manufacturer:\n        extra = device or _getprop("ro.product.name")\n        if extra and 
extra.lower() != manufacturer.lower():\n            return f"{manufacturer} {extra}"[:120]\n        return 
manufacturer[:120]\n    if device:\n        return device[:120]\n    return "Unknown"\n\n\ndef 
sync_install_id_with_config(license_section: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    """Ensure 
``license_section[\'install_id\']`` and ``INSTALL_ID_PATH`` agree.\n\n    Preference order: valid id already in config 
\u2192 valid id on disk \u2192 generate new.\n    """\n    lic = license_section\n    cfg_id = (lic.get("install_id") 
or "").strip().lower()\n    file_id = ""\n    if INSTALL_ID_PATH.exists():\n        file_id = 
INSTALL_ID_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip().lower()\n\n    if re.fullmatch(r"[0-9a-f]{32}", cfg_id):\n        
if file_id != cfg_id:\n            INSTALL_ID_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n            
INSTALL_ID_PATH.write_text(cfg_id + "\\n", encoding="utf-8")\n        return cfg_id\n\n    if 
re.fullmatch(r"[0-9a-f]{32}", file_id):\n        lic["install_id"] = file_id\n        return file_id\n\n    new_id = 
secrets.token_hex(16)\n    lic["install_id"] = new_id\n    INSTALL_ID_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n 
   INSTALL_ID_PATH.write_text(new_id + "\\n", encoding="utf-8")\n    return new_id\n\n\ndef 
_license_api_post_json(url: str, payload: dict[str, Any], *, timeout: int = 30) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """POST to the 
license API.  Uses safe_http which routes through curl on\n    Termux to prevent Python ssl/OpenSSL SIGSEGV from 
killing the main process.\n    """\n    try:\n        return safe_http.post_json(url, payload, timeout=timeout)\n    
except safe_http.SafeHttpStatusError as exc:\n        # Try to parse error JSON embedded in the raised exception 
body.\n        try:\n            parsed: dict[str, Any] = json.loads(exc.body)\n            if isinstance(parsed, 
dict) and parsed.get("result"):\n                return parsed\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError, 
AttributeError):\n            pass\n        return {"result": "server_unavailable", "message": f"HTTP 
{exc.status_code}"}\n    except safe_http.SafeHttpNetworkError as exc:\n        return {"result": 
"server_unavailable", "message": str(exc)}\n    except safe_http.SafeHttpJsonError:\n        return {"result": 
"server_unavailable", "message": "Invalid JSON from license server"}\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
return {"result": "server_unavailable", "message": "Network error"}\n\n\ndef check_remote_license_status(\n    
server_url: str,\n    *,\n    license_key: str,\n    install_id: str,\n    device_model: str,\n    app_version: str,\n 
   device_label: str = "",\n    timeout: int = 30,\n) -> tuple[str, str]:\n    """Call the public license API; return 
``(result, message)``.\n\n    Sends only: hashed install id, key, device model, version, optional label ù\n    never 
Supabase secrets, tokens, or cookies.\n    """\n    base = (server_url or 
DEFAULT_LICENSE_SERVER_URL).strip().rstrip("/")\n    url = f"{base}/api/license/check"\n    install_id_hash = 
hash_install_id(install_id.strip())\n    payload: dict[str, Any] = {\n        "key": 
normalize_license_key(license_key),\n        "install_id_hash": install_id_hash,\n        "device_model": 
(device_model or "unknown")[:120],\n        "app_version": (app_version or VERSION or "unknown")[:40],\n    }\n    
label = (device_label or "").strip()[:80]\n    if label:\n        payload["device_label"] = label\n\n    try:\n        
resp = _license_api_post_json(url, payload, timeout=timeout)\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return 
"server_unavailable", "License server temporarily unavailable."\n\n    result = str(resp.get("result") or 
"server_unavailable").strip().lower()\n    message = str(resp.get("message") or "").strip()\n    if result == 
"wrong_device":\n        return result, WRONG_DEVICE_USER_MESSAGE\n    if result == "key_not_redeemed":\n        
return result, REDEEM_IN_PANEL_HINT\n    if not message:\n        message = {\n            "active": "License 
active.",\n            "not_found": "Key not found.",\n            "revoked": "This key has been revoked.",\n          
  "expired": "This key has expired.",\n            "inactive": "License inactive.",\n            "server_unavailable": 
"License server temporarily unavailable.",\n            "missing_key": "No license key provided.",\n            
"key_not_redeemed": KEY_NOT_REDEEMED_API_MESSAGE,\n        }.get(result, result)\n    return result, 
message\n\n"""Server-side optional encryption for exportable license keys (Fernet).\n\nFull raw keys are not stored in 
plaintext. When LICENSE_KEY_EXPORT_SECRET is set\nand the cryptography package is installed, keys can store a Fernet 
ciphertext so\nthe owning Discord user can export the full key from Key Stats / Download.\nRows without ciphertext can 
be backfilled by the owner (Recover / redeem-again)\nwhen the secret and DB columns exist.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ 
import annotations\n\nimport base64\nimport hashlib\nimport os\nfrom functools import lru_cache\n\nEXPORT_SECRET_ENV = 
"LICENSE_KEY_EXPORT_SECRET"\n\n\n@lru_cache(maxsize=1)\ndef _fernet():\n    try:\n        from cryptography.fernet 
import Fernet\n    except ImportError:\n        return None\n    raw = os.environ.get(EXPORT_SECRET_ENV, "").strip()\n 
   if not raw:\n        return None\n    digest = hashlib.sha256(raw.encode("utf-8")).digest()\n    key = 
base64.urlsafe_b64encode(digest)\n    return Fernet(key)\n\n\ndef is_export_secret_configured() -> bool:\n    return 
bool(os.environ.get(EXPORT_SECRET_ENV, "").strip()) and _fernet() is not None\n\n\ndef 
encrypt_license_key_plaintext(plain_key: str) -> str | None:\n    """Return Fernet token string, or None if encryption 
is unavailable."""\n    f = _fernet()\n    if not f:\n        return None\n    try:\n        return 
f.encrypt(plain_key.strip().encode("utf-8")).decode("ascii")\n    except Exception:\n        return None\n\n\ndef 
decrypt_license_key_ciphertext(token: str) -> str | None:\n    """Decrypt ciphertext to full key, or None on 
failure."""\n    f = _fernet()\n    if not f or not token:\n        return None\n    try:\n        return 
f.decrypt(token.encode("ascii")).decode("utf-8")\n    except Exception:\n        return None\n\n\ndef 
clear_export_key_cache() -> None:\n    """Clear Fernet cache (tests / secret rotation)."""\n    
_fernet.cache_clear()\n\n"""Owner/admin maintenance for license key export backfill and unrecoverable-key 
reset.\n\nSafe helpers used by ``scripts/license_owner_key_recovery.py``. No Discord imports.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ 
import annotations\n\nimport hashlib\nimport json\nimport os\nimport re\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom 
pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom .license import (\n    LicenseKeyError,\n    hash_license_key,\n   
 normalize_license_key,\n)\nfrom .license_store import (\n    BaseLicenseStore,\n    LocalJsonLicenseStore,\n    
SupabaseLicenseStore,\n    _utc_now,\n)\n\nCONFIRM_RESET_TOKEN = "RESET_OWNER_KEY"\nDEFAULT_BACKFILL_ENV_VAR = 
"DENG_REJOIN_BACKFILL_FULL_KEY"\n\n_MASKISH_KEY_RE = re.compile(r"\\.\\.\\.|à")\n\n\nclass 
OwnerRecoveryError(RuntimeError):\n    """User-fixable validation error for recovery tooling."""\n\n\ndef 
visible_license_rows_for_panel(rows: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    """Hide revoked keys from Key 
Stats / Download UI (rows remain in the database)."""\n    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n    for r in rows:\n        
if str(r.get("license_status") or "").lower() == "revoked":\n            continue\n        out.append(r)\n    return 
out\n\n\ndef parse_single_owner_target_from_env() -> str | None:\n    """Return the sole Discord ID from 
LICENSE_OWNER_DISCORD_IDS, or None if not exactly one."""\n    raw = os.environ.get("LICENSE_OWNER_DISCORD_IDS", "")\n 
   ids: list[str] = []\n    for part in raw.split(","):\n        p = part.strip()\n        if p.isdigit():\n           
 ids.append(p)\n    return ids[0] if len(ids) == 1 else None\n\n\ndef backup_filename_timestamp() -> str:\n    return 
datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")\n\n\ndef verify_supabase_export_columns(client: Any) -> 
tuple[bool, str]:\n    """Return (ok, detail) after probing ``key_ciphertext`` / ``key_export_available``."""\n    
try:\n        client.table("license_keys").select("key_ciphertext,key_export_available").limit(0).execute()\n        
return True, "license_keys export columns present"\n    except Exception as exc:\n        err = str(exc)\n        if 
(\n            "PGRST204" in err\n            or "42703" in err\n            or "column" in err.lower()\n            
or "undefined_column" in err.lower()\n            or "schema cache" in err.lower()\n        ):\n            return 
False, "missing columns ù apply supabase/migrations/002_key_export_support.sql"\n        return False, f"probe failed: 
{err}"\n\n\ndef _sanitize_key_row(rec: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    out = dict(rec)\n    ct = 
(out.get("key_ciphertext") or "").strip()\n    if ct:\n        out["key_ciphertext"] = None\n        
out["_key_ciphertext_present"] = True\n        out["_key_ciphertext_sha256_prefix"] = 
hashlib.sha256(ct.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]\n    else:\n        out["_key_ciphertext_present"] = False\n    return 
out\n\n\ndef fetch_owner_snapshot(store: BaseLicenseStore, discord_user_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Return 
serializable snapshot for backup (no plaintext keys)."""\n    if isinstance(store, LocalJsonLicenseStore):\n        db 
= store._load()\n        keys_out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n        bindings_out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n        
for kid, rec in db.get("keys", {}).items():\n            if rec.get("owner_discord_id") != discord_user_id:\n          
      continue\n            keys_out.append({"key_id": kid, **_sanitize_key_row(dict(rec))})\n            bind = 
dict(db.get("bindings", {}).get(kid, {}))\n            if bind:\n                bindings_out.append({"key_id": kid, 
**bind})\n        user_row = dict(db.get("users", {}).get(discord_user_id, {}))\n        users_blob = [user_row] if 
discord_user_id in db.get("users", {}) else []\n        audits = [\n            dict(e)\n            for e in 
db.get("audit_logs", [])\n            if str(e.get("actor_discord_id")) == discord_user_id\n            or 
str(e.get("metadata", {}).get("owner")) == discord_user_id\n        ][-50:]\n        return {\n            
"license_users": users_blob,\n            "license_keys": keys_out,\n            "device_bindings": bindings_out,\n    
        "audit_tail": audits,\n        }\n\n    if isinstance(store, SupabaseLicenseStore):\n        client = 
store._client\n        lu = (\n            client.table("license_users")\n            .select("*")\n            
.eq("discord_user_id", discord_user_id)\n            .execute()\n        )\n        lk = (\n            
client.table("license_keys")\n            .select("*")\n            .eq("owner_discord_id", discord_user_id)\n         
   .execute()\n        )\n        keys_out = []\n        bindings_out = []\n        for rec in lk.data or []:\n        
    kid = rec["id"]\n            keys_out.append({"key_id": kid, **_sanitize_key_row(dict(rec))})\n            b = (\n 
               client.table("device_bindings")\n                .select("*")\n                .eq("key_id", kid)\n     
           .execute()\n            )\n            if b.data:\n                bindings_out.append({"key_id": kid, 
**dict(b.data[0])})\n        aa = (\n            client.table("admin_audit_logs")\n            .select("*")\n          
  .eq("actor_discord_id", discord_user_id)\n            .limit(50)\n            .execute()\n        )\n        return 
{\n            "license_users": lu.data or [],\n            "license_keys": keys_out,\n            "device_bindings": 
bindings_out,\n            "audit_tail": aa.data or [],\n        }\n\n    raise OwnerRecoveryError("Unsupported 
license store implementation")\n\n\ndef write_backup_file(root: Path, discord_user_id: str, snapshot: dict[str, Any]) 
-> Path:\n    """Write timestamped JSON under ``data/backups/``."""\n    backup_dir = root / "data" / "backups"\n    
backup_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    ts = backup_filename_timestamp()\n    path = backup_dir / 
f"license_owner_key_backup_{ts}.json"\n    payload = {\n        "backup_created_at": 
datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n        "discord_user_id": discord_user_id,\n        **snapshot,\n    }\n    
path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2, default=str) + "\\n", encoding="utf-8")\n    return path\n\n\ndef 
list_active_owned_key_targets(store: BaseLicenseStore, discord_user_id: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    """Rows for 
keys owned by user with status != revoked."""\n    snap = fetch_owner_snapshot(store, discord_user_id)\n    rows = 
[]\n    for kr in snap.get("license_keys", []):\n        st = str(kr.get("status") or "active").lower()\n        if st 
== "revoked":\n            continue\n        rows.append(kr)\n    return rows\n\n\ndef 
backfill_plaintext_for_owner_key(\n    store: BaseLicenseStore,\n    discord_user_id: str,\n    plaintext_full_key: 
str,\n    *,\n    key_id: str | None,\n    actor_label: str = "license_owner_recovery",\n) -> str:\n    """Encrypt and 
store ciphertext for one owned key after validating hash match.\n\n    Returns a short status message for logs (never 
includes full key).\n    """\n    from . import license_key_export as lke\n\n    raw = (plaintext_full_key or 
"").strip()\n    if _MASKISH_KEY_RE.search(raw):\n        raise OwnerRecoveryError(\n            "Provided key looks 
masked (contains à or ...). Full plaintext key required."\n        )\n    try:\n        normalized = 
normalize_license_key(raw)\n    except LicenseKeyError as exc:\n        raise OwnerRecoveryError(f"Invalid license key 
format: {exc}") from exc\n\n    expect_hash = hash_license_key(normalized)\n    targets = 
list_active_owned_key_targets(store, discord_user_id)\n    if not targets:\n        raise OwnerRecoveryError("No 
active owned keys found for this Discord user.")\n    if len(targets) > 1 and not key_id:\n        raise 
OwnerRecoveryError(\n            "Multiple active keys owned ù pass explicit --key-id <sha256> "\n            "(see 
inspect output)."\n        )\n    tid = key_id or targets[0]["key_id"]\n    if tid != expect_hash:\n        raise 
OwnerRecoveryError(\n            "Provided full key does not match the stored key fingerprint for this owner."\n       
 )\n    owned_ids = {t["key_id"] for t in targets}\n    if tid not in owned_ids:\n        raise 
OwnerRecoveryError("key_id does not match an active owned key.")\n\n    if not lke.is_export_secret_configured():\n    
    raise OwnerRecoveryError(\n            "LICENSE_KEY_EXPORT_SECRET is not set or cryptography is unavailable."\n    
    )\n\n    ct = lke.encrypt_license_key_plaintext(normalized)\n    if not ct:\n        raise 
OwnerRecoveryError("Encryption returned empty ciphertext.")\n\n    plain_roundtrip = 
lke.decrypt_license_key_ciphertext(ct)\n    if plain_roundtrip != normalized:\n        raise 
OwnerRecoveryError("Encrypt/decrypt round-trip failed.")\n\n    if isinstance(store, LocalJsonLicenseStore):\n        
db = store._load()\n        rec = db.get("keys", {}).get(tid)\n        if not rec or rec.get("owner_discord_id") != 
discord_user_id:\n            raise OwnerRecoveryError("Key row missing or wrong owner.")\n        
db["keys"][tid]["key_ciphertext"] = ct\n        db["keys"][tid]["key_export_available"] = True\n        
db["keys"][tid]["updated_at"] = _utc_now()\n        store._save(db)\n        store.audit_admin_action(\n            
actor_label,\n            "owner_recovery_backfill",\n            target_type="key",\n            target_id=tid[:8],\n 
           metadata={"discord_user_id": discord_user_id},\n        )\n        return "backfilled_local_store"\n\n    
if isinstance(store, SupabaseLicenseStore):\n        ok, msg = verify_supabase_export_columns(store._client)\n        
if not ok:\n            raise OwnerRecoveryError(msg)\n        store._client.table("license_keys").update({\n          
  "key_ciphertext": ct,\n            "key_export_available": True,\n        }).eq("id", tid).eq("owner_discord_id", 
discord_user_id).execute()\n        store.audit_admin_action(\n            actor_label,\n            
"owner_recovery_backfill",\n            target_type="key",\n            target_id=tid[:8],\n            
metadata={"discord_user_id": discord_user_id},\n        )\n        return "backfilled_supabase"\n\n    raise 
OwnerRecoveryError("Unsupported store")\n\n\ndef reset_unrecoverable_owner_keys(\n    store: BaseLicenseStore,\n    
discord_user_id: str,\n    *,\n    confirm_token: str,\n    actor_label: str = "license_owner_recovery",\n
