\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n    def get_license_stats_for_discord_user(\n        self, discord_user_id: str\n 
   ) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        """Return license key statistics for a Discord user.\n\n        This is the canonical 
stats function ù designed to be called from\n        Discord embeds, the /license admin command, and future 
ecosystem\n        integrations.  All counts are computed from the local JSON store.\n\n        Returns dict with:\n   
       key_generated_count  ù active keys created by this user\n          key_redeemed_count   ù keys that were 
redeemed/activated (redeemed_at set OR currently bound)\n          unbound_key_count    ù owned keys with no active 
device binding\n          bound_key_count      ù owned keys with an active device binding\n          reset_hwid_count  
   ù total HWID resets on this user\'s keys\n          key_executed_count   ù public-release tool executions (always 0 
for local store)\n        """\n        db = self._load()\n        generated = 0\n        redeemed = 0\n        unbound 
= 0\n        bound = 0\n        for key_hash, record in db["keys"].items():\n            owner = 
record.get("owner_discord_id")\n            creator = record.get("created_by")\n            status = 
record.get("status", "active")\n            # Generated: only count active keys (not revoked/permanently dead)\n       
     if creator == discord_user_id and status == "active":\n                generated += 1\n            if owner != 
discord_user_id:\n                continue\n            binding = db.get("bindings", {}).get(key_hash, {})\n           
 # Redeemed: count if explicitly redeemed (redeemed_at set) OR currently\n            # bound (handles keys bound 
before migration 003 added redeemed_at).\n            if record.get("redeemed_at") or binding.get("is_active"):\n      
          redeemed += 1\n            if binding.get("is_active"):\n                bound += 1\n            else:\n     
           unbound += 1\n        reset_count = sum(\n            1 for entry in db.get("reset_logs", [])\n            
if entry.get("owner_discord_id") == discord_user_id\n        )\n        return {\n            "discord_user_id": 
discord_user_id,\n            "key_generated_count": generated,\n            "key_redeemed_count": redeemed,\n         
   "unbound_key_count": unbound,\n            "bound_key_count": bound,\n            "reset_hwid_count": 
reset_count,\n            "key_executed_count": 0,  # local store: execution tracking not available\n        
}\n\n\ndef get_license_stats_for_discord_user(\n    store: "BaseLicenseStore", discord_user_id: str\n) -> dict[str, 
Any]:\n    """Module-level wrapper ù callable from any ecosystem code without holding\n    a store reference directly. 
 Delegates to the store\'s own method if\n    available; falls back to a safe empty stats dict."""\n    try:\n        
return store.get_license_stats_for_discord_user(discord_user_id)\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
return {\n            "discord_user_id": discord_user_id,\n            "key_generated_count": 0,\n            
"key_redeemed_count": 0,\n            "unbound_key_count": 0,\n            "bound_key_count": 0,\n            
"reset_hwid_count": 0,\n            "key_executed_count": 0,\n        }\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Supabase implementation 
(production) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\n\nclass SupabaseLicenseStore(BaseLicenseStore):\n    """Production Supabase-backed license store.\n\n    Requires 
SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY in the environment.\n    The service-role key is used server-side only and 
is never exposed to clients.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        import os\n        from dotenv 
import load_dotenv\n\n        load_dotenv()\n        url = os.environ.get("SUPABASE_URL", "").strip()\n        key = 
os.environ.get("SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY", "").strip()\n        if not url or not key:\n            raise 
RuntimeError(\n                "Both SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY must be set."\n            )\n        
try:\n            from supabase import create_client\n        except ImportError as exc:\n            raise 
RuntimeError(\n                "supabase-py is not installed. Run: pip install supabase"\n            ) from exc\n     
   self._client = create_client(url, key)\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 User helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n    def get_or_create_user(\n        self, 
discord_user_id: str, discord_username: str | None = None\n    ) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        res = (\n            
self._client.table("license_users")\n            .select("*")\n            .eq("discord_user_id", discord_user_id)\n   
         .execute()\n        )\n        if res.data:\n            user = res.data[0]\n            if discord_username 
and discord_username != user.get("discord_username"):\n                self._client.table("license_users").update(\n   
                 {"discord_username": discord_username}\n                ).eq("discord_user_id", 
discord_user_id).execute()\n                user["discord_username"] = discord_username\n            return user\n     
   # Create new user\n        res = self._client.table("license_users").insert(\n            {\n                
"discord_user_id": discord_user_id,\n                "discord_username": discord_username or "",\n            }\n      
  ).execute()\n        return res.data[0]\n\n    def get_user_by_discord_id(self, discord_user_id: str) -> dict[str, 
Any] | None:\n        res = (\n            self._client.table("license_users")\n            .select("*")\n            
.eq("discord_user_id", discord_user_id)\n            .execute()\n        )\n        return res.data[0] if res.data 
else None\n\n    def set_user_max_keys(self, discord_user_id: str, max_keys: int) -> None:\n        res = (\n          
  self._client.table("license_users")\n            .update({"max_keys": max(1, int(max_keys))})\n            
.eq("discord_user_id", discord_user_id)\n            .execute()\n        )\n        if not res.data:\n            
raise KeyNotFoundError(f"User not found: {discord_user_id}")\n\n    def count_user_keys(self, discord_user_id: str) -> 
int:\n        res = (\n            self._client.table("license_keys")\n            .select("id", count="exact")\n      
      .eq("owner_discord_id", discord_user_id)\n            .neq("status", "revoked")\n            .execute()\n        
)\n        return res.count or 0\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 Key creation and redemption \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n    def 
create_key_for_user(\n        self, discord_user_id: str, created_by: str | None = None\n    ) -> str:\n        user = 
self.get_or_create_user(discord_user_id)\n        if user.get("is_blocked"):\n            raise UserLimitError("This 
account is blocked from generating keys.")\n\n        # Cooldown check ù use last_key_generated_at from user record\n  
      last_gen = user.get("last_key_generated_at")\n        if last_gen:\n            try:\n                elapsed = 
_seconds_since(last_gen)\n                if elapsed is not None and elapsed < GENERATION_COOLDOWN_SECONDS:\n          
          remaining = int(GENERATION_COOLDOWN_SECONDS - elapsed) + 1\n                    raise 
GenerationCooldownError(\n                        f"Please wait {remaining} seconds before generating another key.",\n 
                       remaining_seconds=remaining,\n                    )\n            except 
GenerationCooldownError:\n                raise\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n\n        
raw_key = generate_license_key()\n        key_hash = hash_license_key(raw_key)\n        parts = raw_key.split("-")\n   
     from . import license_key_export as lke\n\n        ciphertext = lke.encrypt_license_key_plaintext(raw_key)\n      
  row: dict[str, Any] = {\n            "id": key_hash,\n            "prefix": f"{parts[0]}-{parts[1]}",\n            
"suffix": parts[-1],\n            "owner_discord_id": discord_user_id,\n            "status": "active",\n            
"plan": "standard",\n            "expires_at": None,\n            "redeemed_at": None,\n            "created_by": 
created_by or discord_user_id,\n        }\n        if ciphertext:\n            row["key_ciphertext"] = ciphertext\n    
        row["key_export_available"] = True\n        else:\n            row["key_export_available"] = False\n        
try:\n            self._client.table("license_keys").insert(row).execute()\n        except Exception as exc:\n         
   err = str(exc).lower()\n            if (\n                "key_ciphertext" in err\n                or 
"key_export_available" in err\n                or "redeemed_at" in err\n                or "column" in err\n           
     or "pgrst204" in err\n            ):\n                row.pop("key_ciphertext", None)\n                
row.pop("key_export_available", None)\n                row.pop("redeemed_at", None)\n                
self._client.table("license_keys").insert(row).execute()\n            else:\n                raise\n        # Update 
last_key_generated_at in user record\n        try:\n            self._client.table("license_users").update(\n          
      {"last_key_generated_at": _utc_now()}\n            ).eq("discord_user_id", discord_user_id).execute()\n        
except Exception:\n            pass\n        self.audit_admin_action(\n            created_by or discord_user_id,\n    
        "create_key",\n            target_type="key",\n            target_id=key_hash[:8],\n            
metadata={"owner": discord_user_id},\n        )\n        return raw_key\n\n    def redeem_key_for_user(self, 
discord_user_id: str, raw_key: str) -> str:\n        try:\n            normalized = normalize_license_key(raw_key)\n   
     except LicenseKeyError as exc:\n            raise KeyNotFoundError(str(exc)) from exc\n        key_hash = 
hash_license_key(normalized)\n        res = (\n            self._client.table("license_keys")\n            
.select("*")\n            .eq("id", key_hash)\n            .execute()\n        )\n        if not res.data:\n           
 raise KeyNotFoundError("Key not found. Check the key and try again.")\n        record = res.data[0]\n        if 
record.get("status") == "revoked":\n            raise KeyNotFoundError("This key has been revoked.")\n        owner = 
record.get("owner_discord_id")\n        if owner == discord_user_id:\n            from . import license_key_export as 
lke\n\n            backfilled = False\n            ct = lke.encrypt_license_key_plaintext(normalized)\n            if 
ct:\n                existing_ct = (record.get("key_ciphertext") or "").strip()\n                plain = (\n           
         lke.decrypt_license_key_ciphertext(existing_ct)\n                    if existing_ct\n                    else 
None\n                )\n                if not existing_ct or not plain:\n                    try:\n                  
      self._client.table("license_keys").update({\n                            "key_ciphertext": ct,\n                 
           "key_export_available": True,\n                        }).eq("id", key_hash).execute()\n                    
    backfilled = True\n                    except Exception as exc:\n                        err = str(exc).lower()\n  
                      if (\n                            "column" in err\n                            or "pgrst204" in 
err\n                            or "key_ciphertext" in err\n                            or "key_export_available" in 
err\n                        ):\n                            pass\n                        else:\n                     
       raise\n            raise KeyAlreadySelfOwned(\n                "This key is already attached to your 
account.",\n                export_backfilled=backfilled,\n            )\n        if owner and owner != 
discord_user_id:\n            raise KeyOwnershipError("This key belongs to another user.")\n        # Key is unowned; 
check limit before attaching\n        user = self.get_or_create_user(discord_user_id)\n        current = 
self.count_user_keys(discord_user_id)\n        max_keys = user.get("max_keys", DEFAULT_MAX_KEYS)\n        if current 
>= max_keys:\n            raise UserLimitError(\n                f"You have reached your license key limit 
({max_keys})."\n            )\n        self._client.table("license_keys").update(\n            {"owner_discord_id": 
discord_user_id}\n        ).eq("id", key_hash).execute()\n        self.audit_admin_action(\n            
discord_user_id,\n            "redeem_key",\n            target_type="key",\n            target_id=key_hash[:8],\n     
   )\n        return normalized\n\n    def list_user_keys(self, discord_user_id: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n       
 from . import license_key_export as lke\n\n        try:\n            res = (\n                
self._client.table("license_keys")\n                .select(\n                    "id, prefix, suffix, status, plan, 
created_at, key_ciphertext, key_export_available"\n                )\n                .eq("owner_discord_id", 
discord_user_id)\n                .execute()\n            )\n            records = res.data or []\n        except 
Exception:\n            res = (\n                self._client.table("license_keys")\n                .select("id, 
prefix, suffix, status, plan, created_at")\n                .eq("owner_discord_id", discord_user_id)\n                
.execute()\n            )\n            records = res.data or []\n\n        result: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n        
for record in records:\n            key_id = record["id"]\n            b_res = (\n                
self._client.table("device_bindings")\n                .select("device_model, last_seen_at, is_active")\n              
  .eq("key_id", key_id)\n                .execute()\n            )\n            binding = b_res.data[0] if b_res.data 
else {}\n            active = bool(binding.get("is_active"))\n            masked = f"{record.get(\'prefix\', 
\'DENG-????\')}...{record.get(\'suffix\', \'????\')}"\n            ciphertext = (record.get("key_ciphertext") or "") 
if "key_ciphertext" in record else ""\n            full_plain = lke.decrypt_license_key_ciphertext(ciphertext) if 
ciphertext else None\n            if active:\n                bound_device = binding.get("device_model") or 
"(unbound)"\n                last_seen = binding.get("last_seen_at")\n            else:\n                bound_device 
= "(unbound)"\n                last_seen = None\n            result.append(\n                {\n                    
"id": key_id,\n                    "masked_key": masked,\n                    "full_key_plaintext": full_plain,\n      
              "status": record.get("status", "unknown"),\n                    "plan": record.get("plan", 
"standard"),\n                    "bound_device": bound_device,\n                    "last_seen_at": last_seen,\n      
              "created_at": record.get("created_at"),\n                }\n            )\n        return result\n\n    
def list_user_keys_with_binding_state(\n        self, discord_user_id: str\n    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n        
from . import license_key_export as lke\n\n        try:\n            res = (\n                
self._client.table("license_keys")\n                .select("id, prefix, suffix, status, key_ciphertext, 
key_export_available")\n                .eq("owner_discord_id", discord_user_id)\n                .neq("status", 
"revoked")\n                .execute()\n            )\n            records = res.data or []\n        except 
Exception:\n            res = (\n                self._client.table("license_keys")\n                .select("id, 
prefix, suffix, status")\n                .eq("owner_discord_id", discord_user_id)\n                .neq("status", 
"revoked")\n                .execute()\n            )\n            records = res.data or []\n\n        result: 
list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n        for record in records:\n            key_id = record["id"]\n            b_res = (\n  
              self._client.table("device_bindings")\n                .select("device_model, device_label, 
last_seen_at, is_active")\n                .eq("key_id", key_id)\n                .execute()\n            )\n          
  binding = b_res.data[0] if b_res.data else {}\n            active_binding = bool(binding.get("is_active"))\n         
   masked = f"{record.get(\'prefix\', \'DENG-????\')}...{record.get(\'suffix\', \'????\')}"\n            ciphertext = 
(record.get("key_ciphertext") or "") if "key_ciphertext" in record else ""\n            full_plain = 
lke.decrypt_license_key_ciphertext(ciphertext) if ciphertext else None\n            last_seen_at = 
binding.get("last_seen_at")\n            reset_count = self.get_reset_count_24h(key_id)\n            reason: str | 
None = None\n            if not active_binding:\n                can_reset = False\n                reason = "No 
device bound ù start the tool first"\n            elif reset_count >= MAX_HWID_RESETS_PER_24H:\n                
can_reset = False\n                reason = f"Reset limit reached ({reset_count}/{MAX_HWID_RESETS_PER_24H} today)"\n   
         else:\n                # Cooldown is based only on actual reset history, not on last_seen_at.\n               
 # First reset is always allowed immediately if no previous reset has occurred.\n                can_reset = True\n    
        result.append({\n                "key_id": key_id,\n                "masked_key": masked,\n                
"full_key_plaintext": full_plain,\n                "status": record.get("status", "unknown"),\n                
"active_binding": active_binding,\n                "device_model": binding.get("device_model", ""),\n                
"device_label": binding.get("device_label", ""),\n                "last_seen_at": last_seen_at,\n                
"reset_count_24h": reset_count,\n                "can_reset": can_reset,\n                "reason_if_not_resettable": 
reason,\n            })\n        return result\n\n    def list_user_keys_for_stats(self, discord_user_id: str) -> 
list[dict[str, Any]]:\n        from . import license_key_export as lke\n\n        try:\n            res = (\n          
      self._client.table("license_keys")\n                .select(\n                    "id, prefix, suffix, status, 
plan, created_at, key_ciphertext, key_export_available"\n                )\n                .eq("owner_discord_id", 
discord_user_id)\n                .execute()\n            )\n            records = res.data or []\n        except 
Exception:\n            res = (\n                self._client.table("license_keys")\n                .select("id, 
prefix, suffix, status, plan, created_at")\n                .eq("owner_discord_id", discord_user_id)\n                
.execute()\n            )\n            records = res.data or []\n\n        rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n        
exp_cfg = lke.is_export_secret_configured()\n        for record in records:\n            key_id = record["id"]\n       
     b_res = (\n                self._client.table("device_bindings")\n                .select("device_model, 
device_label, last_seen_at, is_active")\n                .eq("key_id", key_id)\n                .execute()\n           
 )\n            binding = b_res.data[0] if b_res.data else {}\n            active_binding = 
bool(binding.get("is_active"))\n            masked = f"{record.get(\'prefix\', 
\'DENG-????\')}...{record.get(\'suffix\', \'????\')}"\n            lic_status = record.get("status", "active")\n       
     ciphertext = (record.get("key_ciphertext") or "") if "key_ciphertext" in record else ""\n            has_blob = 
bool(ciphertext)\n            full_plain = lke.decrypt_license_key_ciphertext(ciphertext) if has_blob else None\n      
      device = (binding.get("device_model") or binding.get("device_label") or "").strip() or None\n            
reset_count = self.get_reset_count_24h(key_id)\n            plan = record.get("plan", "standard") or "standard"\n      
      rows.append({\n                "masked_key": masked,\n                "full_key_plaintext": full_plain,\n        
        "has_stored_ciphertext": has_blob,\n                "export_storage_configured": exp_cfg,\n                
"license_status": lic_status,\n                "used": active_binding,\n                "device_display": device,\n    
            "last_seen_at": binding.get("last_seen_at"),\n                "created_at": record.get("created_at"),\n    
            "plan": plan,\n                "reset_count_24h": reset_count,\n            })\n        
rows.sort(key=lambda r: str(r.get("created_at") or ""), reverse=True)\n        return rows\n\n    def 
recover_key_export_for_user(self, discord_user_id: str, raw_key: str) -> str:\n        from . import 
license_key_export as lke\n\n        if not lke.is_export_secret_configured():\n            raise 
ExportStorageUnavailable(\n                "Full key export storage is not enabled on this server."\n            )\n   
     try:\n            normalized = normalize_license_key(raw_key)\n        except LicenseKeyError as exc:\n           
 raise KeyNotFoundError(str(exc)) from exc\n        key_hash = hash_license_key(normalized)\n        res = (\n         
   self._client.table("license_keys")\n            .select("*")\n            .eq("id", key_hash)\n            
.execute()\n        )\n        if not res.data:\n            raise KeyNotFoundError("Key not found. Check the key and 
try again.")\n        record = res.data[0]\n        if record.get("owner_discord_id") != discord_user_id:\n            
raise KeyOwnershipError("That key does not belong to your account.")\n        if record.get("status") == "revoked":\n  
          raise KeyNotFoundError("This key has been revoked.")\n\n        existing_ct = ""\n        if 
"key_ciphertext" in record:\n            existing_ct = (record.get("key_ciphertext") or "").strip()\n        if 
existing_ct:\n            if lke.decrypt_license_key_ciphertext(existing_ct):\n                return 
"already_exportable"\n\n        ciphertext = lke.encrypt_license_key_plaintext(normalized)\n        if not 
ciphertext:\n            raise ExportStorageUnavailable("Could not encrypt key for storage.")\n\n        try:\n        
    self._client.table("license_keys").update({\n                "key_ciphertext": ciphertext,\n                
"key_export_available": True,\n            }).eq("id", key_hash).execute()\n        except Exception as exc:\n         
   err = str(exc).lower()\n            if "column" in err or "pgrst204" in err or "key_ciphertext" in err:\n           
     raise ExportStorageUnavailable(\n                    "Full key export columns are not available in the 
database."\n                ) from exc\n            raise\n        self.audit_admin_action(\n            
discord_user_id,\n            "recover_key_export",\n            target_type="key",\n            
target_id=key_hash[:8],\n        )\n        return "stored"\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 HWID reset \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n    def 
reset_hwid(self, discord_user_id: str, key_id: str) -> None:\n        res = (\n            
self._client.table("license_keys")\n            .select("owner_discord_id")\n            .eq("id", key_id)\n           
 .execute()\n        )\n        if not res.data:\n            raise KeyNotFoundError(f"Key not found: {key_id}")\n     
   if res.data[0].get("owner_discord_id") != discord_user_id:\n            raise KeyOwnershipError("You do not own 
this key.")\n        # Check for active binding BEFORE counting resets.\n        # No active binding \u2192 nothing to 
clear; do not consume a reset slot.\n        b_res = (\n            self._client.table("device_bindings")\n            
.select("install_id_hash, is_active")\n            .eq("key_id", key_id)\n            .execute()\n        )\n        
binding_row = b_res.data[0] if b_res.data else None\n        if not binding_row or not binding_row.get("is_active"):\n 
           raise NoActiveBindingError(\n                "No device is currently bound to this key. "\n                
"Start the tool once to activate your device binding."\n            )\n        resets_24h = 
self.get_reset_count_24h(key_id)\n        if resets_24h >= MAX_HWID_RESETS_PER_24H:\n            raise 
ResetLimitError(\n                f"HWID reset limit reached ({MAX_HWID_RESETS_PER_24H} per 24 hours). "\n             
   "Please wait before trying again."\n            )\n        # No last_seen_at cooldown ù HWID reset is gated only on 
reset history (5/24h), not heartbeat.\n        old_hash = binding_row.get("install_id_hash")\n        
self._client.table("device_bindings").update(\n            {"is_active": False}\n        ).eq("key_id", 
key_id).execute()\n        self._client.table("hwid_reset_logs").insert(\n            {\n                "key_id": 
key_id,\n                "owner_discord_id": discord_user_id,\n                "old_install_id_hash": old_hash,\n      
          "reason": "user_requested",\n            }\n        ).execute()\n        self.audit_admin_action(\n          
  discord_user_id,\n            "reset_hwid",\n            target_type="key",\n            target_id=key_id[:8],\n     
       metadata={"old_install_id_hash": (old_hash or "")[:8]},\n        )\n\n    def get_reset_count_24h(self, key_id: 
str) -> int:\n        from datetime import timedelta\n\n        cutoff = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - 
timedelta(hours=24)).isoformat()\n        res = (\n            self._client.table("hwid_reset_logs")\n            
.select("id", count="exact")\n            .eq("key_id", key_id)\n            .gte("created_at", cutoff)\n            
.execute()\n        )\n        return res.count or 0\n\n    def get_last_seen_at(self, key_id: str) -> str | None:\n   
     res = (\n            self._client.table("device_bindings")\n            .select("last_seen_at")\n            
.eq("key_id", key_id)\n            .execute()\n        )\n        return res.data[0].get("last_seen_at") if res.data 
else None\n\n    def check_install_download_access(self, raw_key: str, install_id_hash: str) -> str:\n        try:\n   
         normalized = normalize_license_key(raw_key)\n        except LicenseKeyError:\n            return 
RESULT_NOT_FOUND\n        key_hash = hash_license_key(normalized)\n        key_res = (\n            
self._client.table("license_keys")\n            .select("status, expires_at, owner_discord_id")\n            .eq("id", 
key_hash)\n            .execute()\n        )\n        if not key_res.data:\n            self.log_license_check(\n      
          key_id=None,\n                install_id_hash=install_id_hash,\n                result=RESULT_NOT_FOUND,\n   
             device_model="bootstrap",\n                app_version="install",\n            )\n            return 
RESULT_NOT_FOUND\n        record = key_res.data[0]\n        if record.get("status") == "revoked":\n            
self.log_license_check(\n                key_id=key_hash,\n                install_id_hash=install_id_hash,\n          
      result=RESULT_REVOKED,\n                device_model="bootstrap",\n                app_version="install",\n      
      )\n            return RESULT_REVOKED\n        expires = record.get("expires_at")\n        if expires:\n          
  try:\n                exp_dt = datetime.fromisoformat(expires)\n                if datetime.now(timezone.utc) > 
exp_dt:\n                    self.log_license_check(\n                        key_id=key_hash,\n                       
 install_id_hash=install_id_hash,\n                        result=RESULT_EXPIRED,\n                        
device_model="bootstrap",\n                        app_version="install",\n                    )\n                    
return RESULT_EXPIRED\n            except (ValueError, TypeError):\n                pass\n        owner_id = 
record.get("owner_discord_id")\n        if owner_id is None or str(owner_id).strip() == "":\n            
self.log_license_check(\n                key_id=key_hash,\n                install_id_hash=install_id_hash,\n          
      result=RESULT_KEY_NOT_REDEEMED,\n                device_model="bootstrap",\n                
app_version="install",\n            )\n            return RESULT_KEY_NOT_REDEEMED\n        b_res = (\n            
self._client.table("device_bindings")\n            .select("*")\n            .eq("key_id", key_hash)\n            
.execute()\n        )\n        if b_res.data:\n            binding = b_res.data[0]\n            if 
binding.get("is_active"):\n                bound_hash = binding.get("install_id_hash")\n                if not 
install_id_hash.strip():\n                    self.log_license_check(\n                        key_id=key_hash,\n      
                  install_id_hash=install_id_hash,\n                        result=RESULT_WRONG_DEVICE,\n              
          device_model="bootstrap",\n                        app_version="install",\n                    )\n

      self._client.table("license_keys")\n            .select("owner_discord_id")\n            .eq("id", key_hash)\n   
         .execute()\n        )\n        if not key_res.data:\n            return None\n        owner_id =

    key_hash = hash_license_key(normalized)\n        key_res = (\n            self._client.table("license_keys")\n     
       .select("status, expires_at, owner_discord_id")\n            .eq("id", key_hash)\n            .execute()\n      
  )\n        if not key_res.data:\n            self.log_license_check(\n                key_id=None,

            ).eq("key_id", key_hash).execute()\n            else:\n                # Inactive binding ù reactivate 
with current device\n                self._client.table("device_bindings").update(\n                    {\n

0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n    def 
get_license_stats_for_discord_user(\n        self, discord_user_id: str\n    ) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        try:\n      
      gen_res = (\n                self._client.table("license_keys")\n                .select("id", count="exact")\n  
              .eq("created_by", discord_user_id)\n                .eq("status", "active")\n                
.execute()\n            )\n            generated = gen_res.count or 0\n        except Exception:\n            
generated = 0\n        try:\n            red_res = (\n                self._client.table("license_keys")\n             
   .select("id", count="exact")\n                .eq("owner_discord_id", discord_user_id)\n                
.not_.is_("redeemed_at", "null")\n                .execute()\n            )\n            redeemed_direct = 
red_res.count or 0\n        except Exception:\n            redeemed_direct = 0\n        try:\n            bound_res = 
(\n                self._client.table("device_bindings")\n                .select("key_id", count="exact")\n           
     .eq("license_keys.owner_discord_id", discord_user_id)\n                .eq("is_active", True)\n                
.execute()\n            )\n            bound = bound_res.count or 0\n        except Exception:\n            bound = 
0\n        # Redeemed: count keys with redeemed_at set, or treat active bound keys as\n        # at least redeemed 
(handles keys bound before migration 003 added redeemed_at).\n        redeemed = max(redeemed_direct, bound)\n        
try:\n            owned_res = (\n                self._client.table("license_keys")\n                .select("id", 
count="exact")\n                .eq("owner_discord_id", discord_user_id)\n                .neq("status", "revoked")\n  
              .execute()\n            )\n            total_owned = owned_res.count or 0\n        except Exception:\n   
         total_owned = 0\n        unbound = max(0, total_owned - bound)\n        try:\n            reset_res = (\n     
           self._client.table("hwid_reset_logs")\n                .select("id", count="exact")\n                
.eq("owner_discord_id", discord_user_id)\n                .execute()\n            )\n            reset_count = 
reset_res.count or 0\n        except Exception:\n            reset_count = 0\n        try:\n            exec_res = (\n 
               self._client.table("license_key_executions")\n                .select("id", count="exact")\n            
    .eq("owner_discord_id", discord_user_id)\n                .eq("is_public_release", True)\n                
.execute()\n            )\n            exec_count = exec_res.count or 0\n        except Exception:\n            
exec_count = 0\n        return {\n            "discord_user_id": discord_user_id,\n            "key_generated_count": 
generated,\n            "key_redeemed_count": redeemed,\n            "unbound_key_count": unbound,\n            
"bound_key_count": bound,\n            "reset_hwid_count": reset_count,\n            "key_executed_count": 
exec_count,\n        }\n\n    def record_key_execution(\n        self,\n        key_id: str,\n        
owner_discord_id: str,\n        version: str,\n        channel: str,\n        *,\n        is_public_release: bool,\n   
 ) -> None:\n        """Record one tool execution for stats tracking.\n\n        Only public-release builds should set 
is_public_release=True.\n        main-dev / internal / test builds must pass is_public_release=False.\n        Never 
raises ù failures are silently swallowed.\n        """\n        if not is_public_release:\n            return\n        
try:\n            self._client.table("license_key_executions").insert(\n                {\n                    
"key_id": key_id,\n                    "owner_discord_id": owner_discord_id,\n                    "version": (version 
or "")[:64],\n                    "channel": (channel or "")[:64],\n                    "is_public_release": True,\n   
             }\n            ).execute()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Convenience 
factory \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef 
get_default_store() -> BaseLicenseStore:\n    """Return the configured license store.\n\n    Reads DENG_LICENSE_STORE 
from the environment:\n      supabase \u2192 SupabaseLicenseStore (production)\n      local    \u2192 
LocalJsonLicenseStore (dev / tests / fallback)\n    """\n    import os\n    from dotenv import load_dotenv\n\n    
load_dotenv()\n    mode = os.environ.get("DENG_LICENSE_STORE", "local").strip().lower()\n    if mode == "supabase":\n  
      return SupabaseLicenseStore()\n    return LocalJsonLicenseStore()\n\n"""PID and lockfile safety for the auto 
supervisor."""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport signal\nimport 
subprocess\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib 
import Path\n\nfrom .constants import LOCK_PATH, PID_PATH, PRODUCT_NAME\n\n\nclass LockError(RuntimeError):\n    
"""Raised when another DENG agent is already running."""\n\n\ndef _utc_now() -> str:\n    return 
datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()\n\n\ndef is_process_alive(pid: int) -> bool:\n    if pid 
<= 0:\n        return False\n    if pid == os.getpid():\n        return True\n    proc_path = Path(f"/proc/{pid}")\n   
 if proc_path.exists():\n        return True\n    try:\n        os.kill(pid, 0)\n        return True\n    except 
ProcessLookupError:\n        return False\n    except PermissionError:\n        return True\n    except (OSError, 
ValueError) as exc:\n        if os.name == "nt" and isinstance(exc, OSError) and getattr(exc, "winerror", None) == 
87:\n            # Invalid parameter / non-existent PID ù avoid tasklist hang on fake huge PIDs in tests.\n            
return False\n        if os.name == "nt":\n            result = subprocess.run(\n                ["tasklist", "/FI", 
f"PID eq {pid}", "/FO", "CSV", "/NH"],\n                stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                
stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,\n                text=True,\n                timeout=5,\n                shell=False,\n     
       )\n            return str(pid) in result.stdout\n        return False\n\n\ndef _read_json(path: Path) -> 
dict:\n    try:\n        return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))\n    except (OSError, 
json.JSONDecodeError):\n        return {}\n\n\ndef read_pid(pid_path: Path = PID_PATH) -> int | None:\n    try:\n      
  text = pid_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()\n    except OSError:\n        return None\n    try:\n        
return int(text)\n    except ValueError:\n        return None\n\n\ndef is_deng_process(pid: int, lock_path: Path = 
LOCK_PATH) -> bool:\n    proc_cmdline = Path(f"/proc/{pid}/cmdline")\n    try:\n        cmdline = 
proc_cmdline.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").replace("\\x00", " ")\n        if "deng_tool_rejoin.py" in 
cmdline and ("--start" in cmdline or " start" in cmdline):\n            return True\n    except OSError:\n        
pass\n    metadata = _read_json(lock_path)\n    return metadata.get("product") == PRODUCT_NAME and metadata.get("pid") 
== pid\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass LockManager:\n    pid_path: Path = PID_PATH\n    lock_path: Path = LOCK_PATH\n\n    def 
acquire(self) -> None:\n        self.pid_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        
self.lock_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        pid = read_pid(self.pid_path)\n        if pid and 
is_process_alive(pid):\n            if is_deng_process(pid, self.lock_path):\n                raise LockError(f"DENG 
Tool: Rejoin is already running with PID {pid}")\n            raise LockError(f"PID file points to a live non-DENG 
process ({pid}); refusing to overwrite")\n        self.cleanup()\n        current_pid = os.getpid()\n        
self.pid_path.write_text(f"{current_pid}\\n", encoding="utf-8")\n        self.lock_path.write_text(\n            
json.dumps(\n                {\n                    "product": PRODUCT_NAME,\n                    "pid": 
current_pid,\n                    "created_at": _utc_now(),\n                    "command": "agent-start",\n           
     },\n                indent=2,\n                sort_keys=True,\n            )\n            + "\\n",\n            
encoding="utf-8",\n        )\n\n    def cleanup(self) -> None:\n        for path in (self.pid_path, self.lock_path):\n 
           try:\n                path.unlink()\n            except FileNotFoundError:\n                pass\n\n    def 
release(self) -> None:\n        pid = read_pid(self.pid_path)\n        if pid in {None, os.getpid()}:\n            
self.cleanup()\n\n    def is_running(self) -> bool:\n        pid = read_pid(self.pid_path)\n        return bool(pid 
and is_process_alive(pid) and is_deng_process(pid, self.lock_path))\n\n    def __enter__(self) -> "LockManager":\n     
   self.acquire()\n        return self\n\n    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb) -> None:\n        
self.release()\n\n\ndef stop_running_agent(pid_path: Path = PID_PATH, lock_path: Path = LOCK_PATH, *, timeout: int = 
10) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    pid = read_pid(pid_path)\n    manager = LockManager(pid_path=pid_path, 
lock_path=lock_path)\n    if not pid:\n        manager.cleanup()\n        return False, "agent is not running"\n    if 
not is_process_alive(pid):\n        manager.cleanup()\n        return False, f"stale PID {pid} cleaned"\n    if not 
is_deng_process(pid, lock_path):\n        return False, f"PID {pid} is not confirmed as DENG Tool: Rejoin; refusing to 
stop it"\n\n    os.kill(pid, signal.SIGTERM)\n    deadline = time.time() + timeout\n    while time.time() < 
deadline:\n        if not is_process_alive(pid):\n            manager.cleanup()\n            return True, f"stopped 
agent PID {pid}"\n        time.sleep(0.25)\n    return False, f"sent stop signal to PID {pid}, but it still appears 
alive"\n\n"""Readable local logging with URL masking."""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nfrom 
logging.handlers import RotatingFileHandler\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom .constants 
import LOG_BACKUP_COUNT, LOG_MAX_BYTES, LOG_PATH\nfrom .url_utils import mask_urls_in_text\n\n\ndef _safe_value(value: 
Any) -> str:\n    if value is None:\n        return "none"\n    text = str(value)\n    text = 
mask_urls_in_text(text)\n    return text.replace("\\n", " ").replace("\\r", " ")\n\n\nclass 
DengFormatter(logging.Formatter):\n    def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:\n        base = 
super().format(record)\n        return base\n\n\n_PUBLIC_QUIET_NAMESPACES: tuple[str, ...] = (\n    "deng.rejoin",\n   
 "deng_tool_rejoin",\n)\n\n\ndef _silence_namespace_to_file(name: str, file_handler: logging.Handler) -> None:\n    
"""Attach the file handler to a logger namespace and stop propagation to root.\n\n    This prevents Python\'s default 
``lastResort`` stderr handler from emitting\n    ``deng.rejoin.*`` warnings/errors to the public terminal.\n    """\n  
  lg = logging.getLogger(name)\n    lg.propagate = False\n    # Remove any pre-existing stream handlers that might 
leak to stderr/stdout.\n    for h in list(lg.handlers):\n        if isinstance(h, logging.StreamHandler) and not 
isinstance(\n            h, RotatingFileHandler\n        ):\n            lg.removeHandler(h)\n    # Only add file 
handler once.\n    if not any(isinstance(h, RotatingFileHandler) for h in lg.handlers):\n        
lg.addHandler(file_handler)\n\n\ndef configure_logging(log_path: Path = LOG_PATH, level: str = "INFO") -> 
logging.Logger:\n    """Configure and return the DENG logger.\n\n    Also silences the ``deng.rejoin.*`` logger 
namespace (used by window_layout,\n    supervisor child modules, etc.) so their warnings/errors NEVER reach the\n    
public terminal via Python\'s lastResort handler.\n    """\n    log_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   
 logger = logging.getLogger("deng_tool_rejoin")\n    logger.setLevel(getattr(logging, level.upper(), logging.INFO))\n  
  logger.propagate = False\n\n    formatter = DengFormatter("%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s", 
"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")\n\n    if not any(isinstance(h, RotatingFileHandler) for h in logger.handlers):\n        
file_handler = RotatingFileHandler(\n            str(log_path), maxBytes=LOG_MAX_BYTES, backupCount=LOG_BACKUP_COUNT\n 
       )\n        file_handler.setFormatter(formatter)\n        logger.addHandler(file_handler)\n    else:\n        
file_handler = next(\n            h for h in logger.handlers if isinstance(h, RotatingFileHandler)\n        )\n\n    # 
Silence all internal namespaces so they only write to the file.\n    for ns in _PUBLIC_QUIET_NAMESPACES:\n        
_silence_namespace_to_file(ns, file_handler)\n\n    return logger\n\n\ndef silence_public_loggers() -> None:\n    
"""Ensure internal ``deng.rejoin.*`` namespace never leaks to public stdout/stderr.\n\n    Safe to call before any 
configure_logging() ù uses a NullHandler placeholder\n    so messages are simply dropped rather than emitted to stderr 
via lastResort.\n    Called by every public entry point (cmd_start, main, etc.).\n    """\n    for ns in 
_PUBLIC_QUIET_NAMESPACES:\n        lg = logging.getLogger(ns)\n        lg.propagate = False\n        # Drop any 
pre-existing stream handlers\n        for h in list(lg.handlers):\n            if isinstance(h, logging.StreamHandler) 
and not isinstance(\n                h, RotatingFileHandler\n            ):\n                lg.removeHandler(h)\n     
   if not lg.handlers:\n            lg.addHandler(logging.NullHandler())\n\n\ndef log_event(logger: logging.Logger, 
level: str, event_type: str, message: str = "", **fields: Any) -> None:\n    """Write a structured one-line event to 
the local log."""\n    parts = [event_type]\n    if message:\n        parts.append(_safe_value(message))\n    for key 
in sorted(fields):\n        value = _safe_value(fields[key])\n        if " " in value or value == "":\n            
value = f\'"{value}"\'\n        parts.append(f"{key}={value}")\n    line = " ".join(parts)\n    log_method = 
getattr(logger, level.lower(), logger.info)\n    log_method(line)\n\n"""Public-user Termux menu for DENG Tool: 
Rejoin."""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport sys\nfrom collections.abc import 
Callable\n\nfrom . import keystore, safe_io\nfrom .banner import print_banner\nfrom .config import ConfigError, 
enabled_package_entries, load_config\n\nHandler = Callable[[argparse.Namespace], int]\n\n# License entry and New User 
Help are removed from the normal menu.\n# License is a gate before the menu (run by cmd_menu before calling 
run_menu).\n# Users who need to change their key later can run: deng-rejoin license\nMENU_ITEMS = (\n    ("1", "First 
Time Setup Config", "first-setup"),\n    ("2", "Setup / Edit Config", "config"),\n    ("3", "Start", "start"),\n    
("0", "Exit", "exit"),\n)\n\n\ndef _is_interactive() -> bool:\n    return bool(getattr(sys.stdin, "isatty", lambda: 
False)())\n\n\ndef _menu_prelude_lines() -> list[str]:\n    """Return a minimal hint if setup is incomplete; empty 
list when ready."""\n    try:\n        cfg = load_config()\n    except ConfigError:\n        return ["Setup required: 
choose First Time Setup Config to begin."]\n    if cfg is None:\n        return ["Setup required: choose First Time 
Setup Config to begin."]\n    try:\n        pkgs = enabled_package_entries(cfg)\n    except Exception:\n        pkgs = 
[]\n    if not pkgs:\n        return ["Setup required: choose First Time Setup Config to begin."]\n    return 
[]\n\n\ndef print_menu(args: argparse.Namespace, prelude_lines: list[str] | None = None) -> None:\n    
print_banner(use_color=not args.no_color)\n    if prelude_lines:\n        print()\n        for line in 
prelude_lines:\n            print(line)\n    print()\n    print("Menu:")\n    
print("--------------------------------")\n    for number, label, _command in MENU_ITEMS:\n        print(f"{number}. 
{label}")\n\n\ndef run_menu(args: argparse.Namespace, handlers: dict[str, Handler]) -> int:\n    """Show a simple 
public menu and call existing command handlers.\n\n    Safety: all input() calls are replaced with 
safe_io.safe_prompt() to\n    bypass the readline C extension and prevent Termux/Android segfaults.\n    
KeyboardInterrupt and EOF both exit cleanly.\n    """\n    prelude = _menu_prelude_lines()\n    if not 
_is_interactive():\n        print_menu(args, prelude)\n        print("\\nRun this command in an interactive Termux 
session to choose an option.")\n        return 0\n\n    while True:\n        print_menu(args, prelude)\n        
choice_raw = safe_io.safe_prompt("\\nChoose option: ")\n        if choice_raw is None:\n            print("\\nNo 
interactive input was available. Run this command in Termux to choose an option.")\n            return 0\n        
choice = choice_raw.strip()\n        command = next((item[2] for item in MENU_ITEMS if item[0] == choice), None)\n     
   if command is None:\n            print("Please choose a valid option.")\n            safe_io.press_enter()\n        
    prelude = _menu_prelude_lines()\n            continue\n        if command == "exit":\n            
print("Goodbye.")\n            return 0\n        try:\n            result = handlers[command](args)\n        except 
KeyboardInterrupt:\n            print("\\nInterrupted ù returning to menu.")\n            result = 0\n        except 
Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            print("\\nAn error occurred ù returning to menu.")\n            result = 1\n   
     prelude = _menu_prelude_lines()\n        if command == "start":\n            return result\n        ret = 
safe_io.safe_prompt("\\nPress Enter to return to menu...")\n        if ret is None:\n            return 
result\n\n"""Freeform-aware Roblox health checks.\n\nKey design principle:\n  In Android freeform / multi-window mode, 
not every visible Roblox window is\n  the *top foreground* app.  Only ONE app can be foreground at a time.\n  
Therefore, foreground-only detection is wrong for multi-client setups.\n\nHealth result states:\n  "healthy"           
  ù package is alive by at least one evidence source.\n  "roblox_not_running"  ù package is genuinely dead (no PID, no 
task, no window).\n  "roblox_not_installed"ù package is not installed on the device.\n  "network_down"        ù 
network is not reachable (blocking for URL joins).\n\nEvidence hierarchy (any single TRUE = package is alive):\n  1. 
pidof / ps process check\n  2. root pidof / ps (when standard check is unavailable)\n  3. dumpsys activity ù task 
record for the package\n  4. dumpsys window  ù window entry for the package\n\nForeground status is collected as 
metadata for state-machine decisions\n(e.g., promoting to In Server vs Lobby) but does NOT determine whether\nthe 
package is alive/dead.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom typing 
import Any\n\nfrom . import android\nfrom .config import validate_config, 
validate_package_name\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass HealthResult:\n    state: str\n    message: str\n    meta: 
dict[str, Any]\n\n\ndef check_package_health(config_data: dict[str, Any], package: str) -> HealthResult:\n    
"""Freeform-aware health check for one Android package.\n\n    A package is considered healthy (alive) if ANY of the 
following is true:\n      - pidof / ps shows a running process\n      - root pidof / ps shows a running process\n      
- dumpsys activity shows a task for the package\n      - dumpsys window shows a window entry for the package\n\n    
Foreground status is returned as metadata but does NOT determine aliveness.\n    This prevents false "Offline" states 
in multi-window freeform setups where\n    background Roblox clients are running but not the top foreground app.\n    
"""\n    try:\n        validate_config(config_data)\n        package = validate_package_name(package)\n    except 
Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return HealthResult("roblox_not_running", f"Config/package error: {exc}", 
{"package": str(package)})\n\n    try:\n        if not android.network_available():\n            return 
HealthResult("network_down", "network check failed", {"package": package})\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n    
    pass  # network check failure is non-fatal; continue with process checks\n\n    if not 
android.package_installed(package):\n        return HealthResult(\n            "roblox_not_installed",\n            
"Roblox package is not installed",\n            {"package": package},\n        )\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 Multi-source 
aliveness check \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    evidence = android.get_package_alive_evidence(package)\n    alive    = 
bool(evidence.get("alive"))\n    running  = bool(evidence.get("running") or evidence.get("root_running"))\n\n    # 
Foreground is metadata only ù not a health gate.\n    foreground   = android.current_foreground_package()\n    
is_foreground = (foreground == package)\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 Disconnect signal check (only when process is alive) 
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    
disconnect_ev = None\n    if alive:\n        try:\n            from .roblox_health import analyze_disconnect_signals\n 
           disconnect_ev = analyze_disconnect_signals(package)\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            
pass\n\n    if disconnect_ev and disconnect_ev.category in (\n        "disconnected",\n        "server_shutdown",\n    
    "private_server_refresh",\n    ):\n        # Strong disconnect signal overrides aliveness ù treat as not running\n 
       # (the supervisor will attempt a reconnect / reopen).\n        return HealthResult(\n            
"roblox_not_running",\n            f"Disconnect signal: {disconnect_ev.category} (source={disconnect_ev.source})",\n   
         {\n                "package":             package,\n                "foreground":          foreground,\n      
          "running":             running,\n                "task":                evidence.get("task"),\n              
  "window":              evidence.get("window"),\n                "disconnect_category": disconnect_ev.category,\n     
           "disconnect_source":   disconnect_ev.source,\n            },\n        )\n\n    if alive:\n        # Package 
is running / has a task / has a window / has a composited\n        # surface / is the current foreground app ù it is 
healthy.\n        # Background (non-foreground) clients in freeform mode are still healthy.\n        detail = (\n      
      f"fg={is_foreground} proc={running} "\n            f"task={evidence.get(\'task\')} 
win={evidence.get(\'window\')} "\n            f"surf={evidence.get(\'surface\')} 
fgev={evidence.get(\'foreground\')}"\n        )\n        return HealthResult(\n            "healthy",\n            
f"Package alive ({detail})",\n            {\n                "package":      package,\n                "foreground":   
foreground,\n                "running":      running,\n                "task":         evidence.get("task"),\n         
       "window":       evidence.get("window"),\n                "surface":      evidence.get("surface"),\n             
   "fg_evidence":  evidence.get("foreground"),\n                "is_foreground": is_foreground,\n            },\n      
  )\n\n    # No evidence of the package being alive at all.  Preserve the FULL\n    # evidence dict so the 
supervisor\'s heartbeat tracker can still decide\n    # whether this is a transient blind spot vs. a real Offline.\n   
 return HealthResult(\n        "roblox_not_running",\n        "Package not running ù no process, task, window, or 
surface found",\n        {\n            "package":     package,\n            "foreground":  foreground,\n            
"running":     bool(evidence.get("running")),\n            "root_running": bool(evidence.get("root_running")),\n       
     "task":        bool(evidence.get("task")),\n            "window":      bool(evidence.get("window")),\n            
"surface":     bool(evidence.get("surface")),\n            "fg_evidence": bool(evidence.get("foreground")),\n        
},\n    )\n\n\ndef check_roblox_health(config_data: dict[str, Any]) -> HealthResult:\n    cfg = 
validate_config(config_data)\n    return check_package_health(config_data, cfg["roblox_package"])\n\n"""First-run 
onboarding text and setup status for the main Termux menu."""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom typing 
import Any\n\nfrom .config import (\n    DEFAULT_ROBLOX_PACKAGE_HINTS,\n    effective_private_server_url,\n

import PRODUCT_NAME, VERSION\n\nNEW_USER_HELP_TEXT = """\nNew User Help\n=============\n\nWhat this tool 
does\n-------------------\nDENG Tool: Rejoin helps open, reopen, and reconnect the Roblox Android app\n(or a 
compatible clone) on your phone. It runs in Termux on your device.\n\nWhat you need before 
Start\n--------------------------\n- A valid license key (from the DENG Tool: Rejoin Panel in Discord).\n- At least 
one Roblox package chosen in setup (often auto-detected).\n- Optional: a private server or game link if you want 
direct join.\n\nWhat to do first\n----------------\n1) Enter your license key (main menu, option 1).\n2) Run First 
Time Setup Config (option 2).\n3) Pick your Roblox package from the detection table, or enter it manually.\n4) Add a 
private server URL only if you need it (optional).\n5) Choose Start (option 4).\n\nMain menu 
options\n-----------------\n1 ù Enter / Update License Key\n2 ù First Time Setup Config\n3 ù Setup / Edit Config\n4 ù 
Start\n5 ù New User Help\n0 ù Exit\n\nHow to enter a license key\n--------------------------\nFrom the main menu 
choose "Enter / Update License Key", paste your key,\nand wait for "License OK". If you change phones, ask support 
about Reset HWID.\n\nPackage auto-detection\n----------------------\nThe tool scans installed Android packages using 
safe name hints (like roblox,\nrblx, blox) plus hints you add for your clone. Pick a row from the table\n(#, Package, 
App Name, Launchable). If nothing appears, install Roblox,\nopen it once, then try againùor use manual package 
entry.\n\nUsername (account name)\n-----------------------\nThis is only a label in the Start table so you can tell 
accounts apart.\nYou can leave it blank: the table will show "Unknown" and Start still works.\n\nPrivate Server 
URL\n--------------------\nOptional. If you set a full private server https link or a Roblox web URL,\nthe tool can 
use it when launching or reconnecting. If you skip it, the app\nopens normally. Full URLs are never printed on screen 
after saving.\n\nHow to Start\n------------\nChoose Start from the main menu. Keep Termux open while monitoring 
runs.\n\nStart table (normal view)\n-------------------------\nOnly these columns appear:\n\n  # | Package | Username 
| State\n\nIf State becomes Online, Roblox is running and being watched.\n\nCommon State 
values\n-------------------\n- Preparing / Optimizing ù getting ready.\n- Launching ù opening the app.\n- Online ù 
looks healthy.\n- Background ù app may be in the background while the tool decides next step.\n- Reconnecting ù trying 
to reopen or reconnect.\n- Warning ù check messages or run status.\n- Failed / Offline ù something went wrong; try 
opening Roblox manually once.\n- Unknown ù not enough information yet.\n\nExtra detail (cache, graphics, launch trace) 
appears only with --verbose,\n--debug, or DEBUG log levelùnot in the normal table.\n\nWhat to send 
support\n--------------------\n- Screenshot of the Start table.\n- Output of: version and status (see docs).\n- 
Selected package name (e.g. com.roblox.client).\n- Whether Roblox opens when you tap the app icon.\nDo not post your 
full license key or private server link in public chat.\n\nFull install guide:

"License: Not required (development mode)"\n    lic = cfg.get("license") if isinstance(cfg.get("license"), dict) else 
{}\n    if lic.get("disabled_by_user") or not lic.get("enabled", True):\n        return "License: Not required (your 
config)"\n\n    key = (str(lic.get("key") or "").strip() or str(cfg.get("license_key") or "").strip())\n    if not 
key:\n        return "License: Missing"\n\n    st = str(lic.get("last_status") or "").strip().lower()\n    if st == 
"active":\n        return "License: Verified"\n    if st == "wrong_device":\n        return "License: Wrong device

will check, or use option 1 to update your key"\n    return "License: Not verified ù use option 1 if you need to 
change your key"\n\n\ndef _config_ui_line(cfg: dict[str, Any] | None) -> str:\n    if cfg is None:\n        return 
"Config: Could not load config (check install paths)"\n    if not cfg.get("first_setup_completed"):\n        return 
"Config: Not created"\n    return "Config: Saved on this device"\n\n\ndef _packages_ui_line(cfg: dict[str, Any]) -> 
str:\n    try:\n        entries = enabled_package_entries(cfg)\n    except Exception:\n        return "Packages: None 
selected (run First Time Setup or Setup / Edit Config)"\n    if not entries:\n        return "Packages: None 
selected"\n    n = len(entries)\n    return f"Packages: {n} selected ({\', \'.join(e[\'package\'] for e in 
entries[:3])}{\'à\' if n > 3 else \'\'})"\n\n\ndef _detection_options_from_cfg(cfg: dict[str, Any]) -> 
tuple[list[str], bool, bool]:\n    pd = cfg.get("package_detection")\n    if not isinstance(pd, dict):\n        pd = 
{}\n    hints_src = pd.get("hints")\n    if hints_src in (None, "", []):\n        hints_src =

bool(pd.get("include_launchable_only", True)), bool(pd.get("enabled", True))\n\n\ndef _package_scan_line(cfg: 
dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    try:\n        from . import android\n\n        hints, inc_launch, det_en = 
_detection_options_from_cfg(cfg)\n        n = len(\n            android.discover_roblox_package_candidates(\n          
      hints,\n                include_launchable_only=inc_launch,\n                detection_enabled=det_en,\n         
   )\n        )\n        if n == 0:\n            return "Package scan: No Roblox-like packages detected ù install/open 
Roblox, then rerun setup"\n        return f"Package scan: {n} candidate(s) detected on this device"\n    except 
Exception:\n        return "Package scan: Unavailable (run setup to pick a package manually)"\n\n\ndef 
_private_url_line(cfg: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    try:\n        entries = 
validate_package_entries(cfg.get("roblox_packages") or [])\n    except Exception:\n        entries = []\n    any_url = 
False\n    for e in entries:\n        if effective_private_server_url(e, cfg):\n            any_url = True\n           
 break\n    if not any_url and not (str(cfg.get("private_server_url") or "").strip()):\n        if 
str(cfg.get("launch_url") or "").strip() and str(cfg.get("launch_mode") or "") in ("web_url", "deeplink"):\n           
 any_url = True\n    if any_url:\n        return "Private URL: Set (optional ù full link is never shown here)"\n    
return "Private URL: Optional, not set"\n\n\ndef _ready_line(cfg: dict[str, Any], *, dev_mode: bool) -> tuple[bool, 
list[str]]:\n    """Return (is_ready, extra lines to print after Next Step)."""\n    lic = cfg.get("license") if 
isinstance(cfg.get("license"), dict) else {}\n    license_required = bool(not dev_mode and lic.get("enabled", True) 
and not lic.get("disabled_by_user"))\n\n    key_ok = True\n    if license_required:\n        key = (str(lic.get("key") 
or "").strip() or str(cfg.get("license_key") or "").strip())\n        st = str(lic.get("last_status") or 
"").strip().lower()\n        key_ok = bool(key) and st == "active"\n\n    try:\n        entries = 
enabled_package_entries(cfg)\n    except Exception:\n        entries = []\n\n    setup_ok = 
bool(cfg.get("first_setup_completed"))\n    pkg_ok = len(entries) > 0\n\n    ready = key_ok and setup_ok and pkg_ok\n  
  extra: list[str] = []\n    if ready:\n        extra.extend(\n            [\n                "",\n                
"Ready To Start",\n                "----------",\n                "Your setup is ready.",\n                "",\n       
         "Choose Start (option 4) to launch and monitor Roblox.",\n                "",\n                "The public 
Start table shows only:",\n                "  # | Package | Username | State",\n                "",\n                
"Cache cleanup, graphics tuning, and reconnect details appear only in",\n                "verbose or debug modeùnot in 
the normal table.",\n            ]\n        )\n    return ready, extra\n\n\ndef _next_step_line(cfg: dict[str, Any], 
*, dev_mode: bool) -> str:\n    if cfg is None:\n        return "Fix your config path or reinstall, then try 
again."\n\n    lic = cfg.get("license") if isinstance(cfg.get("license"), dict) else {}\n    license_required = 
bool(not dev_mode and lic.get("enabled", True) and not lic.get("disabled_by_user"))\n\n    if license_required:\n      
  key = (str(lic.get("key") or "").strip() or str(cfg.get("license_key") or "").strip())\n        st = 
str(lic.get("last_status") or "").strip().lower()\n        if not key:\n            return "Run deng-rejoin and enter 
your license key, then choose First Time Setup Config."\n        if st != "active":\n            return "Run: 
deng-rejoin license  to verify or update your license key, then try again."\n\n    if not 
cfg.get("first_setup_completed"):\n        return "Choose option 1 ù First Time Setup Config ù to pick your Roblox 
package and options."\n\n    try:\n        entries = enabled_package_entries(cfg)\n    except Exception:\n        
entries = []\n    if not entries:\n        return "Choose option 2 ù Setup / Edit Config ù then Package, and enable at 
least one package."\n\n    return "Choose option 3 ù Start ù when you are ready to launch."\n\n\ndef 
build_onboarding_lines(\n    cfg: dict[str, Any] | None,\n    *,\n    dev_mode: bool = False,\n    version: str = 
VERSION,\n    product: str = PRODUCT_NAME,\n) -> list[str]:\n    """Human-readable lines shown above the main menu (no 
URLs, no shame)."""\n    lines = [\n        f"{product} v{version}",\n        "",\n        "Setup Status",\n        
"------------",\n    ]\n    if cfg is None:\n        lines.extend(\n            [\n                "Config: Not 
loaded",\n                "",\n                "Next Step:",\n                "Install or repair DENG, then run 
deng-rejoin again.",\n            ]\n        )\n        return lines\n\n    lines.append(_license_ui_line(cfg, 
dev_mode=dev_mode))\n    lines.append(_config_ui_line(cfg))\n    lines.append(_packages_ui_line(cfg))\n    
lines.append(_private_url_line(cfg))\n    lines.append("")\n    lines.append(_package_scan_line(cfg))\n    
lines.append("")\n    lines.append("Next Step:")\n    lines.append(_next_step_line(cfg, dev_mode=dev_mode))\n\n    
ready, ready_extra = _ready_line(cfg, dev_mode=dev_mode)\n    if ready:\n        lines.extend(ready_extra)\n\n    
return lines\n\n"""Android/Termux platform and public storage path detection."""\n\nfrom __future__ import 
annotations\n\nimport os\nimport subprocess\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom pathlib import 
Path\n\nPROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5\n\nPUBLIC_DOWNLOAD_CANDIDATES = (\n    Path("/sdcard/Download"),\n    
Path("/sdcard/download"),\n    Path("/storage/emulated/0/Download"),\n    
Path("/storage/emulated/0/download"),\n)\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass PlatformInfo:\n    android_release: str\n 
   android_sdk: str\n    termux_prefix: str\n    home: str\n    download_dir: str\n\n\ndef _run_getprop(prop: str) -> 
str:\n    try:\n        completed = subprocess.run(\n            ["getprop", prop],\n            
stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,\n            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,\n            
text=True,\n            timeout=PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n            shell=False,\n        )\n    except 
(FileNotFoundError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired, OSError):\n        return ""\n    if completed.returncode != 0:\n       
 return ""\n    return completed.stdout.strip()\n\n\ndef get_android_release() -> str:\n    return 
_run_getprop("ro.build.version.release") or "unknown"\n\n\ndef get_android_sdk() -> str:\n    return 
_run_getprop("ro.build.version.sdk") or "unknown"\n\n\ndef get_termux_prefix() -> str:\n    return 
os.environ.get("PREFIX", "")\n\n\ndef detect_public_download_dir() -> str:\n    """Return the best public Download 
folder, or empty string if inaccessible."""\n    for candidate in PUBLIC_DOWNLOAD_CANDIDATES:\n        if 
candidate.exists() and os.access(candidate, os.W_OK):\n            return str(candidate)\n    for candidate in 
PUBLIC_DOWNLOAD_CANDIDATES:\n        if candidate.exists() and os.access(candidate, os.R_OK):\n            return 
str(candidate)\n    return ""\n\n\ndef fallback_launcher_path() -> Path:\n    return Path.home() / ".deng-tool" / 
"rejoin" / "launcher" / "deng-rejoin.py"\n\n\ndef get_platform_info() -> PlatformInfo:\n    return PlatformInfo(\n     
   android_release=get_android_release(),\n        android_sdk=get_android_sdk(),\n        
termux_prefix=get_termux_prefix(),\n        home=str(Path.home()),\n        
download_dir=detect_public_download_dir(),\n    )\n\n"""Heartbeat-based playing-state detection for Roblox / App 
Cloner clones.\n\nWhy this 
exists\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nThe previous state 
machine flipped to "Offline" the instant ANY one evidence\nsource went silent ù even though the user could *see* the 
clone playing.\nThis module fixes that by:\n\n* Combining MULTIPLE evidence sources (process, window, surface, 
foreground,\n  task, UI) per package.\n* Tracking the LAST time each source was positive.\n* Only declaring a package 
Offline when *no* source has been positive for a\n  grace period (``offline_grace_s``, default 60s).\n* Mapping the 
evidence + recent history to one of:\n  ``Playing``, ``Online``, ``Lobby``, ``Background``, ``Join Unconfirmed``,\n  
``Recovering``, ``Failed``, ``Offline``, ``Unknown``.\n\nPublic 
API\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n``StateTracker``                       ù heartbeat 
tracker per package.\n``StateTracker.observe(package, evidence)`` ù feed fresh evidence 
in.\n``StateTracker.decide(package, prev, evidence, *, url_launched, attempt_count)``\n                                
       ù returns the public-facing state label.\n``classify_ui_signal(uiautomator_dump)`` ù classifies a UI dump as 
"lobby" /\n                                       "in_game" / "unknown" (best-effort\n                                 
      string scan, never raises).\n\nThe tracker is intentionally a plain in-memory store; supervisor instances\nhold 
one tracker for the lifetime of a Start session.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport time\nfrom 
dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom typing import Any, Mapping\n\n# Public state labels.  These mirror the 
symbols already defined in\n# agent.supervisor / agent.types so the supervisor can pass them straight\n# into the 
table renderer without translation.\nSTATE_PLAYING = "Playing"\nSTATE_ONLINE = "Online"\nSTATE_IN_SERVER = "In 
Server"\nSTATE_LOBBY = "Lobby"\nSTATE_BACKGROUND = "Background"\nSTATE_JOIN_UNCONFIRMED = "Join 
Unconfirmed"\nSTATE_RECOVERING = "Recovering"\nSTATE_FAILED = "Failed"\nSTATE_OFFLINE = "Offline"\nSTATE_UNKNOWN = 
"Unknown"\n\n# UI classification thresholds.\n_LOBBY_TOKENS: tuple[str, ...] = (\n    "Recommended For You", "Add 
Friends", "Friends", "Discover",\n    "Home", "Profile", "More",\n)\n_IN_GAME_TOKENS: tuple[str, ...] = (\n    "Leave 
Game", "Reset Character", "Roblox menu", "Settings", "Players",\n    "Backpack", "Chat",\n)\n\n\ndef 
classify_ui_signal(text: str | None) -> str:\n    """Best-effort classifier for a uiautomator dump.\n\n    Returns 
``"in_game"``, ``"lobby"``, or ``"unknown"``.  Never raises.\n    The matcher is intentionally simple: it counts how 
many tokens of each\n    class appear and picks the higher; ties resolve to ``"unknown"``.\n    """\n    if not 
text:\n        return "unknown"\n    try:\n        s = str(text)\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
return "unknown"\n    lobby_hits = sum(1 for t in _LOBBY_TOKENS if t in s)\n    game_hits = sum(1 for t in 
_IN_GAME_TOKENS if t in s)\n    if game_hits > lobby_hits and game_hits >= 1:\n        return "in_game"\n    if 
lobby_hits > game_hits and lobby_hits >= 1:\n        return "lobby"\n    return "unknown"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass 
_Heartbeat:\n    """Last-positive timestamps for each evidence source."""\n\n    last_process: float = 0.0\n    
last_window: float = 0.0\n    last_surface: float = 0.0\n    last_foreground: float = 0.0\n    last_task: float = 
0.0\n    last_in_game_ui: float = 0.0\n    last_lobby_ui: float = 0.0\n    last_url_launch: float = 0.0\n    last_any: 
float = 0.0\n\n    def freshest(self) -> float:\n        return max(\n            self.last_process, self.last_window, 
self.last_surface,\n            self.last_foreground, self.last_task, self.last_in_game_ui,\n            
self.last_lobby_ui, self.last_url_launch,\n        )\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass StateDecision:\n    """Result of one 
decide() call ù state + reason for the diagnostic log."""\n\n    state: str\n    reason: str\n    evidence_used: 
Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n\nclass StateTracker:\n    """Per-package heartbeat tracker.  
Thread-safe for a single supervisor.\n\n    ``offline_grace_s`` is how long we wait after the LAST positive signal\n   
 before flipping a package to Offline.  60s default keeps a 5û10s\n    transient process restart from being 
misreported as Offline.\n\n    ``stale_task_grace_s`` is how long we tolerate a "task only" state\n    (task record 
exists but no process / no surface) before treating it as\n    Offline.  20s default ù generous but not forever.\n    
"""\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        offline_grace_s: float = 60.0,\n        
stale_task_grace_s: float = 20.0,\n        join_unconfirmed_grace_s: float = 25.0,\n    ) -> None:\n        
self._beats: dict[str, _Heartbeat] = {}\n        self.offline_grace_s = float(offline_grace_s)\n        
self.stale_task_grace_s = float(stale_task_grace_s)\n        self.join_unconfirmed_grace_s = 
float(join_unconfirmed_grace_s)\n\n    def _hb(self, package: str) -> _Heartbeat:\n        hb = 
self._beats.get(package)\n        if hb is None:\n            hb = _Heartbeat()\n            self._beats[package] = 
hb\n        return hb\n\n    def reset(self, package: str) -> None:\n        self._beats.pop(package, None)\n\n    def 
note_url_launch(self, package: str, now: float | None = None) -> None:\n        """Call right after ``am start ... 
<url>`` to start the join window."""\n        ts = float(now) if now is not None else time.monotonic()\n        
self._hb(package).last_url_launch = ts\n\n    def observe(\n        self,\n        package: str,\n        evidence: 
Mapping[str, Any],\n        *,\n        ui_signal: str | None = None,\n        now: float | None = None,\n    ) -> 
_Heartbeat:\n        """Update heartbeats from an :func:`android.get_package_alive_evidence` dict."""\n        ts = 
float(now) if now is not None else time.monotonic()\n        hb = self._hb(package)\n        if 
bool(evidence.get("running")) or bool(evidence.get("root_running")):\n            hb.last_process = ts\n        if 
bool(evidence.get("window")):\n            hb.last_window = ts\n        if bool(evidence.get("surface")):\n            
hb.last_surface = ts\n        if bool(evidence.get("foreground")):\n            hb.last_foreground = ts\n        if 
bool(evidence.get("task")):\n            hb.last_task = ts\n        if ui_signal == "in_game":\n            
hb.last_in_game_ui = ts\n        elif ui_signal == "lobby":\n            hb.last_lobby_ui = ts\n        hb.last_any = 
hb.freshest()\n        return hb\n\n    def decide(\n        self,\n        package: str,\n        prev_state: str | 
None,\n        evidence: Mapping[str, Any],\n        *,\n        url_launched: bool = False,\n        ui_signal: str | 
None = None,\n        attempt_count: int = 0,\n        max_attempts: int = 5,\n        now: float | None = None,\n    
) -> StateDecision:\n        """Map current evidence + heartbeat history to a public state label.\n\n        Decision 
tree (in order):\n\n        1. UI says in-game            \u2192 ``Playing``\n        2. Process AND (window OR 
surface OR foreground) \u2192 ``Online``\n        3. Window OR surface OR foreground (but no process detection)\n      
                                  \u2192 ``Online`` (visual evidence wins;\n                                          
process detection on cloud phones\n                                          is unreliable for long pkg names)\n       
 4. UI says lobby AND process   \u2192 ``Lobby``\n        5. Process but no visual yet + recent URL launch \u2192 
``Join Unconfirmed``\n        6. Process only (background)   \u2192 ``Background``\n        7. Task only, within grace 
     \u2192 ``Background`` (waiting)\n        8. No evidence at all:\n             - within offline_grace_s of last 
positive \u2192 keep ``prev_state``\n               or ``Recovering`` if prev was healthy\n             - after grace  
                            \u2192 ``Offline``\n        9. After many failed attempts \u2192 ``Failed`` (but 
supervisor keeps\n                                          checking; this is a UI label only).\n        """\n        
ts = float(now) if now is not None else time.monotonic()\n        hb = self.observe(package, evidence, 
ui_signal=ui_signal, now=ts)\n        running = bool(evidence.get("running") or evidence.get("root_running"))\n        
window = bool(evidence.get("window"))\n        surface = bool(evidence.get("surface"))\n        foreground = 
bool(evidence.get("foreground"))\n        task = bool(evidence.get("task"))\n        visual = window or surface or 
foreground\n\n        ev_used = {\n            "running": running, "window": window, "surface": surface,\n            
"foreground": foreground, "task": task,\n            "ui_signal": ui_signal or "",\n            "url_launched": 
bool(url_launched),\n            "attempt_count": int(attempt_count),\n        }\n\n        # 1. Strong in-game UI 
signal ù beats everything else.\n        if ui_signal == "in_game":\n            return StateDecision(\n               
 STATE_PLAYING, "ui_in_game_tokens_detected", ev_used,\n            )\n\n        # 2. Real running process + any 
visual evidence = Online.\n        if running and visual:\n            # If UI explicitly says lobby, prefer that.\n   
         if ui_signal == "lobby":\n                return StateDecision(\n                    STATE_LOBBY, 
"process+visual+lobby_ui", ev_used,\n                )\n            return StateDecision(\n                
STATE_ONLINE, "process+visual_evidence", ev_used,\n            )\n\n        # 3. Visual evidence alone (process 
detection unreliable on cloud\n        # phones with long clone names).  Trust the visible window/surface.\n        if 
visual:\n            if ui_signal == "lobby":\n                return StateDecision(\n                    STATE_LOBBY, 
"visual_with_lobby_ui", ev_used,\n                )\n            return StateDecision(\n                STATE_ONLINE, 
"visual_evidence_only", ev_used,\n            )\n\n        # 4. Recent URL launch with weak evidence = Join 
Unconfirmed.\n        if url_launched or (\n            hb.last_url_launch\n            and (ts - hb.last_url_launch) 
< self.join_unconfirmed_grace_s\n        ):\n            if running or task:\n                return StateDecision(\n  
                  STATE_JOIN_UNCONFIRMED,\n                    "url_launched_recently",\n
