ev_used,\n                )\n\n        # 5. Process exists but no visible window/surface (yet) = Background.\n        
if running:\n            return StateDecision(\n                STATE_BACKGROUND, "process_only_no_visual", ev_used,\n 
           )\n\n        # 6. Task-only ù within grace window, treat as Background; after,\n        # let it fall 
through to Offline logic.\n        if task and (ts - hb.last_task) < self.stale_task_grace_s:\n            return 
StateDecision(\n                STATE_BACKGROUND, "task_only_within_grace", ev_used,\n            )\n\n        # 7. No 
evidence at all.  Did we have evidence recently?\n        freshest = hb.freshest()\n        if freshest > 0 and (ts - 
freshest) < self.offline_grace_s:\n            # Just lost evidence ù assume Recovering, keep the supervisor\n         
   # from showing a hard Offline.\n            return StateDecision(\n                STATE_RECOVERING,\n              
  f"no_evidence_within_grace_age={int(ts - freshest)}s",\n                ev_used,\n            )\n\n        # 8. Cold 
offline.  Show Failed only after attempt budget exhausted.\n        if max_attempts > 0 and attempt_count >= 
max_attempts:\n            return StateDecision(\n                STATE_FAILED,\n                
f"no_evidence_attempts={attempt_count}/{max_attempts}",\n                ev_used,\n            )\n        return 
StateDecision(\n            STATE_OFFLINE,\n            "no_evidence_beyond_grace" if freshest > 0 else 
"never_observed",\n            ev_used,\n        )\n\n"""Comprehensive device evidence collector for live 
debugging.\n\nThe agent has been re-implemented many times based on what we *think* the\nphone returns from 
``dumpsys`` / ``settings`` / ``cmd activity``.  When the\nreal phone disagrees with our parser the whole tool silently 
degrades.\n\n``deng-rejoin probe`` (hidden command) captures everything we would need to\nwrite a real fix:\n\n* 
runtime build proof (version, install root, modules)\n* device info (Android release, SDK, model, kernel, screen, 
density)\n* freeform / resizable / DPI / window settings (global + secure + system)\n* available ``cmd activity`` / 
``am`` / ``cmd window`` verbs\n* per-package: process detection (pidof + pgrep + /proc cmdline),\n  dumpsys window / 
activities / recents / SurfaceFlinger, shared_prefs XML,\n  Roblox presence-API result\n* recent agent logs and the 
latest Start-time self-diagnostics\n\nEverything is captured into a single JSON file.  Secrets are masked\n(cookies, 
tokens, license keys, Discord webhooks, HMAC signing keys, GitHub\nPATs).  Private server URLs are kept because we 
need them to reason about\njoin state ù the user explicitly opted in.\n\nThe module never raises: every 
``run_command`` call is guarded and any\nfailure is recorded under the ``errors`` list with a short 
reason.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport re\nimport shutil\nimport 
socket\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nimport time\nimport traceback\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime, 
timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom . import android\nfrom .android import 
CommandResult, run_command, run_root_command\nfrom .constants import CONFIG_PATH, LOG_PATH\n\nPROBE_VERSION = 
1\nDATA_DIR = Path(os.path.expanduser("~/.deng-tool/rejoin/data"))\nPROBE_DIR = DATA_DIR / "probes"\n\n# 
\u2500\u2500\u2500 Sanitization \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n# Heuristic secret patterns ù applied to every captured string 
field.  We\n# err on the side of dropping suspicious content; the user can re-run with a\n# tighter scope if 
needed.\n# Order matters ù specific identifiers run BEFORE broad key-value matchers\n# so each secret is labelled with 
the most informative kind.  The broad\n# ``env_secret_assign`` pattern uses a negative lookahead so it skips\n# fields 
that already contain a ``<masked:...>`` placeholder.\n_SECRET_PATTERNS: tuple[tuple[str, re.Pattern], ...] = (\n    
("hmac_signing", re.compile(r"(?i)LICENSE_KEY_EXPORT_SECRET\\s*=\\s*\\S+")),\n    ("github_pat", 
re.compile(r"gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9]{30,}")),\n    ("roblosecurity", re.compile(r"(?i)\\.?ROBLOSECURITY[^;\\s]*")),\n    
("discord_webhook", re.compile(\n        
r"https?://(?:discord(?:app)?\\.com|canary\\.discord\\.com)/api/webhooks/\\S+"\n    )),\n    ("bearer", 
re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\\-]+")),\n    ("license_key", 
re.compile(r"(?i)\\blic_[A-Za-z0-9_-]{8,}\\b")),\n    ("rk_key", re.compile(r"\\brk_[A-Za-z0-9_-]{8,}\\b")),\n    
("env_secret_assign", re.compile(\n        
r"(?i)\\b(?:password|secret|api[_-]?key|webhook[_-]?url)\\s*[=:]\\s*(?!<masked:)\\S+"\n    )),\n)\n\n\ndef mask(text: 
str | None) -> str:\n    """Return ``text`` with every detected secret replaced by ``<masked:KIND>``.\n\n    Never 
raises on weird input ù converts to str first.  Empty input is\n    returned unchanged.\n    """\n    if text is 
None:\n        return ""\n    s = str(text)\n    if not s:\n        return s\n    for kind, rx in _SECRET_PATTERNS:\n  
      s = rx.sub(f"<masked:{kind}>", s)\n    return s\n\n\ndef _short_id() -> str:\n    return 
uuid.uuid4().hex[:10]\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Helper for guarded calls \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\n\n\ndef _safe(label: str, fn, errors: list[dict[str, str]], default: Any = None) -> Any:\n    """Run 
``fn`` and append a structured error on any exception.\n\n    Returns ``default`` on failure so the probe JSON stays 
well-shaped.\n    """\n    try:\n        return fn()\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 ù probe never 
raises out.\n        errors.append({"step": label, "error": str(exc)[:200]})\n        return default\n\n\ndef 
_run(label: str, args: list[str], errors: list[dict[str, str]], *, root: bool = False, timeout: int = 8) -> 
CommandResult:\n    """Run a command with timeout; record failure under ``errors`` if it dies."""\n    try:\n        
if root:\n            res = run_root_command(args, timeout=timeout)\n        else:\n            res = 
run_command(args, timeout=timeout)\n        if not res.ok and not res.stdout:\n            # Useful failures still 
record stderr/timeout; we don\'t fail loud.\n            errors.append({"step": f"{label}: rc={res.returncode}", 
"error": res.stderr[:200]})\n        return res\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
errors.append({"step": label, "error": str(exc)[:200]})\n        return CommandResult(tuple(args), 1, "", 
str(exc)[:200], False)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Capture sections \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef _capture_build_info(errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> 
dict[str, Any]:\n    from . import build_info as _bi\n    return _safe("build_info.collect", _bi.collect_version_info, 
errors, default={}) or {}\n\n\ndef _capture_device(errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    out: 
dict[str, Any] = {}\n    def _prop(name: str) -> str:\n        return _run(f"getprop {name}", ["getprop", name], 
errors, timeout=4).stdout\n    for key, prop in (\n        ("android_release", "ro.build.version.release"),\n        
("android_sdk", "ro.build.version.sdk"),\n        ("model", "ro.product.model"),\n        ("brand", 
"ro.product.brand"),\n        ("manufacturer", "ro.product.manufacturer"),\n        ("device", "ro.product.device"),\n 
       ("fingerprint", "ro.build.fingerprint"),\n        ("oem_freeform", 
"persist.wm.enable_remote_keyguard_animation"),\n    ):\n        out[key] = _prop(prop)\n    out["hostname"] = 
_safe("hostname", socket.gethostname, errors, default="")\n    out["is_termux"] = bool(_safe("is_termux", 
android.is_termux, errors, default=False))\n    out["root"] = _safe(\n        "detect_root",\n        lambda: 
{"available": android.detect_root().available, "tool": android.detect_root().tool},\n        errors,\n        
default={"available": False, "tool": None},\n    )\n    return out\n\n\ndef _capture_screen(errors: list[dict[str, 
str]]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Capture screen size / density / display dump.\n\n    Some Samsung builds reject 
``dumpsys display`` from unprivileged\n    callers; we route through :func:`android.run_android_command` so the\n    
helper transparently retries via ``su`` on permission denial.\n    """\n    def _grab(args: list[str], cap: int = 
4000) -> str:\n        try:\n            res = android.run_android_command(args, timeout=8)\n        except Exception 
as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            errors.append({"step": " ".join(args), "error": str(exc)[:200]})\n            
return ""\n        if not res.ok and not res.stdout:\n            errors.append({"step": " ".join(args) + f": 
rc={res.returncode}", "error": res.stderr[:200]})\n        return (res.stdout or "")[:cap]\n\n    return {\n        
"wm_size": _grab(["wm", "size"], 200),\n        "wm_density": _grab(["wm", "density"], 200),\n        
"dumpsys_display": _grab(["dumpsys", "display"], 8000),\n    }\n\n\ndef _filter_settings(text: str) -> dict[str, 
str]:\n    """Pick out keys we care about from ``settings list <ns>`` output."""\n    keys_we_want = (\n        
"freeform",\n        "force_resizable",\n        "enable_freeform",\n        "development_settings",\n        
"auto_dpi",\n        "auto-dpi",\n        "auto_landscape",\n        "force_landscape",\n        
"display_size_forced",\n        "rotation",\n        "window_size",\n        "window_position",\n        
"set_window",\n        "kaeru",\n        "app_cloner",\n        "multi_window",\n        "orientation",\n    )\n    
out: dict[str, str] = {}\n    for line in text.splitlines():\n        if "=" not in line:\n            continue\n      
  k, _, v = line.partition("=")\n        kl = k.strip().lower()\n        if any(w in kl for w in keys_we_want):\n      
      out[k.strip()] = v.strip()[:200]\n    return out\n\n\ndef _capture_settings(errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> 
dict[str, Any]:\n    """Capture ``settings list global/secure/system``.\n\n    On many Android builds (confirmed: 
Samsung One UI Android 13 on the\n    cloud phone) the unprivileged caller gets\n    ``Security exception: Permission 
Denial: getCurrentUser() ...\n    INTERACT_ACROSS_USERS``.  We retry through ``su`` automatically via\n    
:func:`android.run_android_command` so the probe actually returns\n    settings on real hardware.\n    """\n    out: 
dict[str, Any] = {}\n    for ns in ("global", "secure", "system"):\n        try:\n            res = 
android.run_android_command(["settings", "list", ns], timeout=10)\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n  
          errors.append({"step": f"settings list {ns}", "error": str(exc)[:200]})\n            out[f"{ns}_filtered"] = 
{}\n            out[f"{ns}_raw_len"] = 0\n            continue\n        if not res.ok and not res.stdout:\n            
errors.append({"step": f"settings list {ns}: rc={res.returncode}", "error": res.stderr[:200]})\n        
out[f"{ns}_filtered"] = _filter_settings(res.stdout)\n        out[f"{ns}_raw_len"] = len(res.stdout)\n    return 
out\n\n\ndef _capture_command_help(errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, str]:\n    """Capture which verbs the 
host actually supports for resize / launch bounds.\n\n    We bump the captured size to 12 KB so the full activity help 
(which\n    can mention ``resize-task``, ``--activity-options``, ``--activity-bounds``,\n    ``--windowingMode``) is 
included verbatim in the probe.  Anything not\n    in the help text is most likely not supported on this build.\n    
"""\n    helps: dict[str, str] = {}\n    for label, args in (\n        ("cmd_activity_help", ["cmd", "activity", 
"help"]),\n        ("am_help", ["am", "help"]),\n        ("cmd_window_help", ["cmd", "window", "help"]),\n        
("cmd_package_help", ["cmd", "package"]),\n        # Discovery of free-form / resize-task availability.\n        
("cmd_activity_compat", ["cmd", "activity", "compat"]),\n        ("dumpsys_meminfo_one", ["dumpsys", "meminfo", 
"-a"]),\n    ):\n        res = _run(label, args, errors, timeout=8)\n        helps[label] = (res.stdout or 
res.stderr)[:12000]\n    return helps\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Per-package capture \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef _capture_process(package: str, errors: list[dict[str, 
str]]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Run every process-detection variant we care about on the same package."""\n    out: 
dict[str, Any] = {}\n    out["pidof"] = _run(f"pidof {package}", ["pidof", package], errors, timeout=4).stdout\n    
out["pgrep_f"] = _run(f"pgrep -f {package}", ["pgrep", "-f", package], errors, timeout=4).stdout\n    # /proc cmdline 
scan (truncated process names) ù only attempt if root\n    # available.  We embed the package literally in a 
single-quoted ``sh -c``\n    # string; package names contain only ``[A-Za-z0-9._]`` so no escaping\n    # needed 
beyond rejecting anything weird.\n    safe_pkg = package if re.fullmatch(r"[A-Za-z0-9._]+", package) else ""\n    if 
safe_pkg and android.detect_root().available:\n        cmd = (\n            "for f in /proc/[0-9]*/cmdline; do "\n     
       f"grep -lZ {safe_pkg} \\"$f\\" 2>/dev/null; "\n            "done"\n        )\n        out["proc_cmdline_grep"] 
= _run(\n            "/proc/*/cmdline scan",\n            ["sh", "-c", cmd],\n            errors,\n            
root=True,\n            timeout=8,\n        ).stdout[:1500]\n    else:\n        out["proc_cmdline_grep"] = ""\n    # 
ps -A filtered to package.\n    res = _run("ps -A", ["ps", "-A"], errors, timeout=6)\n    out["ps_filtered"] = 
"\\n".join(\n        ln for ln in (res.stdout or "").splitlines() if package in ln\n    )\n    return out\n\n\ndef 
_capture_dumpsys_for_package(package: str, errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, str]:\n    """Capture raw 
dumpsys output filtered to *package*.\n\n    Uses :func:`android.run_android_command` so that:\n      * the bare name 
``dumpsys`` is auto-resolved to ``/system/bin/dumpsys``\n        (Termux does not include ``/system/bin`` in 
``$PATH``);\n      * the call is auto-routed through ``su`` if the unprivileged call\n        returns a 
permission-denial.\n\n    We keep the unfiltered text length so we can prove the host did return\n    something and we 
simply didn\'t match it.\n    """\n    out: dict[str, str] = {}\n    for label, args in (\n        ("window_windows", 
["dumpsys", "window", "windows"]),\n        ("activity_activities", ["dumpsys", "activity", "activities"]),\n        
("activity_recents", ["dumpsys", "activity", "recents"]),\n        ("surfaceflinger_list", ["dumpsys", 
"SurfaceFlinger", "--list"]),\n    ):\n        try:\n            res = android.run_android_command(args, timeout=10)\n 
       except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            errors.append({"step": label, "error": str(exc)[:200]})\n 
           out[label] = "<error>"\n            out[f"{label}_total_len"] = "0"\n            continue\n        if not 
res.ok and not res.stdout:\n            errors.append({"step": f"{label}: rc={res.returncode}", "error": 
res.stderr[:200]})\n        full = res.stdout or ""\n        lines = full.splitlines()\n        matches: list[str] = 
[]\n        for i, ln in enumerate(lines):\n            if package in ln:\n                lo = max(0, i - 2)\n        
        hi = min(len(lines), i + 6)\n                matches.extend(lines[lo:hi])\n                
matches.append("---")\n        out[label] = "\\n".join(matches[:400])[:8000] or "<no package match>"\n        
out[f"{label}_total_len"] = str(len(full))\n    return out\n\n\ndef _capture_shared_prefs(package: str, errors: 
list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Read every shared_prefs XML for the package (root required).\n\n    
Returns ``{filename: content}`` where each content is masked.  Without\n    root we can only enumerate the directory.  
We skip very large files\n    (``cached_app_settings_prefs.xml`` on Roblox is 1.5 MB and contains no\n    layout data 
ù only experiment flags) so the probe stays under the\n    upload size cap.\n    """\n    out: dict[str, Any] = 
{"available": False, "files": {}, "listing": ""}\n    root = android.detect_root()\n    if not root.available:\n       
 out["error"] = "root unavailable"\n        return out\n    prefs_path = f"/data/data/{package}/shared_prefs"\n    
listing = _run(\n        f"ls {prefs_path}",\n        ["ls", "-la", prefs_path],\n        errors,\n        
root=True,\n        timeout=6,\n    )\n    out["listing"] = listing.stdout[:3000]\n    if not listing.ok:\n        
return out\n    out["available"] = True\n    # Parse the listing.  Toybox ``ls -la`` produces 8 whitespace-separated\n 
   # columns (mode, links, owner, group, size, date, time, name); GNU ls\n    # may produce 9.  Match on the filename 
column (last) and the size\n    # column (second-to-last numeric) instead of guessing column counts.\n    
PREFERRED_NAMES = (\n        "pkg_preferences.xml",\n        f"{package}_preferences.xml",\n        "prefs.xml",\n     
   "cloner_settings.xml",\n        "settings.xml",\n    )\n    SKIP_LARGE = ("cached_app_settings_prefs.xml", 
"cached_flag_prefs.xml")\n    MAX_FILE_BYTES = 64 * 1024  # 64 KB per file is plenty for prefs XML\n    for line in 
listing.stdout.splitlines():\n        parts = line.split()\n        if len(parts) < 6:\n            continue\n        
name = parts[-1]\n        if not name.endswith(".xml"):\n            continue\n        if name in SKIP_LARGE:\n        
    out["files"][name] = "<skipped: large cached prefs>"\n            continue\n        # Try to parse the size 
column; if it\'s enormous, skip the cat.\n        size = 0\n        for col in parts[:-1]:\n            if 
col.isdigit():\n                size = max(size, int(col))\n        if size > MAX_FILE_BYTES and name not in 
PREFERRED_NAMES:\n            out["files"][name] = f"<skipped: {size} bytes>"\n            continue\n        cat = 
_run(\n            f"cat {name}",\n            ["cat", f"{prefs_path}/{name}"],\n            errors,\n            
root=True,\n            timeout=6,\n        )\n        out["files"][name] = mask(cat.stdout)[:8000]\n    return 
out\n\n\ndef _capture_presence(entry: dict[str, Any], errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    from . 
import roblox_presence as _rp\n\n    uid = int(entry.get("roblox_user_id") or 0)\n    username = 
str(entry.get("account_username") or "").strip()\n    out: dict[str, Any] = {"configured": False, "user_id": uid, 
"username": username}\n    if uid <= 0 and not username:\n        return out\n    out["configured"] = True\n    try:\n 
       if uid <= 0 and username:\n            uid = int(_safe("lookup_user_id", lambda: _rp.lookup_user_id(username), 
errors, default=0) or 0)\n            out["resolved_user_id"] = uid\n        if uid > 0:\n            p = 
_safe("fetch_presence_one", lambda: _rp.fetch_presence_one(uid), errors, default=None)\n            if p is not 
None:\n                ptype = getattr(p, "presence_type", None)\n                out["presence"] = {\n                
    "presence_type": int(ptype) if ptype is not None else None,\n                    "presence_type_name": 
getattr(ptype, "name", None),\n                    "last_location": mask(getattr(p, "last_location", "") or ""),\n     
               "place_id": getattr(p, "place_id", None),\n                    "root_place_id": getattr(p, 
"root_place_id", None),\n                    "last_online_iso": getattr(p, "last_online_iso", ""),\n                   
 "is_in_game": getattr(p, "is_in_game", False),\n                    "is_lobby": getattr(p, "is_lobby", False),\n      
              "is_offline": getattr(p, "is_offline", False),\n                }\n            else:\n                
out["presence_error"] = "fetch_presence_one returned None"\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
out["presence_error"] = str(exc)[:200]\n    return out\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Config + logs \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef 
_capture_config(errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Load config.json and strip private URLs only 
if requested.  The user\n    explicitly asked for everything \u2192 we keep URLs, mask only secrets."""\n    if not 
CONFIG_PATH.is_file():\n        return {"error": "config.json not present"}\n    try:\n        raw = 
CONFIG_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")\n    except OSError as exc:\n        return {"error": str(exc)[:200]}\n    # 
Pass through mask() so any cookies / webhook URLs / license keys\n    # accidentally pasted into config are 
stripped.\n    safe = mask(raw)\n    try:\n        return json.loads(safe)\n    except json.JSONDecodeError:\n        
# If masking broke JSON (e.g., webhook URL replaced inside a string\n        # making the whole string still valid but 
weird), still return text.\n        return {"raw_text": safe[:8000]}\n\n\ndef _capture_log_tail(errors: list[dict[str, 
str]], lines: int = 200) -> str:\n    """Return last *lines* lines of the agent log, masked."""\n    if not 
LOG_PATH.is_file():\n        return ""\n    try:\n        # Read end of file efficiently.\n        with 
LOG_PATH.open("rb") as f:\n            f.seek(0, os.SEEK_END)\n            size = f.tell()\n            chunk = 
min(size, 256 * 1024)\n            f.seek(size - chunk)\n            tail = f.read().decode("utf-8", 
errors="replace")\n        return mask("\\n".join(tail.splitlines()[-lines:]))\n    except OSError as exc:\n        
errors.append({"step": "log_tail", "error": str(exc)[:200]})\n        return ""\n\n\ndef _capture_all_logs(errors: 
list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:\n    """Capture every file in ``~/.deng-tool/rejoin/logs/`` 
(crash.log, start-debug, à).\n\n    Each entry: ``{size, mtime_iso, tail}`` (last 64 KiB, masked).\n\n    crash.log is 
the C-level traceback written by :mod:`faulthandler` when a\n    true segfault hits.  We always want to surface it 
when present so the\n    diagnoser knows the exact Python frame the process died in.\n    """\n    out: dict[str, 
dict[str, Any]] = {}\n    log_dir = LOG_PATH.parent\n    if not log_dir.is_dir():\n        return out\n    try:\n      
  files = sorted(log_dir.iterdir())\n    except OSError as exc:\n        errors.append({"step": "logs_dir_list", 
"error": str(exc)[:200]})\n        return out\n    for path in files:\n        if not path.is_file():\n            
continue\n        try:\n            stat = path.stat()\n            size = stat.st_size\n            with 
path.open("rb") as f:\n                if size > 64 * 1024:\n                    f.seek(size - 64 * 1024)\n            
        tail_bytes = f.read()\n                else:\n                    tail_bytes = f.read()\n            tail = 
tail_bytes.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")\n            out[path.name] = {\n                "size": size,\n          
      "mtime_iso": datetime.fromtimestamp(\n                    stat.st_mtime, tz=timezone.utc\n                
).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),\n                "tail": mask(tail),\n            }\n        except OSError as 
exc:\n            errors.append({"step": f"log_read:{path.name}", "error": str(exc)[:200]})\n    return out\n\n\ndef 
_capture_installed_build(errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Read 
``~/.deng-tool/rejoin/.installed-build.json`` if present.\n\n    Written by ``install.sh`` after a successful install; 
tells us *exactly*\n    which artifact bytes the runtime is supposed to be running.\n    """\n    p = 
Path(os.path.expanduser("~/.deng-tool/rejoin/.installed-build.json"))\n    if not p.is_file():\n        return {}\n    
try:\n        return json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))\n    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:\n   
     errors.append({"step": "installed_build", "error": str(exc)[:200]})\n        return {}\n\n\ndef 
_capture_wrapper_script(errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Capture the ``deng-rejoin`` shell 
wrapper that PATH resolves to.\n\n    Real-device incident (probe ``p-b30c47d37f``): ``deng-rejoin version``\n    
succeeds but bare ``deng-rejoin`` segfaults.  If the wrapper points at a\n    stale install path, this surfaces it 
immediately.\n    """\n    try:\n        wrapper = shutil.which("deng-rejoin")\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n        errors.append({"step": "wrapper_which", "error": str(exc)[:200]})\n        return {}\n    if not 
wrapper:\n        return {"path": None}\n    out: dict[str, Any] = {"path": wrapper}\n    try:\n        
out["resolved"] = os.path.realpath(wrapper)\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n    try:\n        with open(wrapper, 
"rb") as f:\n            data = f.read(8 * 1024).decode("utf-8", errors="replace")\n        out["head"] = mask(data)\n 
   except OSError as exc:\n        errors.append({"step": "wrapper_read", "error": str(exc)[:200]})\n    return 
out\n\n\ndef _capture_diag_startup(errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Exec ``deng-rejoin 
--diag-startup`` as a child subprocess.\n\n    The child walks the ``cmd_menu`` steps one at a time and prints\n    
``STEP:<name>`` before invoking each.  If the child segfaults, we get\n    ``returncode == -11`` (SIGSEGV) without our 
probe process dying, and\n    the last STEP line tells us *exactly* which sub-routine crashed.\n\n    This is the live 
evidence loop: probe \u2192 diag-startup \u2192 captured output\n    \u2192 real-device crash location.  No more 
guessing from logs.\n    """\n    out: dict[str, Any] = {}\n    wrapper = shutil.which("deng-rejoin")\n    if not 
wrapper:\n        errors.append({"step": "diag_startup", "error": "deng-rejoin not on PATH"})\n        return out\n    
try:\n        proc = subprocess.run(\n            [wrapper, "--diag-startup"],\n            
stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,\n            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n            stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n            
timeout=45,\n            check=False,\n            text=True,\n        )\n        out["returncode"] = 
proc.returncode\n        # SIGSEGV is -11 on Linux/Android; other crash signals also negative.\n        out["sigsegv"] 
= (proc.returncode == -11)\n        out["crashed"] = proc.returncode < 0\n        out["stdout"] = mask((proc.stdout or 
"")[-16384:])\n        out["stderr"] = mask((proc.stderr or "")[-16384:])\n        # The last "STEP:<name>" line 
printed by the child tells us where\n        # it died.  Parse it for convenience.\n        last_step = ""\n        
for line in (proc.stdout or "").splitlines():\n            line = line.strip()\n            if 
line.startswith("STEP:"):\n                last_step = line\n        out["last_step"] = last_step\n    except 
subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:\n        errors.append({"step": "diag_startup", "error": "timeout"})\n        
out["returncode"] = None\n        out["timeout"] = True\n        out["stdout"] = mask((exc.stdout or "")[-8192:] if 
isinstance(exc.stdout, str) else "")\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        errors.append({"step": 
"diag_startup", "error": str(exc)[:200]})\n    return out\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Third-party tool discovery (Kaeru 
/ multi-clone / window manager) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\n# Package-name fragments that identify the 
tools we want to reverse-engineer.\n# We keep the list curated so the probe doesn\'t accidentally exfiltrate every\n# 
random app\'s private prefs ù only tools that overlap our problem space.\n_THIRD_PARTY_TOOL_HINTS = (\n    # Kaeru ù 
the user\'s reference benchmark for "this just works".\n    "kaeru",\n    "shiki",  # the dev\'s other handle is 
"kaerushiki" on some channels\n    # App / multi-clone managers\n    "applauncher",\n    "appcloner",\n    
"appclone",\n    "multispace",\n    "parallel",\n    "multiwindow",\n    "dualspace",\n    "clone",\n    "isolate",\n  
  # Free-form window managers / sidecars\n    "taskbar",\n    "sentry",\n    "freeform",\n    "floating",\n    
"windowmanager",\n    "sidestore",\n    # Roblox-specific launchers and helpers\n    "rolauncher",\n    
"robloxstudio",\n    "rolimons",\n    "joiner",\n    "rejoin",\n    "autojoin",\n)\n\n\ndef 
_capture_installed_packages(errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Return the full ``pm list 
packages -3`` output and a curated subset.\n\n    The curated subset includes:\n      * every package whose id 
contains one of :data:`_THIRD_PARTY_TOOL_HINTS`,\n      * every installed Moons / App Cloner clone (so we have a 
record of which\n        clones the user has installed even if not in the cfg).\n    """\n    out: dict[str, Any] = 
{}\n    try:\n        res = android.run_android_command(\n            ["pm", "list", "packages", "-3"], timeout=15,\n  
      )\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        errors.append({"step": "pm list packages -3", "error": 
str(exc)[:200]})\n        out["pm_list_third_party_raw"] = ""\n        out["third_party_count"] = 0\n        
out["third_party_tools"] = []\n        out["clone_packages"] = []\n        return out\n\n    text = res.stdout or ""\n 
   out["pm_list_third_party_raw"] = text[:60000]  # cap to 60 KB\n\n    pkgs = [\n        ln.split(":", 
1)[1].strip()\n        for ln in text.splitlines()\n        if ln.startswith("package:")\n    ]\n    
out["third_party_count"] = len(pkgs)\n    out["third_party_tools"] = sorted({\n        p for p in pkgs\n        if 
any(h in p.lower() for h in _THIRD_PARTY_TOOL_HINTS)\n    })\n    out["clone_packages"] = sorted({\n        p for p in 
pkgs\n        if (\n            "moons" in p.lower()\n            or "clone" in p.lower()\n            or 
"applauncher" in p.lower()\n            or "appcloner" in p.lower()\n        )\n    })\n    return out\n\n\ndef 
_capture_kaeru_evidence(\n    third_party: dict[str, Any],\n    errors: list[dict[str, str]],\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n  
  """For every third-party tool discovered, capture its install + prefs.\n\n    This is the heart of the "observe what 
the working tool does" loop:\n    every file under ``shared_prefs`` is read and masked, plus the\n    package\'s full 
``pm dump`` (declared permissions, intent filters,\n    activity windowing flags).\n    """\n    out: dict[str, Any] = 
{}\n    targets: list[str] = []\n    for src in ("third_party_tools", "clone_packages"):\n        for pkg in 
third_party.get(src, []) or []:\n            if pkg not in targets:\n                targets.append(pkg)\n\n    
out["targets"] = list(targets)\n    out["pm_dump"] = {}\n    out["shared_prefs"] = {}\n    out["files_dir"] = {}\n\n   
 for pkg in targets:\n        # \u2500\u2500 pm dump <pkg>: declared permissions, intent filters, launch config\n      
  try:\n            res = android.run_android_command(\n                ["pm", "dump", pkg], timeout=12,\n            
)\n            out["pm_dump"][pkg] = mask((res.stdout or "")[:24000])\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n            errors.append({\n                "step": f"pm dump {pkg}",\n                "error": 
str(exc)[:200],\n            })\n            out["pm_dump"][pkg] = "<error>"\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 
/data/data/<pkg>/shared_prefs/*  (full XML, masked)\n        try:\n            out["shared_prefs"][pkg] = 
_capture_shared_prefs(pkg, errors)\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            errors.append({\n    
            "step": f"shared_prefs {pkg}",\n                "error": str(exc)[:200],\n            })\n            
out["shared_prefs"][pkg] = {}\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 /data/data/<pkg>/files/   (root-only; surface filenames + 
small\n        # text contents so we can spot per-clone window-bounds JSON / cfg).\n        try:\n            ls_res = 
android.run_root_command(\n                ["sh", "-c", f"ls -la /data/data/{pkg}/files/ 2>/dev/null | head -200"],\n  
              timeout=6,\n            )\n            out["files_dir"][pkg] = (ls_res.stdout or "")[:8000]\n        
except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            out["files_dir"][pkg] = ""\n\n    return out\n\n\ndef 
_capture_appops(\n    targets: list[str],\n    errors: list[dict[str, str]],\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Capture 
``cmd appops get <pkg>`` ù which Android permissions each\n    third-party tool actually holds at runtime.  Useful to 
know whether\n    Kaeru required SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW / MANAGE_ACTIVITY_STACKS.\n    """\n    out: dict[str, str] = 
{}\n    for pkg in targets:\n        try:\n            res = android.run_android_command(\n                ["cmd", 
"appops", "get", pkg], timeout=8,\n            )\n            out[pkg] = (res.stdout or "")[:8000]\n        except 
Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            errors.append({"step": f"appops {pkg}", "error": str(exc)[:200]})\n     
       out[pkg] = "<error>"\n    return out\n\n\ndef _capture_getprop(errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, 
str]:\n    """Capture ``getprop`` filtered to window/display/freeform-relevant keys.\n\n    Looking for properties 
like ``persist.wm.disable_explicit_size_freeze``,\n    ``ro.config.low_ram``, ``persist.sys.dalvik.vm.lib.2``, OEM 
flags that\n    enable or disable freeform.  Full output is too large; we filter.\n    """\n    out: dict[str, str] = 
{"raw_filtered": "", "all_count": 0}\n    try:\n        res = android.run_android_command(["getprop"], timeout=10)\n   
 except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        errors.append({"step": "getprop", "error": str(exc)[:200]})\n       
 return out\n    raw = res.stdout or ""\n    lines = raw.splitlines()\n    out["all_count"] = len(lines)\n    keep_re 
= re.compile(\n        r"(?i)(window|freeform|resiz|multi|dpi|density|task|surface|"\n        
r"wm\\.|persist\\.sys|ro\\.config|ro\\.build|knox|samsung\\.|game|"\n        
r"split|launcher|home|secur|low_ram|vm\\.lib)",\n    )\n    filtered = [ln for ln in lines if keep_re.search(ln)]\n    
out["raw_filtered"] = "\\n".join(filtered)[:32000]\n    return out\n\n\ndef _capture_dumpsys_global(errors: 
list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, str]:\n    """Capture the FULL ``dumpsys window windows`` / activity / 
SurfaceFlinger\n    output (not just per-package slices).\n\n    HARD BUDGET ù total ~250 KB across all sections (was 
~700 KB which\n    blew past the 4 MB compressed upload cap once we added third-party\n    captures and the user 
hammered the device with tests).  We keep the\n    highest-signal sections (window-windows + activity-activities) 
and\n    drop the redundant ones.  Caller can use ``--diag`` to re-enable\n    SurfaceFlinger when debugging that 
specifically.\n    """\n    out: dict[str, str] = {}\n    # Reduced caps + tighter timeouts.  Each step has its own 
timeout so\n    # one slow `dumpsys SurfaceFlinger` cannot block the whole probe.\n    for label, args, cap, timeout 
in (\n        ("window_windows_full",\n            ["dumpsys", "window", "windows"], 80_000, 8),\n        
("activity_activities_full",\n            ["dumpsys", "activity", "activities"], 80_000, 8),\n        
("activity_recents_full",\n            ["dumpsys", "activity", "recents"], 32_000, 6),\n        ("activity_top",\n     
       ["dumpsys", "activity", "top"], 24_000, 5),\n        ("activity_starter",\n            ["dumpsys", "activity", 
"starter"], 12_000, 5),\n        # SurfaceFlinger is the largest single source (often 80+ KB) and\n        # the 
lowest signal for window-bounds debugging ù keep it tiny.\n        ("surfaceflinger_full",\n            ["dumpsys", 
"SurfaceFlinger"], 24_000, 6),\n    ):\n        try:\n            res = android.run_android_command(args, 
timeout=timeout)\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            errors.append({"step": label, "error": 
str(exc)[:200]})\n            out[label] = "<error>"\n            continue\n        out[label] = (res.stdout or 
"")[:cap]\n    return out\n\n\ndef _capture_logcat(errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> str:\n    """Last ~1000 lines of 
logcat (dump mode), capped at 80 KB.\n\n    Captures every WindowManager / ActivityTaskManager event in the\n    
recent history ù which is where we see Kaeru actually doing\n    ``startActivity ... mode=freeform 
bounds=[l,t][r,b]``.  Masked to\n    drop any URL / cookie that might leak.\n    """\n    try:\n        res = 
android.run_android_command(\n            ["logcat", "-d", "-t", "1000",\n             "WindowManager:V", 
"ActivityManager:V",\n             "ActivityTaskManager:V", "AppOps:V",\n             "TaskLaunchModes:V", 
"SurfaceFlinger:I",\n             "*:W"],\n            timeout=10,\n        )\n        return mask((res.stdout or 
"")[:80_000])\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        errors.append({"step": "logcat -d", "error": 
str(exc)[:200]})\n        return ""\n\n\ndef _capture_termux_prefs(errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n  
  """Termux\'s own shared_prefs ù captures any window/UI state set by\n    a tool that resizes Termux (the same trick 
we want to learn for\n    our 50%-dock).\n    """\n    return _capture_shared_prefs("com.termux", errors)\n\n\ndef 
_capture_last_diagnostics(errors: list[dict[str, str]]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Read 
``data/last_start_diagnostics.json`` if it exists."""\n    p = DATA_DIR / "last_start_diagnostics.json"\n    if not 
p.is_file():\n        return {}\n    try:\n        return json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))\n    except 
(OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:\n        errors.append({"step": "last_diagnostics", "error": 
str(exc)[:200]})\n        return {}\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Public entrypoint \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef collect_probe(*, include_diag_startup: bool | 
None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Run every capture step and return the full probe dict.\n\n    
``include_diag_startup`` (default: env ``DENG_PROBE_DIAG_STARTUP``,\n    else False) controls whether the in-process 
child-subprocess\n    diagnostic runs.  That step can hang for up to 45 s on a stressed\n    device and previously 
caused the probe to appear stuck (which the\n    user reported as "I CANT UPLOAD PROBE AFTER TESTING").  It is now\n   
 opt-in via ``--diag`` on the CLI.\n\n    Never raises.  Errors are captured under ``probe["errors"]``.\n    """\n    
if include_diag_startup is None:\n        include_diag_startup = (\n            
os.environ.get("DENG_PROBE_DIAG_STARTUP", "").strip() in {"1", "true", "yes"}\n        )\n\n    errors: list[dict[str, 
str]] = []\n    out: dict[str, Any] = {\n        "probe_version": PROBE_VERSION,\n        "captured_at_iso": 
datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),\n        "errors": errors,\n    }\n    out["build"] = 
_capture_build_info(errors)\n    out["device"] = _capture_device(errors)\n    out["screen"] = 
_capture_screen(errors)\n    out["settings"] = _capture_settings(errors)\n    out["command_help"] = 
_capture_command_help(errors)\n    out["config"] = _capture_config(errors)\n    # Per-package details.\n    pkgs: 
dict[str, Any] = {}\n    cfg = out["config"]\n    pkg_entries = []\n    if isinstance(cfg, dict):\n        pkg_entries 
= cfg.get("roblox_packages") or []\n        if not pkg_entries and cfg.get("roblox_package"):\n            pkg_entries 
= [{"package": cfg.get("roblox_package")}]\n    for entry in pkg_entries:\n        if not isinstance(entry, dict):\n   
         continue\n        pkg = str(entry.get("package") or "").strip()\n        if not pkg:\n            continue\n  
      pkg_block: dict[str, Any] = {}\n        pkg_block["process"] = _capture_process(pkg, errors)\n        
pkg_block["dumpsys"] = _capture_dumpsys_for_package(pkg, errors)\n        pkg_block["shared_prefs"] = 
_capture_shared_prefs(pkg, errors)\n        pkg_block["presence"] = _capture_presence(entry, errors)\n        
pkgs[pkg] = pkg_block\n    out["packages"] = pkgs\n    out["log_tail"] = _capture_log_tail(errors)\n    out["logs"] = 
_capture_all_logs(errors)\n    out["installed_build"] = _capture_installed_build(errors)\n    out["wrapper"] = 
_capture_wrapper_script(errors)\n    out["last_start_diagnostics"] = _capture_last_diagnostics(errors)\n\n    # 
\u2500\u2500 Third-party tool discovery: the "observe what works" loop 
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    # Find any other launcher / multi-clone / window-manager / 
Kaeru-style\n    # tool the user has installed.  Capture its shared_prefs, declared\n    # permissions, and granted 
app-ops so we can copy whatever technique\n    # makes their freeform resize actually stick on this exact device.\n    
out["installed_packages"] = _capture_installed_packages(errors)\n    out["third_party_evidence"] = 
_capture_kaeru_evidence(\n        out["installed_packages"], errors,\n    )\n    out["appops"] = _capture_appops(\n    
    out["third_party_evidence"].get("targets", []) or [],\n        errors,\n    )\n    # Termux\'s own prefs ù Kaeru 
may set window/dock state on Termux too.\n    out["termux_shared_prefs"] = _capture_termux_prefs(errors)\n    # 
System-property snapshot ù OEM freeform-window flags live here.\n    out["getprop"] = _capture_getprop(errors)\n    # 
Full (non-package-filtered) dumpsys for window/activity/surface state.\n    # This is what we use to see the EXACT 
bounds Kaeru achieved.\n    out["dumpsys_global"] = _capture_dumpsys_global(errors)\n    # Recent logcat ù 
`startActivity ... mode=freeform bounds=[...]` lives\n    # here so we see the actual API calls Kaeru made.\n    
out["logcat"] = _capture_logcat(errors)\n\n    # Heavy in-process child diag: optional, opt-in via --diag.  This\n    
# step can hang up to 45 s on a stressed device.  When skipped we\n    # still attach a marker so the operator knows 
it was deliberate.\n    if include_diag_startup:\n        out["diag_startup"] = _capture_diag_startup(errors)\n    
else:\n        out["diag_startup"] = {"skipped": True,\n                               "reason": "default off; use 
--diag to enable"}\n    return out\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Payload trimming for upload \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n
# The install API rejects payloads larger than this many bytes after\n# gzip compression.  Match the server\'s hard 
cap in license_api.py so we\n# can give the user a clear message instead of a 413.\n_UPLOAD_HARD_CAP_BYTES         = 4 
* 1024 * 1024\n_UPLOAD_SOFT_BUDGET_BYTES      = 3 * 1024 * 1024  # leave headroom\n\n# Order matters ù when over 
budget, we drop the highest-cost,\n# lowest-signal fields first.  Per-package shared_prefs/dumpsys win over\n# the 
global captures because they\'re scoped to the user\'s installation.\n_PROBE_DROP_ORDER: tuple[str, ...] = (\n    
"logcat",                       # ~80 KB, situational\n    "dumpsys_global.surfaceflinger_full",\n    
"dumpsys_global.activity_starter",\n    "dumpsys_global.activity_top",\n    "dumpsys_global.activity_recents_full",\n  
  "third_party_evidence.pm_dump",   # ~24 KB per package\n    "third_party_evidence.shared_prefs",\n    
"installed_packages.pm_list_third_party_raw",\n    "logs",                          # rotating log tails\n    
"dumpsys_global.activity_activities_full",\n    "dumpsys_global.window_windows_full",\n)\n\n\ndef _drop_field(probe: 
dict[str, Any], dotted: str) -> bool:\n    """Drop a (possibly nested) key like ``dumpsys_global.activity_top``.\n\n   
 Returns True if the field existed and was removed.  Never raises.\n    """\n    parts = dotted.split(".")\n    cur: 
Any = probe\n    for p in parts[:-1]:\n        if not isinstance(cur, dict) or p not in cur:\n            return 
False\n        cur = cur[p]\n    if not isinstance(cur, dict):\n        return False\n    last = parts[-1]\n    if 
last not in cur:\n        return False\n    # Replace with marker so the operator knows it was dropped, not absent.\n  
  cur[last] = "<dropped: payload size budget>"\n    return True\n\n\ndef trim_probe_for_upload(\n    probe: dict[str, 
Any], *,\n    hard_cap: int = _UPLOAD_HARD_CAP_BYTES,\n    soft_budget: int = _UPLOAD_SOFT_BUDGET_BYTES,\n) -> 
tuple[dict[str, Any], dict[str, Any]]:\n    """Return ``(trimmed_probe, report)`` where the gzipped JSON size\n    
fits under ``hard_cap`` and is targeted at ``soft_budget``.\n\n    Strategy: serialize \u2192 gzip \u2192 measure 
\u2192 if over, drop the next entry\n    from :data:`_PROBE_DROP_ORDER` and repeat.  Stop as soon as we are\n    under 
``soft_budget`` (or we run out of drop targets).  Never raises.\n    """\n    import gzip as _gz\n\n    trimmed = 
dict(probe)\n    dropped: list[str] = []\n    measurements: list[int] = []\n\n    def _measure(p: dict[str, Any]) -> 
int:\n        try:\n            return len(_gz.compress(\n                json.dumps(p, separators=(",", 
":")).encode("utf-8"),\n            ))\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            return hard_cap + 1   # 
force-trim on any error\n\n    size = _measure(trimmed)\n    measurements.append(size)\n    for field in 
_PROBE_DROP_ORDER:\n        if size <= soft_budget:\n            break\n        if _drop_field(trimmed, field):\n      
      dropped.append(field)\n        size = _measure(trimmed)\n        measurements.append(size)\n\n    # If we\'re 
STILL over the hard cap after dropping everything,\n    # truncate every remaining string field to 4 KB.  
Last-ditch.\n    if size > hard_cap:\n        def _truncate(d: Any) -> None:\n            if isinstance(d, dict):\n    
            for k, v in list(d.items()):\n                    if isinstance(v, str) and len(v) > 4000:\n               
         d[k] = v[:4000] + "...<truncated>"\n                    elif isinstance(v, (dict, list)):\n                   
     _truncate(v)\n            elif isinstance(d, list):\n                for v in d:\n                    if 
isinstance(v, (dict, list)):\n                        _truncate(v)\n        _truncate(trimmed)\n        size = 
_measure(trimmed)\n        measurements.append(size)\n        dropped.append("<truncated all strings to 4 KB>")\n\n    
trimmed["_upload_trim"] = {\n        "final_gzipped_bytes": size,\n        "hard_cap": hard_cap,\n        
"soft_budget": soft_budget,\n        "dropped": dropped,\n        "measurement_bytes_per_pass": measurements,\n    }\n 
   report = {\n        "dropped": dropped,\n        "final_gzipped_bytes": size,\n        "passes": 
len(measurements),\n    }\n    return trimmed, report\n\n\ndef save_probe(probe: dict[str, Any]) -> Path:\n    
"""Persist *probe* to ``$DATA/probes/probe-<short>.json`` and return path."""\n    PROBE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, 
exist_ok=True)\n    ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")\n    path = PROBE_DIR / 
f"probe-{ts}-{_short_id()}.json"\n    path.write_text(json.dumps(probe, indent=2, sort_keys=True), encoding="utf-8")\n 
   return path\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Upload \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef 
_resolve_install_api() -> str:\n    api_path = Path(os.path.expanduser("~/.deng-tool/rejoin/.install_api"))\n    if 
api_path.is_file():\n        try:\n            return api_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()\n        except 
OSError:\n            pass\n    return os.environ.get("DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API", "").strip()\n\n\ndef 
upload_probe(probe: dict[str, Any], *, timeout: float = 30.0) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    """POST the probe JSON to the 
install API\'s dev-probe endpoint.\n\n    Trims the payload first so it fits the 4 MB compressed cap, then\n    POSTs 
the gzipped JSON.  Returns ``(ok, info)`` where ``info`` is a\n    short probe id on success, or a short, 
USER-ACTIONABLE error string\n    on failure.  Never raises.\n    """\n    import gzip\n    import urllib.error\n    
import urllib.request\n\n    api = _resolve_install_api()\n    if not api:\n        return False, "install API URL not 
configured (set DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_API)"\n    url = api.rstrip("/") + "/api/dev-probe/upload"\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 
Size budget \u2500\u2500 trim ANY oversized payload BEFORE we POST so the\n    # server\'s 4 MB cap doesn\'t reject us 
with an opaque 413.  This is\n    # what was blocking the user after running many tests back-to-back.\n    trimmed, 
trim_report = trim_probe_for_upload(probe)\n    body = json.dumps(trimmed, separators=(",", ":")).encode("utf-8")\n    
payload = gzip.compress(body)\n\n    if len(payload) > _UPLOAD_HARD_CAP_BYTES:\n        return False, (\n            
f"payload still {len(payload)/1024/1024:.1f} MB after trim ù "\n            f"server cap is 
{_UPLOAD_HARD_CAP_BYTES/1024/1024:.0f} MB. "\n            f"Dropped: {trim_report.get(\'dropped\') or []!r}"\n        
)\n\n    req = urllib.request.Request(url, data=payload, method="POST")\n    req.add_header("Content-Type", 
"application/json")\n    req.add_header("Content-Encoding", "gzip")\n    req.add_header("User-Agent", 
"deng-rejoin-probe/1")\n    req.add_header("X-Dev-Probe-Token", "deng-rejoin-dev-probe-v1")\n    try:\n        with 
urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp:\n            data = resp.read().decode("utf-8", 
errors="replace")\n        try:\n            obj = json.loads(data)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n            
return False, f"non-JSON response: {data[:200]}"\n        pid = str(obj.get("probe_id") or "").strip()\n        if not 
pid:\n            return False, f"no probe_id in response: {data[:200]}"\n        return True, pid\n    except 
urllib.error.HTTPError as exc:\n        body_text = ""\n        try:\n            body_text = 
exc.read().decode("utf-8", errors="replace")[:300]\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            pass\n      
  # Pretty-print common rejection cases so the user knows what to do.\n        if exc.code == 413:\n            return 
False, (\n                f"server rejected size {len(payload)} bytes (413). "\n                f"Trim dropped: 
{trim_report.get(\'dropped\') or []!r}. "\n                f"Server body: {body_text}"\n            )\n        if 
exc.code == 429:\n            return False, (\n                f"rate-limited (429) ù wait ~60 seconds and try again. 
"\n                f"Server body: {body_text}"\n            )\n        if exc.code == 401:\n            return False, 
f"unauthorized (401) ù check DENG_DEV_PROBE_TOKEN. {body_text}"\n        return False, f"HTTP {exc.code}: 
{body_text}"\n    except urllib.error.URLError as exc:\n        return False, f"network error: {exc.reason}"\n    
except OSError as exc:\n        return False, f"OS error: {exc}"\n\n"""Install version list for DENG Tool: Rejoin 
(GitHub tags + manifest).\n\nSupports **public** stable installs (tags / visible manifest rows) and 
**internal**\ndev/beta/manifest-only rows for owner/admin/tester visibility only.\n\nNo discord.py imports ù safe for 
agent tests and the license panel builders.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport os\nfrom 
dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\nfrom urllib.error import HTTPError, 
URLError\nfrom urllib.request import Request, urlopen\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass RejoinVersionInfo:\n    
"""One selectable install target."""\n\n    version: str\n    channel: str = "stable"\n    label: str = ""\n    
install_ref: str = ""\n    recommended: bool = False\n    description: str = ""\n    #: True \u2192 show 
internal/testing disclaimer in install text; hidden from public list.\n    internal_only: bool = False\n\n    def 
__post_init__(self) -> None:\n        if not self.install_ref:\n            object.__setattr__(self, "install_ref", 
f"refs/tags/{self.version}" if self.version else "")\n        if not self.label:\n            ch = 
self.channel.capitalize()\n            object.__setattr__(self, "label", f"{self.version} {ch}")\n\n\ndef 
default_versions_manifest_path() -> Path:\n    """JSON list path (repo root ``data/rejoin_versions.json``)."""\n    
override = (os.environ.get("REJOIN_VERSIONS_MANIFEST") or "").strip()\n    if override:\n        return 
Path(override).expanduser()\n    return Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / "data" / "rejoin_versions.json"\n\n\ndef 
github_owner() -> str:\n    return (os.environ.get("REJOIN_GITHUB_OWNER") or "dengjiangbin").strip()\n\n\ndef 
github_repo() -> str:\n    return (os.environ.get("REJOIN_GITHUB_REPO") or "deng-tool-rejoin").strip()\n\n\ndef 
fetch_github_tag_names(*, timeout: float = 12.0) -> list[str] | None:\n    """Return tag names from GitHub API, or 
``None`` if the request failed."""\n    owner, repo = github_owner(), github_repo()\n    url = 
f"https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/tags?per_page=100"\n    req = Request(url, headers={"Accept": 
"application/vnd.github+json", "User-Agent": "deng-tool-rejoin"})\n    try:\n        with urlopen(req, 
timeout=timeout) as resp:\n            if resp.status != 200:\n                return None\n            data = 
json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8"))\n    except (OSError, HTTPError, URLError, json.JSONDecodeError, 
ValueError):\n        return None\n    if not isinstance(data, list):\n        return None\n    names: list[str] = 
[]\n    for row in data:\n        if isinstance(row, dict):\n            name = str(row.get("name") or "").strip()\n   
         if name:\n                names.append(name)\n    return names\n\n\ndef _load_manifest_raw() -> 
list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    path = default_versions_manifest_path()\n    if not path.is_file():\n        return []\n    
try:\n        data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))\n    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n       
 return []\n    return data if isinstance(data, list) else []\n\n\ndef _norm_version(v: str) -> str:\n    return str(v 
or "").strip()\n\n\ndef _channel_rank(ch: str) -> int:\n    c = (ch or "stable").strip().lower()\n    return 
{"stable": 0, "beta": 1, "dev": 2}.get(c, 99)\n\n\ndef _manifest_row_public_allowed(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n    
"""Manifest rows marked ``visible: false`` or ``visibility: admin`` are never public."""\n    vis = 
str(row.get("visibility") or "").strip().lower()\n    if vis in {"admin", "internal", "private", "owner"}:\n        
return False\n    if row.get("visible") is False:\n        return False\n    return True\n\n\ndef 
_install_ref_public_allowed(ref: str) -> bool:\n    """Branch installs (``refs/heads/à``) are never offered on the 
public list."""\n    r = (ref or "").strip()\n    if r in {"", "main"}:\n        return False\n    if 
r.startswith("refs/heads/"):\n        return False\n    return True\n\n\ndef _compute_internal_only(\n    channel: 
str,\n    install_ref: str,\n    manifest_public: bool,\n    public_beta: bool,\n) -> bool:\n    """Internal builds 
get extra disclosure text and are omitted from the public picker."""\n    ch = channel.lower()\n    if not 
_install_ref_public_allowed(install_ref):\n        return True\n    if not manifest_public:\n        return True\n    
if ch == "dev":\n        return True\n    if ch == "beta" and not public_beta:\n        return True\n    return 
False\n\n\ndef merge_version_sources(\n    *,\n    tag_names: list[str] | None,\n    include_internal_channels: bool = 
False,\n    include_dev_for_admin: bool | None = None,\n) -> list[RejoinVersionInfo]:\n    """Combine GitHub tags with 
``data/rejoin_versions.json``.\n\n    When ``include_internal_channels`` is False (default), only rows safe for the\n  
  public picker are returned (no ``refs/heads/à``, no admin-only manifest rows,\n    no dev channel, beta only if 
``REJOIN_PUBLIC_BETA``).\n\n    ``include_dev_for_admin`` is a deprecated alias for ``include_internal_channels``.\n   
 """\n    if include_dev_for_admin is not None:\n        include_internal_channels = bool(include_dev_for_admin)\n\n   
 raw_manifest = _load_manifest_raw()\n    by_version: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}\n    for row in raw_manifest:\n   
     if not isinstance(row, dict):\n            continue\n        ver = _norm_version(str(row.get("version") or ""))\n 
       if not ver:\n            continue\n        by_version[ver] = row\n\n    public_beta = 
(os.environ.get("REJOIN_PUBLIC_BETA") or "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}\n\n    out: dict[str, 
RejoinVersionInfo] = {}\n\n    tags = list(tag_names) if tag_names else []\n    for ver in tags:\n        row = 
by_version.get(ver)\n        if row is not None and row.get("enabled") is False:\n            continue\n        if row 
is not None:\n            ref = str(row.get("install_ref") or row.get("ref") or f"refs/tags/{ver}")\n            ch = 
str(row.get("channel") or "stable").lower()\n            label = str(row.get("label") or row.get("title") or f"{ver} 
{ch.capitalize()}")\n            desc = str(row.get("description") or row.get("notes") or "")[:200]\n            rec = 
bool(row.get("recommended"))\n            mpub = _manifest_row_public_allowed(row)\n        else:\n            ref = 
f"refs/tags/{ver}"\n            ch = "stable"\n            label = f"{ver} Stable"\n            desc = ""\n            
rec = False\n            mpub = True\n        io = _compute_internal_only(ch, ref, mpub, public_beta)\n        
out[ver] = RejoinVersionInfo(\n            version=ver,\n            channel=ch,\n            label=label[:256],\n     
       install_ref=ref,\n            recommended=rec,\n            description=desc,\n            internal_only=io,\n  
      )\n\n    for ver, row in by_version.items():\n        if row.get("enabled") is False:\n            continue\n    
    if ver in out:\n            continue\n        ref = str(row.get("install_ref") or row.get("ref") or 
f"refs/tags/{ver}")\n        ch = str(row.get("channel") or "stable").lower()\n        label = str(row.get("label") or 
row.get("title") or f"{ver} {ch.capitalize()}")\n        desc = str(row.get("description") or row.get("notes") or 
"")[:200]\n        rec = bool(row.get("recommended"))\n        mpub = _manifest_row_public_allowed(row)\n        io = 
_compute_internal_only(ch, ref, mpub, public_beta)\n        out[ver] = RejoinVersionInfo(\n            version=ver,\n  
          channel=ch,\n            label=label[:256],\n            install_ref=ref,\n            recommended=rec,\n    
        description=desc,\n            internal_only=io,\n        )\n\n    items = list(out.values())\n\n    def 
sort_key(v: RejoinVersionInfo) -> tuple[int, int, str]:\n        rec = 0 if v.recommended else 1\n        return 
(_channel_rank(v.channel), rec, v.version)\n\n    items.sort(key=sort_key)\n\n    if include_internal_channels:\n      
  return items\n    return [v for v in items if not v.internal_only]\n\n\ndef list_public_rejoin_versions(\n    *,\n   
 include_internal_channels: bool = False,\n    include_dev_for_admin: bool | None = None,\n) -> 
list[RejoinVersionInfo]:\n    """GitHub tags + manifest merge. Pass ``include_internal_channels=True`` for internal 
picker."""\n    if include_dev_for_admin is not None:\n        include_internal_channels = 
bool(include_dev_for_admin)\n    raw = fetch_github_tag_names()\n    tag_list: list[str] = [] if raw is None else 
list(raw)\n    return merge_version_sources(tag_names=tag_list, 
include_internal_channels=include_internal_channels)\n\n\ndef build_full_install_command(owner: str, repo: str, 
install_ref: str) -> str:\n    """Legacy one-liner using GitHub **raw** ``install.sh`` (internal/dev only).\n\n    
Public panel copy uses :func:`build_public_install_curl_command` instead.\n    """\n    ref = install_ref.strip()\n    
raw = f"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/{owner}/{repo}/{ref}/install.sh"\n    return (\n        
f"DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_REF={ref} curl -fsSL {raw} -o install.sh && "\n        f"DENG_REJOIN_INSTALL_REF={ref} bash 
install.sh"\n    )\n\n\ndef build_public_install_curl_command(info: RejoinVersionInfo) -> str:\n    """Public tutorial 
/ panel curl ù ``https://rejoin.deng.my.id/install/...``."""\n    from agent.install_registry import 
public_install_base_url, resolve_latest_public_stable\n\n    base = public_install_base_url().rstrip("/")\n    if 
info.internal_only:\n        return f"curl -fsSL {base}/install/test/latest -o install.sh && bash install.sh"\n\n    
latest = resolve_latest_public_stable()\n    latest_ver = str(latest.get("version") or "").strip() if latest else ""\n 
   if latest_ver and latest_ver == info.version.strip():\n        path = "/install/latest"\n    else:\n        path = 
f"/install/{info.version.strip()}"\n    return f"curl -fsSL {base}{path} -o install.sh && bash install.sh"\n\n\ndef 
format_install_instructions_plain(info: RejoinVersionInfo) -> str:\n    """Plain text for Discord ù only Desktop Copy 
and Mobile Copy blocks."""\n    cmd = build_public_install_curl_command(info)\n    lines = [\n        "Desktop 
Copy:",\n        f"```{cmd}```",\n        "",\n        "Mobile Copy:",\n        f"```{cmd}```",\n    ]\n    return 
"\\n".join(lines)\n\n\nNO_PUBLIC_VERSIONS_MESSAGE = (\n    "No public versions are available yet.\\n\\nPlease wait for 
the next public release."\n)\n\n"""Extra Roblox connectivity / disconnect signals (best-effort, no secret 
scraping)."""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom typing 
import Any\n\nfrom . import android\nfrom .config import validate_package_name\n\n# (regex, reason) ù only use when 
regex matches; keep patterns conservative.\n_LOGCAT_RULES: list[tuple[re.Pattern[str], str]] = [\n    
(re.compile(r"\\b(connection lost|disconnected from|lost connection|network error)\\b", re.I), "disconnected"),\n    
(re.compile(r"\\b(server shut|shutting down|server closed|you were kicked)\\b", re.I), "server_shutdown"),\n    (\n    
    re.compile(r"\\b(private server link (code )?refresh|private server expired|private server access)\\b", re.I),\n   
     "private_server_refresh",\n    ),\n]\n\n# dumpsys / activity text (no secrets expected in these 
fragments)\n_DUMPSYS_RULES: list[tuple[re.Pattern[str], str]] = [\n    
(re.compile(r"\\b(ErrorActivity|Disconnected|ConnectionError|Reconnect)\\b"), "disconnected"),\n    
(re.compile(r"\\b(maintenance|shut\\s*down)\\b", re.I), "server_shutdown"),\n]\n\n_REASON_FALLBACK = 
"unknown_unhealthy"\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass UnhealthyEvidence:\n    category: str\n    source: str\n    
snippet: str\n\n\ndef _pid_for_package(package: str) -> str | None:\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n    
res = android.run_command(["pidof", package], timeout=android.PROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)\n    if res.ok and 
res.stdout.strip():\n        return res.stdout.strip().split()[0]\n    return None\n\n\ndef _brief_logcat_for_pid(pid: 
str) -> str:\n    res = android.run_command(\n        ["logcat", "-d", "-t", "60", "--pid", pid],\n        
timeout=5,\n    )\n    if res.ok:\n        return res.stdout[-4000:]\n    res2 = android.run_command(["logcat", "-d", 
"-t", "80"], timeout=5)\n    return res2.stdout[-4000:] if res2.ok else ""\n\n\ndef _match_rules(text: str, rules: 
list[tuple[re.Pattern[str], str]]) -> tuple[str | None, str]:\n    if not text:\n        return None, ""\n    for rx, 
cat in rules:\n        m = rx.search(text)\n        if m:\n            return cat, m.group(0)[:80]\n    return None, 
""\n\n\ndef analyze_disconnect_signals(package: str) -> UnhealthyEvidence | None:\n    """When process is alive but 
unhealthy, look for non-secret UI/log hints."""\n    package = validate_package_name(package)\n    pid = 
_pid_for_package(package)\n    if pid:\n        log_blob = _brief_logcat_for_pid(pid)\n        cat, snip = 
_match_rules(log_blob, _LOGCAT_RULES)\n        if cat:\n            return UnhealthyEvidence(category=cat, 
source="logcat", snippet=snip)\n    # Activity / window (global dumpsys ù filter lines mentioning package)\n    act = 
android.run_command(["dumpsys", "activity", "activities"], timeout=6)\n    if act.ok:\n        lines = [ln for ln in 
act.stdout.splitlines() if package in ln][:40]\n        blob = "\\n".join(lines)\n        cat, snip = 
_match_rules(blob, _DUMPSYS_RULES)\n        if cat:\n            return UnhealthyEvidence(category=cat, 
source="dumpsys_activity", snippet=snip)\n    win = android.run_command(["dumpsys", "window", "windows"], timeout=6)\n 
   if win.ok:\n        lines = [ln for ln in win.stdout.splitlines() if package in ln][:30]\n        blob = 
"\\n".join(lines)\n        cat, snip = _match_rules(blob, _DUMPSYS_RULES)\n        if cat:\n            return 
UnhealthyEvidence(category=cat, source="dumpsys_window", snippet=snip)\n    cmd_top = android.run_command(\n        
["dumpsys", "activity", "top"], timeout=5\n    )\n    if cmd_top.ok:\n        lines = [ln for ln in 
cmd_top.stdout.splitlines() if package in ln][:24]\n        blob = "\\n".join(lines)\n        cat, snip = 
_match_rules(blob, _DUMPSYS_RULES)\n        if cat:\n            return UnhealthyEvidence(category=cat, 
source="activity_top", snippet=snip)\n\n\ndef categorize_unhealthy(default_reason: str | None, package: str) -> str:\n 
   """Map signals to reason category; never invent specific category without evidence."""\n    ev = 
analyze_disconnect_signals(package)\n    if ev:\n        return ev.category\n    if default_reason in {\n        
"disconnected",\n        "process_missing",\n        "server_shutdown",\n        "private_server_refresh",\n        
"heartbeat_timeout",\n    }:\n        return default_reason\n    return _REASON_FALLBACK\n\n"""Ground-truth Roblox 
presence detection via the official public API.\n\nThe cloud-phone screenshot shows clones playing in-game while our 
local\n``dumpsys``-based detection says ``Offline``.  That happens because\n``dumpsys window``/``pidof`` are not 
authoritative for App Cloner clones ù\nprocess names are truncated, surfaces are reported with varying markers,\nand 
tasks linger after a window closes.\n\nRoblox themselves know the truth: their presence API returns each 
user\'s\nstate (Offline / Online / In-Game / In-Studio) plus the place they are in.\nThis module wraps that API so the 
supervisor can ground every decision\nin what Roblox actually reports for the configured account.\n\nEndpoints used 
(all public, all documented):\n\n    POST https://users.roblox.com/v1/usernames/users\n      body: {"usernames": 
["alice"], "excludeBannedUsers": false}\n      returns: [{"id": 12345, "name": "alice", ...}]\n\n    POST 
https://presence.roblox.com/v1/presence/users\n      body: {"userIds": [12345, ...]}\n      returns: {"userPresences": 
[\n          {"userPresenceType": 0|1|2|3|4,\n           "lastLocation": "...",\n           "placeId": int|null,\n     
      "userId": int,\n           "rootPlaceId": int|null,\n           "lastOnline": "ISO-8601 timestamp"} ...]}\n\n    
  userPresenceType values:\n        0 = Offline    (logged out / app not connected)\n        1 = Online     (lobby / 
home / browsing)\n        2 = InGame     (in a place ù what we want for "Online" in our table)\n        3 = InStudio   
(irrelevant for end users)\n        4 = Invisible  (rare, treat as Offline)\n\nAn optional ``.ROBLOSECURITY`` cookie 
can be supplied per user; the API\nreturns the same shape for anonymous callers but with reduced placeId\ndetail.  
Cookies are NEVER printed or logged ù only their first/last\nchars when masking is needed.\n\nThreading / caching: 
results are cached per user id for ``PRESENCE_TTL``\nseconds.  ``fetch_presence`` is reentrant and safe across 
threads.\n\nNever raises.  Network/JSON/SSL failures all return safe defaults.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import 
annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport re\nimport threading\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, 
field\nfrom enum import IntEnum\nfrom typing import Iterable, Mapping, Sequence\n\nfrom . import safe_http\n\n_log = 
logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.roblox_presence")\n\n# Public endpoints ù both accept anonymous POST with a JSON 
body.\n_USERNAME_LOOKUP_URL = "https://users.roblox.com/v1/usernames/users"\n_PRESENCE_URL = 
"https://presence.roblox.com/v1/presence/users"\n\nPRESENCE_TTL: float = 8.0           # cache window for a single 
user\'s presence\nUSERNAME_LOOKUP_TTL: float = 86400.0  # username \u2192 id resolution rarely changes\nHTTP_TIMEOUT: 
float = 6.0\n\n# User-Agent must look like a real client; some Roblox edge nodes 403 on\n# default Python urllib UA.  
We don\'t impersonate a browser ù just identify\n# ourselves clearly so the request is accepted by their 
CDN.\n_USER_AGENT = "DENG-Tool-Rejoin/1.0 (+presence-check)"\n\n\nclass PresenceType(IntEnum):\n    OFFLINE = 0\n    
ONLINE = 1\n    IN_GAME = 2\n    IN_STUDIO = 3\n    INVISIBLE = 4\n    UNKNOWN = -1   # network failure / not yet 
observed\n\n    @property\n    def label(self) -> str:\n        return {\n            0: "Offline",\n            1: 
"Online",\n            2: "InGame",\n            3: "InStudio",\n            4: "Invisible",\n        }.get(int(self), 
"Unknown")\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass PresenceResult:\n    user_id: int\n    presence_type: PresenceType = 
PresenceType.UNKNOWN\n    place_id: int | None = None\n    root_place_id: int | None = None\n    last_location: str = 
""\n    last_online_iso: str = ""\n\n    @property\n    def is_in_game(self) -> bool:\n        return 
self.presence_type == PresenceType.IN_GAME\n\n    @property\n    def is_lobby(self) -> bool:\n        return 
self.presence_type == PresenceType.ONLINE\n\n    @property\n    def is_offline(self) -> bool:\n        return 
self.presence_type in (\n            PresenceType.OFFLINE, PresenceType.INVISIBLE,\n        )\n\n    @property\n    
def is_unknown(self) -> bool:\n        return self.presence_type == PresenceType.UNKNOWN\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 
in-memory caches \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n
_PRESENCE_LOCK = threading.Lock()\n_PRESENCE_CACHE: dict[int, tuple[float, PresenceResult]] = {}\n\n_USERNAME_LOCK = 
threading.Lock()\n_USERNAME_CACHE: dict[str, tuple[float, int | None]] = {}\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 http helper \u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\n\ndef _post_json(\n    url: str,\n    body: Mapping[str, object],\n    *,\n    cookie: str | None = None,\n    
timeout: float = HTTP_TIMEOUT,\n) -> Mapping[str, object] | None:\n    """POST JSON to a Roblox endpoint and return 
parsed JSON, or None.\n\n    Never raises.  Returns None on any failure.  Caller decides whether\n    "no data" should 
mean Unknown or Offline.\n\n    Probe p-79933739d8: live Start reached watchdog package 1 and then the\n    process 
vanished before state evidence was logged.  The only native-risky\n    path entered there was Python urllib/ssl for 
the Roblox Presence API.\n    Route through ``safe_http`` so Termux uses curl in a child process; if curl\n    
crashes, Python receives a clean network error instead of SIGSEGV.\n    """\n    if not isinstance(body, Mapping):\n   
     return None\n\n    headers = {"User-Agent": _USER_AGENT, "Accept": "application/json"}\n    if cookie:\n        # 
Some endpoints want X-CSRF-TOKEN even for trivial GETs once a\n        # cookie is present.  Roblox returns the token 
in a header on a\n        # 403, then accepts the retry ù but that handshake is fragile.\n        # For presence + 
username lookups, an anonymous POST works fine.\n        # We pass the cookie only when the caller explicitly opts 
in,\n        # and we keep the field name safe (no leaking in error text).\n        masked = mask_cookie(cookie)\n     
   _log.debug("attaching cookie (masked=%s)", masked)\n        headers["Cookie"] = f".ROBLOSECURITY={cookie}"\n\n    
try:\n        payload = safe_http.post_json(\n            url,\n            dict(body),\n            
headers=headers,\n            timeout=max(1, int(timeout)),\n        )\n        return payload if isinstance(payload, 
Mapping) else None\n    except safe_http.SafeHttpError:\n        return None\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n        _log.debug("presence POST error: %s", exc)\n        return None\n\n\ndef mask_cookie(cookie: str | 
None) -> str:\n    """Return a short masked representation of a ROBLOSECURITY cookie.\n\n    NEVER returns more than 
12 chars of the original value.  Safe to log.\n    """\n    if not cookie:\n        return ""\n    s = 
str(cookie).strip()\n    if len(s) <= 12:\n        return "***"\n    return f"{s[:4]}à{s[-4:]}"\n\n\n# 
\u2500\u2500\u2500 username \u2192 userId \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\n\n_USERNAME_VALID_RE = re.compile(r"^[A-Za-z0-9_]{3,20}$")\n\n\ndef 
resolve_username_to_user_id(username: str | None) -> int | None:\n    """Resolve a Roblox username to a numeric user 
ID.  Returns None on failure.\n\n    Alias for :func:`lookup_user_id` ù provided so callers can use the longer,\n    
more descriptive name.  Result is cached for ``USERNAME_LOOKUP_TTL`` seconds.\n    Never raises.\n    """\n    return 
lookup_user_id(username)\n\n\ndef lookup_user_id(username: str | None) -> int | None:\n    """Resolve a Roblox 
username to a numeric user_id.  Returns None on failure.\n\n    Result is cached for ``USERNAME_LOOKUP_TTL`` seconds.  
Never raises.\n    """\n    if not username:\n        return None\n    name = str(username).strip()\n    if not 
_USERNAME_VALID_RE.match(name):\n        return None\n    now = time.monotonic()\n    with _USERNAME_LOCK:\n        
cached = _USERNAME_CACHE.get(name.lower())\n        if cached and (now - cached[0]) < USERNAME_LOOKUP_TTL:\n           
 return cached[1]\n    body = {"usernames": [name], "excludeBannedUsers": False}\n    payload = 
_post_json(_USERNAME_LOOKUP_URL, body)\n    user_id: int | None = None\n    if payload is None:\n        # 
Network/CDN/SSL failures must not poison the username cache with\n        # ``None`` for a day.  The watchdog will 
retry later or use rooted\n        # per-clone prefs evidence.\n        return None\n    if isinstance(payload, 
dict):\n        data = payload.get("data")\n        if isinstance(data, list) and data:\n            first = data[0]\n 
           if isinstance(first, dict):\n                raw = first.get("id")\n                if isinstance(raw, int) 
and raw > 0:\n                    user_id = raw\n                elif isinstance(raw, str) and raw.isdigit():\n        
            user_id = int(raw)\n    with _USERNAME_LOCK:\n        _USERNAME_CACHE[name.lower()] = (now, user_id)\n    
return user_id\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 presence fetch \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef _parse_presence_row(row: Mapping[str, object]) -> 
PresenceResult | None:\n    try:\n        uid_raw = row.get("userId")\n        if isinstance(uid_raw, str) and 
uid_raw.isdigit():\n            uid = int(uid_raw)\n        elif isinstance(uid_raw, int):\n            uid = 
uid_raw\n        else:\n            return None\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return None\n    
pres_raw = row.get("userPresenceType")\n    try:\n        ptype = PresenceType(int(pres_raw))\n    except (TypeError, 
ValueError):\n        ptype = PresenceType.UNKNOWN\n    place_id = row.get("placeId")\n    root_place = 
row.get("rootPlaceId")\n    return PresenceResult(\n        user_id=uid,\n        presence_type=ptype,\n        
place_id=int(place_id) if isinstance(place_id, int) else None,\n        root_place_id=int(root_place) if 
isinstance(root_place, int) else None,\n        last_location=str(row.get("lastLocation") or "")[:80],\n        
last_online_iso=str(row.get("lastOnline") or "")[:32],\n    )\n\n\ndef fetch_presence(\n    user_ids: Sequence[int],\n 
   *,\n    cookie: str | None = None,\n    refresh: bool = False,\n) -> dict[int, PresenceResult]:\n    """Fetch 
presence for a batch of user ids.  Cached per-user for ``PRESENCE_TTL``.\n\n    Returns a dict ``{user_id: 
PresenceResult}``.  IDs the API failed to\n    return are mapped to ``PresenceType.UNKNOWN`` rather than missing 
keys,\n    so callers can rely on the dict containing every requested id.\n    Never raises.\n    """\n    out: 
dict[int, PresenceResult] = {}\n    if not user_ids:\n        return out\n    ids = sorted({int(u) for u in user_ids 
if isinstance(u, int) and u > 0})\n    if not ids:\n        return out\n\n    now = time.monotonic()\n    fresh: 
list[int] = []\n    if not refresh:\n        with _PRESENCE_LOCK:\n            for uid in ids:\n                hit = 
_PRESENCE_CACHE.get(uid)\n                if hit and (now - hit[0]) < PRESENCE_TTL:\n                    out[uid] =
