hit[1]\n                else:\n                    fresh.append(uid)\n    else:\n        fresh = list(ids)\n\n    if 
not fresh:\n        return out\n\n    # Roblox limits the batch to 200 ids per request; we never hit that in\n    # 
practice but chunk defensively anyway.\n    BATCH = 100\n    for i in range(0, len(fresh), BATCH):\n        batch = 
fresh[i:i + BATCH]\n        payload = _post_json(\n            _PRESENCE_URL, {"userIds": batch}, cookie=cookie,\n     
   )\n        seen_in_batch: set[int] = set()\n        if isinstance(payload, dict):\n            rows = 
payload.get("userPresences")\n            if isinstance(rows, list):\n                for row in rows:\n               
     if not isinstance(row, dict):\n                        continue\n                    pr = 
_parse_presence_row(row)\n                    if pr is None:\n                        continue\n                    
out[pr.user_id] = pr\n                    seen_in_batch.add(pr.user_id)\n                    with _PRESENCE_LOCK:\n    
                    _PRESENCE_CACHE[pr.user_id] = (now, pr)\n        # For any id the server didn\'t return, surface 
an Unknown so the\n        # supervisor\'s decision tree can keep its prior heartbeat.\n        for uid in batch:\n    
        if uid not in out:\n                out[uid] = PresenceResult(\n                    user_id=uid, 
presence_type=PresenceType.UNKNOWN,\n                )\n\n    return out\n\n\ndef fetch_presence_one(\n    user_id: 
int | None,\n    *,\n    cookie: str | None = None,\n    refresh: bool = False,\n) -> PresenceResult:\n    
"""Convenience: presence for a single user id.  Returns Unknown if None."""\n    if not user_id or user_id <= 0:\n     
   return PresenceResult(user_id=0, presence_type=PresenceType.UNKNOWN)\n    out = fetch_presence([user_id], 
cookie=cookie, refresh=refresh)\n    return out.get(int(user_id), PresenceResult(\n        user_id=int(user_id), 
presence_type=PresenceType.UNKNOWN,\n    ))\n\n\ndef fetch_presence_for_user_ids(\n    user_ids: Sequence[int],\n    
*,\n    cookie: str | None = None,\n    refresh: bool = False,\n) -> dict[int, PresenceResult]:\n    """Fetch presence 
for a list of user IDs.  Alias for :func:`fetch_presence`.\n\n    Returns a dict ``{user_id: PresenceResult}`` with an 
entry for every\n    requested id (UNKNOWN if the API didn\'t return it).  Never raises.\n    """\n    return 
fetch_presence(user_ids, cookie=cookie, refresh=refresh)\n\n\ndef clear_presence_cache() -> None:\n    """For tests.  
Drops both presence and username caches."""\n    with _PRESENCE_LOCK:\n        _PRESENCE_CACHE.clear()\n    with 
_USERNAME_LOCK:\n        _USERNAME_CACHE.clear()\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Supervisor integration helpers \u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\n\n\ndef classify_presence_result(result: PresenceResult | None) -> str:\n    """Classify a :class:`PresenceResult` 
into a stable internal state string.\n\n    Returns one of:\n      ``"unknown"``           ù no data / not yet 
resolved / UNKNOWN type\n      ``"unavailable"``       ù result is None (API timeout / error)\n      ``"offline"``     
      ù account is Offline or Invisible\n      ``"online_not_in_game"``ù account is in app lobby 
(PresenceType.ONLINE)\n      ``"in_experience"``     ù account is actively in a Roblox experience\n      
``"in_studio"``         ù account is in Roblox Studio (treated like in_experience)\n\n    Never raises.\n    """\n    
if result is None:\n        return "unavailable"\n    try:\n        ptype = result.presence_type\n        if ptype == 
PresenceType.UNKNOWN:\n            return "unknown"\n        if ptype == PresenceType.IN_GAME:\n            return 
"in_experience"\n        if ptype == PresenceType.IN_STUDIO:\n            return "in_studio"\n        if ptype == 
PresenceType.ONLINE:\n            return "online_not_in_game"\n        if ptype in (PresenceType.OFFLINE, 
PresenceType.INVISIBLE):\n            return "offline"\n        return "unknown"\n    except Exception:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n        return "unknown"\n\n\ndef get_presence_state_for_package(package_entry: dict | None) -> str:  # type: 
ignore[type-arg]\n    """Convenience: look up and classify the presence state for one package entry.\n\n    
``package_entry`` is a dict from the config\'s ``roblox_packages`` list.\n    Expected keys:\n      
``account_username``  ù display name / Roblox username\n      ``roblox_user_id``    ù (optional) pre-resolved numeric 
user_id\n\n    Returns one of the :func:`classify_presence_result` state strings.\n    Falls back to ``"unavailable"`` 
for any missing data or network error.\n    Never raises.\n    """\n    if not package_entry or not 
isinstance(package_entry, dict):\n        return "unavailable"\n    try:\n        user_id: int | None = None\n        
raw_uid = package_entry.get("roblox_user_id")\n        if isinstance(raw_uid, int) and raw_uid > 0:\n            
user_id = raw_uid\n        elif isinstance(raw_uid, str) and raw_uid.isdigit():\n            user_id = int(raw_uid)\n  
      if not user_id:\n            username = str(package_entry.get("account_username") or "").strip()\n            if 
username:\n                user_id = lookup_user_id(username)\n        if not user_id:\n            return 
"unavailable"\n        result = fetch_presence_one(user_id)\n        return classify_presence_result(result)\n    
except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return "unavailable"\n\n"""Unified root access helper for DENG Tool: 
Rejoin.\n\nUsed exclusively during package setup / configuration flows.\nNEVER imported or called from the live 
supervisor Start loop.\n\nRoot detection tries these candidates in order:\n  tsu, su, /system/xbin/su, /system/bin/su, 
[magisk su path]\n\nEvery command has a timeout.\nEvery error is caught.\nNo raw traceback in public menu.\nNo hanging 
if su shows a permission prompt.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport shlex\nimport 
subprocess\nimport threading\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom typing import Iterable\n\n_log = 
logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.root_access")\n\n# 
--------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Constants\n# 
--------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\nDETECT_TIMEOUT: int = 5    # seconds 
for root detection test\nCOMMAND_TIMEOUT: int = 10  # default timeout for root commands\nREAD_TIMEOUT: int = 8      # 
timeout for file-read commands\nLIST_TIMEOUT: int = 8      # timeout for glob-list commands\n\n_MAX_DETECT_CACHE_AGE: 
float = 120.0  # 2 minutes\n\n# Root tool candidates in priority order.\n# Absolute paths are tried so the su subshell 
(which may lack Termux PATH)\n# can still find system tools.\n_ROOT_CANDIDATES: tuple[str, ...] = (\n    "tsu",\n    
"su",\n    "/system/xbin/su",\n    "/system/bin/su",\n)\n\n# Magisk-managed su paths (checked only if standard 
candidates fail)\n_MAGISK_SU_PATHS: tuple[str, ...] = (\n    "/sbin/su",\n    "/data/adb/magisk/busybox",\n)\n\n\n# 
--------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Public status labels\n# 
--------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\nclass RootStatus:\n    AVAILABLE   = 
"available"\n    DENIED      = "denied"\n    NOT_FOUND   = "not_found"\n    TIMED_OUT   = "timed_out"\n    ERROR       
= "error"\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Result types\n# 
--------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass 
RootResult:\n    """Result of a single root command execution."""\n    returncode: int\n    stdout: str\n    stderr: 
str\n    timed_out: bool = False\n    error: str = ""\n\n    @property\n    def ok(self) -> bool:\n        return 
self.returncode == 0 and not self.timed_out and not self.error\n\n    @property\n    def summary(self) -> str:\n       
 text = (self.stderr or self.stdout or self.error or "").strip()\n        return 
text[:300]\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass RootCapability:\n    """Cached root detection result."""\n    status: 
str          # one of RootStatus.*\n    tool: str | None     # the working su/tsu command, or None\n    detail: str    
      # human-readable description\n    checked_at: float    # monotonic timestamp\n\n    @property\n    def 
available(self) -> bool:\n        return self.status == RootStatus.AVAILABLE\n\n\n# 
--------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Internal detection cache\n# 
--------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n_cache_lock = 
threading.Lock()\n_cached: RootCapability | None = None\n\n\ndef _run_raw(args: list[str], timeout: int) -> tuple[int, 
str, str, bool]:\n    """Run a subprocess. Returns (returncode, stdout, stderr, timed_out). Never raises."""\n    
try:\n        proc = subprocess.run(\n            args,\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True,\n    
        timeout=timeout,\n        )\n        return proc.returncode, proc.stdout or "", proc.stderr or "", False\n    
except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n        return -1, "", "timed out", True\n    except (FileNotFoundError, 
PermissionError, OSError):\n        return 127, "", "not found", False\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n 
       return -1, "", str(exc)[:200], False\n\n\ndef _probe_tool(tool: str, timeout: int) -> tuple[bool, str, str]:\n  
  """Probe one root tool. Returns (uid0_ok, status_label, detail)."""\n    rc, out, err, timed_out = _run_raw([tool, 
"-c", "id"], timeout=timeout)\n    combined = f"{out} {err}".strip()\n    if timed_out:\n        return False, 
RootStatus.TIMED_OUT, f"{tool}: timed out (no permission prompt accepted)"\n    if rc == 127:\n        return False, 
RootStatus.NOT_FOUND, f"{tool}: not found"\n    if "uid=0" in combined:\n        return True, RootStatus.AVAILABLE, 
combined[:200]\n    # Returned but not uid=0 ù permission denied or restricted\n    return False, RootStatus.DENIED, 
combined[:200]\n\n\ndef _detect_now(timeout: int = DETECT_TIMEOUT) -> RootCapability:\n    """Full root detection. 
Tries all candidates; returns the first available."""\n    now = time.monotonic()\n    all_candidates = 
list(_ROOT_CANDIDATES)\n\n    # Try magisk paths if they look executable\n    import os\n    for mp in 
_MAGISK_SU_PATHS:\n        if os.path.isfile(mp):\n            all_candidates.append(mp)\n\n    last_status = 
RootStatus.NOT_FOUND\n    last_detail = "no root tool found"\n\n    for tool in all_candidates:\n        ok, status, 
detail = _probe_tool(tool, timeout)\n        if ok:\n            return RootCapability(\n                
status=RootStatus.AVAILABLE,\n                tool=tool,\n                detail=f"root via {tool}: {detail[:100]}",\n 
               checked_at=now,\n            )\n        if status == RootStatus.TIMED_OUT:\n            # A timeout 
means su is installed but waiting for permission ù stop trying\n            return RootCapability(\n                
status=RootStatus.TIMED_OUT,\n                tool=tool,\n                detail=detail,\n                
checked_at=now,\n            )\n        if status == RootStatus.DENIED:\n            last_status = RootStatus.DENIED\n 
           last_detail = detail\n            continue\n        # NOT_FOUND ù try next\n\n    return RootCapability(\n  
      status=last_status,\n        tool=None,\n        detail=last_detail,\n        checked_at=now,\n    )\n\n\n# 
--------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Public API\n# 
--------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\ndef detect(*, force: bool = False, 
timeout: int = DETECT_TIMEOUT) -> RootCapability:\n    """Detect root capability. Result is cached for up to 2 
minutes.\n\n    Safe to call from any thread. Never raises.\n    """\n    global _cached\n    now = time.monotonic()\n 
   with _cache_lock:\n        if not force and _cached is not None:\n            if (now - _cached.checked_at) < 
_MAX_DETECT_CACHE_AGE:\n                return _cached\n    try:\n        cap = _detect_now(timeout=timeout)\n    
except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _log.debug("root detect error: %s", exc)\n        cap = 
RootCapability(\n            status=RootStatus.ERROR,\n            tool=None,\n            detail=str(exc)[:200],\n    
        checked_at=now,\n        )\n    with _cache_lock:\n        _cached = cap\n    return cap\n\n\ndef has_root(*, 
force: bool = False) -> bool:\n    """Return True if a working root tool is available. Never raises."""\n    return 
detect(force=force).available\n\n\ndef run_root_command(\n    args: Iterable[str],\n    *,\n    timeout: int = 
COMMAND_TIMEOUT,\n) -> RootResult:\n    """Run a command via the best available root tool.\n\n    Returns a 
:class:`RootResult`.  ``ok`` is False if root is unavailable,\n    the command times out, or any error occurs.  Never 
raises.\n    """\n    cap = detect()\n    if not cap.available or not cap.tool:\n        return RootResult(\n          
  returncode=127,\n            stdout="",\n            stderr="",\n            error=f"root unavailable: 
{cap.detail}",\n        )\n    try:\n        tokens = list(str(a) for a in args)\n        command = 
shlex.join(tokens)\n        rc, out, err, timed_out = _run_raw([cap.tool, "-c", command], timeout=timeout)\n        
return RootResult(\n            returncode=rc,\n            stdout=(out or "").rstrip("\\n"),\n            stderr=(err 
or "").rstrip("\\n"),\n            timed_out=timed_out,\n        )\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n     
   return RootResult(\n            returncode=-1, stdout="", stderr="", error=str(exc)[:200],\n        )\n\n\ndef 
read_root_file(\n    path: str,\n    *,\n    max_bytes: int = 131_072,\n    timeout: int = READ_TIMEOUT,\n) -> str | 
None:\n    """Read a root-protected file.  Returns content (capped) or None.\n\n    Uses ``head -c <max_bytes>`` to 
avoid reading large binaries.\n    Never prints or logs raw content.  Never raises.\n    """\n    if not path or "/" 
not in path:\n        return None\n    try:\n        safe_path = path.replace("\'", "\'\\"\'\\"\'")\n        inner = 
f"test -f \'{safe_path}\' && head -c {int(max_bytes)} \'{safe_path}\' 2>/dev/null"\n        result = 
run_root_command(["sh", "-c", inner], timeout=timeout)\n        if not result.ok or not result.stdout:\n            
return None\n        return result.stdout\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return None\n\n\ndef 
list_root_glob(\n    pattern: str,\n    *,\n    timeout: int = LIST_TIMEOUT,\n    max_results: int = 64,\n) -> 
list[str]:\n    """List files matching a shell glob pattern via root.\n\n    Returns a list of absolute paths.  Never 
raises.  Returns [] on any error.\n    """\n    if not pattern:\n        return []\n    try:\n        safe_pat = 
pattern.replace("\'", "\'\\"\'\\"\'")\n        inner = f"ls {safe_pat} 2>/dev/null | head -{int(max_results)}"\n       
 result = run_root_command(["sh", "-c", inner], timeout=timeout)\n        if not result.ok or not result.stdout:\n     
       return []\n        lines = [ln.strip() for ln in result.stdout.splitlines() if ln.strip()]\n        return 
lines[:max_results]\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return []\n\n\ndef root_status_summary() -> str:\n 
   """Return a short, public-safe status string about root availability.\n\n    Suitable for printing in setup menus. 
Never exposes internal paths or tokens.\n    """\n    cap = detect()\n    if cap.status == RootStatus.AVAILABLE:\n     
   return "Root available"\n    if cap.status == RootStatus.TIMED_OUT:\n        return "Root timed out (permission 
prompt not accepted)"\n    if cap.status == RootStatus.DENIED:\n        return "Root denied (su permission was 
rejected)"\n    if cap.status == RootStatus.NOT_FOUND:\n        return "Root command not found (tsu/su not 
installed)"\n    return "Root not available"\n\n\ndef clear_cache() -> None:\n    """For tests: invalidate the cached 
detection result."""\n    global _cached\n    with _cache_lock:\n        _cached = None\n\n"""Safe HTTPS helper for 
DENG Tool: Rejoin.\n\nOn Termux/Android (TERMUX_VERSION set, or DENG_HTTP_BACKEND=curl):\n    All HTTPS JSON calls run 
through ``curl`` as a subprocess.\n    curl executes in a *separate OS process* ù if OpenSSL inside curl\n    
segfaults (SIGSEGV), only the curl child dies.  The Python main\n    process survives and returns a clean "network 
error" to the caller.\n\nOn other platforms (CI, Windows, macOS, or DENG_HTTP_BACKEND=python):\n    Uses Python\'s 
built-in ``urllib.request`` (no third-party dependency).\n\nBackend selection (in order):\n    1. 
DENG_HTTP_BACKEND=curl   \u2192 always curl\n    2. DENG_HTTP_BACKEND=python \u2192 always urllib\n    3. 
TERMUX_VERSION set       \u2192 curl\n    4. Otherwise                \u2192 urllib\n\nPublic API:\n    post_json(url, 
data, *, headers=None, timeout=30) -> dict\n    get_json(url, *, headers=None, timeout=30)        -> dict\n\nBoth 
raise SafeHttpError subclasses on failure.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport 
logging\nimport os\nimport shutil\nimport subprocess\nimport urllib.error\nimport urllib.request\nfrom typing import 
Any\n\nfrom .constants import VERSION\n\n_log = logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.safe_http")\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Backend 
selection \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n_
CURL_MISSING_MSG = (\n    "curl is required for network access on this device.\\n"\n    "Install it with:  pkg install 
-y curl"\n)\n\n_SHARED_HEADERS: dict[str, str] = {\n    "User-Agent": f"deng-rejoin-installer/{VERSION}",\n    
"Accept": "application/json",\n    "Content-Type": "application/json",\n}\n\n# Maximum response body accepted (256 
KiB) ù prevents OOM from runaway server.\n_MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES = 256 * 1024\n\n\ndef _http_backend() -> str:\n    
"""Return the active HTTP backend identifier: ``\'curl\'`` or ``\'python\'``."""\n    override = 
os.environ.get("DENG_HTTP_BACKEND", "auto").lower().strip()\n    if override == "curl":\n        return "curl"\n    if 
override == "python":\n        return "python"\n    # auto: use curl on Termux to avoid Python ssl/OpenSSL SIGSEGV.\n  
  if os.environ.get("TERMUX_VERSION"):\n        return "curl"\n    return "python"\n\n\ndef _curl_available() -> 
bool:\n    return shutil.which("curl") is not None\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Public exceptions \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\nclass SafeHttpError(Exception):\n    
"""Base class for all safe_http errors."""\n\n\nclass SafeHttpNetworkError(SafeHttpError):\n    """Connection failure, 
timeout, curl unavailable, or child crash."""\n\n\nclass SafeHttpJsonError(SafeHttpError):\n    """Server returned 
non-JSON or malformed JSON."""\n\n\nclass SafeHttpStatusError(SafeHttpError):\n    """Server returned an HTTP error 
status (4xx / 5xx).\n\n    Attributes:\n        status_code: Integer HTTP status code.\n        body:        Raw 
response body (may be empty).\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, status_code: int, body: str = "") -> None:\n        
super().__init__(f"HTTP {status_code}")\n        self.status_code = status_code\n        self.body = body\n\n\n# 
\u2500\u2500 curl backend \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef _build_curl_header_args(headers: dict[str, str]) -> list[str]:\n    
args: list[str] = []\n    for k, v in headers.items():\n        args.extend(["-H", f"{k}: {v}"])\n    return 
args\n\n\ndef _run_curl(\n    args: list[str],\n    *,\n    stdin_bytes: bytes | None = None,\n    timeout: int = 
30,\n) -> tuple[int, bytes]:\n    """Run curl and return (http_status_code, response_body_bytes).\n\n    curl is 
invoked with:\n      -s              silent (no progress bar)\n      -w \'\\\\n%{http_code}\'   append HTTP status 
code on last line\n      --max-time      hard timeout (server response + transfer)\n      --max-filesize  cap download 
size to _MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES\n\n    Raises SafeHttpNetworkError on:\n      - curl not found\n      - curl child process 
exited with signal (SIGSEGV etc.)\n      - process timeout (parent-side Python timeout > curl timeout)\n      - any OS 
error launching the subprocess\n    """\n    if not _curl_available():\n        raise 
SafeHttpNetworkError(_CURL_MISSING_MSG)\n\n    full_cmd = [\n        "curl",\n        "-s",                            
           # silent\n        "--max-time", str(max(5, timeout)),         # hard timeout\n        "--max-filesize", 
str(_MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES), # cap body\n        "-w", "\\n%{http_code}",                     # append status code\n    ] 
+ args\n\n    try:\n        proc = subprocess.run(\n            full_cmd,\n            input=stdin_bytes,\n            
capture_output=True,\n            timeout=timeout + 10,   # python-side timeout > curl --max-time\n            
shell=False,            # never shell=True\n        )\n    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n        raise 
SafeHttpNetworkError("Network request timed out (curl process timeout).") from None\n    except (OSError, 
FileNotFoundError) as exc:\n        raise SafeHttpNetworkError(f"Failed to launch curl: {exc}") from exc\n\n    # 
Negative returncode means the child was killed by a signal (e.g. SIGSEGV).\n    if proc.returncode < 0:\n        sig = 
-proc.returncode\n        _log.debug("curl killed by signal %d", sig)\n        raise SafeHttpNetworkError(\n           
 f"Network check crashed safely (signal {sig}). Please retry."\n        )\n\n    # Non-zero curl exit codes indicate 
network/protocol errors.\n    if proc.returncode != 0:\n        stderr_hint = (proc.stderr or b"").decode("utf-8", 
errors="replace")[:200]\n        curl_errors = {\n            6: "Could not resolve host",\n            7: "Failed to 
connect",\n            22: "HTTP error returned",\n            28: "Connection timed out",\n            35: "SSL 
handshake failed",\n            52: "Empty reply from server",\n            56: "Network data receiving error",\n      
      60: "SSL certificate verification failed",\n        }\n        reason = curl_errors.get(proc.returncode, f"curl 
error {proc.returncode}")\n        _log.debug("curl exit %d: %s | %s", proc.returncode, reason, stderr_hint)\n        
raise SafeHttpNetworkError(f"Network error: {reason}.")\n\n    raw = proc.stdout or b""\n    # Split on LAST newline 
to separate body from the appended status code.\n    last_nl = raw.rfind(b"\\n")\n    if last_nl == -1:\n        
body_bytes = raw\n        status_bytes = b"0"\n    else:\n        body_bytes = raw[:last_nl]\n        status_bytes = 
raw[last_nl + 1:]\n\n    try:\n        http_status = int(status_bytes.strip())\n    except ValueError:\n        
http_status = 0\n\n    return http_status, body_bytes\n\n\ndef _curl_post_json(\n    url: str,\n    data: dict[str, 
Any],\n    *,\n    extra_headers: dict[str, str] | None = None,\n    timeout: int = 30,\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    
headers = {**_SHARED_HEADERS, **(extra_headers or {})}\n    header_args = _build_curl_header_args(headers)\n    
post_args = ["-X", "POST", "--data-binary", "@-"] + header_args + [url]\n\n    json_bytes = json.dumps(data, 
separators=(",", ":")).encode("utf-8")\n    http_status, body_bytes = _run_curl(post_args, stdin_bytes=json_bytes, 
timeout=timeout)\n\n    body_text = body_bytes.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")[:_MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES]\n\n    if 
http_status >= 400:\n        # Try to parse error JSON for useful result/message fields.\n        try:\n            
parsed: dict[str, Any] = json.loads(body_text)\n            if isinstance(parsed, dict) and parsed.get("result"):\n    
            return parsed\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):\n            pass\n        raise 
SafeHttpStatusError(http_status, body_text)\n\n    # 2xx with empty body (e.g. HTTP 204 No Content from Discord 
webhooks).\n    if not body_text.strip():\n        return {}\n\n    try:\n        return json.loads(body_text)  # 
type: ignore[return-value]\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as exc:\n        raise 
SafeHttpJsonError(f"Invalid JSON response: {exc}") from exc\n\n\ndef _curl_get_json(\n    url: str,\n    *,\n    
extra_headers: dict[str, str] | None = None,\n    timeout: int = 30,\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    headers = {k: v for k, 
v in _SHARED_HEADERS.items() if k != "Content-Type"}\n    if extra_headers:\n        headers.update(extra_headers)\n   
 header_args = _build_curl_header_args(headers)\n    get_args = header_args + [url]\n\n    http_status, body_bytes = 
_run_curl(get_args, timeout=timeout)\n    body_text = body_bytes.decode("utf-8", 
errors="replace")[:_MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES]\n\n    if http_status >= 400:\n        try:\n            parsed: dict[str, 
Any] = json.loads(body_text)\n            if isinstance(parsed, dict) and parsed.get("result"):\n                
return parsed\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):\n            pass\n        raise 
SafeHttpStatusError(http_status, body_text)\n\n    if not body_text.strip():\n        return {}\n\n    try:\n        
return json.loads(body_text)  # type: ignore[return-value]\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as exc:\n    
    raise SafeHttpJsonError(f"Invalid JSON response: {exc}") from exc\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Python urllib backend \u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef _python_post_json(\n    url: str,\n 
   data: dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    extra_headers: dict[str, str] | None = None,\n    timeout: int = 30,\n) -> 
dict[str, Any]:\n    headers = {**_SHARED_HEADERS, **(extra_headers or {})}\n    body = json.dumps(data, 
separators=(",", ":")).encode("utf-8")\n    req = urllib.request.Request(url, data=body, headers=headers, 
method="POST")\n    try:\n        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp:  # noqa: S310\n           
 raw = resp.read(_MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES)\n            if not raw.strip():\n                return {}\n            return 
json.loads(raw)  # type: ignore[return-value]\n    except urllib.error.HTTPError as exc:\n        try:\n            
raw = exc.read(4096).decode("utf-8", errors="replace")\n            parsed: dict[str, Any] = json.loads(raw)\n         
   if isinstance(parsed, dict) and parsed.get("result"):\n                return parsed\n        except 
(json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):\n            pass\n        raise SafeHttpStatusError(exc.code, "") from exc\n    
except urllib.error.URLError as exc:\n        raise SafeHttpNetworkError(f"Network error: {exc.reason}") from exc\n    
except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as exc:\n        raise SafeHttpJsonError(f"Invalid JSON response: {exc}") 
from exc\n    except OSError as exc:\n        raise SafeHttpNetworkError(f"Network I/O error: {exc}") from 
exc\n\n\ndef _python_get_json(\n    url: str,\n    *,\n    extra_headers: dict[str, str] | None = None,\n    timeout: 
int = 30,\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    headers = {k: v for k, v in _SHARED_HEADERS.items() if k != "Content-Type"}\n    
if extra_headers:\n        headers.update(extra_headers)\n    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers, 
method="GET")\n    try:\n        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp:  # noqa: S310\n            
raw = resp.read(_MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES)\n            return json.loads(raw)  # type: ignore[return-value]\n    except 
urllib.error.HTTPError as exc:\n        try:\n            raw = exc.read(4096).decode("utf-8", errors="replace")\n     
       parsed: dict[str, Any] = json.loads(raw)\n            if isinstance(parsed, dict) and parsed.get("result"):\n   
             return parsed\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):\n            pass\n        raise 
SafeHttpStatusError(exc.code, "") from exc\n    except urllib.error.URLError as exc:\n        raise 
SafeHttpNetworkError(f"Network error: {exc.reason}") from exc\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as exc:\n 
       raise SafeHttpJsonError(f"Invalid JSON response: {exc}") from exc\n    except OSError as exc:\n        raise 
SafeHttpNetworkError(f"Network I/O error: {exc}") from exc\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Public API \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n
\n\ndef post_json(\n    url: str,\n    data: dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    headers: dict[str, str] | None = None,\n    
timeout: int = 30,\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """POST ``data`` as JSON to ``url``; return parsed JSON response 
dict.\n\n    Selects the backend automatically (curl on Termux, urllib elsewhere).\n    On Termux, a curl SIGSEGV 
cannot kill the Python main process.\n\n    Raises:\n        SafeHttpNetworkError: connection/timeout/curl-crash.\n    
    SafeHttpStatusError:  4xx/5xx HTTP status.\n        SafeHttpJsonError:    malformed JSON from server.\n    """\n   
 backend = _http_backend()\n    _log.debug("safe_http POST %s (backend=%s)", url, backend)\n    if backend == 
"curl":\n        return _curl_post_json(url, data, extra_headers=headers, timeout=timeout)\n    return 
_python_post_json(url, data, extra_headers=headers, timeout=timeout)\n\n\ndef get_json(\n    url: str,\n    *,\n    
headers: dict[str, str] | None = None,\n    timeout: int = 30,\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """GET ``url``; parse and 
return JSON response dict.\n\n    Selects backend automatically.\n\n    Raises:\n        SafeHttpNetworkError: 
connection/timeout/curl-crash.\n        SafeHttpStatusError:  4xx/5xx HTTP status.\n        SafeHttpJsonError:    
malformed JSON from server.\n    """\n    backend = _http_backend()\n    _log.debug("safe_http GET %s (backend=%s)", 
url, backend)\n    if backend == "curl":\n        return _curl_get_json(url, extra_headers=headers, timeout=timeout)\n 
   return _python_get_json(url, extra_headers=headers, timeout=timeout)\n\n"""Safe I/O helpers for DENG Tool: 
Rejoin.\n\nTwo core exports\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\nsafe_prompt()       û Replacement for input() that handles KeyboardInterrupt,\n                      EOFError, 
and (on Termux/Android) bypasses the readline C\n                      extension to prevent segfaults when the 
terminal state is\n                      corrupted (e.g. after screen rotation, ANSI sequences, or\n                   
   a prior ctrl-C inside a readline call).\n\nsetup_faulthandler() û Enable Python faulthandler so that true SIGSEGV 
crashes\n                       (which cannot be caught by try/except) write a C-level\n                       
traceback to a local log file before the process dies.\n                       NEVER writes to stderr ù the crash 
stack must NOT appear\n                       on the user\'s public terminal.\n\nPlatform strategy\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nAndroid / Termux (TERMUX_VERSION 
set):\n    Uses sys.stdin.readline() to bypass the readline C library entirely.\n    This prevents SIGSEGV when 
readline\'s internal state is corrupted.\n\nAll other platforms (Windows, CI, macOS):\n    Uses builtins.input() which 
is compatible with unittest.mock.patch\n    ("builtins.input", ...) patterns used throughout the test suite.\n\nReturn 
value contract\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n  safe_prompt() returns:\n    ò The stripped user input (str, possibly empty when 
allow_blank=True).\n    ò ``default`` when the user pressed Enter with no input, if provided.\n    ò ``None`` on EOF, 
KeyboardInterrupt, or any unrecoverable I/O error.\n      Callers MUST treat None as "cancel / go back to previous 
menu."\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport time\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Platform 
detection \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef 
_on_termux() -> bool:\n    """Return True when running inside Termux on Android."""\n    return 
bool(os.environ.get("TERMUX_VERSION"))\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Public helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef safe_prompt(\n    prompt: str = 
"",\n    *,\n    default: str | None = None,\n    allow_blank: bool = False,\n) -> str | None:\n    """Read one line 
from stdin, handling EOF and KeyboardInterrupt safely.\n\n    On Termux/Android (TERMUX_VERSION env var set), uses 
sys.stdin.readline()\n    to bypass the readline C extension and prevent SIGSEGV crashes.\n\n    On other platforms, 
uses builtins.input() so that test-suite mocks of\n    ``builtins.input`` continue to work without modification.\n\n   
 Args:\n        prompt:      Text to display before the cursor (no trailing newline).\n        default:     Returned 
when the user presses Enter with no input.\n                     Ignored when allow_blank=True.\n        allow_blank: 
When True, an empty response is returned as "" rather\n                     than substituting ``default``.\n\n    
Returns:\n        Stripped string, ``default``, or None (EOF / Ctrl-C / cancel signal).\n    """\n    if 
_on_termux():\n        return _safe_prompt_readline(prompt, default=default, allow_blank=allow_blank)\n    return 
_safe_prompt_input(prompt, default=default, allow_blank=allow_blank)\n\n\ndef _safe_prompt_input(\n    prompt: str,\n  
  *,\n    default: str | None,\n    allow_blank: bool,\n) -> str | None:\n    """input()-based path: compatible with 
builtins.input test mocks."""\n    try:\n        value = input(prompt)\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        try:\n  
          print()\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            pass\n        return None\n    except 
EOFError:\n        return None\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return None\n\n    result = 
value.strip() if isinstance(value, str) else str(value).strip()\n    if not result and not allow_blank and default is 
not None:\n        return default\n    return result\n\n\ndef _safe_prompt_readline(\n    prompt: str,\n    *,\n    
default: str | None,\n    allow_blank: bool,\n) -> str | None:\n    """sys.stdin.readline()-based path: bypasses 
readline C extension on Termux."""\n    try:\n        sys.stdout.write(prompt)\n        sys.stdout.flush()\n    except 
Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n\n    try:\n        line = sys.stdin.readline()\n    except 
KeyboardInterrupt:\n        try:\n            sys.stdout.write("\\n")\n            sys.stdout.flush()\n        except 
Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            pass\n        return None\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 û covers 
EOFError, OSError, etc.\n        return None\n\n    if not line:  # readline() returns "" on end-of-file\n        
return None\n\n    result = line.rstrip("\\n\\r")\n    if not result and not allow_blank and default is not None:\n    
    return default\n    return result\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Press-Enter convenience wrapper \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef 
press_enter(msg: str = "\\nPress Enter to continue...") -> None:\n    """Show ``msg`` and wait; silently returns on 
EOF/Ctrl-C."""\n    safe_prompt(msg)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Crash-log setup \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef setup_faulthandler() -> None:\n    
"""Enable Python faulthandler to log crash tracebacks to a local log file.\n\n    True segfaults (SIGSEGV) cannot be 
caught by try/except.  faulthandler\n    writes a C-level traceback to the crash log *before* the process dies,\n    
which allows post-mortem diagnosis without polluting the user\'s terminal.\n\n    Safety rules:\n        ò The crash 
log only contains Python frame names and line numbers.\n        ò No secrets, keys, or user data are written.\n        
ò The crash stack is NEVER written to stderr or the terminal.\n          If no writable log path can be found, 
faulthandler is simply not\n          enabled (silent failure ù better than spamming the user\'s screen).\n    """\n   
 try:\n        import faulthandler  # noqa: PLC0415\n        from pathlib import Path  # noqa: PLC0415\n\n        from 
.constants import CRASH_LOG_PATH  # noqa: PLC0415\n\n        # Try the preferred path first, then a /tmp fallback.\n   
     candidate_paths = [\n            str(CRASH_LOG_PATH),\n            "/tmp/deng-rejoin-crash.log",\n            
str(Path.home() / ".deng-rejoin-crash.log"),\n        ]\n\n        for crash_path_str in candidate_paths:\n            
try:\n                Path(crash_path_str).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n                # Open in append 
mode ù previous crashes are preserved.\n                # Keep the file open for the lifetime of the process so\n      
          # faulthandler can write to it at any moment, including\n                # during a crash when the GC is no 
longer running.\n                _crash_file = open(  # noqa: SIM115\n                    crash_path_str, "a", 
encoding="utf-8", errors="replace"\n                )\n                # Store reference ù prevents premature GC 
close.\n                _setup_faulthandler._crash_file = _crash_file  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                
faulthandler.enable(file=_crash_file)\n                return  # Successfully enabled; done.\n            except 
Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                continue\n\n        # All paths failed ù do NOT fall back to stderr.\n     
   # Losing crash diagnostics is preferable to printing a giant stack\n        # trace on the user\'s public 
terminal.\n\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass  # faulthandler module unavailable ù ignore 
silently.\n\n\ndef check_and_report_crash_log(max_age_seconds: int = 3600) -> str | None:\n    """Return a 
user-friendly warning string if a recent crash log exists.\n\n    Called at startup to inform the user that a previous 
crash occurred.\n    Returns a one-line message or ``None`` if no recent crash is detected.\n\n    Args:\n        
max_age_seconds: Crash log is "recent" if modified within this window.\n    """\n    try:\n        from .constants 
import CRASH_LOG_PATH  # noqa: PLC0415\n\n        if not CRASH_LOG_PATH.exists():\n            return None\n        
age = time.time() - CRASH_LOG_PATH.stat().st_mtime\n        if age > max_age_seconds:\n            return None\n       
 return (\n            f"Previous crash detected. "\n            f"Crash log saved at: {CRASH_LOG_PATH}\\n"\n          
  f"If this keeps happening, share the log with support."\n        )\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
return None\n\n"""Security utilities for DENG Tool: Rejoin.\n\nProvides:\n  - secure_install_permissions(path)  ù 
chmod 700 dir / 600 files (Unix only)\n  - secure_file_permissions(path)     ù chmod 600 on a single file (Unix 
only)\n  - compute_file_sha256(path)         ù SHA-256 hex digest\n  - verify_sha256(path, expected)     ù True if 
digest matches expected\n  - mask_secret(value, show=4)        ù first N + *** + last N\n\nAll functions are safe to 
call on Windows; chmod operations are silently\nskipped there (Windows ACLs would need to be set via icacls 
instead).\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport hashlib\nimport os\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import 
Path\n\n_IS_UNIX: bool = sys.platform != "win32"\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Permission helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef secure_install_permissions(path: Path 
| str) -> None:\n    """Set secure permissions on an install directory tree.\n\n    Unix:\n      - Directories \u2192 
0o700  (rwx for owner only)\n      - Files       \u2192 0o600  (rw for owner only)\n\n    Windows: no-op; Windows ACLs 
must be managed separately (icacls).\n\n    Errors on individual items are silently ignored so the function never\n    
raises ù best-effort protection is better than a hard crash.\n    """\n    if not _IS_UNIX:\n        return\n    root 
= Path(path)\n    try:\n        if root.is_dir():\n            os.chmod(root, 0o700)\n            for item in 
root.rglob("*"):\n                try:\n                    if item.is_symlink():\n                        continue  # 
Do not follow or chmod symlinks\n                    if item.is_dir():\n                        os.chmod(item, 
0o700)\n                    elif item.is_file():\n                        os.chmod(item, 0o600)\n                
except OSError:\n                    pass\n        elif root.is_file():\n            os.chmod(root, 0o600)\n    except 
OSError:\n        pass\n\n\ndef secure_file_permissions(path: Path | str) -> None:\n    """Set 0o600 on a single file 
(owner read/write only). Unix only."""\n    if not _IS_UNIX:\n        return\n    try:\n        os.chmod(Path(path), 
0o600)\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Hash helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef 
compute_file_sha256(path: Path | str) -> str:\n    """Return the SHA-256 hex digest of a file.\n\n    Reads in 64 KB 
chunks so it works on large packages without loading them\n    entirely into memory.\n\n    Raises:\n        OSError: 
if the file cannot be read.\n    """\n    h = hashlib.sha256()\n    with open(path, "rb") as fh:\n        for chunk in 
iter(lambda: fh.read(65536), b""):\n            h.update(chunk)\n    return h.hexdigest()\n\n\ndef verify_sha256(path: 
Path | str, expected: str) -> bool:\n    """Return True if the file\'s SHA-256 matches *expected* 
(case-insensitive).\n\n    Returns False (not raises) if the file cannot be read or the hash is empty.\n    """\n    
if not expected or not expected.strip():\n        return False\n    try:\n        actual = compute_file_sha256(path)\n 
   except OSError:\n        return False\n    return actual.lower() == expected.strip().lower()\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 
Secret masking \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef mask_secret(value: str, *, show: int = 4) -> str:\n    """Return a display-safe 
version of a secret string.\n\n    Shows up to *show* characters at the start and end; everything in between\n    is 
replaced with ``***``.  If the value is shorter than ``2 ╫ show``\n    characters, the entire string is masked.\n\n    
Examples::\n\n        mask_secret("DENG-ABCDEF012345", show=4)  \u2192 "DENG...2345"\n        mask_secret("short")     
                   \u2192 "***"\n        mask_secret("")                             \u2192 "***"\n    """\n    if not 
value:\n        return "***"\n    if len(value) <= show * 2:\n        return "***"\n    return 
f"{value[:show]}***{value[-show:]}"\n\n"""Optional Android screenshot/snapshot support for webhooks."""\n\nfrom 
__future__ import annotations\n\nimport os\nimport subprocess\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom 
.constants import SNAPSHOT_DIR\n\n\ndef capture_snapshot() -> tuple[Path | None, str]:\n    """Capture a temporary 
Android screenshot if screencap is available."""\n    SNAPSHOT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    path = 
SNAPSHOT_DIR / f"snapshot-{int(time.time())}.png"\n    try:\n        with path.open("wb") as handle:\n            
completed = subprocess.run(\n                ["screencap", "-p"],\n                stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,\n         
       stdout=handle,\n                stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                timeout=10,\n                
shell=False,\n            )\n    except (FileNotFoundError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired, OSError) as exc:\n        
return None, f"screenshot unavailable: {exc}"\n    if completed.returncode != 0 or not path.exists() or 
path.stat().st_size == 0:\n        try:\n            path.unlink()\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            
pass\n        return None, completed.stderr.decode("utf-8", errors="replace") if completed.stderr else "screenshot 
failed"\n    return path, "snapshot captured"\n\n\ndef cleanup_old_snapshots(max_age_seconds: int = 300) -> None:\n    
cutoff = time.time() - max(30, int(max_age_seconds))\n    if not SNAPSHOT_DIR.exists():\n        return\n    for item 
in SNAPSHOT_DIR.glob("snapshot-*.png"):\n        try:\n            if item.stat().st_mtime < cutoff:\n                
item.unlink()\n        except OSError:\n            continue\n\n"""Safe SQLite account detection for Roblox 
packages.\n\nCalled ONLY from Package Setup / Refresh Account Mapping flows.\nNEVER called from Start or the 
supervisor hot loop.\n\nSafety rules enforced:\n- DB files are copied to a DENG-owned temp path via root before 
reading.\n- The copied DB is opened read-only (uri=True with ?mode=ro).\n- The temp copy is deleted after the scan, 
even on error.\n- Max DB file size: 8 MB (configurable).\n- Max DB files scanned per package: 5.\n- Max tables 
inspected: 20 per DB.\n- Max rows read per table: 200.\n- WAL/SHM companion files are ignored.\n- No raw row data is 
logged or printed.\n- Forbidden path/table/column names are skipped silently.\n- Corrupt or unreadable DBs are skipped 
(no crash).\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport os\nimport sqlite3\nimport 
tempfile\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom . import 
root_access\n\n_log = logging.getLogger("deng_tool_rejoin")\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Safety constants \u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nMAX_DB_BYTES: int = 8 * 1024 
* 1024   # 8 MB\nMAX_DB_FILES: int = 5                  # per package\nMAX_TABLES: int = 20                   # per 
DB\nMAX_ROWS: int = 200                    # per table\nMIN_ROBLOX_USER_ID: int = 1\nMAX_ROBLOX_USER_ID: int = 
9_999_999_999\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Forbidden identifiers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\n\nFORBIDDEN_PATH_MARKERS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({\n    "cookie", "cookies", "webview", 
"browser",\n    "token", "auth", "session", "password", "credential",\n    "refresh", "bearer", "jwt", "csrf", 
"secret", "roblosecurity",\n})\n\nFORBIDDEN_TABLE_MARKERS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({\n    "cookie", "cookies", 
"webview", "browser", "token",\n    "auth", "session", "password", "credential", "secret",\n    "roblosecurity", 
"refresh", "bearer", "jwt", "csrf",\n    "cache",  # cookie/session cache tables\n})\n\nFORBIDDEN_COLUMN_MARKERS: 
frozenset[str] = frozenset({\n    "cookie", "token", "password", "secret", "roblosecurity",\n    "credential", "auth", 
"bearer", "jwt", "csrf", "refresh",\n    "session", "webview", "browser",\n})\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Allowed columns \
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nALLOWE
D_USERID_COLUMNS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({\n    "userId", "user_id", "robloxUserId", "roblox_user_id",\n    
"authenticatedUserId", "authenticated_user_id",\n    "currentUserId", "current_user_id",\n    "loggedInUserId", 
"logged_in_user_id",\n    "activeUserId", "active_user_id",\n    "accountUserId", "account_user_id",\n    
"playerUserId", "player_user_id",\n})\n# Lower-case lookup set for case-insensitive matching\n_ALLOWED_USERID_LOWER: 
frozenset[str] = frozenset(c.lower() for c in ALLOWED_USERID_COLUMNS)\n\nALLOWED_USERNAME_COLUMNS: frozenset[str] = 
frozenset({\n    "username", "user_name", "accountName", "account_name",\n    "displayName", "display_name",\n    
"robloxUsername", "roblox_username",\n    "currentUsername", "current_username",\n    "loggedInUsername", 
"logged_in_username",\n})\n_ALLOWED_USERNAME_LOWER: frozenset[str] = frozenset(c.lower() for c in 
ALLOWED_USERNAME_COLUMNS)\n\n# display_name columns ù treated as hint only (Needs 
Confirm)\n_DISPLAY_NAME_COLUMNS_LOWER: frozenset[str] = frozenset({\n    "displayname", "display_name",\n})\n\n\n# 
\u2500\u2500\u2500 Result type \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n@dataclass\nclass SQLiteAccountResult:\n    """Result of scanning a 
package\'s SQLite databases."""\n    user_id: int = 0\n    username: str = ""\n    is_display_name_only: bool = 
False\n    source: str = "not_found"\n    db_path: str = ""\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\n\ndef _has_forbidden_marker(name: str, markers: frozenset[str]) -> bool:\n    low = name.lower()\n    
return any(m in low for m in markers)\n\n\ndef _is_safe_db_path(path: str) -> bool:\n    """Return True if the path 
itself doesn\'t contain forbidden keywords."""\n    basename = os.path.basename(path).lower()\n    dirname = 
os.path.dirname(path).lower()\n    for marker in FORBIDDEN_PATH_MARKERS:\n        if marker in basename or marker in 
dirname:\n            return False\n    return True\n\n\ndef _is_valid_user_id(value: Any) -> bool:\n    """Return 
True for a plain positive integer in the Roblox user ID range."""\n    if isinstance(value, int):\n        return 
MIN_ROBLOX_USER_ID <= value <= MAX_ROBLOX_USER_ID\n    if isinstance(value, str):\n        s = value.strip()\n        
if not s.isdigit():\n            return False\n        return _is_valid_user_id(int(s))\n    return False\n\n\ndef 
_is_email_like(value: str) -> bool:\n    return "@" in value and "." in value.split("@")[-1]\n\n\ndef 
_is_valid_username(value: str) -> bool:\n    """Return True for a plausible Roblox username (3-20 alphanumeric + 
underscore)."""\n    import re\n    value = value.strip()\n    if not (3 <= len(value) <= 20):\n        return False\n 
   if _is_email_like(value):\n        return False\n    return bool(re.match(r"^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$", value))\n\n\ndef 
_is_valid_display_name(value: str) -> bool:\n    """Return True for a plausible Roblox display name (1-20 printable 
chars, no email)."""\n    value = value.strip()\n    if not (1 <= len(value) <= 20):\n        return False\n    if 
_is_email_like(value):\n        return False\n    # Must be printable and not contain obvious non-human tokens\n    
return all(c.isprintable() for c in value)\n\n\ndef _copy_db_via_root(db_path: str, *, timeout: int = 8) -> str | 
None:\n    """Copy a protected DB to a temp file via root. Returns temp path or None on failure."""\n    try:\n        
tmp = tempfile.mktemp(suffix=".db", prefix="deng_sqlite_")\n        result = root_access.run_root_command(\n           
 ["cp", db_path, tmp], timeout=timeout\n        )\n        if result.returncode != 0 or not os.path.isfile(tmp):\n     
       return None\n        # Ensure we own the copy and it\'s not too large\n        size = os.path.getsize(tmp)\n    
    if size > MAX_DB_BYTES:\n            _log.debug("sqlite_detect: DB too large (%d bytes), skipping %s", size, 
db_path)\n            os.unlink(tmp)\n            return None\n        return tmp\n    except Exception as exc:  # 
noqa: BLE001\n        _log.debug("sqlite_detect: copy failed for %s: %s", db_path, exc)\n        return None\n\n\ndef 
_scan_db_file(tmp_path: str, original_path: str) -> SQLiteAccountResult:\n    """Scan a single copied DB for account 
identifiers. Returns best result found."""\n    best = SQLiteAccountResult()\n    try:\n        uri = 
f"file:{tmp_path}?mode=ro"\n        conn = sqlite3.connect(uri, uri=True, timeout=3.0)\n        try:\n            cur 
= conn.cursor()\n            # Get table list\n            cur.execute("SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE 
type=\'table\' LIMIT ?", (MAX_TABLES + 5,))\n            tables = [row[0] for row in cur.fetchall() if 
isinstance(row[0], str)]\n\n            tables_scanned = 0\n            for table_name in tables:\n                if 
tables_scanned >= MAX_TABLES:\n                    break\n                if _has_forbidden_marker(table_name, 
FORBIDDEN_TABLE_MARKERS):\n                    continue\n                tables_scanned += 1\n\n                # Get 
column info\n                try:\n                    cur.execute(f"PRAGMA table_info(\'{table_name}\')")  # noqa: 
S608\n                    cols_info = cur.fetchall()\n                except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n              
      continue\n\n                # Identify allowed columns\n                uid_cols: list[str] = []\n               
 uname_cols: list[str] = []\n                for col in cols_info:\n                    cname = str(col[1]) if 
len(col) > 1 else ""\n                    if not cname:\n                        continue\n                    # Check 
type ù skip blob columns regardless of name\n                    ctype = str(col[2]).upper() if len(col) > 2 else ""\n 
                   if "BLOB" in ctype:\n                        continue\n                    clow = cname.lower()\n   
                 # Explicitly allowlisted columns bypass the forbidden marker check.\n                    # This 
handles names like "authenticatedUserId" which contain "auth"\n                    # but are defined as safe public 
numeric-ID columns.\n                    if clow in _ALLOWED_USERID_LOWER:\n                        
uid_cols.append(cname)\n                    elif clow in _ALLOWED_USERNAME_LOWER:\n                        
uname_cols.append(cname)\n                    elif _has_forbidden_marker(cname, FORBIDDEN_COLUMN_MARKERS):\n           
             # Not in any allowlist AND contains a forbidden marker \u2192 skip\n                        continue\n\n  
              if not uid_cols and not uname_cols:\n                    continue\n\n                # Query only the 
columns we identified\n                select_cols = uid_cols + uname_cols\n                safe_cols = ", 
".join(f\'"{c}"\' for c in select_cols)\n                try:\n                    cur.execute(\n                      
  f\'SELECT {safe_cols} FROM "{table_name}" LIMIT {MAX_ROWS}\'  # noqa: S608\n                    )\n                  
  rows = cur.fetchall()\n                except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                    continue\n\n           
     for row in rows:\n                    values = dict(zip(select_cols, row))\n                    # Try userId 
columns first\n                    for ucol in uid_cols:\n                        val = values.get(ucol)\n             
           if val is not None and _is_valid_user_id(val):\n                            uid = int(val) if 
isinstance(val, str) else val\n                            best.user_id = uid\n                            best.source 
= "sqlite_user_id"\n                            best.db_path = original_path\n                            # Also grab 
username from same row if available\n                            for nc in uname_cols:\n                               
 nv = str(values.get(nc) or "").strip()\n                                nlow = nc.lower()\n                           
     if nv and _is_valid_username(nv) and nlow not in _DISPLAY_NAME_COLUMNS_LOWER:\n                                   
 best.username = nv\n                                    break\n                            return best  # userId 
found \u2192 done\n\n                    # No userId ù try username columns\n                    for nc in 
uname_cols:\n                        nv = str(values.get(nc) or "").strip()\n                        if not nv:\n      
                      continue\n                        nlow = nc.lower()\n                        if nlow in 
_DISPLAY_NAME_COLUMNS_LOWER:\n                            # Display name is a hint only\n                            
if not best.username and _is_valid_display_name(nv):\n                                best.username = nv\n             
                   best.is_display_name_only = True\n                                best.source = 
"sqlite_display_name_hint"\n                                best.db_path = original_path\n                        elif 
_is_valid_username(nv) and not best.user_id:\n                            best.username = nv\n                         
   best.is_display_name_only = False\n                            best.source = "sqlite_username"\n                    
        best.db_path = original_path\n        finally:\n            conn.close()\n    except (sqlite3.DatabaseError, 
sqlite3.OperationalError) as exc:\n        _log.debug("sqlite_detect: DB parse error for %s: %s", original_path, 
exc)\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _log.debug("sqlite_detect: unexpected error for %s: %s", 
original_path, exc)\n    return best\n\n\ndef scan_package_dbs(\n    package: str,\n    *,\n    max_bytes: int = 
MAX_DB_BYTES,\n    max_files: int = MAX_DB_FILES,\n    scan_timeout: int = 10,\n) -> SQLiteAccountResult:\n    """Scan 
SQLite databases under a package\'s data directory for account info.\n\n    Safety:\n    - Only runs if root is 
available.\n    - Copies each DB to temp before reading.\n    - Deletes temp copies after scan.\n    - Never called 
from Start/supervisor hot loop.\n\n    Returns the best :class:`SQLiteAccountResult` found (user_id preferred\n    
over username, which is preferred over display_name hint).\n    """\n    if not root_access.has_root():\n        
_log.debug("sqlite_detect: root not available, skipping for %s", package)\n        return SQLiteAccountResult()\n\n    
data_dir = f"/data/data/{package}"\n    # List .db files only (exclude .db-wal, .db-shm)\n    try:\n        paths = 
root_access.list_root_glob(\n            f"{data_dir}/**/*.db",\n            timeout=scan_timeout,\n            
max_results=max_files * 4,  # over-fetch then filter\n        )\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
_log.debug("sqlite_detect: glob failed for %s: %s", package, exc)\n        return SQLiteAccountResult()\n\n    # 
Filter: only plain .db files, no WAL/SHM, no forbidden paths\n    safe_paths: list[str] = []\n    for p in paths:\n    
    if not p.endswith(".db"):\n            continue\n        if not _is_safe_db_path(p):\n            continue\n       
 safe_paths.append(p)\n        if len(safe_paths) >= max_files:\n            break\n\n    best = 
SQLiteAccountResult()\n\n    for db_path in safe_paths:\n        tmp_path: str | None = None\n        try:\n           
 tmp_path = _copy_db_via_root(db_path, timeout=scan_timeout)\n            if not tmp_path:\n                continue\n 
           result = _scan_db_file(tmp_path, db_path)\n            # Rank: user_id > username > display_name_hint > 
nothing\n            if result.user_id > 0:\n                return result  # immediate best\n            if 
result.username and not result.is_display_name_only and not best.username:\n                best = result\n            
elif result.username and result.is_display_name_only and not best.username:\n                best = result\n        
except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            _log.debug("sqlite_detect: error processing %s: %s", db_path, 
exc)\n        finally:\n            if tmp_path and os.path.isfile(tmp_path):\n                try:\n                  
  os.unlink(tmp_path)\n                except OSError:\n                    pass\n\n    return best\n\n"""Auto rejoin 
supervisor loop."""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport signal\nimport shlex\nimport threading\nimport 
time\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom . import android, db\nfrom .backoff import calculate_backoff_seconds\nfrom 
.config import effective_private_server_url, load_config, validate_config\nfrom .launcher import 
launch_package_for_current_config, perform_rejoin\nfrom .lockfile import LockManager\nfrom .logger import 
configure_logging, log_event\nfrom .monitor import check_package_health, check_roblox_health\nfrom .roblox_health 
import categorize_unhealthy\n\n\ndef _reapply_layout_for_package(package: str) -> None:\n    """Best-effort layout 
re-apply for ONE package during recovery.\n\n    Computes the package\'s slot in the current display layout, writes 
the\n    XML keys + Set-enable booleans, AND triggers a direct (post-launch)\n    resize via root so the relaunched 
window really lands at the desired\n    bounds.  Never raises.  All details go to the file logger.\n    """\n    
try:\n        from . import window_layout\n        from . import window_apply\n        display = 
window_layout.detect_display_info()\n        # We don\'t know the full selected-package set here; compute a 
1-package\n        # layout fallback that uses the right-pane rules.  This keeps the\n        # window 
landscape-shaped on its own; the next full Start cycle will\n        # rebalance for multi-package layouts.\n        
rects = window_layout.calculate_split_layout(\n            [package], display.width, display.height,\n        )\n      
  if rects:\n            window_apply.apply_window_layout_silent(\n                rects, force_stop_before=False, 
verify_after=False, retries=0,\n            )\n            # Layer 3: direct resize via root, with freeform mode 
flip.\n            # This is what actually moves the visible window without\n            # waiting for the next 
force-stop / relaunch cycle.\n            try:\n                ok, detail = 
window_apply.force_resize_package(package, rects[0])\n                import logging as _logging\n                
_logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.supervisor").debug(\n                    "force_resize_package(%s) ok=%s detail=%s",\n 
                   package, ok, detail,\n                )\n            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n     
           import logging as _logging\n                _logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.supervisor").debug(\n           
         "force_resize_package(%s) error: %s", package, exc,\n                )\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n        import logging as _logging\n        _logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.supervisor").debug(\n            
"reapply_layout_for_package(%s) error: %s", package, exc,\n        )\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Status constants (shown 
in terminal and webhook) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nSTATUS_ONLINE            = "Online"\nSTATUS_OFFLINE        
   = "Offline"\nSTATUS_DEAD              = "Dead"              # Process confirmed gone (force-closed / 
crashed)\nSTATUS_LAUNCHING         = "Launching"\nSTATUS_CHECKING          = "Preparing"\nSTATUS_BACKGROUND        = 
"Background"\nSTATUS_RECONNECTING      = "Reconnecting"\nSTATUS_WARNING           = "Warning"\nSTATUS_FAILED           
 = "Failed"\nSTATUS_UNKNOWN           = "Unknown"\n# Richer state constants for improved UX\nSTATUS_LOBBY             
= "Lobby"             # App open at home/lobby, no URL join active\nSTATUS_IN_SERVER         = "In Server"         # 
Strong evidence: game experience loaded\nSTATUS_JOINING           = "Join" + "ing"      # DEPRECATED ù kept for 
backward compat only.\n                                               # WatchdogSupervisor never produces this 
state.\n                                               # Use STATUS_LAUNCHING for post-launch 
transient.\nSTATUS_CLOSED            = "Closed"            # App cleanly not running after a 
session\nSTATUS_JOIN_UNCONFIRMED  = "Join " + "Unconfirmed"  # App healthy but no in-game evidence yet\n# State 
vocabulary aligned to user-facing terminology:\nSTATUS_LAUNCHED          = "Launched"\nSTATUS_DISCONNECTED      = 
"Disconnected"\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Live watchdog vocabulary (WatchdogSupervisor) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\n# These three states are the ONLY public steady states produced by WatchdogSupervisor:\n#   Online        ù 
process running and in-game\n#   No Heartbeat  ù process running but NOT playing (lobby, stuck, frozen, no 
heartbeat)\n#   Dead          ù process not running\n# "In-Lobby" is removed: running-but-not-in-game now maps 
directly to No Heartbeat.\nSTATUS_NO_HEARTBEAT  = "No Heartbeat"  # Process running but not actively playing\n\n# All 
healthy states ù used for legacy _PackageWorker state-machine guards.\n# WatchdogSupervisor never reads 
_HEALTHY_STATES; only _PackageWorker uses it.\n# STATUS_LOBBY stays in _HEALTHY_STATES for _PackageWorker backward 
compat.\n# WatchdogSupervisor never produces Lobby; its display map maps Lobby \u2192 No Heartbeat.\n_HEALTHY_STATES = 
frozenset({\n    STATUS_ONLINE, STATUS_LAUNCHED,\n    # Legacy aliases for _PackageWorker only; never produced by 
WatchdogSupervisor.\n    STATUS_LOBBY, STATUS_IN_SERVER, STATUS_JOIN_UNCONFIRMED,\n})\n\n# Legacy re-launch threshold. 
 With the Kaeru-style _post_launch_state\n# returning Online immediately, this state is no longer entered on normal 
launches.\n# The large value is a safety net for any legacy path that might still set 
it.\n_JOIN_UNCONFIRMED_RELAUNCH_SECONDS = 3600  # 1 hour (effectively disabled)\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Presence-based 
controlled relaunch constants \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n# Relaunch from Presence signal 
requires ALL conditions to be met:\n# - process is alive (running=True)\n# - mapped userId exists\n# - Presence API is 
reachable (not returning unknown/error)\n# - Presence repeatedly reports offline/not-in-experience within the 
window\n# - cooldown has passed since last presence-triggered relaunch\n# - hourly cap not exceeded\n# - only the 
affected package is relaunched (no cross-package cascade)\nPRESENCE_SUSPICIOUS_CONFIRMATIONS: int = 3       # repeated 
"offline" hits needed\nPRESENCE_SUSPICIOUS_WINDOW_SECONDS: int = 180    # within this time 
window\nPRESENCE_RELAUNCH_COOLDOWN_SECONDS: int = 600    # min gap between presence 
relaunches\nPRESENCE_RELAUNCH_MAX_PER_HOUR: int = 2          # hard cap per package per hour\n\n\nclass Supervisor:\n  
  """State-machine based local supervisor (single-package, legacy)."""\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        
self.stop_event = threading.Event()\n        self.failure_count = 0\n        self.unhealthy_since: float | None = 
None\n\n    def _handle_stop(self, signum, frame) -> None:  # noqa: ANN001 - signal handler signature.\n        
self.stop_event.set()\n\n    def _sleep(self, seconds: int | float) -> None:\n        deadline = time.time() + max(1, 
float(seconds))\n        while not self.stop_event.is_set() and time.time() < deadline:\n            
time.sleep(min(1.0, deadline - time.time()))\n\n    def run_forever(self) -> None:\n        
signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self._handle_stop)\n        signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self._handle_stop)\n\n        
cfg = validate_config(load_config())\n        logger = configure_logging(level=cfg.get("log_level", "INFO"))\n        
with LockManager():\n            log_event(logger, "info", "agent_started")\n            db.insert_event("INFO", 
"agent_started", "auto supervisor started")\n            while not self.stop_event.is_set():\n                cfg = 
validate_config(load_config())\n                interval = int(cfg["health_check_interval_seconds"])\n\n               
 if not cfg.get("auto_rejoin_enabled"):\n                    db.insert_heartbeat("disabled", {"auto_rejoin_enabled": 
False})\n                    log_event(logger, "info", "heartbeat", status="disabled")\n                    
self._sleep(interval)\n                    continue\n\n                db.insert_heartbeat("checking_environment", 
{"package": cfg["roblox_package"]})\n                health = check_roblox_health(cfg)\n\n                if 
health.state == "healthy":\n                    self.failure_count = 0\n                    self.unhealthy_since = 
None\n                    db.insert_heartbeat("healthy", health.meta)\n                    log_event(logger, "info", 
"heartbeat", status="healthy", package=cfg["roblox_package"])\n                    self._sleep(interval)\n             
       continue\n\n                if health.state in {"network_down", "roblox_not_installed"}:\n                    
db.insert_heartbeat(health.state, health.meta)\n                    log_event(logger, "warning", health.state, 
message=health.message, package=cfg["roblox_package"])\n                    self._sleep(interval)\n                    
continue\n\n                if health.state == "roblox_not_running":\n                    now = time.time()\n          
          if self.unhealthy_since is None:\n                        self.unhealthy_since = now\n                    
elapsed = now - self.unhealthy_since\n                    grace = int(cfg["foreground_grace_seconds"])\n               
     if elapsed < grace:\n                        db.insert_heartbeat("roblox_not_running", {**health.meta, 
"grace_remaining_seconds": int(grace - elapsed)})\n                        log_event(logger, "warning", 
"roblox_not_running", message=health.message, grace_remaining_seconds=int(grace - elapsed))\n                        
self._sleep(min(interval, max(5, grace - elapsed)))\n                        continue\n\n                    
db.insert_heartbeat("launching", health.meta)\n                    result = perform_rejoin(cfg, reason=health.state)\n 
                   if result.success:\n                        self.failure_count = 0\n                        
self.unhealthy_since = None\n                        db.insert_heartbeat("waiting_after_launch", {"package": 
cfg["roblox_package"]})\n                        self._sleep(max(int(cfg["reconnect_delay_seconds"]), interval))\n     
               else:\n                        self.failure_count += 1\n                        fast_failures = 
int(cfg["max_fast_failures"])\n                        backoff_index = max(1, self.failure_count - fast_failures + 
1)\n                        delay = calculate_backoff_seconds(backoff_index, cfg["backoff_min_seconds"], 
cfg["backoff_max_seconds"])\n                        db.insert_heartbeat("backoff", {"failure_count": 
self.failure_count, "sleep_seconds": delay})\n                        log_event(logger, "error", "backoff", 
failure_count=self.failure_count, sleep_seconds=delay, error=result.error or "")\n                        
self._sleep(delay)\n                    continue\n\n                db.insert_heartbeat("error", {"state": 
health.state, "message": health.message})\n                log_event(logger, "error", "unknown_health_state", 
state=health.state, message=health.message)\n                self._sleep(interval)\n\n            
db.insert_heartbeat("disabled", {"stop_requested": True})\n            db.insert_event("INFO", "agent_stopped", "auto 
supervisor stopped")\n            log_event(logger, "info", "agent_stopped")\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Per-package 
worker \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\nclass 
_PackageWorker(threading.Thread):\n    """Background thread that monitors and auto-revives one Roblox package."""\n\n  
  def __init__(\n        self,\n        entry: dict[str, Any],\n        cfg: dict[str, Any],\n        status_map: 
dict[str, str],\n        stop_event: threading.Event,\n        on_status_change: Any = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        
package = str(entry.get("package") or "")\n        super().__init__(name=f"worker-{package}", daemon=True)\n        
self.entry = dict(entry)\n        self.package = package\n        self.cfg = cfg\n        self.status_map = 
status_map\n        self.stop_event = stop_event\n        self.on_status_change = on_status_change\n        
self.failure_count = 0\n        self.unhealthy_since: float | None = None\n        self.revive_count: int = 0       # 
total per-package relaunches (Kaeru: restart_count)\n        self.last_error: str | None = None\n        
self.online_since: float | None = None\n        self.last_seen_at: float | None = None       # Kaeru: 
last_seen_alive_at\n        self.last_launch_at: float | None = None     # Kaeru: last_launch_at\n        
self.last_foreground_at: float | None = None # Kaeru: last_foreground_at\n        self.desired_url: str = ""           
        # Kaeru: desired_url (canonical launch URL)\n        self.grace_start: float | None = None\n        
self.bg_since: float | None = None\n        self._restart_times: list[float] = []\n        self._last_detail: str = 
""\n        self.logger = configure_logging(level=cfg.get("log_level", "INFO"))\n        # URL-aware state tracking\n  
      self.has_private_url: bool = False   # set at start of run()\n        self._url_launched: bool = False     # was 
the LAST launch done with a URL?\n        self.launching_since: float | None = None\n        self.last_url_launch_at: 
float = 0.0\n        self.url_launch_grace_until: float = 0.0\n        self.join_unconfirmed_since: float | None = 
None  # for URL re-launch timeout\n        # Heartbeat-based playing-state tracker.  Lazily imported so the\n        # 
supervisor still works in test environments that don\'t have the\n        # full agent package installed.\n        
try:\n            from .playing_state import StateTracker\n            self._state_tracker = StateTracker(\n           
     offline_grace_s=float(cfg.get("offline_grace_seconds", 60.0)),\n                stale_task_grace_s=20.0,\n        
        join_unconfirmed_grace_s=25.0,\n            )\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            
self._state_tracker = None\n        self._last_evidence: dict[str, Any] = {}\n        
self._consecutive_offline_checks: int = 0\n        # Roblox presence ù ground truth via the public Roblox API.\n       
 self._roblox_user_id: int | None = None\n        self._roblox_username: str = ""\n        self._roblox_cookie: str | 
None = None\n        self._presence_resolved: bool = False  # username \u2192 id resolved yet?\n        
self._last_presence_label: str = ""    # for diagnostic logging\n        # Kaeru-style extended tracking (stable 
rebuild probe p-9e3f2a8d1c)\n        self.last_presence_check_at: float | None = None  # when presence was last 
fetched\n        self.last_presence_state: str = "unknown"         # classify_presence_result() output\n        
self.hourly_restart_count: int = 0                # restarts in last 60 minutes\n        self.failed_reason: str = ""  
                    # set when Failed state is entered\n        # Presence-based controlled relaunch tracking\n
