self._presence_suspicious_count: int = 0          # consecutive offline results\n        
self._presence_suspicious_window_start: float = 0 # when window opened\n        self._last_presence_relaunch_at: float 
= 0        # last presence-triggered relaunch\n        self._presence_relaunch_times: list[float] = []   # for hourly 
cap\n\n    def _sup(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        raw = self.cfg.get("supervisor")\n        return raw if 
isinstance(raw, dict) else {}\n\n    def _set_status(self, status: str, detail: str = "") -> None:\n        old = 
self.status_map.get(self.package)\n        self.status_map[self.package] = status\n        self._last_detail = detail 
or self._last_detail\n        if old != status and callable(self.on_status_change):\n            
self.on_status_change(self.package, status)\n\n    def _sleep(self, seconds: float) -> None:\n        deadline = 
time.time() + max(0.5, float(seconds))\n        while not self.stop_event.is_set() and time.time() < deadline:\n       
     time.sleep(min(1.0, deadline - time.time()))\n\n    def _restart_budget_ok(self) -> bool:\n        now = 
time.time()\n        self._restart_times = [t for t in self._restart_times if now - t < 3600]\n        cap = 
int(self._sup().get("max_restart_attempts_per_hour", 10))\n        return len(self._restart_times) < cap\n\n    def 
_record_restart(self) -> None:\n        now = time.time()\n        self._restart_times.append(now)\n        
self.hourly_restart_count = len([t for t in self._restart_times if now - t < 3600])\n\n    def _can_auto_reopen(self) 
-> bool:\n        return bool(self.entry.get("auto_reopen_enabled", True)) and 
bool(self._sup().get("auto_reopen_enabled", True))\n\n    def _can_auto_reconnect(self) -> bool:\n        return 
bool(self.entry.get("auto_reconnect_enabled", True)) and bool(self._sup().get("auto_reconnect_enabled", True))\n\n    
def _url_grace_seconds(self, base_grace: int | float) -> float:\n        return float(max(90, int(float(base_grace)) * 
4))\n\n    def _note_url_launch_grace(\n        self,\n        reason: str,\n        *,\n        now: float | None = 
None,\n        grace_seconds: int | float | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        if not self.has_private_url:\n       
     return\n        ts = time.time() if now is None else float(now)\n        seconds = float(grace_seconds if 
grace_seconds is not None else self._url_grace_seconds(30))\n        self.last_url_launch_at = ts\n        
self.url_launch_grace_until = max(self.url_launch_grace_until, ts + seconds)\n        self._url_launched = True\n      
  log_event(\n            self.logger,\n            "info",\n            "[DENG_REJOIN_SUPERVISOR_GRACE]",\n           
 package=self.package,\n            grace_reason=reason,\n            grace_until=int(self.url_launch_grace_until),\n  
          current_state=self.status_map.get(self.package, ""),\n            relaunch_blocked="true",\n        )\n\n    
def _url_launch_grace_active(self, now: float | None = None) -> bool:\n        if not self.has_private_url:\n          
  return False\n        ts = time.time() if now is None else float(now)\n        return 
bool(self.url_launch_grace_until and ts < self.url_launch_grace_until)\n\n    def _relaunch_allowed(self, reason: str, 
now: float | None = None) -> bool:\n        ts = time.time() if now is None else float(now)\n        blocked = 
self._url_launch_grace_active(ts)\n        elapsed_ms = -1\n        if self.last_url_launch_at:\n            
elapsed_ms = int(max(0.0, ts - self.last_url_launch_at) * 1000)\n        log_event(\n            self.logger,\n        
    "info",\n            "[DENG_REJOIN_RELAUNCH_DECISION]",\n            package=self.package,\n            
reason=reason,\n            post_launch_action=str(self.cfg.get("post_launch_action") or ""),\n            
url_launch_recent=str(blocked).lower(),\n            elapsed_since_url_launch_ms=elapsed_ms,\n            
allowed=str(not blocked).lower(),\n        )\n        return not blocked\n\n    def 
_presence_relaunch_cooldown_ok(self) -> bool:\n        """Return True if enough time has passed since the last 
presence-triggered relaunch."""\n        if not self._last_presence_relaunch_at:\n            return True\n        
return (time.time() - self._last_presence_relaunch_at) >= PRESENCE_RELAUNCH_COOLDOWN_SECONDS\n\n    def 
_presence_relaunch_budget_ok(self) -> bool:\n        """Return True if the per-hour presence-relaunch cap has not been 
reached."""\n        now = time.time()\n        self._presence_relaunch_times = [\n            t for t in 
self._presence_relaunch_times if now - t < 3600\n        ]\n        return len(self._presence_relaunch_times) < 
PRESENCE_RELAUNCH_MAX_PER_HOUR\n\n    def _should_relaunch_from_presence(self, running: bool) -> bool:\n        
"""Return True only if all conditions for a presence-based relaunch are met.\n\n        Conditions (all required):\n   
     - process is alive (running=True)\n        - userId is configured for this package\n        - presence suspicious 
count reached threshold within window\n        - cooldown since last presence relaunch\n        - hourly cap not 
exceeded\n        - normal restart budget also has room\n        """\n        if not running:\n            return 
False\n        if not self._roblox_user_id:\n            return False\n        now = time.time()\n        # Window 
expired ù reset counter\n        if (now - self._presence_suspicious_window_start) > 
PRESENCE_SUSPICIOUS_WINDOW_SECONDS:\n            return False\n        if self._presence_suspicious_count < 
PRESENCE_SUSPICIOUS_CONFIRMATIONS:\n            return False\n        if not self._presence_relaunch_cooldown_ok():\n  
          return False\n        if not self._presence_relaunch_budget_ok():\n            return False\n        if not 
self._restart_budget_ok():\n            return False\n        return True\n\n    def _record_presence_relaunch(self) 
-> None:\n        now = time.time()\n        self._last_presence_relaunch_at = now\n        
self._presence_relaunch_times.append(now)\n        self._presence_suspicious_count = 0\n        
self._presence_suspicious_window_start = 0\n\n    def _fetch_roblox_presence(self) -> Any:\n        """Return a 
:class:`PresenceResult` for the configured account, or None.\n\n        Resolves username \u2192 userId once (cached), 
then asks Roblox\'s public\n        presence endpoint.  Never raises.  Returns None when no username /\n        no 
user_id is configured or the API was unreachable AND we have no\n        cached presence ù in that case the supervisor 
falls back to its\n        local heuristics (process-alive check).\n\n        Always updates 
``last_presence_check_at`` and ``last_presence_state``\n        so the supervisor can expose them for diagnostics 
without blocking.\n        """\n        try:\n            from . import roblox_presence as _rp\n        except 
Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            self.last_presence_state = "unavailable"\n            return None\n        
try:\n            if not self._roblox_user_id:\n                if not self._roblox_username:\n                    
self.last_presence_state = "unavailable"\n                    return None\n                uid = 
_rp.lookup_user_id(self._roblox_username)\n                if uid:\n                    self._roblox_user_id = uid\n   
         if not self._roblox_user_id:\n                self.last_presence_state = "unavailable"\n                
return None\n            pres = _rp.fetch_presence_one(\n                self._roblox_user_id, 
cookie=self._roblox_cookie,\n            )\n            self.last_presence_check_at = time.time()\n            # 
Classify and store the presence state using the module helper.\n            classified = 
_rp.classify_presence_result(pres)\n            self.last_presence_state = classified\n            if pres is None or 
pres.is_unknown:\n                return None\n            return pres\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n            self.last_presence_state = "unavailable"\n            log_event(\n                self.logger, 
"debug", "roblox_presence_error",\n                package=self.package, error=str(exc),\n            )\n            
return None\n\n    def _post_launch_state(self) -> str:\n        """Process confirmed alive after launch \u2192 
Online.\n\n        Kaeru-style supervision (probe p-f1a4aaafe5): if the process is running,\n        it is Online.  We 
no longer distinguish "in game" vs "lobby" via\n        logcat/dumpsys because:\n          1. The uiautomator probe 
caused SIGSEGV on Termux with App Cloner packages.\n          2. Lobby detection caused an endless private-link 
re-send loop even\n             when the user was actually playing (URL joined correctly).\n          3. Kaeru works 
correctly with process-alive = Online logic.\n\n        Relaunch only triggers when the process is completely dead 
(not running),\n        not when it "might be at the lobby screen."\n        """\n        log_event(\n            
self.logger, "info", "post_launch_online",\n            package=self.package,\n            
url_launched=self._url_launched,\n        )\n        return STATUS_ONLINE\n\n    def run(self) -> None:\n        # All 
worker setup wrapped: a thrown exception here would silently\n        # kill this worker thread and the package would 
never reconnect.\n        try:\n            cfg = self.cfg\n            sup = self._sup()\n            interval = 
int(sup.get("health_check_interval_seconds") or cfg.get("health_check_interval_seconds", 30))\n            grace = 
int(sup.get("launch_grace_seconds") or cfg.get("foreground_grace_seconds", 30))\n            backoff_base = 
int(sup.get("restart_backoff_seconds", 10))\n            # Determine URL awareness once at startup (Kaeru: 
desired_url)\n            from .config import effective_private_server_url as _epsu\n            self.desired_url = 
str(_epsu(self.entry, cfg) or "").strip()\n            self.has_private_url = bool(self.desired_url)\n            # 
Launch timeout: how many seconds before forcing a re-check\n            _launching_timeout = max(90, grace * 4)\n      
      if self.has_private_url and self.status_map.get(self.package) in {STATUS_LAUNCHING, STATUS_JOINING}:\n           
     now_launch = time.time()\n                if self.launching_since is None:\n                    
self.launching_since = now_launch\n                self._note_url_launch_grace(\n                    
"configured_link_launch",\n                    now=now_launch,\n                    
grace_seconds=_launching_timeout,\n                )\n\n            # Roblox presence setup ù read username + optional 
user_id/cookie\n            # from the per-package entry, then resolve via the public API.\n            
self._roblox_username = str(self.entry.get("account_username") or "").strip()\n            try:\n                
uid_raw = self.entry.get("roblox_user_id")\n                if isinstance(uid_raw, int) and uid_raw > 0:\n             
       self._roblox_user_id = uid_raw\n                elif isinstance(uid_raw, str) and uid_raw.isdigit():\n          
          self._roblox_user_id = int(uid_raw)\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                
self._roblox_user_id = None\n            self._roblox_cookie = (str(self.entry.get("roblox_cookie") or "").strip() or 
None)\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            log_event(self.logger, "error", 
"worker_setup_error", package=self.package, error=str(exc))\n            # Defaults so the loop still runs forever\n   
         cfg = self.cfg\n            sup = self._sup()\n            interval = 30\n            grace = 30\n            
backoff_base = 10\n            self.has_private_url = False\n            _launching_timeout = 120\n\n        while not 
self.stop_event.is_set():\n            try:\n                if not sup.get("enabled", True):\n                    
self._set_status(STATUS_OFFLINE, "supervisor disabled")\n                    db.insert_heartbeat("disabled", 
{"package": self.package})\n                    self._sleep(interval)\n                    continue\n\n                
# \u2500\u2500 Launching timeout guard \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\n                # STATUS_JOINING is no longer set by live paths (Kaeru-style).\n                # Only 
STATUS_LAUNCHING is used post-relaunch.\n                current_st = self.status_map.get(self.package, "")\n          
      if self.launching_since is not None and current_st in {STATUS_LAUNCHING, STATUS_JOINING}:\n                    
elapsed_since_launch = time.time() - self.launching_since\n                    if elapsed_since_launch > 
_launching_timeout:\n                        # App hasn\'t become healthy after 4╫ grace ù force-check now\n           
             timeout_health = check_package_health(cfg, self.package)\n                        if timeout_health.state 
== "healthy":\n                            target = self._post_launch_state()\n                            
self._set_status(target, "Launch timeout ù app confirmed running")\n                        else:\n                    
        self.failed_reason = "Launch timeout ù app not responding"\n                            
self._set_status(STATUS_FAILED, self.failed_reason)\n                        self.launching_since = None\n             
           # Skip normal health check this iteration ù we just ran one\n                        self._sleep(min(5, 
interval))\n                        continue\n\n                # Save state before we run the slow probes.  We do 
NOT\n                # overwrite the public status with STATUS_CHECKING here ù\n                # doing so makes the 
row visibly flap to "Preparing" on\n                # every tick and looks broken to the user.  Only show\n            
    # Preparing when we genuinely have no idea yet (first\n                # iteration before any state was 
established).\n                prev_before_check = self.status_map.get(self.package, "")\n                if not 
prev_before_check or prev_before_check in (\n                    STATUS_UNKNOWN, "",\n                ):\n             
       self._set_status(STATUS_CHECKING, "")\n                health = check_package_health(cfg, self.package)\n\n     
           # Feed the heartbeat tracker.  Use the full evidence dict so\n                # decide() can see process / 
window / surface / foreground\n                # signals independently ù not just the collapsed `running`\n            
    # field that older code paths used.\n                _meta = health.meta if isinstance(health.meta, dict) else 
{}\n                evidence_now = {\n                    "running":      bool(_meta.get("running")),\n                
    "root_running": bool(_meta.get("root_running")),\n                    "task":         bool(_meta.get("task")),\n   
                 "window":       bool(_meta.get("window")),\n                    "surface":      
bool(_meta.get("surface")),\n                    "foreground":   bool(_meta.get("fg_evidence")),\n                }\n  
              self._last_evidence = evidence_now\n                if self._state_tracker is not None:\n                
    try:\n                        self._state_tracker.observe(self.package, evidence_now)\n                    except 
Exception as _exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n                        log_event(\n                            self.logger, 
"debug", "state_tracker_observe_error",\n                            package=self.package, error=str(_exc),\n          
              )\n\n                # \u2500\u2500 Roblox presence (ground truth) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n                
# Ask Roblox directly: is the configured account InGame /\n                # Online / Offline / InStudio?  When the 
public presence\n                # endpoint says InGame, the supervisor uses that as the\n                # 
*authoritative* state regardless of local dumpsys output.\n                # This is what fixes the "stuck in 
Preparing" symptom: even\n                # when our local checks are slow or wrong, the truthful\n                # 
state still surfaces every interval.\n                presence = self._fetch_roblox_presence()\n                if 
presence is not None:\n                    if presence.is_in_game:\n                        self._set_status(\n        
                    STATUS_ONLINE,\n                            f"Roblox presence: InGame 
({presence.last_location[:40]})",\n                        )\n                        self.failure_count = 0\n         
               self.unhealthy_since = None\n                        self.grace_start = None\n                        
self.bg_since = None\n                        self.launching_since = None\n                        self.online_since = 
self.online_since or time.time()\n                        self.last_seen_at = time.time()\n                        
self._last_presence_label = presence.presence_type.label\n                        # In-game confirms the account is 
active ù clear suspicious counter\n                        self._presence_suspicious_count = 0\n                       
 self._presence_suspicious_window_start = 0\n                        self._sleep(interval)\n                        
continue\n                    # Online (lobby) ù Kaeru-style: record internally but do NOT\n                    # set 
legacy transient or Lobby as public state.  The process-alive\n                    # check below will determine the 
public Online/Reopening/Failed.\n                    # This eliminates the old transient loop caused by presence\n     
               # lobby detection triggering the 120s URL resend timer.\n                    if presence.is_lobby:\n    
                    self._last_presence_label = presence.presence_type.label\n                        
self.last_seen_at = time.time()\n                        # Fall through to process health check (no continue).\n       
             if presence.is_offline:\n                        # Authoritative Offline.  Accumulate the suspicious 
counter.\n                        # We do NOT immediately relaunch ù that would recreate the\n                        
# old transient loop.  Controlled relaunch only happens after\n                        # 
PRESENCE_SUSPICIOUS_CONFIRMATIONS hits within the window,\n                        # cooldown passed, and hourly cap 
respected.\n                        self._last_presence_label = presence.presence_type.label\n                        
now_p = time.time()\n                        if (now_p - self._presence_suspicious_window_start) > 
PRESENCE_SUSPICIOUS_WINDOW_SECONDS:\n                            # Start a fresh window\n                            
self._presence_suspicious_count = 1\n                            self._presence_suspicious_window_start = now_p\n      
                  else:\n                            self._presence_suspicious_count += 1\n                        # 
Check if threshold is met for a controlled presence relaunch.\n                        # Only trigger when process 
appears alive (avoid double-relaunch\n                        # with the normal dead-process path below).\n            
            _cur_running = bool((health.meta or {}).get("running") if isinstance(health.meta, dict) else False)\n      
                  if self._should_relaunch_from_presence(_cur_running) and self._can_auto_reconnect():\n               
             if not self._relaunch_allowed("presence_offline_controlled", now_p):\n                                
self._set_status(STATUS_LAUNCHING, "Waiting For Roblox Link")\n                                self._sleep(min(5, 
interval))\n                                continue\n                            log_event(\n                         
       self.logger, "info", "presence_controlled_relaunch",\n                                package=self.package,\n   
                             suspicious_count=self._presence_suspicious_count,\n                                
user_id=self._roblox_user_id,\n                            )\n                            
self._record_presence_relaunch()\n                            _reapply_layout_for_package(self.package)\n              
              _pkg_cfg = dict(cfg)\n                            _pkg_cfg["roblox_package"] = self.package\n            
                _pr = perform_rejoin(\n                                _pkg_cfg, 
reason="presence_offline_controlled",\n                                package_entry=self.entry, no_force_stop=True,\n 
                           )\n                            self._record_restart()\n                            if 
_pr.success:\n                                self.launching_since = time.time()\n                                
self._note_url_launch_grace(\n                                    "configured_link_launch",\n                          
          now=self.launching_since,\n                                    grace_seconds=_launching_timeout,\n           
                     )\n                                self._set_status(STATUS_LAUNCHING, "Presence offline ù 
controlled relaunch")\n                                _reapply_layout_for_package(self.package)\n                     
       else:\n                                self._set_status(STATUS_WARNING, "Presence relaunch failed")\n           
                 self._sleep(max(int(cfg.get("reconnect_delay_seconds", 8)), interval))\n                            
continue\n                        # Fall through so the existing "no evidence" path\n                        # decides 
between Reconnecting / Offline.\n                    # InStudio / Invisible / Unknown \u2192 fall through (reset 
suspicious on in_game handled above).\n\n                if health.state == "healthy":\n                    
self.failure_count = 0\n                    self.unhealthy_since = None\n                    self.grace_start = None\n 
                   self.bg_since = None\n                    now_ts = time.time()\n                    if 
self.online_since is None:\n                        self.online_since = now_ts\n                    self.last_seen_at 
= now_ts   # Kaeru: last_seen_alive_at\n                    # Track foreground evidence (Kaeru: last_foreground_at)\n  
                  _meta_fg = (health.meta or {}) if isinstance(health.meta, dict) else {}\n                    if 
_meta_fg.get("fg_evidence") or _meta_fg.get("foreground"):\n                        self.last_foreground_at = now_ts\n 
                   # Promote from Launching to an appropriate healthy state.\n                    # Use 
prev_before_check because STATUS_CHECKING is transient.\n                    self.launching_since = None\n             
       if prev_before_check in {STATUS_LAUNCHING, STATUS_JOINING}:\n                        # Kaeru-style: process 
alive = Online (stable rebuild p-9e3f2a8d1c).\n                        # STATUS_JOINING is a legacy alias; live paths 
now use STATUS_LAUNCHING.\n                        target = self._post_launch_state()\n                    elif 
prev_before_check in _HEALTHY_STATES:\n                        target = prev_before_check  # stay in current healthy 
state\n                    else:\n                        target = STATUS_ONLINE\n                    
self._set_status(target, "Heartbeat OK")\n                    db.insert_heartbeat("healthy", {"package": 
self.package})\n                    log_event(self.logger, "info", "heartbeat", status="healthy", 
package=self.package)\n\n                    # \u2500\u2500 Private-link timeout: re-launch with URL 
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n                    # If Roblox 
has been healthy but stuck without in-game proof\n                    # for too long (share code expired, lobby 
landed, auth\n                    # needed), re-send the private URL to put the user back in\n                    # 
the server.  Only triggers when a URL was launched AND\n                    # the package has been in this state past 
the timeout.\n                    current_after = self.status_map.get(self.package, "")\n                    if (\n    
                    current_after == STATUS_JOIN_UNCONFIRMED\n                        and self.has_private_url\n       
                 and self._can_auto_reconnect()\n                        and self._restart_budget_ok()\n               
     ):\n                        if self.join_unconfirmed_since is None:\n

self._set_status(STATUS_UNKNOWN, str(exc))\n                self._sleep(interval)\n\n\nclass MultiPackageSupervisor:\n 
   """Supervisor that monitors multiple Roblox packages independently."""\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        
entries: list[dict[str, Any]],\n        cfg: dict[str, Any],\n        *,\n        initial_status: dict[str, str] | 
None = None,\n        on_status_change: Any = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self.entries = list(entries)\n        
self.packages = [str(e["package"]) for e in self.entries]\n        self.cfg = cfg\n        self.stop_event = 
threading.Event()\n        self.status_map: dict[str, str] = {pkg: STATUS_LAUNCHING for pkg in self.packages}\n        
if initial_status:\n            self.status_map.update(initial_status)\n        self.on_status_change = 
on_status_change\n        self._workers: list[_PackageWorker] = []\n\n    def _handle_stop(self, signum, frame) -> 
None:  # noqa: ANN001\n        # Silent stop ù no public print.  All shutdown details go to the log.\n        try:\n   
         import logging as _logging\n            _logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.supervisor").debug(\n                
"Supervisor received signal %s, stopping cleanly.", signum,\n            )\n        except Exception:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n            pass\n        self.stop_event.set()\n\n    def run_forever(self, *, display_interval: float = 
10.0, render_callback=None) -> None:\n        """Run until stopped.\n\n        Args:\n            display_interval: 
seconds between live status refreshes.\n            render_callback: optional callable invoked every 
``display_interval``\n                seconds instead of the default ``_print_live_status`` one-liner.\n               
 Use this to inject a full clear+banner+table dashboard renderer.\n        """\n        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, 
self._handle_stop)\n        signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self._handle_stop)\n\n        logger = 
configure_logging(level=self.cfg.get("log_level", "INFO"))\n        log_event(logger, "info", 
"multi_supervisor_started", packages=self.packages)\n        db.insert_event(\n            "INFO",\n            
"multi_supervisor_started",\n            f"monitoring {len(self.packages)} packages: {\', \'.join(self.packages)}",\n  
      )\n\n        for entry in self.entries:\n            worker = _PackageWorker(\n                entry,\n          
      self.cfg,\n                self.status_map,\n                self.stop_event,\n                
self.on_status_change,\n            )\n            worker.start()\n            self._workers.append(worker)\n\n        
try:\n            while not self.stop_event.is_set():\n                
self.stop_event.wait(timeout=display_interval)\n                if not self.stop_event.is_set():\n                    
if render_callback is not None:\n                        try:\n                            render_callback()\n         
               except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                            self._print_live_status()\n              
      else:\n                        self._print_live_status()\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            
pass\n\n        self.stop_event.set()\n        for worker in self._workers:\n            if worker.is_alive():\n       
         worker.join(timeout=5)\n\n        db.insert_event("INFO", "multi_supervisor_stopped", "session ended by 
user")\n        log_event(logger, "info", "multi_supervisor_stopped")\n\n    def get_status_snapshot(self, entries: 
list[dict] | None = None) -> list[dict]:\n        entry_map: dict[str, str] = {}\n        use = entries or 
self.entries\n        for e in use:\n            pkg = str(e.get("package") or "")\n            username = 
str(e.get("account_username") or "").strip()\n            entry_map[pkg] = username if username else "Unknown"\n\n     
   snapshot: list[dict] = []\n        worker_map = {w.package: w for w in self._workers}\n        for pkg in 
self.packages:\n            status = self.status_map.get(pkg, STATUS_OFFLINE)\n            worker = 
worker_map.get(pkg)\n            snapshot.append(\n                {\n                    "package": pkg,\n            
        "username": entry_map.get(pkg, ""),\n                    "status": status,\n                    
"revive_count": worker.revive_count if worker else 0,\n                    "failure_count": worker.failure_count if 
worker else 0,\n                    "last_error": worker.last_error if worker else None,\n                    
"online_since": worker.online_since if worker else None,\n                    "last_seen_at": worker.last_seen_at if 
worker else None,\n                }\n            )\n        return snapshot\n\n    def _print_live_status(self) -> 
None:\n        parts = []\n        for pkg in self.packages:\n            status = self.status_map.get(pkg, 
STATUS_OFFLINE)\n            short_pkg = pkg.split(".")[-1][:12]\n            parts.append(f"{short_pkg}:{status}")\n  
      print("  [Monitor] " + "  ".join(parts), flush=True)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 WatchdogSupervisor \u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\nclass WatchdogSupervisor:\n   
 """Continuous sequential watchdog with deterministic live detection.\n\n    States produced (public):\n        Online 
       ù Process running, in game, healthy heartbeat/presence.\n        No Heartbeat  ù Process running, but not 
playing normally, stalled, or offline signal.\n        Dead          ù Process not running (force-closed, crashed, 
stopped).\n        Launching     ù Transient: initial launch sent, waiting for first state detection.\n\n    Key 
design guarantees (vs old _PackageWorker):\n    - Process check runs FIRST before any presence check.\n      
Force-closing a package is always detected in the next round regardless of\n      what the Roblox Presence API last 
reported.\n    - Sequential loop: checks packages one by one, never stops after Online.\n    - "Checking Package X/Y" 
updated on each package check (stored in\n      self.checking_label, read by the dashboard callback).\n    - The 
removed join-pending state is NEVER produced.  All post-launch transients use Launching.\n    - Blank 
private_server_url = app-only launch (no setup-required error).\n\n    [DENG_REJOIN_WATCHDOG_FIX] 
probe_id=p-ea167faf5f\n    Root cause of old bug: _PackageWorker used StateTracker.decide() which\n    returned 
"Recovering" for 60s after force-close (offset_grace_s).  That\n    reset grace_start to None each iteration, blocking 
Dead detection for 60s+.\n    Additionally, presence.is_in_game short-circuited process checks so force-\n    close 
was invisible until the Presence API updated (30-60s delay), then\n    needed 3 consecutive "offline" confirmations 
before triggering relaunch.\n    Fix: process check FIRST, presence supplementary. Sequential loop avoids\n    
thread-per-package complexity and gives deterministic X/Y progress lines.\n    """\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 Grace window 
after launch ù do not check immediately 
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    
DEFAULT_GRACE_SECONDS: int = 45\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 No-Heartbeat relaunch cooldown ù brief pause before force-stop 
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    # Prevents instant force-stop/relaunch loops if the heartbeat check 
fires\n    # repeatedly in quick succession.  Set to 0 to disable.\n    NHB_RELAUNCH_COOLDOWN_SEC: int = 5\n\n    # 
\u2500\u2500 Presence offline confirmation counter retained for probe history \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    
NHB_OFFLINE_CONFIRMATIONS: int = 2\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        entries: list[dict[str, Any]],\n       
 cfg: dict[str, Any],\n        *,\n        initial_status: dict[str, str] | None = None,\n        on_status_change: 
Any = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self.entries = list(entries)\n        self.packages = [str(e["package"]) for e in 
self.entries]\n        self.entry_by_pkg: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {\n            str(e["package"]): e for e in 
self.entries\n        }\n        self.cfg = cfg\n        self.stop_event = threading.Event()\n\n        # Public 
status dict ù mutated in-place (read by dashboard callback).\n        self.status_map: dict[str, str] = {\n            
pkg: STATUS_LAUNCHING for pkg in self.packages\n        }\n        # Normalize initial_status: remove legacy/transient 
labels.\n        # "In-Lobby" is gone ù running-but-not-in-game is No Heartbeat.\n        # Any lobby-like state from 
old sessions also maps to No Heartbeat.\n        if initial_status:\n            _legacy_to_launching = {\n            
    STATUS_JOINING, STATUS_JOIN_UNCONFIRMED, "Join Failed", "Reconnecting",\n            }\n            
_legacy_to_no_heartbeat = {\n                "In-Lobby", "Lobby",   # old states: running but not in game\n            
}\n            for pkg, st in initial_status.items():\n                if pkg in self.status_map:\n                    
if st in _legacy_to_no_heartbeat:\n                        self.status_map[pkg] = STATUS_NO_HEARTBEAT\n                
    elif st in _legacy_to_launching:\n                        self.status_map[pkg] = STATUS_LAUNCHING\n                
    else:\n                        self.status_map[pkg] = st\n\n        self.on_status_change = on_status_change\n\n   
     # Dashboard: "Checking Package X/Y" ù updated by inner loop, read by callback.\n        self.checking_label: str 
= f"Checking Package 0/{len(self.packages)}" if self.packages else "Checking Package 0/0"\n\n        self._round: int 
= 0\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 Per-package mutable tracking \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        self._prev_state: dict[str, str] = {}\n        
self._last_online_ts: dict[str, float] = {}   # last time confirmed Online\n        self._online_start_ts: dict[str, 
float] = {}  # when package first became Online (for Runtime display)\n        self._grace_until: dict[str, float] = 
{}      # no relaunch until this ts\n        self._nhb_offline_count: dict[str, int] = {}  # consecutive offline 
hits\n        self._nhb_cooldown_until: dict[str, float] = {}  # no NHB relaunch until this ts\n        
self._revive_count: dict[str, int] = {}\n        self._failure_count: dict[str, int] = {}\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 
Per-package presence tracking \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\n        self._presence_user_ids: dict[str, int] = {}\n        self._presence_usernames: dict[str, str] 
= {}\n        self._presence_cookies: dict[str, str | None] = {}\n        self._presence_id_resolved: set[str] = set() 
 # username\u2192id done\n        self._presence_lookup_attempt_at: dict[str, float] = {}\n        
self._presence_last_detail: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}\n        self._root_info = android.detect_root()\n\n        
for e in self.entries:\n            pkg = str(e["package"])\n            try:\n                uid_raw = 
e.get("roblox_user_id")\n                if uid_raw:\n                    self._presence_user_ids[pkg] = 
int(uid_raw)\n            except (ValueError, TypeError):\n                pass\n            uname = 
str(e.get("account_username") or "").strip()\n            if uname:\n                self._presence_usernames[pkg] = 
uname\n            self._presence_cookies[pkg] = (\n                str(e.get("roblox_cookie") or "").strip() or 
None\n            )\n\n        self._logger = configure_logging(level=cfg.get("log_level", "INFO"))\n        
log_event(\n            self._logger, "info", "[DENG_REJOIN_SEGFAULT_FIX]",\n            probe_id="p-79933739d8",\n    
        segfault_source="python_ssl_urllib_presence_api",\n            disabled_path="roblox_presence._post_json 
urllib_ssl",\n            replacement_path="safe_http.post_json curl_on_termux",\n            
live_start_safe="true",\n            joining_removed="true",\n            uiautomator_live="false",\n            
logcat_live="false",\n            xml_dump_live="false",\n        )\n\n    # \u2500\u2500\u2500 Internal helpers \u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n    def _handle_stop(self, signum: Any, 
frame: Any) -> None:\n        self.stop_event.set()\n\n    def _set_status(self, pkg: str, status: str) -> None:\n     
   old = self.status_map.get(pkg)\n        self.status_map[pkg] = status\n        if old != status and 
callable(self.on_status_change):\n            self.on_status_change(pkg, status)\n\n    def _set_grace(self, pkg: str, 
now: float, seconds: int | None = None) -> None:\n        self._grace_until[pkg] = now + float(seconds or 
self.DEFAULT_GRACE_SECONDS)\n\n    def _in_grace(self, pkg: str, now: float) -> bool:\n        return now < 
self._grace_until.get(pkg, 0.0)\n\n    def _sup_interval(self) -> int:\n        sup = self.cfg.get("supervisor") if 
isinstance(self.cfg.get("supervisor"), dict) else {}\n        raw = sup.get("health_check_interval_seconds") or 
self.cfg.get("health_check_interval_seconds", 30)\n        return max(10, int(raw))\n\n    def 
_fast_alive_evidence(self, pkg: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        """Bounded package evidence for the live 
watchdog.\n\n        Probe p-0899246178 showed the watchdog getting stuck after\n        
``[DENG_REJOIN_PACKAGE_CHECK_START]`` for package 1.  The old path used\n        the full 
``android.get_package_alive_evidence`` helper, which can stack\n        repeated process scans before the loop ever 
reaches package 2.  Start\n        needs a short, deterministic per-package read: cached root process\n        proof 
first, then lightweight foreground / visual hints.\n        """\n        dead: dict[str, Any] = {\n            
"running": False,\n            "task": False,\n            "window": False,\n            "root_running": False,\n      
      "surface": False,\n            "foreground": False,\n            "foreground_package": "",\n            "alive": 
False,\n            "strict_alive": False,\n        }\n        try:\n            package = 
android.validate_package_name(pkg)\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            return dead\n\n        
running = False\n        root_running = False\n        root_tool = getattr(self._root_info, "tool", None)\n        if 
getattr(self._root_info, "available", False) and root_tool:\n            try:\n                res = 
android.run_root_command(\n                    ["pidof", package],\n                    root_tool=root_tool,\n         
           timeout=2,\n                )\n                root_running = bool(res.ok and res.stdout.strip())\n         
   except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                root_running = False\n            if not root_running:\n         
       try:\n                    qpkg = shlex.quote(package)\n                    script = (\n                        
"for p in /proc/[0-9]*/cmdline; do "\n                        "tr \'\\\\0\' \' \' < \\"$p\\" 2>/dev/null | "\n         
               f"grep -F -q -- {qpkg} && echo hit && exit 0; "\n                        "done; exit 1"\n               
     )\n                    res = android.run_root_command(\n                        ["sh", "-c", script],\n           
             root_tool=root_tool,\n                        timeout=3,\n                    )\n                    
root_running = bool(res.ok and "hit" in (res.stdout or ""))\n                except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n       
             root_running = False\n        else:\n            try:\n                res = 
android.run_command(["pidof", package], timeout=2)\n                running = bool(res.ok and res.stdout.strip())\n    
        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                running = False\n\n        foreground_package = ""\n        
try:\n            foreground_package = android.current_foreground_package() or ""\n        except Exception:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n            foreground_package = ""\n        foreground = bool(\n            foreground_package\n            
and (foreground_package == package or package in foreground_package)\n        )\n\n        need_visual = foreground or 
not (running or root_running)\n        window = False\n        surface = False\n        if need_visual:\n            
try:\n                window = bool(android.is_package_window_visible(package))\n            except Exception:  # 
noqa: BLE001\n                window = False\n            try:\n                surface = 
bool(android.is_package_surface_in_surfaceflinger(package))\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n           
     surface = False\n\n        strict_alive = bool(running or root_running or window or surface or foreground)\n      
  return {\n            "running": running,\n            "task": False,\n            "window": window,\n            
"root_running": root_running,\n            "surface": surface,\n            "foreground": foreground,\n            
"foreground_package": foreground_package,\n            "alive": strict_alive,\n            "strict_alive": 
strict_alive,\n        }\n\n    # \u2500\u2500\u2500 Presence fetching (process-check-first; presence is 
supplementary) \u2500\u2500\n\n    def _fetch_presence(self, pkg: str) -> Any:\n        """Fetch Roblox presence for 
the package\'s account.  Never raises.\n\n        Returns a PresenceResult or None.  This is always called AFTER\n     
   process-alive check so that a dead process (force-closed) is caught\n        by step A and presence is never 
consulted for that case.\n        """\n        detail: dict[str, Any] = {\n            "roblox_api_used": "false",\n   
         "roblox_api_status": "skipped",\n            "roblox_user_id": self._presence_user_ids.get(pkg, 0),\n         
   "roblox_user_id_source": "config" if self._presence_user_ids.get(pkg) else "",\n            "roblox_presence_type": 
"",\n            "roblox_place_id": "",\n            "roblox_universe_id": "",\n            "presence_error": "",\n    
    }\n        self._presence_last_detail[pkg] = detail\n        try:\n            from . import roblox_presence as 
_rp\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            detail["roblox_api_status"] = "failed"\n            
detail["presence_error"] = f"import_failed:{exc}"[:160]\n            return None\n        try:\n            uid = 
int(self._presence_user_ids.get(pkg) or 0)\n\n            # Resolve from rooted per-clone Roblox prefs before username 
lookup.\n            # This handles cloned packages whose configured display label is not\n            # the exact 
Roblox username, and avoids using one account for every\n            # clone.  The Android helper extracts numeric ids 
only.\n            if uid <= 0 and pkg not in self._presence_id_resolved:\n                pref_uid = 
android.discover_roblox_user_id_from_prefs(pkg, timeout=3)\n                if pref_uid:\n                    uid = 
int(pref_uid)\n                    self._presence_user_ids[pkg] = uid\n                    
detail["roblox_user_id_source"] = "prefs"\n\n            # Resolve username \u2192 user_id (cached), but never 
permanently give\n            # up after one transient network miss.  Retry at a modest cadence.\n            if uid 
<= 0:\n                uname = self._presence_usernames.get(pkg, "")\n                last_attempt = 
self._presence_lookup_attempt_at.get(pkg, 0.0)\n                should_try = bool(uname) and (time.monotonic() - 
last_attempt) >= 30.0\n                if should_try:\n                    self._presence_lookup_attempt_at[pkg] = 
time.monotonic()\n                    uid_lookup = _rp.lookup_user_id(uname)\n                    if uid_lookup:\n     
                   uid = int(uid_lookup)\n                        self._presence_user_ids[pkg] = uid\n                 
       detail["roblox_user_id_source"] = "username"\n\n            if uid > 0:\n                
self._presence_id_resolved.add(pkg)\n                detail["roblox_user_id"] = uid\n            elif not 
self._presence_usernames.get(pkg):\n                self._presence_id_resolved.add(pkg)\n\n            if not uid:\n   
             detail["roblox_api_status"] = "skipped"\n                detail["presence_error"] = "missing_user_id"\n   
             return None\n            detail["roblox_api_used"] = "true"\n            presence = 
_rp.fetch_presence_one(uid, cookie=self._presence_cookies.get(pkg))\n            ptype = getattr(presence, 
"presence_type", None)\n            detail["roblox_api_status"] = "success"\n            
detail["roblox_presence_type"] = getattr(ptype, "name", str(ptype))\n            detail["roblox_place_id"] = 
getattr(presence, "place_id", "") or ""\n            detail["roblox_universe_id"] = getattr(presence, "root_place_id", 
"") or ""\n            return presence\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            
detail["roblox_api_used"] = "true" if detail.get("roblox_user_id") else "false"\n            
detail["roblox_api_status"] = "failed"\n            detail["presence_error"] = str(exc)[:160]\n            return 
None\n\n    # \u2500\u2500\u2500 State detection \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\n\n    def _detect_package_state(\n        self, pkg: str, entry: dict[str, Any]\n    ) -> tuple[str, 
dict[str, Any]]:\n        """Detect public state for one package.\n\n        Decision tree (deterministic):\n        
A. Process not running                              \u2192 Dead\n        B. Process running + presence InGame          
      \u2192 Online\n        C. Process running + was recently Online\n           + presence offline N times 
consecutively         \u2192 No Heartbeat\n        D. Process running, no in-game evidence             \u2192 No 
Heartbeat\n\n        Process check runs FIRST.  Presence is supplementary and never overrides\n        a dead 
process.\n\n        [DENG_REJOIN_PACKAGE_CHECK] probe logged by caller.\n        """\n        t0 = time.monotonic()\n  
      # \u2500\u2500 A. Process check (ALWAYS FIRST) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        try:\n            evidence = self._fast_alive_evidence(pkg)\n        except Exception:  # 
noqa: BLE001\n            evidence = {}\n        process_running = bool(\n            evidence.get("alive")\n          
  or evidence.get("running")\n            or evidence.get("root_running")\n        )\n\n        foreground_package = 
str(evidence.get("foreground_package") or "")\n        root_available = bool(getattr(self._root_info, "available", 
False))\n        local_in_game_hint = bool(\n            process_running\n            and evidence.get("foreground")\n 
           and (evidence.get("window") or evidence.get("surface") or evidence.get("task"))\n        )\n\n        
elapsed_ms = int((time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000)\n        if not process_running:\n            detail = {\n            
    "process_running": "false",\n                "in_game": "false",\n                "heartbeat_ok": "false",\n       
         "warning_detected": "false",\n                "elapsed_ms": elapsed_ms,\n                "root_available": 
str(root_available).lower(),\n                "foreground_package": foreground_package,\n                "activity": 
"",\n                "in_game_proof": "false",\n                "reason": "process_not_running",\n            }\n      
      self._log_state_evidence(pkg, detail, self._presence_last_detail.get(pkg, {}), STATUS_DEAD)\n            return 
STATUS_DEAD, detail\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 B / C / D. Presence check (supplementary) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    
    in_game = False\n        presence_offline = False\n        presence_unknown = False\n        presence = None\n\n   
     try:\n            presence = self._fetch_presence(pkg)\n            if presence is not None:\n                if 
getattr(presence, "is_in_game", False):\n                    in_game = True\n                elif getattr(presence, 
"is_offline", False):\n                    presence_offline = True\n                elif getattr(presence, 
"is_unknown", False):\n                    presence_unknown = True\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
    pass\n\n        elapsed_ms = int((time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000)\n\n        if in_game:\n            # Reset 
offline counter when confirmed in-game.\n            self._nhb_offline_count[pkg] = 0\n            detail = {\n        
        "process_running": "true",\n                "in_game": "true",\n                "heartbeat_ok": "true",\n      
          "warning_detected": "false",\n                "elapsed_ms": elapsed_ms,\n                "root_available": 
str(root_available).lower(),\n                "foreground_package": foreground_package,\n                "activity": 
"",\n                "in_game_proof": "true",\n                "heartbeat_age_sec": "",\n                "reason": 
"roblox_presence_in_game",\n            }\n            self._log_state_evidence(pkg, detail, 
self._presence_last_detail.get(pkg, {}), STATUS_ONLINE)\n            return STATUS_ONLINE, detail\n\n        # Track 
consecutive offline presence signals (for No Heartbeat)\n        last_online = self._last_online_ts.get(pkg, 0.0)\n    
    was_recently_online = (time.time() - last_online) < 300.0  # 5 min\n        heartbeat_age_sec = int(time.time() - 
last_online) if last_online else ""\n\n        if presence_offline and was_recently_online:\n            count = 
self._nhb_offline_count.get(pkg, 0) + 1\n            self._nhb_offline_count[pkg] = count\n            if count >= 
self.NHB_OFFLINE_CONFIRMATIONS:\n                detail = {\n                    "process_running": "true",\n          
          "in_game": "false",\n                    "heartbeat_ok": "false",\n                    "warning_detected": 
"false",\n                    "elapsed_ms": elapsed_ms,\n                    "root_available": 
str(root_available).lower(),\n                    "foreground_package": foreground_package,\n                    
"activity": "",\n                    "in_game_proof": "true",\n                    "heartbeat_age_sec": 
heartbeat_age_sec,\n                    "reason": "presence_offline_after_recent_online",\n                }\n         
       self._log_state_evidence(pkg, detail, self._presence_last_detail.get(pkg, {}), STATUS_NO_HEARTBEAT)\n           
     return STATUS_NO_HEARTBEAT, detail\n\n        if not presence_offline and (presence is None or presence_unknown) 
and local_in_game_hint:\n            self._nhb_offline_count[pkg] = 0\n            detail = {\n                
"process_running": "true",\n                "in_game": "true",\n                "heartbeat_ok": "true",\n              
  "warning_detected": "false",\n                "elapsed_ms": elapsed_ms,\n                "root_available": 
str(root_available).lower(),\n                "foreground_package": foreground_package,\n                "activity": 
"",\n                "in_game_proof": "true",\n                "heartbeat_age_sec": heartbeat_age_sec,\n               
 "reason": "foreground_window_surface_hint",\n            }\n            self._log_state_evidence(pkg, detail, 
self._presence_last_detail.get(pkg, {}), STATUS_ONLINE)\n            return STATUS_ONLINE, detail\n\n        # D. 
Process alive, but no confirmed in-game signal.  This is a\n        # recovery state now: close/relaunch only this 
package instead of\n        # letting a clone sit at Roblox home.\n        reason = (\n            
"presence_not_in_game_no_heartbeat"\n            if presence is not None and not presence_unknown\n            else 
"missing_in_game_proof_no_heartbeat"\n        )\n        detail = {\n            "process_running": "true",\n          
  "in_game": "false",\n            "heartbeat_ok": "false",\n            "warning_detected": "false",\n            
"elapsed_ms": elapsed_ms,\n            "root_available": str(root_available).lower(),\n            
"foreground_package": foreground_package,\n            "activity": "",\n            "in_game_proof": "unknown" if 
presence is None or presence_unknown else "false",\n            "heartbeat_age_sec": heartbeat_age_sec,\n            
"reason": reason,\n        }\n        self._log_state_evidence(pkg, detail, self._presence_last_detail.get(pkg, {}), 
STATUS_NO_HEARTBEAT)\n        return STATUS_NO_HEARTBEAT, detail\n\n    def _log_state_evidence(\n        self,\n      
  pkg: str,\n        detail: dict[str, Any],\n        presence_detail: dict[str, Any],\n        final_state: str,\n    
) -> None:\n        log_event(\n            self._logger, "info", "[DENG_REJOIN_STATE_EVIDENCE]",\n            
package=pkg,\n            process_running=detail.get("process_running", "unknown"),\n            pid="",\n            
root_available=detail.get("root_available", "false"),\n            foreground_package=detail.get("foreground_package", 
""),\n            activity=detail.get("activity", ""),\n            
roblox_user_id=presence_detail.get("roblox_user_id", 0),\n            
roblox_user_id_source=presence_detail.get("roblox_user_id_source", ""),\n            
roblox_api_used=presence_detail.get("roblox_api_used", "false"),\n            
roblox_api_status=presence_detail.get("roblox_api_status", "skipped"),\n            
roblox_presence_type=presence_detail.get("roblox_presence_type", ""),\n            
roblox_place_id=presence_detail.get("roblox_place_id", ""),\n            
roblox_universe_id=presence_detail.get("roblox_universe_id", ""),\n            
in_game_proof=detail.get("in_game_proof", "unknown"),\n            heartbeat_age_sec=detail.get("heartbeat_age_sec", 
""),\n            heartbeat_ok=detail.get("heartbeat_ok", "unknown"),\n            
warning_detected=detail.get("warning_detected", "unknown"),\n            final_state=final_state,\n            
reason=detail.get("reason", ""),\n        )\n\n    # \u2500\u2500\u2500 Recovery \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n    def _do_launch(\n    
    self, pkg: str, entry: dict[str, Any], reason: str\n    ) -> bool:\n        """Launch the package using the 
canonical launcher.  Returns success."""\n        logger = self._logger\n        url = 
str(effective_private_server_url(entry, self.cfg) or "").strip()\n        url_configured = bool(url)\n        
launcher_label = "private_url" if url_configured else "app_only"\n        t0 = time.monotonic()\n        try:\n        
    result = launch_package_for_current_config(entry, self.cfg, reason)\n            elapsed_ms = 
int((time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000)\n            log_event(\n                logger, "info", 
"[DENG_REJOIN_RECOVERY_LAUNCH_RESULT]",\n                package=pkg,\n                reason=reason,\n                
launcher=launcher_label,\n                return_code=0 if result.success else 1,\n                
success=str(result.success).lower(),\n                stdout="",\n                stderr=str(result.error or ""),\n    
            elapsed_ms=elapsed_ms,\n            )\n            if result.success:\n                
_reapply_layout_for_package(pkg)\n            return result.success\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n            elapsed_ms = int((time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000)\n            log_event(\n                logger, 
"error", "[DENG_REJOIN_RECOVERY_LAUNCH_RESULT]",\n                package=pkg, reason=reason, 
launcher=launcher_label,\n                return_code=1, success="false",\n                stdout="", stderr=str(exc), 
elapsed_ms=elapsed_ms,\n            )\n            return False\n\n    def _handle_state(\n        self,\n        pkg: 
str,\n        entry: dict[str, Any],\n        state: str,\n        prev: str,\n        now: float,\n    ) -> None:\n   
     """Apply recovery action based on current state.\n\n        Recovery rules:\n        - Dead        \u2192 
launch_package_for_current_config\n        - No Heartbeat \u2192 am force-stop <pkg>, then 
launch_package_for_current_config\n        - Online      \u2192 update last_online_ts, keep monitoring\n        """\n  
      logger = self._logger\n        url = str(effective_private_server_url(entry, self.cfg) or "").strip()\n        
url_configured = bool(url)\n\n        if state == STATUS_DEAD:\n            action = "private_url_relaunch" if 
url_configured else "app_only_relaunch"\n            log_event(\n                logger, "info", 
"[DENG_REJOIN_RECOVERY_DECISION]",\n                package=pkg, state="Dead",\n                
private_url_configured=str(url_configured).lower(),\n                action=action,\n                
reason="process_not_running",\n            )\n            success = self._do_launch(pkg, entry, "dead_recovery")\n     
       if success:\n                self._revive_count[pkg] = self._revive_count.get(pkg, 0) + 1\n                
self._set_grace(pkg, now)\n                self._set_status(pkg, STATUS_LAUNCHING)\n            else:\n                
self._failure_count[pkg] = self._failure_count.get(pkg, 0) + 1\n\n        elif state == STATUS_NO_HEARTBEAT:\n         
   # \u2500\u2500 Cooldown guard ù prevent instant force-stop/relaunch loops \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n            
cooldown_sec = self.NHB_RELAUNCH_COOLDOWN_SEC\n            cooldown_until = self._nhb_cooldown_until.get(pkg, 0.0)\n   
         if now < cooldown_until:\n                # Still in cooldown window: log and skip this round.\n              
  log_event(\n                    logger, "debug", "[DENG_REJOIN_NO_HEARTBEAT_COOLDOWN]",\n                    
package=pkg,\n                    cooldown_sec=cooldown_sec,\n                    remaining_sec=round(cooldown_until - 
now, 1),\n                    started_at=round(cooldown_until - cooldown_sec, 3),\n                    
relaunch_at=round(cooldown_until, 3),\n                    reason="avoid_crash",\n                    
status="waiting",\n                )\n                return\n            # Set (or renew) the cooldown window before 
acting.\n            self._nhb_cooldown_until[pkg] = now + cooldown_sec\n            log_event(\n                
logger, "info", "[DENG_REJOIN_NO_HEARTBEAT_COOLDOWN]",\n                package=pkg,\n                
cooldown_sec=cooldown_sec,\n                started_at=round(now, 3),\n                relaunch_at=round(now + 
cooldown_sec, 3),\n                reason="avoid_crash",\n                status="cooldown_set_proceeding",\n          
  )\n\n            action = (\n                "close_then_private_url_relaunch" if url_configured\n                
else "close_then_app_only_relaunch"\n            )\n            log_event(\n                logger, "info", 
"[DENG_REJOIN_RECOVERY_DECISION]",\n                package=pkg, state="No Heartbeat",\n                
private_url_configured=str(url_configured).lower(),\n                action=action,\n                
reason="heartbeat_stalled_or_presence_offline",\n            )\n            # Force-stop ONLY this package, then 
relaunch.\n            try:\n                android.force_stop_package(pkg)\n                time.sleep(1.5)\n        
    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                pass\n            success = self._do_launch(pkg, entry, 
"no_heartbeat_recovery")\n            if success:\n                self._revive_count[pkg] = 
self._revive_count.get(pkg, 0) + 1\n                self._nhb_offline_count[pkg] = 0\n                
self._set_grace(pkg, now)\n                self._set_status(pkg, STATUS_LAUNCHING)\n                # Reset 
online_start_ts ù package is relaunching.\n                self._online_start_ts.pop(pkg, None)\n            else:\n   
             self._failure_count[pkg] = self._failure_count.get(pkg, 0) + 1\n\n        # STATUS_ONLINE: just update 
timestamp, no recovery needed.\n\n    # \u2500\u2500\u2500 Main loop \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n    def run_forever(\n        self,\n      
  *,\n        display_interval: float = 3.0,\n        render_callback: Any = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        """Run the 
sequential watchdog loop until stop() is called.\n\n        For every round:\n          1. Log 
[DENG_REJOIN_WATCHDOG_ROUND]\n          2. For each package (index/total):\n             - Update self.checking_label 
= "Checking Package X/Y"\n             - Detect state (process first, presence second)\n             - Log 
[DENG_REJOIN_PACKAGE_CHECK]\n             - If not in grace window: handle recovery\n          3. Log 
[DENG_REJOIN_WATCHDOG_CONTINUES]\n          4. Sleep interval (interruptible by stop_event)\n\n        The dashboard 
callback is called approximately every display_interval\n        seconds during both the package checks and the sleep 
phase.\n        """\n        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self._handle_stop)\n        signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, 
self._handle_stop)\n\n        logger = self._logger\n        log_event(\n            logger, "info", 
"watchdog_supervisor_started",\n            packages=self.packages,\n        )\n        db.insert_event(\n            
"INFO", "watchdog_supervisor_started",\n            f"watching {len(self.packages)} packages: {\', 
\'.join(self.packages)}",\n        )\n\n        _next_render = time.time()\n\n        def _maybe_render(*, force: bool 
= False) -> None:\n            nonlocal _next_render\n            if render_callback is not None and (force or 
time.time() >= _next_render):\n                try:\n                    render_callback()\n                except 
Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                    pass\n                _next_render = time.time() + 
float(display_interval)\n\n        while not self.stop_event.is_set():\n            self._round += 1\n            now 
= time.time()\n            interval = self._sup_interval()\n            total = len(self.packages)\n\n            # 
Determine if any entry has a private URL (for probe log).\n            _any_url = any(\n                
bool(str(effective_private_server_url(e, self.cfg) or "").strip())\n                for e in self.entries\n            
)\n            log_event(\n                logger, "info", "[DENG_REJOIN_WATCHDOG_ROUND]",\n                
round=self._round,\n                total_packages=total,\n                interval_sec=interval,\n                
private_url_configured=str(_any_url).lower(),\n            )\n            log_event(\n                logger, "info", 
"[DENG_REJOIN_WATCHDOG_ROUND_START]",\n                round=self._round,\n                total=total,\n            
)\n\n            # \u2500\u2500 Sequential per-package check \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n            checked = 0\n            round_started = time.monotonic()\n            for 
idx, pkg in enumerate(self.packages, 1):\n                if self.stop_event.is_set():\n                    break\n\n  
              entry = self.entry_by_pkg[pkg]\n                self.checking_label = f"Checking Package 
{idx}/{total}"\n                _maybe_render(force=True)\n                check_started = time.monotonic()\n          
      log_event(\n                    logger, "info", "[DENG_REJOIN_PACKAGE_CHECK_START]",\n                    
round=self._round,\n                    index=idx,\n                    total=total,\n                    
package=pkg,\n                )\n\n                prev = self._prev_state.get(pkg, self.status_map.get(pkg, ""))\n    
            error_text = ""\n                try:\n                    state, detail = self._detect_package_state(pkg, 
entry)\n                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n                    error_text = str(exc)[:180]\n    
                state = prev if prev in {STATUS_ONLINE, STATUS_NO_HEARTBEAT, STATUS_DEAD} else STATUS_NO_HEARTBEAT\n   
                 detail = {\n                        "process_running": "unknown",\n                        "in_game": 
"unknown",\n                        "heartbeat_ok": "unknown",\n                        "warning_detected": 
"unknown",\n                        "elapsed_ms": int((time.monotonic() - check_started) * 1000),\n                    
    "reason": "detector_exception",\n                    }\n\n                log_event(\n                    logger, 
"info", "[DENG_REJOIN_PACKAGE_CHECK]",\n                    round=self._round,\n                    index=idx,\n       
             total=total,\n                    package=pkg,\n                    
process_running=detail.get("process_running", "unknown"),\n                    in_game=detail.get("in_game", 
"unknown"),\n                    heartbeat_ok=detail.get("heartbeat_ok", "unknown"),\n                    
warning_detected=detail.get("warning_detected", "false"),\n                    state=state,\n                    
previous_state=prev,\n                    elapsed_ms=detail.get("elapsed_ms", 0),\n                )\n                
log_event(\n                    logger, "info", "[DENG_REJOIN_PACKAGE_CHECK_END]",\n                    
round=self._round,\n                    index=idx,\n                    total=total,\n                    
package=pkg,\n                    state=state,\n                    elapsed_ms=detail.get("elapsed_ms", 
int((time.monotonic() - check_started) * 1000)),\n                    error=error_text,\n                )\n           
     checked += 1\n\n                self._set_status(pkg, state)\n                self._prev_state[pkg] = state\n\n   
             # Update Online timestamp when confirmed in-game.\n                if state == STATUS_ONLINE:\n           
         self._last_online_ts[pkg] = now\n                    # Track when the package FIRST became Online this run\n  
                  # (for Runtime column in live dashboard).\n                    if prev != STATUS_ONLINE:\n           
             self._online_start_ts[pkg] = now\n                elif pkg in self._online_start_ts:\n                    
# Package left Online state ù clear its runtime start.\n                    del self._online_start_ts[pkg]\n\n         
       # Grace window: skip recovery immediately after a launch.\n                if not self._in_grace(pkg, now):\n   
                 self._handle_state(pkg, entry, state, prev, now)\n\n            # \u2500\u2500 Watchdog continuity 
probe \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n        
    _counts = {\n                "online":       sum(1 for v in self.status_map.values() if v == STATUS_ONLINE),\n     
           "dead":         sum(1 for v in self.status_map.values() if v == STATUS_DEAD),\n                
"no_heartbeat": sum(1 for v in self.status_map.values() if v == STATUS_NO_HEARTBEAT),\n            }\n            
log_event(\n                logger, "info", "[DENG_REJOIN_WATCHDOG_CONTINUES]",\n                
online_packages=_counts["online"],\n                dead_packages=_counts["dead"],\n                
no_heartbeat_packages=_counts["no_heartbeat"],\n                next_round_in_sec=interval,\n            )\n           
 log_event(\n                logger, "info", "[DENG_REJOIN_WATCHDOG_ROUND_END]",\n                round=self._round,\n 
               checked=checked,\n                expected=total,\n                duration_ms=int((time.monotonic() - 
round_started) * 1000),\n            )\n\n            # \u2500\u2500 Cycle "Checking Package" back to 1/N immediately 
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n            # Without this, the 
label stays at "N/N" for the full sleep interval\n            # which makes the watchdog appear frozen after the last 
package.\n            # Resetting to "1/N" right after the round ends gives the user the\n            # visible  3/3 
\u2192 1/3  transition that confirms the loop is alive.\n            # Guard: only reset while still running ù if 
stop_event was set\n            # inside the last detect call, preserve "N/N" for clean shutdown.\n            if not 
self.stop_event.is_set():\n                self.checking_label = f"Checking Package 1/{total}"\n                
_maybe_render(force=True)\n\n            # \u2500\u2500 Sleep (interruptible, keep rendering) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\n            _sleep_deadline = time.time() + interval\n            while not self.stop_event.is_set() and 
time.time() < _sleep_deadline:\n                _maybe_render()\n                time.sleep(min(1.0, _sleep_deadline - 
time.time()))\n\n        db.insert_event("INFO", "watchdog_supervisor_stopped", "session ended by user")\n        
log_event(logger, "info", "watchdog_supervisor_stopped")\n\n    def stop(self) -> None:\n        """Signal the 
supervisor loop to stop."""\n        self.stop_event.set()\n\n    def get_status_snapshot(\n        self, entries: 
list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None\n    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n        entry_map: dict[str, str] = {}\n        
use = entries or self.entries\n        for e in use:\n            pkg = str(e.get("package") or "")\n            
username = str(e.get("account_username") or "").strip()\n            entry_map[pkg] = username if username else 
"Unknown"\n\n        snapshot: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n        for pkg in self.packages:\n            status = 
self.status_map.get(pkg, STATUS_DEAD)\n            snapshot.append(\n                {\n                    "package": 
     pkg,\n                    "username":     entry_map.get(pkg, ""),\n                    "status":       status,\n  
                  "revive_count": self._revive_count.get(pkg, 0),\n                    "failure_count": 
self._failure_count.get(pkg, 0),\n                    "last_error":   None,\n                    "online_since": 
self._last_online_ts.get(pkg),\n                    "last_seen_at": self._last_online_ts.get(pkg),\n                
}\n            )\n        return snapshot\n\n"""Resize the Termux window to free up screen space for Roblox 
clones.\n\nWhen ``deng-rejoin Start`` runs on a small phone screen, the Termux task\nitself is normally full-screen.  
That covers the area where the layout\nalgorithm wants to place clones, so the user can\'t actually see whether\nthe 
windows landed in their pane.  This module dock-resizes Termux into\nthe left "log/status" pane so the right pane is 
free for clones.\n\nPipeline\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n1. Detect display size (re-uses 
:func:`window_layout.detect_display_info`).\n2. Compute the dock rect ``(0, 0, int(W * fraction), H)``.  Fraction is\n 
  the user\'s chosen size ù default :data:`window_layout.TERMUX_LOG_FRACTION`\n   (35 %).  The user-tunable knob is 
``config.termux_dock_fraction``.\n3. Find the Termux task via ``dumpsys activity activities`` /\n   ``dumpsys window 
windows`` (matches package ``com.termux``).\n4. Flip the task into freeform windowing mode (``cmd activity\n   
set-task-windowing-mode <tid> 5``).  Some Android forks no-op resize\n   on fullscreen tasks until this flip 
succeeds.\n5. Try a cascade of resize commands (``cmd activity resize-task``,\n   ``am task resize``, ``am stack 
resize``, ``wm task resize``).  First\n   one that runs cleanly wins.\n6. Optionally read back the bounds via 
``dumpsys window windows`` for\n   verification.\n\nReturns a small :class:`MinimizeResult` so callers (the Start 
path,\n``last_start_diagnostics.json``, tests) can introspect what happened.\nNever raises ù every command is 
guarded.\n\nPublic API\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n* 
:func:`minimize_termux_to_dock(display, *, fraction)` ù perform the dock.\n* :class:`MinimizeResult` ù outcome 
dataclass.\n\nImporting this module is free of side effects.  It writes to the\n``deng.rejoin.termux_minimize`` logger 
only.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport re\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import 
dataclass, field\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom . import android\nfrom .window_layout import (\n    DisplayInfo,\n    
TERMUX_LOG_FRACTION,\n    detect_display_info,\n)\n\n_log = logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.termux_minimize")\n\n# 
Termux\'s own package ù fixed.  The minimizer ONLY ever touches this\n# package, not user-selected 
ones.\nTERMUX_PACKAGE = "com.termux"\n\n# Minimum width/height in pixels.  Anything smaller than this gets clipped\n# 
to keep the terminal usable ù a 60-px-wide column with 1-char of text\n# would just frustrate the 
user.\n_MIN_DOCK_WIDTH = 240\n_MIN_DOCK_HEIGHT = 320\n\n# Maximum fraction allowed.  We refuse to dock Termux to >90% 
of the\n# screen because that defeats the purpose (no room for clones).\n_MAX_FRACTION = 0.9\n_MIN_FRACTION = 
0.15\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass MinimizeResult:\n    """Outcome of a Termux dock-resize attempt."""\n    ok: bool = 
False\n    skipped: bool = False\n    reason: str = ""\n    task_id: int | None = None\n    fraction: float = 
TERMUX_LOG_FRACTION\n    desired: tuple[int, int, int, int] | None = None\n    actual: tuple[int, int, int, int] | 
None = None\n    method: str = ""\n    attempts: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n    display: tuple[int, int] 
| None = None\n\n    def as_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        return {\n            "ok":       self.ok,\n        
    "skipped":  self.skipped,\n            "reason":   self.reason,\n            "task_id":  self.task_id,\n           
 "fraction": self.fraction,\n            "desired":  list(self.desired) if self.desired else None,\n            
"actual":   list(self.actual) if self.actual else None,\n            "method":   self.method,\n            "attempts": 
list(self.attempts),\n            "display":  list(self.display) if self.display else None,\n        }\n\n\n# 
\u2500\u2500 Task ID lookup \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\n_TASK_ID_PATTERNS = (\n    re.compile(r"taskId=(\\d+)"),\n    
re.compile(r"Task\\s*id\\s*#?(\\d+)", re.IGNORECASE),\n    re.compile(r"\\* Task\\{[^}]*#(\\d+)\\b"),\n)\n\n\ndef 
_find_termux_task_id() -> tuple[int | None, str]:\n    """Return ``(task_id, source)`` for the running Termux task, or 
(None, reason).\n\n    Uses :func:`android.run_android_command` so it transparently retries via\n    root when the 
unprivileged ``dumpsys`` call returns a permission denial.\n    """\n    sources = (\n        (["dumpsys", "activity", 
"activities"], "dumpsys activity activities"),\n        (["dumpsys", "activity", "recents"],    "dumpsys activity 
recents"),\n        (["dumpsys", "window", "windows"],      "dumpsys window windows"),\n    )\n    last_err = ""\n    
for args, label in sources:\n        try:\n            res = android.run_android_command(args, timeout=6)\n        
except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            last_err = f"{label}: {exc}"\n            continue\n        if 
not res.ok or not res.stdout:\n            last_err = f"{label}: rc={res.returncode}"\n            continue\n        
tid = _scan_for_termux_task(res.stdout)\n        if tid is not None:\n            return tid, label\n        last_err 
= f"{label}: no com.termux task found"\n    return None, last_err or "no dumpsys output"\n\n\ndef 
_find_termux_stack_id(task_id: int) -> int | None:\n    """Return the ``StackId`` that currently holds the Termux 
task, or None.\n\n    ``am stack resize`` operates on STACK IDs, not task IDs.  On Android 10\n    the dumpsys 
activities block contains lines like::\n\n        * TaskRecord{3169de3 #78 A=com.termux U=0 StackId=1 sz=1}\n\n    We 
parse those to get the real stack ID so the resize lands on the right\n    freeform stack.  Without this fix, passing 
the task ID (e.g. 78) as the\n    stack ID resizes the wrong stack (or nothing).\n\n    Never raises.  Returns None 
when the dump is unavailable or the pattern\n    is not found.\n    """\n    try:\n        res = 
android.run_android_command(\n            ["dumpsys", "activity", "activities"], timeout=8,\n        )\n        if not 
res.ok or not res.stdout:\n            return None\n        text = res.stdout\n        # Match "TaskRecord{... 
#<task_id> ... StackId=<n>}" ù same pattern\n        # used by window_apply._find_stack_id_for_package.\n        for m
