in re.finditer(\n            r"TaskRecord\\{[^}]*?#(\\d+)[^}]*?StackId=(\\d+)",\n            text,\n        ):\n       
     if int(m.group(1)) == task_id:\n                return int(m.group(2))\n        # Fallback: find the "Stack #N" 
header whose block contains\n        # "com.termux" near a "#<task_id>" reference.\n        blocks = 
re.split(r"\\n(?=Stack #\\d+:)", text)\n        for blk in blocks:\n            head = blk.split("\\n", 1)[0]\n        
    sm = re.match(r"Stack #(\\d+)", head)\n            if not sm:\n                continue\n            if 
"com.termux" in blk and f"#{task_id}" in blk:\n                return int(sm.group(1))\n    except Exception:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n        pass\n    return None\n\n\ndef _scan_for_termux_task(text: str) -> int | None:\n    """Scan *text* (a 
dumpsys dump) for the first task entry naming Termux.\n\n    Looks for a ``taskId=<n>`` (or equivalent) within ▒15 
lines of a\n    ``com.termux`` reference.  The default app activity is\n    ``com.termux/.app.TermuxActivity`` but 
Termux:Boot, Termux:API, and\n    Termux:Float each register additional activities ù we accept any\n    
``com.termux*`` package so we don\'t miss a forked Termux.\n    """\n    lines = text.splitlines()\n    for i, line in 
enumerate(lines):\n        if "com.termux" not in line:\n            continue\n        # Search forward + backward for 
taskId on the same activity block.\n        window = lines[max(0, i - 15): i + 16]\n        for cand in window:\n      
      for pat in _TASK_ID_PATTERNS:\n                m = pat.search(cand)\n                if m:\n                    
try:\n                        return int(m.group(1))\n                    except (TypeError, ValueError):\n            
            continue\n    return None\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Resize cascade \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef _clamp_fraction(fraction: float) -> 
float:\n    try:\n        f = float(fraction)\n    except (TypeError, ValueError):\n        return 
TERMUX_LOG_FRACTION\n    if f < _MIN_FRACTION:\n        return _MIN_FRACTION\n    if f > _MAX_FRACTION:\n        
return _MAX_FRACTION\n    return f\n\n\ndef _dock_rect(display: DisplayInfo, fraction: float) -> tuple[int, int, int, 
int]:\n    f = _clamp_fraction(fraction)\n    # ``round`` not ``int`` so 720 ╫ 0.35 \u2192 252 instead of 251 
(floating-\n    # point round-down).  One-pixel difference is invisible but the\n    # rounder number reads better in 
diagnostics.\n    w = max(_MIN_DOCK_WIDTH, round(display.width * f))\n    h = max(_MIN_DOCK_HEIGHT, 
int(display.height))\n    # Clip to the screen so a tiny screen doesn\'t produce out-of-bounds\n    # numbers that the 
window manager would reject.\n    w = min(w, display.width)\n    h = min(h, display.height)\n    return (0, 0, w, 
h)\n\n\ndef _try_resize(task_id: int, rect: tuple[int, int, int, int],\n                root_tool: str | None,\n       
         stack_id: int | None = None) -> tuple[bool, str, list[str]]:\n    """Walk a cascade of resize commands, 
return (ok, method, attempts).\n\n    Each attempt is recorded so the caller can surface the full trail in\n    
diagnostics.  When ``root_tool`` is None the cascade is restricted to\n    unprivileged ``cmd``/``am`` invocations ù 
most Android builds reject\n    resize-task without root, so the result is usually a clean ``False``.\n\n    
``stack_id`` is the Android stack ID that owns ``task_id``.  When\n    provided it is used for ``am stack resize`` 
instead of ``task_id`` ù\n    that command operates on stacks, not tasks, and using the wrong ID\n    silently no-ops 
(probe ``p-52801790db``).\n\n    Real-device note (probe ``p-47fa33562a``): ``am stack resize`` returns\n    rc=0 on 
modern Android even when the command no-ops (it was deprecated\n    in favour of ``cmd activity`` on Android 12+).  We 
keep it in the\n    cascade as a "best effort", but rc=0 from this command MUST NOT be\n    treated as a real success 
ù the caller verifies via dumpsys read-back.\n    """\n    l, t, r, b = rect\n    attempts: list[str] = []\n    _stack 
= stack_id if stack_id is not None else task_id\n\n    # Step 1 ù set freeform windowing mode (root-only).  No-op if 
already\n    # freeform.  Resize-task is otherwise silently ignored on fullscreen.\n    if root_tool:\n        try:\n  
          wm_res = android.run_root_command(\n                ["cmd", "activity", "set-task-windowing-mode", 
str(task_id), "5"],\n                root_tool=root_tool, timeout=4,\n            )\n            attempts.append(\n    
            f"set-windowing-mode rc={wm_res.returncode} err={(wm_res.stderr or \'\')[:60]}"\n            )\n        
except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            attempts.append(f"set-windowing-mode exc={exc}")\n\n    # Step 2 
ù resize cascade.  Labelled "trusted" commands have well-\n    # defined behaviour; "best_effort" includes deprecated 
APIs that lie\n    # about success and require explicit read-back verification.\n    cmds: list[tuple[list[str], str, 
bool]] = [\n        # (command, label, trusted)\n        (["cmd", "activity", "resize-task", str(task_id),\n          
str(l), str(t), str(r), str(b)],          "cmd resize-task",          True),\n        (["cmd", "activity", 
"resize-task", str(task_id),\n          str(l), str(t), str(r), str(b), "1"],     "cmd resize-task (mode=1)", True),\n 
       (["am", "task", "resize", str(task_id),\n          str(l), str(t), str(r), str(b)],          "am task resize",  
         True),\n        (["wm", "task", "resize", str(task_id),\n          str(l), str(t), str(r), str(b)],          
"wm task resize",           True),\n        # NOTE: am stack resize uses the STACK ID, not the task ID.\n        # 
_stack is set to the real StackId from dumpsys when available\n        # (probe p-52801790db: using task_id here 
silently no-ops).\n        (["am", "stack", "resize", str(_stack),\n          str(l), str(t), str(r), str(b)],         
 f"am stack resize (stack={_stack},task={task_id})", False),\n    ]\n    best_effort_winner: tuple[str, list[str]] | 
None = None\n    for cmd_args, label, trusted in cmds:\n        try:\n            if root_tool:\n                res = 
android.run_root_command(\n                    cmd_args, root_tool=root_tool, timeout=4,\n                )\n          
  else:\n                res = android.run_command(cmd_args, timeout=4)\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n            attempts.append(f"{label} exc={exc}")\n            continue\n        if res.ok:\n            if 
trusted:\n                attempts.append(f"{label} OK")\n                return True, label, attempts\n            # 
Best-effort command ù record but don\'t claim victory yet.\n            attempts.append(f"{label} rc=0 (best-effort, 
needs read-back)")\n            if best_effort_winner is None:\n                best_effort_winner = (label, 
list(attempts))\n            continue\n        attempts.append(\n            f"{label} rc={res.returncode} 
err={(res.stderr or \'\')[:80]}"\n        )\n    if best_effort_winner is not None:\n        # No trusted command 
succeeded.  Surface the best-effort outcome\n        # but signal "unverified" so the caller treats readback as the\n  
      # source of truth.\n        label, _ = best_effort_winner\n        return True, label + " [unverified]", 
attempts\n    return False, "", attempts\n\n\ndef _read_back_termux_bounds() -> tuple[int, int, int, int] | None:\n    
"""Best-effort read of Termux\'s current window bounds. None on failure."""\n    try:\n        res = 
android.run_android_command(\n            ["dumpsys", "window", "windows"], timeout=6,\n        )\n    except 
Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return None\n    if not res.ok or not res.stdout:\n        return None\n    # 
Match the first ``Window{... com.termux/...}`` block and pull its\n    # frame.  Format: ``mFrame=[0,0][720,1280]`` or 
``Frame: [...]``.\n    text = res.stdout\n    m = re.search(\n        
r"com\\.termux[\\s\\S]{0,400}?[mM]Frame[=:]?\\s*\\[?(\\d+),(\\d+)\\]?\\[?(\\d+),(\\d+)\\]?",\n        text,\n    )\n   
 if not m:\n        m = re.search(\n            
r"com\\.termux[\\s\\S]{0,400}?Frame:\\s*\\[(\\d+),(\\d+)\\]\\[(\\d+),(\\d+)\\]",\n            text,\n        )\n    if 
not m:\n        return None\n    try:\n        return (int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2)),\n                
int(m.group(3)), int(m.group(4)))\n    except (TypeError, ValueError):\n        return None\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Public 
entrypoint \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef 
minimize_termux_to_dock(\n    display: DisplayInfo | None = None,\n    *,\n    fraction: float = 
TERMUX_LOG_FRACTION,\n    verify: bool = True,\n) -> MinimizeResult:\n    """Resize Termux to the left dock pane.\n\n  
  Args:\n        display:  Pre-fetched display info.  Pass ``None`` to auto-detect.\n        fraction: Width of the 
dock as a fraction of the screen, clamped\n                  to ``[0.15, 0.9]``.  Default: 
:data:`TERMUX_LOG_FRACTION`.\n        verify:   If True, re-read Termux bounds after the resize.\n\n    Returns:\n     
   :class:`MinimizeResult`.\n    """\n    result = MinimizeResult(fraction=_clamp_fraction(fraction))\n    try:\n      
  disp = display or detect_display_info()\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        result.skipped = 
True\n        result.reason = f"detect_display_info failed: {exc}"\n        return result\n    result.display = 
(disp.width, disp.height)\n    result.desired = _dock_rect(disp, result.fraction)\n\n    # Find the task.\n    try:\n  
      tid, source = _find_termux_task_id()\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        result.skipped = 
True\n        result.reason = f"task lookup failed: {exc}"\n        return result\n    if tid is None:\n        
result.skipped = True\n        result.reason = f"no Termux task found ({source})"\n        return result\n    
result.task_id = tid\n    result.attempts.append(f"task lookup: tid={tid} via {source}")\n\n    # Look up the actual 
stack ID for am stack resize (it needs STACK id, not task id).\n    stack_id: int | None = None\n    try:\n        
stack_id = _find_termux_stack_id(tid)\n        if stack_id is not None:\n            result.attempts.append(f"stack 
lookup: sid={stack_id} for tid={tid}")\n        else:\n            result.attempts.append("stack lookup: StackId not 
found in dumpsys")\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        result.attempts.append(f"stack lookup 
exc={exc}")\n\n    # Resize cascade.\n    try:\n        root_info = android.detect_root()\n    except Exception:  # 
noqa: BLE001\n        root_info = None\n    root_tool = getattr(root_info, "tool", None) if (\n        root_info and 
getattr(root_info, "available", False)\n    ) else None\n    if not root_tool:\n        result.attempts.append("root 
unavailable ù trying unprivileged cascade")\n    ok, method, attempts = _try_resize(tid, result.desired, root_tool, 
stack_id=stack_id)\n    result.attempts.extend(attempts)\n    if not ok:\n        result.reason = "all resize variants 
failed"\n        return result\n    result.method = method\n\n    # Whether the command itself was "trusted" ù i.e. a 
rc=0 from one of\n    # the modern cmd/wm/am-task commands.  Best-effort wins are flagged\n    # with "[unverified]" 
so we can require readback for them below.\n    method_is_trusted = "[unverified]" not in method\n\n    if verify:\n   
     # Tiny wait so dumpsys reflects the new bounds.\n        time.sleep(0.3)\n        actual = 
_read_back_termux_bounds()\n        result.actual = actual\n        if actual is None:\n            # Readback failed. 
 ONLY claim OK when the command was trusted\n            # (modern cmd/wm/am-task with rc=0).  Real-device evidence\n  
          # (probe ``p-47fa33562a``) showed ``am stack resize`` returning\n            # rc=0 without resizing 
anything, then the dashboard reported\n            # ``ok=True`` while Termux remained fullscreen ù exactly what\n     
       # the user complained about.\n            if method_is_trusted:\n                result.ok = True\n             
   result.reason = "resize ran; readback unavailable"\n            else:\n                result.ok = False\n          
      result.reason = (\n                    "best-effort resize ran but readback unavailable ù "\n                    
"cannot confirm Termux was actually resized"\n                )\n            return result\n        # Treat ▒64 px as 
a match (status bars / IMEs eat some pixels).\n        wl, wt, wr, wb = result.desired\n        al, at, ar, ab = 
actual\n        within = (abs(al - wl) <= 64 and abs(at - wt) <= 64\n                  and abs(ar - wr) <= 64 and 
abs(ab - wb) <= 64)\n        result.ok = bool(within)\n        if not within:\n            result.reason = f"resize 
verified out-of-tolerance: actual={actual}"\n        return result\n\n    result.ok = method_is_trusted\n    if not 
result.ok:\n        result.reason = "best-effort resize without readback verification"\n    return result\n\n\ndef 
minimize_termux_silent(fraction: float = TERMUX_LOG_FRACTION) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Thin wrapper that returns 
``MinimizeResult.as_dict()``; never raises."""\n    try:\n        return 
minimize_termux_to_dock(fraction=fraction).as_dict()\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
_log.debug("minimize_termux_silent error: %s", exc)\n        return {\n            "ok": False, "skipped": True, 
"reason": f"exception: {exc}",\n            "task_id": None, "fraction": fraction, "desired": None,\n            
"actual": None, "method": "", "attempts": [], "display": None,\n        }\n\n"""Client-side licensed package updater 
for DENG Tool: Rejoin.\n\nFlow\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n1. Read license config (key, server_url, install_id, 
channel) from config.json.\n2. POST /api/download/authorize  \u2192 download_token + package metadata.\n3. Compare 
remote version with local VERSION.\n4. If newer (or --force): GET /api/download/package/<token> \u2192 
stream-download.\n5. Verify SHA-256 of downloaded zip.\n6. Backup current install (skip data/, logs/, 
config.json).\n7. Extract zip into install dir (skip .env entries, skip path traversal).\n8. Apply secure 
permissions.\n9. If extraction fails: rollback to backup 
automatically.\n\nSecurity\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nò Never stores or transmits Supabase 
service role key.\nò Never stores or transmits Discord bot token.\nò Never stores or transmits raw install_id ù only 
its SHA-256 hash is sent.\nò Full license key is never logged; masked version only.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import 
annotations\n\nimport hashlib\nimport json\nimport os\nimport shutil\nimport tempfile\nimport urllib.error\nimport 
urllib.request\nimport uuid\nimport zipfile\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom 
typing import Any\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Exceptions \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\nclass 
UpdaterError(RuntimeError):\n    """Raised when an update step fails unrecoverably."""\n\n\nclass 
HashMismatchError(UpdaterError):\n    """Downloaded package SHA-256 does not match the manifest."""\n\n\nclass 
LicenseCheckError(UpdaterError):\n    """License API rejected the request (wrong_device, revoked, expired, 
etc.)."""\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Internal helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef _utc_now() -> str:\n    return 
datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()\n\n\ndef _mask_key(key: str) -> str:\n    parts = (key 
or "").split("-")\n    if len(parts) >= 5:\n        return f"{parts[0]}-{parts[1]}...{parts[-1]}"\n    return (key[:8] 
+ "...") if len(key) > 8 else "***"\n\n\ndef _http_post_json(\n    url: str,\n    payload: dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n   
 timeout: int = 30,\n    bearer_token: str = "",\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """POST *payload* as JSON to *url* and 
return the parsed response.\n\n    Raises UpdaterError on HTTP errors or network failures.\n    """\n    data = 
json.dumps(payload).encode("utf-8")\n    headers: dict[str, str] = {\n        "Content-Type": "application/json",\n    
    "Accept": "application/json",\n        "User-Agent": "DENG-Tool-Rejoin-Updater/1.0",\n    }\n    if 
bearer_token:\n        headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {bearer_token}"\n\n    req = urllib.request.Request(url, 
data=data, headers=headers, method="POST")\n    try:\n        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as 
resp:  # noqa: S310\n            body = resp.read(256 * 1024)  # cap at 256 KB\n            return json.loads(body)  # 
type: ignore[return-value]\n    except urllib.error.HTTPError as exc:\n        body = exc.read(4096).decode("utf-8", 
errors="replace")\n        raise UpdaterError(f"HTTP {exc.code} from {url}: {body[:200]}") from exc\n    except 
urllib.error.URLError as exc:\n        raise UpdaterError(f"Network error reaching {url}: {exc.reason}") from exc\n    
except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:\n        raise UpdaterError(f"Invalid JSON from {url}: {exc}") from exc\n\n\ndef 
_http_download(\n    url: str,\n    dest: Path,\n    *,\n    bearer_token: str = "",\n    timeout: int = 120,\n) -> 
None:\n    """Stream-download *url* into *dest* file.\n\n    Raises UpdaterError on HTTP/network errors.\n    """\n    
headers: dict[str, str] = {"User-Agent": "DENG-Tool-Rejoin-Updater/1.0"}\n    if bearer_token:\n        
headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {bearer_token}"\n\n    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers)\n    
try:\n        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp, open(dest, "wb") as out:  # noqa: S310\n      
      while True:\n                chunk = resp.read(65536)\n                if not chunk:\n                    
break\n                out.write(chunk)\n    except urllib.error.HTTPError as exc:\n        raise 
UpdaterError(f"Download HTTP {exc.code}: {exc.reason}") from exc\n    except urllib.error.URLError as exc:\n        
raise UpdaterError(f"Download network error: {exc.reason}") from exc\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Version comparison \u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef 
_parse_version(ver: str) -> tuple[int, ...]:\n    """Parse \'X.Y.Z\' \u2192 (X, Y, Z). Non-numeric segments default to 
0."""\n    parts: list[int] = []\n    for segment in (ver or "0").strip().split("."):\n        try:\n            
parts.append(int(segment))\n        except ValueError:\n            parts.append(0)\n    while len(parts) < 3:\n       
 parts.append(0)\n    return tuple(parts)\n\n\ndef is_newer_version(remote: str, local: str) -> bool:\n    """Return 
True if *remote* version is strictly newer than *local*."""\n    return _parse_version(remote) > 
_parse_version(local)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 License config I/O \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef load_license_config(config_path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    
"""Read the ``license`` section from agent config.json.\n\n    Returns an empty dict if the file is missing or has no 
``license`` key.\n    Raises UpdaterError if the file cannot be parsed.\n    """\n    if not config_path.exists():\n   
     return {}\n    try:\n        data = json.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))\n    except (OSError, 
json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:\n        raise UpdaterError(f"Cannot read config at {config_path}: {exc}") from exc\n    
return data.get("license", {})\n\n\ndef save_license_status(config_path: Path, status: str) -> None:\n    """Update 
``license.last_status`` and ``last_check_at`` in config.json.\n\n    Non-fatal: errors are silently ignored to not 
interrupt normal operation.\n    """\n    try:\n        data = json.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))\n   
     lic = data.setdefault("license", {})\n        lic["last_status"] = status\n        lic["last_check_at"] = 
_utc_now()\n        config_path.write_text(\n            json.dumps(data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), 
encoding="utf-8"\n        )\n    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n        pass\n\n\ndef 
_save_install_id(config_path: Path, install_id: str) -> None:\n    """Persist a newly-generated install_id into the 
license config block."""\n    try:\n        data = json.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))\n        
data.setdefault("license", {})["install_id"] = install_id\n        config_path.write_text(\n            
json.dumps(data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8"\n        )\n    except (OSError, 
json.JSONDecodeError):\n        pass\n\n\ndef _ensure_install_id(config_path: Path, lic: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    
"""Return the configure install_id, generating and saving one if absent."""\n    install_id = (lic.get("install_id") 
or "").strip()\n    if not install_id:\n        install_id = uuid.uuid4().hex\n        _save_install_id(config_path, 
install_id)\n    return install_id\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Core API calls \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef request_download_token(\n    
server_url: str,\n    key: str,\n    install_id: str,\n    device_model: str,\n    app_version: str,\n    channel: str 
= "stable",\n    *,\n    api_secret: str = "",\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Call ``POST /api/download/authorize`` and 
return the response dict.\n\n    On success the dict includes: ``download_token``, ``expires_at``,\n    ``version``, 
``channel``, ``filename``, ``sha256``, ``size_bytes``,\n    ``download_url``.\n\n    Raises:\n        
LicenseCheckError: if the server rejects with a known result code.\n        UpdaterError: on network failures or 
unexpected server errors.\n    """\n    # Hash install_id before sending ù never transmit raw\n    install_id_hash = 
hashlib.sha256(install_id.encode()).hexdigest()\n    url = server_url.rstrip("/") + "/api/download/authorize"\n    
payload: dict[str, Any] = {\n        "key": key,\n        "install_id_hash": install_id_hash,\n        "device_model": 
device_model[:120],\n        "app_version": app_version[:40],\n        "channel": channel,\n    }\n    resp = 
_http_post_json(url, payload, bearer_token=api_secret)\n\n    result = resp.get("result", "")\n    if result and 
result not in ("active", ""):\n        raise LicenseCheckError(\n            resp.get("message") or f"License check 
returned: {result}"\n        )\n    if "download_token" not in resp:\n        raise UpdaterError(f"Server did not 
return a download_token: {list(resp.keys())}")\n    return resp\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Package operations \u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef download_package(\n  
  download_url: str,\n    dest: Path,\n    *,\n    api_secret: str = "",\n) -> None:\n    """Download the package zip 
from *download_url* to *dest*."""\n    _http_download(download_url, dest, bearer_token=api_secret)\n\n\ndef 
verify_package(path: Path, expected_sha256: str) -> None:\n    """Verify SHA-256 of *path*. Raises HashMismatchError 
on mismatch."""\n    from .security import compute_file_sha256\n\n    actual = compute_file_sha256(path)\n    if 
actual.lower() != expected_sha256.strip().lower():\n        raise HashMismatchError(\n            f"SHA-256 mismatch ù 
expected {expected_sha256[:16]}..., "\n            f"got {actual[:16]}... ù refusing to install."\n        )\n\n\ndef 
backup_install(install_dir: Path) -> Path:\n    """Copy current install to a timestamped backup directory.\n\n    
Skips user-data directories (data/, logs/, run/, backups/, cache/) and\n    the sensitive .env file so we backup only 
installable files.\n\n    Returns the backup directory path.\n    """\n    ts = 
datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")\n    backup_dir = install_dir.parent / "backups" / 
f"pre-update-{ts}"\n    backup_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    _skip = {"data", "logs", "run", 
"backups", "cache", "keydb.json", ".env"}\n\n    for item in install_dir.iterdir():\n        if item.name in _skip or 
item.name.endswith(".env"):\n            continue\n        dest = backup_dir / item.name\n        try:\n            if 
item.is_dir():\n                shutil.copytree(item, dest, dirs_exist_ok=True)\n            else:\n                
shutil.copy2(item, dest)\n        except OSError:\n            pass\n    return backup_dir\n\n\ndef 
extract_package(zip_path: Path, install_dir: Path) -> list[str]:\n    """Extract *zip_path* into *install_dir*.\n\n    
Security:\n      - Skips any entry whose path starts with \'/\' or contains \'..\'.\n      - Skips .env files 
regardless of path depth.\n      - Verifies each resolved extractee path is within *install_dir*.\n\n    Returns a 
list of extracted member filenames.\n    """\n    install_root = install_dir.resolve()\n    extracted: list[str] = 
[]\n\n    with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, "r") as zf:\n        for member in zf.infolist():\n            name = 
member.filename\n\n            # Guard: absolute path or traversal\n            if name.startswith("/") or ".." in 
name.replace("\\\\", "/"):\n                continue\n\n            # Guard: .env files at any depth\n            
parts = name.replace("\\\\", "/").split("/")\n            if any(part == ".env" or part.endswith(".env") for part in 
parts):\n                continue\n\n            # Guard: must resolve within install_dir\n            resolved = 
(install_dir / name).resolve()\n            try:\n                resolved.relative_to(install_root)\n            
except ValueError:\n                continue\n\n            zf.extract(member, install_dir)\n            
extracted.append(name)\n\n    return extracted\n\n\ndef rollback_install(backup_dir: Path, install_dir: Path) -> 
None:\n    """Restore *backup_dir* contents over *install_dir*."""\n    for item in backup_dir.iterdir():\n        
dest = install_dir / item.name\n        try:\n            if item.is_dir():\n                shutil.copytree(item, 
dest, dirs_exist_ok=True)\n            else:\n                shutil.copy2(item, dest)\n        except OSError:\n      
      pass\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Full update flow \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef perform_update(\n    config_path: Path,\n    install_dir: Path,\n    
*,\n    force: bool = False,\n    verbose: bool = True,\n) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    """Complete licensed package 
update flow.\n\n    Returns ``(success, message)`` ù never raises.\n    Callers should inspect ``success`` and display 
``message`` to the user.\n    """\n    from .security import secure_install_permissions\n\n    try:\n        from 
.constants import VERSION as CURRENT_VERSION\n    except ImportError:\n        CURRENT_VERSION = "0.0.0"\n\n    # 
\u2500\u2500 Read license config \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    
try:\n        lic = load_license_config(config_path)\n    except UpdaterError as exc:\n        return False, 
str(exc)\n\n    if not lic.get("enabled"):\n        return False, (\n            "License mode is disabled. "\n        
    "Set license.enabled=true in config.json to enable licensed updates."\n        )\n    if lic.get("mode") != 
"remote":\n        return False, (\n            "License mode is not \'remote\'. "\n            "Switch to 
license.mode=\'remote\' and set license.server_url to enable licensed updates."\n        )\n\n    key = 
(lic.get("key") or "").strip()\n    server_url = (lic.get("server_url") or "").strip()\n    channel = 
(lic.get("channel") or "stable").strip()\n    api_secret = (lic.get("api_secret") or "").strip()\n    device_model = 
(lic.get("device_label") or "termux-android")[:120]\n\n    if not key:\n        return False, "No license key 
configured in license.key."\n    if not server_url:\n        return False, "No server_url configured. Set 
license.server_url in config.json."\n\n    install_id = _ensure_install_id(config_path, lic)\n\n    if verbose:\n      
  print(f"Checking for update... (key={_mask_key(key)}, channel={channel})")\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 Authorize download \
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    try:\n        meta = 
request_download_token(\n            server_url, key, install_id, device_model,\n            CURRENT_VERSION, channel, 
api_secret=api_secret,\n        )\n    except LicenseCheckError as exc:\n        save_license_status(config_path, 
"rejected")\n        return False, str(exc)\n    except UpdaterError as exc:\n        return False, f"Update check 
failed: {exc}"\n\n    remote_version = meta.get("version", "0.0.0")\n    sha256 = meta.get("sha256", "")\n    
download_url = meta.get("download_url", "")\n    filename = meta.get("filename", "package.zip")\n    token = 
meta.get("download_token", "")\n\n    if not force and not is_newer_version(remote_version, CURRENT_VERSION):\n        
return True, f"Already up to date (version {CURRENT_VERSION})."\n\n    if verbose:\n        print(f"Downloading 
version {remote_version} ({filename})...")\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 Download + verify \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="deng-update-") as tmp:\n    
    zip_path = Path(tmp) / filename\n        try:\n            download_package(download_url, zip_path, 
api_secret=token)\n        except UpdaterError as exc:\n            return False, f"Download failed: {exc}"\n\n        
if sha256:\n            if verbose:\n                print("Verifying package SHA-256...")\n            try:\n         
       verify_package(zip_path, sha256)\n            except HashMismatchError as exc:\n                return False, 
str(exc)\n\n        # \u2500\u2500 Backup + extract \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   
     backup_dir = backup_install(install_dir)\n        if verbose:\n            print(f"Backup created: 
{backup_dir}")\n\n        try:\n            extracted = extract_package(zip_path, install_dir)\n            if 
verbose:\n                print(f"Extracted {len(extracted)} files.")\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n            if verbose:\n                print(f"Extraction failed: {exc}. Rolling back...")\n            
rollback_install(backup_dir, install_dir)\n            return False, f"Update failed and was rolled back: {exc}"\n\n   
 save_license_status(config_path, "active")\n    secure_install_permissions(install_dir)\n    return True, f"Updated 
to version {remote_version}."\n\n"""Roblox launch URL validation, normalization, and masking."""\n\nfrom __future__ 
import annotations\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nimport re\nfrom urllib.parse import ParseResult, parse_qsl, 
urlencode, urlparse, urlunparse\n\nfrom .constants import SENSITIVE_URL_PARAM_NAMES\n\nAPPROVED_WEB_HOSTS = 
{"roblox.com", "www.roblox.com"}\nAPPROVED_WEB_PATH_PREFIXES = (\n    "/games/",\n    "/share",\n    "/share-links",\n 
   "/communities/",\n    "/catalog/",\n    "/users/",\n    "/games/start",\n)\n\n\nclass 
UrlValidationError(ValueError):\n    """Raised when a launch URL is not safe enough to 
use."""\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass UrlValidationResult:\n    valid: bool\n    warning: str | None = 
None\n\n\ndef _normalized_host(parsed: ParseResult) -> str:\n    return (parsed.hostname or 
"").lower().strip(".")\n\n\ndef detect_launch_mode_from_url(url: str | None) -> str:\n    """Infer the DENG launch 
mode from a URL-like value."""\n    if not url or not url.strip():\n        return "app"\n    parsed = 
urlparse(url.strip())\n    if parsed.scheme.lower() == "roblox":\n        return "deeplink"\n    if 
parsed.scheme.lower() in {"http", "https"}:\n        return "web_url"\n    return "web_url"\n\n\ndef 
normalize_launch_url(url: str) -> tuple[str, str | None]:\n    """Normalize a launch URL where it is unambiguous.\n\n  
  The function intentionally avoids over-normalizing Roblox links. Some share\n    and private-server URLs are opaque, 
so preserving the original link is safer.\n    """\n    raw = (url or "").strip()\n    if not raw:\n        raise 
UrlValidationError("launch URL is empty")\n    parsed = urlparse(raw)\n    scheme = parsed.scheme.lower()\n    if 
scheme == "roblox":\n        return raw, None\n    if scheme not in {"http", "https"}:\n        raise 
UrlValidationError("launch URL must use roblox://, http://, or https://")\n\n    host = _normalized_host(parsed)\n    
if host not in APPROVED_WEB_HOSTS:\n        raise UrlValidationError("web launch URL must be on roblox.com")\n\n    
rebuilt = parsed._replace(scheme="https", netloc=host)\n    normalized = urlunparse(rebuilt)\n    return normalized, 
None\n\n\ndef to_roblox_deep_link(url: str | None) -> str | None:\n    """Convert a Roblox web URL to its 
``roblox://`` deep-link equivalent.\n\n    Real-device evidence (Kaeru probe ``p-1239f2b5f9`` on cloud SM-N9810\n    
/Android 10): the dumpsys recents block for a successfully joined\n    clone showed::\n\n        
dat=roblox://navigation/share_links?code=<CODE>&type=Server\n\n    while our launches were sending the 
``https://www.roblox.com/share?...``\n    form which Android resolved to the *browser* ù landing the user in a\n    
Roblox lobby instead of the private server they configured.  This\n    helper performs the conversion so we behave the 
same way Kaeru does.\n\n    Returns the original URL unchanged for non-Roblox web URLs and for\n    any ``roblox://`` 
URL.  Returns ``None`` for falsy / empty input.\n\n    Known conversions\n    -----------------\n\n    * 
``https://www.roblox.com/share?code=X&type=Server``\n        \u2192 
``roblox://navigation/share_links?code=X&type=Server``\n    * ``https://www.roblox.com/share?code=X``  (no type)\n     
   \u2192 ``roblox://navigation/share_links?code=X``\n    * 
``https://www.roblox.com/games/123/abc?privateServerLinkCode=Y``\n        \u2192 
``roblox://placeId=123&privateServerLinkCode=Y``  (legacy\n          private-server format Roblox still accepts)\n    
* Everything else is returned unchanged.\n\n    The function NEVER raises ù bad input falls back to the original 
URL.\n    """\n    if not url:\n        return url\n    raw = url.strip()\n    if not raw:\n        return url\n    
try:\n        parsed = urlparse(raw)\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return raw\n    scheme = 
(parsed.scheme or "").lower()\n    if scheme == "roblox":\n        return raw  # already a deep link\n    if scheme 
not in {"http", "https"}:\n        return raw\n    host = _normalized_host(parsed)\n    if host not in 
APPROVED_WEB_HOSTS:\n        return raw\n\n    path = (parsed.path or "/").rstrip("/")\n    query = parsed.query or 
""\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 /share | /share-links | /share/ \u2500\u2500 new share-link format\n    if path in ("/share", 
"/share-links"):\n        # Preserve every original query param (code, type, possibly more)\n        return 
f"roblox://navigation/share_links?{query}" if query else \\\n               "roblox://navigation/share_links"\n\n    # 
\u2500\u2500 /games/<placeId>/<name>?privateServerLinkCode=...  (legacy)\n    m = re.match(r"^/games/(\\d+)/?", path + 
"/")\n    if m:\n        place_id = m.group(1)\n        params = dict(parse_qsl(query, keep_blank_values=True))\n      
  # `gameInstanceId`, `linkCode`, `privateServerLinkCode` all flow through.\n        passthrough = []\n        for k, 
v in params.items():\n            passthrough.append(f"{k}={v}")\n        suffix = "&" + "&".join(passthrough) if 
passthrough else ""\n        return f"roblox://placeId={place_id}{suffix}"\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 
/games/start?placeId=... ù also used by Roblox shortcuts\n    if path == "/games/start":\n        params = 
dict(parse_qsl(query, keep_blank_values=True))\n        place_id = params.pop("placeId", "")\n        if place_id:\n   
         passthrough = [f"{k}={v}" for k, v in params.items()]\n            suffix = "&" + "&".join(passthrough) if 
passthrough else ""\n            return f"roblox://placeId={place_id}{suffix}"\n\n    # Path we don\'t know how to 
translate ù return original; the OS will\n    # still launch it via the Roblox app\'s https intent filters.\n    
return raw\n\n\ndef validate_launch_url(url: str | None, launch_mode: str | None = None, *, allow_uncertain: bool = 
False) -> UrlValidationResult:\n    """Validate that a URL is appropriate for Android VIEW launching.\n\n    
`allow_uncertain=True` permits same-domain Roblox URLs that are not in the\n    known path allow-list. Callers should 
show the returned warning before use.\n    """\n    if launch_mode == "app":\n        return 
UrlValidationResult(True)\n    if not url or not url.strip():\n        raise UrlValidationError("launch URL is 
required for deeplink or web_url mode")\n\n    raw = url.strip()\n    parsed = urlparse(raw)\n    scheme = 
parsed.scheme.lower()\n\n    if launch_mode == "deeplink" and scheme != "roblox":\n        raise 
UrlValidationError("deeplink mode requires a roblox:// URL")\n    if launch_mode == "web_url" and scheme not in 
{"http", "https"}:\n        raise UrlValidationError("web_url mode requires a Roblox http(s) URL")\n\n    if scheme == 
"roblox":\n        if not parsed.netloc:\n            raise UrlValidationError("roblox:// URL must include a 
target")\n        return UrlValidationResult(True)\n\n    if scheme not in {"http", "https"}:\n        raise 
UrlValidationError("launch URL must use roblox://, http://, or https://")\n\n    host = _normalized_host(parsed)\n    
if host not in APPROVED_WEB_HOSTS:\n        raise UrlValidationError("web launch URL must be on roblox.com")\n\n    
path = parsed.path or "/"\n    if path == "/" or any(path.startswith(prefix) for prefix in 
APPROVED_WEB_PATH_PREFIXES):\n        return UrlValidationResult(True)\n\n    if allow_uncertain:\n        return 
UrlValidationResult(True, "Roblox URL path is not in the known allow-list; it will be launched unchanged")\n\n    
raise UrlValidationError("Roblox URL path is not approved for launch")\n\n\ndef _should_mask_param(name: str) -> 
bool:\n    lowered = name.lower()\n    if lowered in SENSITIVE_URL_PARAM_NAMES:\n        return True\n    return 
any(marker in lowered for marker in ("private", "token", "secret", "cookie", "session"))\n\n\ndef mask_launch_url(url: 
str | None) -> str | None:\n    """Mask private/sensitive query parameters while preserving readability."""\n    if 
url is None:\n        return None\n    raw = str(url)\n    if not raw:\n        return raw\n    parsed = 
urlparse(raw)\n    if not parsed.query:\n        return raw\n\n    masked_pairs = []\n    for key, value in 
parse_qsl(parsed.query, keep_blank_values=True):\n        masked_pairs.append((key, "***MASKED***" if 
_should_mask_param(key) and value else value))\n    query = urlencode(masked_pairs, doseq=True, safe="*")\n    return 
urlunparse(parsed._replace(query=query))\n\n\nURL_IN_TEXT_RE = 
re.compile(r"(roblox://[^\\s\\"\']+|https?://(?:www\\.)?roblox\\.com/[^\\s\\"\']+)", re.IGNORECASE)\n\n\ndef 
mask_urls_in_text(text: str) -> str:\n    """Mask any Roblox launch URLs embedded in a log/status line."""\n    return 
URL_IN_TEXT_RE.sub(lambda match: mask_launch_url(match.group(0)) or match.group(0), text)\n\n"""Safe Discord webhook 
helpers.\n\nThis module never handles Roblox credentials and masks webhook URLs anywhere\nthey may be 
displayed.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport time\nimport 
urllib.error\nimport urllib.parse\nimport urllib.request\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib import 
Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom .url_utils import mask_launch_url\nfrom . import safe_http\n\nWEBHOOK_MODES = 
{"new_message", "edit_message"}\nMIN_WEBHOOK_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 30\nMASK = "***MASKED***"\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 
Discord embed color constants \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nEMBED_COLOR_GREEN  = 0x57F287  # online / 
success\nEMBED_COLOR_RED    = 0xED4245  # offline / error\nEMBED_COLOR_YELLOW = 0xFEE75C  # warning / captcha 
alert\nEMBED_COLOR_ORANGE = 0xE67E22  # starting / preparing\nEMBED_COLOR_GREY   = 0x36393F  # neutral / 
unknown\n\n\nclass WebhookError(ValueError):\n    """Raised for invalid webhook configuration."""\n\n\ndef 
validate_webhook_url(url: str | None) -> str:\n    cleaned = (url or "").strip()\n    if not cleaned:\n        raise 
WebhookError("Discord webhook URL is required when webhook is enabled")\n    parsed = urllib.parse.urlparse(cleaned)\n 
   host = (parsed.hostname or "").lower()\n    if parsed.scheme != "https" or host not in {"discord.com", 
"discordapp.com"}:\n        raise WebhookError("Webhook URL must be a Discord https webhook URL")\n    parts = [part 
for part in parsed.path.split("/") if part]\n    if len(parts) < 4 or parts[:2] != ["api", "webhooks"]:\n        raise 
WebhookError("Webhook URL must look like https://discord.com/api/webhooks/...")\n    return cleaned\n\n\ndef 
mask_webhook_url(url: str | None) -> str:\n    if not url:\n        return ""\n    parsed = 
urllib.parse.urlparse(str(url))\n    host = parsed.netloc or "discord.com"\n    parts = [part for part in 
parsed.path.split("/") if part]\n    if len(parts) >= 4 and parts[:2] == ["api", "webhooks"]:\n        webhook_id = 
parts[2]\n        visible_id = webhook_id[:4] + "..." if len(webhook_id) > 4 else webhook_id\n        return 
urllib.parse.urlunparse((parsed.scheme or "https", host, f"/api/webhooks/{visible_id}/{MASK}", "", "", ""))\n    
return f"{parsed.scheme or \'https\'}://{host}/api/webhooks/{MASK}"\n\n\ndef validate_webhook_interval(seconds: int) 
-> int:\n    try:\n        value = int(seconds)\n    except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:\n        raise 
WebhookError("Webhook interval must be a number") from exc\n    if value < MIN_WEBHOOK_INTERVAL_SECONDS:\n        
raise WebhookError("Webhook interval must be at least 30 seconds to avoid spam/rate limits")\n    return 
value\n\n\ndef should_send_webhook(config_data: dict[str, Any], *, now: float | None = None) -> bool:\n    if not 
config_data.get("webhook_enabled"):\n        return False\n    now = time.time() if now is None else now\n    last = 
config_data.get("webhook_last_sent_at") or 0\n    try:\n        last_value = float(last)\n    except (TypeError, 
ValueError):\n        last_value = 0.0\n    interval = 
validate_webhook_interval(config_data.get("webhook_interval_seconds", 300))\n    return now - last_value >= 
interval\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Embed builder helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef _format_uptime(start_iso: str | None) -> str:\n    """Format uptime string like \'1h 22m\' from 
an ISO-8601 launch timestamp."""\n    if not start_iso:\n        return ""\n    try:\n        start = 
datetime.fromisoformat(start_iso)\n        if start.tzinfo is None:\n            start = 
start.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n        elapsed = max(0, int((datetime.now(timezone.utc) - 
start).total_seconds()))\n        hours = elapsed // 3600\n        minutes = (elapsed % 3600) // 60\n        return 
f"{hours}h {minutes:02d}m"\n    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n        return ""\n\n\n# Status category mapping 
(supervisor STATUS_* \u2192 display bucket)\n_STATUS_CATEGORY: dict[str, str] = {\n    "Online":        "online",\n    
"Ready":         "ready",\n    "Preparing":     "preparing",\n    "Launching":     "preparing",\n    "Checking":      
"preparing",\n    "Warning":       "warning",\n    "Reviving":      "warning",\n    "Reconnecting":  "preparing",\n    
"Background":    "warning",\n    "Unknown":       "warning",\n    "Offline":       "offline",\n    "Failed":        
"failed",\n    "Not installed": "failed",\n    "Disabled":      "failed",\n}\n\n\ndef build_status_embed_payload(\n    
config_data: dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    event: str = "status",\n    error: str | None = None,\n    app_stats: 
dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n    supervisor_snapshot: list[dict] | None = None,\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    """Build 
a Discord embed payload for periodic status updates."""\n    from .config import mask_license_key\n    from .license 
import get_public_device_model\n\n    raw_packages = config_data.get("roblox_packages") or 
[config_data.get("roblox_package", "unknown")]\n    entries: list[dict[str, str]] = []\n    for item in 
raw_packages:\n        if isinstance(item, dict):\n            package = str(item.get("package") or "unknown")\n       
     username = str(item.get("account_username") or item.get("label") or "").strip()\n        else:\n            
package = str(item)\n            username = ""\n        entries.append({"package": package, "username": username})\n\n 
   app_stats = app_stats or {}\n\n    # Count status categories from supervisor_snapshot or fall back to app_stats\n   
 counts: dict[str, int] = {cat: 0 for cat in ("online", "ready", "preparing", "warning", "offline", "failed")}\n    if 
supervisor_snapshot:\n        for snap in supervisor_snapshot:\n            category = 
_STATUS_CATEGORY.get(snap.get("status", ""), "offline")\n            counts[category] = counts.get(category, 0) + 1\n  
      total = len(supervisor_snapshot)\n    else:\n        # Backward-compatible: use app_stats boolean\n        for e 
in entries:\n            if app_stats.get(e["package"], {}).get("online"):\n                counts["online"] += 1\n    
        else:\n                counts["offline"] += 1\n        total = len(entries)\n    online_count = 
counts["online"]\n\n    # Embed color\n    if error:\n        color = EMBED_COLOR_YELLOW\n    elif online_count > 0:\n 
       color = EMBED_COLOR_GREEN\n    else:\n        color = EMBED_COLOR_RED\n\n    # System stats (injected by caller 
via _mem_info, _cpu_pct, _temp_c keys)\n    mem_info: dict[str, int] = config_data.get("_mem_info") or {}\n    cpu_pct 
= config_data.get("_cpu_pct")\n    temp_c = config_data.get("_temp_c")\n    sys_lines: list[str] = []\n    if 
mem_info.get("free_mb"):\n        sys_lines.append(f"\U0001f4be RAM: {mem_info[\'free_mb\']}MB free 
({mem_info.get(\'percent_free\', 0)}%)")\n    if cpu_pct is not None:\n        sys_lines.append(f"\u2699\ufe0f CPU: 
{cpu_pct:.0f}%")\n    if temp_c is not None:\n        sys_lines.append(f"\U0001f321\ufe0f Temp: {temp_c}░C")\n    
sys_value = "\\n".join(sys_lines) or "N/A"\n\n    # Status overview ù full 7-category breakdown\n    overview_parts = 
[\n        f"\U0001f7e2 Online: {counts[\'online\']}",\n        f"\U0001f7e1 Ready: {counts[\'ready\']}",\n        
f"\U0001f535 Preparing: {counts[\'preparing\']}",\n        f"\U0001f7e0 Warning: {counts[\'warning\']}",\n        
f"\U0001f534 Offline: {counts[\'offline\']}",\n        f"\u274c Failed: {counts[\'failed\']}",\n        f"\U0001f916 
Total: {total}",\n    ]\n    overview = "\\n".join(overview_parts)\n\n    # Per-app application details\n    
detail_lines: list[str] = []\n    for e in entries:\n        stats = app_stats.get(e["package"], {})\n        
indicator = "\U0001f7e2" if stats.get("online") else "\U0001f534"\n        label = (e["username"] or "").strip() or 
"Unknown"\n        detail_lines.append(f"{indicator} {label}")\n        sub: list[str] = []\n        uptime = 
_format_uptime(stats.get("uptime_start"))\n        if uptime:\n            sub.append(f"\u23f1\ufe0f {uptime}")\n      
  mem = stats.get("memory_mb")\n        if mem is not None:\n            sub.append(f"\U0001f4be {int(mem)} MB")\n     
   cpu = stats.get("cpu_pct")\n        if cpu is not None:\n            sub.append(f"\u26a1 {cpu:.1f}%")\n        if 
sub:\n            detail_lines.append("\u2514 " + " | ".join(sub))\n    detail_value = "\\n".join(detail_lines) or "No 
accounts configured"\n\n    # License + tags\n    masked_key = mask_license_key(config_data.get("license_key", ""))\n  
  webhook_tags = config_data.get("webhook_tags") or []\n    tags_value = f"[{len(webhook_tags)}]"\n    phone_type = 
get_public_device_model()\n    host_name = str(config_data.get("device_name", "unknown"))\n    device_value = 
host_name\n    if phone_type and phone_type != "Unknown":\n        device_value = f"{host_name}\\n\U0001f4f1 Type: 
{phone_type}"\n\n    fields: list[dict[str, Any]] = [\n        {"name": "\U0001f4f1 Device",           "value": 
device_value,                               "inline": True},\n        {"name": "\U0001f511 License",          "value": 
masked_key,                                     "inline": True},\n        {"name": "\U0001f3f7\ufe0f Tags",            
 "value": tags_value,                                     "inline": True},\n        {"name": "\U0001f5a5\ufe0f System 
Stats",     "value": sys_value,                                      "inline": False},\n        {"name": "Status 
Overview",     "value": overview,                                       "inline": False},\n        {"name": 
"Application Details", "value": detail_value,                                   "inline": False},\n    ]\n    if 
error:\n        fields.append({"name": "\u26a0\ufe0f Last Error", "value": error[:512], "inline": False})\n\n    
version = config_data.get("agent_version", "1.0.0")\n    return {\n        "username": "DENG Tool: Rejoin",\n        
"embeds": [\n            {\n                "title": "\U0001f4ca DENG Status Monitor",\n                "description": 
f"Event: **{event}**",\n                "color": color,\n                "timestamp": 
datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n                "fields": fields,\n                "footer": {"text": f"DENG 
Tool: Rejoin ò v{version}"},\n            }\n        ],\n    }\n\n\ndef build_alert_embed_payload(\n    config_data: 
dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    account: str,\n    reason: str,\n    event: str = "alert",\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    
"""Build an Account Alert embed payload (captcha, failures, etc.)."""\n    from .config import mask_license_key\n    
from .license import get_public_device_model\n\n    masked_key = mask_license_key(config_data.get("license_key", 
""))\n    webhook_tags = config_data.get("webhook_tags") or []\n    tags_value = f"[{len(webhook_tags)}]"\n    
phone_type = get_public_device_model()\n    host_name = str(config_data.get("device_name", "unknown"))\n    
device_value = host_name\n    if phone_type and phone_type != "Unknown":\n        device_value = 
f"{host_name}\\n\U0001f4f1 Type: {phone_type}"\n\n    reason_lower = reason.lower()\n    if "solved" in reason_lower 
or "success" in reason_lower or "\u2705" in reason:\n        color = EMBED_COLOR_GREEN\n    elif "captcha" in 
reason_lower or "detect" in reason_lower:\n        color = EMBED_COLOR_YELLOW\n    else:\n        color = 
EMBED_COLOR_ORANGE\n\n    version = config_data.get("agent_version", "1.0.0")\n    return {\n        "username": "DENG 
Tool: Rejoin",\n        "embeds": [\n            {\n                "title": "\u26a0\ufe0f Account Alert",\n           
     "description": "Attention needed immediately.",\n                "color": color,\n                "timestamp": 
datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n                "fields": [\n                    {"name": "\U0001f464 
Account", "value": account or "unknown",                              "inline": False},\n                    {"name": 
"\U0001f4f1 Device",  "value": device_value, "inline": False},\n                    {"name": "\U0001f511 License", 
"value": masked_key,                                     "inline": True},\n                    {"name": 
"\U0001f3f7\ufe0f Tags",    "value": tags_value,                                     "inline": True},\n                
    {"name": "\U0001f4dd Reason",  "value": reason[:512],                                    "inline": False},\n       
         ],\n                "footer": {"text": f"DENG Tool: Rejoin ò v{version}"},\n            }\n        ],\n    
}\n\n\ndef build_status_message(config_data: dict[str, Any], *, event: str = "status", error: str | None = None) -> 
str:\n    from .license import get_public_device_model\n\n    raw_packages = config_data.get("roblox_packages") or 
[config_data.get("roblox_package", "unknown")]\n    packages = []\n    for entry in raw_packages:\n        if 
isinstance(entry, dict):\n            package = str(entry.get("package") or "unknown")\n            username = 
str(entry.get("account_username") or entry.get("label") or "").strip()\n            packages.append(f"{username or 
\'Unknown\'} ({package})")\n        else:\n            packages.append(str(entry))\n    launch_url = 
mask_launch_url(config_data.get("launch_url")) or "not set"\n    phone_type = get_public_device_model()\n    lines = 
[\n        f"DENG Tool: Rejoin v{config_data.get(\'agent_version\', \'1.0.0\')}",\n        f"Event: {event}",\n        
f"Device: {config_data.get(\'device_name\', \'unknown\')}"\n        + (f" | {phone_type}" if phone_type and phone_type 
!= "Unknown" else ""),\n        f"Android: {config_data.get(\'android_release\', \'unknown\')} / SDK 
{config_data.get(\'android_sdk\', \'unknown\')}",\n        f"Roblox packages: {\', \'.join(packages)}",\n        
f"Launch link: {launch_url}",\n        f"Auto rejoin: {\'enabled\' if config_data.get(\'auto_rejoin_enabled\') else 
\'disabled\'}",\n        f"Root: available={config_data.get(\'root_available\')} 
enabled={config_data.get(\'root_mode_enabled\')}",\n        "Auto resize: automatic",\n    ]\n    if error:\n        
lines.append(f"Last error: {error}")\n    return "\\n".join(lines)\n\n\ndef send_webhook_update(\n    config_data: 
dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    event: str = "status",\n    snapshot_path: Path | None = None,\n    force: bool = 
False,\n    app_stats: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n    alert_account: str | None = None,\n    alert_reason: str | 
None = None,\n) -> tuple[bool, str, str | None]:\n    """Send a status or alert update via Discord webhook embed.\n\n  
  Returns ``(success, message, message_id)``.\n\n    Pass ``alert_account`` and ``alert_reason`` to send an Account 
Alert embed\n    instead of the standard Status Monitor embed.\n    Pass ``app_stats`` (mapping package \u2192 
stat-dict) to populate per-app fields.\n    """\n    if not config_data.get("webhook_enabled"):\n        return False, 
"webhook disabled", None\n    if not force and not should_send_webhook(config_data):\n        return False, "webhook 
interval has not elapsed", None\n    url = validate_webhook_url(config_data.get("webhook_url"))\n\n    # Choose embed 
type\n    if alert_account and alert_reason:\n        payload = build_alert_embed_payload(\n            config_data, 
account=alert_account, reason=alert_reason, event=event\n        )\n    else:\n        payload = 
build_status_embed_payload(config_data, event=event, app_stats=app_stats)\n\n    data: bytes\n    headers: dict[str, 
str]\n    if snapshot_path and snapshot_path.exists():\n        boundary = f"deng-{int(time.time())}"\n        
file_bytes = snapshot_path.read_bytes()\n        parts = [\n            f"--{boundary}\\r\\nContent-Disposition: 
form-data; name=\\"payload_json\\"\\r\\nContent-Type: 
application/json\\r\\n\\r\\n{json.dumps(payload)}\\r\\n".encode("utf-8"),\n            
f"--{boundary}\\r\\nContent-Disposition: form-data; name=\\"files[0]\\"; 
filename=\\"snapshot.png\\"\\r\\nContent-Type: image/png\\r\\n\\r\\n".encode("utf-8"),\n            file_bytes,\n      
      f"\\r\\n--{boundary}--\\r\\n".encode("utf-8"),\n        ]\n        data = b"".join(parts)\n        headers = 
{"Content-Type": f"multipart/form-data; boundary={boundary}"}\n    else:\n        data = 
json.dumps(payload).encode("utf-8")\n        headers = {"Content-Type": "application/json"}\n\n    # On Termux, route 
JSON-only webhook through safe_http/curl to prevent\n    # Python ssl SIGSEGV.  Multipart (snapshot) falls back to 
urllib since\n    # snapshots are rarely used on cloud phones, and the request is not\n    # part of the 
license-critical path.\n    on_termux = bool(os.environ.get("TERMUX_VERSION")) or os.environ.get("DENG_HTTP_BACKEND") 
== "curl"\n    if on_termux and not (snapshot_path and snapshot_path.exists()):\n        try:\n            resp_dict = 
safe_http.post_json(url, payload, timeout=10)\n            message_id = resp_dict.get("id") if isinstance(resp_dict, 
dict) else None\n            return True, "discord webhook OK (curl)", message_id\n        except 
safe_http.SafeHttpStatusError as exc:\n            return False, f"webhook HTTP {exc.status_code}", None\n        
except safe_http.SafeHttpError as exc:\n            return False, f"webhook failed: {exc}", None\n        except 
Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            return False, f"webhook error: {exc}", None\n\n    request = 
urllib.request.Request(url, data=data, headers=headers, method="POST")\n    try:\n        with 
urllib.request.urlopen(request, timeout=10) as response:  # noqa: S310 - validated Discord URL\n            body = 
response.read().decode("utf-8", errors="replace")\n            message_id = None\n            if body:\n               
 try:\n                    message_id = json.loads(body).get("id")\n                except json.JSONDecodeError:\n     
               message_id = None\n            return 200 <= response.status < 300, f"discord webhook HTTP 
{response.status}", message_id\n    except urllib.error.URLError as exc:\n        return False, f"webhook failed: 
{exc}", None\n\n"""Real-window apply layer for landscape-block layout.\n\nPipeline (per package)\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n1. 
DISCOVER  ù scan shared_prefs and identify real layout keys (cached).\n2. WRITE     ù write every known position/size 
alias AND every known "Set X"\n               enable boolean to pkg_preferences.xml (direct \u2192 root fallback).\n3. 
STOP      ù force-stop the package so prefs are re-read on next launch.\n4. RELAUNCH  ù optionally relaunch via 
``launcher.perform_rejoin`` so the new\n               clone window is created with the new bounds.\n5. READBACK  ù 
wait until a task/window for the package is visible, then parse\n               actual bounds out of ``dumpsys window 
windows`` /\n               ``dumpsys activity activities``.  We pick the bounds from the\n               window/task 
that actually belongs to *this* package, never a\n               random first match.\n6. RESIZE    ù if the actual 
bounds differ from desired by more than the\n               tolerance, try ``cmd activity resize-task <id> l t r 
b``,\n               ``am task resize``, and ``am stack resize`` via root.\n7. RETRY     ù re-write keys, re-stop, 
re-launch, re-readback up to N times.\n8. STATUS    ù mark each package one of:\n                  Layout Applied     
ù actual bounds verified within tolerance\n                  Layout Unverified  ù write succeeded but bounds 
unreadable\n                  Layout Failed      ù bounds wrong after every fallback\n\nAll output goes to the 
``deng.rejoin.window_apply`` logger (file only).  This\nmodule NEVER prints to stdout/stderr.  Public dashboard reads 
the ``status``\nfield on each :class:`ApplyResult`.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport 
logging\nimport re\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom typing import Iterable, 
Sequence\n\nfrom . import android\nfrom .window_layout import (\n    WindowRect,\n    _is_layout_excluded,\n    
update_app_cloner_xml,\n    update_app_cloner_xml_root,\n)\n\n_log = 
logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.window_apply")\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Public status labels (read by supervisor / 
dashboard) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nLAYOUT_APPLIED    = "Layout Applied"\nLAYOUT_UNVERIFIED = "Layout Unverified"\nLAYOUT_FAILED     
= "Layout Failed"\nLAYOUT_SKIPPED    = "Layout Skipped"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ApplyResult:\n    package: str\n    
desired: WindowRect\n    pre_write_ok: bool = False\n    pre_write_method: str = ""\n    actual_bounds: tuple[int, 
int, int, int] | None = None  # (l, t, r, b)\n    actual_method: str = ""\n    direct_resize_ok: bool = False\n    
final_ok: bool = False\n    status: str = LAYOUT_FAILED   # one of LAYOUT_APPLIED/UNVERIFIED/FAILED/SKIPPED\n    
detail: str = ""\n    attempts: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Capability probes \u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef 
_capability_probes() -> dict[str, bool]:\n    """Detect which apply methods are available on this device.  Never 
raises."""\n    caps = {\n        "root":             False,\n        "cmd_activity":     False,\n        "am_stack":  
       False,\n        "dumpsys_activity": False,\n        "dumpsys_window":   False,\n        "wm_size":          
False,\n    }\n    try:\n        caps["root"] = android.detect_root().available\n    except Exception:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n        pass\n    for cmd, key in (\n        (["cmd", "activity", "-h"], "cmd_activity"),\n        (["am", 
"-h"],              "am_stack"),\n        (["dumpsys", "activity"],   "dumpsys_activity"),\n        (["dumpsys", 
"window"],     "dumpsys_window"),\n        (["wm", "size"],            "wm_size"),\n    ):\n        try:\n            
res = android.run_command(cmd, timeout=3)\n            caps[key] = bool(res.ok or (res.stdout and "Usage" in 
res.stdout))\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            pass\n    return caps\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Read 
actual bounds (package-correct) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n_BOUNDS_RE = re.compile(r"\\[(\\d+),(\\d+)\\]\\[(\\d+),(\\d+)\\]")\n_TASK_ID_RE = 
re.compile(r"#(\\d+)\\s")\n_WINDOW_BLOCK_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"Window\\s*\\{[^}]*\\b([\\w.]+)/[\\w.]+\\b")\n\n\ndef 
_parse_bounds_line(text: str) -> tuple[int, int, int, int] | None:\n    """Extract first ``[l,t][r,b]`` rect from a 
text blob."""\n    m = _BOUNDS_RE.search(text)\n    if not m:\n        return None\n    try:\n        l, t, r, b = 
(int(g) for g in m.groups())\n        return l, t, r, b\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return 
None\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass _WindowEntry:\n    package: str\n    bounds:  tuple[int, int, int, int] | None\n    
has_surface: bool\n    is_focused: bool\n    task_id: int | None\n    raw_block: str\n\n\n_WINDOW_HEADER_RE = 
re.compile(r"^\\s*(?:Window\\b.*|.*\\bWindow\\s*\\{).*$")\n\n\ndef _is_window_header_line(line: str) -> bool:\n    
"""Heuristic: does this line look like the start of a window block?\n\n    Matches both real Android dumpsys lines 
(``Window{abc1234 ...}``) and the\n    looser sample used in tests (``Window foo {``).\n    """\n    return ("Window{" 
in line) or ("Window {" in line) or (\n        line.lstrip().startswith("Window") and "{" in line\n    )\n\n\ndef 
_parse_window_dumpsys(text: str, package: str) -> list[_WindowEntry]:\n    """Parse ``dumpsys window windows`` and 
yield candidate entries for ``package``.\n\n    A "block" is the text between consecutive ``Window ... {`` headers.  
We\n    only keep blocks whose body mentions the package.\n    """\n    entries: list[_WindowEntry] = []\n    if not 
text:\n        return entries\n    lines = text.splitlines()\n    i = 0\n    while i < len(lines):\n        line = 
lines[i]\n        if not _is_window_header_line(line):\n            i += 1\n            continue\n        block_lines 
= [line]\n        j = i + 1\n        while j < len(lines) and not _is_window_header_line(lines[j]):\n            
block_lines.append(lines[j])\n            j += 1\n        block = "\\n".join(block_lines)\n        i = j\n        if 
package not in block:\n            continue\n        bounds = None\n        for key in ("mFrame=", "containingFrame=", 
"mBounds=", "Bounds="):\n            idx = block.find(key)\n            if idx >= 0:\n                slice_ = 
block[idx : idx + 200]\n                m = _BOUNDS_RE.search(slice_)\n                if m:\n                    
bounds = (int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2)),\n                              int(m.group(3)), int(m.group(4)))\n         
           break\n        if bounds is None:\n            bounds = _parse_bounds_line(block)\n        has_surface = 
"mHasSurface=true" in block\n        is_focused = ("mCurrentFocus" in text and\n                      package in 
text.split("mCurrentFocus", 1)[-1][:160])\n        task_id = None\n        m = re.search(r"taskId=(\\d+)", block)\n    
    if m:\n            task_id = int(m.group(1))\n        entries.append(_WindowEntry(\n            package=package,\n 
           bounds=bounds,\n            has_surface=has_surface,\n            is_focused=is_focused,\n            
task_id=task_id,\n            raw_block=block[:1000],\n        ))\n    # Fallback: if no real Window{} block was found 
but the text mentions the\n    # package + has bounds, return a single weak candidate.  This keeps the\n    # bounds 
parser working on the loose sample format used in tests.\n    if not entries and package in text:\n        bounds = 
_parse_bounds_line(text)\n        if bounds:\n            entries.append(_WindowEntry(\n                
package=package,\n                bounds=bounds,\n                has_surface="mHasSurface=true" in text,\n            
    is_focused=False,\n                task_id=None,\n                raw_block=text[:1000],\n            ))\n    
return entries\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass _TaskEntry:\n    package: str\n    bounds: tuple[int, int, int, int] | None\n    
task_id: int | None\n    visible: bool\n    raw_block: str\n\n\ndef _parse_activity_dumpsys(text: str, package: str) 
-> list[_TaskEntry]:\n    """Parse ``dumpsys activity activities`` for task records mentioning ``package``."""\n    
entries: list[_TaskEntry] = []\n    if not text:\n        return entries\n    lines = text.splitlines()\n    
cur_task_id: int | None = None\n    cur_block_lines: list[str] = []\n    cur_pkg_in_block = False\n\n    def 
_flush():\n        nonlocal cur_block_lines, cur_pkg_in_block, cur_task_id\n        if cur_pkg_in_block and 
cur_block_lines:\n            block = "\\n".join(cur_block_lines)\n            bounds = None\n            for key in 
("Bounds=", "mBounds=", "mLastNonFullscreenBounds=", "userBounds="):\n                idx = block.find(key)\n          
      if idx >= 0:\n                    slice_ = block[idx : idx + 200]\n                    m = 
_BOUNDS_RE.search(slice_)\n                    if m:\n                        bounds = (int(m.group(1)), 
int(m.group(2)),\n                                  int(m.group(3)), int(m.group(4)))\n                        break\n 
           if bounds is None:\n                bounds = _parse_bounds_line(block)\n            visible = 
"visible=true" in block or "mResumedActivity" in block\n            entries.append(_TaskEntry(\n                
package=package,\n                bounds=bounds,\n                task_id=cur_task_id,\n                
visible=visible,\n                raw_block=block[:1000],\n            ))\n        cur_block_lines.clear()\n        
cur_pkg_in_block = False\n\n    for line in lines:\n        m = re.search(r"TaskRecord\\{[^}]*\\s+#(\\d+)\\b", line)\n 
       if m:\n            _flush()\n            cur_task_id = int(m.group(1))\n        cur_block_lines.append(line)\n  
      if package in line:\n            cur_pkg_in_block = True\n    _flush()\n    return entries\n\n\n# Minimum 
surface size that counts as "the main activity window".\n# Probe ``p-1239f2b5f9`` showed our parser picking up 
status-bar windows\n# like ``mFrame=[0,0][1280,25]`` (25 px tall) and ``[0,25][1,26]`` (1╫1 px)\n# as the package\'s 
bounds ù both belonged to the same UID but were not\n# the activity surface.  Anything under this threshold is 
definitely\n# chrome (status bar / IME stub / nav bar) ù skip it.\n_MIN_REAL_WINDOW_W = 200\n_MIN_REAL_WINDOW_H = 
100\n\n\ndef _is_real_activity_bounds(\n    bounds: tuple[int, int, int, int] | None,\n) -> bool:\n    """Heuristic: 
is this a real activity surface, or a chrome sliver?"""\n    if bounds is None:\n        return False\n    l, t, r, b 
= bounds\n    w, h = r - l, b - t\n    return w >= _MIN_REAL_WINDOW_W and h >= _MIN_REAL_WINDOW_H\n\n\ndef 
read_actual_bounds(package: str) -> tuple[tuple[int, int, int, int] | None, str]:\n    """Read the actual on-screen 
bounds for ``package``.\n\n    Selection priority (highest \u2192 lowest):\n        1. Real activity window (size 
\u2265 200╫100), surface alive AND focused.\n        2. Real activity window (size \u2265 200╫100), surface alive.\n   
     3. Real activity window (size \u2265 200╫100), any.\n        4. Legacy fallback ù any window with bounds (kept 
for unit-test\n           fixtures that don\'t model the full dumpsys layout).\n\n    The size threshold filters out 
status-bar / IME / nav-bar windows\n    that share the package\'s UID and would otherwise mask the activity\n    
surface (probe ``p-1239f2b5f9`` ù every clone "fits" in 25 px).\n    Source labels: ``dumpsys_window``, 
``dumpsys_activity``, ``unavailable``.\n    Never raises.\n    """\n    # 1. dumpsys window windows ù prefer windows 
with mHasSurface=true.\n    try:\n        res = android.run_command(["dumpsys", "window", "windows"], timeout=6)\n     
   if res.ok and res.stdout:\n            cands = _parse_window_dumpsys(res.stdout, package)\n            for c in 
cands:\n                if (c.has_surface and c.is_focused\n                        and 
_is_real_activity_bounds(c.bounds)):\n                    return c.bounds, "dumpsys_window"\n            for c in 
cands:\n                if c.has_surface and _is_real_activity_bounds(c.bounds):\n                    return c.bounds, 
"dumpsys_window"\n            for c in cands:\n                if _is_real_activity_bounds(c.bounds):\n                
    return c.bounds, "dumpsys_window"\n            for c in cands:\n                if c.has_surface and c.bounds:\n   
                 return c.bounds, "dumpsys_window"\n            for c in cands:\n                if c.is_focused and 
c.bounds:\n                    return c.bounds, "dumpsys_window"\n            for c in cands:\n                if 
c.bounds:\n                    return c.bounds, "dumpsys_window"\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
pass\n\n    # 2. dumpsys activity activities ù fall back to task bounds.\n    try:\n        res = 
android.run_command(["dumpsys", "activity", "activities"], timeout=6)\n        if res.ok and res.stdout:\n            
cands = _parse_activity_dumpsys(res.stdout, package)\n            for c in cands:\n                if c.visible and 
_is_real_activity_bounds(c.bounds):\n                    return c.bounds, "dumpsys_activity"\n            for c in 
cands:\n                if _is_real_activity_bounds(c.bounds):\n                    return c.bounds, 
"dumpsys_activity"\n            for c in cands:\n                if c.visible and c.bounds:\n                    
return c.bounds, "dumpsys_activity"\n            for c in cands:\n                if c.bounds:\n                    
return c.bounds, "dumpsys_activity"\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n\n    return None, 
"unavailable"\n\n\ndef _get_task_id(package: str) -> int | None:\n    """Best-effort: find the task id for 
``package``."""\n    try:\n        res = android.run_command(["dumpsys", "activity", "activities"], timeout=6)\n       
 if res.ok:\n            cands = _parse_activity_dumpsys(res.stdout, package)\n            for c in cands:\n           
     if c.task_id is not None:\n                    return c.task_id\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
pass\n    try:\n        res = android.run_command(["dumpsys", "window", "windows"], timeout=6)\n        if res.ok:\n   
         cands = _parse_window_dumpsys(res.stdout, package)\n            for c in cands:\n                if c.task_id 
is not None:\n                    return c.task_id\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n    return 
None\n\n\ndef _get_stack_id(package: str) -> int | None:\n    """Find the stack id currently holding ``package``\'s 
task.\n\n    On Android 10 (SDK 29) the working resize verb is::\n\n        am stack resize <STACK_ID> 
<LEFT,TOP,RIGHT,BOTTOM>\n\n    which operates on the STACK rather than the task, so we need to know\n    the stack the 
task currently belongs to.  Probe ``p-1239f2b5f9``\n    (SM-N9810 / Android 10) showed task records like::\n\n        
* TaskRecord{3169de3 #78 A=com.moons.litesc U=0 StackId=3 sz=1}\n\n    where the trailing ``StackId=3`` is what we 
need.  Returns ``None``\n    when the stack id cannot be discovered.  Never raises.\n    """\n    try:\n        res = 
android.run_command(["dumpsys", "activity", "activities"], timeout=8)\n        if not res.ok or not res.stdout:\n      
      return None\n        text = res.stdout\n        # The TaskRecord line contains both #<task_id> and 
StackId=<n>.\n        for m in re.finditer(\n            
r"TaskRecord\\{[^}]*?#(\\d+)[^}]*?A=([\\w.]+)[^}]*?StackId=(\\d+)",\n            text,\n        ):\n            
tid_str, pkg_in_block, stack_str = m.group(1), m.group(2), m.group(3)\n            if pkg_in_block == package:\n       
         return int(stack_str)\n        # Fallback: any "Stack #N" header whose body contains the package.\n        # 
Stack blocks are delimited by "Stack #" headers in dumpsys output.\n        blocks = re.split(r"\\n(?=Stack #\\d+:)", 
text)\n        for blk in blocks:\n            head = blk.split("\\n", 1)[0]\n            sm = re.match(r"Stack 
#(\\d+)", head)\n            if not sm:\n                continue\n            if package in blk:\n                
return int(sm.group(1))\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n    return None\n\n\ndef 
_wait_for_window(package: str, timeout: float) -> bool:\n    """Poll until ``package`` has any task/window evidence.  
Best-effort.\n\n    Returns True if evidence appeared; False on timeout.  Never raises.\n    """\n    deadline = 
time.time() + max(0.5, float(timeout))\n    while time.time() < deadline:\n        try:\n            ev = 
android.get_package_alive_evidence(package)\n            if ev.get("task") or ev.get("window") or ev.get("running"):\n 
               return True\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            pass\n        time.sleep(0.5)\n    
return False\n\n\n# Android 10 (SDK 29) freeform workspace stack id ù confirmed by AOSP\n# source 
``WindowConfiguration.WINDOWING_MODE_FREEFORM = 5`` and by Samsung\n# One UI 5 behavior.  On most Android 10 builds 
the freeform workspace\n# stack is dynamically allocated, so callers should prefer\n# ``move-task <tid> <stack> true`` 
over hard-coding 5.\nANDROID10_FREEFORM_WINDOWING_MODE = 5\n\n\ndef _direct_resize_via_root(\n    package: str, rect: 
WindowRect, root_tool: str\n) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    """Try multiple direct-resize commands for the package\'s 
current task.\n\n    Recipe (probe ``p-1239f2b5f9``, SM-N9810 / Android 10 / SDK 29):\n\n        1. Flip task \u2192 
freeform windowing mode (``cmd activity\n           set-task-windowing-mode`` if available; otherwise\n           ``am 
stack move-task <tid> <freeform_stack> true``).\n        2. Resize the STACK (not the task) with COMMA-separated
