bounds ù\n           Android 10 syntax is ``am stack resize <STACK_ID> <L,T,R,B>``.\n        3. Fall back to Android 
11+ verbs (``cmd activity resize-task``,\n           ``am task resize``) which take SPACE-separated bounds.\n        
4. Final attempt: ``wm task resize`` (some custom ROMs).\n\n    Each variant is logged with its rc / stderr.  Returns 
the first one\n    that runs without error.  Never raises.\n    """\n    task_id  = _get_task_id(package)\n    
stack_id = _get_stack_id(package)\n    if task_id is None:\n        return False, "no task id"\n    l, t, r, b = 
rect.left, rect.top, rect.right, rect.bottom\n    bounds_comma = f"{l},{t},{r},{b}"     # Android 10 syntax\n    
bounds_args  = [str(l), str(t), str(r), str(b)]  # Android 11+ syntax\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 Step 1: move task into 
freeform \u2500\u2500\n    flipped = False\n    try:\n        res = android.run_root_command(\n            ["cmd", 
"activity", "set-task-windowing-mode",\n             str(task_id), str(ANDROID10_FREEFORM_WINDOWING_MODE)],\n          
  root_tool=root_tool, timeout=4,\n        )\n        flipped = bool(res.ok)\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n  
      pass\n    if not flipped:\n        # Android 10 path: move task to the freeform workspace stack.\n        # Pass 
the freeform windowing mode (5) as the target stack id ù\n        # the system will route it into the freeform 
stack.\n        try:\n            android.run_root_command(\n                ["am", "stack", "move-task", 
str(task_id),\n                 str(ANDROID10_FREEFORM_WINDOWING_MODE), "true"],\n                root_tool=root_tool, 
timeout=4,\n            )\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n            pass\n\n    # Re-detect stack id now 
that the task may have been moved.\n    new_stack = _get_stack_id(package)\n    target_stack = new_stack if new_stack 
is not None else stack_id\n\n    candidates: list[list[str]] = []\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 Android 10 (CONFIRMED working 
on probe SM-N9810): stack-based,\n    # COMMA-separated bounds \u2500\u2500\n    if target_stack is not None:\n        
candidates.append(\n            ["am", "stack", "resize", str(target_stack), bounds_comma]\n        )\n        
candidates.append(\n            ["am", "stack", "resize-animated", str(target_stack),\n             bounds_comma]\n    
    )\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 Android 11+ task-based verbs, SPACE-separated bounds \u2500\u2500\n    
candidates.extend([\n        ["cmd", "activity", "resize-task", str(task_id), *bounds_args],\n        ["cmd", 
"activity", "resize-task", str(task_id), *bounds_args, "1"],\n        ["am", "task", "resize", str(task_id), 
*bounds_args],\n    ])\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 Stack-resize Android 11+ form (SPACE-separated, in case the\n    # device 
accepts both syntaxes) \u2500\u2500\n    if target_stack is not None:\n        candidates.append(\n            ["am", 
"stack", "resize", str(target_stack), *bounds_args]\n        )\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 Last resort: wm task resize 
(custom ROMs only) \u2500\u2500\n    candidates.append(\n        ["wm", "task", "resize", str(task_id), 
*bounds_args]\n    )\n\n    last_err = ""\n    for cmd_args in candidates:\n        try:\n            res = 
android.run_root_command(\n                cmd_args, root_tool=root_tool, timeout=4,\n            )\n            if 
res.ok:\n                # On Android 10 ``am stack resize`` returns rc=0 even when\n                # the stack id is 
wrong.  We accept ok=True optimistically;\n                # the post-launch verifier will check the actual bounds.\n  
              return True, (\n                    f"resize via {cmd_args[0]} {cmd_args[1]} "\n                    
f"(stack={target_stack},task={task_id})"\n                )\n            last_err = (res.stderr or res.stdout or 
"").strip()[:120]\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            last_err = str(exc)[:120]\n           
 continue\n    return False, (\n        f"all direct-resize variants failed (task #{task_id} "\n        f"stack 
#{target_stack}) ù {last_err}"\n    )\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 High-level apply \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef _bounds_close_enough(\n    actual: tuple[int, int, 
int, int],\n    desired: WindowRect,\n    tolerance: int = 32,\n) -> bool:\n    return (\n        abs(actual[0] - 
desired.left)   <= tolerance and\n        abs(actual[1] - desired.top)    <= tolerance and\n        abs(actual[2] - 
desired.right)  <= tolerance and\n        abs(actual[3] - desired.bottom) <= tolerance\n    )\n\n\ndef 
_write_one_package(\n    rect: WindowRect,\n    *,\n    root_tool: str | None,\n    known_keys: Iterable[str] | 
None,\n    result: ApplyResult,\n) -> bool:\n    """Pre-write the XML for one rect.  Updates ``result`` in place.\n\n  
  Returns True if at least one write method succeeded.\n    """\n    try:\n        ok, msg = 
update_app_cloner_xml(rect.package, rect, known_keys=known_keys)\n        result.attempts.append(f"xml-direct: 
{msg}")\n        if ok:\n            result.pre_write_ok = True\n            result.pre_write_method = "xml-direct"\n  
          return True\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        
result.attempts.append(f"xml-direct-error: {exc}")\n    if root_tool:\n        try:\n            ok, msg = 
update_app_cloner_xml_root(\n                rect.package, rect, root_tool, known_keys=known_keys\n            )\n     
       result.attempts.append(f"xml-root: {msg}")\n            if ok:\n                result.pre_write_ok = True\n    
            result.pre_write_method = "xml-root"\n                return True\n        except Exception as exc:  # 
noqa: BLE001\n            result.attempts.append(f"xml-root-error: {exc}")\n    return False\n\n\ndef 
_discover_known_keys(packages: Sequence[str], root_tool: str | None) -> dict[str, list[str]]:\n    """Best-effort 
discovery wrapper: returns ``{package: [key_names...]}``."""\n    try:\n        from .layout_discovery import 
get_cached_or_discover\n        discs = get_cached_or_discover(list(packages), root_tool=root_tool)\n        out: 
dict[str, list[str]] = {}\n        for pkg, d in discs.items():\n            out[pkg] = [k.name for k in d.keys]\n     
   return out\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _log.debug("_discover_known_keys error: %s", 
exc)\n        return {pkg: [] for pkg in packages}\n\n\ndef apply_window_layout(\n    rects: Sequence[WindowRect],\n   
 *,\n    force_stop_before: bool = False,\n    relaunch_after: bool = False,\n    verify_after: bool = True,\n    
retries: int = 1,\n    tolerance: int = 32,\n    wait_for_window_seconds: float = 6.0,\n) -> list[ApplyResult]:\n    
"""Apply landscape-block layout to a list of WindowRect.\n\n    Pipeline:\n      1. Discover known keys (cached, 
package-specific).\n      2. For each rect:\n         a. Pre-write XML (direct \u2192 root fallback) with EVERY alias 
and the\n            "Set X" enable booleans.\n         b. (optional) Force-stop package so XML is honored on next 
launch.\n      3. After launch grace, read actual bounds.  Pick the right window/task\n         for the package 
(surface, focus, visibility).\n      4. If actual bounds differ, try direct resize via root.\n      5. Retry: 
re-write, re-stop, re-readback.\n      6. Set result.status = LAYOUT_APPLIED / UNVERIFIED / FAILED.\n\n    Returns one 
:class:`ApplyResult` per rect.  Never raises.  Never prints.\n    """\n    caps = _capability_probes()\n    
_log.debug("apply_window_layout caps=%s", caps)\n\n    results: list[ApplyResult] = []\n    root_info = 
android.detect_root()\n    root_tool = root_info.tool if root_info.available else None\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 Layer 1: 
enable freeform / resizable-activity capabilities \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    # 
Without enable_freeform_support=1 and force_resizable_activities=1,\n    # the system refuses to honor non-fullscreen 
launch bounds or\n    # ``cmd activity resize-task`` for Roblox.  This is the missing\n    # foundation that App 
Cloner XML alone cannot replace.\n    try:\n        from .freeform_enable import setup_freeform_capabilities\n        
freeform_result = setup_freeform_capabilities()\n        _log.debug(\n            "freeform_setup: root=%s enabled=%s 
already=%s failed=%s",\n            freeform_result.root_available,\n            freeform_result.enabled_keys,\n       
     freeform_result.already_enabled_keys,\n            freeform_result.failed_keys,\n        )\n    except Exception 
as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        _log.debug("freeform setup error: %s", exc)\n        freeform_result = None\n\n    
packages = [r.package for r in rects if not _is_layout_excluded(r.package)]\n    known_keys_map = 
_discover_known_keys(packages, root_tool)\n\n    for rect in rects:\n        result = 
ApplyResult(package=rect.package, desired=rect)\n\n        if _is_layout_excluded(rect.package):\n            
result.status = LAYOUT_SKIPPED\n            result.detail = "excluded (Termux/system)"\n            
result.attempts.append("skip-excluded")\n            result.final_ok = True   # excluded packages are not "failures"\n 
           results.append(result)\n            continue\n\n        # Step 1: pre-write\n        _write_one_package(\n  
          rect,\n            root_tool=root_tool,\n            known_keys=known_keys_map.get(rect.package, []),\n      
      result=result,\n        )\n\n        # Step 2: force-stop so prefs reload on next launch\n        if 
force_stop_before and result.pre_write_ok:\n            try:\n                
android.force_stop_package(rect.package)\n                result.attempts.append("force-stop ok")\n            except 
Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n                result.attempts.append(f"force-stop error: {exc}")\n\n        
results.append(result)\n\n    if not verify_after:\n        for r in results:\n            if r.status == 
LAYOUT_SKIPPED:\n                continue\n            if r.pre_write_ok:\n                r.final_ok = True\n         
       r.status = LAYOUT_UNVERIFIED\n                r.detail = r.detail or "pre-write succeeded; verification 
skipped"\n            else:\n                r.final_ok = False\n                r.status = LAYOUT_FAILED\n            
    r.detail = r.detail or "pre-write failed; verification skipped"\n        return results\n\n    # Step 3-5: 
post-launch verification + direct resize + retry\n    for attempt in range(max(1, retries + 1)):\n        all_ok = 
True\n        for result in results:\n            if result.status == LAYOUT_SKIPPED:\n                continue\n\n    
        # Make sure a window/task actually exists before we judge bounds.\n            
_wait_for_window(result.package, timeout=wait_for_window_seconds)\n\n            bounds, source = 
read_actual_bounds(result.package)\n            result.actual_bounds = bounds\n            result.actual_method = 
source\n            result.attempts.append(f"verify-attempt-{attempt}: {source}={bounds}")\n\n            if bounds is 
None:\n                # Could not verify ù keep current status, mark Unverified.\n                result.final_ok = 
result.pre_write_ok\n                result.status = LAYOUT_UNVERIFIED if result.pre_write_ok else LAYOUT_FAILED\n     
           all_ok = False\n                continue\n\n            if _bounds_close_enough(bounds, result.desired, 
tolerance):\n                result.final_ok = True\n                result.status = LAYOUT_APPLIED\n                
continue\n\n            all_ok = False\n\n            # Step 4: direct resize via root\n            if root_tool:\n    
            ok, detail = _direct_resize_via_root(\n                    result.package, result.desired, root_tool\n     
           )\n                result.direct_resize_ok = ok\n                result.attempts.append(f"direct-resize: 
{detail}")\n                if ok:\n                    time.sleep(0.6)\n                    bounds, source = 
read_actual_bounds(result.package)\n                    result.actual_bounds = bounds\n                    
result.actual_method = source\n                    if bounds and _bounds_close_enough(bounds, result.desired, 
tolerance):\n                        result.final_ok = True\n                        result.status = LAYOUT_APPLIED\n  
                      continue\n\n            # Step 5: re-write keys then force-stop again so next launch\n           
 # picks up the corrected prefs.\n            if attempt + 1 < max(1, retries + 1):\n                rewrote = 
_write_one_package(\n                    result.desired,\n                    root_tool=root_tool,\n                   
 known_keys=known_keys_map.get(result.package, []),\n                    result=result,\n                )\n           
     if rewrote:\n                    try:\n                        android.force_stop_package(result.package)\n       
                 result.attempts.append("retry-force-stop ok")\n                    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: 
BLE001\n                        result.attempts.append(f"retry-force-stop error: {exc}")\n            # Default to 
FAILED for now ù the next attempt may upgrade it.\n            result.final_ok = False\n            result.status = 
LAYOUT_FAILED\n\n        if all_ok:\n            break\n\n    # Finalize details\n    for r in results:\n        if 
r.status == LAYOUT_SKIPPED:\n            continue\n        if r.status == LAYOUT_APPLIED:\n            r.detail = (\n  
              f"applied via {r.pre_write_method}, verified by {r.actual_method}"\n            )\n        elif r.status 
== LAYOUT_UNVERIFIED:\n            r.detail = (\n                f"pre-write OK via {r.pre_write_method}; bounds not 
readable"\n            )\n        else:\n            r.detail = (\n                f"layout not honored "\n            
    f"(pre_write={r.pre_write_ok}, actual_bounds={r.actual_bounds})"\n            )\n\n    return results\n\n\ndef 
apply_window_layout_silent(\n    rects: Iterable[WindowRect],\n    *,\n    force_stop_before: bool = False,\n    
relaunch_after: bool = False,\n    verify_after: bool = True,\n    retries: int = 1,\n) -> tuple[int, int]:\n    
"""Silent wrapper: returns (success_count, total_count).  Never raises.\n\n    "Success" means status is 
``LAYOUT_APPLIED`` OR ``LAYOUT_SKIPPED``.\n    """\n    try:\n        results = apply_window_layout(\n            
list(rects),\n            force_stop_before=force_stop_before,\n            relaunch_after=relaunch_after,\n           
 verify_after=verify_after,\n            retries=retries,\n        )\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n   
     _log.debug("apply_window_layout_silent error: %s", exc)\n        return 0, 0\n    ok = sum(\n        1 for r in 
results\n        if r.status in (LAYOUT_APPLIED, LAYOUT_SKIPPED)\n    )\n    return ok, len(results)\n\n\ndef 
force_resize_package(package: str, rect: WindowRect) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    """One-shot resize for a single package 
ù used during supervisor recovery.\n\n    Pipeline:\n      1. Ensure freeform/resizable system settings are on.\n      
2. Direct resize via root (cmd activity resize-task / am task resize /\n         am stack resize / wm task resize / 
windowing-mode flip).\n      3. Read back bounds; return whether they match desired ▒ 32 px.\n\n    Never raises.  
Returns ``(ok, detail)``.\n    """\n    try:\n        from .freeform_enable import setup_freeform_capabilities\n       
 setup_freeform_capabilities()\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n    root_info = 
android.detect_root()\n    if not root_info.available or not root_info.tool:\n        return False, "no root"\n    
try:\n        ok, detail = _direct_resize_via_root(package, rect, root_info.tool)\n    except Exception as exc:  # 
noqa: BLE001\n        return False, f"resize error: {exc}"\n    if not ok:\n        return False, detail\n    
time.sleep(0.6)\n    try:\n        bounds, _src = read_actual_bounds(package)\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n 
       return ok, f"{detail} (post-readback failed)"\n    if bounds and _bounds_close_enough(bounds, rect, 32):\n      
  return True, f"{detail} \u2192 bounds verified {bounds}"\n    return False, f"{detail} \u2192 bounds still {bounds} 
(want {rect.left,rect.top,rect.right,rect.bottom})"\n\n"""Landscape-block window layout and safe App Cloner XML 
updates.\n\nLayout model\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n \u250c\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n \u2502  Left panel (35%)                 \u2502  Right pane (65%)            
\u2502\n \u2502  DENG Tool / Termux status        \u2502  Roblox clone windows        \u2502\n \u2502  logo, package 
table, logs        \u2502  (freeform, landscape-shaped)\u2502\n \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n\n
Landscape-block rules (all window counts):\n  - Every Roblox window MUST be landscape-shaped: width >= height * 
1.25.\n  - No two windows may touch or overlap; minimum gap is GAP_PX on each side.\n  - Windows are stacked 
vertically inside the right pane at full pane width\n    and 16:9 height.  When they do not fit at 16:9, height is 
compressed\n    until either landscape is impossible or 2-column layout is needed.\n  - 2-column layout is used only 
when single-column compression would make\n    windows too short to remain landscape.\n  - Outer margin separates 
windows from screen edges.\n\nFailure handling: every write path is wrapped in try/except.\nNo crash, no block to 
Start.  All layout details go to debug log.\n"""\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport 
math\nimport os\nimport re\nimport shutil\nimport time\nimport xml.etree.ElementTree as ET\nfrom dataclasses import 
dataclass\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Iterable\n\nfrom . import android\n\n_log = 
logging.getLogger("deng.rejoin.window_layout")\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Safety: packages that must NEVER be resized or 
repositioned 
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n_LAYOUT_EXCLUDE_PREFIXES: 
tuple[str, ...] = (\n    "com.termux",\n    "com.android.",\n    "android",\n    "com.google.",\n    "com.samsung.",\n 
   "com.huawei.",\n    "com.miui.",\n    "com.oneplus.",\n    "com.oppo.",\n    
"com.vivo.",\n)\n\n_LAYOUT_EXCLUDE_EXACT: frozenset[str] = frozenset({\n    "com.termux",\n    "com.termux.boot",\n    
"com.termux.api",\n    "com.termux.styling",\n    "com.termux.widget",\n    "android",\n    "com.android.launcher",\n  
  "com.android.launcher3",\n    "com.android.systemui",\n})\n\n\ndef _is_layout_excluded(package: str) -> bool:\n    
pkg = package.strip().lower()\n    if pkg in _LAYOUT_EXCLUDE_EXACT:\n        return True\n    return 
any(pkg.startswith(pfx) for pfx in _LAYOUT_EXCLUDE_PREFIXES)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Layout constants \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nAPP_CLONER_KEYS = {\n    # 
Moons multi-clone: launch keys control initial position at app start.\n    # Confirmed by probe p-2dbada99a0 (Kaeru) ù 
these are what Kaeru writes.\n    "app_cloner_launch_window_left":    "left",\n    "app_cloner_launch_window_top":     
"top",\n    "app_cloner_launch_window_right":   "right",\n    "app_cloner_launch_window_bottom":  "bottom",\n    # 
Current-window keys are updated by the running app and also read at\n    # startup on some App Cloner variants.\n    
"app_cloner_current_window_left":   "left",\n    "app_cloner_current_window_top":    "top",\n    
"app_cloner_current_window_right":  "right",\n    "app_cloner_current_window_bottom": "bottom",\n}\n\n# Left side 
reserved for DENG Tool / Termux status panel.\nTERMUX_LOG_FRACTION = 0.35          # 35% left \u2192 65% 
right\nRIGHT_PANE_FRACTION  = 1.0 - TERMUX_LOG_FRACTION\n\n# Landscape aspect ratio: height = width * 
LANDSCAPE_H_RATIO\n# 16:9 \u2192 ratio = 9/16 \u2248 0.5625; we use 9:16 as denominator\nLANDSCAPE_H_RATIO: float = 
9.0 / 16.0   # window height = width ╫ this\n\n# Gap between adjacent windows (pixels). 0 = windows touch side-by-side 
with no space.\nGAP_PX: int = 0\n\n# Outer margin between screen edge / pane boundary and nearest window.\n# 0 = 
windows fill the pane edge-to-edge and start at the very top.\nOUTER_MARGIN: int = 0\n\n# Minimum window dimension 
(safety floor ù below this layout is abandoned).\n_MIN_WIN_W: int = 160\n_MIN_WIN_H: int = 90\n\n# Landscape 
threshold: width must be at least this times height.\nLANDSCAPE_MIN_RATIO: float = 1.25\n\n# Legacy cascade constant 
(kept for backward-compat test references only).\nKAERU_TITLE_BAR_H: int = 48\n\n# \u2500\u2500 App Cloner extended 
key aliases \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n#\n# App Cloner / cloud-phone versions disagree on the key names for window\n# 
bounds, the "Set window position" / "Set window size" enable booleans, and\n# the per-orientation portrait/landscape 
variants.  Without the enable booleans\n# turned ON, App Cloner ignores the position ints on next launch ù that is 
the\n# root cause of "windows still use last manually opened position".\n#\n# We write ALL known aliases.  Extras are 
harmless (App Cloner only honors the\n# ones it knows; unknown keys are kept in shared_prefs without 
effect).\nAPP_CLONER_POSITION_ALIASES: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {\n    "left":   (\n        # Moons multi-clone 
(probe p-2dbada99a0): the **launch** key is\n        # what the app reads on startup to set its initial position ù 
this\n        # is the KEY Kaeru writes and the one that actually works.\n        # The "current" key is what the app 
updates while running.\n        "app_cloner_launch_window_left",    # \u2190 Moons: initial position (Kaeru writes 
this)\n        "app_cloner_current_window_left",   # \u2190 Moons: last known position (also write for safety)\n       
 "app_cloner_window_position_left",\n        "app_cloner_window_position_landscape_left",\n        
"app_cloner_window_position_x",\n        "app_cloner_window_x",\n        "app_cloner_saved_bounds_left",\n        
"app_cloner_saved_bounds_landscape_left",\n        "app_cloner_task_bounds_left",\n    ),\n    "top": (\n        
"app_cloner_launch_window_top",     # \u2190 Moons: initial position (Kaeru writes this)\n        
"app_cloner_current_window_top",\n        "app_cloner_window_position_top",\n        
"app_cloner_window_position_landscape_top",\n        "app_cloner_window_position_y",\n        "app_cloner_window_y",\n 
       "app_cloner_saved_bounds_top",\n        "app_cloner_saved_bounds_landscape_top",\n        
"app_cloner_task_bounds_top",\n    ),\n    "right": (\n        "app_cloner_launch_window_right",   # \u2190 Moons: 
initial position (Kaeru writes this)\n        "app_cloner_current_window_right",\n        
"app_cloner_window_position_right",\n        "app_cloner_window_position_landscape_right",\n        
"app_cloner_saved_bounds_right",\n        "app_cloner_saved_bounds_landscape_right",\n        
"app_cloner_task_bounds_right",\n    ),\n    "bottom": (\n        "app_cloner_launch_window_bottom",  # \u2190 Moons: 
initial position (Kaeru writes this)\n        "app_cloner_current_window_bottom",\n        
"app_cloner_window_position_bottom",\n        "app_cloner_window_position_landscape_bottom",\n        
"app_cloner_saved_bounds_bottom",\n        "app_cloner_saved_bounds_landscape_bottom",\n        
"app_cloner_task_bounds_bottom",\n    ),\n    "width": (\n        "app_cloner_window_width",\n        
"app_cloner_window_size_width",\n        "app_cloner_window_size_landscape_width",\n        
"app_cloner_custom_screen_size_width",\n    ),\n    "height": (\n        "app_cloner_window_height",\n        
"app_cloner_window_size_height",\n        "app_cloner_window_size_landscape_height",\n        
"app_cloner_custom_screen_size_height",\n    ),\n}\n\n# Boolean "Set X" enable flags ù these MUST be true for App 
Cloner to honor the\n# corresponding int values on next launch.  Writing the int alone is not 
enough.\nAPP_CLONER_ENABLE_ALIASES: tuple[str, ...] = (\n    "app_cloner_set_window_position",\n    
"app_cloner_set_window_position_enabled",\n    "app_cloner_window_position_enabled",\n    
"app_cloner_set_window_size",\n    "app_cloner_set_window_size_enabled",\n    "app_cloner_window_size_enabled",\n    
"app_cloner_custom_screen_size",\n    "app_cloner_enable_custom_screen_size",\n    "app_cloner_enable_resize",\n    
"app_cloner_force_resize",\n    "app_cloner_allow_resize",\n    "app_cloner_freeform",\n    
"app_cloner_freeform_window",\n    # Orientation enable flags (force landscape on the cloned window)\n    
"app_cloner_force_landscape",\n    "app_cloner_orientation_landscape",\n    "app_cloner_set_orientation_landscape",\n  
  # Auto-DPI landscape flag (Kaeru clue: "look for Set in auto-DPI pot/land")\n    "app_cloner_auto_dpi_landscape",\n  
  "app_cloner_set_auto_dpi_landscape",\n    "app_cloner_set_dpi_landscape",\n)\n\n# Keys that App Cloner / cloud-phone 
window managers may use to remember the\n# LAST manually-positioned window state.  If we leave these populated with 
a\n# stale value, the OS happily restores that stale rect right back on top of\n# the one we just wrote.  By 
overwriting these to the *desired* rect too,\n# the "restore last window" code path becomes a no-op (the "last" 
position\n# is what we want).\nAPP_CLONER_LAST_POSITION_ALIASES: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {\n    "left": (\n       
 "last_window_position_x",\n        "last_window_x",\n        "user_window_position_x",\n        
"manual_window_position_x",\n        "freeform_last_x",\n        "freeform_window_last_x",\n        
"floating_window_last_x",\n    ),\n    "top": (\n        "last_window_position_y",\n        "last_window_y",\n        
"user_window_position_y",\n        "manual_window_position_y",\n        "freeform_last_y",\n        
"freeform_window_last_y",\n        "floating_window_last_y",\n    ),\n    "width": (\n        "last_window_width",\n   
     "user_window_width",\n        "manual_window_width",\n        "freeform_last_width",\n        
"freeform_window_last_width",\n        "floating_window_last_width",\n    ),\n    "height": (\n        
"last_window_height",\n        "user_window_height",\n        "manual_window_height",\n        
"freeform_last_height",\n        "freeform_window_last_height",\n        "floating_window_last_height",\n    
),\n}\n\n# Categories whose keys we will always set when writing ù even if not\n# pre-existing in the file (App Cloner 
reads them if present, ignores if not).\n_WRITE_CATEGORIES_FOR_VALUES: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {\n    # category 
\u2192 list of *aliases* to write with the same integer value\n    "position_left":   
APP_CLONER_POSITION_ALIASES["left"],\n    "position_top":    APP_CLONER_POSITION_ALIASES["top"],\n    
"position_right":  APP_CLONER_POSITION_ALIASES["right"],\n    "position_bottom": 
APP_CLONER_POSITION_ALIASES["bottom"],\n    "size_width":      APP_CLONER_POSITION_ALIASES["width"],\n    
"size_height":     APP_CLONER_POSITION_ALIASES["height"],\n}\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Data classes \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n@dataclass(f
rozen=True)\nclass DisplayInfo:\n    width:   int\n    height:  int\n    density: 
int\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass WindowRect:\n    package: str\n    left:    int\n    top:     int\n    right:  
 int\n    bottom:  int\n\n    @property\n    def win_w(self) -> int:\n        return self.right - self.left\n\n    
@property\n    def win_h(self) -> int:\n        return self.bottom - self.top\n\n    def as_dict(self) -> dict[str, 
int | str]:\n        return {\n            "package": self.package,\n            "left":    self.left,\n            
"top":     self.top,\n            "right":   self.right,\n            "bottom":  self.bottom,\n        }\n\n    def 
preview_line(self, index: int) -> str:\n        w = self.win_w\n        h = self.win_h\n        ratio = f"{w/h:.2f}" 
if h > 0 else "\u221e"\n        return (\n            f"  {index}. {self.package[:36]:<36} "\n            
f"l={self.left} t={self.top} r={self.right} b={self.bottom}"\n            f"  ({w}╫{h}, ratio={ratio})"\n        
)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Display detection \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef parse_wm_size(output: str) -> tuple[int, int] | None:\n    for token in 
output.replace(":", " ").split():\n        if "x" in token:\n            left, right = token.lower().split("x", 1)\n   
         if left.isdigit() and right.isdigit():\n                return int(left), int(right)\n    return 
None\n\n\ndef parse_wm_density(output: str) -> int | None:\n    for token in output.replace(":", " ").split():\n       
 if token.isdigit():\n            return int(token)\n    return None\n\n\n# Display-info parsing regexes.  We 
deliberately do NOT try to balance\n# the outer ``mOverrideDisplayInfo=DisplayInfo{...}`` braces ù the\n# string 
contains nested braces from ``modes [{...}]`` which break naive\n# ``[^}]*`` patterns.  Instead we locate the marker, 
then scan the next\n# few thousand characters for ``app W x H`` and ``density N``.\n_OVERRIDE_MARKER_RE = 
re.compile(r"mOverrideDisplayInfo=DisplayInfo\\{")\n_BASE_MARKER_RE     = 
re.compile(r"mBaseDisplayInfo=DisplayInfo\\{")\n_APP_SIZE_RE        = 
re.compile(r"\\bapp\\s+(\\d+)\\s*x\\s*(\\d+)")\n_DENSITY_RE         = 
re.compile(r"\\bdensity\\s+(\\d+)\\b")\n_LOGICAL_FRAME_RE   = re.compile(\n    
r"logicalFrame=Rect\\((\\d+),\\s*(\\d+)\\s*-\\s*(\\d+),\\s*(\\d+)\\)",\n)\n# Max chars we\'ll scan past a DisplayInfo 
marker for app/density values.\n# A real ``mOverrideDisplayInfo`` block is ~1.5 KB; 2500 is a safe 
ceiling.\n_DISPLAY_INFO_SCAN_CHARS = 2500\n\n\ndef _scan_display_info_block(text: str, marker_re: re.Pattern[str]) -> 
tuple[int, int, int] | None:\n    """Find a ``DisplayInfo{...}`` block by marker, then extract\n    ``app W x H`` and 
``density N`` within the next ~2.5 KB.\n\n    Returns ``(width, height, density)`` or ``None`` if the block or its\n   
 fields cannot be found.  ``density`` is 0 when not present in the\n    block (callers should treat 0 as "unknown").\n 
   """\n    mark = marker_re.search(text)\n    if not mark:\n        return None\n    block = 
text[mark.end():mark.end() + _DISPLAY_INFO_SCAN_CHARS]\n    sm = _APP_SIZE_RE.search(block)\n    if not sm:\n        
return None\n    w, h = int(sm.group(1)), int(sm.group(2))\n    dm = _DENSITY_RE.search(block)\n    density = 
int(dm.group(1)) if dm else 0\n    return (w, h, density)\n\n\ndef _detect_display_from_dumpsys() -> tuple[int, int, 
int] | None:\n    """Read ``dumpsys display`` for the ACTIVE (rotation-adjusted) logical\n    size and density.\n\n    
Real-device evidence (probe ``p-1239f2b5f9`` on SM-N9810 / Android 10):\n    ``wm size`` returned ``Physical size: 
720x1280`` (the sensor\'s native\n    portrait orientation), but the display was actively in **rotation 1\n    
(landscape)** with::\n\n        mOverrideDisplayInfo=DisplayInfo{ ... app 1280 x 720 ...\n                             
             rotation 1, density 164 ... }\n        mViewports=[DisplayViewport{ ... logicalFrame=Rect(0, 0 - 1280, 
720),\n                                      deviceWidth=1280, deviceHeight=720 }]\n\n    Our layout calc was 
computing on a 720╫1280 grid \u2192 bounds landed off-\n    screen for the actual 1280╫720 surface.  
``mOverrideDisplayInfo.app``\n    is the runtime-effective logical size we want.\n\n    Returns ``(width, height, 
density)`` or ``None`` if dumpsys is\n    unavailable or the patterns don\'t match.\n    """\n    try:\n        res = 
android.run_android_command(["dumpsys", "display"], timeout=8)\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        return 
None\n    if not res.ok or not res.stdout:\n        return None\n    text = res.stdout\n\n    # Priority 1 ù override 
block (rotation-adjusted active geometry).\n    override = _scan_display_info_block(text, _OVERRIDE_MARKER_RE)\n    if 
override:\n        return override\n\n    # Priority 2 ù first viewport\'s logicalFrame.\n    m = 
_LOGICAL_FRAME_RE.search(text)\n    if m:\n        l, t, r, b = (int(g) for g in m.groups())\n        return (max(1, r 
- l), max(1, b - t), 0)\n\n    # Priority 3 ù base block (rotation 0 reference).\n    base = 
_scan_display_info_block(text, _BASE_MARKER_RE)\n    if base:\n        return base\n\n    return None\n\n\ndef 
detect_display_info() -> DisplayInfo:\n    """Probe the real display size + density of the host.\n\n    Strategy (in 
priority order):\n\n    1. ``dumpsys display`` \u2192 ``mOverrideDisplayInfo.app W x H`` ù the\n       
rotation-adjusted ACTIVE logical size, which is what bounds need\n       to be expressed in.  Falls back to 
``logicalFrame``, then base.\n\n    2. ``wm size`` \u2192 physical pixel grid.  This is the SENSOR orientation\n       
and is wrong when the device is rotated (probe ``p-1239f2b5f9``:\n       wm reports 720╫1280 portrait while the 
user-facing display is\n       1280╫720 landscape).  Used only as a backstop.\n\n    3. Hardcoded ``(1080, 1920)`` 
final fallback so the layout calculator\n       never sees a zero-size grid.\n\n    Uses 
:func:`android.run_android_command` so:\n      * the bare name ``wm``/``dumpsys`` is auto-resolved to its absolute\n   
     ``/system/bin/`` path (Termux\'s ``$PATH`` excludes ``/system/bin``);\n      * the call is routed through ``su`` 
on permission denial.\n    """\n    # Layer 1 ù dumpsys display (rotation-aware).\n    rot = 
_detect_display_from_dumpsys()\n    if rot:\n        w, h, density = rot\n        if w >= 320 and h >= 320:\n          
  return DisplayInfo(width=w, height=h, density=density or 420)\n\n    # Layer 2 ù wm size + wm density 
(rotation-naive).\n    size_result    = android.run_android_command(["wm", "size"],    timeout=6)\n    density_result 
= android.run_android_command(["wm", "density"], timeout=6)\n    size    = parse_wm_size(size_result.stdout)     if 
size_result.ok    else None\n    density = parse_wm_density(density_result.stdout) if density_result.ok else None\n    
width, height = size or (1080, 1920)\n    return DisplayInfo(width=width, height=height, density=density or 
420)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Validation helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef validate_layout_rects(\n    rects: list[WindowRect],\n    pane_x0: int,\n    pane_y0: 
int,\n    pane_x1: int,\n    pane_y1: int,\n) -> list[str]:\n    """Validate a list of layout rectangles.\n\n    
Returns a list of violation strings (empty = all OK).\n    Checks: landscape, no-overlap, no-touch, inside pane, 
unique bounds.\n    """\n    errors: list[str] = []\n    for i, r in enumerate(rects):\n        w, h = r.win_w, 
r.win_h\n        if w < _MIN_WIN_W or h < _MIN_WIN_H:\n            errors.append(f"rect[{i}] {r.package}: too small 
({w}╫{h})")\n        if w < h * LANDSCAPE_MIN_RATIO:\n            errors.append(\n                f"rect[{i}] 
{r.package}: NOT landscape ({w}╫{h}, need w>={h*LANDSCAPE_MIN_RATIO:.0f})"\n            )\n        if r.left < pane_x0 
or r.right > pane_x1:\n            errors.append(f"rect[{i}] {r.package}: x out of pane ({r.left}-{r.right} vs 
{pane_x0}-{pane_x1})")\n        if r.top < pane_y0 or r.bottom > pane_y1:\n            errors.append(f"rect[{i}] 
{r.package}: y out of pane ({r.top}-{r.bottom} vs {pane_y0}-{pane_y1})")\n\n    # Pairwise: no overlap, no touch\n    
for i in range(len(rects)):\n        for j in range(i + 1, len(rects)):\n            a, b = rects[i], rects[j]\n       
     # Touch check: requires gap >= 1 between any two windows\n            if not (a.right + GAP_PX <= b.left or 
b.right + GAP_PX <= a.left or\n                    a.bottom + GAP_PX <= b.top or b.bottom + GAP_PX <= a.top):\n        
        errors.append(\n                    f"rect[{i}] and rect[{j}] overlap or touch too closely "\n                 
   f"(gap required={GAP_PX}px)"\n                )\n\n    seen: set[tuple[int, int, int, int]] = set()\n    for i, r 
in enumerate(rects):\n        key = (r.left, r.top, r.right, r.bottom)\n        if key in seen:\n            
errors.append(f"rect[{i}] {r.package}: duplicate bounds")\n        seen.add(key)\n\n    return errors\n\n\n# Minimum 
top-of-screen safe area to avoid placing windows behind the\n# Android status bar.  Probe p-ea167faf5f confirmed the 
status bar frame\n# is mFrame=[0,0][1280,25] ù i.e. 25 px tall.  We detect this dynamically\n# from dumpsys window 
policy and fall back to this constant when unavailable.\nSAFE_TOP_INSET_PX: int = 25\n\n_status_bar_height_cache: int 
| None = None\n\n\ndef detect_layout_orientation(width: int, height: int) -> str:\n    """Return ``landscape`` or 
``portrait`` for layout planning."""\n    return "landscape" if int(width) >= int(height) else "portrait"\n\n\ndef 
_detect_status_bar_height() -> int:\n    """Return the Android status bar height in pixels.\n\n    Queries ``dumpsys 
window policy`` for the TYPE_STATUS_BAR frame.\n    Result is cached for the lifetime of the process.\n    Falls back 
to :data:`SAFE_TOP_INSET_PX` (25 px, confirmed from probe\n    p-ea167faf5f) if the query fails or produces no usable 
result.\n    Never raises.\n    """\n    global _status_bar_height_cache\n    if _status_bar_height_cache is not 
None:\n        return _status_bar_height_cache\n    # Set fallback before calling android to avoid re-entrance on 
error.\n    _status_bar_height_cache = SAFE_TOP_INSET_PX\n    try:\n        res = android.run_command(["dumpsys", 
"window", "policy"], timeout=4)\n        if res.ok and res.stdout:\n            # Match: mAttrs={(0,0)(fillxN) 
ty=STATUS_BAR}\n            # or:    (0,0)(fillxN) ... TYPE_STATUS_BAR\n            for pattern in (\n                
r"ty=STATUS_BAR[^;]*fillx(\\d+)",\n                r"fillx(\\d+)[^;]*ty=STATUS_BAR",\n                
r"TYPE_STATUS_BAR[^;]*fillx(\\d+)",\n                r"fillx(\\d+)[^;]*TYPE_STATUS_BAR",\n            ):\n             
   m = re.search(pattern, res.stdout)\n                if m:\n                    h = int(m.group(1))\n                
    if 10 <= h <= 120:  # sanity bounds\n                        _status_bar_height_cache = h\n                        
_log.debug(\n                            "[DENG_REJOIN_LAYOUT_SAFE_Y] status_bar_height=%d 
source=dumpsys_window_policy",\n                            h,\n                        )\n                        
break\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        pass\n    return _status_bar_height_cache\n\n\ndef 
_landscape_rows_for_count(count: int) -> int:\n    """Return the safe landscape row count for up to six packages."""\n 
   if count <= 2:\n        return 1\n    if count <= 4:\n        return 2\n    return 3\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Core 
landscape-block layout engine \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef calculate_landscape_blocks(\n    packages: Iterable[str],\n    display_w: int,\n    
display_h: int,\n    *,\n    gap: int = GAP_PX,\n    outer: int = OUTER_MARGIN,\n    left_fraction: float = 
TERMUX_LOG_FRACTION,\n) -> list[WindowRect]:\n    """Calculate freeform-window landscape blocks for Roblox 
packages.\n\n    Algorithm:\n    1. Left panel (35%) reserved for DENG/Termux ù never touched.\n    2. Right pane = 
remaining 65% minus outer margins.\n    3. Target window aspect ratio: 16:9 (width:height).\n    4. PHASE 1: All 
windows at full pane width, 16:9 height, stacked vertically.\n       If total height fits \u2192 top-align the stack 
at the top of the pane.\n    5. PHASE 2: If single column is too tall, compress window height until\n       the stack 
fits. Continue only while the window remains landscape\n       (width >= height * LANDSCAPE_MIN_RATIO).\n    6. PHASE 
3: If even compressed single-column windows would be too short\n       to remain landscape, use 2 columns with 16:9 
windows.\n    7. PHASE 4: 3 columns (for very large N).\n    8. Validate all results; fall back to safer layout if 
validation fails.\n\n    All coordinates are absolute screen coordinates (ready for App Cloner XML).\n    """\n    
pkgs = [p for p in packages]\n    n = len(pkgs)\n    if n == 0:\n        return []\n\n    # Screen bounds\n    W = 
max(1, int(display_w))\n    H = max(1, int(display_h))\n    g = max(1, int(gap))\n    om = max(0, int(outer))\n    
orientation = detect_layout_orientation(W, H)\n    _log.debug(\n        "[DENG_REJOIN_LAYOUT_ORIENTATION] 
orientation=%s screen_w=%d screen_h=%d",\n        orientation, W, H,\n    )\n\n    # Left panel reservation\n    
left_end = round(W * max(0.1, min(0.9, float(left_fraction))))\n\n    # Right pane absolute bounds (with outer 
margins).\n    # [DENG_REJOIN_LAYOUT_SAFE_Y] probe_id=p-ea167faf5f\n    # The top of the right pane must never be 
below the Android status bar.\n    # Probe confirmed status bar = 25 px (mFrame=[0,0][1280,25]).\n    # 
_detect_status_bar_height() reads the live value from dumpsys and\n    # falls back to SAFE_TOP_INSET_PX if 
unavailable.\n    _sb_h = _detect_status_bar_height()\n    px0 = left_end + om           # left edge of pane\n    py0 
= max(om, _sb_h)          # top edge of pane ù at least below status bar\n    px1 = W - om                  # right 
edge of pane\n    py1 = H - om                  # bottom edge of pane\n    pane_w = max(_MIN_WIN_W, px1 - px0)\n    
pane_h = max(_MIN_WIN_H, py1 - py0)\n    _log.debug(\n        "[DENG_REJOIN_LAYOUT_BORDER] detected_border_h=%d 
fallback_border_h=%d applied_top_y=%d",\n        _sb_h, SAFE_TOP_INSET_PX, py0,\n    )\n\n    if orientation == 
"landscape" and n <= 6:\n        cols = 2\n        rows = _landscape_rows_for_count(n)\n        usable_h = 
max(_MIN_WIN_H, py1 - py0)\n        cell_w = max(_MIN_WIN_W, (pane_w - g * (cols - 1)) // cols)\n        # Keep row 
height stable at one third of usable landscape height so\n        # future >6 layouts can extend without changing <=6 
window sizing.\n        cell_h = max(_MIN_WIN_H, usable_h // 3)\n        rects: list[WindowRect] = []\n        for i, 
pkg in enumerate(pkgs):\n            row = i // cols\n            col = i % cols\n            x = px0 + col * (cell_w 
+ g)\n            y = py0 + row * cell_h\n            rects.append(WindowRect(pkg, x, y, min(px1, x + cell_w), 
min(py1, y + cell_h)))\n        _log.debug(\n            "[DENG_REJOIN_LAYOUT_GRID] package_count=%d cols=%d rows=%d 
cell_w=%d cell_h=%d bounds=%s",\n            n, cols, rows, cell_w, cell_h,\n            [(r.left, r.top, r.right, 
r.bottom) for r in rects],\n        )\n        errors = validate_layout_rects(rects, px0, py0, px1, py1)\n        if 
not errors:\n            return rects\n        _log.debug("smart landscape grid validation failed: %s", errors)\n    
elif orientation == "landscape":\n        _log.debug(\n            "[DENG_REJOIN_LAYOUT_GRID] package_count=%d 
cols=skeleton rows=skeleton cell_w=0 cell_h=0 bounds=[]",\n            n,\n        )\n    else:\n        _log.debug(\n 
           "[DENG_REJOIN_LAYOUT_GRID] package_count=%d cols=portrait_skeleton rows=portrait_skeleton cell_w=0 cell_h=0 
bounds=[]",\n            n,\n        )\n\n    def _make_rects_single_col(win_h: int) -> list[WindowRect]:\n        
"""Stack N windows in one column with the given height."""\n        total_h = n * win_h + (n - 1) * g\n        y_start 
= py0  # top-aligned: start at top edge of pane\n        result = []\n        for i, pkg in enumerate(pkgs):\n         
   y = y_start + i * (win_h + g)\n            result.append(WindowRect(pkg, px0, y, px0 + pane_w, y + win_h))\n        
return result\n\n    def _make_rects_grid(cols: int) -> list[WindowRect]:\n        """Lay out in a grid of `cols` 
columns, 16:9 windows."""\n        cell_w = (pane_w - g * (cols - 1)) // cols\n        win_h_target = round(cell_w * 
LANDSCAPE_H_RATIO)\n        rows = math.ceil(n / cols)\n        total_h = rows * win_h_target + (rows - 1) * g\n       
 y_start = py0  # top-aligned: start at top edge of pane\n        result = []\n        for i, pkg in 
enumerate(pkgs):\n            row = i // cols\n            col = i % cols\n            x = px0 + col * (cell_w + g)\n  
          y = y_start + row * (win_h_target + g)\n            result.append(WindowRect(pkg, x, y, x + cell_w, y + 
win_h_target))\n        return result\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 PHASE 1: ideal 16:9 single column \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    ideal_win_h = round(pane_w * LANDSCAPE_H_RATIO)\n    total_ideal 
= n * ideal_win_h + (n - 1) * g\n\n    if total_ideal <= pane_h:\n        rects = 
_make_rects_single_col(ideal_win_h)\n        errors = validate_layout_rects(rects, px0, py0, px1, py1)\n        if not 
errors:\n            _log.debug("landscape_blocks: phase1 (ideal 16:9 single-col), n=%d", n)\n            return 
rects\n        _log.debug("landscape_blocks: phase1 validation failed: %s", errors)\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 PHASE 2: 
compressed single column \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    # Maximum 
compression: make windows shorter until they still fit.\n    # Require: win_w >= win_h * LANDSCAPE_MIN_RATIO \u2192 
min win_h = pane_w / LANDSCAPE_MIN_RATIO\n    min_landscape_h = max(_MIN_WIN_H, int(pane_w / LANDSCAPE_MIN_RATIO))\n   
 compressed_win_h = (pane_h - (n - 1) * g) // n if n > 0 else pane_h\n\n    if compressed_win_h >= min_landscape_h:\n  
      rects = _make_rects_single_col(compressed_win_h)\n        errors = validate_layout_rects(rects, px0, py0, px1, 
py1)\n        if not errors:\n            _log.debug("landscape_blocks: phase2 (compressed single-col h=%d), n=%d", 
compressed_win_h, n)\n            return rects\n        _log.debug("landscape_blocks: phase2 validation failed: %s", 
errors)\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 PHASE 3: 2-column grid \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    for cols in (2, 3):\n        
rects = _make_rects_grid(cols)\n        if not rects:\n            continue\n        errors = 
validate_layout_rects(rects, px0, py0, px1, py1)\n        if not errors:\n            _log.debug("landscape_blocks: 
phase3 (%d-col grid), n=%d", cols, n)\n            return rects\n        _log.debug("landscape_blocks: phase3 %d-col 
validation failed: %s", cols, errors)\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 PHASE 4: emergency fallback ù just spread evenly 
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    # No 
further validation ù at least they\'re unique and inside pane.\n    # Log at DEBUG only ù public stdout must never see 
this.\n    _log.debug("landscape_blocks: all phases failed, using emergency spread for n=%d", n)\n    rows = 
math.ceil(math.sqrt(n))\n    cols = math.ceil(n / rows)\n    cell_w = max(_MIN_WIN_W, (pane_w - g * (cols - 1)) // 
cols)\n    cell_h = max(_MIN_WIN_H, (pane_h - g * (rows - 1)) // rows)\n    win_h = min(cell_h, round(cell_w * 
LANDSCAPE_H_RATIO))\n    result = []\n    for i, pkg in enumerate(pkgs):\n        row = i // cols\n        col = i % 
cols\n        x = px0 + col * (cell_w + g)\n        y = py0 + row * (cell_h + g)\n        
result.append(WindowRect(pkg, x, y, x + cell_w, y + win_h))\n    return result\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Legacy / compat 
wrappers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\
u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef calculate_kaeru_layout(\n    packages:  
   Iterable[str],\n    right_width:  int,\n    total_height: int,\n    gap:          int = GAP_PX,\n) -> 
list[WindowRect]:\n    """Backward-compatible wrapper.  Delegates to calculate_landscape_blocks.\n\n    Coordinates 
are relative to the top-left of the right pane\n    (caller must add the left-pane offset to X if needed).\n\n    
Note: this function no longer applies the old kaeru cascade algorithm.\n    It now produces proper landscape windows 
using the new block engine.\n    The `right_width` and `total_height` args are used as the full canvas\n    so the 
block engine can compute its own pane from them using fraction=0.\n    """\n    pkgs = list(packages)\n    if not 
pkgs:\n        return []\n    # Treat right_width ╫ total_height as the full right-pane canvas\n    # (no further left 
reservation ù caller already passed right-pane dims).\n    rects = calculate_landscape_blocks(\n        pkgs,\n        
right_width,\n        total_height,\n        gap=gap,\n        outer=max(0, gap // 2),\n        left_fraction=0.0,   # 
no additional left reservation\n    )\n    return rects\n\n\ndef calculate_grid_layout(\n    packages: 
Iterable[str],\n    width:    int,\n    height:   int,\n    gap:      int = GAP_PX,\n) -> list[WindowRect]:\n    
"""Uniform grid layout.  Delegates to calculate_kaeru_layout for compat."""\n    return 
calculate_kaeru_layout(packages, width, height, gap)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Primary split layout (used by Start) \u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u
2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef 
calculate_split_layout(\n    packages:             Iterable[str],\n    width:                int,\n    height:         
      int,\n    gap:                  int = GAP_PX,\n    *,\n    termux_log_fraction:  float = TERMUX_LOG_FRACTION,\n) 
-> list[WindowRect]:\n    """Reserve the left panel for DENG/Termux; place landscape blocks on the right.\n\n    
Returns absolute screen coordinates ready for App Cloner XML.\n    """\n    package_list = list(packages)\n    if not 
package_list:\n        return []\n    return calculate_landscape_blocks(\n        package_list,\n        
display_w=max(1, int(width)),\n        display_h=max(1, int(height)),\n        gap=gap,\n        outer=OUTER_MARGIN,\n 
       left_fraction=termux_log_fraction,\n    )\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 App Cloner / generic clone XML writers \u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n#\n# Different clone wrappers store 
their per-package preferences in different\n# files.  App Cloner uses ``pkg_preferences.xml``; Moons multi-clone 
(real\n# probe id p-368a65d699, packages ``com.moons.litesc/d/e``) uses\n# ``<package>_preferences.xml`` plus a global 
``prefs.xml``; some OEM clone\n# managers use ``settings.xml`` or ``cloner_settings.xml``.  We try each\n# candidate 
in turn so a single tool works across all of them.\n\n\ndef app_cloner_prefs_path(package: str) -> Path:\n    
"""Return the canonical App Cloner prefs path for ``package`` (legacy API).\n\n    Kept for backwards-compatibility 
with callers/tests that still ask for a\n    single Path.  New code should use :func:`clone_prefs_candidates`.\n    
"""\n    return Path("/data/data") / package / "shared_prefs" / "pkg_preferences.xml"\n\n\ndef 
clone_prefs_candidates(package: str) -> list[Path]:\n    """Return every clone-wrapper prefs file we know how to 
write.\n\n    Order matters: more-specific names first so we touch the file that\'s\n    most likely to actually steer 
the clone\'s window manager.\n\n    Discovered empirically from real device probes:\n\n    * App Cloner            
\u2192 ``pkg_preferences.xml``\n    * Moons multi-clone     \u2192 ``<package>_preferences.xml`` (e.g.\n      
``com.moons.litesc_preferences.xml``) and ``prefs.xml``\n    * Generic OEM wrappers  \u2192 ``settings.xml`` / 
``cloner_settings.xml``\n    """\n    base = Path("/data/data") / package / "shared_prefs"\n    return [\n        base 
/ "pkg_preferences.xml",\n        base / f"{package}_preferences.xml",\n        base / "prefs.xml",\n        base / 
"cloner_settings.xml",\n        base / "settings.xml",\n    ]\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Multi-alias XML mutators (shared by 
direct and root writers) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef 
_values_from_rect(rect: WindowRect) -> dict[str, int]:\n    """Return position/size values keyed by canonical 
name."""\n    return {\n        "left":   int(rect.left),\n        "top":    int(rect.top),\n        "right":  
int(rect.right),\n        "bottom": int(rect.bottom),\n        "width":  int(rect.win_w),\n        "height": 
int(rect.win_h),\n    }\n\n\ndef _ensure_int_child(root_el: ET.Element, name: str, value: int) -> bool:\n    """Update 
or create ``<int name=name value=value/>``.  Returns True if changed."""\n    for child in root_el:\n        if 
child.attrib.get("name") == name:\n            if child.tag != "int":\n                child.tag = "int"\n             
   if child.text:\n                    child.text = None\n            cur = child.attrib.get("value")\n            
new_val = str(int(value))\n            if cur != new_val:\n                child.set("value", new_val)\n               
 return True\n            return False\n    new = ET.SubElement(root_el, "int")\n    new.set("name", name)\n    
new.set("value", str(int(value)))\n    return True\n\n\ndef _ensure_bool_child(root_el: ET.Element, name: str, value: 
bool) -> bool:\n    """Update or create ``<boolean name=name value=value/>``.  Returns True if changed."""\n    
new_val = "true" if value else "false"\n    for child in root_el:\n        if child.attrib.get("name") == name:\n      
      if child.tag != "boolean":\n                child.tag = "boolean"\n                if child.text:\n              
      child.text = None\n            cur = child.attrib.get("value")\n            if cur != new_val:\n                
child.set("value", new_val)\n                return True\n            return False\n    new = ET.SubElement(root_el, 
"boolean")\n    new.set("name", name)\n    new.set("value", new_val)\n    return True\n\n\ndef 
_apply_layout_keys_to_root(\n    root_el: ET.Element, rect: WindowRect, *, known_keys: Iterable[str] | None = None\n) 
-> int:\n    """Apply every layout-relevant key to a parsed XML root.\n\n    Writes:\n      * Every alias in 
:data:`APP_CLONER_POSITION_ALIASES` for left/top/right/\n        bottom/width/height.\n      * Every flag in 
:data:`APP_CLONER_ENABLE_ALIASES` set to ``true``.\n      * Any pre-existing keys whose name matches one of the 
position/size\n        aliases or the legacy ``app_cloner_current_window_*`` set ù even if\n        they were stored 
as ``string`` or ``long`` ù are updated.\n\n    Returns the number of keys created/modified.\n    """\n    values = 
_values_from_rect(rect)\n    changed = 0\n\n    # 1. Legacy compat: update old "current_window" mapping if present 
(string/int)\n    for child in root_el:\n        legacy_key = APP_CLONER_KEYS.get(child.attrib.get("name") or "")\n    
    if not legacy_key:\n            continue\n        new_val = str(values[legacy_key])\n        if child.tag == 
"int":\n            if child.attrib.get("value") != new_val:\n                child.set("value", new_val)\n            
    changed += 1\n        else:\n            if (child.text or "") != new_val:\n                child.text = new_val\n 
               changed += 1\n\n    # 2. Position/size aliases ù write every known alias as <int>.\n    for canon, 
aliases in APP_CLONER_POSITION_ALIASES.items():\n        v = values[canon]\n        for name in aliases:\n            
if _ensure_int_child(root_el, name, v):\n                changed += 1\n\n    # 3. Critical "Set" enable booleans ù 
these must be true so App Cloner\n    #    actually honors the position/size ints on next launch.\n    for name in 
APP_CLONER_ENABLE_ALIASES:\n        if _ensure_bool_child(root_el, name, True):\n            changed += 1\n\n    # 3b. 
Override last/manual position keys (Layer 5 from the spec).\n    # When App Cloner / the cloud phone "restores the 
last window position"\n    # on relaunch, that path reads keys like ``last_window_position_x``.\n    # Make those 
equal to the rect we just wrote so the restore is a no-op.\n    for canon, aliases in 
APP_CLONER_LAST_POSITION_ALIASES.items():\n        v = values[canon]\n        for name in aliases:\n            if 
_ensure_int_child(root_el, name, v):\n                changed += 1\n\n    # 4. Honor pre-discovered known_keys names 
by also writing matching\n    #    int/bool variants (covers App Cloner versions we have not enumerated).\n    if 
known_keys:\n        from .layout_discovery import _classify  # local import (avoids cycle at import time)\n\n        
for name in known_keys:\n            if not name:\n                continue\n            if any(name in aliases for 
aliases in APP_CLONER_POSITION_ALIASES.values()):\n                continue  # already handled in step 2\n            
if name in APP_CLONER_ENABLE_ALIASES:\n                continue  # already handled in step 3\n            cats = 
_classify(name)\n            target_value: int | None = None\n            if "position_left" in cats or "size_width" 
in cats and "left" in name.lower():\n                target_value = values["left"]\n            elif "position_top" in 
cats:\n                target_value = values["top"]\n            elif "position_right" in cats:\n                
target_value = values["right"]\n            elif "position_bottom" in cats:\n                target_value = 
values["bottom"]\n            elif "size_width" in cats:\n                target_value = values["width"]\n            
elif "size_height" in cats:\n                target_value = values["height"]\n            if target_value is not 
None:\n                if _ensure_int_child(root_el, name, target_value):\n                    changed += 1\n          
      continue\n            # Boolean-y enable categories\n            if any(\n                c in cats\n            
    for c in (\n                    "set_position_enable",\n                    "set_size_enable",\n                   
 "set_dpi_enable",\n                    "freeform",\n                    "auto_dpi",\n                    
"orient_landscape",\n                )\n            ):\n                if _ensure_bool_child(root_el, name, True):\n  
                  changed += 1\n\n    return changed\n\n\ndef _serialize_xml(root_el: ET.Element) -> str:\n    
"""Serialize ``root_el`` to a valid Android shared_prefs XML string."""\n    body = ET.tostring(root_el, 
encoding="unicode")\n    return "<?xml version=\'1.0\' encoding=\'utf-8\' standalone=\'yes\' ?>\\n" + body\n\n\ndef 
_validate_xml(text: str) -> bool:\n    try:\n        ET.fromstring(text)\n        return True\n    except 
ET.ParseError:\n        return False\n\n\ndef update_app_cloner_xml(\n    package: str,\n    rect: WindowRect,\n    
*,\n    known_keys: Iterable[str] | None = None,\n) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    """Write window position+size+enable 
flags via direct file access.\n\n    Walks every candidate in :func:`clone_prefs_candidates`; the first one\n    that 
exists and is writable wins.  Returns (ok, message); never raises.\n    """\n    attempted: list[str] = []\n    
last_error = ""\n    for path in clone_prefs_candidates(package):\n        if not path.exists():\n            
attempted.append(f"{path.name}: missing")\n            continue\n        try:\n            backup = 
path.with_suffix(f".xml.bak-{int(time.time())}")\n            try:\n                shutil.copy2(path, backup)\n       
     except OSError:\n                pass\n            tree    = ET.parse(path)\n            root_el = 
tree.getroot()\n            changed = _apply_layout_keys_to_root(root_el, rect, known_keys=known_keys)\n            if 
changed == 0:\n                attempted.append(f"{path.name}: no keys changed (already desired)")\n                
continue\n            serialized = _serialize_xml(root_el)\n            if not _validate_xml(serialized):\n            
    attempted.append(f"{path.name}: XML invalid after mutation")\n                continue\n            
path.write_text(serialized, encoding="utf-8")\n            _log.debug("Direct XML write OK: %s \u2192 %s (%d keys)", 
package, path.name, changed)\n            return True, f"Updated {path.name} ({changed} keys)"\n        except 
PermissionError:\n            last_error = "Permission denied"\n            attempted.append(f"{path.name}: permission 
denied (try root)")\n        except (OSError, ET.ParseError) as exc:\n            last_error = str(exc)\n            
attempted.append(f"{path.name}: {exc}")\n    if not attempted:\n        return False, "no clone prefs candidates 
checked"\n    return False, "no writable clone prefs file: " + "; ".join(attempted[:5])\n\n\ndef _layout_tmp_dir() -> 
Path:\n    """Return a writable temp dir inside the DENG install root for staging XML files."""\n    home = 
os.environ.get("HOME", "/data/data/com.termux/files/home")\n    tmp = Path(home) / ".deng-tool" / "rejoin" / ".tmp"\n  
  tmp.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    return tmp\n\n\ndef update_app_cloner_xml_root(\n    package:    str,\n  
  rect:       WindowRect,\n    root_tool:  str,\n    timeout:    int = 10,\n    *,\n    known_keys: Iterable[str] | 
None = None,\n) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    """Write clone-wrapper window prefs via root.\n\n    Strategy: Python writes 
the XML to a Termux-accessible temp file (no root\n    needed for that step), then root ``cp`` + ``chown`` + ``chmod`` 
moves it\n    into the app\'s private data dir.  This avoids any dependency on ``base64``\n    or ``python3`` being 
present in the ``su`` shell\'s PATH (they are Termux\n    binaries and are *not* on the system PATH the root shell 
inherits).\n\n    Returns (ok, message); never raises.\n    """\n    attempted: list[str] = []\n    for path in 
clone_prefs_candidates(package):\n        path_str = str(path)\n        try:\n            # Check existence via root 
(avoids permission errors for direct stat).\n            exists_res = android.run_root_command(\n                
["sh", "-c", f"test -f \'{path_str}\' && echo Y || echo N"],\n                root_tool=root_tool, timeout=timeout,\n  
          )\n            exists = (exists_res.stdout or "").strip().startswith("Y")\n            if not exists and 
path.name != "pkg_preferences.xml":\n                attempted.append(f"{path.name}: missing")\n                
continue\n            # Read existing content (create empty <map> if file is absent/unreadable).\n            read_res 
= android.run_root_command(\n                ["sh", "-c", f"test -f \'{path_str}\' && cat \'{path_str}\' 
2>/dev/null"],\n                root_tool=root_tool, timeout=timeout,\n            )\n            if not read_res.ok 
or not (read_res.stdout or "").strip():\n                root_el = ET.Element("map")\n            else:\n              
  try:\n                    root_el = ET.fromstring(read_res.stdout)\n                except ET.ParseError as exc:\n   
                 attempted.append(f"{path.name}: parse error: {exc}")\n                    continue\n            
changed = _apply_layout_keys_to_root(root_el, rect, known_keys=known_keys)\n            if changed == 0:\n             
   attempted.append(f"{path.name}: no keys changed (already desired)")\n                continue\n            new_xml 
= _serialize_xml(root_el)\n            if not _validate_xml(new_xml):\n                attempted.append(f"{path.name}: 
XML invalid after mutation")\n                continue\n\n            # \u2500\u2500 Write strategy: temp file in 
Termux space \u2192 root cp \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n            # Python writes 
the XML to a Termux-writable staging file.  Root\n            # then copies it to the target with correct 
owner/permissions.\n            # This avoids needing ``base64`` or ``python3`` in the su PATH.\n            safe_pkg 
= package.replace(".", "_")\n            local_tmp = _layout_tmp_dir() / f"layout_{safe_pkg}_{path.name}"\n            
local_tmp_str = str(local_tmp)\n            try:\n                local_tmp.write_text(new_xml, encoding="utf-8")\n    
        except OSError as exc:\n                attempted.append(f"{path.name}: local tmp write failed: {exc}")\n      
          continue\n\n            copy_cmd = (\n                f"mkdir -p \'/data/data/{package}/shared_prefs\' && 
"\n                f"cp -f \'{local_tmp_str}\' \'{path_str}\' && "\n                f"chown 
--reference=\'/data/data/{package}\' \'{path_str}\' 2>/dev/null || "\n                f"chown $(stat -c \'%u:%g\' 
\'/data/data/{package}\') \'{path_str}\' 2>/dev/null; "\n                f"chmod 660 \'{path_str}\'; "\n               
 f"rm -f \'{local_tmp_str}\'"\n            )\n            write_res = android.run_root_command(\n                
["sh", "-c", copy_cmd], root_tool=root_tool, timeout=timeout,\n            )\n            # Clean up local staging 
file regardless of outcome.\n            try:\n                local_tmp.unlink(missing_ok=True)\n            except 
OSError:\n                pass\n            if write_res.ok:\n                _log.debug("Root XML write OK: %s \u2192 
%s (%d keys)", package, path.name, changed)\n                return True, f"Wrote {path.name} via root/cp ({changed} 
keys)"\n            err = (write_res.stderr or write_res.stdout or "")[:120]\n            
attempted.append(f"{path.name}: cp failed: {err}")\n        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            
_log.debug("update_app_cloner_xml_root error for %s @ %s: %s", package, path.name, exc)\n            
attempted.append(f"{path.name}: writer error: {exc}")\n    if not attempted:\n        return False, "no clone prefs 
candidates"\n    return False, "Root write failed: " + "; ".join(attempted[:5])\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Apply layout 
(high-level) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25
00\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2
500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef apply_layout_to_packages(\n    packages:  
       Iterable[str],\n    *,\n    gap:              int  = GAP_PX,\n    write_xml:        bool = False,\n    
use_split_layout: bool = False,  # kept for compat; split is always used\n) -> tuple[list[str], list[dict[str, int | 
str]]]:\n    """Calculate landscape block layout and optionally write App Cloner XML.\n\n    Returns (messages, 
preview_list).\n    """\n    try:\n        display = detect_display_info()\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n    
    display = DisplayInfo(width=1080, height=1920, density=420)\n\n    package_list = [p for p in packages if not 
_is_layout_excluded(p)]\n    if not package_list:\n        return ["No Roblox packages to lay out (all excluded or 
empty)."], []\n\n    rects = calculate_split_layout(package_list, display.width, display.height, gap)\n\n    preview  
= [rect.as_dict() for rect in rects]\n    messages = [rect.preview_line(i) for i, rect in enumerate(rects, 1)]\n\n    
if not write_xml:\n        return messages, preview\n\n    root = android.detect_root()\n    root_tool: str | None = 
root.tool if root.available else None\n\n    write_msgs: list[str] = []\n    for rect in rects:\n        try:\n        
    ok, msg = update_app_cloner_xml(rect.package, rect)\n            if not ok and root_tool:\n                ok, msg 
= update_app_cloner_xml_root(rect.package, rect, root_tool)\n            write_msgs.append(f"{rect.package}: {msg}")\n 
       except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            _log.debug("Layout write skipped for %s: %s", 
rect.package, exc)\n            write_msgs.append(f"{rect.package}: layout skipped ({exc})")\n\n    return write_msgs, 
preview\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Verification / preview helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u250
0\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ndef build_layout_preview(\n    
packages: Iterable[str],\n    display:  DisplayInfo | None = None,\n    gap:      int = GAP_PX,\n) -> list[str]:\n    
disp  = display or detect_display_info()\n    rects = calculate_split_layout(list(packages), disp.width, disp.height, 
gap)\n    return [rect.preview_line(i) for i, rect in enumerate(rects, 1)]\n\n\ndef verify_split_layout(\n    
packages: Iterable[str],\n    display:  DisplayInfo | None = None,\n    gap:      int = GAP_PX,\n) -> list[str]:\n    
disp         = display or detect_display_info()\n    package_list = list(packages)\n    left_end     = int(disp.width 
* TERMUX_LOG_FRACTION)\n    lines        = [\n        f"Display: {disp.width}╫{disp.height}  
density={disp.density}",\n        f"Left pane (Termux): 0û{left_end}px ({int(TERMUX_LOG_FRACTION*100)}%)",\n        
f"Right pane (Roblox): {left_end}û{disp.width}px ({int(RIGHT_PANE_FRACTION*100)}%)",\n    ]\n    rects = 
calculate_split_layout(package_list, disp.width, disp.height, gap)\n    errors = validate_layout_rects(\n        
rects,\n        left_end + OUTER_MARGIN,\n        OUTER_MARGIN,\n        disp.width - OUTER_MARGIN,\n        
disp.height - OUTER_MARGIN,\n    )\n    if errors:\n        lines.append("VALIDATION FAILURES:")\n        
lines.extend(f"  ! {e}" for e in errors)\n    for i, rect in enumerate(rects, 1):\n        
lines.append(rect.preview_line(i))\n    return lines\n\n"""DENG Tool: Rejoin phone-agent package."""\n\n__all__ = 
["__version__"]\n\n__version__ = "1.0.0"\n\n# BROKEN LEGACY CODE ù DO NOT USE IN LIVE START PATH.\n#\n# Archived after 
live probes p-f1a4aaafe5 / p-8b025e8c3c because this smart\n# Android/UI detection caused SIGSEGV, false Joining/Join 
Unconfirmed states,\n# private URL relaunch failures, and endless restart loops.\n#\n# This code is kept only for 
historical reference.\n# Live Start must not import or call this module.\n# Future real state detection must use 
Roblox Presence API, not\n# uiautomator/logcat/UI dump.\n#\n# Specific issues:\n#   - _probe_uiautomator: triggered 
SIGSEGV on Termux via Android Accessibility Service\n#   - _probe_logcat: unreliable for App Cloner packages 
(truncated process names)\n#   - _probe_dumpsys_activity: produced false Lobby detection, caused Joining loop\n#   - 
detect_experience_state: used by old supervisor for post-launch state classification,\n#     triggering the "stuck in 
Joining" loop and URL resend storms\n#\n"""Best-effort Android experience-state detector for Roblox. (ARCHIVED ù 
BROKEN)\n\nClassifies what Roblox is currently doing by combining evidence from:\n  1. dumpsys activity (top/resumed 
activity name and task stack)\n  2. logcat (bounded recent entries for this PID)\n  3. uiautomator dump (optional XML 
UI hierarchy, graceful failure)\n\nDesign goals:\n  * Never raises ù all paths silently degrade to weak evidence.\n  * 
Never reads secrets (cookies, tokens, sessions, account files).\n  * Bounded execution ù each probe has an explicit 
short timeout.\n  * All side-effects via android.run_command which is already testable.\n  * Runs only when 
transitioning FROM Launching/Joining states, never on\n    steady-state healthy polls (no performance cost in normal 
operation).\n"""\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import 
IntEnum\nfrom typing import Optional\n\n# Archived: import removed to prevent accidental live use\n# from . import 
android\n# from .config import validate_package_name\n\n\nclass EvidenceLevel(IntEnum):\n    """Strength of evidence 
about the current Roblox runtime state."""\n\n    PROCESS_ONLY             = 1  # Process exists; foreground/activity 
unknown\n    FOREGROUND_APP           = 2  # Package confirmed as foreground; activity unclear\n    
ROBLOX_HOME_OR_LOBBY     = 3  # Signal indicates home screen / lobby / menu\n    JOINING_PRIVATE_URL      = 4  # URL 
intent dispatched; waiting for join\n    EXPERIENCE_LIKELY_LOADED = 5  # Strong signal: game / experience screen 
active\n    JOIN_FAILED_OR_HOME      = 6  # After URL: evidence shows home/lobby; join likely 
failed\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass ExperienceEvidence:\n    """Evidence record returned by 
``detect_experience_state``."""\n\n    level: EvidenceLevel\n    detail: str\n    source: str           # "logcat", 
"dumpsys_activity", "uiautomator", "foreground", "none"\n    raw_snippet: str = "" # Brief safe excerpt for debug 
logging (no secrets)\n\n    def is_in_game(self) -> bool:\n        return self.level == 
EvidenceLevel.EXPERIENCE_LIKELY_LOADED\n\n    def is_home_or_lobby(self) -> bool:\n        return self.level in (\n    
        EvidenceLevel.ROBLOX_HOME_OR_LOBBY,\n            EvidenceLevel.JOIN_FAILED_OR_HOME,\n        )\n\n\n#
